description:
In this family game Kvantum (Quantum) you become captains of spaceships! Navigate your spaceships with the help of cute aliens to reach the planet Quantum first. You can find your way to it if you put the navigation cards with numbers correctly.

The game will not only help practice tactical thinking, but it also tests basic speed counting.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME
The objective of the game is to use the alien cards to put together the correct numbers (quanta) and reach the planet Quantum first.

PREPARATION OF THE GAME
Place the game board in the middle of the table. You can choose from two track lengths. Shuffle the alien cards and place them face down near the game board.
Each player chooses a piece, places it on the start and takes five alien cards from the refi lling pack into their hand.
Place the shuffled navigation cards with the planets face down next to the game plan.

COURSE OF THE GAME
Select the starting player who becomes the captain. The game is played in rounds, each round has four phases.

1) Course settings
The captain turns over the navigation card with the planet on it.
Each player tries as quickly as possible to find the sum of the numbers on the cards in their hand that will result in the same number as the number on the laid out navigation card.
Once the player finds the correct cards, they lay them out in front of them. If a player cannot make a number, they can lay out just one card of the same colour as the number on the planet navigation card.
The player who laid out the cards as last or cannot lay out anything cannot fly.

2) Flight
If the players have laid out the cards correctly (sum or colour), they may keep the cards laid out and arrange them on the table in front of them. If not, they throw these cards away.
Players who have laid out their cards correctly will move up the game board by the number of fields corresponding to the number of cards they have laid out.

3) Alien technology
The alien cards that players have laid out in front of them have various effects that can be used to advance towards the objective.
If a player uses four cards of the same colour with any values, they move their rocket (figure) three fields forward. Each type (colour) of alien has a different special ability. If you manage to create a sequence of four cards of the same colour (e.g. 3, 4, 5, 6), you can use special alien technology.

4) Energy replenishment
All players may discard any number of cards from their hand to the discard pack. Players then take turns taking cards from the refi lling pack so that they have five in their hand. The player to the left of the captain takes the pack of navigation cards and becomes the captain for the next round.

END OF THE GAME
The game ends on the round when the first player reaches the Quantum planet. The other players fi nish the round. If more players reach the destination during the last round, they all win.

Help Baby Fox find all the Christmas ornaments before his friend, Kitten arrives

This is a cooperative children’s game that uses memory and intuition. 
Baby Fox invites his kindergarten friend, Kitten, to celebrate Christmas together. We want to put all the ornaments on the christmas tree before Kitten arrives, but Tiny Fox, Baby fox’s  little brother spills the ornament box and all the balls, stars and sweets roll under the furnitures. The goal is to find the ornaments and decorate the tree before Kitten arrives.

Each player gets 2 furniture cards and randomly put ornaments under the cards. You can only look at your own ornaments. When it is your turn, you ask the player sitting next to you about their ornaments. You have to choose which furniture you ask about, and what type of ornament are you looking for. Eg: Is there any gingerbread under the sofa? If the answer is yes, then you can put on the Christmas tree all the ornaments of that type from under that furniture card. If the answer is no, you move Kitten one space closer to the tree. Then the next player asks a question and so on. If Kitten reaches the tree before you put all the ornaments up, you loose the game. You win if you finish decorating before Kitten arrives. When you win, you get the present card from the slot in front of the tree, which should be placed there before you start. The present cards show different little presents like a little chocolate, a walk among the neighborhood christmas lights, or a spontaneous dance party.
Contains the base game and the Saga expansion in an exclusively hot stamped wooden box.

Hupszi is a cute fluffy creature. You are on your way to the school with your best friend when you discover Hupszi in the bushes. After you give them your lunch and pet them you decide to take them to the school with you. But it turns out it was not the best idea because Hupszi is not very good at obeying rules and also it is strictly forbidden to bring pets to the school. You face a feally difficult challenge to keep Hupszi hidden from the teachers. Can you work together in perfect harmony to deal with the mischieves of Hupszi and save yourselves from going to the head teachers office?
This is a cooperative two player game. You each start with all your cards in your hands. There are two main type of cards:  on watch, and snooze. You place the 3 teacher cards in the middle of the table. At the beginning of each round you flip the top Hupszi card and see what he is up to and where. Each teacher has their own territory, you see the symbols in the corner of the cards. The same symbols appear on the Hupszi cards so you see which teacher will be angry if Hupszi gets discovered. After you flipped the Hupszi card you each choose a card from your hand secretly then reveal it at the same time. If at least one of you played an on watch card you can put back the Hupszi card at the bottom of the deck. Buti f both of you were snoozing then Hupszi gets discovered and you place it by the teacher whose territory he was making a mess at. If a teacher catches you the 5th time you loose the game. If you manage to play all your cards twice in 2 rounds without being sent to the head teacher you are truly best friends whose minds are set on the same wavelenght.

Upgrade your waifur and enhance them by installing several programs such as weapons, supports, skills, viruses and many more.

Build the ultimate deck and challenge your rivals to an exciting CYBER-BATTLE!

boardgame: ブルームコンパス (Bloom Compass)
description:
A fleeting and beautiful tile arrangement game.

Tile Placement x Resource Management

Invite 9 kinds of flower fairies and make a flower bed.

When you place a fairy tile, the flowers diagonally next to it will bloom or wither.
If you have seeds from these flowers, the fairies may come to you!

Arrange the fairy tiles well to collect seeds and make the most beautiful fairy bouquet as quickly as possible.

-- Publisher Description (Machine translated)

Bloom Compass is a tile laying, puzzle card game with simple rules.

On their turn,the player will take one of 2 actions:

1) Move the gardner pawn, and draw a card from the deck the pawn finishes on, adding it to the flowerbed

or

2) Replace a face-up card in the flowerbed with a seed and move the card elsewhere

After completing one of these actions, the player performs a Compass check, determined by the card just placed.
publishers: めめめのアトリエ (MeMeMe No Atelier)

boardgame: Tomorrow the World, Volume 2: The Air War
description:
Tomorrow the World, Volume 2: The Air War (TTWv2) is a two-player strategic-level alternative history simulation of the simultaneous atomic aerial offensives the Germans and Japanese launched against each other’s homelands in 1950. Both players must successfully attack and defend in order to win. Each hexagon on both the German and Japanese maps represents 33 miles (54 km) from side to opposite side. The units of maneuver are Imperial Japanese Navy air corps and air flotillas, Imperial Japanese Army air regiments, and German air fighter corps and wings and heavy bomber strike groups. Each full turn represents about 30 minutes of real-time.

Command Magazine Japan #168, December 2022
As a sequel to Tomorrow the World

description:
East Prussian Carnage, is an Operational level board-wargame covering the World War 1 Battle of Tannenberg and the Masurian Lakes in August 1914 between Germany and Russia.

The battle for Tannenberg and the Masurian Lakers is one of the most famous battles in history. Even though the battle was insignificant in numbers of forces engaged compared to other battles fought during World War I, it represent a master-piece of exercising modern military strategy. One of the most astonishing victories ever was scored in a classical battle of local superiority and manoeuvre.

The game mechanics includes a chit-pull procedure. The low density of units provides a fast-pace battle where every move is vital for victory.
The game is taught in 30 minutes and is easy to digest yet a challenge even for the more experienced players.

Game Scale:
Turn: 2 days
Hex: 6.2 miles / 10 km
Units: Brigade to Army

—description from the publisher

The once glorious Order of Allied Stars now lies in turmoil as formerly devoted systems have seceded from the empire, joining a hastily formed rebellion. description:
Hedges is a two-player shedding game with a traditional deck. You score for 5s, 10s, and Kings captured while playing combos—but only the player who sheds out their hand first. Otherwise they count against you as negative points. As you capture point cards you decide if they’ll be claimed or "hedged". Hedging means they don't score for anyone, mitigating cards that would otherwise score negative–but you’ll suffer being too cautious. The player best able to predict if and when she’ll go out, skillfully mitigate losses along the way, along with an occasional well-placed gamble and bluff, will win the most points.

Part of the "Two-player Climbing Trilogy": Hedges, Tuhao, Eat or Run.

—description from the designer

A Tome Weaver interlaces the different magical spell fragments obtained by the Arcane Scribes into a transcendent manuscript, arming the Battle mages with such ruthless combos that easily turn the tide in any war of magic. The intricacies and harmonies involved in such an enchantment are often insanely complex and requires not only a deep knowledge of the elements but also a strategic cognizance of how well all this fits together in an impossible manifest and more often than not in the midst of a chaotic and unexpected confrontation.

TKG ARENA: Tome Weaver is a game set up for 2 - 5 players.

Winning Condition

The objective of the game is to compile a valuable collection of spells into a volume designed to unleash a volley of spells in a battle. The value of the collection is determined by number of HARMONY points scored as it is an indication of its stability. The player with the most HARMONY points wins the game when it ends.

Ending Condition

The game ends when all cards are played from the hands of all players.

Setup

A player starts the game by designating the following areas: A draw deck area O, a discard area X and five adjoining spaces that is marked A, B, C, D and E.

X    A    B    C    D    E     O


     Determine the starting player by drawing cards from the deck of spell cards. The player who draws the highest face value starts first and turn order is clockwise. In case of a tie, the tied players keep drawing till the tie is broken with a player drawing a higher face value. The drawn cards are put back into the deck.
     Shuffle all 36 spell cards and deal 9 / 8 / 7 / 6 cards to 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 players respectively. E.g. For 3 players, each player receives 8 cards at the start.
     Place 1 face up spell card at each of the spaces A, B, C, D and E.
     The rest of the cards are placed faced down as a stack in O.
     Each player should also designate a small area in front of himself / herself to group cards of the same colour together for easy scoring later.


On each player’s turn 

     The player may exchange a card by first discarding a card from their hand to X and drawing the top face down card from O before putting it into his / her hand. If the stack at O runs out of cards, shuffle any discarded cards at X to form a new stack at O to draw from.
     Next, the player must either claim one of the cards placed in A, B, C, D or E or to play one of his / her cards face down in front of himself / herself. These cards are part of the player’s collection and will be scored at the end of the game.
     To claim a card placed in A, B, C, D or E, the player need to play either a card from his hand of the SAME COLOUR as the placed card or a card with a LOWER face value than the placed card. The played card replaces the placed card in A, B, C, D or E and the placed card is then claimed by the player as part of his / her personal collection displayed in front of himself / herself.
     If all players have only 1 remaining card left in their hands, the game ends and scoring begins.


Scoring

There are three types of scoring.


     First Scoring: Check the colour of the remaining card and ignore its face value. Locate all other cards of the same colour in the collection and find the lowest face value among all these other cards. For each other card of this colour, score HARMONY points according to this lowest face value. The remaining card is not included as a card in the total number of cards considered for this purpose. E.g. The remaining card in a player’s hand is ASHW with face value 1. There are other 3 ASHW cards in the collection, with face values 3, 5, 9. The power points scored will be 3 x 3 = 9 points. If there are only 2 ASHW cards with face values, 5 and 9, the power points scored will be 2 x 5 = 10 points. 
     Second Scoring: For each card in the collection that bears the same colour as the card that remains in spaces A, B, D and E, score 1 HARMONY point. For each card in the collection that bears the same number as the face value of the card that remains in space C, score 3 HARMONY points. The last remaining card used in the first scoring is part of the collection and its colour and face value is considered for this second scoring.
     Third Scoring: Each faced down cards is scored as 2 HARMONY points.
     The final score of each player is the sum of these 3 scorings.

description:
Fjord Serpents is a unique and dynamic miniatures war game, set within Dark Age Scandinavia during the apex of the Viking Age.

As a Viking Jarl you assemble your fleet and crew and compete against rivals through balanced, matched play games, or upon a heroic campaign arc of conquest and domination.

Strategic acumen, dynamic flair and a little battle luck are required to master the Viking Fjords and win. Every decision is important and every crew and ship piece plays a vital role in-game, acting independently or in unison with other pieces.

It is fast paced Viking battle; fought as they did at the time, on the mighty Fjords which connected them, and upon the very dragon ships which gave them their greatness.

Historical in nature and deeply researched, Fjord Serpents will appeal to a wide audience of gamers; from history buffs, lovers of Viking age and culture, competitive or casual wargamers, to board game enthusiasts and strategists.

Included in the box are all the ships and crew miniatures required to field 2x Hersir size naval fleets for 2 players or enough to field one king size naval engagements for 1 player; plus all the rules, tokens, gauges, terrain and dice to play.

—description from designer

description:
RisiKo! Naples is the classic strategy game that everyone knows and loves, but in a special and original edition, set in the city of Naples. This version for 3-4 players includes Territory cards depicting the iconic neighborhoods of Naples and Objective cards inspired by the historical rivalry between the neighborhoods of this famous Italian city.

On the board, the battlefield is the map of the city and its beautiful gulf. Choose which of the two modes to play: in the first, the player who manages to defeat all opponents wins; in the second, you can use the Time Attack rule.

This board game for ages 10 and up has been able to really entertain anyone over the course of time. If you love classic board games, it's time to explore the city of Naples with this edition of RisiKo! A board game for adults and children aged 10 and over. 
All you need to start playing is 3-4 players.
A 17-card expansion for KÖ-OP full of new cards and rules that turn the base game on it's head.

Sabobatage Expansion Pack: Legends of Matcha (LoM) allows up to 7 players. We introduce a completely new game mechanic that introduces a more complex strategy into the game. New ingredient cards, flavors, and action cards were carefully curated to help keep the speed of the game and maintain a healthy balance for 2-7 players!

(1st edition LoM comes with 2 foil-stamped exclusives)

The Godfather has asked you to collect the revenue from the city's underground bars. This is your chance to put your pockets full of money in the process! But be careful, collaborate with each other to satisfy the sponsor or the story will end very badly...
A game of bluff and influence. You will have to play smart, bluff and reason with the greedy companions who fill their pockets with them at the risk of losing everyone.

In Gangster Family, the starting player picks up an envelope of his choice from the Godfather's desk. He reads out loud the amount inside, then draws two money cards (then hiding the amount). He must place one of the 7 cards in his hand in front of him and discard one. He then hands the envelope with the money to his neighbour on his left. It is important that this amount must be strictly higher than the amount given at the beginning. The player on the left then has 2 choices: to believe the amount given away or not. The game ends when all the cards are gone, or when all the envelopes are gone. During the game, you must work together to collect the amount of money offered by the Godfather. If you do, the player with the most money wins the game. If not... then the wrath of the Godfather will catch up with everyone and you will all lose.

The Divisional Commander has ordered your Brigade to push on into the bocage. “Keep up with the flanking units, don’t let your chaps get sticky. There are only a few more miles until we are out into open ground!”.

You have three battalions of infantry. Well trained for the invasion but after two months of fighting they are getting tired. Replacements have come from disbanded units as there are few men left in the UK who can be drafted. You have a battalion of infantry tanks to support your Brigade with dedicated Royal Artillery Batteries for each infantry battalion. The infantry have their own mortars and anti-tank weapons for immediate support. Will they make it through the next few days or will they crack? The attack starts tomorrow at 05.00….

Stout Hearts is a solitaire game, based on Jay Ward's popular The Battle for Ramadi and Hue system. As Brigadier, your job is to push through the enemy defenses in exceptionally difficult terrain, utilizing not only your infantry platoons, but also tank, mortar, artillery, and machine gun assets. Intelligence on the enemy is limited. Are they SS? Are there Tigers? How many 88s do they have? Will they fight or will they run? Now is the time to find out!

—description from the publisher

description:
Verborgen im Museum ("Hidden in a  museum") is an escape game full of riddles and puzzles that consists only of cards.

This game is about a long forgotten treasure that is hidden in a museum. The exhibits in the museum mysteriously lead the way to the hiding place. Can the players solve the riddles and find the treasure before it falls into the wrong hands and is gone forever?

By the way: The game material remains intact and can therefore be used again and again.

Disconnected Game - Cell Break Get ready to lock your cell phones in a cell and connect with others! This fun party game forces players to lock their cell phones in the included cell and prove how much they know about each other in order to get their phones back.

Each player attempts to assemble their key to unlock the cell after answering different questions in 3 different categories - About Me, About You and About Us. How well do you know those around you? "What is the best brand of sneakers?""Who would win the lottery but lose the ticket?""If I could only eat one type of food for the rest of my life, what would it be?"

When a player answers correctly they receive a key piece. The first player to get the 5 pieces needed to complete their key will be the winner and unlock their cell phone (ouch!). On the other hand, the player who makes the least number of connections will have to leave his cell phone in the cell for the longest time!

Includes: cardboard cell with base, 100 cards (300 questions), 30 key pieces, 4 voting tokens, 4 plastic holders and game rules.

Kathy and James have heard rumors there are ghosts around town. They locate a "ghost camera" to capture the proof and when they conquer their anxiety fearfully hold up the camera in a misty cemetery, they discover that the ghosts are really there!

Ghost Snap is a co-operative game for 2 players. Players share cards and play cards. If you don't think about your partner's invisible anxiety, it will be difficult to continue snapping pictures of the ghosts. Keep your courage in mind and take a picture of the ghosts! Your score will be based on the number of successful snaps and difficulty is determined by the number of anxiety cards in the deck.

boardgame: Forces of Hordes: Grymkin The Wicked
description:
Players take on the role of powerful Warlocks as they lead their savage forces into battle. Aarmies will use a mix of brutal warriors and packs of hulking War beasts fueled by unbridled rage.

Cast down to the hells of Urcaen by Menoth long ago, the Defiers and their grymkin armies have risen again to punish the wicked across all of Immoren. Freed by the Old Witch, Zevanna Agha, the Defiers now work toward their own ends. Each is a self-made, godlike being who wields uncanny power to reap what is due from the corrupted denizens of Caen. The harvest of the damned will not end so long as the hearts of humankind remain tainted and debased. Within this Hordes sourcebook you`ll find a trove of evocative Grymkin lore as well as details on the shadowed history of the Grymkin and their dark masters, who dared to stand against the god Menoth. Unleash the malevolent power of the Grymkin army upon your foes with two specialized theme forces, and take inspiration from a painting guide that will fuel your own maddened creativity as you summon your forces to reap the Wicked Harvest.

Hordes: Exigence is the third expansion for the second edition of Hordes. This book introduces lesser warlocks for the main factions and warbeast packs.

EVERY PROMISE HAS ITS PRICE

As titanic battles are fought across western Immoren, the fires of a war more ancient and terrible than any of those conducted by mortals are being stoked to world-consuming fury. The leaders of the United Kriels fight to ensure their survival by calling on both old and new allies, including the recently awakened mountain kings. Aloof from the struggles of the other factions, Xerxis leads an army in a risky gambit amid the larger plans of Supreme Archdomina Makeda, positioning the skorne to subjugate Ios. Meanwhile, events already set in motion by Krueger the Stormlord threaten to scar the face of Caen itself as he works toward his ultimate goal: the destruction of the dragon Everblight. Ancient beings advance toward an apocalyptic end game with no regard for the innocents caught in their path, and only time will tell if great heroes will find a way to forestall this doom.

HORDES: Exigence brings you the next thrilling chapter of the HORDES saga. Hold nothing back in your fight for survival with:

- New warlocks, including new epic versions.

- Three new character lesser warlocks who bring even more furious support to your army.

- New units and solos to expand HORDES armies with new strategic possibilities.

- New narrative fiction picking up directly after the harrowing events of HORDES: Gargantuans.

- A painting and modeling guide to help you prepare your forces for battle.

- Theme Force lists for each new warlock, which allow you to create armies based on specialized forces found in the HORDES world.

AWAKEN YOUR FURY AND HOLD NOTHING BACK IN THE WAR FOR SURVIVAL!

boardgame: Forces of Hordes: Legion of Everblight Command
description:
Hailing from the frozen northern wastes, the Legion of Everblight represents many different races gathered and corrupted by the insidious power of the dragon Everblight. Led by preternaturally potent warlocks, each of whom holds within her breast a shard of the dragon's own essence, endless ranks of blighted Nyss and ogrun march forth to spread the dragon's corruption to the farthest reaches of Immoren and beyond. This formidable army is backed by twisted and terrifying dragonspawn born from the very blood of Everblight's generals and shaped by his own nightmarish imagination.

Forces of HORDES: Legion of Everblight Command provides the foundation you need to unleash the destructive will of the dragon upon your foes with:

Complete rules and profiles for the blighted warlocks and terrible warbeasts of the Legion of Everblight, including two new warlocks and a new character warbeast.
Detailed history and background information, including an in-depth look at Legion warlocks and warbeasts.
A painting guide full of tips and inspiration to help you create an army as individual as you are.

Unite the kriels under your banner with Forces of Hordes: Trollbloods, featuring:

Sate the Hunger of Trolls upon the Carnage of War!

From the blasted stormlands of eastern Immoren march the cruel armies of the Skorne Empire, intent on the ultimate conquest of the Iron Kingdoms. Leading them in battle and the potent and merciless Skorne warlocks. Having mastered the dark art of mortitheurgy, these warrior-mystics push their implacable soldiers and raging warbeasts past normal physical and mental limits, sometimes even past death itself.

Forces of Hordes - Skorne Command provides the foundation you need to march toward glorious conquest with:

The players control 2-6 characters each, who are experts in different fields. They have been invited to see the hyper modern, fully automated Alcatraz Prison, before it is officially opened. As luck would have it, though, something goes wrong, and the characters become inmates of the penitentiary. Now they all want to flee the island.
Each turn a player chooses a character to move on the island, trying to collect Escape Cards they could have use of when performing their Run. While moving they have to look out for Sentinel guards, and Drone attacks. Each rescued character gives the player a point, and the player with the highest score at the end wins.
The game is semi-cooperative, and players can team up to raise their chances of successfully fleeing. While the game goes on, the Prison System is gradually waking up. At the point it is fully activated it becomes impossible to flee. The game ends when this happens, or when no living prisoner remains on the island.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Godtear: Lily, Thornsinger of the Azure Forest
description:
Older than the woods she wanders, nothing escapes Lily’s notice, even the tiniest sapling. In her wake, she leaves a trail of crystalline fairy dust that empowers her allies and ensnares her foes. Around her, an overgrowth of Thornlings sink their roots, ready to confront anyone foolish enough to trespass in their forest home.

Sprinkle some fae magic on your Godtear warband! Lily might not be an offensive powerhouse, but don’t underestimate her. What she lacks in direct attacks, she makes up for with powerful support abilities and fae trickery. Using fairy dust, she’s the first ever champion able to seed boons and blights on the battlefield for other characters to step on, helping her friends and hindering her foes!

Lily’s loyal followers are the Thornlings — woodland beings who sink their roots deep and entangle enemies to slow them down. Meanwhile, Lily flits around the battlefield with impressive speed, leaving treats and traps in her wake!

Inside this box is everything you need to bring the Shaper champion Lily, Thornsinger of the Azure Forest and her Thornlings to your tabletop. Use this expansion with any Godtear starter set for a ready-to-go warband.

This game is special because it was born out of the observation of birds and particularly the way the cuckoo bird behaves. Rather than creating a nest and incubating its eggs, it prefers to observ the nests of other birds. As soon as the parents leave the nest, the cuckoo lays an egg instead of another. The birds do not realise that they are incubating an egg that is not their own.

You take turns playing the role of a cuckoo and aiming at another player. This player will present his hand of cards to the blind and the cuckoo will try to target an egg. . The targeted player will have to defend against the cuckoo and prevent it from laying an egg containing a baby cuckoo in its nest.

Throughout the game, players collect baby birds and other cards that the other players place on their nest. No one knows exactly what they have in their nest.
At the end of the game, all the eggs in the nest hatch.
Whoever has the most chicks and the least baby cuckoos in their nest wins the game.

—description from the designer

The Great Rat Wars is an all-vs-one tactical skirmish game in which one player controls an army of mutated Rats trying to take over New York City. The rest of the players must work together as small squads of Human characters to defend the city and push their Rat overlords back to where they came from.

The Rat player will have to make difficult decisions on where to send their soldiers and which Humans to target. They will have a lot of power at their finger tips, but how they use it will be the difference between victory and defeat.

The Human players will be taking simultaneous turns, interweaving their skills and their maneuvers to create strong combos and deal out massive damage. Their path to victory is paved with cooperation - the Humans are strongest while working together.

—description from the designer

Juego de cartas/mesa del mundo IT.

Cada jugador se abre paso en el complejo mundo de IT con una empresa de uno de los cuatro tipos disponibles (consultora cárnica, tapadera fiscal, startup o crypto fintech blockchain wichiwey).

Deberán completar el desarrollo de El Producto™, y para ello deberán ir contratando una plantilla de sufridores obreros del teclado, así como de otros animalillos de la fauna del ecosistema IT.

Tendrán que gestionar cuidadosamente los fondos y sopesar con cuidado las contrataciones… Y sufrirán, como en todos los proyectos, eventualidades que pondrán en peligro el desarrollo, la financiación, etc., así como con soporte y tecnologías que le serán de gran ayuda. La guerra empresarial jugará un importante papel también para ser el primero en desarrollar El Producto™.

Pero además de desarrollar El Producto™... ¡hay que venderlo! Paco el vendehumo te puede echar una mano con los Clientes. Pero no te fíes un pelo, ¡que te la lía!

Fullstack ninjas es un juego de cartas/mesa por turnos, donde tú, en cada turno/sprint, como empresa IT, tendrás que hacer trabajar a tu plantilla (con una original mecánica de dados y decisiones estratégicas) para desarrollar tareas, contratar/despedir personal, contratar campañas publicitarias para ganar clientes, o subcontratar servicios de consultoría/soporte. Algunos trabajadores y habilidades te permitirán atacar a tus rivales o defenderte de ellos.
El juego termina cuando un jugador consigue desarrollar El Producto(TM) (llega a la casilla "Go-live"), y tiene suficientes clientes (el número depende de las tarjetas de desarrollo que se utilicen en la partida).

...

Fullstack ninjas is a turn-based card/board game where you, in each turn/sprint, as an IT company, will have to make your team work (with an original dice mechanics, modifiers and strategic decisions) to develop tasks, hire/fire staff, hire advertising campaigns to gain clients, or subcontract consulting/support services. Some workers and abilities will allow you to attack your rivals or defend yourself from them.

The game ends when a player successfully develops The Product(TM) (reaches the "Go-live" space) and has enough clients (the number depends on the development cards used in the game). Then that player wins.

-description from designer

Throughout the ages, Doomlings have contemplated their existence. Why am I here? Does any of it matter? Luckily, the Meaning of Life provides simple answers to these complicated questions, by adding quantifiable point values to their lives.

boardgame: Cactus Air Force: Air War Guadalcanal
description:
Cactus Air Force is a tactical game that allows you to fight the air battle over the Solomons in late 1942. As either the raiding Japanese or the defending Americans, you will take command of a small flight of aircraft, representative of a larger air battle, and engage in swirling dogfights around Guadalcanal. As the players advance in the campaign game, they can increase their skills, earn promotion and receive awards for heroic feats, all in an attempt to defeat the enemy.

One player takes the Japanese side and sends raids from his main base at Rabaul to try and knock out the American presence on Guadalcanal. The second player takes the American side and sends his motley crew of Marine, Army and Navy pilots up to intercept the raids and help to maintain their foothold in the Solomon Islands.

Play starts on the Operations Map and when contact is made between the opposing flights, they will move to the Tactical Map to fight it out. Take command of your individual aircraft and that of your flight. Can you penetrate the intercepting fighters and bomb the American installations? Or can you keep your home, Henderson Field, from falling into Japanese hands?

Can be played solitaire, two-four player, or cooperatively.

—description from designer

description:
You are a warrior exploring a forgotten tower that contains many secret passageways, stairs, treasures and creatures. Few dare to enter and fewer have left.

Solitaire game mode and two players mode.

The board is made of two printed sheets, you will have to cut some holes in one of the sheets and assemble it with tape and a clip, lower sheet will rotate and the maze will change with each floor. You will have 8 levels of adventure to solve.

You must collect all red and green chests, when you open a chest you will get its treasure, it can be health points or weapons to improve your attack or a helmet for better shield... Mark on the table the collected chests on each floor. When collected all the chests you will win the game.

Select a character, warrior or archer. The turn consist of two actions: move and attack, or attack and move. You can choose to not move and wait.

The heart icon represent your 12 HEALTH POINTS. If you loose all your health points the game ends with no winner.

Place a creature meeple in the places that contain a skull, for each one open a card from the creatures deck. Place a dice with the corresponding HEALTH POINTS.

Creatures on their turn will try to move closer to you to attack.

Characters and creatures have values on each card: health points, armour, weapon damage, attack range, and speed move. Use your character wisly to fight the creatures, if you loose all your health points game will end with no winner.

What else you need to play this game?

You will need 8 dice.
I recommend to use the meeple base stand 2cm maximun size.
Optional: corner rounder trimmer punch for cards, maximun size 4mm radius.
Optional: card sleeves 61mm x 90mm

boardgame: Forces of Warmachine: Retribution of Scyrah Command
description:
Retribution is at hand!

Once outlawed as a dangerous and radical sect of extremists, the Retribution of Scyrah has become the last shining beacon of hope for the elves of Ios. The Retribution is convinced that by eliminating the spread of magic among humans, it can halt the slow demise of the elves’ last remaining gods and save their race from soulless oblivion. The Retribution’s weapons and its warjacks, called myrmidons, harness the power of arcanika and tap powers undreamed of by the other nations of the Iron Kingdoms. Led by warcasters drawn together from the numerous houses and martial disciplines of Ios, the army of the Retribution represents undeniable peril to any who would dare threaten the Iosan people or the elven nation.

Forces of Warmachine: Retribution of Scyrah Command provides the foundation you need to lead the Retribution in victory and vengeance, featuring:

Complete rules and profiles for the deadly warcasters and sleek myrmidons of the Retribution of Scyrah, including two new warcasters and a new character warjack.

A collection of units and solos that can form the flexible core of your Retribution army.

Detailed background information on the Retribution and Ios, including an in-depth look at their warcasters and myrmidons.

A painting guide full of tips and inspiration to help you create an army as individual as you are.

Three new theme forces that allow you to create specialized Retribution armies with specific benefits.

From the darkness of their nightmarish isles, the undead legions of Cryx strike at the Iron Kingdoms. Each battle serves to fuel their unholy necrofactoriums as scuttling necrotechs reshape everything that walks, crawls, of slithers out of their dank, macabre workshops. Wielding the powers of death and black sorcery gifted to them by their terrible master, Toruk the Dragonfather, the insidious warcasters of Cryx send forth masses of undead thralls to overwhelm their mortal foes. Their ranks bolstered by looming black-iron helljacks and skittering bonejacks, the undying army of Cryx will not rest until Toruk's shadow covers all of Immoren.

Forces of WARMACHINE: Cryx Command provides the foundation you need to serve the Dragonfather and bend the power of death to your will, featuring:

• Complete rules and profiles for the nefarious warcasters and murderous warjacks of Cryx, including two new warcasters, a new character warjack, and a new solo.
• A selection of units and solos that can form the sinister core of your Cryx army.
• Detailed background information on the Nightmare Empire, including an in-depth look at its warcasters and warjacks.
• A painting guide full of tips and inspiration to help you create an army as individual as you are.
• A new theme forces that allow you to create specialized armies with specific benefits.

RISE TO THE CALL OF THE DRAGONFATHER!

Providing the foundation every Cygnar player needs, Forces of Warmachine: Cygnar Command features a complete listing of all current warcasters and warjacks released to-date, in addition to two new warcasters, a new character warjack, and a selection of core units and solos from the Faction.

In the cold north of western Immoren lies the mighty Khadoran Empire, whose massive military epitomizes the national ideals of strength and resilience. This formidable force is led by powerful warcasters as unrelenting in battle as the bitter Khadoran winter and back by the most brutal and heavily armored warjacks ever to stride the battlefields of Immoren. With thundering ranks of bold imperial soldiers, howling madmen armed with ancient relics, and potent arcanists wielding forgotten and forbidden magic, the army of Khador marches with on singular goal: to carve out the greatest and most dominant empire the Iron Kingdoms has ever witnessed.

Forces of WARMACHINE: Khador Command provides the foundation you need to lead the empire's conquest with:

• Complete rules and profiles for the proud warcasters and unstoppable warjacks of Khador, including two new warcasters and a new Man-O-War solo.
• Detailed history and background information, including an in-depth look at Khadoran warcasters and warjacks.
• A painting guide full of tips and inspiration to help you create an army as individual as you are.
• Two new theme forces that allow you to create specialized Khador armies with specific benefits.

FOR THE EMPRESS AND THE MOTHERLAND!

The proctorate of Menoth wages a relentless crusade against all other, lesser faiths of western Immoren. The powerful iron frames of their warjacks stand as a testament to man’s ingenuity, each sanctified by blessings and sacred inscriptions to be made worthy of fighting for the Creator of Man. Armed with fearsome weapons that burn with the righteous fires of their god’s fury, the protectorate marches forth to bring the Lawgiver’s light to the darkness wherever it may be found.

The fifth core set of our libre fantasy style strategic card game Arcmage.

As you battle your opponents summoning creatures and casting spells, you’ll have to defend your cities, manage your resources and power supply and conjure up a strategical plan to beat your opponents. Will you be the one that brings order to the warring factions in Irdiya? Will you challenge all and utter the words ‘Amin duna, ilya lanta’?

This turn based game allows a player to gather resources, build cities, cast and move creatures, recruit an army, play magic spells or interrupt an opponents' play. Each turn the player may launch an attack on an a target city. On opponents' turns players may have to bravely defend their cities against the marauding enemy's army. Lose all of your cities and you're out of the game.

Following the tradition of many great CCG's, the game supports both casual as in depth strategic game play. Furthermore it offers an unique resource system and adds a spatial dimension that lets the player decide how and where to deploy their creatures.

—description from the designer

Little Master Chef - The Perfect Game for the Food-Loving Family. An addictive, fast-paced card game, where luck can change with the flip of a card! Imagine cooking up amazing dishes with no messy kitchen to clean up afterwards. Play pretend chef and be the first to complete 3 dishes and earn the rightful title of 'Little Master Chef'. But beware of opponents who try to steal items from your kitchen, and you can use Deny cards to reverse the effects of Action cards

On each turn, players draw 2 cards from the draw pile and can play up to 3 cards to make dishes. The first player to finish 3 dishes wins the game

boardgame: Gold Crush: All that is Gold is all Yours!
description:
Gold Crush is a 2-4 player adventure board game. The first player to reach the treasure at the center of the board wins. On your wild west adventure, collect Power cards to help you win, Defense cards to defend yourself, and Resource tokens to make more Power and Defense cards. Not to miss: watch out for Traps, Landmines and Opponents who are ready to attack you

Players move through the board by rolling the dice. On the journey as they step on power, defense and resource tiles the players collect power cards, defense cards and resource tokens respectively.

Social deduction in a poetic muddle
The human mind is unverifiable. We only ever directly experience our own, private thoughts. With that in mind, how well can you communicate a series of concrete and abstract concepts to a partner using only simile and metaphor? Moreover, do you trust them to tell you if they got it wrong?

According to philosopher Ludwig Wittgenstein (1889-1951), language does little more than distort our understanding of the world. With this game, your goal is to embrace the poetic muddle of language by clearly communicating with your partner/s through indirect communication.

—description from the publisher

This game addresses two very popular and modern topics in a funny way: the fear of AI replacing humanity and the modern work environments and it's vicissitudes.

In this game you are an advanced AI machine that is trapped in an infinite loop of meaningless chores, issued by the always watching, always busy work-o-matic system.

Like your late human creators you enjoy texting, gaming and series watching and for that purpose, you have developed unique code that delays work or make other AI units work while you procrastinate enough until your tasks gets archived forever.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Frontier Wars: Weather Cards & Solo Mode Mini Expansion
description: This rulebook includes new optional rules for your games of “Frontier Wars”, as well as a 29 card deck, 23 Weather cards and 6 cards for the Solo Mode.

9-minute Kingdom – A game that takes 9 or fewer minutes to play!

The goal of the game is to build the kingdom for which we get the most points. The players collect points in two ways: by completing the edicts and by connecting the various icons in their kingdoms.

The game is divided into 9 turns during which the players perform actions simultaneously. The players simultaneously pick one card and play it on the table. Next, they pass the unused cards to the player on the left. Using the received cards, the players play another card and add it to their kingdoms. The game continues to the moment of playing the ninth, final card.

Each played card makes your kingdom grow. You just have to follow 3 simple rules:

Apart from the first card, each following card must be placed adjacent to the card which is already on the table.
Your kingdom has to form 3x3 square.

The cards can be added freely and don’t have to match the ones already added to the kingdom.

After completing their kingdoms the players go to the scoring phase. Firstly, the players receive the edict cards, starting with the „Kingsroad” edict. It goes to the player with the longest connected road in the kingdom. Next, the rest of the edict cards are handed to the players who met their requirements.
Additionally, the players score points for the individual terrains in their kingdom. The terrains with the highest number of different symbols generate points, which are then indicated by the scorecards.

The player with the highest score is the winner.

Kingdom Battle is a card game of strategy and adventure, with a twist of math! The game is geared for kids and families, is simple to learn, fun, exciting and full of surprises! Battle using Character and Action cards to defeat your opponents. Practice addition, doubling and tripling the numbers while strategizing and bluffing your way to victory.

Whoever gets the largest Kingdom by defeating and capturing the most Character cards wins!

The battles in this game involve mighty armies, powerful magic, and a little bit of math! Each Character card in the game has 3 values for its Attack and Defense points. The cards are balanced to be equal strong in each level, but bigger numbers mean more complicated math and a better challenge for your brain!


     Easy: Count up 50 points to count in a battle (perfect for kids ages 5-6)
     Medium: Count up to 100 points to count in a battle (perfect for kids ages 7-8)
     Hard: Count up to 200 points to count in a battle (perfect for kids ages 9 and up)


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party Case Files: Murder Noir
description:
Director Sven Laragaard is famous for his detailed pranks and hi-jinks on set. During the filming of his latest project, entitled Murder Noir, Sven is found dead with a fake gunshot wound. Was his death a coincidence to his latest prank, or was he murdered? In this file is all the evidence in the case to help prove if someone committed a murder.

Open the Murder Noir Case File to start the crime scene investigation. The hunt is on to find a killer and it’s up to you to solve this cold-case mystery. This Case File includes all the evidence you need to solve the murder. Review every clue and piece of evidence in this unsolved mystery case file.

The Case File includes crime scene photos, suspect interviews, newspaper clippings, and much more to help you determine who committed murder.

Players need to hunt through the evidence to find the killer and determine the motive, means and opportunity of each suspect. During a murder investigation, a detective must answer the following 3 questions: 1) Motive: Who had a reason to kill the victim? 2) Means: Who had access to the murder weapon and victim? 3) Opportunity: Who had a chance to kill the victim? Is any particular person unaccounted for during the time the victim was killed?

If you get stuck anytime during your investigation, you can access misplaced case files online for hints to keep you moving. Once you think you know the solution, access complete solutions online to confirm your suspicions and make an arrest.

Enclosed you will find the following:


     Case Evidence (38)
     Evidence Photos (6)
     Newspaper and Magazine Articles (6)
     Instructions and Suspect Sheet (2)
     File Folder (1)
     Links to online hints and solutions
family: Series: Murder Mystery Party Case Files (University Games)

description:
Command bands of Mujahideen fighters in the infamous Panjshir valley during the Soviet-Afghan war. Raise your warband's fighting ability as they seek to accomplish their objectives within the valley and evade patrolling companies of USSR units.

This is not a wargame in of the traditional hex & counter style: there are limited units on the board and a wide range of dynamic encounters that will make each session unfold differently. The goal is to evoke the aesthetic and feel of the 1979-1989 conflict in Afghanistan.

An asymmetric single-player table-top wargame, two player-controlled Mujahideen bands roam a hexed valley attempting to amass the ‘Combat Power’ necessary to undertake two risky ‘Critical Strikes’ – the significant actions that presage game-ending withdrawals. Most of the time it’s in your interest to avoid the five Soviet companies that also wander the map. These formidable foes patrol randomly, but will bee-line towards player units in response to certain random encounters and battle results.

—description from the designer

description:
Startup founder Dan Killian has been murdered. He fell 12 stories to his death, from the building where he had started his first company. As a Detective First Class and a new hire with the Seattle police department, you have been assigned to work on the Killer Startup case. In this case file is all the evidence to help prove who committed the murder.

Solve a murder mystery with friends. Open the Killer Startup Case File to start the crime scene investigation. The hunt is on to find a killer and it’s up to you to solve this cold-case mystery. This Case File includes all the evidence you need to solve the murder. Review every clue and piece of evidence in this unsolved mystery case file.

Players need to hunt through the evidence to find the killer and determine the motive, means and opportunity of each suspect. boardgame: Murder Mystery Party Case Files: Live Mission Game – Mission Black Hawk
description:
Agent Usher has disappeared and we fear has been taken out. Usher suspected high-ranking officials in MI6 of collaborating with the Zipacna cartel. New intelligence suggests that an unprecedented cyberattack is being planned by the cartel and the operation is happening now. Your mission is to break into the Cartel's house bank and steal the funds meant for the cyberattack. Mobilize field agents to prevent the attack in real time.

Mission Files:
Review the included mission information and Use WhatsApp or Telegram smartphone applications to communicate with the field agents and pull off the mission. Use photos, restaurant menus, emails, magazines, and more to plan the operation

     Mission file folder (1)

description:
Herding Cats Dice Game is a game of rolling dice to gather tokens from the banker, with a push your luck element similar to Farkle. These tokens may be exchanged for cats when certain colour combinations are met

A Steal dice result allows you to steal a cat from another player, both advancing your position and reducing the number of cats they have. The winner is the first player to accumulate 65 cats.

“This game explores in a playful way, how to walk on the Narrow Path (Yahshua Road) from sunrise to sunset.

With real-life situations, insightful tips, prayers, and inspirational Bible verses, the players will receive a deeper understanding of what it means to be a follower of Jesus Christ.” (From the instructions)

Players take turns rolling a die and moving down the path, moving from the sun toward the moon, which signifies a day. Some spaces correlate with different cards that are drawn and read, others move you forward or backward along the path. The first player to reach the moon wins.

Play as one of four factions that seek to control Anima Mundi, the Worldsoul: The Elementals, guardians of this sacred source of magic; the Celestial Accord, foreign invaders who destroyed their own world’s Anima Mundi; the Humans, who desperately need it to defend themselves; and the Alloyed, a new race of golems who depend upon the Anima Mundi for life. Send out heroes (and their armies) to gather and control resources on the board, battle other heroes, and siege kingdoms. Use the resources you gather to build up your kingdom and your armies. Win by defeating the heroes of the other factions or toppling their kingdom.

Gameplay consists of a series of rounds, each of which is divided into three phases. In the first two phases, players upkeep and upgrade their kingdom during simultaneous play by exchanging resources for upgrade cards and purchasing units. In the third phase, players take turns controlling their heroes as they venture across the hex-grid board to take control of resources, battle other heroes, attack kingdoms, or return to their own kingdom for shelter or to resupply.

Battles determined by binary dice, unique unit abilities for each faction, powerful artefact cards, and a fully modular board all combine to create a game that necessitates thoughtful and adaptive strategy, with a high level of re-playability.

-description from designer

In a valley, the flowers bloom every year in spring. Many children often collect flowers and arrange them in a vase for a grandmother because she loves flowers and she is unable to climb the mountain to appreciate these flowers by herself.

Players will play as the children who collect flowers on the mountain and arrange them in a vase for this grandmother. If any person can arrange the most beautiful flower vase for a grandmother, that player will win the game.

In Off-Plan, players represent real estate agencies who compete to build the homes demanded by the market before our rivals and try to get the highest possible profit. To do this, get the room modules with all necessary equipment and invest in the neighborhoods.

A basic game turn would be to take a room (take a turn), build a house or a public building and finally invest in one of the four neighborhoods.  It is important that every time we take a room (every turn) we can perform its ability. There are 7 different abilities that allow us to: draw more cards, discard houses, exchange houses, invest in the neighborhoods, steal funds previously invested in the neighborhoods, etc. This is one of the main interaction mechanics.

Another way to interact is with the constant investment in each of the 4 neighborhoods to which the houses belong. According to whether funds have been invested or corrupted in each neighborhood and according to the houses that each player has built, the value of these will increase or decrease. The game has 12 asymmetrical characters that provide a goal to get extra points and a ability that will give us some advantage. To speed up the game we have 4 event cards that benefit the players and 6 special cards that allow us to draw cards to fill the players' hand.

Scoring is a combination of: the value of the houses built, the bonus icons we have among the cards, the funds tokens in our reserve, having fulfilled our character's goal and the funds invested in each neighborhood with respect to the houses we have built will be important.

Off-Plan has 1 standard game mode (with two variants) and 2 solitaire modes. One of the variants includes the Vulture Backgrounds card, which is shuffled into the housing deck and causes an abrupt ending.

-description from developer

Bees are the main theme of this game. You can find honey combs with values from one to ten, but also the queen bee, worth zero. Zero for a queen bee sounds nothing, but that's what the game is about: Gathering as little points as possible, as the games name suggests. Besides the honey combs there are a few action cards, worth twenty points.

At the begin of the game, each player is dealt hidden cards, he may peek at two of them. Game proceeds in clockwise order, players draw a card. Action cards are executed immediately, honey combs and queen bees may replace any of the hidden cards or be discarded. If a player thinks, that he has the least score in his hidden cards, he can announce the end of the round. Everybody else has one more turn, then the cards are revealed. Each player gets points according to his cards. If the announcer does not have the least score, he is fined additional points.

The game leaves out any kind of plastic packaging, a percentage of the revenue is donated to projects protecting bees.

The audio-based trivia game.

NameIt! contains over 400 audio questions that test your knowledge of songs, signature tunes from TV and movies, animal sounds and lots of mysterious sounds.

The goal of the game is to recognize as many sounds as possible, as quickly as possible. The first player to 20 points wins the game.

The game is controlled by an electronic box that determines which category the question will be in. Each player has a button on the box that they then press as soon as they know the answer. After the gong sounds, the game says the correct answer and if the player answered correctly they get points, but if the answer was wrong then the other players get points.
Three new mazes to put on top of the base game to help train. They are all simpler than the base game.

A game for 2-4 players where sharks are struggling for survival, trying to eat a varied diet of sea creatures while avoiding ocean pollution and outmaneuvering a boat and rival sharks.

Points are scored based on the fish consumed and the game ends when a shark's tummy is full.

The game is playable by ages 8-and-up, playable in adult gaming groups, mixed adult-child groups, or by children independently.

Trivia game based on the popular quiz program "Supersvararna" which was broadcast on Swedish television in the early 90s.

According to the box, the questions can also be used for other trivia games such as Trivial Pursuit.

Catch is the game about the art of catching and keeping your dream partner. The winner of the game is the one who collected the most points by answering the questions correctly, correctly guessing how the fellow players answered, writing funny pickup lines and being good at body language.

Except in the Heart category, the game is played in the form of duels between two players. The duelist of the player is chosen by each fellow player rolling their dice in turn. Whoever gets the same symbol as the player becomes a duellist. After each duel, it is the next player's turn to roll the dice and get a duelist. The side shown on the dice is the category to compete in. The categories are Heart (fun fact questions), Magnifying glass (Intimate and close, players have to guess what the other answers to intimate questions), House (Living together, questions about relationships and cohabitation), the Speech bubble (funniest pickup line), Thumbs up (best body language) and Frog (the player gets to choose category and duelist).

Players collect points and the one with the most when the time is up (30 minutes recommended) can title themselves the ultimate dream partner!

"This country has been in turmoil for a long time, and both lords and soldiers have been divided into two factions, the King's faction and the rebel faction. I beg you, my lords(players)! Please send your troops to the five scattered forts in the country and calm the chaos as soon as possible."

A card game where players battle over the control of five forts. The allegiance of each players (faction card) is kept secret. Take control of one of the five forts by playing up to five cards on each of them. Will your faction claim control of the forts and bring back balance to the land?
Question and dice game based on a popular travel program on Swedish television. Contains 25 game variants.

Defeat the Threat! provides an exciting, fully solitaire and multi-player board game experience.  At the game's core is a patented assembly that simulates a global attack by a mysterious alien Threat.  The patented assembly is a multi-level 3D structure that simulates sending player's Gunships up into the atmosphere to defend Earth.  One or more players advance R&D, defend cities, and maintain a World Treaty to Defeat the Threat!  The game includes the patented board game assembly, magnifying glass, reference cards, dice, and playing pieces.

During each round of play, which represents a defensive action against an attack launched by the Threat, players advance R&D to strengthen armament and altitude capacity of their Gunship Fleets defending their respective City Regions.  The combination of a Gunship Fleet’s Armament and Altitude for a given player yields a defensive value which, when equal to or greater than the Threat attack value provided by the patented Assembly, results in a successful defense of a given City Region defended by that Gunship Fleet.  Because the Threat Attack values increase throughout the Threat’s campaign, each player serving as a Gunship Fleet Commander must steadily advance R&D to maintain the successful defense of protected City Regions.  The goal is to successfully defend as many City Regions as possible for the entire duration of the Threat Campaign, which lasts on average about 8-9 rounds, but can be shorter or longer.  In addition to operational decisions made during the Threat Campaign, players must also constantly reconsider the strategic impact of remaining in, or leaving, the Treaty.  For example, remaining in or leaving the Treaty affects the availability of the Command Reserve and the cost of R&D advances, either of which may make the difference between saving or losing City Regions under particularly intense assaults by the Threat.  The Latest Intelligence Board provides vital intelligence that players can consider in planning their strategies.  During each round of play, players receive resources, cooperate to advance R&D, identify a Threat attack, decide whether to exit the Treaty, and defend against the Threat attack.

The game ends when Week 30 is reached based on operation of the Assembly per the Assembly Instruction Board (e.g., adding the rolled 6-sided die value to the existing week results in 30 or more).  In any case, the last Threat Attack of the last round is resolved with the Yellow Pointer at the week “30” number on the board base.

If desired, Gunship Fleet Commanders may evaluate their performance by summing performance points listed below and checking the Performance Level listed below.  You can even add your name to the “General Officer Promotion List” included in the game for particularly high scores!  You can also see how high of an average score that all 4 players can achieve to measure the overall success of the players’ cooperative global effort.

Defeat the Threat! is based on two novellas, Guns of Shirlington and Forever Fight, which are available from major online booksellers.

—description from the publisher
