description: Air shooter is an action game where you use an air gun to shoot tiles to hit targets with different scores. Whoever gets the most points wins.

Bhargage plays similarly to Five-card Cribbage, with cards of the Extended Decktet instead of regular cards. The linear deck of Cribbage is substituted by the fluid, interwoven deck of Bhargage. The Decktet cards and scoring combinations give Bhargage an entirely different flavor. Frequently, Bhargage presents interesting decisions that are quite alien to Cribbage thinking.

Sets of 2 or more cards of the same value score points, as in Cribbage. Players score for cards totaling 11, not 15, and the play of the cards is up to 23, not 31. Sequences don't score in Bhargage, but Bhargage uses the bharg combination, which is common to other Decktet games—a bharg is a set of cards containing each of the six suits exactly once.

The Pawns, Courts, and Excuse of the Extended Decktet are zero-valued cards in Bhargage, and can multiply the counts for 11 in the scoring. The Excuse also has no suit and can likewise multiply the score for bhargs. Lastly, players score for "shortage," for the number of suits not present in the opponent's hand. Bhargage is played up to 111, not 121.

A fast paced adventure for the whole family. Somewhere on the island is a treasure. Find it and hurry back to the ship. Look out for sculls, sea creatures, cannons and the other cattrickies! Roll the dice, decide your next step, move your cattricky and look out for threats.

Quickdraw is a quick-shooting game where, based on cards, you find out how to shoot and which target to hit. Whoever hits the target the fastest gets it as a point. Whoever has the most points after all the cards are played wins.

—user summary

Compete against each other alone or in teams, in seven different disciplines that require precision, skill and a bit of luck. Load the ring slinger, aim carefully and launch the rings.

The game is divided into seven separate branches consisting of different courses that are built on the table. With the help of your ring lunge, you must either hit the mark, be the fastest through a course or what is meant in the next branch. Each player gets points based on their position and whoever collects the most after seven branches wins.

—user summary

Stockholms-spelet is a trivia game (basically a Trivial Pursuit: Genus Edition clone), with questions about the Swedish capitol.

—user summary

Myror i brallans naturspel is based on a popular nature program for children that was broadcast on Swedish television. One of the most popular features was guessing which animal a specific poo was from and which you can also do in the game.

You roll dice, walk around the board and try to find all the tiles that belong to your animal as quickly as possible. The first person to reach the center with all the pieces wins.

—user summary

Panama is a board game for the entire family. The game involves conducting various forms of trade and business activities that generate returns and contribute to welfare, products at competitive prices, pension payments, etcetera. During the game, the participants come into contact with both local and international trade in, for example, oil, cotton, pharmaceuticals and weapons.

—user summary

Each player rolls a die to determinate ones key figure. Then the players gets a player board (youth hostel construction site) and take turns to roll three dice. If a player rolls his own key figure, he may insert the foundation stone to his youth hostel. From this point on he may set a stone (chopstick) on a white space each time any player rolls his key figure. If his key figure is rolled on two dice, he may place a stone on a yellow space. If his key figure is rolled on all three dice, he has won the game prematurely.

Only after the ground floor is finished, a player may start to build the upper floors. The last stone may only be placed if the player rolls his own key figure before another player does, else the youth hostel is breaking down and only the foundation stone remains. The player has to start building again. The first player to finish the construction of his youth hostel wins the game.

The game was published between 1935 and 1945 as the HJ-Flag in front of the youth hostel reveals.

The player on turn is the seeker who draws a card and reads the answers. All players, including the seeker, use their answer tiles to choose the option they like best. All players who have the same choice as the seeker may move forward on the board. Both the seeker and the other players (soulmates) move towards the center on two separate tracks.

When a player reaches the middle, that player must find their soul mate by performing the final match. This is done by asking three questions in turn with a new question, all of which must match the player's answer. If not, it moves on to the next player and so on until a player has answered correctly three times in a row. The two players are then chosen as winners.
Tabletop variant of a pinball game. Compete to get the highest score after four balls.

boardgame: Entropia Game: World of endless fantasy and mystery
description:
Welcome to Entropia, the NEW board game of endless fantasy and mystery. We are opening our gates to everyone.

The game uses dice rolls and ability cards to determine the outcome of combat, monster encounters, and boss battles. Players can also gather resources such as food and coins and use them to build cities and upgrade their character.

The game takes place on a map with different biomes and locations that players can explore. The map is divided into hexagonal tiles and players move their figures across the map to engage in combat, gather resources, and build cities.

The goal of the game involve defeating all of the monsters and bosses on the map, building the most powerful cities, or achieving other objective.

Players choose a main character card with a unique ability and take on the role of that character. They then move their figures across the map, engage in combat with other players, monsters, and bosses, and gather resources to build cities and upgrade their character. Players can also use ability cards to gain an advantage in combat and make strategic decisions about where to move and what actions to take.

-description from publisher

A warband expansion for Warhammer Underworlds: Gnarlwood, including seven madcap miniatures

Grinkrak the Great leads his Looncourt into the darkness under the Gnarlwood. His Loonknights are a unique breed of grot - convinced by Grinkrak's stirring rhetoric (and some judiciously applied psychoactive fungi) that they are truly formidable warriors. Their king encourages them in this delusion, issuing them quests that, miraculously, they sometimes survive. Snorbo sounds the charge on his spore trumpet, sending both Pokin' Snark and Grib, Da Wonky Lance, bounding and charging towards their foes. Squiggly beast missiles sail overhead as they clash with their enemies, launched by Skolko and Pronk, while Pointy Burk and Moonface Nagz bring up the rear, eager for the dregs of glory.

Grinkrak and his Looncourt are an unusual warband, to say the least. Weighing in at seven models, they’re one of the largest warbands in the game, making them ideal for board control and overwhelming your opponent. By undertaking Quests - objectives unique to this warband - fighters can earn glory points and become Inspired at the same time. This set includes a full Rivals deck to use straight out of the box, and the models are all easy to build - so you can get playing nice and quick.

This set includes:
- 7x push-fit Grinkrak’s Looncourt miniatures – no glue required to assemble, cast in gloomy green plastic to stand out even when unpainted

- 7x double-sided fighter cards, one for each warrior in the warband – Grinkrak the Great, Snorbo da Spore, Pokin’ Snark, Pointy Burk, Moonface Nagz, Skolko and Pronk, and Grib, da Wonky Lance

- 1x Grinkrak’s Looncourt Warband Background / Rivals Deck Card

A 32-card Rivals deck consisting of:
- 12x Grinkrak’s Looncourt objective cards
- 10x Grinkrak’s Looncourt gambit cards
- 10x Grinkrak’s Looncourt upgrade cards

Miniatures in this box are supplied unpainted and require assembly - we recommend using Citadel Colour paints.

Be the owner of a mining company and go after the gold before your friends. Hire more miners, bribe miners and steal ore from friends. Almost anything goes in the Gold Rush.

How Game Works?
Each player gets a starting miner that cannot be fired or bribed by another player.
This miner has the actions to mine and reserve a trading card.
Being possible to do both only if the letter says.

When mining, if the mined ore has the time symbol, you can only reveal it on your next turn, if you don't, you can reveal it and use it on the same turn.

When reserving a charter, it is possible to pay to use it. When paying, you can use it and take effect immediately.

It is possible to hire new miners, some are better and some are worse. But it's always good to have a lot of workers, because they are the ones who do the actions in the game.

It is possible to equip workers with pickaxes to increase the amount of mining, being able to pull more cards when mining.

The player with 3 gold wins the game. Gold nuggets are worth 1/3 of gold, so we need 3 gold nuggets to get one gold.

If all possible actions of the players end and no one has the 3 gold, whoever has the most gold wins.

-description from publisher

What’s something you can’t do in public? Or something you can lick? A word that starts with a P that a 7-year-old can’t understand? Or a superhero that starts with L?

A game that tests your speed and creativity, Letterpool won’t leave you and your friends yawning. Show off your wit by being the first one to come up with words and grab letters worth points.

REMEMBER: Brevity is the soul of wit!

WITH 2 MODES TO PLAY: PARTY -OR- TRIVIA - for double the fun

—description from the publisher

Print'n'Play 130 card multi-deck, each card having 10 values, in three sections. Can play a variety of games, and people are encouraged to create new games. The expansion adds a letter for word games.

Players choose a color, and collect their pieces.

On the count of three everyone drops their pieces onto the table.

Of the pieces that are still upright and not laying flat, collect them up into your hand again, putting the others to the side.

On the occasion where there is no one left standing, collect your pieces and start again.

Everyone repeats the steps until there is a Last Man Standing.

boardgame: Tall Tales Expansion Pack: People Edition
description: Add 14 people and 4 environments to your tall tales game.

The sky is full of wonders. We are starting to understand them but not always it has been like that. The devoted people of Carcassonne, lead by superstition, would read the signs in the skies as omens, premonitions, divine messages, or even punishments from the heavens... Repent or you will all be damned.

The effect of the tiles in this mini expansion can be summarized as follows:

     A solar eclipse will fill the hearts of the followers in the area with terror. They will flee to the nearest religious place to pray for salvation: monasteries, cathedrals in cities, German cathedrals,...
     A moon eclipse will disband any thieves on a road, as they will fear for their lives.
     A comet will serve as a heavenly sign for the next player founding a new city, who will persevere in this endeavour moved by their faith. The player will be granted with up to three turns while extending this new city.
     A meteor shower will be a sign of luck for players. Fate will grant them the favour to choose between two tiles in their next three turns.

boardgame: Cosmic Portal: Mirror Zone (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description: A strange vortex suddenly appears over Carcassonne, shifting the entire landscape into an alternate dimension where nothing is what it seems.  This expansion features 8 new tiles for Carcassonne C1 and C2 that when played have major effects on the way points are given...or taken away.  This expansion is the first of a series; the 2nd, Cosmic Portal II: Exploring the Multiverse, will explain what happens when you place a follower inside the cosmic portal

Tangram is a geometric puzzle that originated in China over 1,000 years ago and consists of 7 shapes that can be arranged to create endless designs. The TTangram is a geometric puzzle that originated in China over 1,000 years ago and consists of 7 shapes that can be arranged to create endless designs. The Tangram Race from Mudpuppy is a fresh take on the classic game and teaches spatial relationships, geometry, and problem-solving in a fun, fast-paced game!

2 players can race to complete each design, or 1 player can master all the designs!

Includes 2 colorful tangram sets and 30 challenge cards. The challenge cards are double-sided for 2 levels of difficulty.angram Race from Mudpuppy is a fresh take on the classic game and teaches spatial relationships, geometry, and problem-solving in a fun, fast-paced game!

2 players can race to complete each design, or 1 player can master all the designs!

Includes 2 colorful tangram sets and 30 challenge cards. The challenge cards are double-sided for 2 levels of difficulty.

—description from the publisher

A mini expansion for Carcassonne inspired by the city of Antwerp, Belgium. no additional rules are made for this expansion. It contains 9 tiles with drawings of famous Antwerp landmarks like City Hall, Brabo statue, cathedral of Antwerp and more.

<MAGMAG Baseball> is a three-dimensional baseball game that can be enjoyed by two people. Experience intuitive mechanisms and special fun using magnets that children can enjoy. You can enjoy real baseball on the table with various characters and realistic ways of playing!

This is the stadium where the final game of the MAG series takes place. The ace pitcher takes the mound to determine the win in the last nine innings. A clean-up trio is preparing for the lineup. Who will be the final winner?

1. Place the card in the middle of the plastic box, and the pitcher rolls the 12-sided dice. The game begins in the strike zone that matches the number.
2. The pitcher selects the ball and moves the ball magnet. The batter also moves the bat magnet by predicting the pitch. Pitchers and batters bat power tokens one by one.
3. Raise the card and check if the position of the ball and the bat matches. If it does not match, it is determined by a strike or ball depending on the position of the ball. If matched, the result is processed by comparing power tokens.

—description from the designer

Angry birds meets monopoly. Roll the dice, collect bird and pig tokens and start the shooting action for collectibles. Buy, buy, buy and fly, fly, fly in the Angry Birds edition of the classic game of Monopoly! You can play as Red, Bomb, Ice or Stella, and your goal is to buy as many Angry Birds properties as you can in this fast-trading game. You’ve got to launch your tokens to get extra money, and if you finish with the most money, you win.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
The King has issued a Royal decree:

BUILD!

Tiny Kingdom Builder is a small worker placement game for 2-4 players.

Who will build their project first?

Utilize your workers to gather the material you need to finish your Building first. The first player to collect all their material wins! But be careful, with prosperity comes wandering eyes. Make sure the Thief doesn't steal from you!

boardgame: Battles of Napoleon: Volume II – QUATRE BRAS 1815
description:
Battles of Napoleon is an epic wargame series that simulates the most famous battles of Napoleon on a Tactical level sharing the same basic rules.

The series portrays the most important aspects of battles of the Napoleonic era with easy to learn and remember rules, intuitive mechanics and procedures. Players may focus on taking decisions and playing rather than consulting rules.

The system focusses on the “cat-and-mouse game” of coordination and timing of the various formations of the different arms without too much crunchy chrome.

Infantry was the predominant and most important element in every European army of the period. It usually formed between 60 and 90 per cent of an army in battle, and was both the cheapest and the most versatile type of troops. The foot soldiers had to endure most of the fighting and casualties. Depending on their task, they were either deployed in line (fire power), column (maneuver and assault) or square formation (protection against enemy cavalry).

Cavalry were screening the own army and provided the shock element on a Napoleonic battlefield. Due to their shock power they fixed enemy infantry and forced them to take shelter in square formation. If infantry failed to form square they were overrun. If infantry was fixed in semi-static squares they were very vulnerable to artillery fire.
 Artillery, the queen of the battlefield, was on the one hand having devastating effects on square and closely packed formations, on the other hand artillery alone was extremely vulnerable when getting charged by enemy units.

Generals are tasked with taking advantage of favorable terrain like the famous Duke of Wellington who mastered the reverse slope tactic.

By a simple game mechanic, players will never know the exact strength and power of an enemy unit. Units have a number of strength points that will gradually decrease while engaged with enemy units.

An innovative chit-based Command system, where players randomly draw activation chits determines which army may activate a formation, leads to highly interactive game play. Reaction follows action. We bring the chit-based Command system to a new level: In this games series, players decide which formation is activated when a chit is drawn. A formation may be activated several times per turn. But by each new activation it performs less effective.

During a game turn the players are under constant pressure to decide which formation to activate. By efficient timing of activation players are in the “driver’s seat” to dictate the pace of a battle.

Formations are given orders at the beginning of a game turn and are therefore limited by the actions they can perform on the battlefield. The more “aggressive” orders formations will receive, the more likely is the army will collapse due to fatigue. On the other hand, an army is more likely to gain the initiative on the battlefield by “aggressive” orders.

The scale of the series is appr. 150 meters per hex, infantry units are battalions, artillery is batteries and cavalry is represented as regiments.

Quatre Bras 1815, depicting the encounter at the Quatre Bras crossroads, is the second volume in this series. Special rules like the possibility to activate the French light cavalry brigade, the defense of special structures, the possible arrival of d'Erlon's I Corps add distinctive flavor of the battle.

—description from the publisher

description:
The hunting season commences at the Valley. Tribes hailing from the four equators of the world join forces every year to battle the wild animals and the valley dwellers. The tribes must return home with sufficient prey before the winter sets, or they will not survive the cold season. Their centuries-old alliance is fragile. One chief secretly plans to break the pact to attain sole power. Another cares only about self-interest, even to the detriment of others. Who’s a friend, and who’s an enemy? Decide, and lead your tribe to victory!

The teams and the goals of the players are allotted randomly at the start. These remain secret until the end of the game. The players’ task is to find out the motivations and teams of others while aiming to complete their own goals. Players may openly support or block others, while they also possess action cards that secretly influence the outcome of actions.

The game consists of turns. The players gather berries at first, then carry out steps consecutively in the order defined by their roles, and finally, they defend together. At the beginning of a new turn, the roles alternate. Therefore the players’ order and abilities change. When the players are out of their shared vigor, the game ends. Players who have completed their victory goals win the game. Multiple teams may win, or there can be no winner.

Many of the illustrations of the game depict nudity, therefore the game is for 18+ only.

—description from the designer

boardgame: My First Magic School Board Game
description:
Help the little wizards save the school from Goblin slime! The sneaky goblins have been leaving their slime all over the school, making a big mess. Race through the magical lands around the school to be the first little wizard to reach the goblin caves and convince them to clean up their mess to become the hero of the magic school!

In My First Magic School, kids will race through around the lands surrounding the magic school, using numbered cards to move ahead, while trying to be the first one to reach the goblin caves. But they better be careful! Along the path there are slime-covered spots that could send them back, as well as spells and potions to collect. Players move by using their numbered cards to get ahead. Use your cards strategically to avoid landing in slime, pick up spells and potions, and slow other wizards down. Once a player reaches the goblin caves, they roll the dice to see how the goblins will respond. The first player to reach the goblins and convince them to clean up is the winner!

The bright magical board, characters, and cards make this story driven game a great way to help kids develop their strategic thinking and counting skills. Limited reading and simple counting make this an easy game for kids 5 and up to learn and enjoy!

-description from publisher

Oh no! The candy bandit has taken all of the train tracks connecting the candy fields to the town. Work together to help to rebuild the missing train tracks and get all the trains full of candy back to the station before the candy bandit gets back.

In Candy Trains you must work together to rebuild the missing train track tiles and move all the trains full of candy across the candy fields and back into town before the candy bandit returns. In this cooperative game, players work as a team to draw cards that match missing track pieces to rebuild the path to the train station, while also using cards to move the trains along your newly built tracks. But watch out, the candy bandit is on his way back and if all the candy bandit cards are drawn and he makes it back before all the trains get the station, he will take all the towns candy. If all the trains get to the station before the candy bandit gets back, then everybody wins!

Colorful and sweet images make this a fun and creative way for kids to learn teamwork, communication, strategic thinking and simple counting through hands-on play.

-description from publisher

The Inferno expansion book and card deck is the first major expansion in the Hell Dorado universe. The greatest war of Hell has escalated to new heights of destruction as the demon Legions under command of the Fallen Angel Abezeth rise to eradicate the upstart invaders from Lucifer's realm.

The Inferno expansion book includes 48 new profiles across all six Hell Dorado Factions. This full color book will also feature: New Scenarios, new battlefields, and new terrain types to make your games more exciting than ever before. Hell Dorado: Inferno will give players more army strategies, more game variety, and more of the miniatures that make it one of the best looking miniature games around. The expansion also includes a card deck for all the units that are included in the book for use in the game with your models and as quick reference of your opponent's forces.

boardgame: たすけてプリンセス (Save the Princess)
description:
The gluttonous Demon Lord has kidnapped the Princesses of the Land of Sweets!
Save the Princesses from the Demon Lord's clutches and take back our sweets!

One player is the Demon Lord, and the rest of the players are rescuers (players take turns being the Demon Lord). Line up Princess cards equal to the number of players +1. The Demon Lord places ◯ and ☓ cards below the Princess cards face-down. When a rescuer reveals a ◯, they successfully gain a Princess card; if they reveal a ☓, they fail to do so.

Castles - Duel of Kings is a medieval themed, turn-based card game. Set in a time where many kingdoms are in turmoil, the nation is in desperate need of one ruler to unify the lands and settle the chaos. Players will take turns conquering lands and furthering their reign, showcasing their tactical skill to outwit and outmatch their opponents. The players who is best able to utilize multiple strategies to attack, defend and out-maneuver their opponents will have the best chance of being the ruler of this land. In this game luck rewards the bold. Testing your luck and exploring the lands to recruit the powerful gladiators and sorceresses will work greatly in ones favor.

Each new turn poses a problem to other players as the state of the lands continue to change. Players fight to maintain the ownership of a castle as they further their  efforts to obtain more. After each turn players will gather all newly acquired warriors and alter their strategy to better their chances of becoming the one true king

The winner of the game is the player who successfully holds all castles simultaneously.

Chase gem thieves through an unknown city in this race to see who can find the escape route the quickest. Make your map, roll to see where the thief started, then see who can figure out their destination the fastest by following the streets, turning at all intersections and warping between matching points. First to capture 2 thieves wins.

description:
Bring Guns to Bear (BGB) is a simulation of modern era (WWI & WWII) tactical naval combat.

[All game files available below: Rule Book, Player Aids, Ship Database.] SCENARIOS AVAILABLE UNDER 'VARIANTS' FORUM.

Simple rules are employed for fast play while still retaining the essence of ‘big gun’ naval surface engagements.

Typically, up to 14 Individual ships may be represented in this hex and counter game (no rulers or protractors). Played by two players in opposition or as a solitaire game using the AI.


    Movement by impulse allows reduced footprint and avoids extended range hex counts.



    Dice rolls are always to some immediate purpose (not just to see what further table to roll on), either 2d6 or (2d6 + 1d6); no buckets of dice!



    Separate targeting tables for WWI and WWII.



    Ships weakened by damage become progressively more susceptible through reduced damage control capability.



    Minimal logging requirements using simple strikethrough.



    Includes random scenario generator.


All games files are posted with this listing for free use (I retain copyright), and I am open to ongoing design tweak suggestions from the community. I'm also open to approaches from publishers interested in producing a 'deluxe' version.

Pilgrim is a fast-paced, fighting, strategic card game for 2 to 4 players. Each player acts as a leader of his army, whose composition from round to round changes by representatives of four different races - Elves, Dwarves, Orcs, and Undead, as well as rare representatives of the Pilgrims.

Players fight each other in a big battle for gold, consisting of three chains of rounds. In the 1st chain, the number of bouts increases by one each round and decreases by one in the 3rd. In the middle 2nd chain, the number of bouts is always 8.

During all chains, the game goes the same way - players try to win as many coins as possible, which can be obtained by beating off cards according to certain rules. At the end of the game, players count all earned coins, and the player with the highest amount of them wins.

Venn is a bluffing card game.

With only three different types of cards, this colour based card deck has two completely different card games that are quick to learn.

'Venn Classic' is game one and 'Venn Party' is game two.

In both games, there is no such thing as a bad hand or a weak card, winning and losing are down to how you play. You’ll need to bluff in order to prevent your opponents from winning easily, but bluff too much and you’ll be easily found out.

The object of Venn Classic (game one) is to find cards of a certain colour while stopping your opponents from doing the same. The game is played in rounds and at the end of each round, cards are lost. The last player to have cards in their hand is the winner.

The object of Venn Party (game two) is to push a card to a player while stating a colour and get the receiving player to incorrectly guess if they are telling the truth or not. The game is played in rounds and at the end of each round, one card is discarded. The game ends when all cards have been used and the winner is the player with the fewest cards in front of them.

—description from the designer

An expansive 4X empire building strategy game where you and up to 5 other players vie to be the most dominant empire in the land. Achieve victory by being the first empire to reach 10 Victory points through any combination of Economic, military, societal and technological means. A simplified and accessible version of our Ecologies game, Ecologies Junior: Forest allows younger children to practice building food webs with 32 pairs of matching organism tiles for a total of 64 tiles.

The game can be played as a simple memory game with children flipping tiles or extended to include food web building and trophic level practice. It is flexible enough to be played cooperatively or competitively, depending on the audience.

It contains much of the art from the original Ecologies card game, with a few bonus additions as well.

Designed by a biology teacher, it can be played for fun or used in preschools, outdoor schools, homeschools, and elementary schools to teach children about forest ecology.

description:
Drylands are fragile ecosystems where deserts border savanna and/or forest biomes. These places can easily be degraded into desert like conditions or nurtured to be greener and more productive. It depends on how the land is managed.

In this two player game the desert player competes with the forest player to decide the outcome of a small section of drylands.

Each turn players can “grow” by placing a new tile next to their established tiles, or they can “leap” using an existing tile to move to a location farther away. Whenever you place one of your tiles next to opponent tiles, either through growing or leaping, those opponent tiles flip and become yours.

The game ends when all spaces on the board are occupied. The player with the most tiles of their type wins!

A game that’s easy to learn but hard to master, it is suitable for a wide range of ages, from 5 to 105.

Gain as many dice, rerolls, and multipliers as possible for the final big roll! High Score Wins! Axolotl Dice? More like that’s a lotta dice!

The game is played over 12 rounds, with players playing simultaneously, trying to earn the most dice, rerolls, and multipliers by advancing on the 18 number tracks. All players will use the same dice pool for each roll, using the dice however each player determines. At the end of the game, each player will make a final Big Roll depending on how many dice they gained. The player with the highest score wins!

-description from designer

House of Influence takes place in a historical age of diplomacy where influence is everything. Set after a war that has ravaged the lands people thought the golden age of peace had finally arrived. But underneath deceptive smiles the five different houses are still fighting for power and will use any means to become the house at the top: Influential people with unique skills are at the houses’ disposal to win the game. Be it the selfless Martyr who will sacrifice everything to gain influence for their house, the idealistic diplomat and her silver tongue, the valiant general or other powerful roles.
Which house will win? A house that holds honor and valor in high regard or a house preferring the subtle tactics of a cunning Charlatan?

House of Influence is a Team vs Team semi co-op Hidden role game where 3-10 players become members of a majority or minority. But players need to be careful, their allies might not be who you think they are. In order to accumulate the highest influence score in four rounds, the houses have up to 6 different characters at their disposal to manipulate the flow of influence and information.In this turn-based game each player rolls two available characters with their own set of abilities but can only use one to gather influence or information on the other players.

The appeal of House of Influence is the quick pace of the game with easy to understand rules automated and managed by the game app and unidice - the digitized die. Replayability in this Social Deduction game of intrigue and information is guaranteed. The different houses give different advantages to their house members and the characters at their disposal - depending on the flow of the game. This Hidden Role game combines decision making with the fun of rolling a dice console in the shape of a D6.

-description from publisher

“Ding-Dong!” The elevator door opens as the bell goes off. Everyone hastily steps into the cabin. It is just another boring morning where all of you have to cramp yourselves into the same old elevator to go up to your office. The very moment the door shuts tight, a rumbling sound breaks the silence. The atmosphere suddenly intensifies. A foul smell immediately hits you in the face. It is as simple as that - an eye for an eye, a toot for a toot!

Fart & Furious is a putridly-amusing party game for 3-8 players where you and your friends will fare against each other in a battle of PFFT!

Sid Sackson's classics Samarkand & Bazaar are now combined as two games in one box with this new edition of both games.

Samarkand is a fast-paced trading and selling game set in exotic Asia Minor. Cunning Merchants buy, exchange, and sell goods to build wealth. When visiting Nomad Camps and after offering gifts for their hosts’ hospitality, Merchants trade for the goods they desire. When visiting Oases, they may purchase random goods. Ultimately, these Merchants travel to the bazaars in Cities such as Samarkand or Isfahan to sell the goods they have acquired. Merchants must plan which desert paths to use to travel efficiently between the Nomad Camps, Oases, and Cities so they can earn the quickest (and greatest) profit!

In Bazaar, players attempt to gain the right combination of colored cubes through skillful trading to purchase the wares displayed in the Bazaar. Values of the various wares are determined by the number of cubes the purchaser has left over following the transaction. Trading is governed by the current rates posted at the Exchange. When the wares from two stalls have been completely sold, the Bazaar is closed, and the game ends. The player with the highest score wins!

-description from publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces the players have to make a jump ahead or back. The players lose a turn on spaces 29 and 34. The first player arriving at space 121 wins the game.

The game was published for the opening of the department store at the corner of Hohe Straße and Gürzenichstr. in Cologne.
Try to be the first to reach the finish line on the modular track. On the way you have to pass various mini-games in the interactive app and avoid the attacks of your opponents.

Thematic roll and write set in Georgian England. You are a society matchmaker, using your dice rolls to build relationships between the 4 suitors. Each suitor has it's own mini-game to progress to build up romantic and/or financial connections between the suitors.

Play solo, or race against friends, to maximise your points. The game ends when a player either complete matches between all 4 suitors, or makes too many mistakes.

A game that brings to life how data flows through Internet networks. Put simply, over the course of the game, from two to five participants seek to avoid busy city centres and routes blocked by diggers to deliver data blocks from one city Internet Exchange to another.

Data, however, scores differently and is also subject to cyber attacks from other nasty trouble-making players. But at the end of the day, the first player to fifteen points can celebrate a well-earned victory.

How to Play:
There are a variable number of rounds, with each player, during their individual turn, able to perform any two of the following actions:

Bring a data block into play 
Move a data block along a route
Carry out a cyber-attack

The core of the game are the Location tokens.
Each location token bears the name of a location on the board where there is an Internet Exchange. 
From time to time, players either leave these locations or have to reach them to deliver their data blocks and gain the points they need to win the game. 
Players need to find a container where they can put the tokens, shake them well, and then pick them out one by one as the game progresses.
Data are represented by wooden cubes; they move along the routes on the playing board towards the locations that host Internet Exchanges.

In addition, players can place a digger (only one) on any route after finishing their turn and before the turn passes to the next player. Placing a digger on a route blocks the route.

-description from designer

description:
Creativity Wins!: Outrageous is a hot new party game, where players use their creativity to come up with witty, unique, & hilarious answers to modern-day prompts. Laugh all night long with witty answer prompts. Ex: "I'd hide if ___ showed up at my door." "They should sell ____ in vending machines."
How to Play: Designated judge picks a card & reads aloud. Players write their answers on a sheet & place into box. Answers are read by judge & points are awarded based on best answers. Most points wins!

Adaptable: Outrageous is great for any groups, parties, or birthdays. Depending on participants, games can be family friendly, wild, or "adults only". Inspiration play make the perfect card games for families on game night. Everyone can be OUTRAGEOUS!

Fun for the Family: Recommended for 4-8 players, 13 Years and up. More players can play with additional pencils. You will have the best games with your friends and family.
Game Play: Takes just 5 mins to learn. Each game lasts approximately 30 mins. Play a short round or all night long!

COMES WITH: 220 prompt cards, answer pad, 8 pencils, and instruction booklet

Take turns placing cards down and shouting out the correct color. Mess up and start over. Each player starts with 10 cards and takes turns placing cards down and shouting out the correct color on the card. The game is fast-paced, and turns quickly move around the table from player to player as they call out their colors. If a player misspeaks or says the wrong color, they collect the whole pile of cards on the table.

Oh yeah... and there are BEE cards. If a person places a BEE card in the center of the table, everyone has to slap the angry BEE. The person who slaps the BEE card last gets stung and has to add the whole pile to their hand.

The first person to get rid of all of their cards wins!

Deck the halls and roll the dice in this holiday game of chance and strategy! In Decorations, you will use your dice rolls to carefully place ornaments and lights on your Christmas tree, aiming for the perfect combination to earn the most points. With a mix of luck and skill, can you create the most dazzling tree?

Designed for one player and taking just 15 minutes, Decorations is perfect for kids aged 8 and up. So grab your dice and get ready to trim the tree in this merry and exciting game!

Michiru and Nobara compete to see who can best control Tempo. The winner will be crowned Drum Major of the marching drum and fife band!

The goal is to arrange your Temposticks ("Tempos") in a specific pattern within the Finish Zone of your Scale board. Each round, players will draft dice and assign them to their dice coasters. The value of a die, combined with which coaster it is assigned to, determines which Conducting Technique you will perform to move your Tempos.

Under certain circumstance, such as if your opponent drafts a die of your color, you may be able to move a cube around your Baton Performance Board. When your cubes complete laps on this board, you gain special benefits.

Additional features include:

     Tuning Tokens (dice manipulation)
     Chance Cards (once-per-game abilities and/or end-game points)
     Target Cards (bonus points for achieving certain Tempo patterns throughout the game)
     Point Cards (cumulative and increasing end-game points)


—user description
publishers: Yureru tsubomi wo mederu kai (揺れる蕾を愛でる会)

description:
In Galle Fort, the players are attempting to build a lighthouse in the port of Galle, an important trading post in Sri Lanka.

During the game, players will either take resources from a central pool or trade them for other resources.  One of the key actions is turning in tea to invite another player to a tea party, where you will demand all of one of their types of resources unless they pay one of the available bribes.

The first player to acquire three building materials, thereby erecting the lighthouse, wins.

Casino Seventh Heaven is a set of rules for 7 card games played with most of the same components. The game is designed to be like a trip to the casino where you can choose which games you will play, and you take your winnings from one game with you to the next game.  The player who has the most money at the end of the trip to the casino wins.

The games include various game mechanisms, such as a poker variant, a roulette type game, and others.

Alliance: Supergra Strategiczna is a "Strategy and Diplomacy" multi-player board wargame that simulates a theoretical War in Europe in the '30s.
Seven Nations: Britain, France, Italy, Germany, Romania, Poland, and Turkey are trying to eliminate the competitors.
When four Nations are defeated (no more cities controlled) the remaining three nations share the victory.
Diplomacy is required to gain an advantage.

Gameplay - key rules:

     All Players write down their commands on the paper, then all moves take place at the same time.
     There are two types of orders: attack (move), or defend.
     Attack or defend can be declared for the current territory or for the territory next to it.
     Each unit can move to the territory that shares the border with the current one.
     To conquer a hostile space - attacker power (number of units) needs to be higher than defender power.
     Each year has two phases (Spring and autumn campaigns)
     When the Year ends - units calculation takes place - for each conquered terrain - the player may build a new unit.
     Special units = cost of 3 regular units.


Win Condition:
Each Nation starts with three cities, and when all cities are lost - that Nation is eliminated. When 4 Nations are eliminated - the game ends and the remaining 3 Nations share the victory.

Note: While this game may seem similar to the Alliace from Columbia Games, it has different mechanics, counties, and background.

description: First polish paragraph game. Uses a specific system for character creation and progression rules.

It is late in the summer the land is dry and the rivers and creeks are low. It is bushfire season. Word gets around that the surrounding lands are like a tinderbox ready to explode in flames. Then it happens. A Raging Inferno is ignited and starts to bare down on your homes. It is too late to flee - you must defend the town until the Raging Inferno has been extinguished. You and your fellow residents are all that stands in the way of saving your homes and the lives of your loved ones. The weak and the injured are seeking safety and shelter in the Community Hall in the centre of town.

Game Play
Raging Inferno is a collaborative board game for 1-6 players. The players must work together to save the town from the Raging Inferno. Wild winds and a climate crisis induced heat wave make the situation very dangerous. Dangers (in the form of fires) come at the town from all different directions. The players must rally the townsfolk to Defend the town.

Dangers come at the Town from eight random directions.

Defenders need to be strategically placed to combat these Dangers - ranging from Arsonists, Spot Fires, Bush Fires and the deadly “Boss” Dangers.  But watchout just when you think you have everything covered the wind can change and move the fires around.

Defenders operate in different zones - Scrublands, Bushlands, Farmlands and the Town Centre. The Defenders consist of Volunteers, Fire Fighters, Fire Trucks and the all powerful Fire Chief. You are also helped by lots of fire fighting assistance - Fire Breaks, Water Bombs and occasionally the weather might help.

You will need luck, lots of teamwork and some clever game play to save the town from the Raging Inferno.

—description from the publisher

In Robot Roll & Write, each player builds their own robot over the course of twelve turns, adding components to the shell of the body, then connecting them with circuits in order to score points.

At the start of every third turn, lay out five cards on the display in the dice slots 1-5. Each card shows a component or two different circuits; some cards also have a die depicted on them. One card at random has a special token on it. On a turn, each player rolls two six-sided dice, then uses the results of the dice to choose one or two cards and add the components or circuitry to their robot shell. A result of 6 always lets you choose any card, but if a card has a second die depicted on it then you need both that die result and a 6 (or the designated die slot 1-5).

All dressed up in a brand-new box, this Anniversary Edition comes with the best of the original game and a huge collection of all NEW names! The party-perfect celebrity charades game builds the laughter and excitement in three hilarious rounds. You have 30 seconds to get your team to guess as many celebrity names in the Deck of Fame as possible. Round 1, say or do anything; Round 2, use just one word; and Round 3, gestures only ... no words allowed! Comes with hundreds of new names, the basic sand timer, PLUS expanded rules that allow for an odd number of players! Winner of Party Game of the Year and a host of other awards!

-description from publisher's website

Offensive le Jeu is a simple and effective educational tool to explain military operations up to the brigade level. It is particularly interesting for officer or student training. Generic in nature, it allows a wide range of player-generated scenarios, can be played on a 1:50'000 military map and can be easily house-ruled.

Each side starts with a set of forces and special units, as well as a hand of event and asset cards. The scale of the units' movement on the square grid is realistic, as well as their spotting range and firepower. The games teaches combined arms on the higher tactical level, while allowing for a skillful combination of obstacles, mines, demonstrations and paramilitary groups - the game conveys the idea of a hybrid engagement.

The use of blocks and the combination with event & asset cards allows for good bluffing strategies. Combat is very simple but introduces unit-specific terrain modifiers which work really well together. A lot of additional "chrome" is left out, but the system works and it is simple enough to be extended with logistical rules, command & control, asymmetric forces, or new characteristics for units.

Victory can either be achieved by the destruction of enemy units or after agreeing to a specific scenario victory condition.

—user summary

As you may have understood, Offensive the game is not a game like the others! It was developed for instruction and then adapted for all audiences! So it has kept some characteristics that make the difference:


      The possibility to play on all Swiss topographic maps at 1:50'000 scale.
      Rules that adapt to the level of the players to accompany you on the path of knowledge and tactical understanding.
      Simple rules so that you can get the hang of it quickly! 


Offensive the game is a board game that includes:

      A basic game board;
      66 units to command;
      Offensive cards to influence the battle;
      2 dice to play the confrontations;
      2 types of rules according to your level of play (Novice or expert) which are downloadable on the page of the Internet site.


—description from the publisher (Translated)

Vous l'aurez sans doute compris, Offensive le jeu n'est pas un jeu comme les autres! Il a été développé pour l'instruction est adapté ensuite pour tous les publics! Alors il a gardé certaines caractéristiques qui font la différence:

​*  La possibilité de jouer sur toutes les cartes topographiques suisses à l'échelle 1:50'000.

      Des règles qui s'adaptent au niveau des joueurs pour vous accompagner sur le chemin de la connaissance et de la compréhension tactique.
      Des règles simples afin de pouvoir le prendre en main rapidement! 


Offensive le jeu est donc un jeu de société qui comprend:

      Un plateau de jeu de base;
      66 unités à commander;
      Des cartes Offensive pour influencer la bataille;
      2 dés pour jouer les affrontements;
      2 types de règles suivant votre niveau de jeu (Novice ou expert) qui sont téléchargeables sur la page du site internet.


—description from the publisher (French)

description:
Hands-On coding fun with the whole family!

Use coding cards to compute to your mouse's path around the board and collect cheese wedges. Block other players using mayswal's or duck through warp zones to zoom ahead. Develop problem solving in critical thinking as you race your mouse to victory!

boardgame: Impetus: No one gets left behind
description:
Rescue the Little Mushrooms from the Evil Stain, guide them through the darkness but be careful with your orders, they can be misinterpreted.

Impetus: No one gets left behind is a solo game where the player needs to program the directions he wants to give, using the cards in their hand.

In your turn: You will place the cards in your hand on a "Orders Row" using the color or shape of the card, so the corresponding character moves to the nearest shape or color; but be careful, the mushroom have the same colors as the cave and they could move into the wrong direction. Also, you are not alone in the darkness...

The Evil Stain will use the card you left in your hand to place their traps on those colors and/or shapes in the cave making your mission more difficult. Also, it will move a step closer to you every turn.

A game for the puzzle lovers, it creates an unique problem each turn that must be resolve the most effective way, in order to save the little mushrooms and win the game.

Dicevania: Quintet or Rolls is a double-faced print at home roll & write game, loosely inspired by a famous videogame. Roll your dice, conquer the map, and save the Countess girl!

SIDE A
Use 5d6 trying to score the needed combination in order to clear a room, then move to the nex one. Freely explore the castle, finding and managing your items to modify the rolls, recover HP and score points. Fill the rewards bars with scored points to get more items. Will you explore the whole map?

SIDE B
Roll 5d6 to determine which tetraminoes you'll have to put in the map. Try to cover it completely.

-description from designer

Robot roll and write is a game for 1 to 99 players lasting approximately 15 minutes.

Each player must roll his dice and choose the appropriate shape drawn on cards arranged on a common game board. He draws the chosen shape or track on his game sheet trying to connect the various parts as best as possible, which give victory points. In the end the player with the best score is the winner.

-description from designer

Family Board Games for Kids and Adults: Spoof is the hilarious bluffing board game that will have you talking and laughing all night. Provides the best party games for adults and family.

Be Creative & Fool Your Friends: "Spoof" your friends by making up clever, fake answers to fun trivia such as "_______ is the national bird of Japan." "_______ is illegal to have on your porch in Arizona." Be careful with the correct answers!

A moderator reads a card and everyone gives a word to complete the sentence. For example, “it is against the law in Australia to leave this on your porch.” Everyone has to write down their best guess and turn it in. Then the moderator writes down the answers in the small dry erase board. The moderator also mixes in the correct answer too. Everyone guesses which answer is the truth. Players get points when someone chooses their answer and gets double points if they choose the correct answer.

How to Win? Your mission is to RUIN any given topic with our hilarious adult game. Pull a topic card and ruin the topic in 4 words or less! Convince the judge why you should win the round. The player with the most points WINS.

Comes with 255 topic cards, 8 whiteboards, and 10 markers.

Be the boss of a TV station yourself - it's possible! With the game "Auf Sendung!" Here, pupils can create the programme of a public and a private broadcaster themselves and learn what they have to pay attention to.

boardgame: A Fading Star: Insurgency and Piracy in Somalia
description:
A Fading Star: Insurgency and Piracy in Somalia is Volume XV of the COIN Series originally designed by Volko Ruhnke. The year is 2007. We reach the climax of the several decades of civil war that have ravaged Somalia since the fall of dictator Siad Barre in 1991. An African Union coalition, assisted by the Ethiopian army, enters Mogadishu to support a nascent but corrupt Transitional Federal Government. They face a relentless and violent insurgency waged by Harakat al-Shabaab al-Mujahideen, an Islamic group seeking both to expel the "invaders" and to transcend the traditional clan-based government structure of Somalia. In the middle of this power vacuum, minor clans and warlords organize themselves to administrate otherwise ungoverned regions, while others engage in large-scale piracy operations, painting their acts as a fight against foreign trawlers pillaging Somali fishing resources.

Highlights:

     An updated take on contemporary aspects of insurgency and peacebuilding. The Al-Shabaab insurgent enjoys intelligence supremacy that allows them to conduct deadly out-of-turn ambushes, while facing a slightly less pronounced asymmetrical disadvantage than insurgent factions in previous volumes (due to the comparative weakness of the Transitional Federal Government). On the other hand, the African Union Mission's logistical, kinetic, and peacebuilding capacities will evolve as contributing countries join or leave the coalition throughout the game. 
     Intense urban warfare is depicted in more detail through the struggle for control of the capital city Mogadishu. Controlling a majority of districts will provide the ruling faction with further legitimacy and enhanced capabilities. However, limited operational effectiveness, mobility restrictions, and the looming threat from a major Al-Shabaab offensive will greatly slow the COIN factions' progress toward stabilizing the city.
     A new ‘Clan Struggle’ interphase draws out some of the conflict's unique uncertainties. All sides bid to forge new alliances with local clans, bringing new blood to the battlefield, while the African Union must deal with the consequences of delayed reinforcements or unilateral withdrawals by member countries, and the Pirate warlords collect lucrative tolls from roads and ports that they control. 
     A unique piracy subsystem debunks the Somali pirate mythos and explores how piracy stakeholders' interests actually intersected with other local actors during the golden age of Indian Ocean hostage-taking.  


Each turn eligible players will select from a faction-specific menu of Operations and Special Activities in an order determined by the current Event card, or choose to trigger the Event text. Typical Operations include Sweep, Assault, March, and Attack, with more unique Operations and Special Activities reflecting the specific nature of this conflict and the factions involved. Regular play is periodically interrupted by Propaganda rounds, during which early victory is possible and some upkeep is conducted; and by Clan Struggle rounds (unique to this game), during which all players have the opportunity to bid for the loyalty of an unaligned regional clan. If nobody achieves an early victory then the winner will be determined by comparing victory thresholds in the final Propaganda round. Each faction has unique victory conditions relating to their aims and objectives during this period of history.

Faction descriptions:

     Al-Shabaab wages a deadly Islamist insurgency against the African Union ‘occupiers’ and their dependent Transitional Federal Government allies. A clear edge in media warfare provides them with significant recruiting capacities that will help them build a Somali caliphate transcending the customary clan system.  
     Ugandan and Burundian troops serving in the African Union Mission in Somalia (AMISOM) aim to enforce peace, build support for the Transitional Federal Government, and train the weak federal army. Neighboring Ethiopia and Kenya may also enter the scene to stabilize their respective borders while interfering in Somali politics. 
     An abysmally weak Transitional Federal Government seeks to survive in a besieged Mogadishu while reaching out into the rest of the country to advance the federalization process. Unprecedented embezzlement capacities and partnerships with like-minded clans may offer the consecutive administrations an opportunity to survive the conflict unharmed and more prosperous than ever, and a new kind of militia unit emphasizes the opportunistic nature of the Somali clans' agendas. 
     Pirates, minor clans, and unaligned warlords pursue their enrichment projects by raiding the high seas or setting up roadblocks across unruly parts of the country. They'll settle for a "customary" Somalia, neither rooted in sharia law nor totally anchored to the liberal world order. 


-description from developer

description:
CATNIP Auction House is where the world’s elite cats go to get the best items for their personal collections!

Overview: CATNIP Auction House is a bidding game for 3-8 players. Each player represents a cat that will spend its precious catnip to bid on various items until the inventory is gone.

Game Objective: Have the most Victory Points at the end of the game to win! There are 4 steps to each round:

Step 1: Reveal the item up for auction.
The Auctioneer flips over the top card of the deck to reveal the item that is up for auction this round.

Step 2: Activate Action Item Cards. 
Any player that has a card labelled “instant” can activate its ability by flipping it over. Instant abilities are a one-time use. Any VP labelled on the card counts toward end game scoring regardless of whether the card is active or not.

Step 3: Bid on items.
Starting to the left of the auctioneer, in a clock-wise order, players either bid on the item or they PAWS (pass). If they BID, they place one Catnip Token in the centre of the table.
If that player chooses to PAWS, they place their PAW Token in front of their screen and take all Catnip Tokens from in the centre of the table. They may no longer bid on this item.

Once a player PAWS’ and they are the only player that still hasn't PAWSed, that player will win the item. The last player to PAWS will get the number of mice shown in the bottom right corner of that item card.

Step 4: A new Auctioneer
After the auction item is claimed, the winner of the auction becomes the Auctioneer for the next round and receives the Auctioneer Marker.

-description from publisher
Repeat steps 1 to 4 until all Auction Items are won.

So, what will it be? Bombs, Burning, or Bedlam?
Goblins - start your bickering!

Tricky Goblins is a trick-taking card game for 2 - 4, where players gain gold coins by winning tricks and meddling with dice to align with their secret objective cards, or "plans". The player with the most gold coins at the end of the game wins, and is dubbed the Trickiest Goblin!

You are a girl who is very busy preparing for a date that was suddenly decided. You want to be cute and fashionable, but you don't have any of those items! Borrow a lot of fashionable items from other people and aim to go on wonderful dates with as many boys as possible.

In マッチングフォーユー (Matching 4U), players will be dealt 8 cards each. They will draft their hand, 2 cards at a time, until they have 8 cards again. Players will then play tricks and determine the strength of their card from highest to lowest.

Starting with the highest strength, players will acquire one of the played cards and leave it face up in front of themselves for scoring at the end of the round. All cards have various set collection bonuses associated with them. You want the most diamonds, you want pairs of shoes, perfume will make your clothes score more, etc.

The player with the most points after 3 rounds is the winner!
designers: Takanari Yamaguchi (ヤマグチ タカナリ)

description: A guess/collection game for 2-5 players (elves).  4 tiles (presents) are up for collection at any given time and randomly assigned around a board to spaces labeled one through four.  The current player uses a die and secretly rotates the die to match the number a present is assigned to (one through four.  Keeping the die hidden, the next player attempts to guess which number the die was turned to (one through four).  If the guess is correct the present corresponding to that number is stolen and added to that person's toy chest.  If an incorrect guess is made, then the current player who selected the number/present receives it and adds it to their toy chest.  The missing tile is replaced, turn order changes and the game continues until there are no tiles left to replace the missing tile.  The presents have various ways points are collected and there are also negative point presents (broken toys) as well as tiles that effect game play.  The elf with the post points after bonuses and negative points are counted is declared the winner.

Each player starts with 2 spaceships at the yellow line. Movement is forward or sideways, never backwards, to the next ring (filled or empty). Each constellation has two filled rings. Whenever a player first occupies these two filled rings in a constellation, he gets points awarded. If other players do so at a later time they get no points. The game ends when all players have rounded the game board and are back at the yellow line. The player with the most points wins the game.

"A star has slipped away," headlines The Inquerer on the front page of its morning edition. Natalia Wilford, a famous actress on the rise in 1950s New York, has been murdered in her home. Who could have committed such a murder?

In this collaborative game, play as John Blacksad, Clerk Smirnov, Weekly, the nosy reporter with keen senses, and Jake Ostiombe, boxer and former bodyguard to Natalia. Scour the city, follow the clues, gather evidence and bring the truth to light

Age of Alexander is the second SAGA supplement to cover Antiquity. It describes the many events that forged the Greek world, starting with the Persian Wars and ending in the chaotic agony of the Successors. This Universe presents six new faction, all totally compatible with the previously released Age of Hannibal and the soon to come Age of Caesars. Some factions include multiple variants, and all of them come with their Battle Boards, historical summary, Legendary Units and Special Rules.

Factions included: The Greek Cities, Persians, Thracians, Macedonians, Indians and the Successors.
family: History: Greek & Persian conflicts of the 4th century BC
History: Wars of Alexander the Great
History: Wars of the Diadochi and Syrian Wars

description:
Get ready for an adventure at sea! Are you ready to sail to the end of the world?

Each player controls a ship with a hold that has room for up to four Trade Discs.
New action cards are drafted each round and then used for either travelling or fighting. Each card can be used only for one of the two options, so some kind of sacrifice is done each time a card is used.

The players sail their ships between the Northern half of Europe and the Southern half picking up Trade Discs from active cities and delivering them to the other side of the known world. The Trade Discs are kept secret from the other players and can contain either trade goods or manpower to be used for attacking. The attacking can be aimed at an active city, but also against another player's ship. When stopping in a city dock the player has to make a choice: Raid or Trade.

By both raiding and trading the players can accumulate gold, and the first player to 150 wins a spot for the final battle. The other players then race to be the first to reach the dock where the battle will happen. The single player who wins the final battle is the winner of the game.

boardgame: Detecteam Kids: Once upon an Island
description:
Who doesn’t love a fun journey? You’re off to adventure on a tropical island! Sunshine, surf and… 3 new cases? Detectives, team up! Meet the residents and tourists on an amazing beach and help the animals figure out what happened!

Once upon an Island is a co-operative adventure for young detectives. Your goal is to piece the full picture, just like a jigsaw puzzle, and re-create the events. Find out what actually happened in this stand-alone game from the Detecteam Kids range!

The title translates to "The mysterious House".

Three valuable objects are hidden inside a mysterious house. In order to find them, the players have to solve riddles on each card. Their faithful companion Snuffles is always by their side. But the players can only reveal the house's secrets and solve all the riddles with the help of an UV lamp.

Three exciting chapters provide 20 minutes of fun playing time each. To help solve the riddles, various clue cards can be used for help. Kids can set up the house and explore it in 3D.

Kids who enjoy adventures and riddles will have a great time exploring the 3D house and all its secrets.

Title translates to "From Err to Z"

A crazy and fun explaining and guessing game for everyone. The players try to explain as many words as possible to the other team which has to guess the correct term. The clue: The wanted term can only be explained with the displayed letter. If, for example, one player draws a card with the letter "S" on it, his/her teammates can only use this letter to explain the wanted word until the next letter card is drawn. Assuming the wanted word is "Fish", the players could give hints like "Swims! Scales!" A stop watch with 90 seconds adds extra suspense.

In this solitaire game that aims to recreate fictitious events of 18th century France you will roam around the city of Paris looking for targets, followers of the order. For this you will need to carefully navigate the streets and rooftops, avoiding guards or neutralizing them from the back. But be careful, don’t look ahead or ignore your action grid and you will find yourself cornered and you will have to flee. Your goal is to assassinate as many targets as you can before getting to the end of the deck.

To start the game you will gather the map cards into deck in front of you. All cards are face-up and oriented upright. These are the cards you will be moving through throughout the game.
Behind this deck of cards you will place the action tracker card, sideways and with the action grid visible..
These cards you will hold in the palm of your left hand. Using your left index finger to keep track of the game's rounds and your right thumb will be held onto the last used action on the action grid (this is important for later on).

The map cards:
The map cards are the cards your character will be traversing. Your position on the cards must be remembered when playing in-hand. Each map card has one target and many guards, all of which can be assassinated. However the target is of course... the target. And you will gain points depending on how many targets you have assassinated at the end of the game.
The map cards are divided into squares, and most of these squares have one of two "heights", ground level or rooftop. Movement through connecting squares of the same height is unlimited, however to change height or do other parkour maneuvers you will have to use the action grid.

The action grid:
The action grid contains 4 possible actions, of which you may perform one each round. The catch is that you can never do the same action 2 rounds in a row, and you can only do an action that is connected via an arrow (or line without arrow) to the last used action.

A summary of the actions:

     Jumping. Take a run-up of one square to jump from one over a ground square in the same direction as the run-up.
     Descending. Go down a height.
     Climbing. Go up a level.
     Assassinating. Assassinate a target to claim that card as a point. Or assassinate guards to open up new paths.


These actions are explained in greater detail in the game's rulebook.

Game end:
As explained above you attempt to beat each card by assassinating the target, thus getting a point. Or getting caught by a guard and having to flee to the next card.
You play until you've finished the deck and then tally up your points to see how skilled an assassin you are.

Welcome to the sisterhood!

Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine transports you to the blistering, hard-hitting BattleMech conflicts that ravage the war-torn future of the 31st century and beyond!

In this issue, you’ll sign up for a notorious mercenary unit and fight through your first day on the job. You’ll jockey to be first across the finish line in a hovertank race fraught with danger. You’ll chase the glory days of a once-noted BattleMech manufacturer in a quest to reinvigorate a declining enterprise and secure your birthright. Will you defeat your demons from the Battle of Tukayyid during battle with would-be conquerors? Will you escape from murderous cartel members, or succumb to the elements in the unrelenting, sun-beaten desert?

Load your chamber with technical articles, a Northwind Highlanders unit digest, a MechWarrior’s catalog of cockpit must-haves, a snow-filled RPG adventure, a BattleForce scenario, and more—all by BattleTech veterans and new enlistees:

Thank you for purchasing our latest expansion pack for the award-winning Arcade Operations system: UrbanFest! As you wait for your system to install this new, exciting, downloadable content, please take a few moments to review our updated Terms of Service… [Click!] Are you sure you want to skip the ToS? And prepare yourselves for a real treat with this, our first (and only?) expansion to the game catalog meant to spice up your BattleTech games in a variety of new (and, frankly, ridiculous) ways: UrbanFest! Feel the power and excitement that can only come from thirty tons of slow-moving metal, and limited firepower!
