UrbanFest! expands on the playability of the sim-game rules first introduced in PseudoTech: Arcade Operations, with new features ranging from the amazing Urbify! power-up, to all new medal-worthy achievements, and a host of new scenario types with which to earn them! Even better yet, this expansion introduces new UrbanMech variants and never-before-seen technologies bound to raise an eyebrow or two both on and off the arena circuit! So, park your favorite standby in the garage, don your neurohelmet, and climb into the giant trashcan-’Mech of your choice; it’s time to show the Inner Sphere that size (and speed, and firepower, and armor, and fully actuated arms) isn’t everything!

"Angriff auf die Schneefestung" (German for "Assault on the Snow Fort") is an asymmetric microgame for 2 players in postcard format.

The game is played on a grid of 5x5 spaces. Both players secretly choose their actions and then reveal and perform them simultaneously.

One player attacks the snow fort and can only move forward, either straight or diagonally. They win the game by reaching the row right in front of the fort without being caught.

The other player defends the fort and can only move left or right or stay in place. The defender always throws a snowball at the end of their turn. If the attacker is located in the same column as the defender, they get hit and have to retreat 2 spaces. The defender wins by pushing the attacker out of the grid or by ending their action on the same space as the attacker.

-description from designer

MoonArk is a brand new 'In-hand' tactical card game from Abandoned lighthouse Games...

Alert! Alert! impact is imminent! As air support for the Anti-Extinction Squad, It's your job to help the ground team rescue the last few stranded animals before a giant asteroid strikes the planet.

With good tactical decisions, great memory and little bit of luck, you must work your way through a deck of beautifully illustrated tarot sized cards, matching animals with their own specific Containment Pods and airlifting them to safety.

There are two fire cards that you pull out of the deck. Shuffle the deck, hold it in your hand with the faces down, then put those fire cards at the back. Flip over the top card and see what it is. This is your view from your vehicle.

Sometimes you will find an animal. It will be an herbivore or a carnivore. Each animal is assigned a rescue pod number, and a displacement number. If the animals rescue pod is nowhere to be seen in the deck near this animal, then you must use the displacement number to push the animal backwards in the deck. But be careful that you don't put her before is in front of carnivores or they will be eaten and discarded.

If you find a rescue pod, you can remove it from the top of the deck and blindly place it anywhere in the deck. This is where some of the memory aspect comes in. You're hoping to get that pod next to its matching animal. And you don't want either of them to end up between the two fire cards which are slowly moving towards the front of the deck, or you will lose them to the fire.

Occasionally you will find a poacher. You have to move them back into the deck, hoping that you get them between the fire cards.

Every time you are able to manipulate the cards so that a carnivore is in front of its matching pod, or an herbivore is behind its matching pod, as those two cards make their way to the front of the deck, you get to remove both cards from the deck because you have saved that animal.

Once the fire cards have made their way to the front of the deck, look to see what's been lost between them, and then move that whole batch of cards to the back of the deck again. Keep going, moving cards around in the deck, until there isn't anything else left that you can do.

Save as many animals as you can, and if you haven't saved them all, try to do better during your next play!

A hand-building game where players try to get as close to 10 or -10 as possible without going beyond. However, if any player reaches exactly either score, someone else can swipe the victory with a score of exactly 0.

On your turn, you can either play a card and draw a card to improve your hand, or you may call, letting everyone else know that the pressure is on them to finish up. The game can be finished in less than 5 minutes, making it great on the go, and as a warm-up on game night.

Several variations are mentioned in the rules to let you customize the game to your need.
The journey starts and ends in east Berlin (GDR). The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The last player to return to Berlin wins the game.
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to find the sun wins the game.

The players, as Rolli, Flitzi and Schnapp, take turns to roll and move. They have to move from the three starting spaces (pedestrian, bicyclist, car driver) to the lake and back to their "Ziel" space, while minding the traffic rules. Some spaces hinder or benefit the players. The first player to arrive back at their "Ziel" space wins the game.

boardgame: Bone to Pick: Rise of the Necromancers
description:
Fight against enhanced Undead and their masters, the mighty Necromancers while surviving dreadful events! Become true Legends by slipping into the role of unique characters and fight with your favorite pet by your side and new weapons in your arsenal!

This expansion adds various modules to Bone to Pick. These modules give extra variety, but on the other hand, they increase it to a more chaotic level, giving it extra fun, if desired. The new Undead and the Necromancers can be added to any game mode. The new Upgrades, Characters, Events, and the corresponding Pets can only be used in the Campaign Mode. During a Bone to Pick game any of these modules can be used, even all of them at once. The main goal remains the same: to beat the Undead and to become a Legend.

boardgame: Der Tankfix deckt die Karten auf ...
description:
There are three triplets of cards. Each player gets three cards and three cards constitute the talon. Then each player takes a card from the talon and puts a card under the talon. The first player to get a triplet wins the game.

The game was published about 1956.

Moto Cross is an old racing dice game by Piatnik, presumably published in Austria in the 1960s or 1970s.

Players roll dice to go forward. If you stop on a yellow flag, special good or bad events happen, either slowing you down or giving you some extra steps forward. The first player to complete two rounds on the board wins the game.

The board has nice artwork, and the motor bike miniatures are made from metal and painted.

The box contains:

     the game board
     six motor bikes
     two dice


(rules are printed on the inside of the box cover)

Except for the rules, the game is language independent.

boardgame: Pop It! Battle Game:  Yoda vs. Darth Vader
description: Roll the character die and whoever rolls higher gets to "pop" the bubbles on the opposing pop it.  If both players roll the same result then the dice are up for grabs and whoever grabs them first gets to pop the sum of the dice.  The player who completely pops the opposing character is the winner.

boardgame: Star Trek: Alliance – Dominion War Campaign: Part II
description:
The Klingons join the fight, as you become a starship captain, upgrade your ship, and take on the Dominion yourself or with friends. Star Trek Alliance is a cooperative miniatures experience, featuring starship battles in the Star Trek universe! This box contains components for 1-2 players to play the Dominion War Campaign: part II, including painted plastic ships, cards, tokens, and a 7-mission campaign book. With multiple copies of this set, up to 6 players can battle the Dominion together!

You can either jump in here, or continue your story for a truly epic adventure! Create new Klingon captains, or carry forward Federation captains from the Star Trek Alliance: Dominion War Campaign and play a series of campaign missions. During each mission, battle against Dominion ships controlled by the game in tactical space combat while completing objectives to earn experience points. Between missions, spend experience points to upgrade your ships and advance the campaign!

A good general knows that he climate in which they fight is as important as any other factor during a battle. It does no good if their troops are falling over dead from heat stroke from wearing cumbersome gear, or freezing to death in the bitter cold with nothing but thin mail to protect them.

The hot, oppressive, Dornish climate means that the Starfall Knights, the heavy cavalry from House Dayne, vassals of House Martell, are less armored than most heavy cavalry from across Westeros, but their lighter armor and barding means they are generally faster and able to strike at enemies more tactically. These skilled horsemen can strike from unexpected quarters, using their spears to great effect in close combat with a devastating charge.

boardgame: メイドマフィアK (Maid x Mafia x Kai)
description:
A long time ago, the British Empire colonized the world and propagated the consumption of tea. The british forces were eventually repelled by a small country in the Far East. And so, tea was banned as it had grown to become a symbol of the british imperialism in many countries. However, people who couldn't resist the allure of tea secretly had tea brought to their homes by mafia organizations and held tea parties. The mafia members in charge of delivering tea were dressed in maid uniforms. They became known as "Maid Mafia."

Maid x Mafia x Kai is the second game in the serie after the first Maid x Gun x Tea which was a smaller scale prototype.

Battle on the map of Kobe with your fellow players. First player to reach 25 points on the score track win the game. Gain points by smuggling tea in a pick-up and delivery gameplay and bringing them from the ports/warehouses to the shop that will serve it. Hire additional maids that will share their movement points to deliver more tea and battle henchmen or other player's maid with the use of the confrontation card system.
But be careful, you are all part of the same organization so attacking another player will brand you as an enemy. Yet, among the many actions at your disposal, it may sometimes be the best option. Happy smuggling!

boardgame: パンピンポンゲーム (Pang Ping Pong Game)
description:
パンピンポンゲーム (Pang Ping Pong Game) is a speed card game from Japan. Similar to Uno, players must play their cards to the discard pile as quickly as possible. But the cards must be played in order: Pang>Ping>Pong>pang and so on. Players play as quickly as they can in no turn order, but a player cannot play twice to pile; another player must play a card before they can play again. The first player to shed their hand wins the round, and winning three rounds wins the game.

There are also special cards such as double cards like PangPing which counts as player two in a row, and Skip, which lets you skip a card in order: Pang>Skip>Pong.

One player composes a deck of role cards (with some guidance from the rulebook), one for each player plus one.
That player passes the deck of cards to the next player around the table who chooses one card before passing the deck along, each player takes one role.
The deck comes back to the player who created the deck, they choose one between the remaining two. The leftover card is placed in the middle of the table.
After discussing, the players simultaneously vote to eliminate the other team. Each player with two votes against them is eliminated.
The winning team is assessed by revealing the surviving roles.
Match-play golf simulation game, playing item, prank and caddie cards, rolling club dice to solve the direction.

Monkey Wise is a brand new trivia game! Every single question is based on a real and bizarre headline collected over the last 30 years!

The turn player (the Reader) takes a question from the box and reads it aloud presenting multiple choice options. The other players select the answer they believe is correct. Then the Reader select one of the wrong answers. For every reader they catch-out for selecting an incorrect answer, they gain points.

Finally, the correct answer is revealed and any players that chose correctly score as well.

With over 100 question cards, this game will keep you family guessing for games to come!

SHOOT-A-LOOP is a marble-shooting, point-scoring, dexterity game for any number of players. There are many ways to play, but all involve loading a marble or marbles onto the loop-track and launching them into scoring positions by pulling back and releasing a spring.

Original box included:
Tin loop-track with integrated scoring tray and launch spring
6 glass marbles in different colors
Packing slip
Instructions printed on box

The box reads:

DIRECTIONS FOR PLAYING No. 59 SHOOT-A-LOOP

Place all marbles in the track at the lowest point. The marbles are propelled forward by pulling button found on the end of flat spring, away from the upright and allowing it to spring back with a snap. After doing this a few times, the player can judge what force is necessary to shoot the marbles into the scoring tray. Scores are plainly marked beside each hole in the tray.

In most cases, players will have their own ideas about method of play and scoring, but we find the following to game very interesting. Players will have to use their own ideas in deciding who is to play first, second, etc. One player continues to shoot until all marbles are in the scoring tray. The total score for this player is then taken and the next player takes his turn. This continues until one of the players has accumulated a total count of one thousand or over. He or she is the winner.

MADE BY
WOLVERINE SUPPLY & MFG. CO.
N. S. PITTSBURGH, PA.

CATAN – Starfarers – New Encounters adds three exciting new scenarios with unique rules to CATAN - Starfarers for an even more epic race to explore the galaxy. This set of three separate scenarios adds new plastic pieces with space amoebas and pirate capsules, a new lost-people sector, game boards and tokens that bring the scenarios to life in epic sci-fi fashion.

A pair of giant space amoebas have been detected in our system. Find out more about these mysterious creatures and stop the chaos before it's too late.

A print-and-play game of daring adventurers in dangerous dungeons.

You play as a brave adventurer in a fantasy world of magic and monsters.

Each hero has their own advantages and special abilities. Each map presents a different set of challenges. By playing different combinations of heroes and maps, the game provides a variety of experiences and stays fresh through many plays.

The Wicked Wizard of Oz is a portal to the Marvellous Land of Oz, in which you take on the role of the one of the protagonists of L. Frank Baum’s The Wonderful Wizard of Oz as they embark upon a new and perilous quest, as they find themselves called upon to save the world once more.

Hunted by the Wicked Witch of the West's winged monkeys and joined by her friends – the Scarecrow, the Tin Woodman, and the Lion – Dorothy Gale sets off for the Emerald City. Surrounded by enemies on all sides, will you uncover the secrets of The Wicked Wizard of Oz?

You decide which route to take, which perils to risk, and which of Oz’s strange denizens to fight. But be warned – whether you succeed in your quest or meet a dire end, somewhere over the rainbow, will be down to the choices YOU make. Are you ready to return to Oz?

991 AD: Olaf the Dane came with his ships and army to the towns of east England. The Danes overran these places, one by one, until they arrived at Maldon. Here Ealdorman Byrhtnoth marched against the Danes with his army. The English held the ford between the island where the Danes had landed and Maldon on the mainland. The Danes asked permission to cross uncontested and fight on equal terms. For some reason, Byrhtnoth agreed. After great struggle, the Danes killed the ealdorman. The brave English fell in battle, the cowardly fled, and the Danes took possession of that place of slaughter.

Battle of Maldon is a two-player game commemorating this famous battle. Players take on the role of either the Danes or the English, and attempt to capture the enemy through maneuver and attack. All you need is a regular deck of cards to play.

—description from the designer

Nelly and her friends hunt a vampire in the mountains. To get there, they need to use Calm, Learning and Cunning!

Nelly Rapp's Vampire Hunt is a game for 2-4 players that trains deduction skills and logic.

The player who can best balance planning and risk-taking will catch the vampire!

The players start at a school and have to pass through several tiles using command cards. The first person reaching the end tile defeats the vampire.

description:
The Wizard's First Trial is a hand-made PnP riddler set in a world of Harry Potter. 
It suits well for the birthday party and can help to give a present or win some souvenirs for all of the guests. Yet it is still fun to play with friends solving riddles cooperatively.

The game consists of a set of puzzles that follow each other till the final envelope. Once parents (or those who set-up the game) print, cut through, glue and hidden all the puzzle envelopes around the house, players will have to find them and solve the riddles hidden, following from one to another.

All elements needed for the game can be printed in one of three options: in black and white print, in color or in color on kraft paper.

This game is designed for home use and is appropriate for age 10+.

—description from the designer

Alien Siege allows you to take part in the Marines’ first encounter with the Aliens, in the chamber under the reactor. It is based in Aliens, the 1986 science fiction/horror film.

The game may be played by one or more people. The player (or players) run the squad of 9 Marines; the Aliens pretty much run themselves, following an easy set of rules.

The game ends when all the Marines have left the room through the doorway at the north end of the Map, or have been dragged off by the Aliens.

This game is a simplified form of the standard Leading Edge system. Used, for example, in Phoenix Command.

The authorship of the game is not indicated, only the © of Leading Edge Games is mentioned, but quite possibly the authors are Barry Nakazono and David McKenzie, designers also of Phoenix Command and Aliens.

Was included in Gateways Magazine #10.

What can you do in 8 seconds?

Place a sufficiently fat deck of cards face down in the middle of the table. This is not a complicated start, so you can continue the preparations. Choose a starting player, he will be the judge. There are several ways to do this. He could be the player with the lowest IQ or the person who has most recently ate garlic. You can only hope that they will treat others gently.

The judge reads the top card of the draw deck aloud to his neighbor on the left. The asked player must answer within 8 seconds.

If the player does not hesitate and manages to answer the question within 8 seconds, the judge either accepts the answer and gives him 1 point (hands over the card), or puts the answer to a vote if he doubts it. After that, the questioned player becomes the new judge, draws another card and challenges the player sitting to his left. Now it's your turn to be cruel!

The first player to collect 7 cards wins the game. This is a really enjoyable party game that gets players talking about some of the strangest things.

—description from the publisher (translated)

description:
In いぬねこサンチーム (Dog & Cat The Team), players divide into two teams, either the dog or cat team. Deal out 5 cards per player (7 cards in a 2 or 3 player game). The first player can play any card they want. The next player must play a card that is equal to or greater than the previously played card or they can play a special card.
When playing a regular card, you may play 1 card or 2 cards that have matching numbers. However, the 2 cards can only be a combination of milk and dog cards or milk and cat cards (dog and cat cards cannot be played together). 
If you cannot or do not want to play, you can either pass and draw a card from the deck or pass without drawing.
If you only have one card left in your hand, make sure to cry out your team's call (dog team: "bow wow", cat team "meow") or else you will have to draw 1 card as a penalty.

As soon as one player runs out of cards, they win the round and gain a point chip matching the suit of the last card they played.
After 3 rounds, the team (or the individual in 3/5 player games) with the most points wins.

Christmas Would You Prefer? is a conversation game, best played in a group, but it can also be played between two individuals.

Everyone takes a turn at answering a question.  They choose by picking a card at random.  Once they have given their answer out loud, the rest of the group then takes a poll about the same question, and if the player's answer matches that of the majority, the player earns a point.  Players choose how many rounds of questions there will be.

The game concludes when each player has answered a question in each round.  The person with the most points wins.

12 turns.  1-6 players.  A winning player can do so as soon as at the end of his turn he or his Alliance have colonized sufficient discovered (initially upside-down) planets, dead but mineable, friendly or, less easily, unfriendly.

A player's turn consists of adding a starship for each new planet he acquired in his previous turn, and then moving his starships to visit other planets, attack enemy starships or bases, or beat back Dead Zones if the Death Star has been activated and not yet destroyed. He can also move his base to another live planet.

The Alliance starts on Jord/Earth, but no more than the 2 highest scoring players in it can get credit for winning the game if the Alliance does, so others may emigrate to other planets to set up their base there and become independent and/or piratical Vikings.

Negotiations and alliances can become as cut-throat as in classic Diplomacy. After the 7th of 12 turns the Alliance can *start* a war with a competitor/Viking, if the latter hasn't already started a war with *it*.

Planets to be found and diplomatically (or militarily) colonized (for game-winning credit) are even out in the outer reaches, but then so is the Death Star which is a threat to everyone if awakened and usually requires everyone to set aside their competitions and even wars to defeat!

Bases - Earth being the first and central - 6 starships, 5 friendly planets, 5 unfriendly planets, 4 dead but mine-able planets, 5 asteroid markers, empty space markers, and the Death Star on a 37 hexagon space field.

-description from designer

description:
The year is 1954. The Algerian people are rising up against French colonizers who have oppressed them for over a century. But this is no regular war - victory will not be achieved on the battlefield, but by winning the hearts and minds of everyday Algerians.

Insurgent: Algeria is a simple, asymmetric card game for 1 to 2 players which recreates the Algerian War for independence on your tabletop. Players take the role of the French government and the Algerian resistance [the FLN], which have separate faction decks with unique abilities.

The game can be won in two ways - attrition and domination. An attrition victory involves collecting the most public support, represented by the icons on the top of the cards, in two of three fronts - military, political and diplomatic. A domination victory occurs when a player overwhelms their opponent in any one front. Players must balance these competing objectives strategically in order to win the game.

The solo game pits the Algerian player against a French AI, which is simple to implement but provides a difficult challenge since the AI plays cards faster than the two-player game allows.

-description from designer

Björn Borg TENNIS is a simulation of a real tennis match with the help of rolling a dice. It's the same rules as real tennis.

The simulation is done by rolling a dice and following what the dice result shows in a guide. Based on the result, a ball is moved across the playing field (which is a tennis court). For example, on the first serve, a one makes the ball go into the net and a six makes it a serve-ace. If the ball is still in play, you take turns rolling the dice and follow the results in the table. Game and set wins are marked on a score marker and whoever wins three sets first wins the match.

There are also rules for playing a doubles match and holding a tournament with more players.

—user summary

Var i Världen? ("Where in the World?") tests your knowledge of our world. You may know that the Serengeti is a famous national park, but can you point it out on the map too? In Var i Världen? all players answer (or guess) the same questions on topics such as: history, food, culture, sports, music, architecture, literature, nature, geography, art and language.

After a question is read, each player takes turns placing a cube on the board (which is a world map divided into squares) where they think is the correct place for what is being asked. An example of a question might be "This is the southernmost capital city in the world".

Several players can place in the same square and everyone who has placed in the correct square gets a point. First player to collect 10 points wins. Each player also has special cubes marked with +1 or +2 and which allow you to expand the area for correct answers. These can only be used once and then removed from play. Some questions can also give extra points if you also mark the correct year on a timeline.

—user summary

Cyberrun throws you right into the action in a technologically advanced city filled with symbiotic alien organisms, cybernetically enhanced humans, and AI powered robots.
A group of mercenaries is hired to take on missions no matter the danger. Without further thought the merciless Brute uses the strength of their four arms to charge right into a mob of out-of-control robots to create a path for the Runner to strike the huge Alien. But the Alien only gets into a raging fit and spits toxic poison on the Runner’s arm. The Medic quickly and efficiently comes to their aid and does everything in their power to save them. One of the robots is about to shoot the Medic but the Hacker intervenes, hijacking the robot’s firewall. Finally the Drone pilot finishes off the mob with heavy artillery robots. Only the giant Alien still stands and charges at the pilot with its powerful claws. The mental attack of the psychic stops the Alien in its tracks, trapping the Alien’s mind in agony. Another mission is accomplished when the last gunshot stops Alien’s screaming. The Runner nods off to the Medic as they return with the Alien’s head in their grasp. What might their employee have in mind with such an obnoxious trophy one might wonder but mercenaries ask no questions.

Cyberrun dives right into the sci-fi experience offering six unique starting classes and offers solo-mode and co-op gameplay for up to four players. Players find new augments and upgrades to optimize and customize characters which can be found after accomplishing missions in turn-based strategy combat style. The mercenaries have to defeat a wide variety of Alien swarms, hordes of robots and hostile human factions using mob action mechanics tracked via app on the cellphone. If players use their items strategically they can cancel the opponents’ move before their turn. The party shares a single health pool, ensuring an exciting adventure for the whole party until the end of an encounter.

Cyberrun’s gameplay is designed to combine the cooperative, exciting sci-fi experience with the fun of rolling a dice console in the shape of a traditional D6 -  the unidice. Players choose up to six different item options they can roll during their turn using the unidice like a dice gaming controller and cellphone serves as a display. Mercenaries use Action Points to roll/reroll or use the rolled gear.

Players try to break the money pot, the player to achieve that goal wins.

Komma Lika (Come Equally)

Every time you do something for the shared household – the dishes, replace a broken light bulb, take the car to the auto shop, etc. – you get points. The points consist of triangular shaped magnets of different sizes. Each member of the household gets his/her own color, and together the different colored magnets form a nice pattern. When the game period is finished, the colors should ideally be balanced and equally distributed. Everybody wins by getting equal.

It is up to you to decide how long you want the game period to be. A week or two, until you run out of magnets, or until you together have formed one of the figures shown on the cards that come with the game.

Try playing a first round while leading your lives as usual, collecting points according to the rules. Evaluate the results. Are the colors balanced? Play again – this time, try to get as equal as possible.

Who wins? It’s not about winning – it’s about getting equal.

A game of averts and spells for subversive witches! Diana, the queen of the witches, has passed on, through her daughter Aradia, the knowledge of the old religion to the women of the people; explaining to them how to perform averts to avenge the oppressions they have suffered.
Today, on the sacred day of the Moon, the most powerful witches have gathered to challenge each other to find out who will be the chosen one to whom Aradia will reveal the secret of Magic!

Goal of the game
To complete the greatest number of Conjuration with the required Ingredient Cards, thus accumulating points.
Each Conjuration has a point value.

Conduct of the Game
Distribute to each player 4 Ingredients Cards at random (5 in the case of Morgana), place on the table 5 Conjurors to complete and 4 free Ingredients Cards face up, thus forming the Witches "Market".
Starting with whoever last stroked a cat (in case of a tie, whoever stroked a black cat wins), each player must place a minimum of 1 ingredient up to a maximum of 3 on one or more of the Conjurors on the table.
If he or she cannot do so, he or she must reveal his or her hand to his or her opponents and turn over the cards in the Ingredient Cards deck until he or she finds one that can be used on a Conjuring. Cards thus uncovered and unused should be shuffled back into the deck.
In addition to playing Ingredient Cards, during his or her turn, each player may also perform the following actions in any order (even BEFORE placing the ingredient on the Conjuring)

      Draw an ingredient card by choosing it from the Marketplace or drawing it covered from the deck
     Use the abilities of one's Witch.
     Use the Spell of a Witch (see below)
     Place a Corrupted Ingredient on an already played Grain, Water, or Lemon (also outside your turn)


On a Conjuration, placing both cards in the discard pile.
The following steps are also performed before passing the turn:

     A check is made to see if one or more Conjurors have been completed. In that case, the player who placed the last ingredient takes the completed and places them next to his Witch card. As many Conjurors are drawn and placed on the table as needed to return to 5.
     You replenish the Market deck until you return to 4 the Ingredients Cards shown as soon as they are less than 4
     You draw cards from the Covered Ingredient Cards deck until you reform your hand of 4 cards (5 in the case of Morgana)


Spell of a Conjuration
Each Conjuration has a Spell attached to it. When a player who has already completed a Conjuration wants to use it, he must-in his turn unless otherwise indicated-read aloud the text portion of the ritual and then use the Spell listed on the card. Each spell can be used only once; to remember it turn the Conjuration card upside down with the spell that was used.

—description from the publisher

To begin, players must choose 3 champions to build a deck around them and their classes.

Decks are built of cards representing magical artifacts, weapons and armor, legends of the world, allied creatures, minions, realms, and campaigns. If your strategy is well-planned, these cards will work synergistically and lead you to victory.

Combat is essential to achieve victory. Your champions, allies, and minions will fight under your command against your opponent’s forces. Every time you attack, all unblocked damage points will become victory points.

Each turn has a structure, based in phases. During each phase, you can do certain actions, including playing cards. However, playing them has a cost: you must use experience points. These points are earned every turn from your champions, destroying opposing allies and minions, or completing campaign objectives.

The player who defeats the three opposing champions, or the first to collect 20 victory points, wins the game. A player with no more cards to draw loses the game.​​

—description from the designer

In Germ War: Invaders, you are a newly introduced germ in a petri dish, surrounded by hostile germs. One section at a time, you will have to fight the “home team” in order to gain superiority of the petri 
dish. 
Germ War: Invaders is a solo (with a two-player option), area control game using a traditional deck of cards. Each turn you will place your cards on the petri dish by their value, trying to gain and maintain the majority control of the sections to win the game.

In the solo game, you will play again the AI who can spread very quickly in the petri dish through a chain reaction but is limited on his growth ability. In the two-player game, its germ vs. germ. Both have the same ability, so the trick is to play the most efficient game.

The game consist of one cheese piece and 12 mice figures.

There are two variants of the rules:

     Players stack the mice on top of the cheese. The first player that makes the tower collapse loses. All other players win.
     Players stack the mice on the table. When a player makes any mice fall, they take all the fallen mice. The first player to place their last mouse wins.

In 筋肉（マッスル）テイキング (Muscle Taking), players will become bodybuilding trainees and will be practicing various poses for an upcoming bodybuilding competition. As the training progresses, the poses to be used during the competition will be decided.
At the tournament, the poses will be called in the order announced during training, so players should pose accordingly.
However, depending on the content of the training, you may take the wrong pose. The trainee who strikes the most elegant poses during the competition will win.

The legendary pirate 'Golden Hook' has disappeared! Countless treasures he left behind are buried in this island and that island!
4 pirate captains fighting for treasure island! Whose pirate crew will become a legend?

In Scribble Pirates, each player becomes a pirate boss, protects your own pirate ship, destroys the opponent's pirate ship, captures the treasure island, and develops your base to earn points. The game ends and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

You are a Superhero trying to save the city! Travel to different locations, rescue citizens, defeat minions, and save the day! But this time… it is an epic Saga event played over an entire year!

A special Saga event! - Each month in 2023, a new Super Dice Heroes mission will be released. Play along throughout the year to experience the story, impact the future missions, and even try to earn the Superhero of the month.

Impact the Story! - Did you defeat the villain, rescue a certain bystander, or earn the key to the city? Share it in real time each month to impact the story in the months to follow. What you do matters and will change the story in the months to come.

Superhero of the Month! - Compete for the high score each month. Play the monthly mission as many times as you want to try to earn the highest score. Submit your scores for that month and if you have the highest score, you will be the Superhero of the Month!

Check out the Beluga Bliss Games website and here on the BGG forums and files section to participate in this Saga event

—description from the designer

Welcome to the future! Welcome to what counts as parole in today's prison system. So, you want out? You're tired of making money for The System through hard labor? Well, if you want out early, you'll have to make up for lost profits by going through the Robot Maze. An event televised to a paid viewing audience where you have to make it out of a maze while being tracked by Robots!

In this PnP game you have an A4 map of a maze printed out. You start out in the middle of the bottom of the maze and have to move through it to one of the two exits at the top corners in order to win. You can move up to one square each turn.

But in the middle of the maze 4 robots await you and move according to the die result that you throw for each robot each turn. When you come into their line of sight, they will move onto your square and you lose. So be sure to plan your movement carefully!

Do you have what it takes to make it out safely?!?

description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Domri Rade is a brash young human whose small stature belies his ferocious nature and formidable power over wild beasts. Summon a horde of feral creatures and goad them into a frenzy as you stampede to victory!

—description from publisher

Crypto Collector is a 2-4 player competitive game. In this game, players participate in auctions as collectors of digital art, aiming to own more valuable art than anyone else.

To win:
The Player who gets the most points is the winner.

How to Play:
The game is played over 7 rounds, with 2 phases in each round.
Auction Phase:

     An auction is held to acquire the Owner Cards needed to purchase Art Cards. At the beginning of the game, six Art Cards are lined up in a row. Also, at the beginning of the round, the number of Owner Cards corresponding to the number of players is arranged in a horizontal row, face down.
     The auction markers held by the first player in turn are used. Each player can perform one of the following actions by using one Marker in turn in ascending order from 1.


[Bidding]
Bid on one Owner Card on the field.
[Buy]
Buy an art card in the lineup. The cost of the Art Card will be paid.
[Sell]
Discard an Owner Card you are holding and immediately earn the revenue indicated on the card's icon.

Revenue Phase:
In this phase (only in certain rounds), each player earns the total amount of revenue indicated on the icons of all Owner Cards held by the player.
designers: Takanari Yamaguchi (ヤマグチ タカナリ)

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Forgotten Age: Campaign Expansion
description:
We think we know the history of the Earth, but there are secrets that lie beyond our ken and truths that threaten to undo our understanding of the universe. When renowned historian Alejandro Vela discovers one of these secrets—the ruins of an ancient and forgotten Aztec city—it sets into motion a plot that could unravel the very fabric of time itself.

In The Forgotten Age Campaign Expansion, one to four investigators join an expedition to explore these ruins, venturing deep into perilous rainforests and forbidden caverns. Will they survive this dangerous journey, or will it be their undoing?

This expansion combines all of the campaign and scenario content from the original The Forgotten Age cycle, which was the third cycle of expansions for Arkham Horror: The Card Game. Players only need a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game Revised Core Set to dive into the story.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Forgotten Age: Investigator Expansion
description:
In secret and forbidden ruins buried in the uncharted places of the world, there hide ancient relics humanity was never meant to find. But a brave few will sacrifice everything to set things right and protect the Earth from its own undoing.

The Forgotten Age Investigator Expansion features five investigators for Arkham Horror: The Card Game and contains all of the player cards from the original The Forgotten Age cycle of content. Guide your party to safety as the expedition leader, Leo Anderson; discover untouched wilds as the explorer, Ursula Downs; make a quick escape as the bootlegger, Finn Edwards; call forth miracles as the priest, Father Mateo; and seek strength in a dark pact as the haunted, Calvin Wright.

This expansion is a fantastic addition to any Arkham Horror: The Card Game player’s collection, and the cards within can be used to build or enhance investigator decks for any of the game’s scenarios or campaigns.

—description from the publisher

description:
Cooperative Movementgame. One kid draws a card and puts it in a cardholder onto his head. The other kids try to reenact what is depicted on the card. The kid tries to guess what is on the card.
If the kid guesses right the bear moves one step closer to the bearcave. If on the card drawn is a snowflake or the kid guesses wrong, the snowflake descends one step closer to cave.
If the bear reaches the cave before the snowflake all kids win.

Today you dreamed your favorite dream again - the same as always. The cows barely fit in the barn, the hens are laying like crazy, and the ears of grain are bending under their own weight. The weather is fine, so you spend a beautiful afternoon outside polishing the paintwork of your new tractor. A neighbor is standing behind the fence, and the edges of his face are slowly but surely turning green with envy... Eh! Fairy tale!

But wait a minute - why a fairy tale right away? Maybe it's time to roll up your sleeves and make this dream come true? Why not? Grab the pitchfork, prepare a plan and turn your farm into a real "dream farm" - the decoration of the area and the habitat of all abundance!

Sit under a tree, put your hands under your head, roll your eyes around your estates and fat livestock and think with satisfaction "it couldn't have been better developed!" What do you have to do to be able to think like that? Earn the greatest wealth: money, equipment, plants and animals. The richest farmer wins!

—description from the publisher

The game contains of 4 phases: development, fortune, trade and race. In development phase players breed their animals. During fortune phase everyone spins the spinner in order to gain or lose some resources. Next is the trade phase, in which players buy and sell plants and animals. And finally the race phase - players flick their tractor pawns on the back of the box from a side to the stump in order to get a animal/plant token corresponding with their specialization. After 12 rounds (or 6 rounds in short game) the player who gather most points wins. The point are awarded for: tiddlywinks tokens, money, specialization tokens, animal/plant tokens, additional cards, green cards.

—user summary

THE CARDS:
A 56 card pack is used

GOAL:
The goal is to win all of the cards and the loser must clean the toilet.

TO DEAL:
Shuffle and deal out all the cards face down, one at a time, beginning to the dealer's left. Each player puts their cards in a pile, face down in front of them.

PLAY EASY
The game begins with each player in turn flipping over the card, creating a new centre pile with the cards face up.
When two identical cards are flipped over consecutively, the players who placed the matching cards must call out "shits" or "sausages" determined by the imagery on the card. The lucky player who calls out first correctly takes the centre pile of cards, adding them to the bottom of their own face down pile.
The ultimate winner is the player who ends up with all the cards.

PLAY HARD
The game begins with each player in turn flips over the card on top of their deck in front of them, creating a new deck with the cards face up.
When two identical cards are flipped over consecutively, the players who placed the matching cards must call out "shits" or "sausages" determined by the imagery on the card. The lucky player who correctly calls out first takes the oppositions pile of face up cards, adding them to the bottom of their own face down pile.
If the cards placed down are matching but a player shouts incorrectly, the opposing player wins that round, taking the
face up cards. For example, if the player calls out "shits" when being faced with a pair of cards showing "sausages" they lose that round.
When all the cards in a pile are face up, the player turns over the pile and starts again.
If two players correctly call out "shits" or "sausages" simultaneously, everyone's pile of face-up cards are placed in the centre as one pile.
When two identical cards are turned over and match a card already face-up on the centre pile, the first person who calls out "double shits" or "double sausages" wins the centre pile.
The game continues with each player in turn flipping over the card on top of their deck in front of them again. The ultimate winner is the player who ends up with all the cards.

—description from the designer

A 90-page campaign for Sellswords and Spellslingers where players build a party of 2-3 heroes to fight across city streets, alleys, squares, markets, gardens, meadows, and forests all while completing scenarios to unveil an intricate plot. Can be played solo or with up to 3 players, each controlling a single hero.

Contents: Complete rules for smaller, quicker games (1-2 hours). A story-driven campaign in 12 scenarios (6 mandatory acts and 6 optional side quests). An NPC management system to control sidekicks. Random tables (Loot, Wandering Monsters, Complications). A magic item generator and 18 new monsters.

—description from publisher

boardgame: マストフォローの建塔士 (Masuto Forō no Kentōshi)
description:
In マストフォローの建塔士, roughly "Must Follow Tower Builders", the players are erecting a tower through trick-taking.

Each card in the deck shows a specific 3D wooden piece. At the end of each trick, the players which followed suit will place the piece shown on their card onto a central tower.  Points are gained for building higher, any parts of a piece which overhang the edge of the tower, and the person to win the trick.  Points are lost if a player causes part of the tower to collapse.

The game ends when a player has 0 or fewer points or the hand is complete.  The person with the most points wins.

Originally available in pack C of the Dice Tower 2022 Kickstarter campaign.

Each player receives containers for glass, paper, plastic and organic waste. A waste dump is formed. A plum card market is created. On each turn, players sort waste by placing it in their bins, then have the option to exchange it for plums if they have enough collected waste. In addition to waste, the dump also hides special cards that can help players trade more plums by outsmarting others.

The player who exchanges the most Plums for Trash wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Punic Wars: The Struggle for the Mediterranean, 264-146 BC
description:
The Punic Wars is a two-player strategy game that takes place from the Roman landing in Sicily in 264 B.C. to the destruction of Carthage in 146 B.C. One player takes the Roman army and the other player takes the Carthaginian army.

Up to three wars can occur during the game. The winner of the game is the player who wins two of the three wars. In history, three wars were fought, but Rome won the first and second Punic Wars, so in game terms, the Roman player is the winner at the end of the second Punic War.

The core of the game is resource points, which represent the ability of both Rome and Carthage to continue the war, and the mobilization of soldiers; as well as card play, which recreates strategy and tactics. The aim is not only to win the war, but also to fight for the next war.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Kitchen Sabotage is a turn-based card/board game with a goal of gaining points by cooking recipes with various ingredients; through the game, though, players can sabotage each others' creations to try to get ahead!

2-8 players ("chefs") can play (or more with expansion pack madness and/or multiple decks). Each chef works in their own kitchen, gathering/assembling ingredients, cooking/baking them just the right amount of time, and serving them to happy (or not-so-happy) customers. Each round progresses through turns in that order (assemble ingredients, cook, and serve). But here's the twist - there is ample opportunity to sabotage each others' ingredients and cooking methods, to turn their fine cuisine into a gross heap of mush and get ahead in the restaurant game!

If you make your food to perfection, you'll be rewarded as such. But if your cooking is off too much thanks to your opponents' sabotaging, you won't be rewarded as much (or maybe even take a penalty). Players tally their earnings and losses at the end to see if anyone made it through the kitchen sabotage with edible food.

—description from the publisher

Along with these miniatures, players will find stat cards for all four characters and reprints of the Sucker, Trip Up, Lethal Protector, Uneasy Allies, All Webbed Up, and Web Barrier Team Tactics cards, ensuring they have everything they need to swing into the thick of battle.

boardgame: Five Leagues From The Borderlands: 3rd Edition – The Ancient Land of Dust
description:
Everywhere you turn, you sense the struggle between ancient death and new life. They say a great war was fought here in forgotten times. I don’t think it ever stopped.

Known to those who live here only as “The Dust”, this is one of the least hospitable regions of the world. A land of rocky ground and endless desert, saturated with magic. It is also a land rich with history and a sprawling civilization, making the most of a dangerous and precarious situation. The Dust was not always as it is today. Records tell of an ancient land so green it was known as the Emerald Fields before the rise (and fall) of a great evil. Deep in the sands, the minions of the Warlord are stirring again. Dead soldiers rise, and bands of warriors find themselves stolen across time, knowing that the Warlord calls them to service. Even the spirits that embody the desert itself feel restless, mocking mortals that a great time of war will soon be upon them again.

The Ancient Land of Dust is an adventure expansion for the Five Leagues From the Borderlands solo adventure wargame. Use the new rules, tables, and guidance to bring your characters to The Dust, or create new characters from the region. Fight ancient evils, seek fame and riches, or simply try to make your mark. The Ancient Land of Dust is filled with danger and rewards for all!

This 58-page pdf only expansion includes:

New options for character creation.
Environment and region rules for playing in The Dust.
Undertake a quest to learn the Serpentine Arts - a new form of magic with new spells and mechanics.
Details on joining The Desert Scouts who watch for danger in The Dust.
New settlements, events, and modifications to campaign activities.
Desert encounters, Temples of the Old War, deadly new enemies and Aberrations.
Guidance on preparing your tabletop and terrain. New items, equipment, quests, rewards and riches!
Requires the Five Leagues From the Borderlands core rules
family: Series: Five Leagues From the Borderland

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Hydra Tank Terrain & Ultimate Encounter
description:
Players test their skills against the pinnacle of Hydra might in Marvel Crisis Protocol: Hydra Tank Terrain & Ultimate Encounter.

A twisted machine of occult warfare, the Hydra Tank found in this pack has one purpose: total annihilation. No mere terrain feature, this pack contains everything players need to wage battle across a Hydra-dominated field in the all-new "Fire at Will!" Ultimate Encounter. Whether they take control of the Hydra Tank itself or those who oppose it, all will know the power of Hydra.

—description from the publisher

A new villain charges onto the battlefield in Marvel Crisis Protocol: Rhino.

With a durable second skin grafted all over his body, the aptly named Rhino is stronger, tougher, and more inured to pain than your average supervillain. This pack adds this rampaging villain to players' rosters, giving them another powerful option in the Spider-Foes and Criminal Syndicate affiliations. Additionally, three Team Tactic cards enhance Rhino's abilities, making him even more versatile in battle.

—description from the publisher

A pure luck roll-and-move game themed after Monopoly as a McDonald’s Happy Meal item. The gameplay is different from the previous Happy Meal game. When a player lands on an unowned property, they claim it’s deed. The game ends when all properties are taken.

The components include puzzle pieces that form a board, deed cards, four plastic tokens, and a punched card that folds into a die.

... God created the Heavens and gave it to the Angels. The Angels live in the Silver City. The Angel Lucifer took issue with God over giving Earth to the humans, and he was exiled from the Silver City. Many, known now as the The Fallen, that agreed with Lucifer, left with him, and the Red City was born. Lucifer schemes to take the Silver City. Now war is on the horizon. The Faithful Angels look to stop the Fallen roaming the Heavenly Realms. These are the Heralds of War.

Welcome to Angels: Heralds of War, a rules-lite skirmish-level war game for 2 players, but a solo mode is also available. This game uses the ZipWarz game system. In the game, one player plays as the Fallen Angels, while the other player plays as the Faithful Angels. Each side has their own goals they are looking to achieve on the battlefield. Each goal earns them Victory Points. The player with the most Victory Points at the end of the 4th round wins the game.

It's a PnP game where you can use your available minis, tokens and dice with the PnP material provided. Included with Angels: Heralds of War are 8 unique armies ready to battle. Upwärts! is a fun dice game where the players can pile the numbers they have rolled into a grid. Depending on the different options to get points (arranging numbers in lines, snakes or bricks) the players have to smartly stack the rolled numbers to win. As an additional challenge, the players can use the optional "event die".

How to play: When it's the players turn, he/she rolls all three dice. All players put down the shown numbers on their notepad and smartly sort them into the grid by letting them "fall" into the rows. Each player decides how to stack the numbers to get the highest possible number of points. The different lines, snakes or bricks  have to be made up of the same number, for example, a player could attempt to build a line of number "5" whenever a 5 is rolled in the game.

When the notepad is filled out completely, the players can count the points.

This fun children's game encourages communication and imagination at the same time. Each player draws a secret motive card  without looking at it. He/she then shows the other players the card. Everything is in butter, boss! is a card game about common phrases. If you translate German idioms into the English language you don't get the correct English equivalent, but rather a funny English sentence. Then it's not English, it's Denglish ("D" for Deutsch = German). So one by one the players draw a card and have to translate the Denglish sentences into German and then also have to find the correct English phrase. Only if a player can translate the Denglish phrase into German and then correctly into English they can keep the card. The more cards the players collect the more points they earn. Whoever earns most points wins.

description:
Tenacity is a quasi-real time arithmetic game. Each turn, a player plays a card to a central stack and announces the sum of the cards in the stack - the card they played and the number said by the previous player.

Each player has a face down "target" chip in front of themselves. If the number announced matches a player's target, the player should grab a stone from the middle of the table. Once the other players see someone grab a stone, they all try to grab a stone as well.

The player who is not able to grab a stone takes the cards from the central stack as a penalty.

The game ends when the deck of cards or target chips are exhausted and the person with the most points wins.

description:
You wake up in an endless desert, with only a door on the horizon.

What happened?! Is this - the great beyond? Why are your friends here? And HOW ON EARTH did you get here??

In Epilogue you'll form words using fragments to cooperatively tell the story of your group's final moments. Was it shenanigans? Tragedy? Or a whole factory of banana peels? How does your story end?

Market Share is an economic head-to-head game where players take the role of company owners specialising in producing and selling a product in a duopoly setting. Each economic quarter, the companies will hire experts, pick up contracts, and decide on advertising expenditure (that will shape the potential market share) and the amount of product to be produced. The demand, which is uncertain and affected by the outcome of random events, needs to be fulfilled from the current quarter's production but also excess production from previous quarters (inventory), converting potential market share into actual market share. Unfulfilled demand and unfulfilled contacts will lead to reduction of the company’s share value.

The game is played over 8 rounds (8 quarters or 2 years). At the end of the 8th round, each company owner sums up the wealth accumulated during the game (company cash, shares, value of inventory stock). The winner is the player with the most total wealth. However, an early finish is possible. The game may end before the end of the 8th round if one of the two companies has dominated the market or if a company goes bankrupt.

You are the respected Dr. Jekyll, an honorable and philanthropic gentleman of Victorian London. Your life is divided between doing good deeds, making elixir, and controlling your inner monster — the dangerous and impulsive Mr. Hyde. Of course it's not all smooth sailing, but indulging the desires of the odious scoundrel and making money from him helps you to carry out your charitable work. How could it go wrong?

To win Jekyll / Hyde, the Good Deeds Counter must be at 6 and the Impulses Counter at 0 at the beginning of Dr. Jekyll's turn. You lose the game if, at the beginning of Mr. Hyde's turn, the Elixir Counter is 0 or the Tracked Counter is 6. If both counters meet these conditions, you also lose.

description:
The Winter Park is a special playmat which adds some special rules to its gameplay:

Each round one park is transformed into the Winter Park before the Draw and pick cards action in the deck phase. 
 When you have to put 1 or more cards on your bench during the match phase, you choose for each of them on which seat you want to put it: On one of the 4 normal seats (with campfire) or on one of the two frozen seats.

On each frozen seat you can put 1 or more cards up to the power threshold of the current round (= number of the round), even if they do not share the same name.

boardgame: 1758 Fort Carillon: Abercromby's Fumble at Ticonderoga
description:
In July 1758, the British army made a major push north against Fort Carillon, a.k.a. Fort Ticonderoga. This was part of a three-pronged campaign against New France, led by Major-General Abercromby.

With a massive army of 17,000 men supported by 44 pieces of artillery, the 3,500 French and Canadian defenders were facing overwhelming odds. Due to unforeseen events, the battle turned out to be a decisive victory for the French commander Montcalm and his forces.

1758 Fort Carillon - Abercromby's Fumble at Ticonderoga is a detailed simulation of the battle, starting from the British landing at day break of July 6th. Lead elements included Rogers Rangers.

Each artillery piece and key leader or engineer is individually represented in the game. Combat units are 100 men each. You can build or destroy bridges or earthworks. The game also includes small boats such as bateaux that can be carried over land and larger rafts for artillery. Optional rules include ships from the small French navy on Lake Champlain.

—description from the designer

description: Eight promo cards: three new restrictions and five new characters.

The players choose a game master (traffic inspector) to monitor the exact observance of all traffic rules during the game. He notes the number of moves drawn and the number of penalties for each player. He manages the till. 
One half of the tokens goes into the cashbox, the other half is distributed evenly to the players.
At the start of the game, the traffic signs should now be placed on the empty, white circles. The game master shows the players one sign after the other and the players place them on the appropriate place according to the landscape of the game board. If a player gets it wrong, he pays a token into the till and the next player must now indicate the correct place, etc. If the answer is correct, the player receives a token from the till. If someone violates the same sign the second time, he pays 2 tokens, the third time 3 tokens, etc.
Each player places his figure on his starting space S in the color of his own vehicle (car, truck, racing car or motorbike). Each player has to get to his destination Z (which again has the color of the vehicle). He is free to choose his path. 
All players always make a move one after the other. The streets are divided into equal spaces. Each space means one turn at a driving speed of 30 km/h. This is the initial speed for each vehicle. On the second move the vehicle may go to the next higher speed of 60 km/h. Each vehicle may increases its speed by a further 30 km each time it moves up to its specified maximum speed (truck 60km/h, motorbike 90 km/h, car 120 km/h, racing car  150 kn/h). This rule is interrupted immediately if the vehicle comes to a road obstacle, which is indicated by a traffic sign. Such a sign forces the player to drive more slowly or even to stop or turn around.

Whoever gets to their goal Z in the shortest time - i.e. with the fewest moves - and with the fewest penalty points and the most money is the winner. If two players arrive at the same time, the number of penalty points and the money decides. The winner receives half the till as a prize. Only the driver who gets to his destination in 35 moves without making any mistakes, becomes the champion driver. He wins the title and in this case the whole till.

In 209X, Nigaleos, who was contracted for development by the Veil Army, was developing two types of Mechs, one specialized in attack and one specialized in defense. The airframe was built at both the research base and the production base.
The introduction of this 'Mech to the front line should have brought the battle on Ganymede to an end quickly. MEC engineers believed so and continued development. But Nigaleos executives had a different idea.

Ganymede Senki Zero2 is a standalone expansion for ガニメデ戦記Zero. You can play with it alone or mix it with ガニメデ戦記Zero and play with up to 4 people.

description:
An expansion for Yokai Battle that adds action cards, as well as, new monsters to the game.

This expansion includes new type of cards to be added to players decks, named action cards, separated into three different types:
Spells are played and placed in the discard pile after resolving.
Traps are placed face-down on the board and activate when a monster is played on top of them.
Equipment are played on monsters to modify their characteristics.

Included are 18 action cards per player (6 spells, 6 traps, 6 equipment), and 8 new monsters per player.

Multi Level Mania is a game where you join a multi-level marketing company (totally different from a pyramid scheme, we swear!) and hustle your way to the top by pitching the products or the company to character cards. The goal of the game is to make $10,000 before the Cubicle Troll (an NPC with a regular paycheck) and before all the other players.

The game can be played by 2-6 players and takes between 1-2 hours.

Each round consists of four phases: the Hustle phase, the Payday phase, the Restock phase, and the Event phase:

During the Hustle phase, players choose character cards and roll a die to either try to sell product to that character, or to recruit that character as a downline.

During the Payday phase, each player gets paid money according to the number and type of downlines they have - and the Cubicle Troll gets their paycheck.

Next comes to Restock phase, when all players must buy at least the minimum required inventory to maintain membership in their chosen MLM - don't worry, you can take out credit cards if you need to!

Finally, during the event phase, each player selects an event card that can either help or hurt their chances of success. Then it's time for a new round with another Hustle phase!

This game allows for some sabotage of other players by stealing or alienating their downlines, and all players have the opportunity to spend money to make money - just like a real pyramid scheme - I mean, multi-level marketing company!

—description from the designer

Illimat: The False Baron's Set features six additional Luminary options for Illimat, bringing new strategies and surprises to your experience.

Will you take a chance on The Gambler, or use the Astrologer to harness the power of the Stars? Will your summer plans wither away in The Drought, or will you reach across The Universe to seize a glorious victory?

The damage depends on the creatures basic strength and equipment. Creature cards has special abilities to avoid or reduce damage. To activate the ability players has to roll the dice.

The goal of Tales are Real is to destroy as many creatures of your opponent as possible. The player who lost the most creature cards within five rounds, loses the game.

Take advantage of your innate talents and combine the best wine to enhance the flavors and outperform the competition.

The aim of this game of cards and recipes is to get the highest score by combining the ingredient cards to get the best dishes. Match them with drinks to get more points.
