boardgame: Fiesta de los Muertos: 5 cartes pour mettre à jour
description: Five promo cards: three new restrictions and two new characters.

A reimplementation of Muumi viidakkoseikkailu, Coral Reef and Etsi ja löydä Safariseikkailu is a game of fast reactions that has the players flipping two cards and trying to find the animals depicted in the cards from the board as soon as possible.

First player to find both animals scores a point, five points wins the game. Some rounds reward players with looking glass tokens and it's also possible to win the game by collecting two camera tokens.

Simple roll and move game for children in the Moomin world. If you land on an action space, you draw a card for some physical tasks like "skip around the players on one leg". Watch for Stinky to see if you should follow the card instructions or do the opposite.

In a zombified Tahiti Island, players draw cards from a pile placed in the center of the table. Among these cards are zombies (cats, lizards, sharks, whales, pigs, roosters…, each with a value point. On your turn you play as many card as you want or you pass, then you draw a card. When you draw a zombie card you have to play it in front of you or give it to a neighbor using action card you have in hands, which refer to a tahitian object or action. The player with the least "zombies points" when the draw pile is empty win the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Race around on a mine cart collecting treasure, stealing eggs, and running from dragons!

In Cartastrophe, you are a Dwarf in search of treasure. Lay the tracks as you whip around in your mine cart through the labyrinth, picking up loot, including priceless Dragon Eggs! But once you steal an egg, Mama goes out for the hunt. Outrun the dragons and collect the most treasure to win!

Each turn, attach one new tile to the tracks already placed onto the play area. Place treasure tokens onto the matching icons of the newly placed tile.

Then move your cart three spaces, following the tracks, picking up any treasure tokens you pass over.

Use collected Nitro tokens to move additional spaces, or place one of your few bonus tiles to help maneuver through the maze.

If you have taken the risk and stolen a Dragon Egg, the player to your left will move the marching Dragon standee in an attempt to get their egg back! The Dragon's speed increases every turn.

Collect matching treasure sets, evade the Dragons, and have the most points at the end of the game to win!

The Battle of Saratoga is a low to moderate complexity tactical game covering the British attempt to break through the American lines. One player commands the British and their Hessian mercenaries and and the other player commands the American forces. The game takes about 90 minutes to play.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
From Tijuana to Merida, from Matamoros to Merida, the entirely of Mexico is encompassed in Mexican Railways. Foreign connections to the United States and Guatemala provide lucrative incentives in this luckless economic game where the most money at the end of the game wins. Business interests drive players in continual expansion. Loans are taken for intensive capital investment, with the game ending when one player has expended all their cubes. Family money provides an initial boost to these ventures.

Mexican Railways is a stand-alone game that uses the wood cubes, the money and the base rules of Iberian Railways. The game is for 3-5 players and lasts about an hour. The 'International Mexico' bonus is a new, very powerful addition to the game as are the international connections.

Mexican Railways is the fourth game using Winsome’s Regional Railways System. The prior three games were Rhodesian Railways, Balkan Railways and Iberian Railways. The base rules for this system may be downloaded from Rio Grande Games page for Iberian Railways.

—description from the publisher

A party game combining reflex and speed.

The cards are in a pile in the middle of the table. On each of them, 2 mathematical operations and 2 words in the center, but also 4 numbers and 4 images on the edge. Only one correct association is possible. 
Ex : On a card, you have the word "lion" and a picture of lion. You can also have "24-5" and "19".

You have to be the first to find the right answer to win the card. The winner is the one with the most cards at the end of the stack.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Core Set 2019 Planeswalker Deck: Liliana, the Necromancer
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Liliana has mastered death and bends it to her will, defying the laws of nature in pursuit of power. Use her skills to raise an army of undead and drain the life from your opponent.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Harry Potter: Houses in a Row
description:
This is a basic tile matching game based in the theme of Hogwarts house crests from the Wizarding world of Harry Potter.

It's a very simple and quick to learn game about drawing tiles and configuring them on the playspace.

This game is fairly quick to play, the first player successfully match up 4 of their house token will win.

Very simple concept but quite more enjoyable for a fan of Harry Potter or Wizarding world themed games.

boardgame: Quest for the Lost Pixel: Deities of Time
description:
New Expansions

Deities of Time is the FINAL expansion for Quest for the Lost Pixel. This expansion (just like Hack Slash Loot) comes with a bonus expansion called GAME OVER.

Deities of Time

Deities of Time comes with a new time deck and deity cards. These can help heal you through your exploration, but come with restrictions that your hero must follow in order to stay faithful to your deity.
Village Game Mode

This expansion also comes with a new Village Game Mode for solo players. This allows the player to build a Village in between floors. Each new build comes with special abilities that will help aid the hero throughout their journey. Building these buildings will also give you Crown Points. These Crown Points will give you a score at the end of the game. Try to beat your last score or a friends high score.
Nightmare Mode

There is now a new harder mode for veterans of the game or those of you that enjoy a challenge. Tweaks to the Artifacts, Spells, and even how you attack with different elemental damage will give the player more decisions to make.
GAME OVER

Want more stuff? Well here it is, more stuff. This includes more cards for your decks, replaces (and adds to) your potion deck, and provides 2 new Heroes - The Chanter and Construct
Rainbow Rift

One of the most requested add-ons to the game has been dungeon variety. Although this isn't a huge variety of dungeons, here is the new Rainbow Rift. A fun yet challenging new area with goofy monsters and high rewards and XP.
A HUGE THANK YOU

Again, this is the final expansion for Quest for the Lost Pixel and I just wanted to say a HUGE thank you to everyone who has supported this game and me throughout the years. The game came out in 2015 and just like the ‘Little Train that Could’, this game kept chugging. It truly wouldn’t be as well known if it weren’t for Geek Gamers and Dungeon Dive. Also, I couldn’t give enough thanks to some of this game’s biggest fans such as David Waller, bark, Prinz Eugen, Scrap Baby, Hlorridi, Nico Farhat, funkboyg, Dani Miller, Jack the Flowerchild, Away111, LordAmaury, coned, Carol (the love of my life), and so many others. Again, thank you all so much and I truly hope you enjoy the new content. There is truly an infinite amount of replayability in this game now. But can YOU get to the Lost Pixel?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Letterkenny!
Join the hicks, skids, and hockey players and experience all of their problems in the World’s Most Popular game!

Buy a Hockey rink or team, your favorite Modean’s Pub or the Fishing Hole. Build shacks and barns on your properties. Try to avoid “Chorin’!” Find out who is the toughest guy in Letterkenny. Host a bush party, surf the dark web and win “back to back to back ships” in the local hockey championship!

Features special game tokens from the show including a beer bottle, truck, jeep, hockey skate, Shamrock and a German Shepherd dog – plus one mystery token you’ll find inside the box!

—description form the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Gnarlwood – Fearsome Fortress Rivals Deck
description: A Rivals deck of 32 cards usable by any warband. The deck consists of 12 objectives, 10 gambits and 10 upgrades all centered around a theme of defensive play and withstanding the attacks of your adversary.

Discordia Parades is a fan-expansion for Discordia.

It replaces the Germanic raids with new opportunities for bonuses, rather than penalties for not fulfilling the random strength requirements of the original game.

The new parade tiles that replaces the A/B/C/D-tiles of the original game are revealed at the same times as before, but instead give 1 or 2 opportunities to have enough strength to get a reward. In order of total player strength, each player chooses to perform in 0, 1 or 2 parades and claim their rewards. Ties are resolved in reverse player order (where the player that was the "first player" in round 5 being last).

This expansion makes the game easier to finish before the end of the last round as you are more likely to get an additional reward or two.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Dino Park! You have been chosen to enter the T-Rex enclosure. Don't worry, he's sedated... Or maybe not? Run away from the lizard and lure it toward your opponents to avoid becoming its snack!

—description from the publisher

Goal of the game is to be the first to reach the park gate, while avoid the attention of the T-Rex.

Player turn has two phases:

A. Gather information

You can ask other players a yes/no/number question (eg. Have I a swiss pocket knife?/How many meat cards do I have?)

B. Dump the weight

You must choose between three actions:


     Let's try this: play a card from your hand and resolve its effect
     Take this: give another player a card from your hand and resolve its effects
     Pull a "MacGyver": choose two cards and ask other player if you can combine them. If yes, discard them and resolve a Rock card action. Otherwise, shuffle your hand and end your turn.


If at the end of your turn the T-Rex is focused on you, you get devoured.
If at the end of your turn you have no cards in hand, you win, leaving the other players trapped inside the park.

The game also has a team mode, and a couple of game variants.

—user summary

boardgame: Volfyirion Guilds: Dice Tower 2022 Promo Cards
description:
A set of six Cards for Volfyirion Guilds. They include three additional Command cards and three additional Building cards.
The cards include:
-Call to Arms
-Vault Depletion
-Coral Fortress
-Future Investment
-Agate Palace
-Jade Tower

Released as a part of the Dice Tower 2022 kickstarter in promo pack Dive.

Your mission is to journey through outer space in your rocket ship trying to catch your opponents before they catch you! Be aware of black holes, they could lead to your undoing or they may be your flight path to victory!

Players roll a randomized number with the "ray gun" and moves the corresponding amount of spaces. If you land on another rocket ship, they're out! Be the first one to tag all your opponents!

boardgame: Cross Matc: A Competitive Immunohematology Learning System
description:
In this roll and move game, 2 to 4 players roll a d6 then move their tokens around a triangular board in a clockwise fashion, attempting to land on strategically marked positions where they can draw cards from one of two card decks (Patient's Serum or Laboratory decks). The first player to acquire all 8 units of the different blood types (3 Group O; 3 Group A; 1 Group B and 1 Group AB) from the Blood Bank is the winner. 
The Patient's Serum card deck represents different antibodies and are trivia questions related to the antibody. Incorrect responses result in an immediate lost of a Blood Bank card showing a blood group. The Laboratory deck gives "play immediately" cards that either penalizes common errors in technique or rewards good technique. The Blood Bank card deck provide the blood units that the players require to win the game.  Players who loss all their blood group cards are immediately eliminated from the game.
Produced and sold by Ortho Diagnostics in 1978 as a learning tool for technicians in immunohematology.

—user summary

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: Murder in the Dance Hall
description:
It's the 1940s, and one of the judges, Hank Hankinson, is found murdered just prior to the start of the annual Chicago Dance Competition!  The dancers and staff of the Talk of the Town Dance Hall must search for clues to bring the killer to justice.

The host and up to 12 friends participate in a thematic murder mystery where everyone plays a role and everyone is a suspect.  The game contains two different outcomes.

Demo game.
10 cards with an exclusive story.

Discover new worlds by exploring, card after card, all the places you can visit! Enjoy each place along the way and discover what is hidden at the end of each adventure!


     Choose the different places you wish to explore.
     Turn each card over one after another.
     Successfully reach your goal !


In short, Bubble Stories is solo narrative card game without text.

A brand new Taskmaster game with all-new tasks.

The Secret Series Game invites you and your family and friends to take part in your own series of Taskmaster games at home. Carry the scores from each individual game forward into the next until you complete 10 'episodes' to crown an overall Series Winner!

Who will be the ultimate champion?

—description from the publisher

Apocalypse: War Zone Damnos is an expansion to the 6th Edition release of Apocalypse. This book contains a detailed background of the Damnos Incident as well as battle reports, minitature showcases, 4 new Apocalypse missions, 9 new strategic assets, and 22 new Apocalypse datasheets detailing key formations used by the Ultramarines and Necrons.

It's the UNO matching card game everyone knows but featuring favorite characters from Encanto. Players still race to get rid of all their cards by matching color, number and character. When someone plays a special 'Wild Don't Talk' card, they choose a player who will not be allowed to talk (except to call "Uno") until Uno is called. And just like always, when a player is down to one card, they must shout, 'UNO!' Makes a great gift for card game lovers and Encanto fans ages 7 years and older. Colors and decorations may vary.

The classic UNO card game builds fun on game night with themes inspired by Encanto.
Each game features a deck decorated with character graphics from beloved show!
As in classic UNO game, players match colors, numbers and symbols to the card on top of the discard pile!
A special 'Wild Don't Talk' rule may force some player to not talk.
Makes a great gift for kid, teen, adult and family game nights with 2 to 10 players ages 7 years and older!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description: The game of Monopoly, set in Toronto.

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000 (Ninth Edition): Chapter Approved – Grand Tournament Mission Pack and Munitorum Field Manual 2020
description:
Chapter Approved: Grand Tournament 2020 is a Chapter Approved supplement for the 9th Edition of Warhammer 40,000.

This year’s edition of Chapter Approved is the essential gaming toolbox for all fans of Warhammer 40,000 matched play. Whether you're a tournament regular or simply like to ensure your battles are always fought on an even footing, this is the essential supplement for matched play games in the this edition of Warhammer 40,000. Inside this 96-page softback book, you'll find:

Two mission packs, comprising nine missions each for Incursion- and Strike Force-scale battles, each designed to offer a different challenge to the players.

Guidelines for setting up and playing a matched play or Grand Tournament-level competitive game, including suggested battlefield size, time limit and how best to determine secondary objectives.

This comes packaged with the Munitorum Field Manual which contains all the latest matched play points updates, painstakingly balanced and tuned in conjunction with major tournament organisers and our playtesters

This is a limited-time promotional item from KFC from December 2022.

Kentucky Duo features the same game play as its parent Spot it! but there are fewer symbols in the cards, so it follows that there are fewer cards as well.

Each card features six different KFC related symbols, with the symbols varying in size from one card to the next. Any two cards have exactly one symbol in common. For the basic Spot it! game, reveal one card, then another. Whoever spots the symbol in common on both cards gets rid of they first card, then another card is revealed for the player to search, and so on. Whoever has got rid of all their cards first, wins!

—user summary

Roll and write that tries to diver a dungeon crawler solo game experience that fits in the back of a business card.

The player tries to defeat a monster, fighting or running away each turn. But, as he fails the attack or decides to run, the stress increases since there are limited options to do this. Decisions can be hard, although the game is very simple. Push your luck mechanisms and dice rolling provide uncertainty and deliver simple and meaningful choices.

This is a free-to-play roll-and-write game. Print the file or draw the squares and play it immediately. The author developed this idea during a conference when waiting to deliver his communication. Because he had no card to give when he attended Essen Spiel 2022 as Press. An Adventure on the Card is an example of how analogue games can be applied to anything and anywhere.

—description from the designer

Whether in the big city or in the country, you'll find planting beds everywhere. What could be better than growing a plant from a letter, watching it grow, and then proudly looking at the words you've grown yourself?

Words Querbeet - the somewhat different dice game! Ideal for all those who love to fiddle with letters. Roll the three letter dice and enter at least one of the diced letters into your crossword grid. Whether vertically or horizontally - the goal is to put together as many valid words as possible. With a bit of luck, the joker is rolled and you have the free choice of letters.
Four exciting game variants are waiting to be discovered by you - and you can also garden alone. But now into the rubber boots and off into the bed!

The game principle:
The game is played with three letter cubes. In each round, at least one of the diced letters must be entered into the crossword grid on your own game sheet. Jokers allow tactical decisions. The player who assembles the most meaningful words vertically and horizontally wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Viva Quiz Vegas!: Quizzen ohne Wissen!
description:
Viva Quiz Vegas is a tricky multiple-choice quiz where you bet on right or wrong answers. If you use your cards skillfully, you can get points even if you are clueless. Are you drawing a blank? Then use your Thief card to steal points from other players. Lure the copycats with the bluff card on the wrong track or let their bet go up in smoke with the bomb! In this quiz, your knowledge is only worth something if you also play your hand correctly!
Play poker with bets on right and wrong answers.
500 creative multiple-choice questions test general knowledge.
Bets are placed face down - bluffing and nasty tricks guaranteed!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Medical Crime Files: Patientenakte – Julia H.: Unter Beobachtung
description:
How did the bizarre chain of events that occurred on March 22, 2022 in Kratworn near Hensfeld come about? Does the main suspect Julia Hiffner pose a danger to the public and how can she be helped? Julia H.'s crime has shocked Hensfeld, but she has not spoken to anyone since her arrest. Is the young woman sane? What happened to her? And how can she be helped? By sifting through various clues and evidence, you must reconstruct the bizarre chain of events and diagnose the patient's condition. As a team of forensic psychologists, can you figure out what's going on in Julia H.'s head?
The "Crime Files" now on the theme of forensics and psychology.
Investigate and find diagnoses with realistically designed circumstantial evidence.
Written with advice from a board-certified psychologist.
The game is reusable, no material needs to be destroyed.

—description from the publisher

Introducing a brand new way to play Superclub! Scout, sign, train and play matches with real players from all 18 Bundesliga clubs, either in combination with the original Superclub players or exclusively with the Bundesliga players you know and love.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Twin Stars: Moonset – Scenario #2: Protect the Village
description:
A new episodic game that reimplements Twin Stars.

Each episode includes a Scenary postcard and 2 cards.

A few big updates from Twin Stars. A lot mostly in the visual side. It uses cubes, and it uses them on the card instead of using the edges of the card for tracking. Characters have skills, store items and other things on their cards. You can also gain Hide or Hunt. With Hide, you can re-roll an alert die (which is an extra die, not a die on an alert symbol), and Hunt allows you to try for an extra star.


Scenario: Protect the Village

The village of Behyrst is being plagued by Addos the Vampire Princess, who has been using her bloodlust to reduce the already small village down to a small band of survivors. It's currently at a meager six, including you. But you can turn this around with some morale, support and defences. Her bite is fatal, and she's out for blood.

You need to successfully banish the Vampire Princess with three villagers to win before she kills too many more.

Characters:

     Machilo
     Unora-Vae

In this trivia game, the players will first go through eight randomly chosen categories, earning switch tokens and/or coloured dice. Then they will go through the same eight categories in a final round where correct answers will allow players to take away life points from the other players. Last player standing wins.

One of the most famous battles of the Zulu war, a small band of British defended against thousands of Zulu warriors. Watch for Zulu Snipers! Fall Back to gain strategic advantages! Hold the position to earn medals and respect in England. It is all here in this amazing, simple to learn game.

Background:

(1879) The Battle of Rorke's Drift, was an engagement in the Anglo-Zulu War. The successful British defense of the mission station of Rorke's Drift, began when a large contingent of Zulu warriors broke off from their main force at the day-long Battle of Isandlwana on 22 January 1879, diverting 6 miles (9.7 km) to attack the British, under the command of Lieutenants John Chard of the Royal Engineers and Gonville Bromhead, 24th Regiment of Foot at Rorke's Drift later that day and continuing into the following day. The massive but piecemeal attacks by the Zulu on Rorke's Drift came very close to defeating the much smaller garrison, but were consistently repelled. Just over 150 British and colonial troops defended the station against attacks by 3,000 to 4,000 Zulu warriors. Eleven Victoria Crosses were awarded among the defenders, along with a number of other decorations and honors.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Can you defend the Alamo against the overwhelming Mexican forces? Use your Crockett cards wisely to defeat the attackers! Hold the Alamo and become a hero of the Mexican war.

Background:

(February 23 – March 6, 1836) The Battle of the Alamo. This was a pivotal event in the Texas Revolution. Mexican troops under President General Antonio López de Santa Anna started a 13-day siege, reclaiming the Alamo Mission near San Antonio de Béxar (modern-day San Antonio, Texas, United States), Santa Anna's refusal to take prisoners during the battle, killing most of the occupants inside, inspired many Texians and Tejanos to join the Texian Army. Motivated by their written desire to preserve a border open to immigration, and a desire for revenge, the Texians defeated the Mexican Army at the Battle of San Jacinto, on April 21, 1836, ending the rebellion in favor of the newly formed Republic of Texas.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Commonly known as the Stand of the 300, Thermopylae was an amazing battle. The Persian Empire troops, led by Xerxes I, faced 300 Greek forces, led by the Spartans, led by Leonidas. Are you able to hold off the Persians? Are you able to defeat them before the flanking forces attack from the surrounding hills? Stand and become the object of legends!

Background:

(480 BC) The Battle of Thermopylae was fought between the Achaemenid Persian Empire under Xerxes I and an alliance of Greek city-states led by Sparta under Leonidas I.The engagement at Thermopylae occurred simultaneously with the Battle of Artemisium: between July and September 480 BC.  Lasting over the course of three days, it was one of the most prominent battles of both the second Persian invasion of Greece which, under Xerxes I was a delayed response to the failure of the first Persian invasion which had been initiated by Darius I and ended in 490 BC by an Athenian-led Greek victory at the Battle of Marathon.  Xerxes had amassed a massive land and naval force, and set out to conquer all of Greece. The Athenian politician and general Themistocles proposed that the allied Greeks block the advance of the Persian army at the pass of Thermopylae.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
大名 (Daimyo) is a two-player worker placement game from Japan.

Become a Daimyo in the Edo period, work with people in various positions over eight periods to enrich your country, and ultimately aim to become a Rōjū.

At the end of the game, the winner is the player who has earned the most Honor Points by acquiring territories, advancing technologies, and earning the general's trust by presenting artifacts.

江戸時代の「大名」となり、8つの期間に渡り様々な立場の人々と協力して自国を豊かにし、最終的に「老中」を目指します。
領地を獲得したり、技術を進めたり、将軍に工芸品を献上し信頼を得たりすることで、ゲーム終了時に最も多くの名誉点を獲得しているプレイヤーが勝者となります。
publisher's summary
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
publishers: Green Games (III) (グリーンゲームズ)

description:
モグッテ MOGUTTE is a hand-building game from Japan, with the theme of sea otters' ecology.

In this game, each player is a sea otter that aims to eat its fill of favorite foods! The otters predate on foods like clams, urchins, and crabs. These all have a hard shell, so you need to use the stone to break shells, in order to eat the prey. You can also find many of these preys that live on the seafloor by diving deeper, with the stone as a weight.

Orcas and sharks, the natural enemies of the sea otter, may be lurking offshore. Don't forget to wrap yourself in the kelp bed so you don't get swept offshore.

『MOGUTTE』は、ラッコの生態をテーマにした手札構築ゲームです。
このゲームでは、プレイヤーたちはラッコとなって、3種類しか持てない手札をやりくりしながら、カイやウニ、カニといった獲物をお腹いっぱい食べることを目指します。

これらの獲物を食べるには、獲物ごとに適した「石」で、彼らを覆う硬い殻を割る必要があります。
同じ獲物を重ねて食べればより多くの勝利点を得られますが、同じだけ重ねた石も必要になります。
石は、海底を模した山札を深くまで潜り、ほしいカードを手に入れるための「重り」としても役立ちます。

ゲーム中、ラッコの天敵であるシャチやサメが姿を現すことがあります。
彼らが近づいてきたら、「昆布のベッド」に巻きついて失点を逃れましょう。
publisher's summary

Mordsgaudi: In the Alps the two villages Obersteissbruch and Untersteissbruch do not like each other. Inbetween the two villages lies the Restaurant Jodelhoh with its charismatic owner Jodl Franz. One night six strangers appear and Jodl Franz gets quite nervous. Nobody can trust anybody and then somebody is gone missing. Has the mountain ghost gotten to him? Put on some warm cloths and go to investigate!

It's a 6 player murder mystery game were you can lie, betray, investigate and accuse! Included in the mix is a cook book to fully enjoy a perfect crime dinner evening.

boardgame: ア・デイ・イン・ザ・八戸 (A day in the Hachinohe)
description:
ア・デイ・イン・ザ・八戸 (A day in the Hachinohe) is a 'butting' card game (like Hols der Geier) from Japan, themed on a day trip to Hachinohe City in Aomori Prefecture in Japan. Hachinohe is in the far North East of Japan, and all the images in the game were taken by the designer on their trips there.

The game has the players spending the day going around the city, visiting the various tourist attractions and beauty spots. Sometimes they share a ride, sometimes they go alone, which gets them a more valued experience. At the end of the game, players accumulate their score from icons on the scenic cards and on their travel cards.

The main deck of scenic cards is laid out in a tableau of 8 columns, representing the 8 hours spent day tripping, with various scenic cards making each column. Players bid from a hand of 3 travel cards numbered 1 to 5, being the mode of travel: walking, bicycle, bus, train, and taxi. Players that chose a unique mode get to collect more scenic cards, but spend that card. If players spend travel cards too quickly, they could fail to collect anything in the later rounds.

At the end, players compare the icons on the scenic cards and on their travel cards to calculate their score.
user summary

青森県の八戸市での日帰り旅行をテーマにしたバッティングゲームです。ゲームは全部で8ラウンド。それに対して、手札は3枚。一人なら、得点となる八戸カードが取れます。誰かと移動手段が被ると、使った移動カードが得点になるかもしれません。最後、四種あるアイコンのうち二種類をかけて一番得点の高いプレイヤーが一番八戸を満喫したとして勝ちます。
publisher's summary
publishers: シュピールディスオーダー (Spieldisorder)

description:
文字禍ウント (Mojika unto) (Spell count) is an unusual word game from Japan that uses leaflets and flyers which would be thrown away. The game has an A4 plastic file sleeve with a print design (a very common item in Japan). The design has 5 clear windows of different shapes. The players insert an A4 sheet from a pamphlet, catalogue or flyer into the sleeve, and predict which window will show the most characters of one type. For example, which will show the most Katakana characters, or the most numbers. Players with the best or worst guesses score or lose points in the game.
user summary

A4サイズのクリアファイルにチラシを入れ、クリアファイルの枠から見える文字の数を予測して当てる、語彙力やスピードを問わないワードゲームです。
文房具×ボードゲームブランド「BUNGUGAMES」作品。
publisher's summary

A fun and simple matching game for children aged 2-6. Turn over welly cards to find matching pairs of wellies for your monsters. The first player to fill their board wins!

There are two ways to play this colourful game, for both younger and more advanced players. This means children of all ages can play and enjoy the game together, either as a simple pairs matching or more complex memory game. The patterned welly cards can also be used to encourage children to identify the objects they can see on them.

Featuring an array of colourful character illustrations, the quirky monsters are bound to get children giggling! Why not ask your child to pick their favourite monster?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ゾンビVSサメVS? (Zombie VS Shark VS?)
description:
ゾンビVSサメVS? (Zombie VS Shark VS?) is a card game from Japan, the third in a series of B-movie themed games. A big brawl battle between B-movie characters, zombies, sharks, aliens, mad scientists, all in unexpected developments.

Each round, players choose a card to play face-down in the brawl. When they are turned up, the highest card should win. But the characters have special powers too, which change events and even cause deaths.

Then, a fresh card is dealt off the deck as an Intruder, who could change the result of the battle again. A chaotic fight with unpredictable results.

手札からカードを伏せて出し一斉に公開します。最も数字の大きい人の勝ちです。2回先取です。
ただしカードには効果があります。カードの数字が変わったり、誰かが死亡したりします。
また、全員の効果を解決した後乱入者が現れます。山札の上のカードを1枚公開し、それが乱入者となります。
乱入者の効果を解決します。結末は予測不能です。
publisher's summary

イッパイ烏賊が？ (Ippai Ikaga?) is a party game about counting from Japan. Players can make connections between different objects, according to how they are counted in Japanese. For example, Soba noodles are 'pieces', and fields are 'pieces', so that's a match.

数え方でセットを作ろう！
日本語でちょっと変わった 「数え方」 をする物や行動のなかから、
同じ ”読み” のモノを探して当ててみよう！ というゲームです。

例：蕎麦（枚）・　畑（枚）　⇒　点数ゲット

「えぇ？そんな数え方もあるの！？」という日本語における数え方の面白さを体験しながら自然と覚えることが出来るパーティーゲームです。
publisher summary
A fast reaction game played simultaneouly where players try to flick a wooden stick to the middle of the wooden board while keeping their own and all other players' cards in mind.

Global War 1936-1945v4


     After more than 1000 hours of development over the last 4 years, Historical Board Gaming is proud to present Global War 1936 version 4.
     Listing the changes is an impossible task. Every component in the game and every sentence in the rules has been scrutinized. In many ways, version 4 is a totally new game compared to version 3.  
     We have done a lot of streamlining to make the game faster to play, and easier for new players to pick up. This includes removing things that did not work well or were unnecessarily complicated. 
     A tremendous amount has been added to the game: an all-new map, revised diplomacy, more balanced units, and enhanced technologies. Everything is now much more integrated. One of the biggest new additions are the “in port” rules, which will transform the game in many ways.
     In any 3-alliance system, the rules open up possibilities to gaming the system. Although a lot has been done to reduce/eliminate this in version 4, Global War has been designed to be played like gentlemen. As such, it is our hope that players aspire to this, and “play the game”, instead of “playing the rules”. The former is a lot more fun!
     Printed on new 13oz vinyl.
     Improved Jungle and Tundra terrain.
     Sea Floor Topography.
     Play aids such as Monroe Doctrine, kamikaze, Objectives, etc printed on the map.
     Sea Zones numbering (Atlantic A1. A2, A3..., Pacific P1, P2..., Indian Ocean and the Mediterranean.)


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Car Wars: Road Sections (道路セクション)
description: Car Wars: Road Sections (道路セクション) is an expansion for Car Wars that features 4 straight road sections, 16 curves, 2 on ramps, and 2 intersections, plus familiar features such as oil puddles, smoke bombs, mines, and spikes. Additionally, this expansion includes 124 tokens, such as potholes, 16 barricades in two types and 8 grenades, as well as a booklet containing sample layouts of arenas/circuits and mini-scenarios.

You only have one job, so don't mess it up! Climb up the corporate ladder by memorizing the fun sound effects and gestures for your task and those of the other players while keeping the rhythm going. If you make three misses, you will be fired. The last worker standing wins the game!

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Las Vegas, Saint Petersburg & Taiwan
description:
(Las Vegas) Money, money, money makes you funny, Honey...

(Saint Petersburg) Winter... Snow... Ice... Water... Will you be able to use Nature's effects on the environment to make the most profit?

(Taiwan) Thanks to the High-Speed Train, Taiwan has developed a great railroad network and now transports more than 2 million people a day per train!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Miami 2030 & New York Subway
description:
(Miami 2030) It's 2030 and water is still devastating the city! Will you manage to save all the people from the map before the end of the game?

(New York Subway) I am fascinated by the big cities on Earth. The verticality and underground below the high skyscrapers. This is my attempt at modelling them for Age of Steam.

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: On the Night Train
description: "All aboard! The crew welcomes you onto the European Belle for an evening of timeless luxury and terror." The train goes dark as it enters a tunnel. Was that a scream from the dining carriage? Take a seat and upgrade your next dinner party or family games night and host your own murder mystery evening. Dress up in character and put your magnifying glass to work as you and your guests attempt to work out whodunnit?

Learn how to say “hello” in over 50 languages with this brightly coloured bingo game!

Discover an array of global greetings, from the familiar to the unusual, and test your linguistic abilities as you play the fast-moving and beautifully illustrated Hello World Bingo.

A perfect family game for up to 9 players.

Crosschecking is the most rough boardgame ever. In every championship you play, you can face up friends and foes through the exciting experience of a hockey match full of unexpected events. The only thing that matters is the Prestigio Cup.

Use your sense of position and your strategic skills to leverage the right cards and sink your opponents. Build up the final roster and win the league.
Your team will consist of one Captain, one Goalie, and four skaters. Bear attention to the skills of your team members, they could be the difference between win and defeat.

—description from the designer

お嬢様人狼 (Ojousama Jinrou) is a Werewolf style hidden identity game from Japan.

The players are young ladies at a young lady's school. They learn that a Kansai man has been reincarnated and slipped into their noble tea party. Each player aims to find the old man while role-playing as the young lady according to the Lady card and Attribute card they have been dealt.

During the tea party, the players arrange their discussion to try to expose (or conceal) the hidden old man. When the tea party ends, a vote is held, and if the old man is banished, the young ladies win.

Each Lady has a particular role-play attribute (their personality), and it is necessary to role-play accordingly.

「お嬢様」たちの中に紛れ込んだ「関西人のおっさん」を見つけ出す招待隠匿系バカゲー。
プレイヤーはお嬢様学園のお嬢様として、高貴なお茶会に紛れ込んだ、転生してお嬢様になって紛れ込んでいる関西人のおっさんを見つけ出す人狼風パーティーゲームです。
各プレイヤーは、手元に配られた「お嬢様カード」と「属性カード」に従って、お嬢様のロールプレイをしながら、「おっさん」を見つけ出すことを目指します。
お茶会（議論）ののちに投票を行い、おっさんを追放したらお嬢様たちの勝利です。
それぞれの「お嬢様」はロールプレイ上の属性（個性）があり、それに従ってロールプレイする必要があります。
Themed on the popular Swedish TV show Antikrundan. Players bid on antiques, trying to estimate their true value.

Space Infantry: Zero G is a solitaire game of combat in the future set in the world of Space Infantry: the Player will control the soldiers of the human race against the endless enemies lurking in the universe.

The game has been designed to have Zero setup, Zero space required (you only need one A4/Letter sheet) and simple rules, but capable of providing enough challenge and obviously fun. Perfect as a filler or when traveling, the game include nine battles with a progressive complexity (read: challenge), each battle playable in 10 to 20 minutes.

—description from the designer

description:
どっちぼーい (DOCCHI BOY) is a communication party game from Japan. One player is the leader and answers questions (for example, "Have you ever been hit by bird droppings?", "What do you like about the deep sea and space?"). Players try to understand how the leader will respond, whether they will say Yes or No.

The set of 15 question cards for the round are laid out in a pyramid, 1 to 5 wide, and each card points down to the left or right, Yes/No. Players bet on which way the leader will answer and progress down the pyramid. The more they understand about the leader, the easier they can win bets and risk betting higher stakes.

Once all the players have been leader, the winner is the player with the most money, being the best at understanding other people.

Y/Nの質問チャートで遊ぶギャンブルっぽいパーティゲームです。
レースの基準となる｢親｣を決め、5段計15問のピラミッドを作ります。
子は親がどこのカードに乗るのか。はたまた、その最後の質問の答えはYESかNOのどっちなのかを予想し、親は予想を当ててくれそうな人に賭けます。
予想が終わったら出走。YESなら左、NOなら右に落ちていきます。
予想した場所に親が着地したら、大当たり！倍率がかかってゲーム内の通貨が戻ってきます！
最後にチップを一番多く持っていた人が勝利します。

boardgame: 妄想リストランテ (Delusion Restaurante)
description:
妄想リストランテ (Mousou Risutorante) (Delusion Restaurante) is a party game from Japan. Players are part of an editorial meeting for the gourmet magazine 'Delusion Restaurante'. The editor wants to know the next eatery to be featured in the magazine. You have to come up with a new restaurant on the spot and pitch it to the editor.

The game has decks of cards, some with names that can be combined to form a new restaurant, some with special themes for a restaurant. Players are dealt some cards and then think up how the restaurant would be. What is it called? What food does it serve? Who are the clientele?

Each player then has time to pitch their discovery to the meeting. Once everybody has made their pitch, they all choose their favourite simultaneously. The one chosen by the most players goes into the feature and the player gets a Delusion star. Most stars wins after three rounds.

手札の店名カードを並べてお題にぴったりなお店の名前を妄想する、グルメ系大喜利パーティーゲーム。妄想した店舗名を順に紹介し、グルメ雑誌の編集部になりきってお店の名前やどんな特徴があるのかをプレゼンします。すべてのお店を紹介し終えたら、お題にぴったりなお店を選んで、グルメ企画に載せる店を決めます。全員で一斉に特集したいと思うお店を指差しましょう。投票数の一番多かったプレイヤーは妄想スターを１つもらえます。1~5を３回繰り返して、最もスター獲得が多かったプレイヤーの勝利です。

Curling Dice!: Just 10 is a two-player dice action game inspired by the Finnish sport [gamedid=186279 Molkky].

Players take turns flipping dice onto the board scoring points by knocking down monsters. The player that scores exactly 10 points from this wins!

The designer has produced other games under the 'Curling Dice' brand, each with different modulations.

boardgame: Da ist Der Wurm Drin: Das Kartenspiel
description:
Each player gets a worm head and place the worm heads next to one another at the same level.
Each player takes 3 worm sections cards in his hand. Then place 6 worm section cards in the middle of the table and the remaining cards form the face-down draw pile.
On his turn, each player puts a card from your hand face down next to the 6 cards in the middle of the table. After that, takes a different card from there, reveal it and add it as a worm section to your worm.
When all players have added their ninth worm section, the game ends. The player who has the longest worm wins.

In Lucha Kings you control a team of wrestlers in search of glory. 
Each turn you will take the tokens from a tile and move them in a mancala style, after this if you fulfill the requirements you'll take a token from each tile you just left one and the tokens of a chosen color from the last tile, as well as any empty tile.
Each tile and token fo your color gives you 2 points at the end of the game and watch tile and token not of your color gives you 1 point. The player with the most points wins the game.

En Lucha Kings tú controlas un equipo de luchadores en busca de la gloria. 
Necesitarás todas tus habilidades tácticas para recuperar a tus luchadores, convencer a uno que otro de otros colores de unirse a tu equipo y vencer a los demás.

Borix is a game that takes place in a small village haunted by werewolves. Each player is secretly assigned a role: Wolf, Gofrix, Fairy, etc
A narrator is needed as well, the Moon player covers this role. 
The game alternates between the night and day phases. During the night the wolves kill a Gofrix. During the day the players still alive will deliberate and vote to get rid of the player they think is a wolf.
This is a social deduction game, either the wolves win by eating all Gofrix or the Gofrix win by getting rid of the wolves.



En el reino de Borix suuceden asesinatos espontáneos cada noche, los Gofrix tienen sus poblados en los cuales intentan resolver día con día queines son los causantes de estos sucesos, antes que los hombres los erradiquen por completo, desafortunadamente estos se reproducen rápidamente y podrían dominar la localidad hasta extenderse a todo el reino.

A million likes, comics, magic dice and an android invasion in the classrooms that will have to be stopped?

To win more likes on the interactive platform of the new trendy series "PUNCH and KiSS" you have to look like the main characters.

But this obsessive quest for popularity has gotten out of hand for most of the students and they have become a kind of zombie-humanoids with catastrophic consequences: they behave according to the dictates and models of fiction and harass anyone who still retains their difference?

4 WAYS TO PLAY!

Las Aventuras de Tueli is an award-winning and critically acclaimed trilogy of children's literature made by a teacher that wanted to create exercises, so his student could prove their reading comprehension. Now you will have the opportunity to create your own adventures by playing this fast-paced card game with the whole family.

Each turn, players can play up to three cards from their hands, to earn adventure points,send threats to the other players, or use cards with special effects.

The winner is the first player who gets to 25 adventure points.

—description from the designer

Completion is a complete-the-quote Bible game with three suggested playstyles. The box even suggests using the game cards to put on a Church Pageant play.

The first playstyle involves one player reading the prompt cards aloud to a group of players. In this version, the player who correctly completes the Bible quote receives the card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

The second playstyle involves one player reading the prompt cards and two teams of players competing much like a team spelling bee. The player reading the cards will read to one team, and that team will continue to be asked prompt cards until a teammate misses the answer. After giving an incorrect answer, that player drops out of play, and the reader begins reading to the other team. This continues until only one team remains or the cards are all read. The team with the most players remaining wins.

Another playstyle involves all the cards being distributed equally to players. Players go around clockwise asking the player to their left to finish the quote. If someone answers correctly, they receive the card. If someone fails to answer correctly, the asker is allowed to steal a card from them. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

—user summary

オルターネオ (ALTER-NEO) is a card game from Japan, revised from an earlier game called Alter. The publisher notes the new game has better card production, which you cannot see through.

In this new game, the players try to affect the future of the round, and achieve bonus scores or an outright win. The game rules are for four players, with variants for two or three.

Each player gets a Pick card in their colour, and an aide showing the various Bonus point combinations they can win.

The game has a deck of 16 scoring cards numbered 0-3. Players will try to pick a score card each round, but risk being bumped on to the next card. A dealer is selected (the player who has recently altered the future). The dealer shuffles the draw deck, and turns up four cards so all can see. These are then shuffled and placed randomly face-down around the draw deck. The dealer placers their Pick card on one at random.

The players take turns going clockwise. They can either Watch - look at an uncovered card and if they wish, place their Pick on a card they have Watched - or they can Change - place their Pick on a covered card, which bumps that player's Pick onto the next card, and so on.

The player who goes last gets to Watch the remaining uncovered card if they want, and can place their Pick card there or choose to Change, and place on a covered card, and force the other Pick cards to move on.

Once the four Pick cards are down, the players collect their Score cards and Pick cards, and begin a new round by passing the Dealer to the next player.

Players try to accumulate four Score cards that will trigger various Bonus points at the game end. Players can check the value of their Score cards at any time.

When the deck is exhausted and all score cards collected, players total up the base points on their Score cards, and any Bonuses. For example, by collecting 0011, or 1111, a player gets 5 points.

If a player manages to get 0033, they automatically win. otherwise, most points wins.

The game was published in three different coloured plastic boxes, but the contents in each were the same.

English rules are available online.
user summary

In this roll and write game for 1-4 players, planners are tasked with creating routes around the city that net them the most income. Use dice to draw paths through the city, and also to gain access to shops. At the end of 12 rounds, the player with the most money is the winner!

Pencil Planner  is a print and play game designed by Wayne Koenig and published by Koenig Kreations. It is designed to be played with one sheet of paper and pencil per player, and 2 D6 dice. Whether you are playing this by yourself to get the highest score, or playing with friends we hope that the gameplay with keep bringing you back for more.

—description from the designer

Trivia game where teams take turns to preview the possible answers to questions and choose how many points to offer to each team.

Fixed number of round with a closed economy of points on offer.

Big disaster in town today! Trains and buses are not running. The only saving grace for the locals are taxis. Which of the taxi drivers will turn out to be the best and deliver their passengers first?

Roll the dice and move your taxi to the randomly drawn space. If this location is one of your three objectives, place an objective token there. If you place all of them, you win!
family: Series: Taxi Board Game (Taxi Game Ltd)

boardgame: せーの! おやつゲット! (Seno! Oyatsu Get!)
description:
せーの! おやつゲット! (Seno! Oyatsu Get!) is a card game from Japan. Players are taking shares of sweets on the table. But if they cannot divide them up, they get nothing.

Each round, some different sweet cards are dealt face up on the table, and sorted into matching types. Players then simultaneously call out Seno! and touch the group of sweets they want.

If only one player points at a set, they collect all the sweets there. But if multiple players choose the same sweet, they can only be shared out if none are left over.

The game ends when the deck is exhausted and the player with the most sweets wins.

description: A spinning, sneezing panda blows leaves and petals all over the place. Players race to get the petals back in the pandas basket before the music stops, playing either co-operatively to get all the petals or competitively to get all of their own coloured petals to win.

description: Each round you select an object, then you spin for a unit of measurement. Once the measurement has been selected you have to turn your back to the selected object and guess how big it is in the unit of measurement. Units of measurement include gummy bears, Wiener dogs, basketballs, playing cards, and several others.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Core Set 2019 Planeswalker Deck: Ajani, Wise Counselor
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

A veteran warrior of unmatched skill, Ajani protects the weak and mentors aspiring heroes. Gather battalions of small fighters, bolster their strength, and overwhelm your opponent.

—description from publisher

A reimplementation of the Game of the Goose, themed after Peacocks going to a fancy party. The only gameplay-related change is the placement of the hazard spaces: these are shifted around such that it is not possible to land on the go-back-to-start space on the first throw.

boardgame: Nova: Conflict in the last frontier
description:
NOVA is a card-based 4x space strategy game in which players lead rival space civilizations to achieve supremacy in a region of space. To do so, they must employ different strategies.

The game features an original card game system with three decks (military, civilian and diplomatic) that allows players to customize their style of play. The rules cover all the typical aspects of a 4x game: exploration, expansion, colonization, technological advances, diplomacy ... and include a solo game system that is also integrated into multiplayer games.

Although the mechanics are card-based, it has wargame elements and, above all, many ideas that make it unique not only in its genre. The rules for playing solo games are well integrated into the multiplayer scenarios. They are combined in a way that creates a lot of variability within the same scenario.

The scenario system is also elastic, even allowing players to design their own, set in their favorite Sci-Fi universes. This edition provides four solo scenarios (one of which is introductory), one two-player scenario, and a sixth 3 - 4 player scenario.

—description from the designer

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. The journey begins and ends in Berlin. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player back in Berlin wins the game.

boardgame: Pattie et la Colère de Poséidon
description:
Pattie et la Colère de Poséidon ("Pattie and the Wrath of Poseidon") is a cooperative game. Together you will try to appease the Wrath of Poseidon by completing 4 missions without being slowed down by the Cyclops! If you manage to free Jason from prison, reprogram Nazar the robot, confront the Hydra and forge the Trident, you win the game. On the other hand, if the Cyclops manage to slow you down and you don't manage to accomplish these 4 missions before you run out of cards, you lose the game.

The Pony Meadow is a fun, developmental board game.
During the game, participants fill a pasture of 5x5 fields with ponies and hay bales.
The game involves playing out sets of cards from the cards in hand, with the construction of a fence being mandatory. If the fence comes around before the players fill it with ponies, the players lose, if they manage to fill 5x5 fields before the fence comes around, the players win.

A Pónilegelő egy különleges, vidám, szórakoztató és fejlesztő társasjáték, amely az együttműködésre tanít, a játékosoknak közösen kell megtölteniük a legelőt pónikkal és szénabálákkal, mielőtt a kerítés bezárulna a rét körül. A játékosok csak együtt nyerhetnek, ezért a lehető legügyesebb módon kell segíteniük egymásnak, hogy kedvenc lovacskáik szabadon barangolhassanak a mezőn. A játék során a résztvevők egy 5x5 mezőből álló legelőt töltenek meg pónikkal és szénabálákkal, miközben versenyt kell futniuk az idővel, mert a rét körül folyamatosan épül egy kerítés. Minden egyes parti más, mert a pónik, a szénabálák és a kerítés elemei véletlenszerűen követik egymást, ráadásul a pónik letételéhez a résztvevőknek össze kell dolgozniuk. Nagyon fontos, hogy ebben a játékban a gyerekek csak közösen tudnak nyerni, így nem kell egymást legyőzniük! A játékot könnyen magunkkal vihetjük bárhová, így a család és a barátok bármikor összeülhetnek egy kis legeltetésre. A Pónilegelő csapatjáték, ahol a játékosok közösen küzdenek az idő és a balszerencse ellen. Az együttműködés fontosságára tanít, és arra, hogy csak a közösen hozott döntések lehetnek eredményesek. Fejleszti a problémamegoldó készséget, a kommunikációs képességeket és a logikát is.

boardgame: World of Warcraft: Miniatures Game Spoils of War Booster
description:
Spoils of War is the second set in the World of Warcraft Miniatures Game, a premium collectible miniatures game based upon the award-winning massively multiplayer online role playing game, World of Warcraft.

It consists of 51 figures and 70 cards.

Boldness, intuition and strategy are essential in this game of pure bluff. The goal is to be able to pass cards by bluffing their value, without making a mistake or being caught by your opponents. Whoever cleverly keeps the most chips at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: お前の罪は何色だ！ (Omae no Tsumi ha Naniiro da!)
description:
お前の罪は何色だ！ (Omae no Tsumi ha Naniiro da!) is a card game from Japan, themed on the Seven Deadly Sins. The sins on the cards are colour coded, giving the game title as What Colour Is Your Sin?.

Players are trying to build up colour sets of cards of the Mortal Sins, taking from a tableau or the draw deck, and then pay them down to take the Deadly Sins. When a player has enough Deadly Sins, they win.

But the Mortal and Deadly Sin cards also carry special effects, which make life easier for the player, or harder for the other players. For example, Gluttony or Greed let players draw more cards. Securing the Deadly Sin card Pride means the other players cannot draw from the deck, forcing them to take cards they don't want from the tableau. The sin Wrath reduces their hand limit from 7 to 6 cards.

boardgame: David Walliams Gangsta Granny Stash The Swag Board Game
description:
Based of David Walliams best-selling book 'Gangsta Granny', race around the board collecting jewels and be the first ‘Granny and Ben’ team to find and stash the Crown Jewels without being caught by the Queen!

Once you've collected a jewel, turn it over to reveal if it's one of your 'gangsta' coloured letters, stash the swag and race home. But the Queen is always on patrol, so don't let HM catch you or back to the start you go!

For 2 to 4 players, the first to spell out GANGSTA in your colours wins. Put your cunning skills to the test...you Gangsta!

お前の罪は何色だ！拡張セット (Omae no Tsumi ha Naniiro da! Expansion Set) is a small expansion pack for the original game Omae no Tsumi ha Naniiro da! .

boardgame: Matchmaking: The Jane Austen Memory Game
description:
PUT YOUR MEMORY SKILLS TO THE TEST as you pair up Austen's most famous couples.

SIMPLE GAME PLAY that everyone will understand and enjoy
THE PERFECT GIFT for fans of Jane Austen.

LEARN MORE ABOUT THE CHARACTERS and their relationships in the accompanying booklet.

It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune must be in want of a wife'! Help Jane Austen's most eligible characters find their perfect partners in this matchmaking memory game. With all the cards face down, you must turn over two at a time in hopes of pairing up Elizabeth Bennet and Mr Darcy, Emma Woodhouse and Mr Knightley, and many more couples besides. Put your literary knowledge and memory skills to the test as you piece together romantic courtships, rash, ill-advised matches, long, comfortable marriages and everything in between.

Vedur is an action selection deck-builder game for 2 – 4 players, ages 12 years and older. There are two versions of the game, the Basic game and the Advanced game. The playing time is about 10 minutes per player in the basic game, 20 minutes per player in the advanced game.

In Vedur, players take on the role of one of the 4 village Seers, each having their own way to foresee and accumulate their visions of the future in order to reveal their weather prophecies.

They visit their village townsfolk to search for omens that they can then use to predict the weather in the most powerful and reliable way through ancient omen-lore, rituals and spirit-talking. They can even open up a portal to their preferred spirit realm, seeking aid directly from the elemental spirits.

When the King is content, he will call the most accurate Seer to the court to become his new High-seer.

May the most resourceful Seer prevail!

A part of Gamia games Nordic Herritage collection, along with Runir and Sagnir (previously known as Mythical Island).

アオイトリ (Aoitori) is a set collecting game from Japan. Aoitori is the Bluebird, and the game is themed on the bluebird of happiness. Players are trying to collect chips showing happy things (Family 4pts or Cake 3pts) and avoid the negative (Death -1pt & War -2pt), and also to get the most Bluebirds too.

The game has a deck of cards, showing a birdcage on one face, and on the other, either a bluebird or a blank space. The deck is shuffled, and groups of six cards are dealt face-down to form birdcages, one cage for each player.

Then the chips are shuffled face-down and one chip put on each card. These are turned face-up.

Players take turns taking a chip of their choice from a cage, and then turning the card over to reveal either a bluebird or a blank space.

If a birdcage has at least one card turned over, a player can take the whole birdcage home, but this puts them out of the round.

Once each player has taken a birdcage (the set of six cards), the round is scored. Players get points for having the majority in each type of chip (lose points for the negative chips), and for having got bluebirds.

After playing some rounds, the player with the most points wins.

description:
ドラ猫 (Doraneko) is a deck-building game from Japan, where herds of cats explore an island. Players draft cards into their hand and then play them to expand the map of the island. The cards have effects or themes, which the players try to complete to earn treasures.

When adding cards to the map, they must follow the rules, connecting roads, and always keep a connection to their cat on the road. But they can choose whether to increase the number of cats in the herd, or aim for better road cards. At the end of the game, players score for the number of cats on the island.

The deck has over 100 cards to explore and was designed by a champion Dominion player.

Harklaad is an area control game in which the areas are also action spaces of a rondel.

In this game, you summon fluffy hluups and cast various spells, in order to control islands full of gems.
What’s unique about the game is that your actions are decided by your opponent, and you decide theirs.
Also, the value of each island for you is secretly decided by your opponent (and vice versa).
So be careful not to try too hard to control worthless islands!

◆Features
Rondel Control: Spaces of rondels are the areas you try to control.
By using the rondel / areas, you not only select an action, but also which area to execute it.

Double Modular Rondels: The actions on each area / rondel space is different for each player. This is represented as 2 separate player-rondels.
Actions (= spell tiles) on each rondel goes to the opposite rondel after casting them. (I.e. the action spaces move back and forth between 2 rondels).

Mutual Action Selection: You choose your opponent’s spells, and they choose yours.

Secret Value of Areas (deduction and bluff): The value of each area is different for each player. You decide the value for your opponent, and they decide that for you

Collect, Attack, Take Naps!
Who doesn't love The Child? Featuring everyone's favourite character from popular series The Mandalorian, this officially licensed Star Wars game is great fun for all the family.
Collect Baby Yoda's favourite things in this cute yet cunning card game.

From tasty soup to delicious frogs, use your Force powers to get ahead and collect the most cards to win!

Players take it in turns to try and build up their stash of 'cute loot', or use one of three action cards to attempt to outwit their opponents. The first player to complete a set wins the game.

Players race against the clock to complete challenges while their partner's chin rests on the Hydro Pump.  If the player can't complete the challenge and hit the red button in time, their partner gets sprayed in the face with water.  The game contains 200 different challenges on 100 double-sided cards.

Wizards and dragons abound, it’s a fantasy take-over! Elevate the role-playing experience using the D20, epilogue, coins, bestiary, and more in this epic adventure.

An introduction to role-playing games where you use your 
skills, tools, and imagination to devise a foolproof story to save the day!

The first victim is the person who lives the most dangerously. Each of the other players is called a rescuer. The victim draws and reveals a danger card containing the perilous situation they are caught in. The rescuers then place one skill card and one tool card from their hands which they will utilize to tell a story of how they will save the victim. While a Rescuerer presents their rescue story another can play a plot twist on their cards tripping them up and changing the story for more engaging play. the Victim awards the Danger card to the Rescuer with the best Rescue.

Cards are written for embellishment and lateral thinking. "Ph.D. in Dwarfish customs" well, what kind of custom?! Beard growing, axe throwing, anger managing, earth mining?

A 20-sided die is included. Whenever there is a prompt to roll you'll use this to find out what happens! Match what you rolled to the given prompts in order to reveal your fate.

A bestiary that documents the characters and critters of these worlds is also included. This also includes other optional ways to play to deepen the role-playing experience!

description:
Just like their owners, cats across America are ready to leave the house and end quarantine. Protect your cats (or don't) with your arsenal of PPE. Roll the die as your cats re-explore events and destinations. Fully protect your kitties and they'll enjoy tomorrow; fall short with your protections and find yourself with a thin (and ill) litter.

Game contents: 50 state cat cards, 50 defense cards, 16 social cards, 10 event cards and one standard die.

On your turn you'll draw a card from the deck and play it. It may be a mask or vaccine that goes into your hand for later defenses, it may be a requirement to send one of your cats to a social event in the middle of the table, or it may be a start of a social event for all cats in the middle of the table already.

If it's a social event and you hat cats in the social space from previous draws, you'll have an opportunity to give them masks or vaccines to protect them or to play a virtual defense card to have them attend virtually and bypass the event for your cats altogether.

Once everyone has defended their cats to the best of their ability you'll roll a die and determine the threat value. Any cats appropriately protected will survive and return to your home while any cats who didn't have enough masks or weren't vaccinated for the event will be discarded.

The winner is whoever has the last cat(s) standing.

description:

This expansion adds House Ecaz and House Moritani, to DUNE: The Board Game.

description:
ラビット・イン・ユー (Rabbit in you) is a social deduction party game from Japan. Players take turns with topics, and consider how much they like something on the topic. They choose a rabbit card in secret that is numbered 1 to 5. The other players take turns asking one question each, to deduce how the person feels. Then they vote using their fingers to show which card was chosen, 1 to 5.

The players who got it right score points, but if all the players guessed right, the leader gets no points. The most points wins, but the game is aiming to introduce people socially rather than be the highest score.

俺の鍋 (Ore no nabe) is a card game from japan, with players putting together special meals. Nabe is the Japanese version of a hotpot, so the game is My Hotpot.

Each player is dealt a Nabe card and a Dashi card (Dashi being the base stock used in much cooking in Japan.) Players then start drawing ingredient cards and try to form a new dish.

The Nabe has a certain capacity shown by icons, and the dashi has certain characteristics that must be matched. Players need to combine new ingredients that fit the Dashi, but do not go bust on the Nabe card.

Once everyone has formed their new recipt, they are all turned up to be inspected. Players discuss what a meal might be like. The meal with the largest gap left in the Nabe is eliminated (the chef didn't try to fulfil the meal). Then there is a voting round to select the best recipe. The recipe most most attractive wins, and players try another round.

皆の鍋 (Mina no Nabe) is an expansion set for Ore no Nabe, and adds 40 new cards to the game, and a dark hotpot for stranger recipes.
user summary

Start
Deal four hands of 13 cards, one hand to each of three players and one face down to the table. Turn the extra hand face up and spread it out in a row, overlapping the cards slightly so that all are identifiable.

Object
Count how many row cards are red and how many black. Whichever colour there are more cards of is the 'good' colour, the other 'bad'. The aim is to capture cards of the good colour and avoid those of the bad.

Play
The player at dealer's left leads to the first of 13 tricks. You must follow suit if you can, but may otherwise play any card. The trick is taken by the highest card played, regardless of suit. For this purpose cards rank from high to low AKQJ1098765432. Of equally high cards, the last played wins. Upon winning a trick, you take the current top card of the row (the one at the uncovered end), lay it face up before you, discard the trick face down to a wastepile, and lead to the next.

Score
When all tricks have been played and row cards taken, you total the face value of all cards you have taken of the good colour and subtract the total face value of those of the bad colour. For this purpose Aces count 1 each, numerals 2 to 10 count at face value, Jack counts 11, Queen 12, and King 13. If your count is positive, you record it as your score; if negative, you score zero for that round. (Thus you never actually score a minus number.) Play up to any previously agreed target.

ヒポカンコール (Hipocan CALL) is a push-your-luck memory game from Japan.

The game has a deck of cards with a white side and a black side. Each side has an image of some animal and a number, one or two digits. Some cards are dealt up to the table.

A player declares a target they will reach and start by selecting the first card, and then turning it over and adding the value together. They can choose whether to go on or stop there. If they go on, they add the next card and its flipside too. If they go bust, they are out of the round.

If the player chooses to stop, another player can decide they will continue the run, at the risk of going bust. The last player standing wins.

The game relies on a certain amount of memory of the values on the flipsides of the cards, by tying the numbers to the animals in the players' minds.
user summary

Bloox is a two-player trick-taking game in which cards in your hands has no colors, until you paint them by playing them.
Unlike many other trick-takers, or card games with extra board elements, Bloox is a full board game in which you play cards directly on the board and their positions decide who wins.
The game features unique “you must sandwich” system, which gives this “every card is wild” game a robust structure, and allows you to pull off clever tricks.

Other unique features, such as double-edged sword win-con, unfinished cards, harmony cards, value of colors, are explained in the official page.

Xerz is a two-player abstract strategy game, in which players move pawns in order to trap a pawn. 
The twist is, players share all the pawns, so trying to trap a pawn willy-nilly can backfire!

◆Features
・A unique hunting game where players share all the hunters / prey.
・The pawn to move is decided by your opponent.
・Pillars allow dynamic maneuvers.
・Pillars create a unique maze, make the game finite and drawless, and make the early- and the mid-game meaningful.
・Elegant gameplay. The only thing you do is to move a pawn chosen on the previous turn.

シルとり (Shirutori) is a communication game from Japan. The game has a deck of tiles showing silhouettes of various objects or ideas, or showing numbers or colours and so on.

Three tiles are dealt out and a player must come up with a good idea or word or sound that combines the three things shown. The other players vote on whether it is successful or not.

Players then go further and try to establish more elaborate ideas. For example, instead of just saying bag, or shopping bag, can you show it says Louis Vuitton bag?

The player getting the most votes by the end is the winner.

boardgame: 魔導士のエレコレ (Wizard's Element Collection)
description:
魔導士のエレコレ (Wizard's Element Collection) is a roll and move, set collection game from Japan. Wizards move around the edge of the Elemental field, and try to capture elements to make even pairs.

The game has a deck of 68 Elements in 12 different types (such as Heart, Wind, Earth, Brass...). These are shuffled and a 3x3 grid is dealt out. A set of 16 Mass discs are placed around the grid, in line with all the rows, columns and diagonals. The players move their wizards around the discs using dice rolls, and point along the line they want to collect from.

Where wizards want to take the same card from the grid, the priority is based on the Mass disc they stand on. But the Elements carry special effects, and cards already taken by a Wizard can be used to strengthen their priority.

Wizards are trying to build an even collection to gain positive scores. Odd sets will lose points.

As cards are taken from the grid, they are replaced until the deck is exhausted. The wizard with the highest score is the winner.

boardgame: Hello Kitty and Friends: A Lotería Game

boardgame: Battles in the East 3: Operation Sonnewende/Pomerania and Drive to the Sea
description:
Battles in the East (BITE) is a series simulating World War II Eastern Front battles. The system rules are an evolution of the SPI classics Panzergruppe Guderian, Army Group South, and Cobra. Unit integrity bonuses include Soviet Corps and vary to show the increasing Soviet cohesion and decreasing German cohesion as the war progresses. HQ units provide higher level combat support and supply advantages to corresponding units in range. BITE also incorporates standardized scales (unit size, distance, time) for easy comparisons. While the series rules allow players to play many games with the same core rules, the scenario rules add unique situational elements of each battle. Each volume includes two battles from one year with two copies of the charts and tables as well as scenario rules and set-up cards for easy reference.

Drive to the Sea
West Prussia, January 1945: Stalin’s agenda focused on the conquest of Prussia, the first German territory reached by the advancing Red Army. The German Army, ahead of a total collapse, prepared a ‘defense in depth’ while deploying many Prussian recruited units to defend the region. The Soviet plan was simple—drive from the east while also cutting Prussia from the rest of Germany. The Soviets earmarked Rokossovsky’s 2 Belorussian Front for the task with the veteran 5th Guard Tank Army selected for the drive to the sea. The game runs from 13th January 1945–1st February 1945, a total of 10 game turns.

Battle for Pomerania
Pomerania, February 1945: By February 1945 the German Army faced a desperate situation. The Soviets under Zhukov regrouped at the Oder River preparing to press forward to capture Berlin. However, the northern flank of his Belorussian Front was thinly held and the besieged German garrison in the town of Arnswalde continued to hold. Himmler, with his personal credibility in terminal decline, opted to utilize vital German reserves and SS units to launch a relief effort of limited forces to drive south, relieve Arnswalde, and severely disrupt the Soviet plans. Once the Stavka, the Soviet General Headquarters, recognized the danger, clearing Pomerania became their objective for February and early March 1945. Himmler soon found himself out of his depth in a bloody campaign in Northeast Germany. The game runs from 15th February 1945–5th March 1945, a total of 10 game turns.

Game Contents
• One double-sided 22×34 inch game board
• 440 die-cut 5⁄8-inch counters
• 16-page system rules booklet
• System charts card (2 sets)
• Scenario rules/setup cards (2 sets of 2)
