boardgame: Battles in the East 4: Mius River and Vitebsk
description:
Battles in the East (BITE) is a series simulating World War II Eastern Front battles. The system rules are an evolution of the SPI classics Panzergruppe Guderian, Army Group South, and Cobra. Unit integrity bonuses include German divisions and Soviet corps and vary to show the increasing Soviet cohesion and decreasing German cohesion as the war progresses. HQ units provide higher level combat support and supply advantages to corresponding units in range. BITE also incorporates standardized scales (unit size, distance, time) for easy comparisons. While the series rules allow players to play many games with the same core rules, the scenario rules add unique situational elements of each battle. Each volume includes two battles from one year with two copies of the charts and tables as well as scenario rules and set-up cards for easy reference.

Mius River
July–August 1943: This summer is mainly remembered for the failed German Operation Citadel near Kursk, followed by the advance of the Red Army westward to the end of the year. Often overlooked is the battle on the Mius River after the Kursk offensive. Here the Red Army looked to drive into the German rear, capture Stalino and prevent the orderly withdrawal of Army Group South. Field Marshal Manstein still had Hitler’s support and was aided by the able “new” 6th Army commander, General Hollidt. The Wehrmacht fought a classic defense and counter thrust battle in late July. The II SS Pz Corps counterattack in open terrain showed what might have been achieved that summer, if Manstein had been allowed to complete a mobile defense in Southern Russia. The game runs from 17 July–6 Aug.

Vitebsk
Panther Line, November 1943: The summer and early autumn of 1943 was a period of steady retreat and defensive line battles for Army Group Center. Often overlooked until recently, these were bloody campaigns. The key strategic cities of Nevel and Vitebsk were central to the defense of the Panther Line—the link between Army Group North and Center. Large Soviet Guards formations were tasked with taking both and breaching the Panther Line defenses. Defending this area was the depleted 3rd Panzer Army, a panzer army in name only. OKH recognized the importance and the only panzer reserves available were rushed there to stem the Soviet offensive. The battle was fought in awful conditions impacting coordination and supply logistics. The game runs from 1–21 Nov. 1943.

—description from the publisher

Looking for an exciting new game for family game night? Family board games for kids and adults can lose their fun after years of the same options staring you in the face from your game closet. So, whether you're looking for a family card game, board games for family night, or a dice game for adults, Roll the Bones will keep the energy high.

This fast-paced winner-take-all dice game is easy to learn and combines, luck, strategy and may even scratch your gambling itch. Do you risk your 'bone die' now or wait for a better opportunity? As pirates ante-up their jewels (dice), they test their luck, hoping to be the one who takes down the pot. The pirate who collects all of the jewels wins.

Each game lasts just 6-8 minutes, so no pirate gets left stranded long. In fact, pirates sitting on the sidelines will have just as much fun rooting for (or against) the remaining pirates in the game.

Your sides will be aching as you laugh the night away, heckling and cheering in fits of excitement and disappointment.

—description from the publisher

As Wellington’s line comes under pressure and starts to buckle, his prayer is answered. Not nightfall, but Prussian forces under Feldmarschall Blücher arrive behind the French and impulsively attack!

ATO Pocket Battle Game #29.

description:
First expansion for Within Walls a paranoia driven hidden traitor game with social deduction elements set in the early 20th century horror setting.

The "Infiltration" is the story response by the "Agency" to gather information while obstructed by the "Cult"

Within Walls: Infiltration deepens the mystery of the original Within Walls story. 
While the conflict between the Agency and the Cult escalated, it also became obvious 
that the real threat was somewhere else and far more malicious than just human 
organizations with different agendas.

See what happens when the Ghost is added to the equation!

Up to 8 player games
New professions
New interactions with the "Change" cards
A new Agenda!

The game is played in the same setting as the original since the goal of the game is playing the interaction between players instead of with the board.

—description from the publisher

The game is set in a medium fantasy, dark themed, medieval world. The aim of the game is to encapsulate local folk stories and take you through an unique journey where you can rediscover old legends as you might know them. The game takes inspiration from some TTRPG games but at its core, it comes with its own set of unique combat rules and creatures that keeps the combat realistic and dynamic. The game can be played around a table with as many people wishing to participate but, I personally advise you to keep it up to a maximum of 5 players for a better immersion. I hope I piqued your interest and that you’ll stick around!

• How is it played?
- Like most TTRPG games, you will need your character sheet that you will compile with the help of the source book where you will find all necessary information about the game mechanics and it will basically teach you how to play the game. Reading through, you will find out the various combat mechanics that are divided in chapters:

STATISTICS:
- There is a list of statistics that you may invest points in and they all boost your character's strength, agility, dexterity, spirit and other. There are 9 statistics in total and some of them have various functions in order to keep the list small and compact.

COMBAT:
- First thing first, you have to know that there are 4 types of damage and each benefits from a said "Main" statistic out of the 4 main ones. For example, there is Slicing Damage on a weapon, it means that your agility will serve as a damage multiplier for that kind of weapon. This will prevent that dexterous rogue to pickup a great sword out of the blue and use it as effectively as a fighter. There is no rule for what you can equip so you can let your imagination run wild with the actual 23 available subclasses, over 120 magical abilities and many many more to come, you can make your own unique character and set it free into the dark ages of mythical Transylvania. You will find a lot more in the source book about bleeding effects, wounds and how they can turn the tide of the combat, a progression chart where each level, your character is allowed x points that you can spend in increasing your statistic's value thus making your character stronger... There is a lot but, let's move on...

PROFESSIONS

Your character can learn professions that will allow them to gain access to certain consumables and more. Professions are meant to bring to the table unique interactions between players that can alter the course of the story drastically, from the Thaumaturgist rogue that can either create poisons or cure them, to the alchemist vedmak who can transmute certain weapon materials, these are all things that as you progress and level up, you will be able to unlock.

MAIN STORY

The game starts you off slowly, with your character not knowing about the existence of the supernatural and then slowly discovering of it's existence, you will find yourself in a situation where you've seen things and experienced but can't share them with other people freely as freedom of speech was not so common back then and being accused of witchcraft was quite common for even the most ridiculous things, a situation where you have to measure your words carefully and having to find a way to survive and make moral choices that will shape the world around you, in other words, you are the story, you make the world shift with your actions.

ENEMIES:

There are all sorts of monsters and beasts, some of them, are inspired off of mythical creatures from local folk stories that I grew up with as a child, like iele or moroi, pricolici and more, but, there are also simple creatures that you can find in the wilds, simple, but very dangerous sience the game tries to achieve a realistical combat feel, so, one can only imagine, if a simple wild animal is that strong, how strong can a werewolf be... or a vampire... Guess you'll find out soon. I just hope that whatever it is you decide to do while playing the game you're having fun!

スキットドゥ (Skit Do) is a poker-style 2 player game from Japan. The players select pairs of cards to win, but there are special effects on the cards and set winning conditions too. Of the five tricks played, highest value will win three, lowest will win won, and highest total wins the fifth. These are turned up randomly at the start of each of the five tricks.

The game has a deck of 25 cards numbered 1 to 5 in five colours. This shuffled and players are dealt a hand of 10 cards, plus a zero card. The remaining cards are used as victory points for each trick.

Each player gets an aide showing the different number combos that rank in the game. A combo of 1 and 5 in the same colour is the highest rank, then a run in the same colour, then any pair, then any two in the same colour, then any run, then any two.

At the start of a round, the winning condition and the scoring card are revealed. Players choose two cards to play face-down as their trick. The starting layer can choose to Open or pass. Opening means they force the opponent to reveal a number card, but the effect on that card takes place. If they passed, the opponent can Open or pass. After this, the cards are revealed and the trick resolved.

The winner takes the scoring cards as victory points and the game moves on to the next trick. After five tricks, the player with the most victory points is the winner.
user summary

ノンドランカ (Non Drunka) is a card game from Japan themed on drinking but not getting drunk.

The game has a deck of 49 cards showing sets of seven cocktail cards, seven cards in each set. The front of each card shows the alcohol value of that glass, the back shows the range in the set. So the pink cocktail goes 1,1,2,2,3,4,5 but the red cocktail set goes -2,0,2,4,6,8,10.

Players are dealt some cards face-down and do not look at their drinks. Instead, they select some to hold up in front of themselves, so the other players can see the values. Players then take turns to ask questions about their drinks, until the start player stops asking, after which everyone else gets one more question.

Players must deduce from what they see, and what they learn, which cards to drink and which to discard. They must drink under their limit, which is set by the hand cards they chose not to pick up.

If they finish under their limit, they score points equal to the number of cards held, so being able to drink more glasses but not get drunk scores most. If they go over their limit, they get zero. After player a few rounds, the player with the most points wins, being best at social drinking.

In addition, players may draw and discard during the round, and certain cards also have special effects that apply to the player or to the others.
user summary

boardgame: Drag Racing: The Dice Game Pro Stock & Pro Stock Motorcycle
description:
Welcome race fans and gaming enthusiasts to the world of pro-stock drag racing featuring factory hot rods and motorcycles.  Drag racing is the ultimate extreme competitive sport where cars race over a short distance as a test of acceleration. This all results in a fast and exciting racing experience and in pro stock this is all performed on a straight quarter mile race track. Drag Racing: The Dice Game is an exciting and fast game that captures the competitive racing of professional pro stock drag racing right on your tabletop.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Hunger: Golden Rose Promo Card
description:
This is a promo card

10 points
Ready, Unique, Permanent

Discard this card to play a card from your discard pile.

CRACK THE CODEWORD

A two-player word deduction game using dry erase cards and markers.

RULES:

Each player uses a dry erase marker to write a secret 5-letter word at the top of their card, then encodes their word as follows:

Above each letter, write the row or column where the letter is found (0-9). Below each letter write a single code: V for vowel, C for consonant or the letter’s point value (#). You must encode at least one of each type.

Each player gives their opponent the ten codes to write at the bottom of their cards.

Players then use the codes to crack their opponent’s secret word. They can attempt a maximum of five guesses. The person who guesses first gains one point. Play until one person has guessed five words.

—description from the designer

Dive into the library of 320 books! Introduce reading as a hobby to everyone with best selling books from various well known publishers!

Supports 3-8 Players, where each player takes turn playing as a “Customer” while the other players have to recommend books to. The best recommendation will be voted and rewarded with points! Players with the best points wins!

A conflict between the world's most legendary creatures, all controlled by a single deck of cards in your hand!

Each player has their own creature deck, and performs attacks on their opponents by 'scrolling' through their cards. The more powerful cards make you scroll faster through the deck, which is risky, because the instant you pass the dreaded 'Fraxure' card, your creature takes Fraxture damage.
Accumulating 6 Fraxures results in your defeat!!!

The Legends of the Sea boxset includes 4 decks:
Jörmungandr, Charybdis, The Kraken and Umibozu

Fraxure is a fast back-and-forth dueling game, so fast that players are barely in control! 
The boxset includes reference, scoring cards and score gems.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ヤミヤミバーガー (Yummy Yami Burger)
description:
ヤミヤミバーガー (Yummy Yami Burger) is a game from Japan themed on constructing hamburgers with unusual ingredients. Will a hamburger taste good with tofu or seaweed?

The game has 50 ingredient cards (strips showing the layers of food). Players must construct their burgers to fit themes, such as a burger that's good with beer, or one that is most healthy.

Players then vote on the most suitable burger to fit the theme. The player winning the most rounds is the winner of the game.

The idea of the game is to stimulate discussion of food and generate surprising new ideas for recipes. The designer suggests, even a joke burger could be a good burger.
user summary

88アイスクリーム (88 Ice Cream) is a stacking dexterity game from Japan. The logo and package design resembles an American brand that boasts a lot of different flavours. In the game, there are 8 flavours customers want to be scooped onto their cone, represented in a deck of cards that are turned up one by one.

Players take turns to hold the cone (made of folded card) and place a scoop of ice cream on top. The scoops are paper slips that fold out to form a square of four walls. As they build up, the players can turn them about so they stack well. But some flavours are thicker or thinner than others, and after a few scoops, the stack is unsteady.

Each turn, a new card is turned up and the other players pull out the matching slip from the slip and make it square for the player, who takes it and adds it to the top. Then they pass it clockwise to the next player.

Any scoops that fall mean the cards in the order go to the player that dropped them. When the game ends, the player with the fewest cards is the best scooper and wins the game.

The designer recommends players buy a tub of ice cream before playing, as they will want to eat it when done.
user summary

As each customer

Only a dirty pig is a happy pig!

In Drecksau, each player has 3-5 clean pigs in front of him and three cards in hand. Each turn one card is played. By playing a mud card, one pig can be sent into the mud. The pig card is flipped over to show a dirty pig on the back. Rain cards clean all pigs, even your own! Barn cards protect pigs from the rain, while lightning cards destroy barns – but lightning rod cards protect barns from lightning cards. Farmer cards can be sent to dirty pigs: the farmer likes clean pigs... Pigs in a barn that are protected with a lock card are safe from the farmer. The first player who has dirtied all of his pigs wins!

Drecksau Total is 10 years anniversary edition in the practical Open & Play Magnetbox, includes two new action cards (extra mud and lipstick) and both the Drecksau base game and the Sauschön expansion that lets players decide how they want to participate in the pig parade, whether with full makeup and styling fit for a beauty pageant or a more traditional wallow in the mud.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: こそドロパンケーキ (Kosodoro Pancake)
description:
こそドロパンケーキ (Kosodoro Pancake) is a cute dexterity game from Japan. Players have to grab pancakes from a pile using a knife and fork. There are three pancake monsters asleep on the pile, and they must not be disturbed.

The game has a set of discs showing pancakes with lots of different toppings; honeylemon, macha, chocolate, strawberry and so on. The players make up a random pile of discs no wider than 15cm. Three monster standees are put onto the pile. Players take turns using the small disposable knife and fork supplied in the game to gentle lift a pancake free from the pile, without waking the monsters. If a monster is woken, they eat the pancake instead and the player misses out that turn.

As the game progresses, the more pancakes people collect, then a series of traps come into play. Tokens are turned up which put restrictions on the player. For example, they must get the pancake in under 15 seconds, or they can use one hand only.

At the end of the game, the player with the most pancakes is the winner.

The game is packaged in a glass jar with a metal screwtop lid.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Champions – Order Campaign Deck
description:
Campaign decks allow those new to the Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Champions universe to quickly field an army and get straight into the action. There are 4 Campaign decks available each with their own individual playstyles.

Stalwart and righteous warriors looking to free the Mortal Realms from the clutches of Chaos. The forces of Order are home to Stormcast Eternals and Idoneth Deepkin. Their strength lies in empowering individual units so that they can tear through the opposition while exercising precision board control.

boardgame: パフェdeフィーバー (Parfait de fever)
description:
パフェdeフィーバー (Parfait de fever) is a set collecting game from Japan. Players are putting together ice cream parfaits for customers. The game has three decks to the make up the bottom, middle and top of the dish. There are customer cards requesting certain dishes to be made. A player takes turns flipping cards to form the parfait to best fit the request.

The parfait cards have 6 colours and three background patterns. If the player can get these matching, they can score bonus points. When the player has turned enough cards, the other players vote on whether the parfait does fit the request from the customer.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points is the winner.
user summary

boardgame: 怪盗レオンの予告状 (Kaito Leon no Yokokujo)
description:
怪盗レオンの予告状 (Kaito Leon no Yokokujo) is a butting game from Japan.

There is a notoriously phantasmagorical jewel thief, Leon. Leon cannot be caught or even identified. This is because Leon is actually several different people. These thieves are trying to steal more jewels for themselves and so send out letters to the other thieves telling them which jewels to leave.

The players have a set of cards numbered 1 to 5 in three colours, red, yellow, blue. They play two cards face-down to the table to decide which jewels they will take. The jewels are in 6 colours, red, yellow, blue and also green, orange and purple. Some gems are drawn at random from a bag and are the jewels Leon is trying to steal.

Players reveal their cards and work out, by combination and value, who gets which gems. So if a player plays two red cards, they will get the red gems. But a red and yellow card means they will get the orange gems.

Where a player has a unique colour combo, they take the gems right away. But where players have the same combo, then the values of the cards determine the winner.

In addition, players have fake letters which can be used to bluff their oponents.

The player with the most gems at the end wins the game as the most successful Leon.

Play colorful triangular pieces trying to match the colors (numbers relate to colors), but if the numbers don't quite match your opponents get points. If you can complete certain shapes, though you can win the day.

The cards are printed with representative battleships, aircraft carriers, cruisers, and destroyers of World War II.
In the game, the first player to attack organizes an invasion fleet from a hand of five cards, while the second player organizes a defense fleet from a hand of seven cards.
The first player to attack wins the game when he or she withstands the attack of the defending fleet and succeeds in a direct attack. Otherwise, the player with more victory points gained by sinking the opponent's battle ships wins the game.
Cards are discarded when used for attack, so it is important to organize the invasion fleet efficiently.

An endless stream of green runs wherever the forces of destruction find enemies to crush. Orruks, Grots and vicious beasts flood the battlefield, quickly overwhelming their opponents with the sheer volume of their forces. These ferocious warriors are known to group together to make their attacks even more deadly.

Add more dynamism to your Sly Foxes game with 6 new types of cards. Unexpected twists, counterattacks, spies, and stealthy magpies. Expect the unexpected or call the helpful badger for help.

These cards are an expansion to the base game and cannot be played without it.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Hour of Devastation Planeswalker Deck: Nicol Bolas, the Deceiver
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Ancient and powerful, the dragon Planeswalker Nicol Bolas will stop at nothing to dominate the Multiverse. Join his cause to raise an army of afflicted undead and sap the strength of your foes until all bow before you.

—description from publisher

アクマトリツク (Akuma Trick) is a shedding / ladder game from Japan. Players are trying to play cards in sequence, but not to actually empty their hand.

The game ends when one player clears their hand, but they cannot win the game. Instead, the winner is the player with the lowest total value of cards still in hand.

In Japanese folklore, Akuma are malevolent demons that afflict humans. In the game, you are possessed by the demons. The game has a deck of cards numbered 0 to 9 (zero is treated as higher than 9), in six colours. The deck is shuffled, and players are dealt a hand (according to the number of players). Players take turns to play cards from their hand to the table in sequence.

A player can place a single card, or a pair of the same value, or a run of numbers in the same colour.

In addition, some cards have special effects, such as the 5 card reverses the direction of play.

When a player cannot play a card at all, the current tableau is discarded and a new one started. But that player must discard cards adding up to a value determined by the tableau.

As the game progresses, players are trying to stay in the round playing cards to the table, but not emptying their hand, but also not keeping their hand value high. 
user summary

Players take turns as the Taskmaster, challenging their fellow players to complete tasks over which the Taskmaster is the sole judge.

Featuring favourite tasks from the TV show, this travel-sized card game brings the essence of Taskmaster to your very own home. Judge and be judged. Your time starts now...!

Players attempt to recognize photos while the picture is steadily revealed.  The faster you recognize the photo, the more points you score.

Comes with a DVD with 300 photos in various categories.
Can be played individually or in teams.

boardgame: UNO Ultimate Marvel: Add-on Pack – Miles Morales
description:
Grab the UNO Ultimate Marvel Miles Morales Add-On Pack and swap it with a deck in core UNO Ultimate Marvel (sold separately) to switch up the Super Heroes in the card game! This pack contains a full Character Deck with special cards, rules and powerful graphics devoted to Miles Morales!

—description from the publisher

Miles Morales
Miles Morales is no stranger to danger. Use his Spider Sense power to ensure you keep your best cards from being burned. Use his Wild Cards to control what Danger Cards players can get. Avoid dangerous turns by skipping any player and avoiding all negative effects on cards. Use your camouflage cards when you are down to a few cards to ensure you can go out.

Included in this add-on pack:

     One Uno Ultimate Miles Morales Deck
     Character specific Wild Cards
     Danger Deck Cards with Villains and more!
     2 Chase Foil Cards

In Legit Business, each player oversees the success of their family business. Legitimate business dealings will bring you prestige and respect, but to keep the cash flowing in, you may have to resort to less respectable means. How much are you willing to risk to come out on top?

Each player will command a syndicate of characters of whom only a select few can represent the family in legitimate business deals. You'll recruit additional associates to handle the shadier side of the operation. These characters will be deployed to different locations in the city to manage finances and cash flow, acquire and sell goods, obtain trucks for transport, recruit new associates and curry favor with City Hall.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 魅惑 (?) のスムージー (Miwaku(?) no Smoothie)
description:
魅惑 (?) のスムージー (Miwaku(?) no Smoothie) is a deduction game from Japan. One player is the master of the smoothie shop, the other players are customers. The customers are trying to deduce the ingredients by working out what is not in the smoothie. If the customer gets one of the ingredients correct, the master gets points. If they get it wrong, they score points.

Players ask questions of the master, who cannot lie. But the master does not have to tell the truth. If they can get away with making the customers laugh, that's okay.

The game has a deck of cards and a board showing all the possible ingredients. These range from obvious choices such as fruit, less common ones like avocado, and strange ones like garlic.
user summary

Solo mode for Anno 1800 - The Board Game by Martin Wallace. The player can either play 10 missions of the campaign, which tell a coherent story and thus face new challenges again and again. In addition to the campaign, there are general solo rules that can be used to play an (almost) normal Anno 1800 game in order to hunt for high scores!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Pixel is a fun family & party card game. Pixel is a deck of 50 beautiful picture cards, based on the premise - "A picture is worth a 1000 words". It’s fun, educational, and for the entire family (2-8 players). The pictures are selected to cover a very broad range of topics and every round of the game is guaranteed to be unique. All you have to do is, ask yourself - “What do I see?” 

Pixel can be played in a few variations. Players are expected to come up with a caption by looking at the pictures. This caption could be an object, a country, a person, a colour, or anything that can be connected to the picture. For example - there is a picture in which kids are playing in a beach. One could say - “Sun, Water, Ocean, Atlantic, Barbados, Umbrella, Sand, Holiday, etc.”. The caption/words can be in any language.

Variation 1 :- The objective of the game is to be the first one to spell a word using the pictures. Distribute 4 cards to each player. Each player should find a 3-letter word from one of the pictures and use the other 3 cards to spell it out. 
For a 4 letter word, distribute 5 cards (and so on….). Shuffle and deal again. First one to complete is the winner.

Variation 2 :- Someone shouts a common word (e.g: CAT, LOVE, WATER, etc.). Distribute as many cards as the letters in the word to each player. Players have to spell out the word using the pictures. One picture per letter. 
For example - if the word was LOVE, one possible answer could be – Lemonade, Orange, Volcano and Exercise.
First one to complete is the winner.

—description from the designer

Deal Damage - the fast-paced, easy-to-learn card game where players compete to deal the most damage to their opponents.

Players 2-5 | Time – 15 to 30 minutes | Cards – (with Rules Cards) 135 and 5 markers. (Kickstarter Exclusive additional 30 event cards)

In Deal Damage, players use cards from their hand to attack other players and reduce their health points. The player with the most damage dealt at the end of the game wins. With simple rules and quick gameplay, Deal Damage is a great way to spend time with family and friends while engaging in strategic and exciting battles.
The game includes over 100+ high-quality cards, featuring simple and clean illustrations. It's small enough to take on the go and easy to set up, making it perfect for game night and parties.

Dobrões is a fun game, very easy to play and easy to explain to people. In it, players are pirates who compete against each other to see who will be the richest of the seven mares.

In this delicious game, not even the most skilled pirate can afford to do without luck, as the other pirates won't let you open all the chests and accumulate their riches.

—description from the designer (translated)

All players start with 10 cards (chests) face down. The goal is to get 10 cards (chests) in numerical order from 1 to 10 before any other player! Every great card game has special cards and DOUBLOONS has 07 special action cards, which give greater gameplay and break the legs of other pirates.

This is THE game that shows you what your nose knows! Smell is the fifth sense in humans, and its specificities are surprising. Olfactory memory is a powerful tool, and Proustian moments are there to prove it!

Anne-Laure Hennequin designed this game of trivia about the world of fragrance to share her life-long passion. It has everything going for it. Players wil have fun, deepen their knowledge, stimulate their creativity, and develop their sense of smell.

The olfactory game comes in a full-featured set so you can have fun with family or friends. It contains an educational booklet with detailed explanations, 120 questions about perfume, 40 olfactory challenges, 12 scent pens, and 100 paper blotters to create pleasing fragrance sketches.

To win the game and become the Master of Perfumes, you’ll have to correctly answer 12 questions (3 levels in each of the 4 categories). The winner can then sketch his or her own perfume using the scent pens and blotters included for that purpose. Get your creative juices flowing today!

Master Parfums is a unique game to explore the world of fragrance and smell while having fun!

Take turns drawing cards and answering the questions about perfume culture. Watch out! Don’t draw a trap card that could slow your progress towards earning the title of Master of Perfumes! Awaken your senses by designing a fragrance and put yourself in a perfumer’s shoes!

—description from the publisher

The Master Parfums Pocket Quiz is the travel-friendly version of the olfactory game. This deck of 120 cards, helps deepen your knowledge about perfume through intriguing and sometimes surprising questions!

This pocket version doesn’t come with scent pens, the booklet, or the paper blotters used to compose your own fragrance. Its small size is designed to be easy to pack in a bag so you can bring it wherever you go. It’s a perfect way to pass the time when traveling or to spice up a friendly gathering!

Through 120 questions/answers on fragrance, embark upon an unforgettable olfactory journey. Four categories of cards help expand your knowledge:

History, Literature, and Mythology
    Perfume Secrets
    The Olfactory Palette
    Brands, Perfumes, and Tips

Try to be the first player or the first team to win 12 correctly answered cards. Are you up to the challenge?

Play the pocket version of Master Parfums!

120 questions, to learn about perfume culture while having fun playing the Pocket Quiz! 

Features: we’ve designed this mini version so you can take it anywhere you go and expand your knowledge of perfume trivia through quizzes. The questions are different from those in the Olfactory Game booklet, meaning the cards can be added so you can enjoy twice as many questions!

If you want to go for the full-featured version that appeals to the senses thanks to its scent pens and blotters, the olfactory game is your best bet.

The box contains 120 perfume trivia questions in a pocket size that’s perfect on the go!

Rules of the game: the first player to correctly answer 12 questions (3 in each category) wins the game and becomes the Master of Perfumes!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

boardgame: Master Parfums: Pocket Quiz – special edition JOVOY
description:
Master Parfums introduces its special Pocket Quiz with JOVOY, the Ambassade des Parfums Rares!

Challenge yourself with the niche brands of the Parisian boutique, and discover all the stories and anecdotes of its selection of exclusive perfumes.

Master Parfums presents its new exclusive Pocket Quiz, a special edition dedicated to JOVOY, “the Embassy of Rare Perfumes”.

120 questions and answers to play and learn about niche perfumery brands found at JOVOY.

Discover these unique perfumery brands along the usual Master Parfums categories: History and Arts, Perfume Secrets, The Olfactory Palette, Brands, perfumes and tips.
Test your knowledge and challenge your friends!

From amateur to connoisseur to expert, who will become the JOVOY MASTER OF PERFUMES?
 
 Rules: the first player to correctly answer 12 questions (3 from each category) wins the game and becomes the Perfume Master!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: Murder On The Underground
description:
Meghan Rogers, a young, aspiring scientist, has been found dead in a remote part of the London Underground, and murder cannot be ruled out. Now it's the players' turn to determine what really happened.

First, create the scene and build the murder board – a 750-piece jigsaw puzzle. Another five 50-piece mini-puzzles and the notes of the investigating officer provide additional missing pieces of evidence that help to get players on the trail of the murderer. Alone or in a team, combine witness statements, toxicology findings, and medical reports with other evidence and observations to track down the solution. Ready to solve the case?

What's For Dinner?  plays much like a simplified version of Silver with a fun food theme that is appealing to a younger audience.

—user summary

How good is your memory? Find out as you swap, vomit and spy on your dinner cards! Be alert to swap out the Dramatic Desserts for Goofy Greens for the Sparkling Sodas for Vacationing Veggies - don't be late if you want to end up with the healthiest plate! Get ready to play this super fun game as you build a meal, worth a reveal!

—description from the back of the box

Pink Panda's Pajama Party is a fun, colorful card collecting game for families.

Roll the big pink die to find out how many pink character cards you will draw, or roll a panda to draw a special blue action card that might let you steal or swap with other players. If cards are stolen from you you may immediately draw more cards up to eight in hand. Collect eight cards in a matching color to win!

Immerse yourself in the medieval fantasy world of Dragons and Ladders! A reinvented version of the classic Snakes and Ladders game in which you play as heroes as they storm a castle and the fabulous treasure it contains. But beware! This castle is well guarded by monsters of all kinds! You will have to fight and use your powers strategically in order to reach the Treasure Room and defeat the three-headed dragon.

—description from the publisher

Find, inside this box, a board game to have fun and challenge your friends with a booklet full of surprises, a game board, pawns, a dice and an hourglass to get into the mood.

Find Minus and his friends and fill up on adventures and challenges! Prove that you're the strongest by answering quizzes on the Minecraft universe and become an army leader to fight the terrible Herobrine! So who is the Noob?

Put yourself in noob mode for a crazy evening!

Ecclesia is a cooperative, historical and missionary board game. You will dive into the first centuries of the Church, like the disciples of Christ, sent to spread the Gospel in the Mediterranean basin.

You will be led to seek relics, found bishoprics, and accomplish other new missions.

Each player embodies a disciple among the 20 available. Each of these characters has a different charisma, an unique and thematic power, and different talents to exercise game actions.

Ecclesia is an immersive turn-based strategy game, but the objectives are common between the players: the team regularly draws divine calls (objective cards) among the 30 available, so careful choices must be made to get the most out of them. Succeeding between 6 and 9 calls is synonymous with victory.

In Sliding Bears, players help the polar bear cubs to cross the ice pack before it disappears!

Players slide the ice tiles with a touch of strategy and by rolling dice to make the cubs jump between tiles and use the wind to move tiles along. But beware or cracking ice and other animals who can block your way.  6 different setups to increase the difficulty and vary the fun of the game.

To cross the ice floe and get to the other side, you'll have to make the best use of the following 3 points:

     Make the cubs jump from one ice sheet to another
     Slide the plates with the help of the wind to go faster
     But be careful, the ice can crack!


The first player to get their cub across the board wins.

From the back of the box:
Battle it out around the duelist kingdom.

Race around the Duelist Kingdom to reach Pegasus' Castle with 10 star chips in your possession FIRST!

Battle it out against other players and opponents using monster cards. Power up your attacks with field power bonus' ... And win battles to gain star chips. Duel, steal and fight your way to win 10 star chips, which grants you access to Pegasus' castle.

Play as either Yami Yugi, Seto Kaiba, Joey Wheeler or Mai Valentine as you battle it out around the Duelist Kingdom to get to the castle first.

—description from the publisher

Polygonotes's 7th card game, 古狼の荊 ("Thorns of the Ancient Wolves"), was made around the themes of distrust and reading other people. Though the rules are simple and easy to learn, it has a high ceiling for strategy and will bring you back to play it again and again.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In Thorns of the Ancient Wolves, players are hunters caught between the conflict of a mad king trying to exterminate the ancient wolves of the nearby forest, and the wolves ready to go to war to protect their home. Each faction is represented by a suit of cards- red for the wolves and blue for the kingdom.

When it is your turn, you draw a card from a face-down pool of 3 and choose a card to play from your hand. Cards are worth points and have special effects. When it is the end of a player's turn and they have 10 or more points from a single faction in their play area, the round ends and that faction is scored as positive for all players- with the losing faction being scored as negative for everyone as well. Players tally their points and add (or subtract) them to victory points that persist between rounds, then begin a new round. If at the end of a round a  player has 15 or more points, the player with the highest victory point total wins.

—user summmary
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Born from the fantasy of the Chambers Of Wonders community, this expansion will bring you the best oddities around! Make room on your showcase, the Strangers Things are coming!

Stranger Things expansion adds 12 new pre-determined cards to increase the replayability and variety in your Chambers of Wonders games!

—description from the publisher

Come on ladies & gentlemen, the cinema has arrived in town! Ready to compete for props from old and new films? The show is about to begin!

Cinématographe Lumière expansion homages to a series of cult films will allow you to take advantage of a special token every time you exhibit one of these wonders.
These tokens will give you a random bonus out of the 4 listed below:

     Get £ 3
     Draw 1 Wonder card
     1 Fame token
     Activate 1 Contact of your choice immediately.


Tokens are not tied to a specific card but will always be drawn randomly. Also, each card has different values than the others in this expansion

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chamber of Wonders: Paleontologists Club KS Promo
description:
A two card KS exclusive promo included for backers.  One card includes the instructions, while the other is the club card.

The Paleontologists Club is a new Club Card that you can use in your games. Membership in this Club will allow you to create complete Beasts using the same anatomical part twice, so you can then form bizarre creatures with 2 heads or 2 bodies!

boardgame: Chamber of Wonders: Christmas Club Promo Card
description:
To celebrate Christmas and the coming of the New Year, the creators of Chamber of Wonders have created a special new Christmas-themed Club Card!

With this card, you gain 2 Fame Points for each Legendary Wonder Card displayed in 2 or more copies.

Conspiracy Theory 2018 Black Box is the first Expansion to our popular Conspiracy Trivia Board Game. The 2018 Black Box Expansion can also be used with the 2020 Travel Edition to maximize your Trivia Fun!!!

A blast for conspiracy buffs and sheeple alike. Tinfoil hat not included.

Every copy of Conspiracy Theory 2018 Black Box comes with 125 New Conspiracy Trivia Cards.

—description from the publisher

Before Waterloo is a two-player strategic level simulation of the Napoleonic Campaign of 1814, extending the play of Beyond Waterloo with two campaigns.

One, "La Patrie en Danger!", runs from January through April, 1814. Napoleon has been sent reeling from his second massive defeat in 2 years at Leipzig and must defend France from a now overwhelming coalition. The Allies must defeat Napoleon and impose a new civil government on France. The French must avoid that outcome.

The other campaign, "Napoleon's Dream", from November 1813 through April 1814, represents an optimal French imperial campaign.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Featuring 32 fun animal cards to match, players shout out 'snap!' for matching pairs, but when they spot a pair of crocodiles they must snap their arms instead! Children will love the quirky characters in this game, from a superhero crocodile to a gorilla drinking tea!

—description from the publisher

Throw, place, turn, remove... and manage to draw the longest worm on your board to earn more points than anyone else.
Colourfull is a fun game for creating polyminoes from colored dice. Each participant, from his point of view, will paint them on his blackboard. But be careful, because these actions will make them change their shape, position or completely.

LAUNCH, PLACE, TURN, REMOVE...
The colored worms have escaped and we must recover as many as we can. You must take into account not to leave free spaces in your grid and above all color the maximum number of contiguous holes with the same color.

Whoever has the most points at the end of the game will win this colorful game.

Karolina, Kazik and the otter Hyacinth continue their pursuit of the mysterious kidnapper of the Puzzlebot. This time they will travel not only through space, but also through time! They will visit 7 different eras where they will meet more friends and face even more puzzles.
• Thoughtful puzzles for young readers
• Independent 2nd part
• Game without internet connection

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Hour of Devastation Planeswalker Deck: Nissa, Genesis Mage
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

The elf Planeswalker Nissa Revane draws power from her connection to the natural world. Enlist her aid to harness primal elemental forces and summon massive creatures as you overrun your enemies' defenses.

—description from publisher

Expansion for the Vudù/Voodoo game (first edition).
This expansion contains a doll, 3 new ancestral artifact cards, 3 location cards, and a set of rule variations.
Players can now activate the doll's power and cast her effect to the other players for new, terrifying curses! But it won't be so easy... When you are on the last step to your victory, you have to visit the doll's location, and survive to her dark power!

boardgame: おにぎり食べられますか？ (Onigiri Taberaremasuka?)
description:
おにぎり食べられますか？ (Onigiri Taberaremasuka?) is a dice game from Japan. Players are trying to predict which Onigiri will be served first. Onigiri are a popular food in Japan, balls of rice formed into triangles, filled with different centres like fish or pickles.

Players are customers in a popular Onigiri store, waiting on their orders. The game has a deck of 42 Onigiri in five different types. Two cards of each type are laid open, and players roll dice to see how the orders progress. the total from the two dice advances a marker forward for that Onigiri. When one marker reaches the fifth point on the board, the game ends.

Players have five markers to bet of which Onigiri will advance. Each Onigiri has different numbers which affect how they advance. Players score points for predicting the winner, and other points for betting. The player with the most points is the winner.
user summary

boardgame: Now or Never: Merchant Caravan Promo Card
description: This promo tile is awarded to the first person to visit the Monument in the game; it offers an exclusive exchange rate for some of the goods, adding a new way to make coins.

boardgame: ゲムマイスターの選択 (Gamemeister no Sentaku)
description:
ゲムマイスターの選択 (Gamemeister no Sentaku) is a game from Japan featuring games from a wide range of doujin publishers with images from over 40 doujin games.

Players take on the role of a boardgame expert working in the doujin game industry in Japan. A customer has ordered a game to fit certain criteria, such as player count, price, age and so on. The players gather at the game market and recognise other game masters looking to complete orders too. Using their memory, intuition and bluff, players try to secure the best game to fit their clients.

The game reflects the huge growth in the Japanese doujin game scene and appeals to payers familiar with boardgame cafes in Japan.

The base game was followed with an expansion offering more doujin games.
user summary
publishers: Friends gathering at Tenganan Yokocho (天岩庵横丁に集う仲間たち)

boardgame: ゲムマイスターの選択：R3 (Gamemeister no Sentaku: R3)
description:
ゲムマイスターの選択：R3 (Gamemeister no Sentaku: R3) is an expansion for ゲムマイスターの選択 (Gamemeister no Sentaku), offering over 18 more cards with more kinds of doujin games, including ones not sold in boxes (as is this expansion).
user summary
publishers: Friends gathering at Tenganan Yokocho (天岩庵横丁に集う仲間たち)

description:
In 8 Planet, players have a hand of 8 cards and hope to get them in order.  The deck of cards consists of some cards showing planets and some special moon cards.

Players are not permitted to rearrange their hands; rather, to get their cards in order, on their turn, they will play any card from their hand, move their pawn that many spaces around a central sun and swap the card they played with the card in that location on the board.  The acquired card can be added to their hand in any position.

At the end of a round, players will earn 2 points if their hand is in order (it can wrap around from one end to the other), but lose points for gaps in the numerical sequence, for each moon, and if two moons are adjacent.

The players start with 8 points and the game ends at the end of a round in which one player has 0 or fewer points. The person with the most points wins.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Champions – Death Campaign Deck
description:
Campaign decks allow those new to the Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Champions universe to quickly field an army and get straight into the action. There are 4 Campaign decks available each with their own individual playstyles.

As one by one the Mortal Realms fell to the grip of Chaos one of the Grand Alliances grew ever stronger. For the armies of Death, there is no relief. These lost souls remain forever in servitude to their dark masters. Mordants, Vargheists, Spirits and Skeletons fill the ranks of an army that will never know death.

Theme is the maneuvering of game pieces on a 6x6, 8x8 and 10x10 square board layout to Trap and Purge the Baron

Game play is similar to Chess, if it were on steroids

There are a lot of game pieces involved, about 102 starting pieces and 2 decks of cards

It's a table top game, meant to be played in a modular fashion, so that no 2 games have to be played in the exact same layout or manner

It's a truly 3 dimensional game

Чист късмет ("Pure Luck") is a family game with lots of fun, knowledge, ambition and care for nature. The board game is entirely dedicated to environmental protection and the opportunity to live in harmony with nature. How does this happen? You will find out by following the rules of the game. Only those of you who know how waste is separated, what are the subtleties of recycling and composting will become winners!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Чист късмет е семейна игра с много забавления, знания, амбиция и грижа за природата. Настолната игра е изцяло посветена на опазването на околната среда и възможността да живеем в хармония с природата. Как се случва това ли? Ще разберете, следвайки правилата на играта. Само онези от Вас, които знаят как се разделят отпадъците, какви са тънкостите при рециклирането и компостирането ще станат победители!

—description from the publisher

"A game of creating ludicrous products & sabotaging the competition. "

The game starts with selecting a judge, or "Buyer". The rest of the players each draw cards and create and pitch a product they think will be enticing to the buyer, by using 1 "Thing" card and 1 "Feature" card. After this "initial pitch", players go around the table again, each playing one more feature card. This time features can be played on ANY product in play. Players can either attempt to improve their item, or sabotage the competition. Throughout the round players pitch their product, or try to knock the competition down a peg or two by arguing over the utility, value, and even the meaning, of the products and their features. After everyone has had a turn, the buyer eliminates their least favorite, and removes that item from the sale... But if you get knocked out, don't worry. Eliminated players get to continue playing by helping a buddy's product, or by crushing someone else's hopes and dreams.

Rounds continue with each player playing one feature card each round before the buyer eliminates another product. Products get more amazing, absurd, hilarious, or disastrous with every card and the game continues until only one product remains, which decides the winner and the buyer for the next game!

Unlike many judge-based games, Card Sale allows players to creatively "build" funny hands rather than just being rewarded for drawing the funniest pre-written punchline card.

—description from the designer

Join the race against time in Clockwise, the worker placement board game played on functioning mechanical gears.

Based on a Jules Verne short story, players collect resources and build clocks to save the city of Geneva. The game board consists of five inner mechanical gears and one large outer gear that rotates all the inner gears when it is moved. The inner gears contain resource spots that correspond to resource items used to build clocks. Players must place one of their workers on the starting position of any gear. The more turns you wait on the inner gears, the more valuable resources you will receive.

With the resources claimed, players can trade them in to build one of the revealed clocks located at the bottom of the board. Each clock has unique resources to build them and each hold a different victory point value when completing them. The game is played for 24 hours and when the clock strikes midnight, the game is over and clock victory points are tallied. The player with the most victory points wins the game and is titled as Geneva's next Clockmaster. Can you restore order before time runs out?

—description from the designer

Escape from Kabul - Doin’ the Bugout Boogie is a four player game where players represent three allied factions and one opposing faction:  The three allies are: The International Security Assistance Force (ISAF), Non Governmental Organizations (NGO), and the Afghanistan Government. They are opposed by a Player representing the Taliban. (For classroom purposes, Players may also be teams instead of individuals.)

The game is based using modified mechanics of the popular game AFTERSHOCK: A Humanitarian Crisis Game, by Rex Brynen, notably with an inverse timeline represented in the game turn sequence.

The allies’ main purpose is to evacuate refugees from Kabul as it falls to the Taliban before the agreed upon withdrawal date of ISAF on 31 August 2021. The Taliban Player’s purpose is to inhibit these evacuations.

Refugee Tokens still on the Kabul map at the end of the game are considered failed evacuations.  The game is a win or lose proposition for the allies.  Since there is no game mechanic that shows whether an ally did better than another, it is suggested that all allied Players cooperate to the best of their ability.

Refugee evacuation is regulated by the Perceived Threat Urgency Index (PTUI).  As the PTUI increases, refugee tokens can be evacuated quicker.

In fixed Player order 1) Taliban, 2) Afghan Gov't, 3) NGO, & 4) ISAF; players may draw Coordination cards, then a mandatory Event Card, move their tokens in the TAD phase, perform two actions (e.g. move refugees to airport, eliminate other player tokens, or media outreach, & evacuate refugees from airport), then get or take supplies.  Too many hostile acts may raise the Public Affairs Tracy and the game may be lost early.  There are more actions than can be mentioned here.  8 pages of rules.

Just remember, “They don't know that we know they know we know." There’s even more to love about your favorite New Yorkers in this hilarious version of the classic mystery game! Based on the unforgettable 90s sitcom, CLUE: Friends is “The One with the Secrets” as one of the crew is keeping a personal revelation under wraps that is bound to be revealed! Take on the roles of Rachel, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler, Joey, and Ross to get to the bottom of WHICH secret is being kept, WHO reveals it, and WHERE in New York City it’s found out!

Test your knowledge, along with friends and family to see who has been paying attention throughout all 236 episodes of Friends the Television Series. Can you name all 3 of Ross' wives? Do you remember where Monica and Chandler first got together? Friends Ultimate Trivia Quiz contains over 2000 questions covering all 10 seasons of Friends.

Supplied in a full colour, closed box where all the pieces can be stored when the game is not in use. The Friends Ultimate Trivia Quiz makes the perfect gift for any fan of this multi-award winning sitcom. Plus, collectors of Friends official merchandise will want to add this Ultimate Trivia Quiz to their collection of memorabilia.

Test your knowledge about all 236 episodes of Friends the Television Series
Contains a total of 2000 questions
Suitable for two or more players
Officially licensed Friends product

—description from the publisher

In 1861, the Southern states had seceded from the Union, attacked Federal positions and forts, and were preparing for a War of Independence. The North responded by building up its own army (prewar Union troops were approximately 20,000; the total number who fought at Bull Run alone.) The armies on both side would swell, so that at their peaks, the South would have close to 1.2 million and the North would have more than 2 million manpower in uniform. What it took to do this was incredible. Some of the factors were the draft, establishment of income taxes, and the further implementation of machines for manufacturing. The organization which took place North and South allowed the war to go as long as it did.

Now, become a "Super-general" in charge of 100,000 manpower, an area's production, moving supplies, moving manpower and material, building war materials, deciding how and when to blockade, or running blockades. Explore what it takes to defeat the Southern Rebellious Slime, or the Northern Yankee Imperialist Oppressor. Can you do it alone, or will you link your survival to that of the others on your side? Will you trade needed goods and units freely and not consider the fame others gain from your generosity, or will you decide to risk defeat so that you might do it all yourself?

Become one of the eight players: 
The General of the Army of the Potomac 
The General of the Army of The Ohio 
The General of the Army of the West 
The Admiral of the Navy, for the North 
The General of the Army of Virginia 
The General of the Army of Texas 
The General of the Army of Tennessee 
The General of the Army of Mississippi for the South

Your goal as the leader of a vast amount of manpower and lands is to generate victory for your side, while gaining fame and prestige for yourself.

"It is a well that war is so terrible, lest we grow too fond of it" 
-Gen. R.E. Lee

—description from the designer

Stack four tiles per space on the sand-printed game board.

Then, start the treasure hunt!

Find matches by using the magnetic shovel to turn over magnetic treasure tiles. Matches are made by finding treasures of the same kind - jewels, rings, crowns, shells, crabs and more.

For example:
Your child flips two tiles and finds two starfish. The matches are added to their loot. Next turn, two jewels are revealed - another match! Your child adds them to his stash. Next turn, one starfish and one jewel. Sweet! Because these match tiles already owned by your child - they get to keep both and add these to their stash.

The hunt has begun... what kind of treasures will you find?

I Spy Treasure Hunt Game
Flip tiles to match treasures
Encourages visual recognition, memory development
A high-interest treasure digging, matching game for kids!
Fun to use magnetic shovel for uncovering tiles
Treasures include pirate and beach treasures - jewels, rings, crowns, candlesticks, coins, shells, starfish, crabs

—description from the publisher

Last War is a small-scale skirmish game, you play with a crew of 5 miniatures per player, against AI controlled monsters in scenarios that take you across the wastes of the Last War. Play as enemies, cooperatively or play solo mode.

Last War is a stand alone game using the Forbidden Psalm rules. You do not require any other book to make use of this new setting, campaign, weapons, monsters and so much more.

Inspired by historic wars, set in a world at its end, you will face such horrors as the Trench Maggots, living clouds of poisonous gas, Things from the Fog and many more horrors from the mud clogged trenches.

Deduce the Truth: Lie your way to a win with ReaLies! This funny, creative and easy-to-play party game is all about convincing others that your lie is the truth, while also attempting to guess the real answer.

How To Play: The host draws a card and reads it out loud. On the Dry Erase boards, players write down their most believable response. The host reads all the answers out loud, and players must vote for which they think is the truth. Win points based on who votes for your response, and by guessing the real truth!

—description from the publisher

This is a guessing game based around marvel characters. It is similar to the game 20 questions that most kids have played growing up. The object is for teams to collect seven cards to win.

Game includes 52 Game Cards, 6 Clue Cards, 1 Card Holder and a handy box to store it all.

This is a spin and move game where you try to smash your opponent's dinosaurs that are made of kinetic sand. The idea is to smash the most dinosaurs before getting around the board. Be the first player with 5 Jurassic tokens and don’t get stomped on by the T-Rex!

Includes 1 Horizontal Gameboard, 1 Vertical Gameboard, 2 Plastic Cups, 1 T-Rex Dino Stomper, 2 Dinosaur Molds, 4 Dinosaur Movers, 4 Plastic Stands, 1 Bag of Kinetic Sand, 1 Sand Container with Lid, 30 Cards, 1 Punch Sheet With 30 Jurassic Tokens, 1 Instruction Sheet

Go for a spin with JJ and the CoComelon crew! Spin and match the character, colour, shape, or pattern. Each player gets their own spinner, and whenever someone spins SWAP!, you all switch spinners! Spin, match, and play at the Pattern Party! As the #1 Kids YouTube channel in the world and consistently featured in Netflix’s Top 10 lists, CoComelon is one of the most beloved and recognizable names in early childhood entertainment and education.

—description from the publisher

ひよころ (Hiyokoro) is a dexterity game from Japan. Hiyokoro means 'chick', and in the game, the players are manipulating glass marbles in the open palms of their hands, is if they were newly hatched chicks.

The game has a set of marbles, 8 yellow and 8 blue marbles, and 4 red and 4 green marbles. There is a deck on cards showing how the player must arrange the marbles, at the start and the finish.

For example, on a turn, a player will have three yellow marbles in their right palm and two yellow and one blue marble in their left palm. The goal is to finish with all the marbles in their left palm, with the yellow marbles surrounding the blue marble in the centre, like a flower. The opposing player would do the same, but surrounding a yellow marble with five blue marbles.

Players can only manipulate the marbles by cupping, flexing and turning their open palms and fingers. A player cannot trap a marble in one finger by curling the finger over the marble, not between two fingers, and so on. Only by freely flowing the marbles around, can they complete each challenge.

When a player completes the challenge successfully, they call out 'Hiyokoro!'. The player who completes the most challenges first is the winner. But the designer emphasises the benefit of play for children and for rehabilitation.

The game can be played solo as the player is trying to beat each challenge.
user summary

Tessella is a simple but addictive two player abstract.

     Easier to learn than checkers
     Has a strategic depth similar to chess or go
     Uses a completely novel board layout and capturing mechanic


Each player has 7 pieces displayed along opposing corners of the board, the goal is to capture 4 pieces of the opponent.

On your turn, you can either:

     MOVE to any empty adjacent spot (be it a square or an octagon) or
     CAPTURE an opponent's piece.


To make a Capture, two of your pieces must be in a line with no other pieces between them. Following this line, you can then capture an opponent's piece, no matter how distant it is, by "shooting" (sliding) the nearer of the two pieces to take its spot.

Helsingin peli (also in Swedish: Helsingfors-spelet) is a roll and move board game. Published game included a game board with 60 squares, and a rules booklet. Players were required to provide their own game money, suggesting that the game has been played as a low stakes gambling game. In the rules booklet there is a description for each game board square, including the information of money gained or lost in that square.

Fruits is no ordinary card game. This juicy twist on fast-paced favorites like Uno and Gin Rummy is chock-full of cheerful claymation art and surprising disruptors to keep you on your toes.

Players will encounter ten types of personality-packed Fruits cards of varying point values and must harvest “bunches” of three or more fruits to earn points. Along the way, action cards will come into play, either multiplying players’ successes or thwarting their efforts to gather the most fruit; for example: a pie card can double the total value of a bunch, but a grabber card gives an opponent the chance to steal a card—and all of its potential points—from another player’s hand.

Gameplay continues until one lucky fruit collector accumulates a thousand points and wins the game! Hope you’ve been eating your fruits, because Fruits requires a dash of luck, a little strategy, and a whole lot of zest.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 学校へ行こう! (Go To The School!)
description:
学校へ行こう! (Go To The School!) is a card game from Japan. The Japanese title translates more correctly as 'Let's Go To School!', but the box title is given as above.

In this game, five schoolgirls are late for school. The players must help them get to school on time. The cards are double-side, and the players sit facing each other. A player can see the back-side of the cards in their hand, and the front-side of the other player's cards. Working together, they place the cards into a tableau, to bring the children to school on time.

The side facing away from the player helps them draw more cards to hand, but also makes the children later for school. Players must deduce which cards they are holding, and which to play face-up or face-down.

If they can get the children to school on time, the players win.

The original game is for 2 players, but a later expansion pack allows 3 or 4 players.
user summary

boardgame: 学校へ行こう! 拡張せっと (Go To The School! Expansion Set)
description:
学校へ行こう! 拡張せっと (Go To The School! Expansion Set) is an expansion for 学校へ行こう! (Go To The School!). It adds more cards to enable 3 or 4 player games along with the rules for the same. The publisher suggests the base game and one or two expansion packs to allow better play.
user summary

The Faceless Amusement Park is the first dungeon crawler with compass and magnets. The main Characters are teenagers and the Action Wheel represents the difficulty of acting in certain situations of its protagonists. What will happen, turn by turn, can be conditioned by the magnetic force on the Action Wheel. Miniatures with magnets have a positive side and a negative side which will influence the movement of the Compass in the center of the Action Wheel.

—description from the publisher

Wildlife is in danger and the animals are calling for help! Little critters with different problems have come into your care and need you to save them. Cure their ailments and get them ready for release. The rescuer with the happiest and healthiest animals at the end of the game wins.

This is a family friendly card game where you have to balance trying to get the most points while also curing as many animals as possible before the game is over.

Each player is dealt 6 animal cards and 6 ailment cards, face up on the table in front of you. These are the animals in your rescue that you will need to care for. Then each player gets a hand of 5 cards from the game deck. The game deck consists of cure cards and ability cards, and are used to progress the game.

Turns are simple; the player draws a card from the game deck, and then plays a card (or discards an unwanted card). The turn is then moved on to the next player, clockwise. Cure cards are played on animal and ailment pairs in your rescue, which turns them into a "success story". Success stories are what give you points at the end of the game.

There are ability and instant ability cards in the game deck. Instant ability cards can be played out of turn, as a reaction (for example to skip a certain player or block an ability card from being played). Normal ability cards are used during your turn and as described on the card. Some abilities will allow you to play cards from the discard pile, take cards from another player's hand, or get extra points for an animal in your care.

The game ends immediately once a player cures their last animal, and then all players count the points gained from their Success Stories. The player with the most points wins! In the case of a tie, the player with the most animals cured wins.

Budget Town is an educational roll-and-move game designed to teach money counting and making change.  Players roll a die to progress along a track, drawing Pay, Collect and Mail cards that cause the gain or loss of money as they go.  They make change using the replica US bills and coins included.

The first player to reach the end by an exact roll earns a $50 bonus, and the player who finishes with the most money wins.

まるぺけ (Maru & Peke) is a noughts & crosses game from Japan, using special effects on the cards.

There is a war on the dark side of the Moon between two tribes of rabbits. Both tribes are using their special abilities to occupy the territory and win, or gain a draw with the fewest cards.

Players spend cards to a 3x3 grid trying to get three cards in a row in the standard fashion. But the cards have abilities, such as switch places diagonally, or blocking movement, or laying traps.

If the game ends in a draw, the player how spent the fewest cards to draw is the winner.
user summary

Mille Fiori: Die Meisterwerke allows players to continue sailing around the city to meet eminent figures and create glass masterpieces.

To set up, place the new game board that extends the sailing track next to the game board. The game now contains Dogate cards that bring points, changed movements, or a different order of play. A little council of influential citizens, the Signoria, advises the Doge and influences the political events of the city.

Nine different masterpieces can be gathered along the advanced sea route that leads to lucrative trading cities, or through the Dogaressa, the Doge's wife. If you receive a masterpiece, you can immediately collect valuable points, while also using the masterpiece to fill the shop window. If you choose wisely, you can score additional points at the end of the game.

Noch mal! Deluxe uses the same gameplay as Noch mal!, which has also been published as Encore!.

On a turn, a player rolls the three color dice and three number dice, then chooses a color and number pairing that allows them to mark off an area on their personal player board. All other players then choose a color-number combination from the dice that remain. By filling all spaces in a column or of a color, you score bonus points.

Noch mal! Deluxe contains erasable player boards as well as a 3D dice tower that's set up inside the box bottom. What's more, Noch mal! Deluxe contains a co-operative variant played with the same rules in which players need to complete one of the five missions by game's end, whether color bonuses, completed rows, blocks of four, stars, or the spelling of "NOCHMAL". The game contains the original solo variant as well as a solo challenge using the new missions.

Admin note: While Noch mal! Deluxe has a 1-4 player count, the Dutch edition of the game — Keer op Keer Deluxe — has a 1-6 player count.

boardgame: なかよし村殺人事件 (Nakayoshi Murder case)
description:
なかよし村殺人事件 (Nakayoshimura Satsujinjiken) (Nakayoshi Murder Case) is a werewolf-style deduction game from Japan. It has a day and night phase, and the players must deduce who the murderer might be, but is not run by a player and the culprit is not actually known until the end of the game.

Nakayoshi is a peaceful little village, and everybody shares everything. Their belongings, their family, even their destiny. But one morning, a body is found, clearly murdered. And suddenly a detective arrives in the village to interrogate all the suspects. Can the murderer be found, or will they escape justice?

The game has only 14 cards, which are shuffled, and a card dealt face-down in front of each player. In the day phase, the players can look at the card dealt to them in secret and discuss the situation. At night, the night-crawler phase occurs. Each player closes their eyes, and in turn, one player may open their eyes and examine one card to the left or right of them, and may, or may not, swap the cards or make it sound is if they have. When all the players have had their own night-crawl, the next day begins and the players can again look at their card and discuss things.

At the end of the game, the culprit is the player with the highest total value of cards against them, being the card in front and to the left and right of them. The cards are things such as murder weapons, or motives such as passion.

If the villagers can identify the culprit at the end of the game, they win. If the culprit is not found, the culprit wins. But the actual culprit is not resolved until the final reveal. By moving cards between one another, players might draw attention to themselves, but might also deduce who is the real culprit.

The game is designed for 3 to 7 players normally, but with 2 player rules too. the designer suggests that the unused cards could be made open to players if they wanted extra information.
user summary
artists: Purin a la mode (プリンアラモード)

description:
Disc Cards is a fun and challenging card and dice game set in the world of disc golf.  Players navigate through 9 or 18 of 555 unique disc golf locations, creating a new course each game: no two games will be alike!  Players choose their Route (Ace, Birdie, or Par), roll the dice, and use their Disc Cards to match the obstacles presented by each location.

Play solo or with a golf mob of up to six players, play competitively or cooperatively: it's up to you!  Create your custom 60-card deck from a pool of cards or use a premade random deck right out of the box.  With 222 unique and powerful discs to choose from, the strategies are endless!

—description from the designer

Here's FIVE new types of tower cards with 99 more challenges for BUILDZI towers! Deal out these oversized cards and race to be first to stack - by Number, with one hand, 3D, by 'crazi-color (shape only), or 'flip-flop' (no colors at all).

How high can you go?
Zoker is a fun new game to play with your friends and family.
Each player is dealt 5 cards. Players can exchange these cards by trading their Zoker chips for new cards. The aim of the game is to obtain the highest number of points with 4-card combination.
The player with the highest points wins the round and collect5 all the Zoker chips played during the round. At the end of the game, the player with the maximum number of Zoker chips is the winner.
Let's ZOKER!

—description from the publisher

In Koole Klötze ("Cool Blocks"), you need to quickly build sculptures from random bits of wood and hope that others can figure out what you've created.

To set up, place the numbered hexagon in the center of the table, then lay out six construction cards next to the numbers. Lay the 17 wooden bits in a circle around the cards, then place the arrow next to a bit to create a starting point in the line. Each player takes a set of cards numbered 1-6.

The first player takes the first six bits in line, along with the building platform and sunglasses, then shuffles their deck and secretly looks at the bottommost card. They now have 30 seconds to use the wooden bits to recreate the image on the associated construction card, balancing bits as they wish, but not moving them to create an animated scene.

The other players can each play one card face down into the central hexagon to guess which construction card the active player is building. Once time runs out or everyone has guessed, the active player forecasts how many others correctly guessed the answer, then reveals all the cards. Whoever first guessed the correct answer scores 2 points, with the second correct guesser earning 1 point. If at least one person guessed correctly, the builder scores 1 point, and if they forecast correctly, they score a bonus 1 point.

The builder then places all used bits at the end of the line and passes unused bits to the next player clockwise, with that player taking bits from the line as needed to have six on hand.

After everyone has been builder twice (with 5-6 players) or thrice (with 3-4 players), whoever has the most points wins.

In Secret Missions, a mini-expansion pack to Mining Colony, players receive a secret missions card showing 6 items in specific spots in the colony.  Players score points at game end for placing the specified items in the specified locations.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kennst du Wien und die Wiener Straßenbahn?
description:
Each player places his house anywhere on the game board, which represents a map of Vienna. Then each player draws an agreed on number of landmark cards. The players start at their house an have to visit each landmark on their drawn cards before returning home. The first to do so wins the game.

Movement is 1 space per turn. If a player uses a tram or train a die is used to determine the rate of movement. If a player reaches a landmark from one of his cards, he puts the corresponding wood model (1st edition) or block with photo on the map.

—user summary

Winter is getting a little closer every day. The squirrels rush to gather the last acorns before the cold makes the last fruits fall from the trees.
In this game the family of squirrels that collects the most acorns is the winner. Played in turns, each player rolls the dice and moves the squirrel family along the path. We have to make simple calculations to work out the most effective strategy. As usual, the dice indicates the number of squares that we can advance, however, we must be careful. The number on the dice gets deducted by the total number of acrons we are carrying, thus reducing the number of squares that we can advance.
The game ends when all the acorns are gone. Winter has arrived and it's time to count....
It is a game for two to four players. It includes two game boards, which can be substituted depending on the number of players.
In this strategy game, the family of squirrels that manages to bring the most acorns to their burrow will be the winner. A sustainable and proximity game.
