あるちん (Aruchin) is a dice gambling game from Japan. Derived from the popular gambling game Cee-lo, the Japanese version is called Chinchiro (チンチロ) or Chinchirorin (チンチロリン). The designer describes Aruchin as the ultimate evolution of Chinchoririn.

In Cee-Lo, players roll three d6 only, and bet on the outcome. There are various combinations that trigger scoring, such as an instant win or an instant loss, and the game is fundamentally a gambling game.

Aruchin develops this using 81 special action cards that can be used to alter the results of dice rolls, or of the betting outcome.

The game was originally published in a Trial version for 2-6 players, and then in a larger version with higher grade components but for 2-4 players. The publisher also sold expansion score boards to allow more players.
user summary

UMA探検隊 (UMA TANKENTAI) (UMA Expedition) is a memo-style card game from Japan themed on collecting strange creatures known as UMA, that must all be collected. However, UMA have unusual abilities that affect the game.

The deck of 36 cards is shuffled and laid face-down across the table. Players take turns flipping two cards over, looking for a matching pair which they then collect. All the UMA have certain ecological information on them, and some have special effects that affect the opponents, or make the UMA harder to collect.

The game is aimed at younger children and includes Furigana to help them read the text.
user summary
designers: Nyanko Toybox (にゃんこのおもちゃばこ)

description:
Be quick! Follow the dice and catch the right creature.

Place six of the cards face up in a circle around the compass and the rest of the deck next to the circle, face down.

The first player starts the game by rolling both dice. The dice refer to one of the cards in the circle. You use them and the compass to figure out which creature to catch. The first
person to take the right card, wins it, and a new card from the deck is placed in its place.
Now, it is the next player's turn to roll the dice. The game is over when there are only two cards left on the table. The winner is the player with the most cards.

Can you pick up all the rubbish floating in the sea? That's your challenge in Captain Nature.

To set up, transform your table or floor into the sea. Set up the buoys to determine the limits of play, then scatter the trash and animal tokens on the "water". Whoever is captain on a turn wears the blindfold (or keeps their eyes closed), and moves the ship by pushing it across the sea, trying to scoop up only trash. Trips that collect only trash are rewarded, while collected fish and other sea creatures result in pollution on the beach. This must be avoided at all costs because the only way to win is to collect all of the trash while placing all the sea creatures back in the bay.

Set in the King of the Court tournament, in this card game you will become a beach volleyball player trying to win points against your opponents and stay as King for as long as possible.
In each turn only 2 players face each other (King versus 2nd), whoever wins the point remains King, and the loser will have to wait for a new opportunity at the end of the row of turns.

—description from the designer

Historically in the Third World, coups have changed more governments than elections since World War II. Most coups involve using part of the armed forces of a country to seize power from a ruler, though there is usually little overt conflict – the coup d’etat is generally a much less bloody way of seizing power than its distant cousin, the popular revolution.

In the game, two or more players act as the leaders of political or professional factions in a fictional country. Some players will be plotting, individually or severally, to effect a change in the existing government. They will be opposed by others who wish things to remain as they are. But who is which, and what do they really want?

The game is played in two phases: 

     the Pre-coup Phase where players attempt to secure support for their faction in the military, paramilitary and civilian sectors and thwart the others; and 
     the Coup Phase where loyalist and rebel sides face off in direct combat.


Games are short and fast, normally 45 minutes or less.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Each round, a card is revealed. Then, the players roll their 6 dice as fast as they can to try to match the card, locking in any dice they want as they roll. First person to match gets the card. There are a few "Slow your roll" cards in the deck that have a little minigame where they players roll at their own pace. First player to win ten cards wins.

Interstellar Miner is a space mining-themed board game for family, in which players play as the captain of a mining team. Each player leads three miners to mine in space, and whoever digs the highest score at the end of the game is the winner.

The gameplay is driven by throwing dice. The player moves his miner piece according to the result of the dice roll and performs mining. However, each miner has different characteristics and therefore moves and mines differently.

At the end of the game, it's basically 1 point for 1 ore, but you can add 2 points for every 4 different colored ores you collect.

Advanced play adds expert cards, people who enter the space station can hire experts to make your team more powerful.

—description from the designer

In this game for young kids (and their grown ups) the players are each one of the Paw Patrol pups. Specifically, these are the "Mighty Pups" super powered version of the Paw Patrol. The setting is a path through Adventure Bay. The goal is to rescue the most animals along the way. When all players have reached the end of the path, the player who rescued the most animals is the winner.

This game is intended for younger kids, so each player in turn spins a spinner to see how far they move along the path. The only decision is whether to take a side path or stay on the main path when reaching one of two forks in the path.

The players don't actually have individual powers, other than when they have mighty Chase in front of them. That player is allowed to ignore backward move spaces and avoid loss of tokens that represent rescued animals. A player gets him when the spinner lands within Chase's arc.

The winner is frequently not the player who reached the path end first. Kids who are behind on the path often rescue more animals and can pull ahead for the victory.

boardgame: Ecologies: Contact Creatures Business Card Expansion
description:
These business cards are made to work with the first three Ecologies games. They have biome abbreviations, possible trophic levels, point totals, and work within the food webs. The only thing they lack is information on "what eats what." If players want to use these cards in their games they'll have to do their own research and agree on "what eats what." There simply wasn't enough room to include this information on the cards.

These 54 unique cards feature organisms from all 21 biomes introduced in the "Ecologies" series so far.

boardgame: 栄光への戦い～乱世の英雄 (Fight for Glory: Heroes of a Turbulent Time)
description:
栄光への戦い～乱世の英雄 ("Fight for Glory: Heroes of a Turbulent Time") is a luck-based game where players each play a card facedown to reveal all at the same time. 
A strategy card (out of 3 different strategies)is picked at the beginning of the round by the first player , serving as the mechanic of the round (either dictating which card will win over which (Hedgemony/Revolution) or similar cards winning against all single ones (Alliance)). The eleminations proceed according to the strategy chosen. The winning player(s) return their card to their hand, the other discard theirs.
Last player still standing with at least one card win the game.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Amonkhet Planeswalker Deck: Liliana, Death Wielder
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Get started playing Magic right away! Harness the power of death itself to debilitate and destroy all who stand in your way, then raise a horde of undead minions to overwhelm your enemies. Call on the dark magic of the Planeswalker Liliana Vess--with a card unique to this deck--to claim ultimate victory.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: Ripper
description:
Your goal is to uncover the identity of the killer and reveal the motive for the murder. You may also be given other assignments to complete during the course of the investigation.

Start by reading the assignment letter to learn specifics of the case and then begin examining all the contents found inside the box.

When you have finished your investigation and are ready to reveal the solution and conclusion of the mystery, you will need to go to the publisher's website. There is no hint system online so you will only need internet access for the solution.

Christopher's Curiosities:  I found the items in the box you now hold....I now give possession of these mysterious items to you.  They relate to the unsolved murders of Jack the Ripper.  I need your help to investigate this new evidence and solve the mystery of the most infamous serial killer of all time."

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: Papa Midnight
description:
Your goal is to uncover the identity of the killer and reveal the motive for the murder. You may also be given other assignments to complete during the course of the investigation.

Start by reading the assignment letter to learn specifics of the case and then begin examining all the contents found inside the box.

When you have finished your investigation and are ready to reveal the solution and conclusion of the mystery, you will need to go to the publisher's website. There is no hint system online so you will only need internet access for the solution.

The perpetrator of the abduction is known as Papa Midnight. He sent a letter to the FBI that includes a bizarre challenge. Identify the abductee from a selection of 6 photographs he provided, and he will release them unharmed, otherwise they will be murdered.

Embrace your culture! With 56 He Pātai cards and 56 He Pānui cards,  Mauri Ora is designed to test your knowledge of Māori history, customs and culture.

To play:
The objective of this game is to collect as many cards as you can by answering the questions correctly on your Mauri Ora journey.
Roll the dice and move the number of squares rolled. Select a question card according to the colour of square you land on. If you answer correctly you may keep the card. Once a player reaches Mauri Ora everyone adds up their points. Each card is worth 1 point and the player that reached the finish first receives another 3 points. The player with the most points wins the kemu!

T8 is a trick-taking card game for four players. The game is based around having eight suits, and eight cards per suit.  The twist is that players can't follow suits previously played, and they play two cards per per turn into the trick. There is a new scoring mechanic built around the two-card-per-trick mechanic: the lower of the two cards played scores positive points, but any cards lower than that (from the other players) score negative points.

There is also a solo mode, in which players seek to give three automated opponents negative scores while earning a positive score themselves.

The game was designed to be given to attendees at the convention T8 in January 2023.

—description from the designer

Like the saying "Manus manum lavat" (washing hands with hands/mutual relationship), connections were of the utmost importance in Ancient Rome. Especially when it comes to starting a new business.

You will utilise your network by seeking protection from influential people and having them be your "patron (Patronus)". A patron (Patronus) can set up a banquet and introduce you to others who are likely to invest in your business.

Observe the trade route conditions, movement of your rivals, and examine carefully which person you should interact with. The connections you built here will be the cornerstone of your business.

The Roman Empire is known for the development of highways, as a result, later generations would say "all roads lead to Rome". However, there are not many opportunities to talk about their maritime traffic.

Despite their dependency on ships to import large quantities of goods, Senators were banned from owning ships (Lex Claudia, 218BC). And thus, all those great profits from maritime trades become the territory of low-ranking knights and foreigners.

Not only do the aristocrats provide you with various demands for your business, they are also prepared to give you the honour you deserve. Your voyages are also the light of hope for the Roman aristocrats who cannot row into the sea.

Gardening seems like a smooth, easy going and chill activity for those lazy summer days, however, becoming a real gardener requires a lot of hard work, knowledge and a ton of practice. Are you up for the challenge?

In My first Garden players will be assuming the role of first time gardeners and will compete against each other to create the most productive and fruit-full garden.


Design by Juan Agustín Maiolino (2021) for the 2nd Edo's "Stuck at Home" Design Contest 2021!

Today is your first day of work as a Chef's assistant at “Di Kujen” pizzeria, the busiest pizzeria in town. Things were going great until you accidentally dropped all the ingredients for the pizzas in the big cooking pot. Will you be able to collect the necessary ingredients to prepare the pizzas that the customers ordered?


A game designed in 10 minutes from http://10minutedesignchallenge.co.uk/ and worked on over the course of one night.

Three colors is a silly simple dexterity game. It is for two players only and just 9 cards are required (you can print the paper sheet we designed or use any standard playing card deck). In this game, you and your partner will be playing against each other trying to be the fastest one.

This is mostly a kids game, but can be played and enjoyed by anyone. It can make a really good drinking game.

In Argentina, every family gets together on Sundays to enjoy the best BBQ, known as “Asado”. But wait! Making a BBQ is not as easy as it seems: the cook, known as “Asador”, has to grill the meat, prepare the salads, put the glasses on the table and serve the wine. Are you up to the challenge and be able to cook the best barbecue? 

Dice BBQ is a roll and write game for 1 to 10 players in which they will attempt to make the best barbecue for their guests.

Axolotl Mix is an abstract game for 2 players where we put Axolotls back in order in their aquariums. It's meant for younger audiences, but anyone can play it. It has a big deal of randomness, but I found it fun to play.

The Abstract project was born with the idea of starting to experiment with the design of abstract games. This type of game is characterized by a lack of theme in it (or a very soft one) and by a great component of strategy as the base mechanic of the game.


The first in the Abstract series of games. In this number 1, two players face each other to try to get the most points, however, when preparing the game, 4 dice will decide the score of the game, making each one a unique and totally captivating experience.

boardgame: Monopoly: Avatar – Nickelodeon The Last Airbender Edition
description: Imagine taking a journey through the four bending nations as a member of Team Avatar, wielding incredible powers to protect the lands from the Fire Lord! In this Monopoly Board Game: Avatar: The Last Airbender Edition, players move around the board as Aang, Katara, Sokka, Toph, Zuko, or Suki. The more properties a player can protect, the more rent they'll be able to collect. The last player with coins when others have gone bankrupt wins! Copyright 2021 Viacom International Inc. All Rights Reserved. Nickelodeon, Nickelodeon Avatar: The Last Airbender and all related titles, logos and characters are trademarks of Viacom International Inc. The Hasbro, Hasbro Gaming, Parker Brothers, and Monopoly names and logos, the distinctive design of the gameboard, the four corner squares, the Mr. Monopoly name and character, as well as each of the distinctive elements of the board and playing pieces are trademarks of Hasbro for its property trading game and game equipment.
family: TV Shows: Avatar The Last Airbender/Legend of Korra
Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Once upon a time, before the Hamster War, all the hamsters in Hamsterland lived peacefully and happily. However, one day the Cheese appeared and everything changed. The hamsters started fighting each other because each wanted to get hold of the Cheese.
War of Hamsters is a fun card game for kids and families, where brave characters like Cookie, Mircho, Lily, Zorkata, and many others will help you win the legendary battle! In the box, you will find 53 pieces of plasticized playing cards, resistant to children's antics.

—description from the publisher (translated)

How to play:
All cards are shuffled and given out to the players except the Cheese card.
Players put their pile of cards on the table with the back up.
On each turn, players turn the upper card at the same time and the one with the highest numbers wins the round and takes all the flipped cards.
The winner is the player who manages to collect all the cards. That player gets the Cheese.

—user summary

Едно време, преди Войната на Хамстерите всички хамсерчета в Хамстерландия живееха спокойно и щастливо. Един ден обаче се появи Сиренцето и всичко се промени. Хамстерчетата започнаха да се сражават помежду си, защото всеки искаше да се докопа до Сиренцето. 
Война на Хамстери е забавна игра с карти за деца и семейства, в която смели герои като Куки, Мирчо, Лили, Жорката и много други ще ви помогнат да спечелите легендарната битка! В кутията ще намерите 53 броя пластифицирани карти за игра, издръжливи на детските лудории.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 松永久秀トリック (Hisahide Matsunaga Trick)
description:
Yoshiteru Ashikaga, who is good at arms. The betrayal of Miyoshi Chokei. Junkei Tsutsui can't be used...In the time of the Sengoku period, will you be the one to conquer the Kinai region!?

In 松永久秀トリック (Hisahide Matsunaga Trick), the goal of the game is to get 5 victory points.

At the start of the round, deal the specified number of cards to each player and you are ready to go. The deck consists of a total of 40 cards, five colors with numbers 1-8. The "face cards" numbered 1, 2, 5, and 8 of each color have portraits of military commanders on them.

The flow of the game follows that of basic trick taking.
Each player plays a card, starting with the lead player. If you have a card in hand that matches the suit of the lead player's card you must play it. After all cards have been played, the "highest" strength card determines who wins the trick. 
What determines "highest" strength can change based on how many face cards were played (0 or an even number, the card with the higher number is stronger. Odd number, the card with the lower number is stronger). Place any face cards you win in front of you so that you can see the number.

Do this until you run out of cards in your hand, then calculate your score. The cards you have won are grouped by number, and for each number, if you collect an odd amount of cards, you will get a total of 1 point , and if you collect an even amount of cards, you will get 0 points. 
Example: You collected three 2s=1 point and two 8s = 0 points.
After calculating the score, if someone's total score meets or exceeds 5 points, the game ends.

Story
Halloween night. The ghosts spent their time competing to see who could surprise people the most. The scariest ghost is forced to give a "trick" or "treat". However, when he becomes the last winner, he ends up in the morning and is eliminated. Basically, try not to be the winner, but only get sweets at the right time!

Overview
Each player will choose one Play card from their hand to play 
faceup to the table. If you have a card that matches the suit of the lead card, you must play it. The player who played the strongest Play card wins the trick and gets the score card. The score cards have positive and negative scores! 
Also, there are special cards within the Play cards that reverse the strength of the numbers on the card. Be wary of special cards and think carefully before issuing Play cards!

A "DEATH" card is placed at the bottom of the score card deck, and the player who has taken the "DEATH" card will not be eligible to score for the game regardless of the points obtained so far. After the "DEATH" is taken (meaning all tricks are taken), calculate your points and the player with the most points wins!

川竜 (Sen-Ryu) is a card game from Japan. Players put together word cards to form spells to summon dragons. The spells must match the form of dragon they are trying to summon.

The designer says the game is intended to evoke the feeling of Chūnibyō, where young teens gain grandiose delusions and feel they are imbibed with special powers.

Played over two rounds, players draft cards, building up a combination of dragon and word cards, to form a summoning chant 'Kawaryu'. Each round, players score points for how well their spell matches their dragon. At the end of the game, the player with the highest total is the winner.

The game features 15 dragon cards with unique illustrations.
user summary

Class is back in session at Monster Girl High School! It’s time to make friends, form rivalries, and not think too much about the schoolwork that is very absent from this game!

In Monster Girl High School, you’ll make long lasting friendships with your new monster girl BFFs, forming a clique ready to dominate the school! Pick your friendships carefully and think about who sits next to whom because one wrong move could spell disaster and tear friendships apart! Can you thrive and make the most popular clique in school?

In Irrgarten der Magier (Magician's Maze), you and your fellow players need to help Linus move through the dark forest to collect thirteen objects before time runs out.

The playing area consists of two levels. On the ground level, you have a game board with plastic ridges that create barriers for movement. As you improve at the game, you can add hedges to the game board to create additional barriers. One player will move the young magician Linus across this board.

But Linus must travel with a star overhead so that he can find these objects in the dark. The second level of play is a plastic sheet that forms the sky. Another player will control a "magic button" that magnetically holds the star, which is attached to Linus, in the sky and keeps it from falling.

On a turn, reveal a route card; three spaces have targets on them, and the other three spaces will be filled with revealed target chips. After discussing how they want to move and starting the electronic timer, two players move Linus and the star (via the magic button) to one target after another until either they've visited all six targets or time has run out. If the star ever falls due to players moving in different directions, the players must first elevate the star again before moving.

Place each chip you've reached in slots in the timer and return unreached target chips face down to the supply. If you claim all thirteen targets by the end of twelve rounds of play, you win!

どろっぷス (Drops) is a "Hide Identity + Trick Taking" card game where you play cards from your hand and pack the drops into boxes.
Boxed up drops will be shipped out if the box has the required number and colors of Drops, otherwise it will not be shipped and will be discarded. After all drops are boxed, the player that ships the most drops out of the 5 colors wins.

At the start of the game, you are assigned which color drop you are in charge of. Your assigned color is not revealed until the end of the game.
After the game starts, in each round, one drop card will be played in order from the factory manager (start player). When playing a card, you must give priority to the colors that have not yet appeared.
Once everyone has played their cards, the box shipment will be determined. If the number and color of drops matches the required amount, shipping is OK. If it is more or less than what is required, it will not be shipped and discarded.

After repeating several rounds, according to the number of players, the game ends and your secret color is revealed.
Successfully shipped drops are counted by color, and the person with the most color wins.

boardgame: By Iron and Blood: The Battle of Koniggratz
description:
“The position of Prussia in Germany will not be determined by its liberalism but by its power … Not through speeches and majority decisions will the great questions of the day be decided… but by iron and blood.” 
– Otto von Bismarck, Sept. 30, 1862

By Iron and Blood is a two-player wargame depicting the final, decisive battle of the Austro-Prussian War. The Battle of Koniggratz (or Sadowa) occurred on July 3, 1866 and it was the largest European land engagement fought between the Battle of Leipzig (1813) and the mammoth battles of the First World War. Koniggratz involved over 450,000 men from multiple European nations and principalities. The battle would decide the destiny of Europe, determining whether Prussia or Austria would be the dominate force binding the various German states together to form a new potential super-power, born with a united Germany. The Prussians under General Helmuth von Moltke invaded Austria in late June of 1866 with three major armies, each following a separate axis of advance. Using divergent approaches was risking defeat in detail, but Moltke counted on superior Prussian maneuverability to outflank the Austrian North Army under Feldzugmeister Ludwig von Benedek. Battles were fought at Nachod, Tratenau, Skalitz, Soor and Gitschen with the Prussians prevailing in nearly all those encounters. The demoralized Austrians gathered their remaining strength in front of the fortress of Koniggratz, hoping that one last titanic defensive battle would win them the war. The Prussians rushed ahead with the 1st and Elbe Armies, engaging the Austrians in their prepared positions. Prussian commanders then looked to the north for the approaching 2nd Army, hoping it would arrive in time to deliver the killing blow. But where was that army and how long would it take to deploy effectively to the battlefield? The race was on – could the North Army defeat the outnumbered Prussians before 2nd Army closed the vise or would the war end under the walls of Koniggratz?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
ワンダフルズー (Wonderful Zoo) is a card game from Japan. Players try to build a better zoo than their opponents, using the special benefits different creatures provide, and tailoring the zoo to suit management cards that enhance the animals attraction. Through card drafting and special abilities, players build up a tableau for their zoo, scoring points as they progress. When the game ends, the player with the highest points total wins, having constructed their wonderful zoo.

The game has a deck of over 90 animals, and 10 different management cards. The game can be played solitaire, trying to build the best zoo possible.
user summary

Disoville Heroes is a dice-drafting, action-selection game in which players use asymmetrical player powers to train up powerful superpowered abilities so they can fight the emerging crime within the town and become the most popular superhero.

—description from the designer

Players try to match shapes and colors or shapes of different colors to get points. There are 4 colors of 7 shapes to match or Matchy Matchy. Get to 200 points to win the game!

In the Matchy Matchy box are 56 cards and easy to follow instructions!

—description from the publisher

In Feast of Olympians, spend Obol to build your deck with fierce monsters, weapons and heroes. Use Swords to conquer your foes. Gain enough Ambrosia, with the help of Olympic Gods, to win the game.

—description from the publisher

The game is a sequel to the fairy tale "Hansel and Gretel". Maybe it's the effect of going to the witch's candy
house. At first glance, it looks like a very cute game of making a candy house, but there's also a slightly dark development that doesn't choose any means to make a house...

In スイーツハウス (Sweets House), players will help Hansel and Gretel by doing one of the following during their turn:

     building
     exchanging
     drawing


The game is like trick-taking, and the player who goes to collects 7 victory points first wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Hansel and Gretel
publishers: みっくすスムージー (Mix smoothie)

boardgame: The Men Who Would Be Kings: The Last Samurai Rebellion
description:
It is an expansion for The Men Who Would Be Kings that explores the upheavals of late-19th century Japan, with its unique mixture of Western European influences and strong local military traditions.

Inside you will find:

     Detailed historical context in the tradition of the H&GL
     New Upgrades
     New Unit Types
     A series of Unique Military Commanders
     Six New Scenarios
     New Leadership Traits Table


—description from the publisher
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description: This expansion for Presidente gets 17 new cards to add to the original game. Includes cards such as "presidential vacancy", "quarantine", "vaccine scandal" and more. (Event cards). Also includes new Power, Moneys and Vote cards.

Introducing Oath to Embers, a strategy campaign game designed for 1-4 players.

Navigate through a 4 Acts of Storybooks, where every decision leads to thrilling Encounters. Engage in multi-phase battles on ever-changing boards, where players delve into a rich legacy story, acquiring new skills, loot, and even memories that permanently alter your character, ensuring each playthrough is unique.

Discover the game's hallmark feature: the revolutionary combo card mechanic. With just six cards in your hand, and hundreds of cards to unlock for your character, there are endless possibilities to adapt your strategy to overcome any challenge. Engage in intense battles on the board, with Heroes facing off against Enemies amidst the fluctuating conditions of the "Battleflux" cards at the board's center.

Choose from six distinct Heroes, each with their own story arcs and unique abilities, ensuring diverse and exciting gameplay experiences. With multiple branching paths and outcomes, coupled with the dynamic mechanics of each Hero, no two adventures are ever alike.

Enhance your journey with the Forteller app, providing immersive storytelling, original music, and over two novels' worth of voice acting to guide you through this epic RPG experience.

—description from the publisher

Every day's an adventure in this hand management game about Letty, a rescue pup from Chicago. In this game players compete for Letty's attention through everyday adventures. From cuddles & treats to car rides & walks in the park, explore all things Letty and gain her attention. The player who has Letty's attention when any player runs out of cards wins the game!

Come join in Letty's adventures in this easy to learn, difficult to master card game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Amonkhet Planeswalker Deck: Gideon, Martial Paragon
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Get started playing Magic right away! Use your courage and passion to assemble a massive army, then empower it with the strength to drive your foes into the ground. Stand alongside the Planeswalker Gideon Jura--with a card unique to the deck--to lead your allies to glory.

—description from publisher

The day has come!  As an Apprentice in the ancient art of Alchemy, you will race to prove your skill by being the first to complete creations from the books of the Master’s library.  Each creation requires the combination of four different components, as well as the correct placement on the arcane circle.  Think fast, outmaneuver your friends, and use your abilities to become the next Master Alchemist!

Alchemist Library is a fast-paced game for two to four people which can be played with in 20 to 30 minutes.  Each turn the players will play a card from their hand, either adding a Component to the board, or using an ability.  However, one player holds the Master Talisman, an artifact which gives them more powerful versions of each ability, which they can then use to their advantage, whether it is their turn or not.  Once the Talisman is used, it is passed to the next player, so use it wisely!

The game is all about set collecting, but with a twist, and while each player is trying to get the correct position matching one of the books in hand.  As the board fills up, matching elements or materials can be stacked on top of one another, to change the layout in your favor, or slow down your opponents.  Every player can also score at any time, so pay close attention to the board, as your friends may give you exactly what you need.  Be the first Apprentice to complete 3 books to win the game!

—description from the designer

Your goal in Bing Boing is to mark off all the numbers on your sheet first. On a turn, someone rolls the dice, then all players use them to mark off the combination they want, with die results of 1 and 5 allowing you to mark 15, 51, or even 6. Along the way, you want as many "boing" bonuses as possible so that you can mark off extra number fields.

In Jungle Dash you will be racing your opponents to complete 2 laps of this jungle circuit! The game is played simultaneously with all players making decisions and moving together. You will pick a direction to move, an item or upgrade to play and then roll your dice to move around the board. Take advantage of every opportunity to finish 1st in this action packed race around the jungle!

—description from the designer

The highly acclaimed micro board game has launched an upgraded version! Add a more challenging table expansion. 
Place number cards in a limited space and turn balloons into numbers you like.
It is suitable for families and seniors to play.
The balloon party is about to start. Hurry up and invite your friends to the party!

How to play:

According to the number of players, divide players into 2~3 teams. Different teams like different colors of balloons.

Every player takes turns placing number card and adding up the number. (There are two colors of semicircles on the number card, and the card can only be stitched together with the same color.)

After calculating, put the corresponding balloon on the number.

You need to place the table at the right time, and try to make balloons of your team color appear in the cross direction of the table to get more bonus.

After the party venue is completely set up (5*5 or 6*6), the team with the most balloons of its own color in the venue wins.(For example: There are 10 red balloons in the venue, red team +20 points.)

—description from the publisher

Transperceneige, the game is an asymmetrical semi-cooperative game. Following a climatic catastrophe, the Earth has been plunged into a new ice age, removing all life from the planet. Only one group of humans has managed to board the Transperceneige, a train that runs around the planet forever, keeping them alive. The players, in command of the different groups of passengers, will have to make choices and collaborate with each other (or not!) to take control of the Transperceneige. But they still have to survive the trip.

To gain influence, you must satisfy your passengers, prepare revolts and achieve secret objectives. However, selfish choices will jeopardize the survival of the whole train, leading to defeat. Can you make the right decisions? Action planning, cooperation, resource management and bluffing will be your best assets on board the train of a thousand and one cars. Welcome to the Transperceneige!

Each player controls a class made up of the passengers of the train. Each class is different and corresponds to a specific hierarchy and function in the train (high, medium, low class). Players will have to manage cars using a deck of cards unique to each player, representing the possible actions to be performed during the game turn. Players will have to cooperate (or not!) to survive together to one of 5 possible endgames: 4 defeat scenarios, one victory scenario.

If the train reaches its destination, symbolized by a "Sun" marker on the central board, the game ends immediately. The player who was the most clever, and therefore with the most influence points at the end of the game, will take control of the Transperceneige!

Otherwise, when one of the conditions listed below is met, the game ends immediately and the game is lost.

     The train stops.
     The train does not go in the right direction (the "Death" marker is reached on the central board)
     There are no more Event cards to draw
     A player loses all his passengers


—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: 眠れない夜の色 (The Color of Dreaming of a Dream)
description:
The world is full of colors, and each color has a name. 
Blue for the sea, black for crows, red for mailboxes, red for sunsets … and so on. Oh? Is the red of sunsets and the red of mailboxes the same red? Is the sunset always the same red in the first place…? In this game you must pay attention to the "similarities" and "differences" of colors and give them new names.

In this game, the questioner is secretly given 1 of 6 colors on a board (variable based on the orientation of a color grid and an overlay) and must come up with a two part name to get across to the respondents which color they are talking about. This name is given in two parts, with the respondents being given 1 guess for each. Points are awarded based on how well respondents guess, with the questioner wanting them to guess it wrong on the 1st guess and right on the 2nd guess.

Example:
Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, and Black are on the board.
The questioner rolls a 5, corresponding to the Orange. They decide to name it “Color of a Sleepless Night”, because they think of someone working by candle light. They first write, show and say, “Color of Night”. The respondents guess. Then they write, show and say “Color of a Sleepless Night”. The respondents then guess a 2nd time. Points are then evaluated.
This expansion adds 7 new cards to the base game: A new breed of Pekin Bantam ("Zwerg-Cochin", 6 cards) and one Predator promo card.

Welcome to Black Metro, a dystopian and chaotic city! Black Metro is a strategic area control game where players battle each other to amass the most power and become the next ruler of the dark city. Gather resources to research powerful technologies, build fortifications and upgrade units. Control districts through military strength and dominate your opponents. Gain massive power through syndicate support and mission objectives.

Each of the six game rounds has four phases: Assignment, Dominance, Resolution, and Standby Phase.

Because Black Metro is akin to a sandbox game, there are various pathways a player can take to ultimately claim victory. Players acquire power throughout the game by dominating districts, fulfilling missions and receiving syndicate backings. Players acquire power at the end of the game based on their total strength, number of researched technologies and stocked resources. Depending on a player’s play style, the ratio of where their power comes from, will vary.

—description from the publisher

A way to play Fluxx for one player from the designer of Fluxx himself, presented as the Looney Labs Holiday Gift in 2022. After removing certain cards (anything that requires more than one player), a single person can experience all the fun of any theme with a solo player version of the card game of ever-changing rules. The list of cards to remove from any given version is provided in multiple formats and a crowd sourced database of player stats lets you compare your score against others in solo mode!

Rule and materials here: Fluxx: Official Solo Rules

In Aves Argentinas you will find a selection of 60 species of birds from all over Argentina.

Each card shows us three characteristics of the species it represents: the way they make nests, their favorite food and the place where they prefer to live.

What is the mission?

Find seven cards with one of these characteristics in common and shout Game! (or flap, or sing, or fly away, all are valid options).

Aves Argentinas is a game to learn about the birds of our country, essential for the natural spaces we inhabit: they pollinate plants, disperse seeds, control pests, and are part of the life cycle of other species. Their presence is an indicator of the health of a natural environment.

El Rey de la Charca ("The King of the Pond") is a board game for children in which you will have to prepare your legs and get ready to jump from water lily to water lily eating delicious insects. Will you be able to eat 5 different bugs to demonstrate your refined palate and be the King of the pond?
The frogs are about to have a feast of assorted insects, a platter of flying bugs? You will have to get a taste of all five different types of insects if you want to win.

—description from the publisher (translated)

El rey de la charca es un juego de mesa para niños en el que tendrás que preparar tus ancas y disponerte a saltar de nenúfar en nenúfar comiendo deliciosos insectos. ¿Podrás comer 5 bichitos diferentes para demostrar tu refinado paladar y ser el Rey de la charca?

Las ranas están a punto de darse un festín de insectos variados, ¿una mariscada de bichos voladores? Tendrás que conseguir probar los cinco tipos diferentes de insectos si quieres hacerte con la victoria.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ステンドおむらいす (Glass Omurice)
description:
ステンドおむらいす (Glass Omurice) is a card game from Japan. The game is themed on making stained glass windows, but is also a pun on Omurice omelettes. The pictures on the cards show different omelettes.

The game has a deck of 36 cards. 12 of them are large 'Om' cards, showing the main stained glass image with six different coloured backgrounds. The remaining 24 small 'Om' cards show four different images in different combinations of colours.

The game has two sets of rules and both can be played solitaire:
かさねおむらいす (Kasane Omuraisu) - Players draw cards and place them to form a large tableau of stained glass. Players are trying to match the colours on the small Om cards to the large Om cards, and place cards to overlap each other. By joining colours together, a player draws more cards and scores more points.

おむらいすいじゃく (Omurai Suijaku) - In a Memo-style game, players are drawing and placing cards to make their own stained glass window. If they draw the same colour twice, they can make extra moves in the game.

user summary
publishers: めめめのアトリエ (MeMeMe No Atelier)

boardgame: UNO Ultimate Marvel: Add-on Pack – Ms. Marvel
description:
With UNO Ultimate Marvel Add-On Ms. Marvel Pack, players can swap a new character into the core UNO Ultimate Card Game (sold separately, subject to availability). The Ms. Marvel Pack has an entire deck of UNO Ultimate cards, with graphics and special rules, designed specifically for this Super Hero to play as Ms. Marvel! This pack also includes two collectible Foil Cards that players can collect or use in the game. Makes a great gift for card game lovers and Marvel fans ages 7 years and older. UNO Ultimate Core Card Game is required -- the instructions for how to play UNO Ultimate can be found only in the core version of the game. UNO Ultimate is best played with up to 4 players. Colors and decorations may vary.

—description from the publisher

Ms. Marvel
What bounces off me sticks to you! Ms. Marvel's fantastic elasticity allows her to make other players burn cards when she adds or draws cards. Her power is great for revenge or spreading out burns to all other players. Keep track of the cards you burn so that you can maximize your Stretch-n-Fetch Wild Cards.

Included in this add-on pack:

     One Uno Ultimate Ms. Marvel Deck
     Character specific Wild Cards
     Danger Deck Cards with Villains and more!
     2 Chase Foil Cards

Primacy is a sci-fi strategy game for 2-4 players, set in a city where the line between reality and cyberspace is blurred. Players represent factions vying for control of the city after a mysterious mind-control program turns off, creating a power vacuum. Seek power through battle, intrigue, public relations, and propaganda.

Conquer 3 types of sector throughout the city for different benefits, including income, soldier recruitment, bonus actions, and access to terminals to spread propaganda. Controlling terminals will score victory points, but won't help you improve your power or position. Balance is key to achieving victory.

Battles are resolved by secretly committing gear cards to 3 different objectives, and simultaneously revealing. Choose wisely between PR to boost your propaganda, loot for resources, and conquest to control new territory.

Each game contains 112 soldiers, 60 control markers, 29 modular hex tiles, 4 3-layer player boards with variable inserts, 4 double-sided faction tiles, a public console board, and tons of cards and credits, all with beautiful illustrations by Calder Moore. The game can be played with symmetrical player boards for an easier teach, or with various combinations of asymmetrical boards and faction powers for additional variability and richness.

Will you build tactical barricades with well-placed discs or power through your opponents with the perfect slide? Either way, it’s the highest score that wins with the 34090 Shuffleshot Game. This strategic game offers a unique competitive experience for the entire family that takes inspiration from shuffleboard, air hockey, curling and crokinole. The objective of the game is for players to score the most points by shooting their discs the closest they can to the centre point of the board. When all discs are shot, the player with the most discs closest to the centre wins!

—description from the publisher

がはくずかん (Gahakuzukan) is a trick-taking game where players first draw animals on the cards.  The suits of the cards are the animals' habitats and their weight is the rank.

After the card play is complete, players attempt to guess who drew the pictures on each of the cards they won.  If they are correct, points are scored.

The player with the most points after two hands wins.

boardgame: アンティークアンフェア (Antique Unfair)
description:
アンティークアンフェア (Antique Unfair) is a card game from Japan. The game offers one mode of play for 2 to 4 players, and a solo variant playing against the game. Both modes come with an easy rules set to introduce the game and more rigid rules set to play a harder game.

In Antique Unfair, players are competing to obtain different antiques and artworks at auction. The items on sale have different qualities (cost/follow/value/points) and players are trying to score points progressively, or achieve an instant win by meeting their specific goals.

The designer says to focus on reading the other players' intentions based on what is offered for sale, and bluff them out of their targets.

In the solo mode, the player is trying to keep fakes out of the market, and build up a special collection. As the player repeats the game, they can learn to achieve higher goals.
user summary
publishers: めめめのアトリエ (MeMeMe No Atelier)

description:
Dungeons & Feelings is the strategy / social board game hybrid where the only way to conquer your dungeon is to open up, dig deep, and share your opinions, stories, and feelings. Dungeons & Feelings is inspired by classic adventure video games and distills their mechanics into easily accessible board game form.

In Dungeons & Feelings, you will proceed through a dungeon of your own design. Along the way, you will fight monsters, avoid traps, and discover treasure as you gain experience, level up, and acquire loot. When you are ready, you will fight the Dragon and emerge victorious!

You will have many items at your disposal to conquer your dungeon, but to craft them, you will need to ask your fellow players to answer an interesting, thought-provoking question about themselves. Laugh, cry, and start a deeper conversation with your friends in Dungeons & Feelings.

Dungeons & Feelings is a 3-4 player, non aggressive board game designed to be played in one sitting. It fuses elements of tile placing, crafting, role playing, with get-to-know-you ice breaker question mechanics quite unlike any game you've played before.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Supermutants: Tabitha and Raul
description: Tabitha and Raul are denizens of Black mountain in the Mojave wasteland. Under Tabitha’s guidance a group of Super Mutant Nightkin have set up shop, ready to create her state of ‘Utobitha’. Her radio broadcasts and night time raids have become the scourge of the area surrounding the encampment. One victim of the raids is Raul, a ghoul mechanic always kept one day away from execution by the Nightkin. His services are his salvation however, and as long as he keeps fixing things, the Super Mutants will continue to have a use for him.

boardgame: The Quest for El Dorado: Dragons, Treasures & Mysteries
description:
Go on long journeys full of crocodiles, rushing rivers, dragons and dark mists... Are you ready for a new adventure?

Dragons, Treasures & Mysteries includes 3 new modules:

The Treasures module provides new treasure chest tokens that each player receives at the start of the game. Each player will choose one to put somewhere on each of the terrain tiles. After all of the tokens are placed, they are flipped face up. If a player's explorer stops next to a token, they may meet a requirement, usually discarding a certain amount or total value of one color of card. If they fulfill the requirement, they receive the token. The token may be spent to move your explorer up to 2 spaces. However, if you collect 4 treasure chest tokens, you win the game immediately!

The Dragons module contains 4 dragon pawns each in different colors corresponding to the cards' colors. The dragons start the game asleep and each on their own terrain tile. Once a player's explorer steps onto a terrain tile with a sleeping dragon, the dragon awakens and no one may walk through or next to it's space. However, once you play a card whose color matches the dragon, you may move the creature one space, potentially moving it out of your way and blocking your opponents! This module also includes new cards that allow players to perform actions relating to the dragons.

The Mysteries module introduces new, smaller terrain tiles with dark mists. They are placed onto normal terrain tiles to add areas of mysterious fog. Once a player stops next to a mystery tile, it's flipped over and the layout underneath becomes visible. The player who uncovered the tile gains a bonus token and may then rotate and place it to create new terrain spots of jungle, water, etc. which are on the back of each mystery tile.
family: Game: The Quest for El Dorado

boardgame: Lion Rampant: A Viking in the Sun – The Mediterranean Adventures of Harald Hardrada
description:
This expansion for Lion Rampant: Medieval Wargaming Rules explores the Mediterranean world before the crusades through the adventures of the exiled Viking prince Harald Hardrada (“hard ruler”). Harald is best known for his invasion of England in 1066 as king of Norway, and for his death at the Battle of Stamford Bridge, which was a prelude to the Norman conquest of England. However, between 1033 and 1042 Harald spent his formative years as a commander in the famous Varangian Guard of Byzantium, taking part in campaigns in Upper Mesopotamia, Palestine, the Aegean Sea, Southern Italy, and the Balkans.

In this supplement you will find historical information, new upgrades (from new tactics, such as Feigned Retreat, to terrain upgrades, among others), and three mini-campaigns based on extracts from Norse sagas and Mediterranean chronicles. The first campaign is a fight against a bandit boss in the Holy Land during an armed pilgrimage to rebuild the Holy Sepulcher in Jerusalem; the second takes place during a Byzantine attempt to retake Sicily from its Muslim rulers; and the third one tackles the early stages of the Norman conquest of Southern Italy.

At the same time, there is no requirement to use Harald and the Varangians. This supplement includes five army lists (Varangians, Muslim Powers, Byzantines, Normans, Lombard and Byzantine Italian principalities), and alternative leaders for each of the campaigns with suggested skills based on their back stories.

The campaigns are also easily adaptable to other medieval contexts and to other rules sets.

—description from the publisher

Your goal here? The four! You have to collect four consecutive numbers as quickly as possible and place them in front of you. The colors of these four cards will tell you what your next task is. Each color stands for a different task. Sometimes you have to bring two pairs, sometimes four cards of the same suit, sometimes a four of a kind. Because each player has different tasks each time, the games are very different and remain exciting until the end. And if a task drives you crazy, maybe a joker will help you out of a jam...

RGB is not a single card game, it is an entire card system based on an old discovery. The discovery is simple and amazing. When cyan, magenta, and yellow ink are paired with a red, green, and blue light only the corresponding ink will be visible. Effectively, one 2d image can hold 3 images and each image can be selected by the player at will. This is the first time that this discovery has been applied to card / board gaming.

The RGB card game system is based on traditional playing cards. The main difference is that there are only 48 regular cards, ranking from 1 – 12 and 6 color changing cards which can also be used as wild cards (you can print more if you see fit). The suits are the colors red, green, and blue. So, keeping that in mind it is possible to play most traditional playing card games but with a twist. It is up to players how they will adapt traditional games to the RGB system.

It is best to use a red/green/blue light bulb to play, it is also best to play in a dark or dimly lit area. The RGB light should be placed above the playing surface and positioned in a manner as to not shine into anyone’s eyes directly. That being said it is also possible to use the screen of a single or multiple smartphone(s) or tablet(s) by displaying a red/green/blue background and using the device(s) as if they were lights. However, using device screens in this manner will not have as good as a result as using a RGB light bulb.

—description from the designer

Skraldeludo ("Garbage Ludo") is an entertaining game with waste sorting as a focal point. Food waste, metal, paper & cardboard and plastic must be sorted and delivered to the appropriate department at the recycling station. While you are in the process of clearing your trash district, you can knock your opponents home, but be careful, you can also be knocked home yourself. Use the parking spaces as sheds. Whoever has cleared their district first and delivered their waste to the right place has won.

The game has both high entertainment value and contains learning elements for both children and adults. The game gives the child the opportunity for strategic and tactical development at the same time as it increases awareness of waste sorting. The game has several tactical layers, so as an adult you can also test your strategic abilities. Playing Skraldeludo with children creates the basis for a dialogue about an important topic in our everyday lives. The game is also fun for adults to play.

Trash Ludo is produced from FSC certified paper or wood. This means that in the production of trash ludo, a social responsibility has been taken, both in relation to the environment and the employees in the production.

The game is for 2-4 participants and can be played by children up to 6 years old.

Knight More was released in Japan at the 2014 Tokyo Game Market and is a trick-taking game for 3 to 5 players. The deck features 4 suits (Knights, Archers, Spearmen, Swordsman) ranked 1 to 10. The deck also includes 2 unsuited Ambush cards. The goal of the game is to avoid getting eliminated from play and to take the final trick of the game.

The game takes place over 6 rounds. At the start of each round players are dealt a hand of cards, receiving one less during the deal in each subsequent round (6 in round one, 5 in round two, etc). A random trump suit is revealed from the top of the undealt card deck. Players will try to take tricks using standard "must follow" rules. The winner of a trick is determined by assessing who played the highest value in the lead suit or the highest valued trump. When following suit, the unsuited Ambush cards may be played at any time whether suit can be followed or trumped. Ambush cards rank higher than any suited card, including trump. However, when resolving a trick that includes an Ambush, if a 1 rank card in any suit is present the Ambush is ineligible to win the trick. If both Ambush cards are played to the same trick, the second played wins.

At the end of each round, any players who have taken zero tricks are eliminated from the game. The player who wins the most tricks in a round will be the lead player of the next. The game continues until one of the following occurs:

1) A player wins the game by collecting all tricks in a round, leaving all other players with zero tricks.

2) A player wins the game by making it to round 6 and winning the final trick.

There is an optional variant called "Guerrilla Operations". The game is played the same, except the first time a player takes zero tricks in a round they are not eliminated. Instead, in the following round they are dealt a single card. During that round, on their turn they may play this card into any trick of their choosing. If they don't want to play it into a trick they may pass. If a player can use this card to win a trick they will re-enter the game in the following round. A player is only eligible to receive the single card second chance if it's the first time they end a round with zero tricks. If a player collects zero tricks for a second time after having re-entered the game previously or fails to win a trick with their single second chance card, they are eliminated.

Each game consists of 3 rounds. For each game, one player is the banker for 3 turns. He gives each player the coins: 2 coins of 10, 4 coins of 5, 10 coins of 2 and 20 coins of 1.
Each player deposits 2 coins into bowls 1-6 and the pot in the middle. The banker does the same but fills the pot up to 12 coins.
The banker begins to roll all 3 dice. Only 3 rounds are rolled. The give/take die determines whether the player has to deposit or withdraw money. The number die indicates the cup you pay into or receive from. The die with the eyes determines the amount of the win or the deposit.
Bowl 1 and 6: Doubles the bet. You pay or receive twice as much as you rolled eyes.
Double: In the case of a double, i.e. if the numbers on the number dice and the number dice are the same, the pot also plays. 
If a bowl becomes empty during the 3 rounds, it is the banker's duty to refill it to the original deposit of all players. In return, the pot in the middle belongs to the banker after the end of the 3 rounds 
Final round: When the 3 rounds have been rolled, the banker only starts with the number die. Each player now gets the total contents of the bowl he rolled. Play continues until all bowls are emptied. In the new game, the next player automatically becomes the banker.

ASL Journal 13 is a 52-page magazine for Advanced Squad Leader and features articles, the latest errata and scenario updates in the Debriefing, 33 new scenarios printed on cardstock (including four “pocket” scenarios, with the map board included on the card), ASL board 77, and overlay X20 (to replace the version that came in the ASL Overlay Bundle).

ASL Journal 13 articles include:

Hatten In Flames: CG I Impressions wherein Gary Bartlett & Mike McGrath discuss approaches both German and American players might take in tackling this Campaign Game.
In (Not So) Free Parking, Phil Palmer uncovers some facts about Hull Down positions and how valuable they may actually be to your AFV.
Advanced Nuggets 2 finds Paul Sidhu & Rich Spilky illuminating even more obscure and easily overlooked rules.
Radio Contact Essentials offers a primer by Rich Spilky on the basics of this critical element of ASL OBA.
Second Impressions finds Rich Spilky musing on OBA draw pile probabilities in this extension of Robert Medrow's essential article in THE GENERAL (and ASL CLASSIC) on OBA in ASL and providing a basis for the “Pleva variant” for OBA chit draws.
Alternative OBA Systems by Jim Bishop explores additional chit replacement methods in this follow-up to Rich's draw pile article.
Ken Dunn gives us a Preview of the Drop Zone: Sainte-Mére-Èglise Historical ASL module.
Forgotten War Designer's Notes author Kenneth P. Katz was a key contributor to the creation and evolution of transitioning ASL to the Korean War, and herein offers insight into that process.
A Tale of Two Scenarios is John Slotwinski’s analysis of the original and updated versions of the YANKS scenario The Mad Minute, revered for its fun factor but notoriously unbalanced.
Per Mare Per Terram is Andrew H. Hershey's follow-up to Ghost Killers from ASL Journal 12 and covers the history of 41 (Independent) Commando of the Royal Marines in the Korean War.
The Debriefing offers this issue's errata updates.

Your ships are being attacked by a Kraken and your only hope of survival is gaining the help from the Mermaids of the deep. The thrashing tentacles threaten to destroy your ships and drag your crews to the depths of Davey Jones' Locker.

Each turn players will simultaneously roll and place their dice on the sheet. Other spaces allow players to use Tridents to fight back against the Kraken while further spaces allow for dice manipulation.

After the players have placed their dice, they will face the Kraken. Each turn the Kraken card is revealed. The players will compare their Tridents against the Kraken, if they succeed, they will earn a Gem which can earn them more benefits for later in the game. If they exceed the Kraken by 2 Tridents, then they save everyone else at the table from the Kraken's attack.

Any player who failed to meet or exceed the Kraken's Trident value will face the Kraken's attack which can destroy collected Gems, change dice for the following round, or block certain levels of Mermaids where the players hope to place their dice.

The youngest player starts. 
He asks another player to give him a card of his choice. If the designated person holds the card requested she must give it to him, otherwise the player takes a card in the stock. If he gets a good card, he can play again immediately. As soon as a player has gathered together the 5 cards of a family, he places them in front of him.
The winner is the player who has put down the greatest number of cards.

An absolute must for everyone concerned (or just plain curious) about health!

At the start of the game, players are secretly and evenly divided into 2 teams (with a couple of adjustments for games with an odd number of players and 2 player matches) and each player has to fulfill missions to gain access to barricade tokens, which will ultimately be the deciding factor for declaring a winning team.

In Obscene - you and other players try and guess what your price would be in these fictitious yet seriously Obscene situations. If players guess right, they get money! Too obscene to list here - buy the game to be shocked and even learn something about yourself and your friends! This is strictly for ages 21 and up, and not those easily offended.

Do you want to create the funniest stories together with your friends?
With its millions of different possible combinations, Миш Маш ("Mish Mash") is a great activity for any gathering with friends. Those afraid to try the game will get countless hours of fun thanks to 176 unique cards that can be combined in any number of ways. The only limit in the game is the imagination of the players and their sense of humor!

Game mechanics:
Players take turns drawing and reading the beginning of a new sentence to choose the coolest and funniest ending.
Play until all the blue cards are gone, until a certain amount of time passes, or until a player reaches a certain number of rounds won.

Components:
Mish Mash contains 44 blue and 132 yellow cards. 4 of the blue and 12 of the yellow cards are blank so you can add text to your liking. Let your imagination run wild!

Искате ли да съчините заедно с приятелите си най-забавните истории?

Механика на играта:

Компоненти:

—description from the publisher

In the game, players take on the role of ski jumpers to perform their best jumps. In each of the jump phases in the form of cards, all jumpers try to get as many points as possible to maximize the length of their jump. A coach can help with each jump, but even here be ready for pitfalls such as injuries or a random wind factor. Jumping will take place both on the large and mammoth jumping hills.
Using kazoos to play songs for opponents to guess.

Find out if you're a one-hit wonder or a musical maestro! Divided into three categories on 80s and 90s sensations, rock hits and chart-topping tracks, this trivia game will have you screaming for an encore!

Set contains 150 cards with 300 questions.

Booted! is a FUN and EASY 1 vs 1 ten minute strategy board game for ages 4+.

OBJECTIVE: Advance all your pawns to the other side of the board...but beware of your opponent's strategies to boot you back!
LEARN FAST. Takes only 3 minutes to learn. Scan QR Code to watch a quick YouTube. Everyone can learn from ages 4 to 124!
1 vs 1 STRATEGY. Sharpen your minds with this fun game!
10 MINUTES. Each game is fast and dynamic, taking approximately 10 minutes to play.
SUPPORT YOUNG ENTREPRENEURS. Created by two teenagers in Silicon Valley. For every game you buy, we will donate a free copy of Booted! to an elementary school.

—description from the designer

In Dire Dinner, players take on the roles of randomly selected minions forced to prepare a six-course feast for their monstrous overlord, Gurth the Odious. It's a cooperative tile-laying game where players have to assemble color-coordinated dishes each round, made with ingredients that often have a multi-step preparation process. There are a hundred unique, bizarre ingredients, unique skills and abilities for each player character, and a kitchen where anything may happen to amuse Gurth - or make him angrier. The goal is to keep Gurth’s mood score high enough by the end of the game so he doesn’t eat the players.

—description from the publisher

Derailed is a game of train to train combat set long after after the wake of an apocalyptic event that tore down the gilded peaks of mankind and replaced it with the capricious rule of the now-unseen Fae. Players will take the role of agents from one of the six great rail factions, assemble their train and crew, and brave the wilds in order to keep humanity’s few remaining cities connected and supplied. There’s a fortune to be made and history to be written by those courageous enough to journey through the rail wilderness.

Part wargame, part worker placement game, part resource management game, Derailed is a fun and exciting experience for 1-6 players with numerous ways to play, adding levels of depth and complexity to suit any player; casual boardgame players through to avid wargamers will find something to love, from the rich setting with a wealth of lore, to a ruleset that's easy to learn but hard to master. Six different factions offer a variety of play styles based on their unique special rules.

The objective of Derailed is simple enough: complete your journey alive and with the most tickets in hand. To do so, you first build your train, hire crew and purchase supplies for the voyage. Six different engine types, eight different carriages and a wide selection of weapons and upgrades affords you plenty of options to create your own unique train. Crew are your resource for performing actions aboard your train, represented by dice. Once you have your train and crew ready, load up with fuel, cargo and passengers to make your trek as profitable as possible. But remember that the rail wilderness is a dangerous place; a place of profit, piracy... and worse…

—description from the designer

description:
Rubik’s Capture is a high intensity board game designed to get your brain and fingers racing at a fast pace. To play, start by searching the colorful gameboard. Find the colors and patterns. Then, capture it with your grid to win. The capture cards show colors and patterns. Flip the cards one at a time and then, it's a race to find the match on the gameboard! It sounds easy enough, but Rubik’s Capture is a board game of skill, speed, and dexterity. Every card has at least one match on both sides of the gameboard- the challenge is finding them! Make sure you find it fast and find it first.

This is a great hands-on board game for 2 people. Both adults and kids ages 7 and up will love this challenging game, so take on your friends or family wherever you are.

An educational card game for children from 7 years up.
 This game includes 85 uppercase letter cards + 6 joker cards.

The title means Think Quickly so this is an educational game first and foremost.

The cards can be used for a crossword-type game and then lots of other variants (including a dominoes variation).

Bag-O-Loot Junior is a game of set building that can be played by 2-4 players ages 6 and up. The object is to get as many sets of cards in front of you as you can earn points and win the game.

You create sets with matching cards. 
yuo can add cards to your sets, and you can take sets from other players. A "Bag-O-Loot" is a set of five matching cards.

The deck consist of 72 cards. Ten of each type of card and two looter cards. You can play a single round which can take between five and fifteen minutes or plays multiple rounds and keep score.

To play with more than four players, just add cards from an addition game. For 5 or 6 players, add half a deck (5 of each tresure card and one additional looter.) For 7 or 8 players, add and entire second deck.

New York, 1977. Hot nightclub Disco 54 is hosting a birthday and only the coolest people are there. But before anyone can 'get down on it', nightclub owner Doctor Disco is found dead in the VIP lounge! Join us to find out whodunit!

The positions of the white pieces (officers) are drawn by lot for each game and are also decisive for the deployment of Black. 
All figures, unless they are in their usual place, have double the possibility of movement from their deployment line and only from this line, depending on their quality and their location. So for example, a knight on square 1a is also a rook (knight rook), a rook on b1 is also a knight (rook knight), a knight on c1 is also a bishop (knight bishop), etc. Any move that contradicts the quality of a piece is called a free move.
The players must always keep the usual line-up in mind. If a figure moves to another square in its deployment row or returns to it in the course of the game, it has twice the possibility of moving from this deployment row if it does not end up in its usual place. The king also participates in the increased freedom of movement of the figures. However, as in castling, he may not step over a square where he could be captured, may not take a free move once he has moved, or evade check by a free move. Castling itself is only allowed in free chess if the king is in its usual place.
Otherwise the regular chess rules apply.

The Game was published in a booklet called "Zwei neue Kriegsspiele".

In this fast-paced board (and safe) version of Russian Roulette, you try to be the last one standing.

You may play risky pulling the trigger or smart by bribing your opponents to take your place. Use energy cards to facilitate your position or make it difficult for your opponents.

In each phase of a round, a player is eliminated and the others gain victory points, until only one remains. Collect the most victory points in a specified number of rounds to be the winner!

—description from the designer

MiniMancala is one of the smallest non-trivial mancala games in existence, although Nano-Wari is smaller and Micro-Wari (played on a board of the same size) needs fewer seeds.

Each hole of the 2x2 board starts with 2 seeds. On his turn, a player distributes the contents of one of their holes, one by one, in a counterclockwise direction over the following holes. Seeds are never removed, so there are always 8 seeds in play.

If a player cannot do their turn because both their holes are empty, they lose.

Perfect play is a non-terminating draw, but imperfect humans often lose quickly against perfect play, even though you have at most 2 possible moves each turn!

CocoLocoColor its a party game where players have six different ways to play with its colorful deck:


     The Fortune Teller: The goal is to be the first to run out of cards. The players must place the cards face down and in order they will have to throw a card while saying aloud one of the colors of the palette of the match. If you hit the color, you collect all the cards up to that point.



     The Snake: The goal is to be the first to run out of cards. Players will have to place a card that says the color of the previous card. If a player cannot place any card from his hand, he must pass his turn. Each player can place as many cards as he can on his turn.



     The Trident: The goal is to get as many trios as possible. Players can put the cards together if they all have the same color or the same word is written on them. The players must always have 6 cards in their turn, so they will have to take as many cards from the central deck as they have used.



     The Duel: The goal is to be the first to run out of cards. Six cards are dealt to each player and the rest are placed, face down, forming a deck on the table. Players will take cards from the central deck at the same time and place them face up on the table. Then, they can put their hand on top of one of them if they can match it with a card from their hand. Every time a player gets a pair, he will discard those cards.



     Pairs: The goal is to get as many pairs as possible. 6 cards are dealt to each player and 9 will be placed face down, forming a grid on the table. The rest of the cards are placed face down in a deck. Each player will pick up 3 cards in turn to memorize their content and quickly turn them over again. He will then search his hand for a card whose text matches one of the cards he had picked up. Only if a match is made will he continue to play. Each player can make up to 5 matches on their turn.



     Stairs: The goal is to be the first to run out of cards. 6 cards are dealt to each player and the rest are placed face down in a central deck. The person who has one of the double cards will start playing, since each stair is formed starting from them. Each player will put a card in their turn and they will be completed according to the order of the colors of CocoLocoColor. In case of not being able to use any of the ones in his hand, he will take one from the central deck.

On the streets of King of the Road, only the law of the strongest is in force. Engines roar, weapons quiver. Who will be the first to cross the finish line?
The aim of the game is to complete an agreed number of laps of the course before the others (recommended minimum: 3 laps)

Add both Wanda Maximoff, the Scarlet Witch, and Vision as they appear in Marvel Studios’ WandaVision to your decks!

It Was Agatha All Along: A fellow magic-user’s agenda leads Wanda down a path of both discovery… and towards darker powers the MCU has never seen before.

This fun and simple game for little ones is perfect for developing recognition, memory and communication skills. The box set contains 36 vibrantly illustrated picture cards, 4 bingo boards and a book with instructions and fascinating facts about bugs. There are lots of bugs to match and talk about, including spiders, ladybirds, butterflies and bees.

Players each have their own individual boards with a shared grid of tiles in the middle of the table.  They take turns flipping a tile over to see if it matches one of the spaces on their board. If it does it is placed on the board.  If it does not, it is flipped face down in the same spot.

This is a memory game for children that also affords the opportunity to learn about the bugs with the included information.
