A serious struggle for control of the candy business has unfolded in the city! Lead a group of characters and use their abilities to build your own Candy Empire!

In the fight for victory, you will need a wide variety of skills, even if they are not always legal.
 Colorful characters, unexpected tactics, original techniques and a lot of valuable candies are waiting for you in the game!

The game takes place in the universe of "Candy Stories". In this unique world, sweets are no longer produced, they are very valuable and there are few of them left, and getting a few is everyone's dream.

—description from the publisher

A game of 10 rounds, a player has to roll the DiiCii to get a number and a color
After the first roll, everyone gets to ask the player rolling the DiiCii, “Are you greedy?”
The objective is to consecutively get numbers on the DiiCii of the same colour. This results in adding on to their scores.
However, if the player gets a different coloured number on the DiiCii the resulting score will come down to zero.
At the end of 10 rounds, whosoever gets the maximum score wins

Blow My Mind is a conversational discussion game in which the goal isn't to win the debate, but to blow the most minds with weird and interesting points while you collectively try to answer the unanswerable.

The rules are simple: Ask the question, talk about the answer, and choose who blew your mind the most.

Made in partnership with Kickstarter, the game is playable with as little as a single card and is generally only available at conventions, starting with the first set of fifty cards at PAX Unplugged 2022.

A solo word game for the ELL deck.

Winner of the 2021 ELL deck game design contest.

Depending on the game mode, you start with 5, 8, or 12 cards and win when you have only 3.

Each turn, make a word and replace all the cards.

If you didn't use a card, take a card with a higher number. If you used the easy letter, take a card with an equal number. If you used the tricky letter, take a card with a lower number.

Each time you use the tricky letters in your words, the numbers on the cards will dwindle, until you have a card with a '1' on it (JKQVXZ), which will get removed when you use those trickiest of letters.

The game will always be won - once a card is removed, it never comes back. For a challenge, count how many turns it takes you and try to get a good rank!

Normal mode is a quick 10-minute game, epic mode can easily take over an hour and rewards you for consistently making long words like 'EARTHQUAKE'.

—description from the publisher

The Circle is broken. Long have the Druids preserved the balance between the natural world and the supernatural, protecting the denizens of both. Yet Time has grown envious and now the spirits of the dead and damned pour out through the ruined hengestones and across ancient boundaries to once more inhabit the land. The broken Circle of Druids will need to be reformed, the unquiet shades of ancestors appeased, and the balance between time and fate restored before the passing of a hundred solstices, or the magic of the henges will fade forever.

In Shaderest, players will assume the mantle of Druids and form a Circle from the remnants of a once majestic order. They will work together to lay the risen shades of their ancestors and the disturbed spirits of the land to rest, and in the process discover the hidden potential of their true power: compassion, sincerity, and truth.

Druids will age, grow, change, and pass their mantles on to later incarnations and successors, combining and matching game elements to create synergies and playstyles. Coordinating the strengths of Seasons, Disciplines, Blessings, and Relics is the key to building a strong Circle.

During each session of Shaderest, the Circle will confront a Cairn, a select grouping of Shades that must be laid to rest in order to restore balance to the land. If a single Circle can complete five Cairns in a row without breaking, they will restore the shattered balance and win the game!

—description from the designer

Curio Black 1962 is a high stakes undercover auction game, lasting around 15 minutes per player for 2-6 players. Outbid, outplay, and outlast the world’s nations to collect the most valuable artifacts, inventions, and deals for your country. The player who escapes the island with the most merit points wins the game.

Players bid on and win auctions to gain points. They then take their turns with playable action cards to try to get an upper hand in the night's dealings. The game is on a set event board, where each turn brings the black market closer to its eventual destruction.

Curio Black 1962 is a fictitious game rooted in dark history—the thick of American / Soviet tensions, the Cuban Missile Crisis, an escalating nuclear arms race—and pits players against the clock of atomic annihilation!

—description from designer

description:
1986. Present day. The Indian Ocean. OPFOR airspace. Across 36 years of technological advancement, can you still prove you’re the best of the best? From its small start in a trailer out back of Miramar’s hangar row and validated in the skies over Vietnam, the role of the Navy Fighter Weapons School exploded into public consciousness at the climax of the Cold War. Hollywood’s flash and fiction can’t distract from one consistent theme — the drive to win. Not as a lone maverick, but part of a team; pilot and RIO, lead and wingman. Together, just like the name — one word, all caps — TOPGUN.

Face an array of Navy adversaries and opposing air forces hell–bent on teaching you very real lessons. Fortunately for you, you are not going it alone! Included are a variety of scenarios teaching the real-life tactics for employing your mighty wings. This game is about combat, so study hard and lean into what you’ve learned. There are no points for second place!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Birds of Prey (Ad Astra Games)

description:
Upon Ni Nan, The Soul Eater's entry into the world of Catharsis, a demon targeted him for a shady deal; however, he beat this demon at its own game, tricking it and consuming its essence. Now, he hunts monsters to claim their souls, simultaneously preventing the world from falling into chaos.

Catharsis Sagas are true solo game boxes that include one character & one boss. If you own the base game, the boss plays up to five players. The game requires strategy, cooperation, and a touch of luck to succeed as the party fights through traps, monsters, and more. You will hunt down the boss while going through an epic narrative. Roll your dice to activate your powers, but be warned, your powers are also your health. You must carefully decide when it's wise to activate a power and how to resolve damage. Spend your morale wisely, or else you and your party will fall to the corruption of Verity.

—description from the description

Pun Intended Too contains 1200 puns, a 1-minute timer, and a scorepad. It is like its predecessor "Pun Intended", but with new puns. (Note: You do not need to have Pun Intended to play Pun Intended, too! - it's a standalone game).

Players are divided into two teams. One player on the team is elected the reader and the others on the same team will guess. "Teams have 1 minute to guess 6 puns that all start with the same letter and score 1 point for each correct guess." Each pun will be written out on the card with a blank somewhere in the phrase. Those guessing need to come up with the "punny" answer for that blank.

Incorrect guesses do not count against the point total. The other team has an opportunity to "steal" once the timer has expired. The option to steal applies to questions that were not correctly guessed during the time limit and those that were not read before time expired.

Discovery is a 1v1, constructed, duelling card game, set in a fantasy world where four guilds battle for control over a mysterious island that’s risen from the sea. Leaning heavily into thematic design, it allows players to pull from faction and archetype-specific mechanics to create their perfect decks, with a variety of win conditions to choose from.

Assuming the role of a powerful Hero, each of your chosen Minions and Spells will follow your every command as you set out to complete your Quest. The Quest system is an elegant marriage of mechanics, art and short-form storytelling that streamlines resource systems and deck building, while offering strategic depth and making the genre more accessible.

—description from the designer

Released at the 2022 Fall Tokyo Game Market in Japan, Fruity Fruit is a trick-taking game that heavily incorporates tile placement mechanics and resource gathering.

Fruity Fruit uniquely does not utilize a deck of cards for its trick play. Instead it uses tiles in 4 different suit colors (red, green, yellow, blue). In each suit color there are tiles valued 1 to 9 in sizes that vary by value. The breakdown of tile sizes are:


     All 1,2,3 value tiles are 4 squares in length
     All 4,5,6,7 value tiles are 3 squares in length
     All 8,9 value tiles are 2 squares in length


At the start of the game and in turn order, each player will draw a hand of 4 scoring cards from a deck of 9 and choose one to keep. When each player has a single scoring card, all unchosen cards are returned to the box unrevealed. Each player will then take a turn blindly drawing 3 tiles from the bag of 36. These tiles are kept secret and are placed behind their player blind. The drawing phase continues in this manner until the bag is empty. Each player is then given one basket tile, which has 4 empty spaces on it where acquired fruit tokens may be placed. On the table is a 10x10 game board with fruit, basket, and insect symbols printed on it.

Tricks are played using the tiles each player has drawn. The game uses standard "must follow" trick-taking rules with the trump suit always being the red suit. To begin a trick, the lead player places one of their tiles onto the game board. In turn order, each other player must follow suit by playing a tile of the same color to the board. If a player can not follow suit they may place any color tile they would like. When placing a tile it must be orthogonally adjacent to a previously placed tile. Anytime a player covers a fruit symbol on the board, a token of that type is taken from the supply and placed in the middle of the table. If covering a basket or insect symbol a player receives immediately receives it. Once all players have placed a tile onto the board the trick's winner is determined:


     If a player's has no empty spaces on their basket tile(s), they are not eligible to win the trick.
     If any player couldn't play a tile to the board during the placement phase, they are not eligible to win the trick.
     The player who played the highest value of the lead color or the highest trump is the winner of the trick.


The trick's winner will take all fruit tokens placed in the middle of the table and place them into their basket(s). Any fruit that can't be placed in an empty basket space must be returned to the center of the table. The trick winner then leads the next trick. If any player isn't able to play a tile to the board due to unavailable space, that player is skipped and the game proceeds in turn order. It is possible for a trick to be comprised of a single tile.

Play continues until either one player is out of tiles or no player is able to place any more tiles. When either happens players proceed to scoring. All players score points based on their score card drawn at the beginning of the game. For each insect token a player has in front of them, one of their highest valued fruit is discarded and will not be scored. The player with the highest score wins.
publishers: るりるりゲームズ (ruri ruri games)

boardgame: L'Ordre de Veiel: Chapitre II – L'Héritage du Roi
description:
This game is the second chapter of a trilogy initiated by The Order of Veiel. This chapter gets deeper into the mechanisms of Chapter 1, with a new combat system, new monsters, new heroes and new equipments. Chapter 2 kicks off the story, quests, scenarios and most of all: choices.

The Order of Veiel is a cooperative game taking place in a dark and complex fantasy universe. Intended for 1 to 7 players, the game offers a team of adventurers the chance to face the merciless dungeon master and his corrupt hordes of monsters. You must do your best to overcome the torments of the crypt. Mutual aid, reflection and decision-making will be key for your journey.

You can either play against the game or against another player. If you face a player, he'll become the dungeon master by creating the dungeon, controlling the monsters and launching events at the other players. If you face the game, all the above are automated.

All the other player will take control of one of the 13 characters. You'll be fighting monsters, avoiding traps and curses, opening chest and doors, spending money to the smith or the alchemist. Everything you gather in this adventure is for the entire team. You'll think together to optimize your progression to have a chance to defeat one of the 4 bosses.

Combats are turn-based. Heroes take action first. They can either use their weapons, use their skills or use a consummable object. Once they all did an action, it's the monsters' turn. Monsters tends to attack the hero holding the defender token. Some of them can choose their target.

The dungeon is a layout of 6 to 15 cards (at the end of the games). Heroes enter the crypt by turning a card face up (Monsters, chests ...). They resolve the card before deciding to go back to the camp or going deeper in the crypt and therefore turning the next card face up. Once they are at the far end of the dungeon, they can decide to face the boss or the go back to the camp. At the beginning of the game, being a coward is the only option. At some point, you'll have to face your ultimate nemesis. Beat him and you'll win but fail to do so and you'll lose the game.

—description from the publisher

description:
A cool financial game where you don't have to purchase boring stocks and acquire boring loans. You will have to trade the most expensive and important thing that an Internet has - MEMES. You will exchange funny jokes with other players, making hidden bets every turn. Player with the highest value on card takes first meme on table. Player with the second largest - the second meme and so on. If you fulfill condition from exchange card, don't forget to get MEMEBACK. At end of the game the value of all cards except the starting cards, in the hand are calculated. Who has more is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Note: Not to be confused with Bohnaparte, the Lookout Games version of the same game which has less cards and markers as well as other minor differences.

The Bohnanza base game is needed. Using new cards in Bohnaparte, the Bohnreich is laid out; each player receives counters of his own color; and play then loosely follows that of normal Bohnanza, with a small difference: earned bean coins are not saved but are used to finance your conquests throughout the Bohnreich. At the end it matters only who has the largest part of the Bohnreich under control. Battling is accomplished using the numbers on the bean cards, which gives new significance to the formerly "weaker" bean types. Designer Hanno Girke describes the expansion set as "Bohnanza meets Risk".

The AMIGO-Edition of Bohnaparte includes 50 Cards, 72 game markers and rules.

Coin Knight is a betting game for 2 players where you will be playing with any 16 random coins you have around your house and a single card you can print yourself at home.
Each coin will a different power based on it's colour or the area of the board the fall on, so try playing with a different set of coins each time for even more variety!

Por fin abre de nuevo sus puertas “El Topillo”, un restaurante dirigido por el prestigioso Topo Chef y que pretende, en su reapertura, ofrecer el queso de mayor calidad a sus comensales. Sólo hay una dificultad, y es que nuestro querido Topo Chef
es ciego, por lo que tendrá que ser muy hábil para recordar cuántos comensales hay en cada mesa para asegurarles así un servicio de calidad.

The doors have finally re-opened of “El Topillo”, a restaurant run by the prestigious Topo Chef and which, in its re-opening, aims to offer the best quality cheese to the mouse diners sitting at its tables. There’s just one problem. Our beloved Topo Chef is blind, so he’ll have to be very skillful to remember how many mouse diners are at each table to guarantee them a quality service.

description:
A lo largo de la historia son muchas las expediciones arqueológicas que se han adentrado en lo más profundo de Mauga, la montaña que, según la leyenda, salvaguarda el mayor tesoro jamás encontrado. Tras mucho tiempo sin intentarlo, 4 grupos de especialistas en arqueología están dispuestos a hallar el paradero del tesoro, algo que acabaría con la leyenda y los convertiría en responsables de uno de los mayores descubrimientos de la historia de la arqueología. ¿Será vuestra expedición la que lo consiga? Pues estad muy atentos porque el espíritu de Mauga no os lo pondrá nada fácil...

Throughout history, many archaeological expeditions have ventured into the deepest depths of Mauga, the mountain which, according to the legend, guards the biggest treasure ever found. After a long time with no attempts, 4 archaeologists are ready to seek the whereabouts of the treasure, something that would put an end to the legend and give them the credit for one of the greatest discoveries in the history of archaeology. Will yours be the expedition that achieves it? Well, be very careful, because the spirit of Mauga won’t make it easy for you...

UNGA BUNGA! is a family oriented card game, where players compete to become the ultimate caveman. Players are required to make items like tools, clothes and fire by matching the cards that make those items and placing the cards on the table next to that item card. Players may also send wild animals after their opponents in the hope of stealing their opponents cards if the wild animal cannot be defeated. When a player has made all six items they are declared the ultimate caveman and they win the game.

—description from the publisher

description:
When the henhouse is assemble and the sticks are inserted, then let the 5 chicks fall through the hole in the roof.
At the start of your turn, turn over a counter and if a colour appears (red, blue, green or yellow), remove a stick from a hole of the corresponding color:

     If the dark chick falls you can turn over another counter and carry out the action again.
     If one or more of the chicks fall when removing the stick, you can keep them. The turn passes to the next player.
     If the chicken appears when turning over the counter, you must return one of your chicks to the henhouse.


The game ends when there are no more sticks in the henhouse. The player with the most chicks wins.

The frozen continent still has many stories to tell. They will be part of a group of documentalists who will travel to Antarctica to record the best images found throughout their territory.

Roll the dice, place them on the image cards and locate the results on the map for 12 turns, divided into two phases, Day and Night.

Adventure and fun will not make you cold. Be the one who gets the best movie: Misión Antártida (Mission Antarctica).

Overview:

You are Planeteers racing across a vibrant alien planet seeded with randomised Discoveries that can act as boons or hindrances. Use wacky Gadgets to boost yourself or delay opponents, in this easy-going, family-friendly, casual race game for 3-5 players with playful 'take that' elements.

Aim of the game:

When the first player reaches the end of the game board (that represents an alien planet)the game ends. The player that has managed to bank the most treasure (victory points) at this moment is declared the winner!

There is also a simple version for younger players that just includes the race to the end, without the complexity of victory points.

How to play:

Players get a hand of gadget cards that are used to propel themselves around the board or influence other players. Gadget cards can be played on a players turn, or traded in at special board spaces for victory points. Light hand management and card 'order of play' dictate the game's tactics.

The game board 'race track' features multiple types of spaces. Checkpoints that act as navigation beacons and trading spots for acquiring victory points. Gadget Drops that replenish Gadget Cards. And Discoveries, which are essentially randomised events that change from game to game, ensuring no two playthroughs are the same.

On a player's turn, they can play cards, bank treasure or take a free move up the board. They may then draw more gadgets and end their turn. Some gadgets can be used out of turn to mess with other player's strategies!

This game is best played casually, and is very family friendly.

4 micro expansions that are included in Power Plants (Kickstarter Edition). They have since been bundled together and available from the publisher.

The Spades micro-expansion provides a set of five spade tokens for you to add to the base game.  Each player will receive a spade token and can discard their token to swap one patch from your hand with one from the Nursery.

A trusty spade will help you dig up the perfect seedlings right when you need them!

The Wizards micro-expansion provides a set of 5 additional wizard meeples (one per player) along with modified rules for you to add to the base game.

Keeping a wizardly presence in the garden ensures that your new patches won't be messed with!

The Sprite Ponds micro-expansion provides 2 new pond tiles with special scoring conditions for you to add to the base game.

Glittering ponds provide refreshment for busy sprites... but overcrowded patches stay thirsty!

The Stewards micro-expansion provides a deck of ten Steward cards for you to add to the base game.

The skillful Stewards bring their exclusive player powers to your games; enjoy a unique advantage that will guide your strategy!

Includes:
2 cardboard Pond tile
1 Pond rulesheet
5 cardboard Spades
1 Spades rulesheet
5 player colour wooden shaped Wizards
10 Steward cards
1 Stewards rulesheet

Requires Power Plants to play.

description:
Approved by the British Chess Foundation, Stratagem is a revolutionary 2 player game of chess which offers all the excitement, challenges and subtleties of the existing game.

Although it takes just minutes to learn, Stratagem takes a lifetime to master.  So whether you are someone who has never played chess before, or a grand master, Stratagem is a game you'll enjoy playing for the rest of your life.

—description from the publisher

The board is circular, with no centre and differs from 'circular chess' by having the baseline as the starting position with 2 fronts. It appears to be a re-implementation of Byzantine Chess, but with only the following changes from normal chess:

     pawns move forward only, but on two fronts
     all major pieces are free to move round the board in both directions and across both baselines
     castling can only be performed with the rook on the same radial as the king


—user summary

Grande Acedrex is a chess-like game, played like chess, shogi or similar games.

Played on a 12x12 board, this game first described in 1283 contains both some standard chess pieces (rook, pawn, bishop and king) and some not present today (Rhino, griffon, lion, giraffe).

Rules first published in Libro de los Juegos, commisioned by the king of Castille, Alfonso X.

Pawns start on the fourth row. Rhinos moves like a combined Knight and Bishop, Griffon like a ferz and a rook combined, giraffe is an elongated knight - and the lion jumps far and agressive.

boardgame: Parhelion: Borealis – Part 2 (fan expansion for Android: Netrunner)
description:
Parhelion is the second set of the Borealis Cycle, which began with Midnight Sun. The hackers, thieves, and eco-terrorists of the Arctic have wounded the corporations; in return, the corporations lash out, hunting down the perpetrators.

Parhelion contains 63 brand-new cards, including four new identities, and expands on the mechanical ideas introduced in Midnight Sun.

Alpine Solitaire is a climbing game for just one player, that can be played with a standard deck of cards. Your goal is to summit a mountaintop by reaching three camps of increasing height, before you are overwhelmed and lose the game. The base game is designed to provide a difficult and replayable experience, but Alpine Solitaire also comes with challenges based around the fourteen "eight-thousander" mountains, which alter the base rules of the game in a number of ways.

Kódem TCG plunges you into the role of a Protector, commanding the powerful Adendei in strategic card battles. This dynamic Trading Card Game challenges you to outwit your opponent by carefully selecting and equipping your cards, choosing the perfect play field, and leveraging your Protector's unique abilities. Your goal? Defeat 10 rival cards while safeguarding your own.

Experience the thrill of tactical combat with Kódem TCG's innovative gameplay, featuring customizable card builds, strategic field selection, and Protector-driven abilities. Dive deeper with Multiplayer and the expansive "Extended Format" game modes.

Kódem TCG: The most affordable TCG in the world, delivering deep strategy and thrilling battles without breaking the bank!

A card game for understand the political, social and economic situation between Spain and Catalonia.
The players goal is achieve the independence of catalonia, given by spanish governement,or get a referendum for independence. The player with most democratic participation counters wins.
If the Spanish goverment made an intervention in catalonia, all the players lose.

Almost nothing has changed in the City version of the Mafia card game since 1980s. But what if there was a new version of  Mafia, where the game experience becomes more diverse, unused and balanced with each card having a special ability. A team of players with 5 years of experience playing Mafia came up with an advanced version of Mafia. Our team has tested this version of Mafia in London for the last half a year. The result is that after playing Fantasy Mafia no one wanted to play the usual city  Mafia ever again. The cards for this Mafia are for sale in two languages ​​​​(English and Russian).There are also two different card designs themed in "Fantasy" and "Science fiction" settings. The cards also exist in two formats - "Poker" size  and "Tarot " size.

Different timeline settings - this version of Mafia has either :


     High Fantasy styled cards
     Science fiction styled cards


In this version of Mafia each Card has a different ability, so up to 21 abilities per pack of 21 cards.

     " After the death a player gains a new card chosen from 5"
     "The Player can choose whom he starts the game as Mafia, Killer or Villager "

Vandée Militaire 1793-1794
Fatherland is in danger! In the west, the Vendeans refuse to fight at the borders and demand their good priests. A terrible war begins! March 1793, the popular revolt and its charismatic generals, Cathelineau, La Rochejaquelein or Charette, upset the young republic. It pulls itself together with Kléber or the terrible Westermann and Turreau. With the game Vendée Militaire, experience a breathless countdown. The Whites must end the war quickly, unlike the Blues, instruments of Jacobin terror.

description: Travel around the world guessing the flags of each country. Each flag has a different value, collect them as much as posible to win.

The wizard students are traveling around Hogwarts; from the dormitories of the four houses, they make their way to the Great Hall — but Hogwarts wouldn't be Hogwarts if the up-and-coming magicians didn't turn almost every challenge into a competition to test who has the most successful house. Whether Gryffindor, Hufflepuff, Ravenclaw or Slytherin, everyone tries to make it to the Great Hall as quickly as possible, outplaying the others and sending them back to the dormitory. Only caretaker Argus Filch makes life equally difficult for everyone.

DOG: Harry Potter adapts DOG to the wizarding world, and round after round, players try to use their action cards to move the figures in their own house from the dormitory to the Great Hall, where they are safe. On the way there, other classmates not only want to overtake them, but prefer to throw them out. With magical tricks such as walking backwards, conjured up jokers, or lucky number witchcraft, they, like the patrolling caretaker, become a real threat to progress. After all, who likes going back to bed when the goal seems so close?

—description from the publisher

Over multiple rounds in Spitze Skizze, you and your fellow players need to figure out how to match the image of a person on a hidden card.

In more detail, one player looks at the figure on a card, while everyone else draws and deduces. In the first round, you draw a stick figure paying attention to four traits: whether the eyes are open or closed, where the arms are, where the legs are, and how the body is shaped. The critic looking at the card writes down how many of these traits you have correct, then you draw another figure, after which the critic again identifies the number of correct traits.

How quickly can you draw the correct figure?

boardgame: Desert Blitzkrieg: Rommel's North African Campaign
description:
"Losing Tobruk was one of the heaviest blows I can recall during the war.” — Winston Churchill

Desert Blitzkrieg is an Operational level two-player board-wargame covering the North African desert Campaign from April 1941 to December 1942. Units are regimental level and Divisional Integrity is key to maximizing the density of your forces on the map covering eastern Libya and western Egypt from Aghelia to El Alamein. The game is of low-moderate complexity and features a ‘Point-to-Point’ system while introducing a number of unique features.

Movement is along three different road types. Limited Port movement is possible. Supply Unit availability is critical for Battles as supply units must be expended to conduct a Battle. Battles use a ‘Battle Board’ with a ‘Front Line’ and ‘Second Line’ where positional tactics to maximize Combat Values are key. Battles can be a ‘game within a game’ and go for many rounds or be over quickly depending upon the player’s decisions. Unit types reflect different capabilities on the ‘Battle Board’ and the main map. A deck of event cards is provided to introduce a limited set of ‘random events’ that will force players to deal with the uncertainty of combat.

While game play is relatively easy, players will be confronted with many decision in a given game turn, with many different outcomes building play tension while delivering great replay value.


     HQs must decide whether to enhance Supply, Movement or Combat each turn.
     Use of one or two ‘Long Range Desert Force’s (specially marked Recon units).
     Player must decide a limited Card draw is to be made (there are only 6 per game, base).
     Supply Units must be used for supply line extension and also are expended for Combat, so decisions about usage are key.
     What boxes to build a limited number of Fortifications?
     How to use the limited air support; Combat Attack, Defense, or Interdiction.
     “Battle Board” management. Deciding where to place units to maximize Combat Value.


Product Information:

Complexity: 4 out of 10 (Low/Moderate)
    Playing time: 2.5 hours for short game, 10+ hours for full campaign
    Solitaire Suitability: High
    Players: Two (suitable for solitaire play)
    Map Scale: Abstract (point to point)
    Time Scale: Monthly turns
    Unit Scale: Regimental equivalents with Divisional Integrity

-description from publisher's website

description: The 60 cards show 60 different people belonging to 5 families located in the Rhineland area named with the typical Rhineland dialect. Each family consists of 12 people marked with one of the 12 symbols. On your turn, play a card face down announcing the person on it. The person must belong to the same family or must have the same symbol as the card played before. The next player may check if his predecessor was right or not. If he decides to check, he reveals the card and the player who was wrong must draw 2 cards as a penalty. Some cards have a special function. The player who first got rid of all his cards wins.

In Unyielding Universe, 2-4 players attempt to make long and beautiful lifelines. They achieve this by drawing "Year" cards and then placing them within their lifeline chronologically, either by adding them at the end or replacing them. All the while the Universe will try to make their lives more challenging and complex. "Universe" cards that add a scoring adjustment or a gameplay modifier will be drawn, or "Life Complete" cards will be drawn and wreak havoc on your lifeline, not allowing you to play cards past a certain age.
The goal is to score the most points at the end of the round. Scoring factors in the length of your lifeline as well as the quality of your lifeline.
Life is challenging and it is up to us to try and make the best of it, even if we must spite the Universe to do so.

"Marvel's mightiest heroes and villains battle each other in a war for the universe. Use your super powers, win this epic battle and be victorious."
— Description from the publisher


In Marvel Total War we will summon the heroes and villains of the Marvel universe using cards. Each character will have an Attack and Defense value, as well as special abilities (we can also use different objects to improve their abilities).

After summoning 3 characters each player, one of the players chooses one of their cards as attacker and a rival card to be attacked. Each player rolls a dice, adding its value to the attack or defense of his card and, after applying the special abilities, the defeated character is removed from the game (and the player will summon a new character in his playing area).

One by one, the character cards are removed from the play area.
The winner will be the player who still has characters in his play area when everyone else has been eliminated.

boardgame: 里山さすてぃなぶる (Satoyama Sustainable)
description:
里山さすてぃなぶる (Satoyama Sustainable) is a card game from Japan, themed on the Satoyama landscape that exists in Japan. Satoyama is the area between arable flatland and the foothills of the mountains (sato 里 means village, and yama 山 means hill).

In the game, players are placing cards in a pyramid, representing the food chain in a Satoyama area. The players' goal is to keep the area healthy by maintaining a good balance between vegetation, herbivores and carnivores.

The cards show anime-style images of the plants and animals as cute girls. Foreign or alien species must be kept out to allow the native speices to flourish. A good balance must be kept, otherwise the ecology will collapse quickly. If there is too much food, the predators will increase and overload the system. If there is not enough food, the predators will die out.

Players can win in different ways. By eliminating alien species, they can achieve an aggressive win. By causing the other players' ecosystems to collapse, or eliminating three native species, they get a passive win. By maintaining a system and scoring more points, they gain a judgement win.

The designer likens the game to Tsume Shogi, which are Shogi puzzles in which a player must force a win or lose immediately. The game is designed to show the fragile nature of ecosystems in Japan.

The game can be played solitaire. A revised edition was published with extra cards, improved graphic design, added  trivia and a supplementary set of rules by the designer.

publishers: めめめのアトリエ (MeMeMe No Atelier)

description:
This is a small version of Chivalry, on a board of 80 squares. Each side has six generals and fourteen regiments that start at opposite sides of the board. Players take turns by either making a Move or a Jump.

Generals can go in any direction, one square at a time, or by jumping over another game piece or pieces (own or opponent). 

Regiments can only move and jump forward (toward the opponent).

If a player jumps over an opponent’s piece or pieces, as in checkers, it is taken off the board.

And as in Chinese checkers, if jumping over a player’s own piece, it remains. Setting up opportunities to traverse the board towards the opponent’s Forts is advantageous.

Goal is the same, though: to capture both of the opponent's forts.

Lily Hop is a quirky and strategic 2-4 player game of hopping about as frogs and catching flies! The game consists of various types of tokens (lily pads), that each present a different pond creature that possesses a special power and ability. While some lily pads will aid a frog (the player) in collecting flies, others will hinder them in accomplishing this goal.

Lily pads are organized in circles and function as constantly changing rondels, leading to a constantly shifting game with strategies and plans changing turn-to-turn.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Beyond the Sun: Leaders of the New Dawn
description:
Following the discovery of hyperspace travel in the year 2246, a renewed sense of unity and purpose ripples across the whole solar system. The factions in power need to position themselves quickly to seize this golden opportunity, and strong leadership is necessary to get ahead in the race to colonize the galaxy.

Thanks to the Omniweb Engine, a data-mining network that covers the entire population of the solar system, each faction can search with pinpoint precision for candidates with the exact skills and traits they need, both domestically and militarily. Can your faction find and groom leaders with the perfect combination of internal ingenuity and external prowess to rise above the others?

—description from the publisher

Beyond the Sun: Leaders of the New Dawn expands core game with a new solo mode, asymmetric factions and leaders, and new viable strategies around the planetary area control board.

description:
Hold on, Earth! is a board game in which participants assume the role of Bulgarian heroes in search of lost fairy tales. They wander through the areas where the map of Bulgaria is divided and try to save the world from dark forces. They must face the horrors that lurk everywhere in order to reach the lost tales and gain influence over the various locales.

Information from the publisher:
Hold on, Earth! is a family strategy game for 3 - 5 players.
The main goal in this fantasy world is to find the lost fairy tales. But horrors lurk everywhere, and the people are divided. With cunning, strategy, and no small amount of luck, players will have to discover the fairy tales and save the world from dark forces!
Players move their characters around the playing field in a predetermined order and aim to find the most fairy tales. However, this is not so easy! Instead of a fairy tale, you may find yourself face to face with а horror, misfortune may happen to you, or good fortune may smile upon you. Characters can also gain influence over localities, which will bring them extra points at the end of the game.

Дръж се, Земьо! е настолна игра, в която участниците влизат в ролята на български герои в търсене на изгубени приказки. Те странстват из месностите, на които е разделена картата на България и се опитват да спасят света от мрачните сили. Те трябва да се изправят пред страшилищата, които дебнат навсякъде, за да достигнат до изгубените приказки и да спечелят влияние над различните местности.

Информация от издателя:
Дръж се, Земьо! е семейна стратегическа игра за 3 - 5 играчи.
Основната цел в този фантастичен свят е да се намерят изгубените приказки. Но навсякъде дебнат страшилища, а и народът е разединен. С хитрост, стратегия и не малка доза късмет играчите ще трябва да открият приказките и да спасят света от мрачните сили!
Играчите местят своите герои по игралното поле по предварително определен ред и се стремят да намерят най-много приказки. Това обаче не е толкова лесно! Вместо приказка може да се озовеш лице в лице със страшилище, да ти се случи неволя или пък добрата съдба да ти се усмихне. Героите могат да печелят и влияние над местностите, което да им донесе допълнителни точки в края на играта.

I'm not afraid is a game of obtaining majority for your cause. Even further north than the lands of House Stark lays The Wall. This imposing edifice protects the relatively calm, rich, and fertile lands from the much wilder civilizations that live beyond it. Manning The Wall are the Night’s Watch. Though they may come from a myriad of origins, they are no rag-tag group, training relentlessly.
