boardgame: たべる（られる）Eat to live, not live to eat.
description: Eating (Eat to live, not live to eat.) The god of the savanna was furious. to greedy animals. One morning, a black panther, a mouse, a hyena and a lion wake up to find that their bodies have been replaced. A black panther becomes a lion, a mouse becomes a black panther, a hyena becomes a mouse, and a lion becomes a hyena. I don't know the work of the savanna god. In order to return to my original form, I must suppress the wrath of the savanna god and abandon my greed. The real intention hidden behind a simple game where you just eat meat. What is the motive of the savanna god... It's a simple game where the player becomes an animal and eats meat. However, the player who ate the most meat at the end of the game is fat and will be eaten and dropped out. Let's fight well and aim for the second place.

The players take turns being the captioner.
The captioner chooses a photograph card from their hand and captions it, after which each other player (the viewers) will find a card that matches the caption.

After that, each viewer tries to guess which card is the captioner's, pointing to their candidate. The captioner may then point to the most interesting alternative card.

Points: If everybody guesses the captioner's card, the captioner gets 0 points and everybody else gets 2.
If nobody guesses the captioner's card, the captioner loses a point.
If at least one, but not all viewers guess correctly, the captioner gets three points and the correct viewers each get 2.

Also, viewers whose cards have been pointed to, get a point for each finger pointing to them.

Special rule: Two of the photographs show a camera. If one of these is played, points for the round are doubled. If both, quadrupled.

Game ends when someone has a pre-agreed number of points, e.g. 36.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Karstark Loyalists
description:
House Stark has many faithful bannermen that gladly march to war alongside the major house. One of the most stalwart allies is House Karstark. The lesser House was originally founded as an offshoot of the major. House Karstark’s soldiers are generally kitted out with flails and shields, making them rather deadly in combat. Though, truly, their greatest weapon is the conviction of House Stark’s cause, meaning they will fight valiantly to the bitter end whenever possible.

The Karstark Loyalists box set for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives House Stark players a new melee unit to field on the tabletop. While the troops are moderately armed and armored, it’s their faith in House Stark’s cause that can see them through combat. When joined by a Loyalist Captain, their zeal reaches almost religious levels, cutting down their enemies in a flurry of attacks.

Approximate Height
Captain: 44mm
Bannerman: 57mm
Loyalist A: 38mm
Loyalist B: 40mm
Loyalist C: 41mm
Loyalist D: 50mm

Contents:
13 Miniatures
  ‣ 1 Bannerman
  ‣ 1 Captain
  ‣ 11 Loyalists
1 Unit Card
1 Attachment Card
1 Movement Tray (120x140mm)
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description:
キャンディポット (Candypot) is a shedding game from japan. Players are dealt two lollipops they must dispose of by either giving them to the candy boy in the centre, or to other players.

Each player begins their round with four cards, each with a different action, that they must choose in secret then show to each other simultaneously. The first two cards allow players to pass a lolly to another player or to the candy boy in the middle of the table, but only if they are the only one doing that action, so if two players both try to pass a lolly to a third player, they deny each other and keep their lollys. The third card blocks attempts to give you a lolly that round, and the final retrieves one card to be played again on a future round, neither of these are affected by other players choosing the same action.

When all players empty their hands, players collect their used cards and continue.

When a player empties their supply of lollipops, they win! The game ends when only one loser remains.

The game is puplished in a small jam jar.
user summary
designers: Takehiro Hosoya (ホソヤ タケヒロ)

description:
Board Game Design Lab Finalist 2020
    


It's Caturday! In Cool Cats, you are helping lazy, entitled felines have the best day ever!

On each player's turn, they must take all cards of one value and assign those numbers by marking off each value on their sheet.  The unique aspect of this adorably addictive flip and fill game is the bingo style play in which you will be able to deduce what numbers might appear as there are only 3 of each value in the deck (including the joker).  Plan your day carefully based on what you are able to get because your opponents might have the same idea!

Each area on the player sheet can earn you style points toward your day or even help you create entertaining combos. Play with your human to keep them sharp (and get sweet bonuses as a reward), sleep in the sun to earn style points and extra numbers, beg for specific belly rubs that only a cat could be satisfied by and eat as much as you can to multiply your style points.  Cool cats need bathroom breaks too-- so fill as many litter boxes as you can for big points!

Cool cats get bored quickly so once you take five cards or more, you are out of the round.  Balance getting large sets of numbers with your aspirations to be the coolest cat on the block and diversify your choices.  Style points can come easy if you plan right, but you'll have to work for them.

Multiplayer is only part of the game, however!  Take on Feline Fiends who specialize in all sorts of feline chaos in two separate modes:  Campaign and Free-For-All.  In Campaign Mode, go solo or bring some friends to take on each Feline Fiend in order from Lucky Cat to Captain Ameowica to the all-powerful Sam and outsmart them in all of their pawfect skills.  Players must work together to reach a point threshold while facing cats who don't like rules and take whatever they want.  Should Campaign Mode be too stressful, take them on solo or with friends in any order you like in Free-For-All.

Cool Cats combines the flip and fill mechanic with drafting, press your luck and deduction into a print and play game that feels bigger than the sheet of paper and marker you are using.

-An earlier version of this game was a finalist for the Board Game Design Lab Challenge in 2020.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Ironborn Reavers
description:
All along the cost of Westeros, sailors know to fear the Ironborn Reavers. Sightings of their longships are met with fear and trepidation. The Reavers are coastal raiders, ready to make quick assaults wherever they please. Now, these pirates loyal to House Greyjoy are bringing their swift hit-and-run tactics inland during all the chaos caused by the war for the Iron Throne.

The Ironborn Reavers unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives House Greyjoy players a new melee assault unit for their forces. They are adept at grabbing loot from fallen foes, getting stronger as they decimate enemy units. However, being lightly armored, a smart commander would know when to pull back and let the Reavers regroup before joining in the fray once more.

Approximate Height
Captain: 39mm
Bannerman: 60mm
Reaver A: 39mm
Reaver B: 37mm
Reaver C: 38mm
Reaver D: 38mm

Contents:
13 Miniatures
  ‣ 1 Reaver Captain
  ‣ 1 Reaver Bannerman
  ‣ 11 Reavers
1 Attachment Card
1 Unit Card
1 Movement Tray (140x120mm)
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description:
Mind Game is a 2 player bluffing game from Japan. Players have a simple set of cards and betting chips, and try to bluff each other to win chips by having the highest card.

Each player has a set of cards numbered 1 to 5 and a Trick Star card (effectively a zero that beats the 5). They also have a card marked Big, and one marked Small, and a set of plastic chips for betting.

The players place their Big and Small cards in front of themselves and then place a number card against each face-down. If the chosen cards are 1 to 5, the higher number goes against the Big, the lower against the Small. If the Trick Star card is chosen, it goes against the Big card if the other card is the 5, otherwise it goes against the Small.

The start player then chooses one of their face-down cards and puts it forward, with some chips bet on it. Their opponent must now advance their matching Big/Small card and match the bet in chips. The two cards are revealed and the highest wins, the player taking all the chips. The revealed cards are set aside and players can check them.

If the card values match, they are set aside and the betting chips are also set aside.

Then the players replenish their face-down cards, and move the cards to the appropriate Big/Small side. This might indicate that the previously Small card is now Big, and therefore what the values of the two cards might be.

Play continues with the Start marker alternating. The game ends when the hands are exhausted or when a player has run out of chips. the player with the most chips is the winner.
user summary
designers: Takehiro Hosoya (ホソヤ タケヒロ)
artists: Takehiro Hosoya (ホソヤ タケヒロ)

description:
The Wilderness Boardgame is a memorable, solo, or cooperative adventure experience for 1-6 players. Set in a beautiful dystopic world where nature has reclaimed the cities. The Survivors will have to fight a horde of wolves and a giant Beast to create a safe heaven amid this chaos.

With unique mechanics, such as limiting the number and places you could draw resources, discarding weapons to upgrade new ones, limiting the number of actors per zone, different skill paths for every character, and many more... The Wilderness Boardgame will challenge your communication and resource management skills.

The Wilderness Boardgame is a unique experience as it's a hybrid between the Boss Battler and Dungeon Crawler genres.

—description from the designer

boardgame: お姫様と結婚詐欺師 (Ohimesama to kekkon sagi-shi)
description:
お姫様と結婚詐欺師 (Ohimesama to kekkon sagi-shi) is a push your luck card game from Japan for exactly 4 players. The title translates roughly as Princess and Marriage Scammer, and one player is the Princess receiving gifts from the other three scammers. The scammers take turns to offer a gift to the Princess, who can choose when to bank the gems she collects. However, if a scammer succeeds in scamming the Princess, they take the gems the Princess has not secured.

user summary
designers: Takehiro Hosoya (ホソヤ タケヒロ)

description:
Rock n Write! is a game where you crank it to 11 and blow the crowds away! Rock n Write combines the control of dice  allocation with a gut-wrenching push-your-luck mechanic that is unlike any other game.

In this roll and write, phase one has players simultaneously use dice to complete their Chords and Scale. Completing those create star-power which allows players to play to the audience in the second phase cards are revealed from the deck, players simultaneously decide if they are staying put in the concert venue of if they need to end the show and hurry to the van to practice some more.

Ten rounds of each will have the players figuring out if they are in fact road worthy rockstars or if they’re still rocking the suburbs in their parents’ garage. Grab your guitars, learn some chords, and let’s rock!!

—description from the designer

A multiplayer science card game featuring the Atomatons, 118 chemical element characters and wild card individually designed by artist Pat Redding Scanlon.

Players ages 6+ learn the elements of the periodic table. First player to smash an element with another of the same type wins the match! Look out for the Radioactive elements - they can also smash other Radioactives!

Whoever smashes the most elements by the end of the game wins!

Game format: Card Game with 126 playing cards. (Card size: 2.5 x 3.5 inch) in a double rigid box with lid and base with accordion-fold rules.

This is a satirical game inspired by circumstances surrounding the Cleveland, Ohio professional football team and the construction of the city’s new stadium in the 1990s. (The rulebook, being written in a comedic and highly satirical tone itself, explicitly denies these connections to actual events.)

Each turn consists of rolling a 6-sided die, then drawing a card from the corresponding “Odd Roll” or “Even Roll” deck and following any instructions to add or remove tokens from one’s player board. When any one section of a player board is fully covered, that player draws a card from the “Build or Bust!” deck, which will either declare that player the winner or penalize them.
Set of 2 special order cards for those who pre-order the Italian edition of Woodcraft.

In Cosmic Chains, a competitive dueling card game, two players build satellite chains in space to provide the best Internet connection for planet Earth. The longer the chain is, the faster people can load their favorite websites.

Both players have identical decks (but with different characters!) and only one action to spend on each turn. They can launch satellites, swap them with one another to move them around the looped chain, and use their special effects to outsmart the opponent or destroy their satellites and break their chain. The competition is real, so do whatever it takes to make your chain the longest!

At the end of the game, players get 1 Victory Point for each connected satellite in their chain. If the chain was coldheartedly broken (space is tough, so it will definitely happen), players count satellites in its longest segment. The player with the most Victory Points survives the battle in orbit and wins the game.

Cosmic Chains also has two additional modes: Superpowers with asymmetrical starting positions, and Space Debris — additional ways to get VPs at the end of the game and gain more actions per turn.

—description from the publisher

Cast The First Stone is a game where it's okay to judge your friends. Each round, a question is asked, and players cast stones to uncover the truth about their friends.

This game is a great way to relive old memories and laugh at ourselves while embracing some spicy gameplay.

—description from the back of the box

The player with the most stones cast their way wins the round, and the first player to win seven rounds wins the game, so the criteria for winning is pretty clear.

Welcome to the black market auction house! 
A place of bluffing bargaining risk taking and bidding against your friends. Buy and sell extraordinary goods at the most unconventional of auctions. 
Acumulate the most wealth by buying and selling unique items from the most fascinating of characters.
As an insider,anonymous buyers have given you purchase lists to buy from four unique sellers at the upcoming auction. Purchase liss don't specify the items you need to buy, but who to buy them from. 
Each purchase list specifies the quantity of items you need from each seller. 
Each of the four sellers is associated with a color red, green, purple and blue on a purchase list card. This will help you decide which color and contraband items to bid on.
The end of the game is triggered when the first player has sold eight purchase list cards. The winner is the player with the most coins!

boardgame: フルーツ＆フルーツ ホリディ （Fruit & Fruit Holiday）
description: In this game, tricks win the prior trick. The trump suit changes whenever a “7” is played. Each card has either a positive point value, negative point value, multiplier, or special star (which increases the chances of winning the last trick).

boardgame: ラッキーメーカー (Lucky Maker)
description: Lucky Maker is a trick-taking game in which cards each have two suits. The winning suit is unknown until all players have played into the trick. At that point, the suit with the majority that matches the "Master Card" become the winning suit. The player with the highest number of that suit wins the trick and scores points.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Highgarden Pikemen
description:
After the death of Robert Baratheon, House Baratheon became a House divided. Robert’s brothers, Stannis and Renly, both decided that they should be the next one to sit on the Iron Throne. As such, different groups have thrown in their loyalty with one or the other. House Tyrell, one of House Baratheon’s most staunch supporters, has chosen to place their faith in Renly, offering their stalwart Highgarden Pikemen to his cause.

The Highgarden Pikemen unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives House Baratheon players who also want to see Renly on the Iron Throne a new melee option for their armies. The soldiers train relentlessly, able to bring their strength to bear on enemies. And such is their conviction, that troops can continue to fight on even if once thought lost. When paired with a Pikemen Captain, the soldier’s numbers can seemingly multiply.

Approximate Height
Captain: 46mm
Bannerman: 64mm
Pikemen A: 64mm
Pikemen B: 64mm
Pikemen C: 64mm
Pikemen D: 60mm

Contents:
13 Miniatures
  ‣ 1 Bannerman
  ‣ 1 Captain
  ‣ 11 Pikemen
1 Unit Card
1 Attachment Card
1 Movement Tray (120x140mm)
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Swoop Bike Riders
description:
Players speed into the battles of Star Wars: Legion with the Swoop Bike Riders Unit Expansion! Whether criminals or freedom fighters, many groups across the galaxy see swoop bikes as the perfect battlefield companions that make their forces even more mobile. The support unit of two Swoop Bike Riders found in this expansion helps players get their forces into the thick of the fight quickly where they can cause the most chaos and destruction before zooming out before the might of planetary authorities can be brought against them. Able to be fielded by either the Rebel Alliance or gangs of mercenaries, these riders add a fast and furious new element to any army.

Contents:
2 Miniatures
1 Unit Card
11 Assorted Tokens
1 Insert Sheet

Welcome to Connieville, a small fishing village that has acquired the reputation of a cursed town. Both the village and its inhabitants hide dark secrets.

Will you be able to solve the mystery that terrifies them and get all your characters to survive?

In The Cottage you will live an incredible story of Mystery / Terror in which your goal will be to get all your characters to survive their terrible fate.

Your decisions affect the story!

The game is designed to be played alone, although it is perfectly possible to play in a group by making decisions together and solving the puzzles as a group.

You will move through different game boards where you will decide which squares to investigate. Then you will read the corresponding paragraphs in the history book and make decisions.

—description from the designer

Once upon a time, things were just fine. You were gathering mercenaries from ruthless factions across the realm to battle it out and claim the dead king’s throne. It was exactly what you wanted to be doing. However, your perfect life has become disrupted: an ancient threat from deep below has risen to claim the whole realm. Now, you must form an alliance with a former rival to defeat this otherworldly evil, so you can set things back to normal.

Work together to fight and defeat the enemy. The game is played over 2 phases. In the first phase you recruit followers and select alliance cards. Alliance cards differs from enemies who you will have to fight and hourglasses which will give you time to think. In the second phase you will collect factions and of course fulfill the requirements of the alliance cards. If players succeed in commanding certain factions and defeat the enemies, they will win together and you can get back to the fight against each other.

In Claim: Alliances players will follow the same flow as in a standard game. The game is played over 2 phases.
Instead of winning the most factions, you now have to work together to defeat the enemies and win the favor of a couple of factions.

—description from the publisher

We may never know what has happened because the current is too strong to let us escape. The water rushes with an unrelenting pull. The world’s ocean plummets off the endless waterfall. What is below, we cannot see for the spray. What is upstream we cannot reach for the current. All we know is what floats past. Civilisation has crumbled and now its edifices bob with the current.

You see one now, a former museum perhaps, whatever it was it looks like it would make a good addition to your city. You wind up the giant electromagnetic harpoon cannon and bring the building into sight. In the distance, on the other side of the flow you see activity. Do they really think they are going to take this building from us? We need it more!

Welcome to the twin cities of Flotsam Falls.

The ends of the earth are just the beginning.

As players you’ll be guiding one of the two cities. Each round the remnants of once great civilisations will slowly drift down the flow towards the waterfall at the end of the world. You’ll use these remnants to expand your city. The player who scores the most points from their city will be the winner.

—description from the designer

Ten Wickets one day cricket inspired auction game. Each player takes on the role of opposing cricket captains trying to take 10 wickets for as few runs as possible. Each wicket you take gives your opponent runs (points) and the player with the most runs will win the game. Take the wickets, pay the runs and don't get caught without ten wickets at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

Super Trooper: Final Conflict is standalone expansion of science fiction fantasy crossover card game Super Trooper.

In the year 2500, humanity is not on a bed of roses. The polluted and contaminated planet Earth has become an inhospitable home for humanity, whose global population has split into two ideological factions.

The Progress Movement professes a techno-optimistic belief in never-ending virtual growth and progress through the constant development of new technologies. Poisonous air and water, along with a weakened atmosphere, have forced this segment of humanity to live in vast, mostly underground metropolises where most of the inhabitants hardly ever see sunlight. They have largely entrusted the fulfillment of their needs to robots and virtual reality.

The rest of humanity, left out in the wilderness, has gradually adapted to the changes. The Balance movement is guided by a fundamentalist philosophy of sustainability and total balance between man and nature. These communities are governed by strict rules regarding the behaviour and lifestyles of their members, which are enforced by severe penalties. Zero waste and near 100% recycling requires a demanding lifestyle, but contributes to their resilience.

In the metropolises of Progress, people had no idea that any civilized society could survive outside. When diminishing geothermal resources, coupled with fluctuating solar activity, began to cause increasingly frequent energy problems, they were forced to send explorers into the wilderness in search of new resources. To their surprise, vegetation returned to parts of the surface in the form of radioactive jungle, and they quickly began to replenish the energy necessary to run their cities by burning the biomass they harvested there.

The clash between Balance and Progress was not long in coming. Any hope of reconciliation is completely unthinkable for ideological reasons. The last clash in human history, the final conflict, begins.

Increase your knowledge of the WW1 terrain.

The Map Cards must be shuffled, cut and dealt in exactly the same way as ordinary playing cards; an equal number of cards being dealt to each player. The player to left of dealer lays a card upon the table, the next player must follow with a card on which the portion of map adjoins any side of the four sides of the card upon the table; if the player is unable to do this from the cards in his possession, or his knowledge of geography is insufficient to enable him to judge which card adjoins the card already out the player must call “ Pass,” and the next player must endeavour to play an adjoining card and so on.
Should a player play a card which does not join he must take back the card and ‘”Pass”.
No cards may be placed near to those on the table for the purpose of discovering the adjoining card, any player doing so forfeits his turn.
Only 30 seconds are allowed for consideration of the card to play.
Should there be one or two cards in excess when an equal number of cards have been dealt to each player, the remaining cards are placed on the table to start the game and the player can play the adjoining card.
The player who first places all his cards is the winner.

—user summary

In a world where technology that saved humanity from extinction is humanity's greatest enemy, the greatest war of all time is waged between two species. And people are about to lose this war. The last hope is a group of people leaving everything behind. If this mission fails, the world will be a very different place.

Klon, the new entertainment of crowded groups with its different characters, offers its players a different scenario in each game. Finding the clones hiding among humans is the only way to successfully finish your mission. Clones, on the other hand, must hide their identities and prevent humans from winning the war. Be careful who you trust. When you reach the final stage, your best friend may turn out to be a clone.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
publishers: Koz Oyuncak Sanayi ve Ticaret Limited Sirketi

description:
Centivide is a math based card game where players participate in up to 6 rounds where 3 numbers between 1-13 are used to Add/Subtract/Multiply/Divide their way to 100 at the end of the game. While performing their operations they will gain points each time they hit 100 points or use division.

—description from the designer

The Measure of the Earth is a small and simple solo postcard game published as a freebie by SNAFU Design, as a gift to customers of SNAFU Store.

In 1736, the Paris Academy of Sciences organized a geodetic mission to Lapland and another one to the Real Audiencia de Quito (now Ecuador). The goal: to measure the length of a degree of meridian at each location, to compare them, and to determine the shape of the Earth, whether flattened by the poles or by the equator.

In the game, the player controls the members of the expedition to Ecuador and, using scarce Action Points, must triangulate the distance between Quito and Cuenca and perform gravity measures, with the best accuracy possible. If the player manages to triangulate a distance long enough and with good accuracy, the mission will be considered a success.

—description from the designer

A lighthearted competition between Ol' Lucy and a mischief of mice. Ol’ Lucy must protect her home from a band of mice that are intent on stealing cheese. The mice are trying to nab as much as possible, as quietly as they can, to keep the feline guardian from spotting them!

Hide & Squeak is a hidden-movement game for two players.

The Greatest Show takes you into the extraordinary and magical world of the circus, where you meet real Polish artists. Among them there will be illusionists, acrobats, jugglers and many other freaks known not only from the Polish circus scene, but also from Got Talent, YouTube and TikTok.

In the game, you take on the role of a circus director, who plans to create the greatest show in the history of entertainment. You will recruit artists for your show, and on the way to your goal, numerous castings, advertisements to increase popularity, and sometimes you will happen to plant a pig on other players.

The Greatest Show game promotes the circus without animals!

—description from the designer

Long ago, the Great State of Ravenshire sent geologists to find new resources.
Nardir Badger went to no-man’s land, an unfriendly, desolate land, where in the near mountains he discovered  a new valuable resource that in the future, was about to change the world as it allowed for rapid technological development. That resource was coal. 
In his honor the land was named Nardir.
News of the discovery rapidly reached the nearby nations of Calestia and Awhar. All three nations created a few settlements around the mountain, and as time passed, they grew into large cities.
New mining technologies emerged thanks to the cooperation of Ravenshire and Awhar allowing for rapid economic growth and development. Calestia was not pleased with such a turn of events and wanted to take over the resources for themselves. Under General Marcus Zaini’s command, it attacked the Brukas settlement. Only thanks to joint defense was the attack pushed back, this is how multiple years of the Great War began.
Through the mutual effort of Ravenshire and Awhar under the command of Sybilla Hawk and Sebastian Harriden, Calestia was defeated.
After the war was over, Ravenshire, Awhar, and Calestia decided to cooperate to secure further development and prosperity.
In this world of difficult negotiations and politics, in striving for peace and setting the terms of the covenant, a major role was played by barons of four factions: the Steambridges, the Iron Horses, the Skycarriers, and the Clockworks.
One of their ideas for further development, and exploitation was to build a railroad that  would connect three nations, this would allow them to trade, exchange goods, resources, and technologies, and states would not interfere with their affairs.
Today, as a descendant of one of the Barons fight for influence by transporting required resources and quietly lobbying clerks in state positions. Faction that will have the most influence on the economy - will dominate the market and win – will it be you?

—description from the publisher

You're the producer of Badass Force, the greatest action film of all time to be shot in Bulgaria. International stars at the end of their career, a script that fits on the edge of a postage stamp, and extras who don't speak the language should ensure your next film has a great career on VOD sites.

Unfortunately, other producers want to shoot the same kind of masterpiece. Under the pretext of a story of clones, twin brothers and other North Korean cosmetic surgeons, the same heads are found in several productions at once. No one can afford to use real stars, so each project hits the screens at the same time, and to decide between them, you have to flatter the viewers' low instincts by being as bad-ass as possible!
 Badass Force is the story of this fight, and a tribute to the heroes of action movies where it was fashionable to shoot dozens of extras between two crappy lines.

In Badass Force, the goal is to be the first to achieve 16 victory points on the badassometer. Points are earned by eliminating the opposing characters, but a player is NEVER ELIMINATED from the game.
At the beginning of the game, seven different characters are selected from the ten in the box. Each player retrieves a copy of each of these seven characters and a weapon card. Players then choose a character from their hand and put it face down in front of them. In turn, a player will increase the level of charge of their weapon AND choose an action from five options (changing characters / changing weapons / fetching ammunition by throwing the 2 weapons dice / using their weapon / announcing a Character to use their power): everyone plays in turn order until a winner is declared.
Badass Force is a thematic game, with strong interaction in which one must use bluffing, guessing, hand management, and a little tactics to succeed and being declared the most Badass of all!

—description from the publisher

3,2,1, Giddy Up! Ride Giddy Up in 3 fun games - Cross Country, Challenge and Training. In Cross Country - place the 10 command cards in an open space and Giddy Up. When you reach the card, do the command and then gallop to the next. How fast can you complete the track that you set? In Challenge, follow the commands that the horse gives you. Be careful though, do an incorrect action and your game is over. How well can you do? In Training, get used to Giddy Up with this freeplay game mod

boardgame: 組み合わ戦隊ヘンセイジャー (Kumiawase Sentai Henseijya)
description:
組み合わ戦隊ヘンセイジャー (Kumiawase Sentai Henseijya) is a card game from Japan. Players form Sentai, squadrons of fighters, in the style of shows like Power Rangers. Can you save the earth from the invaders from another dimension?!

By combining their strengths, the players defeat a series of powerful monsters and villains. Players earn stars in combat and the player with the most stars by the end is the winner.

The player cards come in a wide variation of types and abilities. The costumed warriors have specialities, such as power, or weapons, and appear in nine different classes. Likewise, the attackers (either troops of low level goons, or powerful kaiju monsters) have their own strengths and special abilities. Only by combining the right classes and specialities, the players can defeat the monsters.

The cards come with a lot of text, so the game may be restricted to Japanese readers. However, the publisher rates the game as age 6+, so gameplay is not complex.
user summary

Claw of Sauria is an expansion for Sauria that adds new and exciting elements to what is already an action-packed dinosaur survival game.

In Claw of Sauria, players can choose to play an all new cooperative mode. In this mode, players work together to complete group objectives before time runs out. There's a catch though, a pretty big one. The Hybrid is on the loose.

The Hybrid introduces new mechanics that make completing your team's objectives incredibly difficult. Because of this, players have two ways to win the game: Complete your team's objectives or simply kill the Hybrid. Easier said than done.

This expansion also introduces 4 new characters that add in new mechanics like healing dinosaurs, attacking other players, or having the help of companions that will fight alongside you. It also adds new items, encounters, events, and of course, many new dinosaur species!

—description from the publisher

In the party game Easy Peasy Lemon Squeaky, players have to react quickly! You either get a challenge or a question: Who can do ten push-ups first? In what decade was Maradona born? Who can be the first to take a selfie of themselves with a yellow object? How deep is the lowest point on earth? Whoever knows the answer or completed the challenge first must squeak with a lemon, gaining themselves a lemon wedge. Whoever completes their lemon first wins!

Easy peasy? No way: there are additional nasties waiting on special cards that you can foist on your fellow players!

What is the name of the largest tropical rainforest in the world? What is the climate of the Marshall Islands? Our earth is a planet with breathtaking landscapes, teeming with vibrant life, but always battling pollution and climate change.

Travel the world in 100 Places in search of exciting knowledge and surprising facts through more than seven hundred questions about geography and the environment.

The raven queen of Rababia sends five animals as messengers to send secret messages to the raven king, whose castle is at the other end of the country...but not every animal can take every path. Lakes, swamps, cliffs, caves, and ravines are natural habitats for some animals, insurmountable obstacles for others.

At the beginning of Rababia, each player is secretly assigned an animal. All players use cards to move the five animal messengers on their way through the raven kingdom. Who can get their pet to the Raven King's castle first? Who succeeds in unmasking the role of the other players and gaining additional points for doing so?

—description from the publisher

Agents of M.A.R.S. is a hidden information abstract strategy game for two players.

Players create a secret objective for themselves assigning colours to certain multipliers.

Following that the players take turns drawing pyramids from a bag and placing them on a board with the goal of trying to either create or prevent the creation of clusters of certain colours.

The game ends when no more spaces remain on the board.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Winter Offensive Bonus Pack #14: ASL Scenario Pack for Winter Offensive 2023
description:
ASL Winter Offensive Bonus Pack #14 (2023) features three new scenarios (WO43-WO45) as well as two 8" x 22" ASL maps (89, 90).

Proceeds from sales benefit the WWII Foundation.

The Kickstarter version of the "Endangered: New Species" expansion contains:

- Celebrity character
- Campaign cards


The race is on to save 7 species in 6 new scenarios. Can you convince the UN in time, all while racing to keep populations healthy and destruction at bay?

This is a big box expansion for Endangered. The base game is required to play.

—description from the publisher

Spell Wars is an intuitive, fast-paced strategy card game that is always entertaining and can be played by 2-4 players. Each player masters the eight schools of magic to defeat their opponents by dealing enough damage to reduce their Hit Points to 0. The deck comprises 64 cards, eight cards for each of the eight schools of magic found in DnD. Each of the eight cards has two different effects that allow for an almost unlimited combination of strategic decisions and outcomes.

You must eliminate your opponents by dealing enough damage to reduce their Hit Points to 0. Some cards deal damage, some help players avoid taking damage, still others heal, but all allow for strategic decisions.

The deck comprises 64 cards, eight from each of the eight schools of magic.

Each player has a number of Hit Points equal to the number of players, but a minimum of 3. A player who is reduced to 0 Hit Points is eliminated from the game.

At the start of the game shuffle the cards and place them in a draw pile facedown on the table. All players draw 6 cards from the draw pile. Randomly pick a player to go first.

Players take their turns in clockwise order. Each player’s turn consists of the following phases:

Spellcasting Phase: A player can play up to 2 cards during their turn and only in the spellcasting phase. Abjuration cards (but not Illusion cards used as Abjuration cards) don’t count towards this limit and can also be played during an opponent's spellcasting or monster phases, even interrupting other cards or actions.

Monster Phase: Each summoned monster may attack and deal 1 Hit Point of damage to a target player. A summoned monster can negate an attack by another monster, but both monsters must then be placed in the discard pile.
Draw Phase: The player’s turn ends by drawing 2 cards.
Used, discarded, and eliminated players’ cards are placed facedown in the discard pile. Each player can look at the contents of the discard pile at any time during the game. Reshuffle the discard pile and use it as a new draw pile if the draw pile runs out of cards.

The last player who has Hit Points remaining wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Barrel of Laughs: Set the Scene
description:
Watch a masterpiece take shape. Use the miniature props to act out anything from simple actions to famous movies and TV shows. Each team has one minute per round to get through as many performances as possible in order to win points. The team with the most points at the end wins.

—description from the publisher

Luda Ludo Games is an advance and improve version of the classic game of Ludo. 
A registered modern version of the regular Ludo games. Our games are unique in design, made with strong material. The game is of skill and strategic thinking which comes with everything needed to play, treated frame wood, quality board with firm glass, a token of 4 different colours – red, yellow, blue and green, and two dices. And it comes with plastic to roll your dice. A game interesting to play. 
This is an inexhaustible fun-filled version of Ludo board game. Enjoy this super Ludo game in series. Take a game, let fill your fun.

OVERVIEW
Ludo is an ancient and timeless board game. A popular game that has stood the test of time and will continue to do so, because it’s enjoy by everyone who comes across this game. A fun and calculative game. It can be played by both adults and children, and it is equally very easy to learn. Sharpens your mind and apply your analytical thinking. Easy to play. An educational and social game. One of the best physical game and can serve as both indoors and outdoors game.

Looking out to have a fun-filled weekend and holiday alone or with your friends and family members? Then this modern version of Ludo games make Ludo even sweeter than ever to play. Luda Ludo games is a fun time and quality advance Ludo series that every one will love to always use to exercise.

HOW TO PLAY
This is an advance dice-and-race board game of Ludo. Roll a six and race your 4 tokens from start to finish. It involves two to four players, players take turns in a clockwise order; the one who throw out the six dice starts the game. This means that a player must throw a 6 to move a piece from the starting point. Capture your opponent’s pieces and send them straight back to the starting area.

The basic rules for Ludo Battle-Yard Game is of the same with that of the regular Ludo. In this case, players race from top to down, down to top, moving forward & backward, following the arrow indicator. Start by rolling a six and race your seeds/tokens by moving to and from in order to strike any available opponent. The first that succeed all his game's out wins. Strategies and calculate your movement, watch out or you get strike. Be ahead!

—description from the designer

UPGRADE TO THE NEW LUDO GAME

Luda Ludo Games is an advance and improve version of the regular Ludo game. Luda Ludo games comes in different Ludo series. A registered modern version. This game is of skills, racing and strategic movement.
Quick to play! Endless Fun! Educative! Entertaining!

OVERVIEW
Ludo is an ancient and timeless board game. A popular game that has stood the test of time, because it’s enjoy by everyone who comes across the game. A fun and calculative game. It can be played by both adults and children, and it is equally very easy to learn and play. Sharpens your mind and apply your analytical thinking. An educational and social game. Serve as both indoors and outdoors game.

HOW TO PLAY
This is an advance dice-and-race board game of Ludo. Roll a six and race your 4 tokens from start to finish. It involves two to four players, players take turns in a ANTICLOCKWISE order; the one who throw out the six dice starts the game. This means that a player must throw a 6 to move a piece from the starting point. Capture your opponent’s pieces and send them straight back to the starting area.
Escape or take advantage of your opponent. Strategies and calculate your movement, watch out, you never know from what spot your opponent may come-out to strike you. Be ahead!

Looking out to have a fun-filled weekend and holiday alone or with your friends and family members? Then this modern version of Ludo games makes Ludo games interesting to play. Keep somebody happy by playing physical board game together ?.

Play Luda Ludo fun-filled games, the newest and amazing Ludo variants on the planet.

—description from the publisher

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Father Time is promo card # KBP-001

Act: 1 Energy: Look at the Top 3 cards of your deck. Choose 1 to put in your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your deck.
Act: 2 Energy: At the end of the Discard Phase, choose a card from your Discard pile and put it in your hand.

Torres Family features two ways to play, with the first being similar to the 2000 Spiel des Jahres winner Torres, but with simplified rules. In this scenario, "Who will be the new king?", players trying to impress the king by building tall towers and being in as many castles as possible.

In the second scenario, "Impress the King's Envoys!", players seek to be close to the King's Envoys in order to present their buildings. If you build for an envoy, you get double the number of points, but this isn't easy. When building you have to pay attention to the color of the fields as not every tower may be built on every field.

Ripley´s Nightmare is a Sci-Fi Horror Roll & Write with a 3 chapter mini campaign.

In this game you are Mary Ripley, engineer and space marine, trying to repair your space ship and fight off an alien species that is trying to kill you and destroy the ship. You have a game sheet for each chapter, a pen plus 5 dice.

Roll the dice and assign the numbers to various fields on the page to fight, repair and manage your oxygene level. All the while the aliens advance on a track and when it is completed you lose. Each turn you roll the five dice, mark the alien progress (if any) and mark your action(s) on the game sheet. Then roll again.

In the first chapter, you need to save the crew and repair the ship. In the 2nd, you fight the aliens. And, in the last chapter, you fight the alien queen. There are also other character cards to have more replayability.

Can you rescue the crew and take all aliens?

—description from the designer

Caged In is a fast-paced 2 player fighting game that has players squaring off in MMA combat.

Fighters will choose from four unique styles and try to knock each other out, submit each other, or impress the judges for a decision. Caged In is perfect for those who like games that are simple to learn and hard to master. The strategy comes from managing your hand and trying to exploit your opponent's weaknesses.

The game features a unique hexagonal box design, dual layered player and game boards with damage cubes that sink into the board and custom designed 3D miniatures.

—description from the publisher

Let's work together to remember the names of the insects we found! It's fun just to look at the cards that use insect pictures.

In a round, you deal a certain number of cards face up and read them aloud so that everyone knows all the insects found, then you deal these cards out face down to the players. On a turn, you need to name an insect that's in someone else's hand. Fail to do so, and you're eliminated from play! If you collectively reveal a set number of insects before everyone is eliminated, then you clear the level and are ready to take on tougher challenges...

What's the highest level you can clear?

Kankeri, an outdoor play that fascinated children in the Showa era, is an active hide-and-seek game in which the parents who protect the cans and the demons who try to kick the cans play. If the demon is found by its parents before kicking the kang, it will be caught. Hide and watch what's going on, and jump out at the right time. This play has many generations who were enthusiastic and played until the sun went down. As the Kans of the world became thinner and improved, they were no longer played, but with the cooperation of the General Incorporated Association Tag Game Association, we have compiled the rules and put them in a single package together with easy-to-play Kans.

“Kampo Therapy” is a Kampo medicine brand sold at pharmacies and drugstores. As health consciousness increases and self-medication is emphasized, we are developing brands with the desire to use Kampo medicine as a gentle companion to your precious daily life. Kampo medicines have an image of being difficult to understand, but we have devised easy-to-understand packages so that customers can pick them up according to their symptoms and concerns.

"Kampo Therapy", which was born in 2006, will celebrate its 15th anniversary this year. In the midst of rapid changes in lifestyles and values, we plan the first card game for Kracie Yakuhin, "A game that makes you kind to people," with the desire to create a society where everyone can live their own way.・Developed. It is a content that thinks about the "symptoms" and "worries" of the body, which have not been the subject of communication so far. In this game, the players are divided into "people who are unwell" and "people around them", and the "people who are unwell" who are unaware of their physical condition are called by the "people around them". This is a game that aims to become aware of your symptoms using the therapy word as a hint.

Especially for women, there are many cases where it is difficult for them to confide in their health concerns, even to close people, and they do not understand well. Depending on the group you play this game with, such as family, friends, and partners, different discoveries and new understandings may be born. We hope that by having people enjoy "being gentle", many people will deepen their understanding of each other, and that it will be an opportunity for a "diversity society" where everyone can live in their own way.

"People who are unwell" draw one from 45 types of "symptom cards" with various symptoms and hit it on the forehead so that it can be seen by all players other than yourself. Let the "people around you" look at the "symptom card" of the "unwell person" and apply the [therapy word] according to the method written on the "approach card" to have them notice the symptoms.

Let's say kind words according to the approach card

"People around you" apply [therapy words] to "people who are unwell" in a method based on 9 types of approach cards. In addition to “experience/empathy,” “onomatopoeia,” and “concern,” the approach cards also include “Narikiri” cards that let you pretend to be your boss, child, or lover. Let's look at. However, when using [Therapy Words], please be aware that words that include the name of the symptom cannot be used.

It's good to cooperate with everyone, it's good to compete, let's find your own way to play!

"People who are not feeling well" use the "therapy words" given by "people around" as hints, and while looking at the symptom list, answer what the "symptom card" they have is. You can only answer once. It is a success if the "person with poor physical condition" can guess their own "symptom card". Regardless of success or failure, "a person who is unwell" will give a therapy chip to a person who thinks, "This person's words were kind" and "He made me notice my symptoms well." increase. Repeat this while alternating the role of "the unwell person". The game has a pattern where everyone cooperates and a pattern where you win or lose, so play with your favorite rules.

Cursed Goblins is a competitive card game for 2 to 5 players. Each player takes on the role of a dark lord who curses their opponents’ minions until only one dark lord is left standing.
Players do not know what cards are in their own hands, but they do know what cards are in their opponents' hands, and what cards have already been played. Players will also give each other hints about the cards in their hands throughout the game.

On their turn, a player does the following in order:
1.    Play any number of cards one at a time. Then, if all of those cards were played successfully, discard any number of cards.
2.    Give a hint to the player to your left or right.
3.    Draw until you have 5 cards in hand.

Each card placed in a player's playing area has a damage value. If the sum of all the damage values in a player’s playing area is greater than or equal to their life points, that player is eliminated.

-description from publisher

Telecraft is a board game jointly developed with Oink Games Co., Ltd. With the theme of communication, memory, and comprehension, this work is a board game using the educational toy Mini Geofix.
Let's tell other players the theme made of triangles, squares, pentagons, and hexagons in 1 minute. that? But it's hard because I don't know how to tell you! It is difficult for the listener to understand what he is saying. However, if you don't make it according to the theme, you won't get points unless you make it according to the theme, so let's turn your head! It's a recommended game that can be enjoyed by children and adults who can't help but laugh because they don't understand what they're telling or listening to.

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: The Expansion Box Vol. 1
description: The Expansion Box Vol. 1 comes with 300 hilarious new cards to add to your Cards Christians Like deck. These combos will have your friends rollin', your parents cryin', and your grandma clutchin' her pearls.

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: Worship Expansion Pack
description:
The Worship Expansion is perfect for anyone who has led worship, enjoys worship music or failed an audition to join the worship choir. These spirit-led additions to Cards Christians Like will enrich your experience with 30 never before seen cards (25 white cards and 5 blue cards). Whether you love Reckless Love or theologically find it problematic, this expansion is for you.

A collaboration with @worshiponline – Cards Christians Like base game required for gameplay.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: Pop Culture Expansion Pack
description:
The Pop Culture Expansion is your guide to all things internet, music, fashion, and gaming. These hilarious additions to Cards Christians Like will poke fun at the trends we experience in American culture. Included in this expansion are 30 never before seen cards (25 white cards and 5 blue cards).

A collaboration with @epicchristiansmemes – Cards Christians Like base game required for gameplay.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: Gen Z Expansion Pack
description:
The Gen Z Expansion is your new trendy addition to Cards Christians Like. Enjoy these lit additions that fr slap. Included in this expansion are 30 never before seen cards (25 white cards and 5 blue cards). No cap.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: Cards Christians Hide Expansion Pack
description:
The Cards Christians Hide Expansion Pack is your new edgy addition to CCL. Finally, the inappropriate humor you miss hidden inside of a Christian game you trust (24 white cards and 6 blue cards).

A collaboration with @memesforjesus – Cards Christians Like base game required for gameplay.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: Dating Expansion Pack
description:
The Dating Expansion is your guaranteed way to finally get someone to swipe right. These 30 brand new additions to Cards Christians Like will fix your dating life so you can finally bring someone home for the holidays to appease your concerned parents. (24 white cards and 6 blue cards).

A collaboration with @dateupward – Cards Christians Like base game required for gameplay.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: Trash Theology Expansion Pack
description: A 30-card expansion pack with the worst theology you’ve ever heard. Does the Bible actually say that or did your grandmother read it once in a fortune cookie and canonize it into your family’s theology? Are they Scripture or Hobby Lobby wall art quotes? You decide.

boardgame: Cards Christians Like: Jesus Loves You Expansion Pack
description: A 30-card expansion pack with all the things Jesus ever said…completely out of context. Jesus said a lot, but did he really say that? People be taking things Jesus said out of context like they get paid do it, they might as well win a game of CCL while they’re at it.

“Boom!!” A big explosion stroked your city and your cellphone ring out loud within 5 minutes. As a member of emergency room, you were summoned back to the hospital immediately. Your team were there already. While the ambulance rush in, your mission is to stabilize the emergency room within 30 minutes. Good luck!
Emergency on fire is a simulation game mimic the urgency and requirement under the catastrophic scene of an emergency room dealing with patients just rescued from an explosion. This game includes 2 modes: cooperative mode or competitive mode, and players must decide which mode to play before game started. If you worked as a health caregiver, you must try the cooperative mode! This mode challenge players about triage knowledge, manpower and resources management and to overcome the 30 minutes count-down with strategy and cooperation. It can be use as education supplement for health-care students, or a table-top training simulation game for emergency staffs. The teaching effectiveness have been proved by a study from a medical center. If your faculty have multiple boxes of “Emergency on fire”, you may start a competition between teams and find out how to manage the most effective of dealing with the event.
    As for pure gamers, the competitive mode of Emergency on fire presents a stage for you to overrun your friend. Not only the effectiveness of strategy matters while dealing with numerous patients, but also be aware of your opponents: they will have chance to occupy scarce resource, accidentally “discover” extra injuries of your patient, or even take over the ward your patient need. For the deeds of your patients! You might have to reach a consensus with your colleges on few political issues.
Now, are you ready for the chaotic reality of emergency room?

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Swamp Orcs Faction Deck
description: The lowly followers of Mugglug will not hesitate to sacrifice everything to boost the strength and staying power of their betters! Feed your vitality to Mugglug, and bear witness as she becomes an unstoppable juggernaut in battle!
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): High Elves Faction Deck
description:
Valeria the Just and her High Elves tirelessly persecute the unrepentant who wield Itharia's magic for their own corrupt purposes.

Behold as Valeria calls upon holy laws that alter the rules of engagement! Both sides must obey those laws, yet only the High Elves seem to find them a blessing...

This all-reprint limited edition expansion celebrating the 30th anniversary of Magic: The Gathering features the return of hundreds of cards from across the history of the game, all centered around the world of Dominaria, where it all began.

Dominaria Remastered reprints 261 different cards (101 commons, 80 uncommons, 60 rares, and 20 mythic rares) and was available (in limited quantities) in draft boosters and collector boosters.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Imperial Dark Troopers Unit Expansion
description:
Imperial armies get a terrifying new addition with the Star Wars: Legion – Imperial Dark Troopers Unit Expansion.

Elite combat droids programmed for maximum destruction, Dark Troopers are not deterred by fear, danger, or concern for their own survival. As a new Imperial Special Forces unit, the seven Dark Trooper miniatures in this expansion give players a variety of ways to terrify their opponents, including a XS-IV Assault Cannon Trooper, an SM-9 Frag Launcher Trooper, and an Mertalizer Trooper. Beyond their standard and heavy weapon options, the Dark Troopers are also accompanied by a new programming upgrade that gives players another way to customize their approach to battle.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Moff Gideon Commander Expansion
description:
The cunning Moff Gideon enters the battles of Star Wars: Legion in this new Commander Expansion! Continuing to serve the Empire after the Battle of Endor as a leader of his own remnant of Imperial forces, Moff Gideon is the cornerstone of a new Battle Force players can utilize in their games of Star Wars: Legion. With the Darksaber in hand and his three signature command cards at his disposal, he’s ready to fight to restore the glory of the Empire.

—description from the publisher
Simple card game where you collect card sets to complete long (or tall) animals which span across 2 or 3 cards.

A mini expansion that can be used with Tinderblox Day or Night.

This expansion adds 4 marshmallow cards and 4 marshmallow tokens.

These marshmallow cards work in the same way as other cards, but marshmallow tokens are not allowed to touch ember cubes.

boardgame: Battles of the American Revolution Tri-Pack II: Monmouth, Germantown, Newtown
description:
The second compilation of earlier releases from GMT Games' Battles Of The American Revolution series consisting of:
Monmouth
Germantown
Newtown (which also includes the smaller Oriskany battle)

These are tactical level battles at the regimental level.
The map scale is approximately 200 yd/hex.

Additions to the original releases include:

     maps are hard-mounted
     packaged in a GMT "deep box"
     rules and player aids printed in color
     all replacement counters that have been issued since the games' original releases
     a new one page matrix-style play aid to assist the First Nations player in Newtown
     Joel Toppen's Solitaire Tactics Card play aid
     a significantly upgraded Germantown rulebook addressing errata and ambiguities

family: Series: Battles of the American Revolution (GMT)

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 132 wins the game. The comic characters Alwin and Otto are featured in this game.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 5/87.

—user summary

You are the high priests of Light and Darkness, and your patron deities have been at war for ages. The fate of their struggle rests with the seven goddesses. You’ve come to the Temple of the Seven to seek their gifts and exert your influence, but beware: the Seven do not take sides prematurely. Whenever you ask one for a blessing, she will give another gift to your enemy. Wield these gifts carefully to gain control of the temple.
Piously is a two-player game of area control and tactical combos. You’ll be fighting to place auras on the board and convert enemy auras to your own. If you form a connected region of auras that touches all seven rooms of the temple, you win!

Two games in one! Cut out the cards and put them together in a circle so that they all form a picture. Watch out! The drawings are continued in the adjoining picture cards!
Any number of players can participate. Everyone draws one of the face-down cards and tries to place it. After the first game, the dice are rolled, with two dice at the same time. If the number on the dice matches the dice representation on a card, the player may take the card. Who has the most cards at the end wins the game.

The game was published in the magazine Frösi Nr. 6/88.

—user summary

Empire in Flames is a solo print-and-play strategy game set in the world of Reign Absolute played on a hex-based map.
You play the Empire and your goal is to eliminate the treasonous rebels through various actions and abilities before they reach your capital and the Doom Clock is depleted.

After randomly placing all Empire and Rebel units on the hex map, you alternate taking actions for the Empire and the Rebels. You roll dice to assign actions to your units and for the Rebels you follow the Rebel Logic board to determine their actions.

You win by eliminating all Rebel units and you lose when all your units are defeated or the doom clock runs out.

The players take turns to roll and move. If a ghost appears on the special die, it causes the pawns to flee in panic. If they stand on an even number (2, 4r 6 etc.) they dash 5 spaces forward, from an odd number (1, 3, 5 etc.) they go 5 spaces back. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

The game was published in the magazine Frösi Nr. 8/87.

—user summary

In a Post Apocalyptic world you must build your tribe in do battle in the wasteland for the last bit of livable land and precious resources.

Build a deck of crazy characters and followers and do battle in this all new spin that combines classic TCG deck building with table top miniature strategy.

Choose your leader and roll your dice on your way to victory.
who will you choose? Cannibal Hippies? Rednecks with gators? Perhaps you want to assemble a team of carnival clowns and cosplayers? So many choices, but a the end of the day its going to be total AnARKey!

—description from the publisher

Potions & Spells is a recipe-building card game where 2-6 players cast spells to hinder an opponent’s progress or gain advantages, while collecting ingredients to create powerful potions.

Potions & Spells start the game with 3 random ingredients and 3 random spells (action cards) in their hand. On the active player's turn they can choose  between 3 different options:

Option 1 - Cast & Collect: Player may choose to cast a spell or not. Spells can attack players, gain advantages or defend against other spells. Next, they can collect two ingredients from the market. Then to end their turn, as spell card is drawn from the deck into their hand.

Option 2 - Brew a Potion: Brewing a potion has a unique ingredient recipe requirement. Recipe requirements must be met with an ingredients value exactly (No more, No less) the player then discards the ingredients and scores the potion in front of them, performs the action on the potion and gains the points indicated on the potion.

Option 3 - Activate: A Dragon Crystal doesn’t carry any power on its own, but collect 3 and activate them by placing them face up during the Activate option on your turn. Activated Dragon Crystals are worth 3 points but also may be boosted by a potion action.

To Win: The first player to get 25 points or more

MIRU is a solo-first hexcrawling adventure game. Set in a solarpunk future, a century from now, you play a character whose brother is killed by a robot. The next day, you pack your bag with a few supplies and foolishly head off into the uncharted wilderness to go kill the God responsible.

Rules 15-28mm for interwar for skirmish battles. 
Command is a important rules, it give to your unit the number of order for turn. Units are divided in three types: elite, regular, militia. Dice used for each are not quite good.

Resolutely oriented towards dynamic and fast games, Des tranchées aux barricades rule is based on original playful choices:

     A “skirmish” format of around thirty figurines where neither the scale of the figurines nor the base are imposed.
     Several special dice allowing you to simulate the level of skills, morale or abilities of the different units, banishing any tedious calculation of bonuses/penalties.
     A game board delimited by squares or zones to ensure the legibility of terrain elements, lines of sight and objectives to be achieved and thus put an end to rulers and millimeter measurements.
     Around thirty skills ensuring real customization of troops and equipment and a budget system to recreate your favorite troops and theaters of operations.
     A fluid activation/reaction system which provides action possibilities for the inactive player, guaranteeing the nervousness of the games.


The book focuses on two theaters of operation: The Great Arab Revolt and the Spanish Civil War and offers lists of armies, vehicles and equipment, heroes and thematic scenarios.

You will also find a budget system to create your own army lists for your favorite theaters of operation.

—user summary + description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A game for two! Each player places a Halma stone on the big "1". They alternately move, in each case to the next higher or lower number. (Example: 1-2-3-4-3-2-3-4-5)
The first to find his way to the finish wins the game.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 1/87.

—user summary

boardgame: Otto und Alwin im Labyrinth der Pyramide
description:
Start is space 1, goal is 78. The dice are rolled alternately and each player places their pawn accordingly on the numbered spaces. If a pawn comes to rest on a labyrinth, the player has to look for the right way and get on or through the indicated space with the next throw. The winner is the first to reach the ladder at the top of the pyramid.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 3/84.

—user summary

Noodling is a form of hand fishing for catfish that requires a person to stick their hand into a submerged hole, a place where catfish often lie, and wiggle their fingers in hopes that a fish bites onto their hand.

This card game captures that theme with rounds of hidden bids that each player can opt to try push luck on their own in a fishing hole or potentially team up with a partner just in case they land a huge one that they need help with!  There is always the risk that you find a monster catfish that you are unable to bring in on your own and causes you to lose some of your previous catch.

—description from the designer

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Forests of Adrimon – Fall of the Ancients
description:
This choose-your-own-path, board game/novel hybrid, offers a wondrous new way to experience the world of the Greenflame Forest amidst the return of the goddess Adrimon. Play through the Forests of Adrimon (Volume II) in an entirely new way and drive the unfolding of a complex and epic story with the heroes you choose at the story's core. Your heroes will be pulled into the narrative and their decisions will guide how the story unfolds.

You will only experience a fraction of the content during each play-through, which means your games can unfold differently each time you play. Gain epic gear, unique campaign-only equipment and statuses, and power level your heroes throughout the entire length of the story. You will find that the allies you make, and the statuses and campaign items you gain may affect the story in completely different and unforeseeable ways in the future.

—description from the publisher
