description:
Theme:
The most important thing that sea creatures is to find food and survive in the azure ocean.
Sea creatures will  die becouse from garbage in the ocean.
We hoped that players can  preserve the environment.

Game-play:
The game mechanic is Simultaneous Action Selection.
Each player picks one card from his or her own deck and place it face down on the table.
Perform the following actions according to the card content: "Foraging"、"Group"、"Food chain broken"。

Goal of the game:
When every player does not have any more hand card, the game is over. Add the total numbers on all the cards that a player earned will be the score.

boardgame: Fuß vor Fuß in die Runde
description:
The sole cards are placed on a pile with the numbers face down and the sports equipment is distributed randomly on the spaces of the track. One after another the players draw a sole. They let their athlete run as many spaces forward as there are dots on the sole. Drawn soles are put back under the stack with the number face down.
If an athlete is on a space with sports equipment, he does not draw a sole on his next turn, but moves forward as many spaces as indicated on the equipment. The sports equipment cannot be thrown around a curve, so the athlete has to take the path straight ahead. Only on his next turn does he pull a sole again and goes back to the round track. However, if he gets on another piece of sports equipment on the outside path, he has to walk straight ahead on the track and then continue towards the finish line on his next turn.
On the high jump space: On his next turn the athlete advances only one space. 
Hurdles: Place sports equipment in front of the hurdle, back up two spaces and try again on the next turn.
Arrow space: Athletes get lost and go straight ahead. Only on  the next turn they return to the track.
Finish space: The athletes cross the red finish line and come to a standstill on the striped space. If they miss the finish space and cross the starting line, they have to do another lap around the track.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 3/84.

—user summary

The starting positions of the discs is not important. The first player rolls the die and turns his disc in the direction of the arrow according to the number of spots. If two colored areas are congruent in the middle (orange arrow), he may roll the die again. otherwise it is the turn of the second player. He also rolls the die and then turns his disc in the direction of the arrow. The winner is the person who manages to line up two identical numbers in the middle by spinning their disc.

—user summary

Two or four players can participate. If you play in pairs, each player receives 10 Halma figures (smowmen) of one color to place. With four players each takes only 6 figures. Two dice are rolled alternately.
If it is your turn, you must place one of your snowmen on the black circle in front of your starting square with every 6. According to the number of points rolled, the figures run from here in the direction indicated by the arrow on the starting space. It is up to each player whether he adds up the number of points on both dice and only moves one figure or places one figure for each die. Only one player's figures can occupy a space in twos or threes. Snowmen, who are resting alone on a square can be captured by an opponent's figure. The captured piece is returned to its original square and must be brought back into play with a 6. Only if you have all your snowmen on the track can you remove the figures from the game that have gone through all the spaces and returned to the starting space. The winner is the first to remove all of his snowmen from the track.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 2/79.

—user summary

As basic equipment for this game you need 10 red Halma figures, 10 blue Halma figures and one green Halma figure.
You place the green Halma figure on the middle black field (it counts as a ball), Halma figures of the same color are placed on the base line in front of each goal, the 10th Halma figure is placed on a black dot in the goal.
Each player takes turns making a move. The game is set according to the Halma rules (one space forward, the figures are skipped). However, in our game, the figures may also be reset.
The player who first reaches a space before the center has possession of the ball and may play it. The ball is also placed according to the Halma rules of the game. It may be played over your own and opposing players. If the ball comes to rest between one of your own players and an opposing player after it has been placed, it is deemed to have been taken and the other player may place it. This also applies if the opposing player comes to a standstill next to the ball by moving his halma figure.
The goalkeeper may only move on the black dots, one square at a time.
If the ball lands on a black dot in the goal, this shot is counted as a goal and all figures must be returned to their original positions.
You can limit the time of the game (2x 20 minutes) or set a goal number.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 7/78.

—user summary

Shuffle the number tokens and give each player five of these. In addition, everyone receives a different colored Halma figure.
These pawns are placed on the "Start" space. Now the first player plays a token and places his pawn according to the given number of points. On the left half of the board blue) the pawns may be moved orthogonal in any direction. When the pawn reaches the orange-colored half of the board, it may be moved only to the right and down. The winner is whoever gets their pawn very close to the target space or whoever has the fewest game points left. The spaces with a drawing may not be entered. You can also split the points, for example, on an 8, move three spaces to the right and five spaces down.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 4/79.

—user summary

For this game you need two dice. Glue the cut-out traffic light discs (red and green) onto a die. One player gets the traffic light die and his neighbor gets the right die. In addition, each player gets a Halma figure.
The game starts at the top left in front of the house. The winner is the first to reach the school with a direct throw.
The player with the number die begins. Then his opponent rolls the traffuc light die. If the traffic light die turns green, the figure an be moved. If the dice traffic light shows red, the figure may not be moved and both dice must be passed on to the next players.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 9/80.

—user summary

Each player alternately connects two dots (cities) of his color and tries to block the way of the opponent. The task for each player is to get from one side to the other, only forming a connected line at the end. It does not have to be drawn contiguously.
Red moves from top to bottom, black from left to right or vice versa. The opponent's lines may not be crossed. The first to establish a continuous line between the two sides is the winner.
Lines are always drawn horizontally or vertically, but never diagonally. The small dot boxes are for practice.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 7/78.

—user summary

Each player gets a colored pencil or felt-tip pen of a different color. The die is rolled alternately. The players can choose their starting space as they wish. According to the number of rpoints rolled, continue counting from your starting space. Color in the space have reached with your colored pencil. Then it's the next player's turn.
If you roll a 6, you can roll again. If you get to a space that has already been painted, you may not paint it over with your own color. It is the next player's turn.
Since you can roll the dice again after every 6, you can add up these rolls if you can get to an empty space that way.
If nobody can use their crayon anymore, the round is over. The winner is the one who painted the most flower spaces with their color.
If you want to start the competition again, this time write your character in each space you have reached. Of course you can also use Halma figures to place them if you can no longer overlook which square each one landed on.

This game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 10/78.

—user summary

The players take turns to roll and move.

1st lap: On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. Whoever reaches space 20 first gets 2 points time credit. The second driver 1 point. All others, depending on their arrival, minus points.

2nd lap: Time trial, the events doesn't count. Points are arwarded same as for lap 1.

3rd lap: same as lap 1

The player with the most points at the end of lap 3 wins the game.

This game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 5/69.

—user summary

You need a die and a pawn for each player. Whoever has a 6 starts. The pawns are moved on the yellow and orange stars. Throwing out is allowed. Only when the token is on a cloud is it untouchable.
If the token lands on an orange star, the next roll, based on the number of spots on the die, is placed in the direction of the star tip marked with the corresponding number (example: number of spots 5 - star tip 5). It can happen that a player has to go back the way he just came. 
The first player to reach the red star wins the game.

This game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 12/88.

—user summary

Slumber Tower is a board game with hidden roles. Three groups of characters (Nightmares, Good Dreams, and Dreamers) confront each other. Each player secretly gets the role of a character from one of the groups. Your task is to figure out who is playing against your group and to wake up your opponents.

—description from the publisher (translated)

It's best to play in a group of five or more people. Then it's interesting to hide your personal motives, it's harder to guess what role you're playing and what you're aiming for. You take a character card and read your goals. If you meet them by the end of the game, you'll beat your opponents and win.

But the others can not only guess who is hiding under the mask of their favorite friends, but also check them: for this purpose cards of Insight are useful. They can be obtained after a die roll (if an open eye appears on the die). Each player rolls the die at the beginning of his turn. The Insight has some suggestion written on it, which you give to the person you choose to check. For example, it says that if the card is given to the Dreamer, he loses his sleep strength. If you give it to a player who is actually a Dreamer, he silently, without showing the card to anyone, drops his sleep token to a lower level. The rest of us don't know who he is, and you've already figured it out - yes, he's a tricky Dreamer.

There are a lot of interesting things in the game, such as walking around dream locations, helping another player fall asleep more deeply, and attacking characters. Yes, yes, the most important value here is a sound sleep. That's why the characters try to wake each other up. A successful sleep attack is minus the sleep strength level.

Don't let the game make you think of peaceful pictures. The battles in it are fierce. That's why it likes both those who have long played board games, and those who love beautiful things, but are ready to dive into the intricate rules. After the first game it will be easy to play, but the first place will come to bluff each other, surprise attacks and real teamwork.

—user summary

«Крепость сна» — это настольная игра со скрытыми ролями. Три группы персонажей (Кошмары, Добрые сны и Сновидцы) противостоят друг другу. Каждый игрок тайно от других получает роль персонажа одной из групп. Ваша задача — понять, кто играет против вашей группы, и разбудить соперников.

—description from the publisher

description:
The hats are placed in the treetop. The die is rolled alternately and the pawns are placed according to the number of points rolled. The pawns start with a ladder on the lowest branch. There the ladder is placed to reach the second branch, then the pawn goes down, get the next ladder, back up to the first branch, over the ladder to the second branch, place the second ladder, down, get the next ladder, etc. Upon arriving at the treetop the pawns get their hats and start climbing down.
The first player to stand on the ground next to the tree wearing a hat wins the game.

The game was published in the magazine "Frösi" Nr. 12/88.

Kill Sample Process is a stand-alone tabletop miniatures game and rpg setting from the creator of The Last War and Forbidden Psalm, inspired by and fully compatible with the CY_BÖRG ttrpg and other Forbidden Psalm miniatures games.  Forbidden Psalm is a 28mm miniatures agnostic game. You can use ANY suitable miniatures you already own, or build custom minis for this game. Solo Play and Coop rules are included. All content is compatible with the CY_BÖRG ttrpg.

Kill Sample Process is a small-scale skirmish game, where you play with a crew of 5 miniatures per player, against AI controlled Hostiles in scenarios that take you across the neon hell scape of CY_. Play as enemies, cooperatively or solo.
Kill Sample Process Is a stand alone game using the Forbidden Psalm rules.

You do not require any other book to make use of this new setting, campaign, weapons, monsters and so much more.
Inspired by Neon, Narcotics and set in a world that should have ended, you will face such horrors as the The Un-Met, living clouds of Nano Swarms, The Machine God and many more hostiles from the  rain drenched city.

—description from the publisher

Boardgame based on a Belgian TV quiz. There are 5 different rounds to play. Depending on where your meeple is on the board you get a certain round question. With a good answer(s) you will advance on the board until someone will reach the final.

From that moment the final will be played. All players receive questions from the final round. With each good answer, the other meeples will go back one spot. This will continue until only one player is left.

—user summary

As the old powers surrounding your Home City look hungrily at controlling the mysterious Silk Road, innovations in harnessing wind and water offer your Culture a chance to connect this wider world of trade partners first. Start a new age of exploration for fair trade, to leave a different legacy than rapacious colonisation.

Race your Adventurers out with silk and lacquer wings, effortlessly riding trade winds or striving against them and packing overland. Earn rewards by expanding your city's shared atlas, whether joining old maps or drawing anew.

Establish trade routes by recruiting Innkeepers along your way, giving future Adventurers rest so they can quickly revisit exotic ports and trade for the silks that give your Home City's edge.

Perhaps you'll even discover the Mythical City and a second home far away. But always remember, there are rival cultures even within your city, and they may not be above a little piracy!

Only the culture that gathers silks fastest can weave a different future.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Ecuador's Bellavista Cloud Forest Reserve! This highly diverse ecosystem is home to one of the world's most dangerous and inhospitable terrains. In this date night, you and your partner will be dropped off in the center of this rainforest and must survive a series of challenges, ending with a perilous journey through the thick jungle and across crocodile-infested waters!

Much like surviving in the jungle, your relationship is filled with unexpected twists and turns. It can sometimes feel like you are alone in the wild, with no one there to help. But remember that you have the strongest survival tool around: each other.

The Stranded in Love date night box is perfect for couples that love to solve challenges together and enjoy a good action movie every once in a while.

Welcome to Homeland – A 2-6 player, fantasy, strategy, and barter card game – where an entire world fits into your pocket!
Homeland aims to fill the void between a simple party game and a complex strategy game. It is perfect for those who gravitate towards strategy games, those who standardly don't have interest in games, families, and everyone in between. It is extremely simple to pick up for first timers, and each round only takes about 15-30 minutes.

The game contains 53 cards. Players will live in one of six regions with a distinct terrain and climate, represented through cards. You also have "tamed beasts" cards with which you maximize your strength through combinations of the beast cards whose power is tied to climate and terrain of the region cards. Use Military Might cards which capitalizes on their terrain and brethren. Construction & Agriculture cards provide additional bonuses based on climate. Players can also utilize Climate Change effects to alter all Homelands and World Effects to damage specific climates and terrains.

Players will rely on luck, strategy, and bartering ability to lock-in five cards over the course of the game to create the strongest Homeland!

—description from the designer

HOMELAND - The Rules
PocketableGames.com
Players 2-6 I Time: 15-30min I Ages: 10+
SETUP:
-Each player randomly receives one of the six HOMELAND cards; your HOMELAND has a Climate and Terrain
-Your HOMELAND card is placed faceup and visible to all players
-Remove the remaining HOMELAND cards from play
-The Hand: Shuffle and deal 7 new cards to each player. These cards are MILITARY, AGRICULTURE, TAMED BEAST, WORLD EFFECT, and CLIMATE CHANGE cards -they award or penalize points based on your Climate and Terrain as well as complementary bonuses
GAMEPLAY:
-One randomly selected player begins as the Lead and places a card from their hand faceup on the table as the main offering
-All other players simultaneously offer a card they would be willing to trade the Lead 
-The Lead may either accept a single trade or discard their own offering and draw a new card (if the deck is empty you must trade) 
-The traded/untraded cards are returned to their owner's hand
-The Lead locks in a card from their hand facedown to strengthen their HOMELAND
-Play passes to the right, the new Lead repeats the above steps
-The game is finished when all players have locked in 5 cards finalizing their HOMELAND
SCORING:
-Players reveal, starting with the initial lead and passing right, if they locked in any Climate Change cards; effects take place in order of reveal
-Players then total their remaining locked in cards
-The HOMELAND with the most points is the winner!

boardgame: 精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z / Zero (Spirit Circuit Drive Ctrl Z / Zero)
description:
精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z / Zero (Spirit Circuit Drive Ctrl Z / Zero) aka 精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z ／ ゼロ (Seirei Kairo Drive: Ctrl-Z / Zero) is a co-operative card game from Japan. Previously produced as 精霊回路ドライヴ (Seirei Kairo Drive) and then as 精霊回路ドライヴEXE (Spirit Circuit Drive EXE), this new edition follows the same theme and gameplay, with a refreshed design and artwork. The artwork includes a smartphone style display, to give the feel of playing a videogame.

The game is published as a base set, with seven expansion (six special characters and a raid-box expansion). These expansions build on the previous expansion sets for the prior games. The game includes solitaire play.

The designer says "UNO-like engine + smartphone game theme party building cooperative card game. Each player selects a unit card at the beginning of the game and forms a party.

During the game, play by matching the numbers and colors of the cards, attack with your own units and use skills, to destroy the enemies that appear one after another.

The purpose of this game is to make full use of units with various abilities and stats, and cooperate with friends to defeat powerful boss enemies."

ゲーム中はカードの数字や色を合わせてプレイし、自軍ユニットで攻撃したりスキルを使い、

description:
Tricksdale  is a game of diplomacy and resource management in which 3 to 6 players compete to win the king's favor by building the most illustrious district.

In Tricksdale, there are 5 clans that compete to dominate the valley: cats, mice, canines, owls, and bears. Players must leverage their influence over all clans to obtain the resources they need to attract the right business.

The game is played over 3 hands (e.g., seasons). At the beginning of each season, players receive 7 cards representing the clans and the resources players influence over.

Throughout each round, players will go through the stages that include:


     Assembly: In this stage, players Play cards to gain resources and determine which clan is the most influential one in the next round
     Trade: Each player can gain a business and add to their district. Alternatively, players may sell resources and obtain contracts from the royal court.
     Collection: Each business established in the player's district can provide a special bonus triggered by the result of the assembly stage. Players may gain additional resources, gold, or victory points.


The season ends when all players have exhausted the cards in their hands. After all 3 seasons, the player with the highest victory point wins.

-description from designer

Do you have a passion for Roman history, is Julius Caesar your idol? Would you have liked to witness the glorious crossing of the Rubicon but your birth took place too many centuries later? With Rubico, Caesar versus Senatus, you will be able to take part in one of the key moments in Roman history. Or try to stop it from happening.

Rubico is a collaborative team game in which you will help your faction cross the river and reach Rome or try the opponent senators to stop the advance. Anticipate your opponents' moves and play your cards well. The fate of Rome is in your hands.


     Choose a game faction. Are you a centurion or rather a senator?
     Use Power cards or Senate cards wisely to achieve your goal.
     Cross the Rubicon and reach Rome or do everything to prevent it!


A collaborative team-based game of diplomacy and politics.

—description from the publisher (adapted)

In turns each player becomes the active player and impersonates the Centurion who must blindly yet strategically choose a set of cards from the ones provided face-down by the other players, and then resolve their effects. The "other players" are basically of two rival factions, Julius Caesar and his legions VS Pompeius Magnus and his senators. These roles are kept secret. Each of the "other players" choose their cards as to best pursue their personal goals: Caesar's fans want Caesar to cross the Rubico river and reach Rome as fast as possible; Pompeo's fans try to slow down Caesar's march and reach majority in the Senate.

—user summary

description:
Card Barrage is a head-to-head dexterity-based tower defense game. Players hone their dexterity skills and finesse at card tossing through attacking your opponent’s castle. At the start of the game, players lay out a card to represent their castle and its surrounding wall cards. Each turn, players physically toss cards against the other player’s castle and surrounding wall cards. Players score points when their barrage cards land on the other player’s castle or wall cards. Wall cards can be damaged and destroyed through attacks. You can play solo, head-to-head or team vs team. Rules govern how you can toss cards to attack through the lob, Frisbee, or flip method.

OBJECTIVE – The objective of Card barrage is to score points through attacking your opponents’ castle and castle walls. Successfully tossed cards that land on your opponent’s castle or is surrounding wall cards will score a point.
GAME PLAY – Each turn players may build a wall, upgrade a wall, and attack. Build a wall by playing a card into a previously destroyed wall section. Upgrade a wall by exchanging a wall card with a card in hand. If you did not build a wall, attack an opponent’s castle and wall by tossing a card to score points.
GAME END AND WINNING – The game ends when one player has scored 10 points or destroyed all their opponent’s walls and then hits their opponent’s castle with all players getting an equal number of turns. The player with the most points wins.
THEME – Medieval Setting and Castle sieges

—description from the publisher

description:
精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z 拡張５ 「刃雷」 (Spirit Circuit Drive Zero Expansion 5 [Jinrai]) aka精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z／ゼロ 拡張５ 「刃雷」 (Seirei Kairo Drive: Ctrl-Z/Zero Expansion 5 Jinrai) is the 5th expansion set for The Wet Werewolf, a party-building cooperative card game with a UNO-like engine and smartphone game theme. This set adds gimmicks unique to this expansion set, unit cards with charging ability, and boss cards.

UNO風のエンジン＋スマホゲームテーマのパーティー構築・協力型カードゲーム「精霊回路ドライヴCtrl-Z」の拡張セット５弾です。
この拡張セット固有のギミック、充電能力を持つユニットカード、その他にボスカードが追加されるセットです。
publisher's summary
artists: Jewel Saver FREE (ジュエルセイバーFREE)

description:
The coins in the vault are getting restless because no one is using them anymore. They have decided that they need to find out, once and for all, which is better: Heads or Tails? The Heads and Tails get into position to battle against the other team - but only one will remain as the ultimate champion

Contained in Casual Game Insider Magazine CGI#42(2023).

Welcome to the land of happy dinos! Our dinos are ready to play with you. Roll the dice to find their matching hidden eggs. Be the first to collect six dinosaurs to win! Memory and a little luck will help you succeed in this fun prehistoric eggspedition!

Contents: 12 Wooden Dinosaurs. 12 Wooden Coloured Eggs. 4 Scoring Boards. 2 Wooden Coloured Dice. Illustrated Rules

Missed friend's birthday? No worries, you have a good excuse: you had a sleeping kitten lying on your lap, you had to wait until he wakes up. In this game, you will find out absurd and wild excuses are suits you according to your friends.

Each turn, you make up excuses from random cards and vote which of your friends (or maybe you yourself?) Could really screw up and get off like that.

The goal of the game is to socialize and have fun, but you can also play for victory points.

—description from the publisher

1000 questions from 10 different categories (e.g. science, fairy tales, horses, sports etc.).

Game play is pretty simple: You roll the die to see which category you can choose from. If you answer the question correctly you advance on the scoreboard. Whoever reaches the final space first is the winner.

Although it has almost the same title and is made by the same company it's not to be confused with the game 'Das Wissens-Quiz'. This one has no board (except the scoreboard).

Gent Expert is a question and answer game.
You roll the dice to pick a category and score points.
Categories:

     History, Politics, Geography
     Showbusiness, Sports
     Tourism, Culture and gastronomy
     Shopping, Economics


Gain Points with the right answer, loose points with the wrong one.

It's all about the Beautiful City, Ghent in Belgium.

boardgame: 精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z 拡張６ 「円環」 (Spirit Circuit Drive Zero Expansion 6 [Enkan])
description:
精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z 拡張６ 「円環」 (Spirit Circuit Drive Zero Expansion 6 [Enkan]) aka 精霊回路ドライヴCtrl-Z／ゼロ 拡張 ６ 「円環」 (Seirei Kairo Drive: Ctrl-Z/Zero Expansion 6 Enkan) is the 6th expansion set for 精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z / Zero (Spirit Circuit Drive Ctrl Z / Zero), a party-building cooperative card game with a UNO-like engine and smartphone game theme. In addition to Unit Cards and Boss Cards, this set adds Geass Cards to strengthen the enemy.

UNO風のエンジン＋スマホゲームテーマのパーティー構築・協力型カードゲーム「精霊回路ドライヴCtrl-Z」の拡張セット６弾です。
ユニットカード、ボスカード以外に、敵の強化となるギアスカードが追加されるセットです。
publisher's summary
artists: Jewel Saver FREE (ジュエルセイバーFREE)

boardgame: 精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z レイドボックス (Spirit Circuit Drive Zero Expansion [Raid-BOX])
description:
精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z レイドボックス (Spirit Circuit Drive Zero Expansion [Raid-BOX]) aka 精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z／ゼロ レイドボックス (Seirei Kairo Drive: Ctrl-Z/Zero Expansion 7 Raidbox) is a special expansion set for 精霊回路ドライヴ Ctrl-Z / Zero (Spirit Circuit Drive Ctrl Z / Zero), a party-building cooperative card game with a UNO-like engine and smartphone game theme. This set allows you to challenge the high-difficulty raid mode. A unitized boss card will also be added.

UNO風のエンジン＋スマホゲームテーマのパーティー構築・協力型カードゲーム「精霊回路ドライヴCtrl-Z」の特殊な拡張セットです。高難易度のレイドモードに挑戦できるようになるセットです。
またユニット化したボスカードも追加されます。
publisher's summary
artists: Jewel Saver FREE (ジュエルセイバーFREE)

description:
Ready to have some fun? Trued up is a game that rocks any party. The box contains two decks of cards and a lot of funny situations and fun. Each turn, you choose an "True" or "Dare" card and complete the task. Whoever has the most completed tasks by the end of the game wins. This simple game will rocks even a bored company!

—description from the publisher

With Memo Mission, gamers can once again immerse themselves in the magical world of the Wizardry to the Power of Three game series. In the course of the game, they experience various missions, which they must complete in the correct order to obtain valuable crystals. In order to uncover the motifs sought on the mission cards in the correct order, they must always remember where tiles lie in the 5x5 grid, even during the other players' turns. The player who finds all motifs of the current mission on their own turn can fulfill it. Potions help in tricky situations and allow special actions once per game such as secretly looking at two tiles.

Memo Mission is an adventurous fairy tale game with a memo mechanism that includes introductory rules as well as blank missions on which the adventurers can get creative themselves and create their own stories thanks to the enclosed sticker sheet.

-description from publisher

Objective of the game is to eliminate all the Bomohs and be the last Bomoh standing



     Playstyle is to draw a card and play 2 action cards accordingly.



     Player can play these cards and counter each other out (Ghouls, Talisman, Hex, Summoner), During the game, players may draw divine beings to counter plague cards(Disaster cards). If player fails to counter plague card, they will be eliminated from the game. Last player standings wins.


—user summary

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS ABOUT SHAMANS:

1) Is BOMOH based on a true story?
Nope. All characters and characters are purely fictional.

2) How many people can participate in playing BOMOH?
2-5 players (those aged 18 and over).

3) How long does it take to play BOMOH?
Only 15 minutes to 25 minutes for one round.

—description from the publisher (translated)

SOALAN LAZIM MENGENAI BOMOH:

1) Adakah BOMOH berdasarkan kisah sebenar?
Tidak. Semua watak dan karakter adalah rekaan semata-mata.

2) Berapa orang boleh turut serta bermain BOMOH?
2-5 orang pemain (yang berumur 18 tahun ke atas).

3) Berapa tempoh waktu diperlukan untuk bermain BOMOH?
Hanya 15 minit ke 25 minit sahaja untuk satu pusingan.

—description from the publisher

A game for Havencentrum Antwerpen to teach about important roles in the logistical chain of transport.

The players play the role of either a Forward or a Shipping Agent. By fulfilling assignments and working together, players receive points.

At the end of the game, there's 1 best Forwarder and 1 best Shipping Agent.

Ript Showdown features two options on each card, allowing disc golfers to choose a side to play based on their style.

Players begin the round with 3 cards and draw additional cards based on their score for each hole. You can be Good and play a defensive game or Bad and attack your opponents mercilessly. Either way you hope to be Lucky in the end and pull off a win!

Start at the bear and look way up there... there's an Angel at the top of the Christmas tree!

Grab a button to use as a token, then draw an ornament from the shuffled pile and move ahead along the board track... but watch out for the spaces with setbacks (goats eat your peanuts, ornaments break, monkeyshines and giraffe necks). If you've been nice this year, Santa may smile on you and give you a boost with a tasty peanut or an extra turn.

Keep shuffling and drawing ornaments each round in a race to the top of the tree to see who will arrive first and become the Angel!

This simple boardgame is from the back cover of the book "Christmas at the Little Zoo", written by Beth Vardon and Illustrated by Charlot Byi. The book itself is undated, but was most likely self published sometime in the USA in the 1950's.

Blood on the Water is Carnevale’s first campaign expansion book. Written by returning authors Gav Thorpe and Lewis Clarke and joined by David laPorta, this book introduces a whole new narrative, exploring each faction.

In addition to a wealth of new background story, this book contains the full updated

Carnevale rulebook, including new additions for advanced players.

With Reactions to your opponents’ moves, Advanced Acrobatics, and ways to customise your gang like never before with the fated Ill Tides and Artifacts, this is the most up-to-date, balanced, and exciting Carnevale ruleset ever.

There are also 15 new scenarios, guiding your gangs through the story and letting you fight this new era out on the tabletop yourselves.

At 112 pages, this softback book is every Carnevale player's dream. Filled with beautiful new art, fully updated rules and the next chapter of the story, it really has everything you could need.

You must have "Carnevale: Vicious Fighting Along the Canals of Venice" to use this expansion, It can not be played alone. "Carnevale: Escape from San Canciano" is a simpler version of "Carnevale: Vicious Fighting Along the Canals of Venice" with other scenarios. But the rules are the same.

-description from publisher's website

In Witchy Cakes, you play witches competing to make the tallest, most enchanted cake possible to deliver unto an unsuspecting wedding reception.

Pick from adorable Covens to represent your witchy bakery and match colors to stack your cake cards into a tower. Then, throw cake at your friends, vie for coveted Recipe cards, and time your Secret Ingredients just right to have the tallest cake when the bell chimes thrice!

With easy-to-learn gameplay and a quick playtime, it's the perfect game for all ages, starting at 8 years old. Plays 4-8 players for 15-20 minutes. (Did we mention it's colorblind friendly?)

The Pickleball Card Game is played with 2 teams as singles or doubles, 1 or 2 players on each team. Your offensive and defensive skills are constantly challenged and changing throughout this fast-paced game, that involves a combination of skill, strategy and luck.

Teams compete to be the first to score 11 points by collecting sets and runs of cards to get a complete hand. You can only score when you have control of the serve though so be sure to stop your opponent from getting on a service run.

boardgame: Star Realms: Rise of Empire – Locked Promo Pack
description:
Locked Promo Pack was available as an optional add-on for the Rise of Empire Kickstarter campaign.

This pack contains 20 game cards and 1 rules card for the game Star Realms.

Promo Card List:

     1 Battle Cluster  (Extra copy)
     1 Bombardment Platform
     1 Commander Brandt
     1 Commissioner Jah-El
     1 Data Broker Philippe
     2 Federation Blockade
     1 Firing Point (Extra copy)
     1 Guardsman Arman
     1 Hive Mother
     2 Imperial Convoy
     1 Mech Armory (Extra copy)
     1 Monitor
     1 Reactor Well
     1 Sergeant Teague
     1 Shuttle Bay (Extra copy)
     1 Star Captain Starla
     1 Star Lord Dyal
     1 Vicar Esperia


A single copy of Battle Cluster, Firing Point, Mech Armory, and Shuttle Bay were unlocked as Stretch Goals. These extra copies will make them each a 2-of in the Trade Deck.

Are you for people? Or will you send the kingdom into anarchy? Social deduction meets resource management in this palace intrigue game.

Anarchy is an event-based resource management game putting a twist on the classic social deduction genre. Players are their kingdom's newly elected council members — each with a hidden objective and tasked with balancing their self-interests with the kingdom's well-being.

Players must work together to prevent their kingdom from falling into Anarchy whilst simultaneously plotting against each other to achieve their unique objective. But, be careful because amongst your friendly council members are Anarchists who are trying to sew descent and throw the kingdom into Anarchy.

Anarchy is just a vote away. Trust no one.

—description from the publisher

We all had it, and those of us who survived assumed the risk was over. But the latest variant is different, oh so different…

Each of the 9 cards in this micro party game lists one symptom in your zombie transformation. One card is set aside and represents the BrainZ (yummy yummy!). Each player gets one card face down in front of them, and the rest go face down in the middle.

On your turn, you look at your card and start acting out that symptom (as well as any previous symptoms), then swap your card with any other card. As the symptoms develop and spread you must use observation and elimination to deduce what is on the BrainZ card. If you are right, you are cured and win. Otherwise you die and the other players continue...

-description from designer

Protect the last known survivor of humanity who has awoken from stasis - naked and afraid - to a nuclear winter where doggos are hyper-sentient. 
1-4 players play canine cards who trace their breeds back to famous pups from all across human history.
Your mission?
Defy your common enemy - Felis Catus Duplicitus. Protect the frail and hapless animal known as 'Man' from themselves - they are, after all the last of their breed.

With clever planning and synergy, different dog cards and breeds produce combos and intercept the cruel and canny Cats who call this nuclear waste their hunting grounds.
Canis Lupus Familiaris - assemble!

—description from the designer

Postcard Brittany is a relatively fast and simple - NO "Zones of Control!" - but historical little 6-turn sudden-death postcard wargame about General George Patton's August 1944 coup de main attempts to grab the major French ports of Brittany, before the Germans could organize their defenses.
Regimental level: 14 German units, 3 French FFI groups, and 11 US units in hex-and-counter format.
1-4 player game with at least initially hidden German units spread out in the peninsula.
Allied movement impeded by Strassesperren - roadblocks - as well as German units ... as well as traffic jamming on roads.
FFI groups can recon unknown German units, destroy undefended German roadblocks, and block German retreats.
Patton's special engineer-equipped Task Force A included, and it can be 'decisive.'  (Thanks, George.)

Historically, Patton failed, chafing at the corps commander's hesitancy, but you can do better. Right? :-)

Good solitaire game - or in very large format, team game - with US 4th and 6th Armored Divisions necessarily going down separate east-west roads.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Track down a Danny the Dragon toy for your daughter's Christmas present in this holiday themed at-home escape room.

Cards are shuffled.
The first player takes a card from the pack and calls out the letter in the top right hand corner.
The person to the left of the player takes a card from the pack and asks the question corresponding to the letter given by the first player. The correct answer is underlined.
If the player answers correctly, he keeps the card and the game continues.
If the player answers incorrectly, the cards are put back into the pack and the game continues.
If a Joker is revealed, the person revealing it can keep it.
The winner is the first player who gets the 7 cards of the same colour or who has more cards of the same colour than the others at the end of the game.

Resilience Survive and Thrive is a tabletop card game that aims to teach its players lessons on balanced development and natural resiliency under the climate change lens.

Each round is divided into several phases: Draw, Activity, Impact, Community and Crisis.

In the Draw phase, each player draws 2 cards from the Development deck. If the number of cards from the players' hands exceeds 7, they must discard the extra cards.

In the Activity phase, each player can do up to 3 activities. An Activity can be a combination of any of the following:

     Restoring an Environment - turning over an environment card back to its position on the playmat.
     Playing an Investment - placing an Investment card from your hand into your community.
     Playing a House - placing any card facedown and sideways from your hand into your community. Houses are Gray Investments and give 1 Development Point (DP).



In the Apply Impact phase, players will specific impacts of the Investment cards they have placed onto the playmat. Different Investments have different impacts:

     Sustainable Investments increase DPs depending on how sustainable its associated Environment is.
     Commercial Investment can destroy the environment, removing 1 Environment card from the playmat, but also gives the player 4 DP.
     Conservation Investment restore 1 particular Environment card but does not give any DP.


In the Community phase, players can play 1 Action card. If the player does not play an Action card, they can take 1 card from the Development deck instead or simply pass. Action cards are used to gain an upper hand against the player's opponents. Place the Action card into the discard pile once played. In this phase, players can also cast votes to close down one Investment at play. Voted Investments go into the discard pile.

Lastly, in the Crisis phase, the top card is revealed and its effect applied.

     If the revealed Crisis is an Extreme Weather Event, players must determine its effect by counting the associated Environment, then apply the destruction to each community.
     If the revealed Crisis is an End Game Crisis, destroy all cards of the specific Environment type and place the End Game card on its slot in the playmat. The game continues as long as the 3 End Game slots are not yet occupied.


After the crisis, return to the Draw phase to start a new round.

For weeks, a mad zeppelin, the Relempago, has been roaming the skies.

He has killed his entire crew, and robots in his pay are capturing new victims.

Now it's your turn to be locked up on board the flying ship!

You'll have to solve the machine's riddles and put it out of action in order to be the first to tell the story of your adventure aboard the airship.

Berlin illustrator and author Nadia Budde has created an adorable memo game from her universally appreciated, highly successful ZEIT column "Tiere wie wir" ("Animals like us"). 36 animal encounters populate this game, and they are all tied together with an animal two-liner: Laundry smooth, raccoon flat; octopus at the tailor, two long dresses. This lovingly designed memo game is packaged in a small-format sewing box. As an additional gimmick without function, there is a cardboard tape measure populated by the animals of the game.
The Octopus at the Tailor appeals to young and old and is the perfect family memo.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Full Sauna. A game about the struggle to relax. Meet 12 colorful characters who are trying to spend the best time inside. Chill, sweat, flirt, nap, chat, fight, or even fart to make people leave and clear space inside the sauna.

Outscore your opponents by inserting the right character at the right time in order to get the most “Feel Good” points. Or you can always enter the sauna’s black market and sell second-hand bathrobes, aromas and water buckets.

Get yourself a pair of thermodynamic slippers, which will give you additional points at the end of each turn based on the sauna temperature. Tons of strategies and combos await to be explored in a game, which is easy to learn and fast to play.

Full Sauna is a light mix between a card game, a resource management strategy and tableau building.

A turn starts by players paying for turn order. The first player picks up a card from 4 face up cards which form the sauna queue, and plays a character from their hand inside the sauna.

Characters can score chat or feel good tokens (worth different VPs in the end of the game), change the temperature inside the sauna, or collect and sell loot to other visitors. They have different scoring preferences: some need the sauna hot, others cold, some want it full of people so they can chat or flirt, others like it empty, so they can meditate.

Players have to manipulate the tableau to maximize the conditions for the characters they own, and mix different cards, skills and resources to make the most of each moment.

—description from the designer

The monkeys are loose in the jungle! As fast as they can, they climb the big palm tree to harvest the coconuts. But watch out! The giant tree snake is also on its way to the top of the palm tree. Will the monkeys manage together to get all the coconuts to safety before the snake reaches the top? A fast-paced climbing game with cute monkeys and funny "grimaces" (mouth motor exercises) begins.

The play accessories for "Affenkuss & Kokosnuss" (engl. "Monkey Kiss & Coconut") (16 pcs.) are made exclusively from wood in a small company in Germany. The game works as a cooperation game, but also as a betting game. Classically as a memo or large board game.

The greedy troll has stolen the dwarves' treasure and taken it to his mountain. He knows that the dwarves want to get it back. That's why he runs back and forth between his kingdom and the dwarves' kingdom to catch the dwarves. If he succeeds, the treasure will be his forever and he will have won. Fortunately, the dwarves have the support of Aquarius and Fairy.

The game is suitable for children from three years and has the peculiarity that the group of players together wins or loses against the troll. The players do not compete against each other. Especially for children who are just starting with board games and are still learning to endure the frustration of losing, this game is an enrichment.

The game Troll dich! combines ideas and design of our children's book author and illustrator Kristin Franke with the traditional craft of Erzgebirge folk art. The figures are hand-turned and made by toy maker Tino Günther, the famous inventor of the virologist incense man.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Quetzalcoatl is a promo Bitoku (Meijo) card. It was first available during the convention Mega XP 2022.

This is a unique spirit - only one copy is in the deck. When you reach this card in your Bitoku path, you are awarded with two victory points and the option to obtain a crystal.

Exilegard is an open source miniature skirmish wargame where you lead a warband of exiles through a dying far far future sci-fantasy setting. The game uses preplanned alternating activation and asymmetrical RPS combat with dice. Your warband has a common pool of HP and you choose if miniatures die when they take damage. You will also need to pay attention to cover and flanking in order to win.

—description from the designer

Operacion Dinamita is a game where you, in turns, reveal a pair of cards. Each card usually has a number, and if you are able to get one number from the other using one of the operator cards in your hand, you get they three (scoring three points). For example, a "4" card and a "8" card can be obtained using a "x2" operator card.

Bonus operator cards like the dynamite stick, matches or chronometer make a tweak in the game with special rules.

The BIGGIES are huge space container ships. Their job is to transport cargo, but first the cargo must be loaded inside the cargo bay. Load it up as quickly as possible and be the first to pack your ship to the top!

Each player get a copy of one of six ships with a 28x40 sized cargo bay grid. Now take turns rolling 2 dice to determine the size of the different cargo pieces. For example a roll of 4 and 5 gives you a 4x5 container, that you can rotate and place either way on the grid. In this matter you fill up each row horizontally, with each block having to touch the previous, before you can start the next row on top, going from left to right. Also some special interactions between the players when doubles are rolled.

Be the first one to touch the top of the cargo bay to win!

—description from the publisher

Souvenirs d'Empire is a medium-complexity, two-player Print & Play wargame that recreates battles from the Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars.

Les Insurgés includes the basic rules and is the first volume in the series. It Contains :
- 1 rulebook of 24 pages with the principles of the game and numerous illustrated examples
- 1 double-sided game aid
- 1 board of 216 counters and markers to play the French armies and the Spanish insurgents in 1808
- 4 scenarios: the battles of El Bruch (June 6, 1808), Alcolea Bridge (June 7, 1808), Mengíbar (July 16, 1808) and Bailén (July 19, 1808)
- 1 A4 card and 3 A3 cards
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: This is the first Clan expansion for the Whateley family. In this expansion units can cross between unit types. You can recruit a unit from the Whateleys even it is already on the map, controlled by another player.

This is a satirical game about silly conspiracy theories regarding the ingredients of the COVID-19 vaccine. It was originally released at Tokyo Game Market Fall '22 in a Ziploc bag.

Players play ingredients of a single value face down in front of them, then simultaneously reveal their cards. The player with the highest total value may play their ingredient onto the research board and move up the appropriate track (RIDICULOUS, IMPACTFUL, TASTEFUL, or INTELLECTUAL). Players who did not win draw new cards. At the end of the game, players score for their three lowest tracks.

The rules also include a co-op mode.

Play even more mathematical games with this double-sided deck that commemorates the 50th anniversary of the Association for Women in Mathematics! EvenQuads games use symbols representing four major mathematical organizations and EvenBetter games use the flip sides featuring amazing women mathematicians.

All included games are mathematical. EvenQuads games challenge your matching speed as you collect Quads, as solitaire, with friends or in a rummy version. EvenBetter games help you learn about the featured women. All games are specially designed to accommodate our unique double-sided cards.

Deck 2 (of 4) of EvenQuads includes 64 cards, an insert with instructions in both English y Español for three games, and a description of the EvenQuads mathematics. The Association of Women in Mathematics website includes rules for additional games utilizing the cards from both Decks 1 and 2.

Evenquads: Deck 2 functions as a stand-alone game, or it can be combined with Evenquads: Deck 1 as an expansion.

boardgame: Fighters of the Pacific: Stretch Goals Pack
description:
This additional content contains all of the stretch goals achieved during the 2021 Kickstarter Campaign. These game components expand the original game and are not included within the Core Game.

The Stretch Goal Pack contains:
x24 airplane reference cards
x8 scenarios, including a point system for custom battles
Tokens for:
- x2 PBY Catalina
- x4 E13 Jake
- x6 TBF Avenger
- x6 G4M1 Betty
- x6 B-25 Mitchell
- x6 P-38 Lightning
- x6 N1K Shiden
- x6 D4Y Suisei
- x6 B6N Tenzan
- x6 A6M5 Zero
- x6 SB2C Helldiver
- x6 F4U Corsair
- x6 F6F Hellcat
- x6 MXY Ohka
- x2 B-26 Marauder
- x1 IJN Tanker
- x1 USS Tanker
- x1 USS Heavy Cruiser
- x1 IJN Cruiser
- x1 Yamato Battleship
- x2 Liberty Ship

Everything you need to start playing the Evolve Wrestling card game! The set includes 14 wrestler cards featuring stars from Evolve Wrestling including Johnny Gargano, Riccochet, Rich Swann, and The Best Friends. It also includes the Evolve Handbook, featuring bios for all the wrestlers. This set also includes starter instructions and needed charts.

—description from the publisher

Lucky Pirate is a quick card game with a mix of skill and luck that can be played with 2-6 players. Players get to choose an even number of "up" and "down" cards to lay on the table in front of them and form a hand with the leftover cards. They then take turns playing a card to the middle. Generally each card must be equal or higher to the previous card played but some cards have special powers. Three of the same card in a row will remove the played cards from the game, but if a player finds themself unable to play on the current stack? They must take all the cards into their hand!

Play continues until someone has played all their hand cards, up cards, and down cards. They are then declared winner and Lucky Pirate Captain!

An evil scientist has captured 15 different dinosaurs and is holding them in a "paddock" fenced area for his studies. The players are trying to rescue and free the dinosaurs. The game background story says this occurs on a large island near the US. There is no further reference to a well known movie with a somewhat similar dinosaur park theme.

Each person, in turn, plays card(s) to free the dinos from the paddock; or can play cards to take a dino from another player; or can build up their hand of cards to use on a future turn. Players also interact by trying to play the highest value card when a dino card is played which is marked with a less than symbol at the top. (Somewhat the reverse of actual game play which is to place a greater number than the other players.)

The game can be played using either one of two ending victory conditions: A) be the first player to release 5 dinos, or B) have freed the most dinosaurs when the game ends... after the last dino is freed from the "paddock" area.

The publisher especially intends for this to be an educational game for kids to learn more about these dinos, and to use some basic math.

In the pocket Bowling is a ten-pin bowling simulation using dice and cards based on real player statistics to generate the results of a frame in one or two rolls. The better players will statistically roll more strikes but there is still a chance of them being "out of the pocket" and a second roll, referenced against the OOTP chart, will dictate whether or not they picked up a spare.

It is primarily a solo game but can easily be played with more, each choosing a different player card to run a championship.

The game lasts 3 eras. During every era, players fight over control in provinces. 
Using dice, players can perform the following actions:

     Recruit new troops
     Send troops to the area
     Take control over roads


At the end of the era, players check who has control over provinces, castles, and roads.
The player with the most points after 3rd era is the winner.

Adds 6 new sets of cards with 180 words about non-family-friendly concepts for adults. The game rules don't change.

Remolacha: Picantona es una expansión del juego básico Remolacha, con la que se incluyen 6 nuevos sets de cartas, un total de 180 palabras, especialmente destinada a los adultos.

boardgame: Age of Steam Deluxe: Expansion Volume III
description:
A collection of Age of Steam Deluxe expansion maps:

     Sweden Recycling (3-6) / Pacific Electric (4-6)
     Disco Inferno (3-6) / Southern China (3-5)
     Osaka (2-5) / Lincoln Funeral Car (4-6)


Each Expansion Volume of the set contains multiple maps on mounted game boards, plus the rules and any extra components needed to play each one.

boardgame: The Cheese was in its Place
description:
You make only four moves in The Cheese was in its Place, so make them count!

Each player receives four mouse cards at random from a deck numbered 5-13; the remaining card is set aside face down. In the first round, lay out in a 3x3 grid nine bait cards, which are numbered 1-6 and depict food: acorns, strawberries, and cheese. Each player in turn places a card face down next to one of the rows or columns in the grid, then they reveal them, then they do this again. Whichever mouse is smallest takes food first, but it can grab cards only up to its size, e.g., the 5 mouse takes bait cards from the end of the row or column where it's located, stopping if a card would make the sum of the bait more than 5.

After resolving all four mice, refill the grid with bait cards, then play another round with the remaining two mouse cards in hand. Each trio of acorns or strawberries you collect is worth 1 point, while a trio of cheese is worth 2 points. Whoever scores the most wins.
Try to build highest tower by cleverly buying blocks from auction. Money is limited and players do not get change from their bids. After all blocks have been auctioned, highest tower wins.

Punchline è un gioco cattivo, irriverente, di black humor… e assolutamente non politically correct!
I giocatori devono creare l’abbinamento più divertente e mettere alla prova il senso dell’umorismo degli altri

COME SI GIOCA? Un giocatore legge una carta domanda. Gli altri giocatori mettono una carta risposta. Vince la combinazione più divertente

400 carte scandalose, cattive e trasgressive. Punchline è un gioco vietato ai minori. Siete avvisati!
Un gioco perfetto per feste, compleanni, addii al nubilato o al celibato, serate con gli amici. Insomma: è perfetto per qualsiasi occasione

Numero di giocatori: 3-20 | Età consigliata: 18+ | Durata media: 30-120 min. | Edizione in lingua italiana

L'enfer de la bibliotheque ("The Hell of the Library") offers the possibility to play with the 7 Deadly Sins. The 3 Helpers counters are replaced during the set up by 7 Deadly Sins tiles. Two stages are added:


     Third Hour - Forgiveness: in this step, the Copyist can ask another player for forgiveness by offering his Material (Lectern). In exchange, he turns over a Sin Tile used earlier in the game. He is forgiven.



     Fourth Hour - Temptation: In this stage, a Copyist may use one or more sins on his turn. Each sin is a one-time use and offers a powerful power:


Acedia: your Copist can draw a Sketch by placing it wherever he wishes on his Scroll regardless of placement constraints. He must spend his Inks and earns the Illumination points of the placed Tile.

Avaritia: your Copyist saves his Ink and can execute 2 Sketches without providing the necessary Ink. You do not earn the Illumination points.

Gula : your Copyist takes 5 Inks at random from the bag.

Invidia : your Copyist steals three Sketches of his choice from the Scriptorium and places them in his personal supply.

Ira : your Copyist destroys another Copyist's Material ; it is discarded but the sketches are kept and join the Copyist's personal reserve at the end of the day.

Luxuria: your Copist's piece moves to the square of the Copist's piece in front of it.

Superbia : each golden Ink from his reserve spent gives 3 additional Illumination points to your Copyist. Spent inks are returned to the bag.

At the end of the game, unforgiven sins cause you to lose illumination points.

Don't Feed the Troll, the game of discord! Does the internet mean anything to you? This endless web where trolls, haters and mini vigilantes abound? This strange place where wars and melodramas take place, conspiracy theories and fights with hateful comments. But if, you see, those endless discussions messing up your Facebook news feed...

With Arkham Society, our brand new publishing house, we thought of making a game of atmosphere and strategy that will make you lose all your friends during the game. (Wrong. He was a Troll).

In truth, it's a funny game on the quirks of the interweb that will liven up your best parties and in which you can challenge your worst friends!

—description from the publisher

A print and play sports simulation using cards and dice to replay rugby tournaments, including 15-a-side, 13-a-side and 7-a-side varieties.

The roll of a number of dice will determine the outcome of each possession with each team having a range of dice results determining who has possession and whether a try or kick has been scored. Each match can be completed in a short amount of time, meaning a whole tournament or season is possible in a reasonable time-span.

This is a hilarious game of WHO CAN BLUFF THE BEST with a Southern twist! "A little fibbin' goes a long way!"

To win a round of That Dog Won't Hunt - you have to be the best bluffer - or be really darn lucky.  If you're the only one left with a dice at the end of the game - you WIN!

Liar's dice with a Southern twist - the dice are our own design with Bless Your Heart, Possums, Pecan Pie, Cast Iron Skillets, Steaming Biscuits and of course Kudzu is WILD! HA!

—description from the publisher

Дополнение "Звёздные империи: Командиры" предоставляет игрокам ещё больше возможностей и средств в борьбе за господство в космическом пространстве. В коробке вы найдёте 6 колод командиров, каждая из которых включает в себя стартовую руку из двенадцати карт, 2 гамбита, флагманский корабль, а также 2 карты подсчёта очков влияния и памятку. Кроме того, с этим дополнением можно сыграть как в классический дуэльный вариант "Звёздных империй", так и попытать счастья в одиночку дать отпор сразу против двоих соперников. Объедините под своим началом всё лучшее от двух фракций и воспользуйтесь преимуществом!

The expansion Star Realms: Commanders gives players even more options and tools in the battle for space domination. The box contains six Command decks, each with a starting hand of twelve cards, two gambits, a flagship, as well as two influence score cards and a memo. In addition with this add-on you can play both the classic duel version of Star Realms as well as try your luck against two opponents at once. Combine the best of both factions under your command and take advantage!

Additional command decks were introduced named The Alignment, The Alliance, The Coalition, The Pact, The Union, and The Unity.

boardgame: Fog of Love: Pride 2022 Promo – Walk the Runway
description: Promo card: How do you react to your partner’s Pride outfit choice?

boardgame: Fog of Love: A Visit to La Sagrada Família
description:
Promo card for Fog of Love that came together with a promo card for Sagrada.

“Take it all in! The stained glass! The facades! 200 years of history! A two hour wait! During your trip to Barcelona, you decide to visit Gaudi’s majestic La Sagrada Família. It’s unspeakably beautiful. It’s also unspeakably touristy. How are you feeling about the experience?”

You and your fellow wizards have a bad habit of pilfering each other's dungeons. However, this time you're prepared! You've fortified your dungeon with a bestiary of nasty creatures. Imps. Dragons. Werewolves. Each with their own magical powers, ready to pounce.

You leave them on guard as you depart for your own dungeon-crawl, only to discover: your fellow wizards have done the same. And they have a cave troll.


The game has 3 stages: Fortify, Exchange, and Crawl. In the Fortify Stage, all players take each turn simultaneously, drafting 1 card to place defenders into their dungeon. In the Exchange Stage, players exchange dungeons. Each player shuffles their cards to form a personal draw pile. In the Crawl Stage, all players take each turn simultaneously, using 1 of their cards to clear defenders from their opponent’s dungeon.

The game ends for everyone when a player finishes clearing their opponent’s dungeon. Win by scoring points for fully fortifying your lairs and clearing your opponent’s lairs -- and by not running out of mana or vigor during the Crawl Stage.

—description from the designers

As twilight closes in on once great civilizations, warring rulers make a last push for supremacy. Use the unique abilities of the different tribes to secure the future of your lineage - No matter the cost.

Gameplay overview
Draft: From a starting hand of seven cards, players keep two cards and pass the rest to the player on their left. This is repeated until all players have a new hand of seven cards.

Play: Players take turns playing one card from their hand. Cards can either be played in front of you, adding to your victory points, or be played for their ability.

Score: When the game ends, players sum up their victory points. A series of three or more of the same card type doubles the points for those cards. The player with the highest number of victory points wins.

There you have the basics of the game. Easy to learn rules that make for fun and interesting bouts of wit and timing. This is not a card game... This is a mind game!

—description from the publisher

A train game where you move your chosen engine along the tracks with the objective to pass each of the 24 stations on the board. Each player’s engine starts at one of 4 London stations (Paddington, Euston, King’s Cross, and Waterloo) and moves along twinned track. The track has coloured spaces that when landed upon require the moving player to draw one or more cards that have various consequences. There are also numbered spaces on the track that when landed upon indicate the number of cars to be added behind the engine - thereby affecting the speed at which the engine moves. The number of cars and engine speed is tracked on individual player cards. A chart is consulted and a single die is rolled to determine engine speed/movement, which stays in effect until the number of cars changes. Slower engines can block faster moving engines going the same direction and you may have to wait until an oncoming engine (placed on the board from a drawn card) goes to a siding before moving forward.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Kaladesh Planeswalker Deck: Chandra, Pyrogenius
description:
Invent Your Tomorrow

Get started playing Magic right away! You'll fly rings around the dizzying spires of Kaladesh in airships and other magically powered vehicles, raining down fire upon your enemies as they struggle to fight back. Summon the Planeswalker Chandra Nalaar--with a card unique to this deck--to clinch your victory.

—description from publisher

You are a secret agent and have been leaking secret info to the black market for many years. But now an auditor has been sent after you. One careless word - and you are exposed! Each turn, one of you becomes an agent and the other an auditor. The agent explains the secret word to the rest, and the auditor listens and monitors the list of forbidden words. If the agent calls one of them, he is caught - they are already coming for him! If you don't get caught, you will earn points. Then another couple becomes the auditor and agent. The game ends when everyone has has played as auditor twice.

—description from the publisher

Бомба ("The bomb") is a game of explaining words. Everything is simple: first you explain by words, then by gestures and facial expressions, and then by one word. You choose names, neutral words, and locations and explain them each time in a new order and in a new way. By the end of the game, you can reach such an understanding with the team that, everyone will recognize Lincoln by one wave of hand! The team that guesses the most words wins.

—description from the publisher

Dino Journey is a cooperative dice rolling game where the players help dinosaurs meet each other and escape from the lava together.

Each turn, you roll a die and move the dinosaurs around the island. You have 6 tasks that show you, which dinosaurs should meet and where exactly.

Sometimes lava falls on the cube and an eruption happens: using a 3D-volcano in the middle of the field, throw a Lava token to see, on which part of the field it falls. Now you can't go there and need to find different ways to get to the needed place.

You win if you have six dinosaur encounters before you run out of 6 Lava tokens.

In the cooperative and sustainable board game Summsalabim you take on the role of a colony of bumblebees and try to collect enough nectar for you and your offspring by skillful balancing.
By humming and buzzing, you lure the collecting bumblebee to the right flower.
But beware, there is also a trickier version with the mighty wizard "Pestizido" and his magic dice. Will you manage to collect enough nectar despite your dizzy senses?

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

boardgame: Clades Variana: La bataille de Teutoburg
description:
Clades Variana is a Print & Play solitaire wargame simulating the Battle of Teutoburg which took place from September 7 to 11, 9 AD between the army of the Roman Consul Publius Quinctilius Varus and the coalition of Germanic tribes led by the Cheruscan Arminius.
In this game, four 21x29.7cm format maps represent the historical space of this combat. On this space, 1cm is equivalent to about 200-400m on the ground.
1 turn of the game represents half a day of history, a game is played in 8 turns. The player tries to avoid the announced disaster of the Roman forces while the game directs the efforts of the Germans to annihilate the enemy column; but he can also reverse the roles and like Arminius organize the ambush against Varus' army.
In just 4 days, barbarians annihilated 3 legions, the pride of Rome, forever marking the history of the conquerors. Will you manage to get out of this trap, will you succeed in uniting the Germanic tribes against the common threat? It's up to you to change destiny...

—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Cruelest Month: Air War over Arras, April 1917
description:
An offensive on the Western Front that would be called the "Battle of Arras" but remembered for what the Royal Flying Corps called it - "Bloody April." Still flying mostly outdated aircraft, General "Boom" Trenchard insisted on constant offensive actions and round-the-clock missions for recon, artillery spotting, mapping, etc. With the most common plane involved in this work being the dreadfully vulnerable Be2C, facing up against the likes of Baron von Richthofen's Jasta 11, the results were woeful losses. To the RFC's credit, they continually fulfilled the missions, despite the losses.

The Cruelest Month looks at this struggle, with the focus primarily on air operations and ground battle abstracted (similar to Chennault's First Fight). As the British player, you will marshal your limited numbers of fighters to help secure the skies for 2-seaters that would be better suited to training planes. As the German player, you will employ your well-armed modern fighters against waves of RFC planes that simply keep coming, regardless of how many you shoot down.

Each hex on the map is approximately 2 miles across. A game turn spans two days of time. An Aerial Unit is comprised of 6 to 12 aircraft.
Published in Against the Odds Annual 2020 issue (published in 2023).

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
An abstract strategy game for 2 players inspired by an app that is now gone.

In turn each player adds a piece to the board in any empty space, and if 3 or more pieces of the same height are orthogonally connected, the other pieces are removed from the board and an extra piece is added to the piece just played.

When the board is full , (no more empty spaces) the winner is the player with the most pieces on the board

Survival of the Chilliest

You don't need to know your Chakra from your Barista to have a blast in this quick-to-learn card game. Try to keep your calm and this chill race to become a Zen Master!

Players compete to transcend first by aligning their chakra cards from #1 to #7 with a Mastery card on top. The first player to transcend 4 times wins the game! Want to prove you're the most chill? Be the first to transcend all seven levels and become the ultimate master of Zen Battle.

Battlesuit Alpha takes you to the far future where giant mechanized battlesuits rule the combat zone. Design your own mechs and vehicles and arm them with rockets, lasers and rail guns to blast your opponents to dust.

Each player controls a group of mechs and support vehicles called a Lance. Battlesuit Alpha is miniature agnostic allowing you to use whatever mech models you have, be they 15mm, 10mm or 6mm scale.

Players learn in 1 - 3 turns, but the unique card driven turn mechanic makes each battle a tactical challenge where every player is involved. Like all the Fistful of Lead games, you’ll only need 5 or 6 miniatures per player. The rules can be used for 1-on-1 games or larger skirmishes with up to 8 players.

These rules contain all the information you need to play a game, including rules for building your forces and how to set up the table for different scenarios.

—description from the publisher

Get together in teams and fight against the other teams. 
The active team has 1 minute to draw as many words as possible, and scores point from each word. When times is up the other team(s) get a chance to guess the last drawing. 
A game is eaither 16 or 32 rounds. More than 100 categories and 1000 words.

Gå sammen i hold og kæmp mod de andre i at tegne og forstå hvad din makker tegner. Jo hurtigere i er, jo flere point får i.
I spillet findes der mere end 100 forskellige tegnekategorier og mere en 1000 tegneopgaver. Ens tegneopgave bliver bestemt ved at trække et tilfældigt kort og kaste en terning.

A drawing game, where everyone is playing at the same time either as the one who should guess the word or as drawer.
The faster the word is guessed,  the more point you score.

From beyond the Grave is a Roll and Write, Print and Play Single Player adventure that plays in 15-30 minutes.

The city of Nilam hides a grotesque secret that dates back to the construction of the Cathedral. On an ominous day in December 1819, someone broke the seal that prevented the souls of the slaughtered from pouring into the city. Since that day, corruption of the flesh, depravity and vengeance have run rampant in the streets.

You wear the cloak of one of the Saviors, special members of the High Council, and your task is to unveil the mystery and put an end to the horrors.

Choose one of four characters with special abilities. Then you take the first of three districts maps and start your quest. The story is divided into six chapters with three different tasks that you have to fulifill in order to advance. At the beginning of your turn, roll 2 dice. Pick one die and move of a number of spots up to the obtained result.
The unused die can be saved for later in your Character Card.
When you step on specific spots on the map, you can perform different actions, such as fight, heal, shop or discover mysteries that bring the story forward.

You win by completing all tasks and lose when you get killed by the Horrors or time runs out!

Save Nilam. Save its citizens. Save yourself from the curse.

—description from the designer

Pction SIingems real game is the official bootleg version of the game PotionSlingers. It plays the exact same as the original but contains inconsistencies and printing errors, mistranslated mechanics, downgraded components and lopsided gameplay.

Contains two booster packs of characters and items from the real game, but the printing material is different and the tarot cards are poker sized.

Shadow of the Lich is played over two stages (consisting of nine rounds total), on a grid of nine cards representing your town: the first stage involves finding the three artifacts needed to summon the lich by defeating the undead or searching buildings using dice rolls. The second stage requires finding the lich before all locations come under his control (one is randomly flipped after each round).

A pack of six (two each of three unique cards) equipment cards with adorable references to other Chip Theory franchises!

The cards are: Spiderbot 0.5, Source Well, and Ancient Chariot.

These equipment cards are all single use cards.

Available as part of the burncycle: New Recruits Gamefound campaign.

This fourth Next War supplement contains the following items:

     Rules for playing combined games
     An SOP specifically for Combined Games
     Rules covering backfitting new or updated Series Rules to previous games or just catching up to changes in Orders of Battles
     New optional rules including Limited PGMs, Traffic Jams, Unit Morale & Resiliency, and more…
     A new scenario for Next War: Iran (requiring counters from Next War: Poland and Next War: Vietnam)
     Two Insurgency scenarios for Next War: Iran
     One counter sheet containing additional and updated counters including:
         An Italian AW249
         Tejas Mk2 for India
         Polish AH-64s
         Indonesian F-15C
         Additional German F-35As
         Various additional markers
    
     An update SOP for using the Alternate Advanced Game Air System 2.0 rules


—description from the publisher

Finally, the humans are gone and the animals are free. Time to raid the fridge! One by one the family animals bring delicious snacks like melon, cake, and peppers to their hiding place. Who can fill their stash the fastest?

In Snack Happens, players take on the roles of a cat, mouse, dog, and parrot, whose goal is to fill their secret stash as full as possible with treats. To do this, the active player throws wooden chips as accurately as they can into the bottom box lid, which represents the refrigerator. Afterwards, depending on where the chips landed, the active player may choose to perform a matching action. All the other players in turn may then also choose and perform one of the actions marked by the thrown chips. Each new round, the next player around the table takes a turn tossing the snack chips into the fridge. The special actions Snack Box and Mixer provide additional excitement in this exciting children's game.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
The dinos are waiting for their colorful dino eggs to hatch. Cozy and warm, the eggs lie safely in their nests. But Dori Dino loves to roll the glittering dino eggs around to create a colorful mix. Can the other players quickly roll the eggs back into their original nests before the dino babies hatch?

In Dori Dino, a different player each round will take a turn as Dori, rolling the eggs around and into the wrong nests. The rest of the players are the dino parents who try to roll the eggs into their matching colored nests. All players have a dino wand and can only move the eggs with its tip. The round ends as soon as all the eggs in the nests have disappeared. The eggs will be scored for where they landed. All eggs that the parent team was able to correctly match will hatch and bring points. After a final round in which everyone plays dino parents, the turbulent dino game ends.

—description from the publisher

A pure guessing game, not much room for skill.
The pack is shuffled and placed face-down. The first player has to predict the colour of the top card (Red, Green, Blue or Yellow). If correct he takes the card and can try again. If wrong the turn passes to the next player. Game ends when all the cards have been taken.
As per the title of the game, the winner holds the Smiley Badge and the loser holds the Sad Badge.

—user summary

7 Wonders: Edifice gives players the opportunity to work together to construct communal buildings. If you do your part and the building comes to be, you'll be rewarded. You don't have to participate, but if you don't — and the building never comes to be — you'll suffer a penalty.
