In more detail, this expansion contains fifteen edifice cards: five each in ages I, II, and III. To set up, choose one card from each age at random, then place it project side face up on the table and place 2-5 participation markers on each card, depending on the number of players. The project side lists both the potential reward and potential penalty.

Once during an age, when you construct a level of your wonder, you can pay the cost of the edifice card for the current age (in addition to the cost of that level) and gain a participation marker from that card.

As soon as the last participation marker is removed from a card, flip the card over to reveal its constructed side and immediately give each player the listed reward. If at the end of the age at least one participation marker remains on the card, each player without one of these markers suffers the penalty. (If a player cannot suffer the full penalty, e.g., paying 5 coins, they instead take a debt token worth -2, -3, or -5 points depending on the age.)

7 Wonders: Edifice contains two new double-sided wonders; one of them, Ur, can only be used with this expansion, while Carthage can be used in any game of 7 Wonders.

7 Wonders: Edifice can be used with any edition of 7 Wonders.

Turtle Game: Mini-Europe is a game similar to the Game of Goose. You start at one point of the gameboard and end at the finish.
Throughout the map you get to learn about Europe's Economy, Culture, Buildings, Successes, etc.

This game was designed for Mini-Europe, it's a miniature park in Brussels, Belgium.

boardgame: Spirit Island: Nation Schottland, Szenarien und Kartenerweiterung Promo
description:
When the proud tones of a bagpipe ring out on the beach, it can only mean one thing: the Kingdom of Scotland has reached the far reaches of Spirit Island! And the highland people don't come alone, because this mini-expansion also contains the two scenarios Diversity of Spirits and Varied Landscapes as well as new Aspect and Fear cards.

These contents are Part of the Feather & Flame expansion.

A new Event map to play using the core rules from the base game Forest Trails.

These are premium maps based on special Forest Events which feature new, additional, game actions, player interactions and new objectives.

Play Trick and Treat to please visitors. Plan routes for the ghostly land train and take visitors past areas of interest for more points (The Green Man Experience, pumpkin patch and bat caves).

Stroke of Genius is a card game for 3-5 players aimed at medical students and residents to teach and exercise Neurovascular concepts and differential skills. It replaces traditional faculty led case discussions, and has been rated 5 out of 5 by trainees for fun, efficacy, and ease of play. Participants play cards representing anatomy, predisposing condition, pathology, and pathophysiology to an evolving case. Play is most effective in a proctored setting to settle disputes and explain concepts.

—description from the designer

A Black Jack type game where you play and draw dual-sided cards in an attempt to get to as close to 7 without going over. Players take turns either drawing or playing cards in an attempt to get the perfect score. Use your resources carefully.

—description from the designer

Carrot is a social deduction game for 2 or more players. Players must use their cunning and wit to outsmart their opponents in order to claim the ultimate prize: the one & only carrot. Will you swap with your opponents and risk losing your only chance at victory? Or will you hold onto your card and hope it's the carrot? Be careful, one false move and you'll be left holding the short end of the stick! With endless laughter and endless possibilities, Carrot is sure to be a hit at your next game night.

In the 2 player game, you shuffle together 1 carrot and 1 stick. Each player gets one card facedown. The first player looks at their card, and may make any statement they wish (or remain silent). The second player may now ask the first player any questions or say anything else they'd like (the first player may respond however they'd like, including remaining silent). Then the second player chooses to either swap cards with the first player or keep their card. Afterwards, both players reveal their cards. The player with the carrot wins! If you play again, you swap roles and redeal cards.

—description from the publisher

Cabbage & Rooster is a trick-taking game AND a tile-laying game.

You're trying to farm the most Eggplants, Carrots and Tomatoes.

First you play a trick of cards. Then everyone lays down a tile.

The cards played determine who may put down a farmer, and who may recruit a Wily Cabbage to help against the tyrannical Roosters. The game integrates the card game with the tile game, and includes rules for both. It is appropriate for intermediate to experienced gamers.

—description from the designer

Riddles don't exist! Or so they say... However, when fans started getting the Too Many Bones: The Automaton of Shale pop-up book and found an opening to the biggest riddle ever made by Chip Theory Games (which lead to discovering Too Many Bones: Riffle), some wise ones also discoved a new riddle - within the riddle!

Solving this 2nd level riddle adds Joker cards and a rule cards to be used in Riffle's card deck.

Those sneaky lads at Chip Theory Games are always up to something!

Welcome to Lenox Avenue, Harlem, New York 1935! Where the nightclubs are hot, the entertainment is hotter, and the magic does not begin until midnight! Lenox Ave 35 is a lightweight matching and tableau building game for two players. Your goal in the game is to earn points by signing contracts for performers to your club’s revue, combining with their favorite songs to earn additional points.

In Lenox Ave 35, you will compete with another nightclub owner to put on the best music revue for the evening. To sign contracts with entertainment performers and select songs for the show, both players have to deal with a chaotic agent that controls the entertainment world in Harlem, laying down restrictive rules, but also awarding points as you satisfy their whims. Make the right moves for your club and maximize your points. The player with the most points earned at the end of the game is the winner!

—description from the publisher

In GeoSnakes, players race to be the first to move all of their pieces across the board. Using a bit of luck, players will want to think strategically about how to quickly make their way across without getting tangled up.

This game is easy to learn and one that families will enjoy playing together. With two ways to play, GeoSnakes will provide hours of fun for young and old alike.

—description from the publisher

Build a beer lineup with 5 popular categories: Lager, Wheat, Ale, IPA & Stout, as you experiment with 4 flavor sensations: Sweet, Sour, Bitter & Umami.

To win a round, you’ll also need a Brewery card & one of their 'Signature Brew' cards.

But, if other players develop a ‘Flavor Specialty’, they just might cut into your profits!

Each set features 3 Kansas City area microbreweries and 4 of their signature brews.

—description from the publisher

Sinkgo Academy is a turn-based, competitive card game that throws you into a non-stop adventure around the universe!

Welcome to Singko Academy, the coolest school in the universe. You and your crew go on space hops around the galaxy to collect souvenirs. You’ll meet new people, discover new foods, hit the beach, and maybe even fend off a few pirate attacks.

Players are dealt 1 Souvenir to start, 4 Crew Members whose skills determine your success in Encounters, and two Monkey Wrenches which bring strategy and chaos.

Encounter Cards drive the gameplay and determine if you collect a souvenir. Players pick Encounters in turn and use their Monkey Wrenches to thwart other players, steal their Crew and Souvenirs and occasionally reverse the direction of play.

The first player to collect five souvenirs wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Cyclocross is a bycicle game. It consist of two laps of a  course featuring pavement, wooded trails, grass, steep hills and obstacles. Every player begins with 5 speedcards and 1 action card. The winner is the person who finished as first. With action cards you can follow the racer that is just before you or you can attack. At the beginning of the game you also need to choose your speciality. This can be grass, pavement etc...

—description from the designer

Each player is a transport company.
The parcels arrived in Belgium by sea. The players are ready in Antwerp with your transporter to deliver the parcels to the different cities. At the beginning of the game you draw 8 city cards face down. 1 card of each region. You do not have to deliver one of these to the customer and you may cover it.
The idea is that you can reach the 7 remaining cards first and deliver a package there with your transport truck. You will receive the sum stated on the card for each city you have visited.
During your turn you may first move 3 steps forward with your transporter. Then you can roll dice. You can throw a roadblock, bridge or a car.

—description from the designer

Power Plants: Kickstarter Edition includes the following items not included in retail version of this game:

- Power Plants: Spades micro-expansion
The Spades micro-expansion provides a set of five spade tokens for you to add to the base game.  Each player will receive a spade token and can discard their token to swap one patch from your hand with one from the Nursery.

A trusty spade will help you dig up the perfect seedlings right when you need them!

- Power Plants: Sprite Ponds micro-expansion
The Sprite Ponds micro-expansion provides 2 new pond tiles with special scoring conditions for you to add to the base game.

Glittering ponds provide refreshment for busy sprites... but overcrowded patches stay thirsty!

- Power Plants: Stewards micro-expansion
The Stewards micro-expansion provides a deck of ten Steward cards for you to add to the base game.

The skillful Stewards bring their exclusive player powers to your games; enjoy a unique advantage that will guide your strategy!

- Power Plants: Wizards micro-expansion
The Wizards micro-expansion provides a set of 5 additional wizard meeples (one per player) along with modified rules for you to add to the base game.

Keeping a wizardly presence in the garden ensures that your new patches won't be messed with!
- Custom Trayforge box insert
- Upgraded gem tokens and sprite shapes
- Silkscreened tile bag

About the game:
Every wizard in the neighborhood knows that the best spell components are grown fresh. Unfortunately, only one particular plot of fertile soil in the area is the best for growing magical plants. Everyone agrees to "share" the garden, but you have a plan: Your team of loyal sprites will use the powers of the plants to infiltrate the garden as it grows, so that when everything is in full bloom, the most potent patches will belong to you!

In Power Plants, you are a wizard growing a shared garden of magical plants with your rivals. Each turn, you choose one of the patch tiles from your hand and add it to the growing garden. You can activate the added tile for its dynamic "plant" power or activate all the tiles it touches for their slightly weaker (but still very cool) "grow" powers. As the fields expand, you strategically deploy your sprites to gain control of more and more of the fantastic flora. Will your magical horticulture skills pay off?

Manipulate the garden's growth, gather magical gems, and deploy your team of loyal sprites to repel your competition and be in control of the most valuable fields when the garden is complete!

—description from the publisher

A WICKED expansion for Happy City!

Grozilla and his terrifying friends have arrived in town … and they are about to wreak havoc! They are hiding in a 4th pile, amongst new multi-coloured buildings. Do you dare to poke the beasts?

A wicked add-on with:


     Interaction
     Destruction
     Risk-taking
     And PIOUPIOUZILLAAAAA!

Do you know how to make money selling cars?

Перекуп ("The Car Dealer") offers you to dive into the shady world of car auctions and try on the role of a dealer. Your goal is to get rich buying used cars, upgrading them, and selling them at a higher price.

Who will you play? How about becoming a responsible dealer who will satisfy clients’ needs and expand his business? The second option will allow you to save on repairs and sell cars by "concealing" any tuning defects from your customers but earn large sums quickly.

The Dealer is an economic-and-money card game of medium difficulty, ideal for a large company. The game was entirely created by a Ukrainian author, which makes it unique and worthy of attention. As with most economical strategies, the players' task is to earn $5000 or sell 6 cars. For this, you will have used vehicles in different conditions, picky buyers, many tuning options at your disposal, and not necessarily pleasant surprises on your way.

The game is divided into three main phases. The first is bidding on damaged cars at auctions to get the best models at the lowest price. Then the player must decide whether and to what extent it is worth investing in repairing the purchased car. The sales phase involves selling the vehicle to the buyer at the highest possible price. The final stage is market updates and preparation for new auctions.

Get ready for hot auctions to win the title of the most successful dealer!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach the North Sea wins the game.

The game was published as a supplement to "Meidinger's Kinder-Kalender 1915".

—user summary
Win the most cards by matching primary and secondary colors in different combinations and ratios, going from simple (red + blue = purple) to complicated (red + blue + yellow = black).

Salad Shop is a cooperative word-building card game for 2-6 players.  You and your friends just opened a new restaurant specializing in salads, and the ten biggest food critics in the nation all just walked through your front door.  Get this right, and your salad shop is destined for glory.  But if you fail, your Grand Opening may become your Grand Closing.

Each round starts by revealing an Order card describing the critic’s preferences.  Players take turns playing veggie/letter cards from their hand, then work together to form the longest possible word.  The team scores points both for the length of the word and for satisfying bonus criteria listed on the Order card.  After 10 orders, your cumulative score determines if the critics drive your Salad Shop to success or doom it to failure.

Rules are also included for Veggie Swap, a 2-3 player competitive variant.  In Veggie Swap, players take turns transforming words into new words one letter at a time.  Fail too many times and you’re chopped from the competition.  Last chef standing wins!

—description from the designer

In this family strategy game by Londji you must tend your vegetable garden, making the right decisions to ensure the best harvest.

Test your strategy, decision-making and cooperation skills as you plant seeds and raise herbs and veggies.

Whoever harvests the most fruits and veggies wins.

Game in brief:

     Move the weather forecast: slide the weather cards & change the weather
     Work in the garden: plant seeds, make the plants grow, make the fruits sprout
     Harvest the vegetable garden: collect fruit tokens converting to money & points; store harvested vegetables 
     Open the farm shop: buy new vegetables or improvements
     Deal with ongoing calamities/pests: play pest control cards

The biggest day in the fishing calendar has arrived. Participants from all over the quaint and somewhat quirky English village of Upper Wallingham have arrived to compete for the ‘Golden Trout’ – a trophy only the best can win.

Choose the best fishing locations along 5 stretches of the river, load up your bait and fish like you’ve never fished before.

A colourful roll and write, number placement and fish drawing game. Game Play comes in two modes - Beginner and Pro Fishers with low ink and colour game sheets.

Before the tournament begins, each fisher (player) rolls 1 or 2 dice to see what type of zone their middle stretch of the river will become for the duration of the game.  This alters fishing strategies for individual players for varied game play.

Game Play:

Roll 2 dice (2 six-sided die) and use the numbers to decided where to enter them (fish) on each turn. There are 5 zones along the river to choose from, with 25 fishing spots. The catch (pun intended) is that number entry must be in order with no repeats.

Fishers may alternatively use their turn to buy worm bait or upgrade equipment. Fishers will need to manage worm bait carefully - this is used to alter dice numbers and is essential for tricky fishing spots. You only get a certain number of turns before the tournament is over so make every one count.

Scoring:

Players complete zones to start scoring points and increase their points by fishing twice in the same spot and drawing fish. Completing certain zone combinations will get you bonus points. Pro Fishers have more ways to score and play.

Pro fisher mode:

Pros may use 'hot zones' that change on each turn.

Fishing in hot zones catches high scoring or multiple fish - but avoid river rubbish or points will be deducted!

Pro fishers will need to balance buying and using bait, upgrading fishing weights and utilising hot zones productively to get the high scores.
 —description from the publisher

Cockleshell Heroes is a solitaire play game portraying Operation Frankton. Operation Frankton was a commando raid on ships based in the German occupied French port of Bordeaux during the Second World War. The raid was carried out by a small unit of the Royal Marines know as the “Royal Marines Boom Patrol Detachment”, who were part of a combined operations transported by the submarine, HMS Tuna. Variant rules for two players are included.

—description from the publisher

Family Faceoff is soon to be a Family Game Night Favorite! Imagine if charades, a scavenger hunt, and karaoke night had a baby. That’s Family Faceoff. This game is meant to get you moving. It will exercise your body, your mind, and your funny bone. It’s the kind of game where parents and kids are equal, and you could finish in 20 minutes, or spend an entire rainy day playing.
Participants divide into two teams. There are four decks of cards, each with a different type of challenge: Faceoff, Guess It, Find It, or Act It. The teams take turns spinning a spinner to choose the category of the current challenge. Each team will have a chance to defeat a challenge from the category.
If the category is Guess It, contestants try to get their team to correctly guess the missing word, "BLOBS", of well known song lyrics in the allotted time, replacing one or two words with BLOBS. If the category is Act It, contestants act out clues charades style in the allotted time for one point for each guessed correctly. If the category is Find It, contestants must find an item in their vicinity that matches the stated clue, one point per successful find in the allotted time. If the category is Faceoff, the two teams face off on a physical challenge (like "spin around 10 times as fast as you can") or a mental challenge (like "name all the fruits") and the team to meet the physical challenge first or be the last team standing in the mental challenge wins 3 points.
The first team to score 20 points wins. Ambitious teams are challenged to go to 40 points! House rules are encouraged.

Created in the minds of the virally funny Holderness Family and brought to you by the creators of Watch Ya’ Mouth.

One-hundred years ago, The Infinity Engine was shattered and its reality-bending shards have destroyed most of the world. Now, it falls upon you to gather your forces, defeat your adversaries, and rebuild the Infinity Engine! Will you survive?

This Enhanced edition of Shards of Infinity includes the Relics of the Past expansion with new Core cards and Relic mechanics!

Shards of Infinity: Relics of the Future is the first expansion to the popular deck-building game, Shards of Infinity. This expansion includes powerful cards known as relics that can only be leveraged by each of the leaders of the four factions when the player reaches 10 Mastery points.; when this happens, the player gains one of their two relics, with the other one being forgotten in time. The set also includes cards that can be used by any player, but when matched with the player's designated faction leader, the card receives an additional effect!

In addition, this set includes three new ways to play the game. A three player mode known as "Bloodbath": any damage dealt goes to both opponents rather than just one. In 2v2 Team Attack mode, players pair up to take on an opponent's team. A full solitaire mode, known as "Nemesis", pits solo players against the game.

—description from publisher

Cazadores is a cooperative miniatures game about tracking and hunting monsters, beasts, and other things that go bump in the night. It can be played solo or with up to 4 players with 1-4 miniatures per side.

Game Pitch
Each player creates a Cazador, a brave and formidable hunter hellbent on defeating fearsome mythical beasts, twisted beings, uncanny horrors, and other terrifying Adversaries. Cazadores are joined by a rare few Companions willing to follow them into the wilderness, through the desert, across mountains, and into the lairs of monstrous things and the darkness between the planes.

Cazadores is a narrative-focused game that can be played solo or cooperatively. It only requires 1-4 miniatures per side and a 24"x24" space, making it a perfect kitchen table game and introduction to those new to the miniatures hobby.

Features
A simple d10 dice pool system that resolves actions in a single roll (e.g., to hit, damage, and modifiers)
 A Time-based initiative system where actions cost "Ticks" contributing to a more dynamic flow for combat.
Streamlined stats and decision trees that make Foes' actions easy to determine and execute.
Unique attacks and abilities for Adversaries using a standard deck of playing cards to make them special and intriguing enemies.

Design Goals
The game was inspired by my desire to get more use out of miniatures from big box board games, usually from Kickstarter, like Blood Rage, Kingdom Death Monster, etc., that just don't see the table that often. Frankly, it also helps I can work on it while not being able to meet in person with others.

The current version has five Adversaries to take on but more are coming and the guts of the system are there. With a little imagination you can run with this to set up your own Hunts.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls – Ultimate Collector's Edition
description:
The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls is a tabletop card game about sacrifice, betrayal, and hoarding. Inspired by the massive hit video game The Binding of Isaac, the official card game was launched in 2018.

Experience the haunted and harrowing world of The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls yourself in this faithful adaptation. Collect treasure, gather loot, defeat monsters, and be the first to collect four souls. With over 300 cards in the game, there's a huge amount of replayability. Discover killer combos, enhance your abilities, betray your companions, and win!

The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls – Ultimate Collector's Edition contains the base game, the Requiem and Four Souls+ expansions, and an extra-large box for all cards.

You are the owner of a starship, flying out into the galactic expanse to make your fortune, gather prestige and make scientific discoveries! Hire a captain and crew, warp across the sectors revealing them as you go! Exploring each Quadrant to gather resources, claim them as your own, establish colonies, and gain advantages.

This is a science fiction game of mission solving, victory point gathering, with lightweight 3X elements. Explore, Expand, Exploit, but without Exterminate. (No violence please)

—description from the designer

boardgame: 太陽の子供たち (Children of the Sun)
description:
A powerful ghost called a yūrei attacks the very foundations of the dazzling city of Hamar. You are one of two Masters commanding the Children of the Sun, an organization of famous supernatural warriors with the power to handle the dangerous menace. When it comes to securing your legacy, however, you have some tough competition. Aim for greater achievements than the other Master and establish your reputation as the city’s most skilled ghost fighter!

In 太陽の子供たち ("Children of the Sun"), two players build a grid of cards around the ghost. When the siege is complete, each placed card determines the direction the ghost will move, and the previously occupied card is removed from the grid. If the player catches the ghost in a line of matching colored cards, they may acquire the removed card for their reserve or secretly check the cards making up the ghost's shield.

Once both players have played all their cards, they use any cards they "rescued" to attack the ghost. Players score points based on their ability to match the ghost's weaknesses over several rounds of attacks. The player who damages the ghost the most and receives the most points will win.

Solo rules are also available. When playing alone, the player manipulates two hands of cards to achieve the highest score possible.

boardgame: Just Like Mama Used to Say: Granny's House Expansion
description:
50 card expansion for Just Like Mama Used to Say

From the box back:

Almost as good as Granny's Pecan Pie

50 new cards that are funnier than the time we put that fake snake in Paw Paw's bait bucket

"One day, she wouldn’t stop multiplying! I can't take it anymore. But if I don’t do something, the earth will be engulfed by 'hers.' I’ll fight to destroy them!"
In the solo puzzle game 増殖彼女 ("Proliferating Girlfriend"), rows of cards represent millions of copies of a young woman, which continue to multiply out of control. Every round, a new row is added to the top. If cards reach the fifth row, the game is immediately lost. 
Each round, the player chooses which card to blow up and remove. When a card explodes, it may create a chain reaction through an adjacent card with a higher number. If multiple cards have a higher number, the player chooses which way the explosion will spread. But be careful, because if three adjacent cards are the same color, they will flip over and cannot be destroyed.
The player starts the game with 3 Ultra Girlfriend cards, which have unique positive or negative special effects. The game ends in victory once they collect 10 Ultra Girlfriends. A player aims to get the highest score possible by strategically blowing up cards, playing the right Ultra Girlfriends, and collecting cards to meet scoring conditions.

Operation String Bet is a five-mission campaign supplement for Insurgent Earth – Resist or Die, the science fiction, tabletop miniatures game of survival and combat on Earth after a massive alien invasion.

Designed to accommodate up to five players, (five Resistance characters) Operation String Bet offers new Player Character options, new equipment, and new Alien Occupation Force enemies.

The invaders are building something in the old industrial park south of town. Whatever it is, they're very protective - which means you definitely need to blow it up.

The Global Resistance Network is offering help, so grab your weapon, gather your friends, and head out. This planet isn't going to save itself.

NOTE: This is a supplement for INSURGENT EARTH. You need that core rule book to get the most out of this material.

Story
Becoming a researcher studying poison, I came to the sea to investigate poisonous creatures. I want to collect as many creatures as possible, but if I collect a lot, I will mishandle the creatures and get poisoned. Will you be able to successfully collect them and advance your poison research?

Poison Researcher is a trick-taking game with the theme of poisonous creatures of the sea.
To start a game, first remove cards from deck equal to the number of players and return them back to the box (they are not used in this game). Distribute the remaining cards to the players (12 cards per player).
The starting player chooses any one card from the dealt cards and plays it face up. After that, all other players play one card from their hand in clockwise order, adhering to must follow rules. After all cards have been played, the person with the highest number written on their card among the cards from the lead suit wins. An exception would be if all players played a different suit, the highest card among them wins whever its the lead card or not.

When you run out of cards, count the number of times you won (the creature cards you won). The player with the second most wins receives two Poison tokens, and the player with the third most wins receives one Poison token. The player with the most wins gets nothing. This is repeated as many times as the number of players, and the player who gets the most tokens wins.
If there are multiple 2nd place players, the player with the most poisonous cards wins as 2nd place.

The game comes with 5 difficulty levels. In the two highest levels, it's the second-highest card of the lead suit that wins the trick and it is the one with the most cards that wins the most Poison tokens.

The animals are also divided in two categories: toxic by Stinging (the Cone Snail, the Box Jellyfish and the Stonefish) and toxic by biting (the Sea Snake and the Blue-Ringed Octopus. In the 3rd and 5th advanced levels, the category of the lead suit determines whever the highest/2nd highest card(Toxic by Biting) or the lowest/second-lowest card (Toxic by Stinging) wins.

Host Your Own Pub Quiz is played over eight rounds with both teams answering the questions at the same time from eight given categories. Including bonus points for playing their Joker, the team with the most points after the eight rounds will win the game.

Water is important when traveling in the desert!
However, as the oasis began to dry up, the fellow travelers began to fight with each other. Collect more water than anyone else by winning a strategy game of puzzles, foresight, and interference!

You move the traveler piece and acquire the oasis cards scattered on the board, but the movement method is like a puzzle and it is a game that uses your head.
The pattern on the card indicates the position of the next card that can be moved, and the cards can be rotated, so the players plan their movement route.
As you move more and more, the number of cards on the board decreases and it becomes difficult to move, so the key to strategy is how long you can keep moving.
A cooperative rule instead of a tutorial and a battle rule with two different difficulty levels are prepared, and in the battle, players compete for the number of cards acquired and the survival of pieces while hindering each other's movement.
You can choose the difficulty level according to the friends you play with, and it's a simple puzzle game that you can play quickly in a short time.

植民地資本主義 ("Colonize") is a worker placement game in which players create companies as capitalists, secure colonial interests, and gain social prestige. Players aim to win the final game by gaining victory points through their own actions.
The winner is the player with the most victory points at the end of three generations in the game.
So grab your bag of gold coins and embark on a journey to capitalize the world.
There are two types of cards in the game.
These are the "Corporate Card", which is a workplace where workers are placed to generate funds, and the "Colonial Card", which produces a Colony Concession Icon that strengthens the effect of the Company Card and is a condition for goals.
The basic game flow is that the player pays the cost on the upper left of the card on his turn, purchases the card from the market, demonstrates its effect, and moves to the next player's turn.
The number of yellow shields drawn on the card is the "victory point", and the player who finally got the most of this is the winner.
You can also get victory points for achieving various "goal cards" and the majority of each icon, which will annoy the player.

Catch eels to the point of extinction so as not to be outdone by other fishermen! An eel overfishing card game featuring
bluffing and chicken racing ! Seeking good fishing, misleading rivals, and catching them to the point of extinction. This is an advanced brain battle between humans who are crazy about eels. If extinct, the fisherman with the lowest catch wins. Is there an answer to the question that simple rules pose to modern society...?

Avoid The Angry Avocado Fun Fruit Themed 2-4 Players Easy to Pick Up & Play Family Matching Pair Card Game.

On each turn, players take a card from the player on their left, trying to make pairs with the cards in their hands. Players discard any pairs they get, trying to get rid of all their cards. The player who gets stuck with the Angry Avocado loses the game.

Funny Lines is a party game you can play just with 3 to 50 players. The game can be played in 2 team mode with 4 players or more.

On the front of the cards there are images to guess. On the back of the cards are the costs of the clues.

Each team selects a player to do the drawing. Don’t worry. You don’t need to be good drawer!

Each team buys clues by paying into the pot and each drawer draws the clues bought by their team. Note that both boards are visible at all times to all players and you can thus use the other team’s drawing to guess.

You may guess at any time but guessing is not free. The first team to guess takes all the Fricos from the pot.

The boards are erased and a new round begins with a new card. 
The game ends after 6 cards. The team with the most money wins.

Finally a game you can monkey around with! The goal of the game is simple—see how high you can stack. Don’t worry, the apes won’t mind—just don’t pull the wrong tail, or everything will come crashing!

—description from the publisher

Stacking and balancing games of Monkey pieces.

Miss Match is a very simple massive multiplayer card game for larger parties, events, and conventions. Players start with an equal number of Miss Match cards each (at least 1) and face off in quick 1 vs 1 games. The winner, determined by a couple of card flips, will take up to as many cards as they played from their opponent (while keeping any cards they played). The player with the most cards at the end of the event wins the whole game. But players don't know how many cards are in the pool, how many cards other players have, or how many cards to play against each opponent.

The game can be played in-hand, in less than a minute, while standing or waiting. It's an ideal ice breaker game with player choice, luck, and exciting tie breakers. All the rules are on the card.

Miss Match is distributed as a Forever Free Print and Play Game for personal use.

—description from the designer

A Smurfs themed board game, made in Greece in the early 80's. It can be played from to 2 to 4 players. There is no dice, instead there is a wheel with numbers.

Each player in his turn spins the wheel, and moves in the board the exact spaces, as the number you have in the wheel. There are  some spaces on the board, where when you reach them you have to pick a card and follow the order that is written on it.

The winner is the first player, who reaches the end point (number 100).

—user summary

An additional parade board and 36 dice for a fifth player.

The dice can be included at lower player counts, if desired.

These 31 black dice can be used in three different ways:


     For color-blind players, they can replace one of the six colors of dice that come with the base game.
     They can be added to the game as a seventh color.
     Black dice drawn from the bag cannot be drafted, meaning there will be few dice to choose from.

A print and play sports simulation using cards and dice to replay ski jumping tournaments, including season play for men's and women's events.

The roll of a number of dice will determine the outcome of each jump with each jumper having two attempts with the second-round field being limited to the top qualifiers from the first jump. Scores are determined by distance jumped and style points with each result being influenced by the skier's real life performance in the season being replayed.

Basically just Monopoly, but set in the Italian city of Livorno (Leghorn). Some board adjustment are made (e.g. there are now 2 yellow fields and 3 purples) as well as theming the cards around famous locations or stereotypes of the city.

Everything was re-made from scratch, with all illustrations being hand-drawn. It first came out in 2005 but has since seen numerous prints. Explore the streets of Livorno, their famous statues, squares, but most importantly, their local food.

"E’ un’immersione nella “livornesità”, racconta i livornesi e la cultura popolare di Livorno. Un 5 e 5 da Gagarin, un Ponce dar Civili, un panino da Giovanni, un frate in Piazza Cavallotti…e la domenica tutti allo Stadio a vedere il Livorno."

NUM is a card game where each player will start with 5 cards, each turn is divided into 4 phases: play, replace, summon and discard. When playing cards from your hand to the board, you will take the cards that have a lower number than your card, seeking to complete the requirements of each warrior for his invocation.

Each warrior requires a number of spirits and portals depending on the number of victory points awarded, if any player runs out of cards at the end of their turn, the rest of the players must discard their hands and 5 new cards will be dealt to each player.

The first player to get 5 victory points for quick game or 8 points for normal game wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Nothing Personal: Danny "The King" Geekini Promo Card
description:
A special Gangster card from Game Boy Geek Season 4 Kickstarter.

"If Danny ever becomes Capo, the controlling player immediately receives 6 Respect."

boardgame: I Need a New Butt!: The Game
description:
I Need a New Butt! The Game is the family game that will be your new favorite of the toys & games in your home. Inspired by the best-selling book by Dawn McMillan and Ross Kinnaird, this hilarious 2-4 player card game for ages 5 and up makes a great funny gift for anyone who likes to laugh. Finding out your butt is broken is a bit of a worry! But think of the possibilities for a new one. In this kids game, find the perfect new butt! Board game cards include cheeky new butts such as “rocket butt”, “robo butt”, and “paintball butt”. You will love the hilarious Butt Popper and Butt Cheek Tiles. To play start by pressing the Butt Popper. Then, race around the board and match Butt Cards! To win, be the first player to collect all of your matching butt cheeks on your New Butt! cards.

—description from the publisher

Played with colored Velcro strips and fluffy balls on a special playmat, the goal of TAPLE is to place all of your Velcro strips into the play area.

Players gain the ability to place a strip by rolling or shooting their fluffy balls through opponents' arches or rings. The shots and placement of the strips can be quite creative, though the rules have some guidance on what is not permitted.

Plus! will challenge your SUM and BLUFF skills to achieve victory. To do this, you need to ask your opponents for a COLOR of cards and hope to form a set that adds up to 25.
But be prepared for the unpredictable, as you can receive the GRAY LETTER. That's why BLUFF becomes such an important strategy.
 Gather your crew and join the fun!

boardgame: O.P. Arena: Mr. 7 Minutes Abs Promo Card
description:
A special Promo Card from Game Boy Geek's 2016 Season 4 Kickstarter.

"1: Pecs of Fury: 4 or Higher - Do 3 damage to up to two dudes.
2: Muscle Bulge(persistent): Each damage on this dude increases his base attack damage by 1."

GRIM is a two-player tarot-inspired Expandable Card Game creature battler designed by Grimoire Games. Venture beyond the boundaries of life to a world where The Fiend reigns, imposing his will upon the Grim who war over the Spirits who roam there. For an eternity, the Fiend has cast his shadow upon the Grim, forcing them to battle for his entertainment. But you, a powerful and strategic Grimlord, are positioned to stand against him with your Grim to finally end the strife.

In GRIM, players combine two Elemental Grim Decks each embodying a unique style of play, to face off in an undying war. The strategy of GRIM begins with players' decisions regarding how to pair these Grim Decks together, as each Grim Card has Synergy Effects that will only trigger when played next to certain Elemental Grim. Attach Spirits for powerful Spirit Bonuses, react with Elements to negate and manipulate Attacks, and position wisely as you and your opponent place terrain effect Afterworlds in play. Secure enough knockouts and you will win the war.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Scouts Out: Raids and Reconnaissance in 1914-1918
description: A quick and simple 28mm skirmish game set in the historic Great War of World War 1. Take control of small raiding parties, from raiding the enemy trenches to rescuing downed pilots. The ruleset is designed in mind for the busy gamer, no more 3 hours games, get in play and have a blast all before your little one wakes from a nap.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Trench Offensive is a miniature wargame for two or more players, set in World War 1 trench combat. miniatures raid the trenches and try to get the objective and fight off the other set of miniatures.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Самый ужасный человек (The worst person ever)
description:
How do you like the idea of walking naked through the museum?

Let's be honest: deep down, under certain circumstances, you could. Or not? But your friends unanimously answer yes. In general, in this game you will find out what nasty things you are capable of, according to your friends.

Each turn you choose one nasty thing from random cards. For example, "Which of us would drink to unconscious at own grandfather's funeral?" or “Which of us would spit in the food of a child in a Spider-Man costume?” And then - choose one of the players!

The goal of the game is to socialize and have fun, but you can also play for victory points.

—description from the publisher

Do you put away your toys at night? If you leave them on the floor, night thieves will come for them. Little gnomes think that children don't need toys and take them for themselves! Let's take our toys from the thieves!

Every turn, you roll dice that defines gnome to find. Then everyone race to spot correct gnome on the board and trying to grab the correct toy as quickly as possible.

The winner is the one who takes five toys.

—description from the publisher

Wie die Wombats is a fast-paced cooperative eye and head twister where much is much less than it seems.
Together, the players estimate as quickly and correctly as possible where how many wombats are scurrying across playing cards. To do this, they have to lay out the cards in a pattern that shows the animals in ascending order. With each round, the scurrying gets wilder as more and more marsupials appear. Part of the teamwork is to turn over the sand timer every time a minute consists of fewer seconds than you would have estimated. In 5 short rounds, you learn to appreciate the estimation of your fellow players.

—description from the publisher
A Landlords-variant from Konami. Players move around the board, buying property. Property values increase or decrease based on the weather. The weather is determined by card draws.

Trainsilvania is the wacky rail haunt at dawn.
Dracula wants to go home. With the Trainsilvania Night Express, of course. Each player tries to deliver his 4 monsters to their home stations by train. If only you knew who is hiding under your own figures. And then the night express is full to bursting. There is pushing and shoving in this disorder. You need a good memory not to fall by the wayside here.

—description from the publisher

Da kommt der Wurm raus is a joyful colour game that has a lot crawling for it.
The worms are loose in the garden. On Stripy Toni’s birthday, everyone wants to give him the biggest and most beautiful bouquet of flowers. But each flower can only be picked by one if the six worms. Only those who know which worm comes out where can enlarge their bouquet. But after each turn, a worm changes the hole, which causes colorful confusion.

—description from the publisher

Nusfjord is a tranquil fishing village in the Lofoten archipelago in northern Norway. Fifty years ago, business was blooming when the codfish would come for spawning. Today, Nusfjord is more of a museum than a village, with less than a hundred people living there. Imagine how beautiful this place must be given that you must pay a fee to even look at the houses. Cruise ships used to pass by this long and now mostly abandoned island world.

In the time period in which the game Nusfjord is set, things looked quite different. Sailing ships dominate the fjord. The rocks around Nusfjord are covered in trees. As the owner of a major fishing company in Nusfjord on the Lofoten archipelago, your goal is to develop the harbor and the surrounding landscape, and to succeed you must enlarge your fleet, clear the forest, erect new buildings, and satisfy the local elders. Others do this as well, of course, so the competition is steep.

As with Agricola and Ora et Labora, Nusfjord has a worker placement mechanism, with each player starting with three workers that they place on a central board to trigger certain actions. Whether a player wants to clear a forest on their own board, buy a new cutter, or construct a building, they must place a worker on the appropriate space — which is possible only if room is available for this worker. Money is scarce, and one of the quicker and easier ways to get it is to place shares of your own company on the market. This risky action could be worthwhile because if you succeed in buying these shares yourself, you have usually won money and not suffered any disadvantages; however, if an opponent acquires these shares, then you must allow them to benefit from your hard-earned catches at sea. The village elders might want their own share of your catch as well, especially if you've visited them to take certain actions in the village, so if you don't take care, your catch could end up entirely in the hands of others and your camp will be empty.

Nusfjord Big Box contains the Nusfjord base game, two previously released expansions (Plaice deck and Salmon deck), and two new expansions (Trout deck and Besoekende deck), along with extra components, among them new guest meeples.

Hyperdrive is a disc flicking battle game where the perfect shot can turn the tide of war, but it’s also a strategic game of tough tactical decisions.

In Hyperdrive you will build your army, launch your fighters and target your enemies. If that doesn’t work, ram them into a black hole and claim the galaxy as your prize. Whoever builds two space stations in the neutral zone first, wins the game.

—description from the designer

From a set of 32 cards, 20 different cards are selected for each game, and two players battle using those cards.

The 20 cards at the start of the game and the order of the cards are random, but after that, there is no drawing, etc., and the use of the cards can limit the opponent's actions on his/her next turn.
There is no element of luck other than the order of the cards at the beginning of the game, and the game is a severe game in which victory or defeat is determined solely by playing.

There is a concept of cost, but there is no need to save mana to pay the cost, and accidents do not occur.
(You will know what I mean when you try it!)

It is possible to mix and match cards from the planned second and subsequent expansions.

—description from the designer

The march of technology across BattleTech’s eras is relentless—but some BattleMech designs never die. Each installment of this PDF-only series not only includes a brand new BattleMech or OmniMech, but also details Classic ’Mech designs from both the Inner Sphere and the Clans, now fully rebuilt with Dark Age technology (3085 and beyond).

What happens in Vegas stays in Vegas!
Requires the base game "Ryzyk Fizyk" - Polish edition of Wits & Wagers to play.

Due to the subject matter of many questions - the add-on is suitable for adult players. What is the sum of all the numbers on the roulette fields? What percentage of Poles sleep naked?
This add-on collects party, interesting and often spicy questions aimed at adult gamers. The additional double-sided board introduces new fields for betting and scoring.

—description from publisher

description:
24 hours left until the bomb explodes. Escape from the bomb in a limited amount of time! Extend your time and extend your life!
Advance the time and fall into the opponent! Your choice divides your destiny!

In the play time about 15 minutes, make full use of your intuition and time tactics! Grab the victory!

A BOMB card or SAFE card will be dealt at the beginning of the game.

Defeated if you have a BOMB card when you reach 24 counts. Winner if you have a SAFE card!

You can raise or lower the count with the plus card and a minus card!

boardgame: The Binder of Doom: Monster Strike
description:
Monsters are attacking Stermont! Join the Super Secret Monster Patrol (S.S.M.P.) to defeat the monsters before they destroy the whole town.

Featuring characters and settings from the Notebook of Doom and the Binder of Doom, you and your friends join the SSMP to stop monsters as they tromp through regions of town. Each equipment card has instructions on different and silly dexterous ways to aim your dice roll at the monsters. To win, defeat all 12 monsters before they escape or they destroy the town.

The Ring Routes – Icelandic Summer is family-oriented board game which will guide you through Icelandic landscapes, winding roads and hidden touristic treasures. It will introduce you to many tourist attractions and activities on the island – the very famous ones as well as those still undiscovered. It’s the first guide book which you can play and a perfect game for anyone who is dreaming or planning to visit Iceland!

It is an innovative hybrid board game for 2-5 players supported with a mobile application. It is designed in such a way to help you plan your trip to Iceland in real life. To do so, choose how many days you want to spend on the island and how many companions will you have. This decision will determine the level of the difficulty of the game and time needed to finish it. The more players and the less time you have, the more difficult it will be to achieve your goals. So watch closely what the others are doing and react when it is needed!

It may be a difficult task to plan your trip when you and your friends have different dream locations to visit and unfortunately time is limited for everyone. Moreover, all of you share the same rental 4×4 car, change as a driver frequently and face obstacles on your way, so sometimes you will need to make compromises. Remember, you won’t ever be able to see everything.

There are multiple routes to choose from to reach your goals. Use your skills and planning techniques to seize the opportunities, react to the constantly changing situation on the road and convince other players to go where you would like to. Play it wisely (or mean!). This trip will be a real test for your characters. Travelling in a group is never an easy task however if you manage it well then you can go anywhere together!

—description from the publisher

A route through the Tahuantinsuyo (Inca road), that you will have to cross as a Chaski (messengers of the Incas), carrying the promises of the next ruler.

Your objective will be to cross the Tahuantinsuyo, or perish in the attempt... but be careful, since other Chaskis are looking for the same as you, and may even eliminate you.

It is an exciting game for 2 to 4 players, where the Inca road will never be the same in each game.

—description from the publisher

¡El Inca ha muerto!

Tu misión como Chaski (mensajeros de los Incas) será atravesar el Tahuantinsuyo llevando las promesas del sucesor, pero ten cuidado, pues otros Chaskis están en la ruta buscando el mismo objetivo y podrían eliminarte en su intento por alcanzar su meta. Tendrás que ser el más rápido y fuerte para anunciar al sucesor del trono incaico.

Este es un excitante juego de 2 a 4 jugadores, donde el Tahuantinsuyo nunca será igual en cada juego.

description:
Evil Bunny is the Mario Kart of card games where a simple card can ruin relationships in an instant. It's fun, fast-paced, and ruthless. Players start with three golden eggs. On each player's turn, the top card is drawn and determines where they must pass their golden eggs. To the player on their left, right, or to the center of the table. This continues until there is only one remaining player with golden eggs. Pretty easy, right?

Here's where the game gets pretty ruthless. Scattered throughout the deck are Mystery Boxes. These cards give players the ability to sabotage and attack other opponents. You'll even have the option to target specific players. Let's just say, your definition of family might change after playing a round or two.

—description from the publisher

The game board is an eight-lane alley with a disc launcher at one end and a slot for each lane at the far end. Each slot will reward the person who launched the disc with a cash reward if a multiple choice question is responded correctly, a loss of money, or the opportunity to steal money from an opponent. The risks and rewards increase after every third round.

After fifteen rounds, all lanes are designated either “Win” or “Lose.” Starting with the player or team that has won the most money and going in descending order each entity launches one last disc. The game ends with the first to launch a disc into a Win lane for victory.

This is a faithful adaptation of the television series.

—user summary

Take turns to add an acrobat to the human pyramid. The first player who succesfully adds all six acrobats wins.

An element of randomness is added by spinning a wheel each turn to determine conditions for adding an acrobat.

The Erdei lakoma is a roll and write game, where the players goal to deliver as much animal to the restaurant in the middle of the forest as they can. For that you have to make roads from the edge of the board to it's center so they can reach it.The active player rolls and chooses from the dice. The other players can draw the remaining dice result. The game can end in two ways.
1. One player could manage to reach the restaurant with all of her/his animals.
2. Somebody has 2 Sectors filled on her/his board.
Who got the most points will win the game.
The game contains 3 game variations.
Mouse for 5-6 years old, Llama for 7-8 years old and Elephant for 8+ years old.
These variations are getting more complex.
The game in not going to be available at the market. The Pénziránytű foudation would like to use it for kindergartens and for schools with teaching purpose, to be used as a tool of a financial sensitization program.

—description from the designer

Spy Shot is a drinking game with thematic tasks. Players become spies, intelligence and action heroes. You can be in the shoes of James Bond, spending time with friends.

On your turn, you either complete a task or drink. For tasks you get bonuses, but you decide whether to spend them or not.

Sometimes you can draw animals cards from the deck. If they appears you should start making special move. The game will end when somebody draws "Mission Accomplished" card from the deck. The winner is the one who has the most completed tasks.

—description from the publisher

An evolution of Terachess I, Terachess II is a large Chess variant with 26 different kinds of pieces played on a 16 x 16 board.

Full list of piece types with their values:

N/A - King - (King's move + Initial King's jump that replaces castling)
10.2 - Amazon - (Knight's move + Queen's move)
8.4 - Eagle - (Unique move pattern, similar to Rhinoceros)
8.3 - Queen - (Queen's move)
8.2 - Sorceress - (Combines Cannon and Crocodile)
6.9 - Marshall - (Combines Rook and Knight)
6.1 - Rhinoceros - (Unique move pattern, similar to Eagle)
6.0 - Admiral - (Combines Rook and King)
6.0 - Lion - (Combines Elephant, Machine and Knight)
5.8 - Duchess - (Jumps up to 3 squares in any direction)
5.4 - Buffalo - (Combines Knight, Camel and Giraffe)
5.3 - Cardinal - (Combines Knight and Bishop)
5.0 - Rook - (Rook's move)
5.0 - Cannon - (Cannon's move from Xiangqi)
4.4 - Missionary - (Combines Bishop and King)
4.1 - Centaur - (Combines Knight and King)
3.4 - Bishop - (Bishop's move)
3.3 - Archer - (Diagonal counterpart of the Cannon)
2.4 - Troll - (Combines classic Pawn and 3-step jump)
2.3 - Prince - (Combines King's move and 2-step no-capture move)

2.0 - Knight - (Knight's move)

The main change to the regular Chess rules pertains to obligatory promotion upon reaching the last rank of the board. 
Only the following pieces are promoted:

Knight > Buffalo
Camel > Buffalo
Giraffe > Buffalo
Elephant > Lion
Machine > Lion
Centaur > Lion
Prince > Amazon
Pawn > Queen
Troll > Queen (Must move 1-step to promote)

Detectorama takes place at Village Green, a small town in South England where there is a curious interest in metal detecting. The Royal Society of Detectorists is organizing an exhibition to introduce this interesting hobby, so the Board has asked its members for a big collection of finds to fill their showcases. This demand has energized the entire detectorist community and turned afternoon detecting sessions into real battles for the best finds.

Inspired by BBC's "Detectorists" series, Detectorama is a competitive themed eurogame in which you will play the role of a member of the Royal Society of Detectorists seeking recognition from his fellow detectorists. Buy equipment, get it ready for your field trips, get detecting tips, official permits, discover new locations to detect and find the most valuable finds for the exhibition or even gold... who knows.

The main action of the game is metal detecting and everything revolves around it. This action is represented by a dice roll conditioned mainly by the equipment used for detection. But dice can be manipulated and optimized to reduce randomness; so before going out to the fields you better get ready.

—description from the designer

You are part of a community of neighborhood orchards. Among all the people who form the most sustainable and healthy garden so that products are environmentally friendly. But strange things are happening and some orchards have serious problems that are preventing crops from developing properly.

Will you be able to get your garden to have all the essential elements?

Help Ernest and Celestine to sneak into the town to find themselves back.

Trace a symbol on one of your partner back.
If they guess right , Ernest and Celestine get closer!
But if they are wrong , the cop may block the possibility for our two friend to find each other again.

It's a simple kids/family oriented cooperative game, in the universe created by the book of Gabrielle Vincent.

boardgame: El Timeline de los Derechos Humanos
description:
Are you ready to be the fastest of your friends in history? 
Do not hesitate any more and play with us the TimeLine, of human rights where you will see and learn scenarios and characters where historical events have occurred and passed, from 2000 years ago to our days.

Elea is a simple unification game of pure placement invented by Michael Amundsen in May 2022. There cannot be a tie in Elea.

Rules:
Players take turns placing a stone of their own color on any empty cell of a hexagonal grid of hexagons (a hexhex board).
The game is over when the board is full, and the player with the lower score wins.
Your score is 1 point per odd-sized group and 2 points per even-sized group.

The name is taken from the birthplace of the great ancient monist Parmenides, who argued that plurality is contradictory.

—description from the designer

A l'Ombre de la Louve is a Print & Play Strategic level Solitaire board-wargame simulating the expansion of the Roman Republic from BC 202, the victory of Zama against Carthage, to BC 102 the victory of Aquae Sextiae against the Cimbri. In this game, 1 turn represents 10 years of history. The player tries to conquer territories and develop colonies to increase the power of Rome. In correlation, the game mechanics simulate the opposition of the various nations to this expansion.
Can you do better than the Consuls of glorious Rome? Will you succeed in extending the shadow of the wolf over neighboring civilizations?

—description from the publisher (translated)

description:
At sunrise, the sleepy ducklings wake up. The morning is wonderful, it is sunny, a colourful rainbow stretches across the sky.
While the sun shines from the sky and the morning is warm and pleasant, the mother duck hurries to take her children to the pond for a swim. But a cloud appears in the sky and wants to hide the sun. If it is cloudy, the ducklings will not want to go swimming. Therefore, mum and her young ones should hurry to bathe while the sun is still shining and inviting them to swim.

On your turn roll the die and resolve the result with one of the ducks: if flower/mushroom/cherries comes out go to the first free tile with that symbol; when arrow comes out take a step in the indicated direction (forward/backward); if sun comes out take a step along the rainbow. To dive into the water, any symbol will do: insert the duck in the hole and turn the pond to show the next hole. Important: Mother Goose shows the way and can never be overtaken!
Players win the game when the last duck dives, while they are defeated by the sun reaching the cloud.

There are Outlaws on Whistle Mountain, and they are trying to capture the tower and take all the Mountain's riches for themselves! Can you build scaffolds and machines, save your workers from the whirlpool, and outmaneuver the devious Outlaws?

Whistle Mountain Outlaws is a print & play solo set of rules for Whistle Mountain.

—description from the publisher

Even more classes! Play Hero Realms as an Alchemist, Barbarian, Bard, Monk, Druid or (KS exclusive) Necromancer! Character Packs add a cool new dimension to player vs player games. Each match-up is a unique challenge which will require different strategies. When playing a cooperative campaign (either Ruins of Thandar or Dungeons), your Character Pack represents the starting point for your character that you will customize over time.

Bards are the singers of songs and tellers of tales. Bards embolden commonfolk, rally warriors, and captivate kings.

As the bard you will gain bonuses as you acquire champions, then inspire those champions to perform great feats!

Even more classes! Play Hero Realms as an Alchemist, Barbarian (both included in Hero Realms: Dungeons), Bard, Monk, Druid or the (KS exclusive) Necromancer! Character Packs add a cool new dimension to player vs player games. Each match-up is a unique challenge which will require different strategies. These powerful shapeshifters are one with the forest and its creatures.

As the Druid, the creatures of the forest come to your aid. When danger approaches you can transform into a great bear and protect your furry followers.

Even more classes! Play Hero Realms as an Alchemist, Barbarian (both included in Hero Realms: Dungeons), Bard, Monk, Druid or the (KS exclusive) Necromancer! Character Packs add a cool new dimension to player vs player games. Each match-up is a unique challenge which will require different strategies. When playing a cooperative campaign (either Ruins of Thandar or Dungeons), your Character Pack represents the starting point for your character that you will customize over time.

This pack introduces the monk.

The mental discipline, acrobatic skills and combat prowess of these spiritual warriors is legendary.

As a Monk, you will deliver precision strikes and armor-crushing blows with your bare hands. You'll play your Tao Lu cards in combination to deal devastating damage to your opponent and heal yourself.

Even more classes! Play Hero Realms as an Alchemist, Barbarian (both included in Hero Realms: Dungeons), Bard, Monk, Druid or the (KS exclusive) Necromancer! Character Packs add a cool new dimension to player vs player games. Each match-up is a unique challenge which will require different strategies. When playing a cooperative campaign (either Ruins of Thandar or Dungeons), your Character Pack represents the starting point for your character that you will customize over time.

The secret art of necromancy harnesses the magic left behind when the spirit leaves the body at the moment of death.  A necromancer can harness this magical energy to animate the remains into soulless warriors that know neither pain nor fear.

As a necromancer you will summon a horde of skeletons to fight for you, and use your magic to imbue them with inhuman strength.

Heart of Ice is a solo adventure game in which you are an explorer searching for a mysterious artifact in a dangerous, post-apocalyptic winter world. In 23rd century GAIA, the AI system who controls the weather satellites brings the eternal winter to the Earth. Only you can change the future of humanity. The end of this story depends entirely on your choices.

—description from the publisher

"Bowl your opponents over with your dicemanship! Your challenge is to attain the hightest possible score for a 10 frame game." 
A simple dice game that mimics a game of bowling, using 10 special dice with 5 blank sides and one side showing a bowling pin. "The dice that turn up blank indicate the pin has been knocked down. The dice that show a pin on top indicate the pins have been left standing." Players can re-roll once to try to knock all 10 pins down. The score sheet is identical to a standard bowling score sheet, and points are counted identically. The highest possible score is 300, just like in bowling. (quotes from the rules sheet)

Gotcha is a dice game for the whole family. 
As a hero, try to defeat as many bad guys as possible and collect the most points. Problem is if you have 5 points of damage you are dead.

boardgame: Lock & Spell: A Game of Fortunes
description:
Destiny awaits you within the chests of fate!

Open three chests, and the treasure is yours — but only keys enchanted with the correct spells can unlock your fortune. You must summon the cards and cast your hand upon the keys. If you can't draw the right spells, offer a token of fate and try again. Get caught unable to enchant a key, and you lose a fate token. Run out of fate tokens, and you lose the game.

Lock & Spell is a single player card game of luck and tactics. To begin, reveal a set of keys and draw a hand of five spell cards. Each key shows the required hand needed to enchant it, with some keys being more difficult to obtain than others.

Spell cards come in four suits valued 1-10. If the player has a hand of cards that matches a key's spell, they may cast their magic and gain that key. If not, the player must use a token of fate to discard any number of spell cards and draw back up to five. Fate tokens are placed upon keys, and for the remainder of the current turn that key may not be gained. If during the turn a player's hand matches the spell shown upon a tokened key card, a penalty occurs and both the key and fate token are discarded.

Gameplay continues until the player unlocks three chests and gains the treasures within. If the player should run out of fate tokens or keys, they lose.

Winning a game of Lock & Spell rewards the player with a unique fortune, which is based upon the order of treasures gained. Players also score their game. More difficult chests grant higher scores, and points are gained for the remaining fate tokens not discarded.

Cast your magic upon the keys and unlock your fate!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ヘビーソルジャー (Heavy Soldier)
description:
Powered Suit, "Combat shell" and armored vehicle combat in 2150 on TTRPG "Metal Head" world.
It can use as expansion for TTRPG Metal Head, vice versa.

boardgame: My Very First Games: Let's Cook
description:
Every dish you make is sure to be a success. A large helping of sorting, a heaping spoonful of puzzle solving, and a dollop of memory fun will make it happen. Let's Cook includes three different games that have children practice recognizing and naming vegetables and other foods, guessing their fellow players? Favorite ingredients, or remembering what's needed for a recipe. Play together and find a love for cooking round after round. Get to the kitchen, it's dinner time!

—description from the publisher

The players must try to form whole words from the individual syllable cards. Only those who skilfully collect cards, trade them or negotiate with fellow players can put the words back together correctly.

The correct composition of the individual words is facilitated by the illustrations on the cards.

—user summary

Au-to oder Was-ser-lei-tung - die Abbildungen auf den Karten helfen, die Wörter wieder richtig zusammenzusetzen.

Die Wörter sind leider alle auseinander gefallen und die Silben sind ein einziges Chaos. Nur wer geschickt Karten sammelt, sie tauscht oder mit Mitspielern verhandelt, kann die Wörter wieder richtig zusammensetzen.

Ein Silben-Legespaß von Erika Bruhns für kleine und große Leseratten, mit bunten Illustrationen.

—description from the publisher

Speedy Delivery is a climbing game with a courier theme.
In Speedy Delivery, each round one of four players will take on the role of a courier, attempting to deliver (shed out) their entire hand of cards before the other three players can prevent it from happening by shedding out first.
Each round, players bid to determine who will be the Courier and who will be the Obstacles.
The play is standard ladder climbing play, familiar to anyone who has played Tichu, Haggis, Chimera, or many traditional climbing games.
One player leads with a card combination. Other players must follow with a higher combination of the same type, play a "bomb", or pass.
Points are rewarded for successful rounds and subtracted for failed rounds. The highest score after several rounds wins.

Wargame vault: https://www.wargamevault.com/product/427969/THE-FIRST-WAVE

Despite these preparations, very little went as planned. Ten landing craft, and the soldiers manning them, were lost before they even reached the beach.
Of the nine companies that landed from the first wave, only A Company of the 116th RCT, at Dog Green, and the Rangers to its right, landed where intended.
As soon as the infantry jumped out of the landing vehicles, they found themselves on sandbars 45 to 100 m from the shore. Before reaching the beach itself, they had to wade through the water that separated them, sometimes up to their necks, and then they still had 600 feet or more to go before they made it to shore.
As soon as the boats approached within a few meters of the shore, they received intense fire from automatic weapons and artillery.

This game with Roll and Write mechanics tries to recreate the arrival of US troops on the coast of one of the Omaha beaches. This game contains the necessary sheets so that you can print at home on a home printer without the need for color, you only laminate the sheet of the board, with a couple of dice and erasable markers, you can enjoy this game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Ars Bellum II: Scénarios "Le Prince Noir"
description:
In this expansion, at the heart of the Hundred Years War (1337-1453) find Edward of Woodstock, King of England (1330-1376), known as The Black Prince facing the French forces of King John II the Good or the coalition Franco-Spanish of Enrique Trastamare pretender to the throne of Castile.
It contains :
- 1 11-page scenario booklet to play the battles of La Chaboterie (September 17, 1356), Poitiers (September 19, 1356), Inglesmendi (March 1367) and Nájera (April 3, 1367)
- 1 sheet of 108 pawns and markers and one of 54 pawns and markers representing the English, French and Spanish armies of the campaign
- 2 A4 maps and 2 A3 maps

boardgame: Hero Realms: Watery Grave Campaign Deck
description:
Watery Crave continues the adventure, where "The Lost Village" and "The Ruin of Thandar" left off. It features a full-color Adventure Book, and contains over 100 cards, including 12 oversized Master cards.
Choose one of four different ships and set sails.
Each ship has a different helpful ability, but be careful. It has HP, and if it gets destroyed, you lost!

Each 33-card Adventure Deck contains everything you need to bring the character all the way from 1st to 24th level. 
It contains updated skill and treasure cards from the complete campaign sets (Ruins of Thandar, The lost Village, Watery Grave and two unknown ones) 
So in the future, if you own all 5 campaign sets, you dont need this Deck.

With a Character Pack and Adventure Deck you can take your character through any Hero Realms adventure. For example, it will allow you to play your Druid in the Ruin of Thandar series, or bring your Wizard through the Dungeons 12-part adventure!

You can also create leveled-up characters for PVP play.  Choose any level and you will have the tools you need to build your character.  There are thousands of possible ways to build your character as you level up!

Or, play a legacy style PVP series with a friend. You each start at level 1. After each game, record the winner, then you both level up. First player to 13 wins is the champion!

Each Adventure Deck contains:

5 additional Skill Tree cards 
    7 additional Ability Tree cards
    1 Bonus health card
    20 treasure cards 
    Rules sheet with full leveling instructions including skill and ability tree diagrams.
