description:
Build your dream road from puzzle pieces! Create a track up to 3 meters long, plan where the car park and gas station will be, and race to the finish line!

Automotive enthusiast on board? Let him spark his imagination, create routes of his own design and become a master of the steering wheel in the race around the city.

Droga: Duże puzzle z grą (direct translation: Road. Large puzzle pieces with the game) will appeal to the youngest who love to build their own constructions. The elements can be combined in any order, using all, part of the road, or even several sets of puzzles.

Decide where the START and where the FINISH will be and where to place the signals. Plan the location of the gas station and parking lot. The fun never ends, because each time you can arrange the pieces in a different configuration. Route ready? We're off to the race!

After laying out the route, players choose one of the attached cars or use their toy cars. They take turns rolling the dice, moving the cars by the designated number of spaces. The one who finishes the race first wins.

—description from the publisher

This is a Print and Play partial reinvention of the 1987 game published by FASA. In collaboration with three individuals, Battletech Succession Wars is an attempt to address the shortcomings of the original game and (hopefully) attract a new fan following of enthusiastic grand strategy gamers to the Battletech genre.

Five players command the military, political and economic resources of the five Great Houses of the Inner Sphere: Davion, Steiner, Liao, Kurita, and Marik.

The goal of the game is to neutralize fellow House players through military conquest becoming the next Star Lord of the Inner Sphere.

An expansion for Glory: A Game of Knights that introduces:


     A 5th player with all components needed.
     Errantry - new way to duel players or NPC knights.
     Scenarios. A set of conditions that change the base game and force change of the winning strategies.
     New Titles, Supporters, Travel cards - and more!


In addition to the artists known from the base game, the expansion has a set of great paintings made by Graham Turner who is also a jouster.

boardgame: Complete Murder Mystery: Aboard the RMS Titania
description:
Land ho! Gather your crew for an evening of in-depth investigation aboard the RMS Titania. In this golden age of steam travel, a shocking murder has taken place and you and your guests will either be sinking or swimming as you try to solve this mysterious crime. You will need all hands on deck to stay out of deep water and catch the loose cannon before they can strike again! The Murder Mystery on a Cruise Ship is the perfect way to entertain friends and family, with your very own Murder Mystery Night. Suspense and suspicion will be the name of the evening with this Murder Mystery that is presented in a classy whiskey-style gift box and includes six different plots for multiple fun and entertainment, character profiles, invitations and name badges - all the players need to do is work out whodunnit! This brilliantly entertaining Murder Mystery game is perfect for dinner parties as well as virtual get togethers and is suitable for 6+ players aged 14 years and over.

—description from the publisher

Dive into a unique Gothic horror fantasy board game experience that will keep you on the edge of your seat! Featuring more than 40 beautifully-sculpted miniatures, stunning gothic horror artwork, and a compelling campaign style gameplay, this tile based dungeon crawler will keep you and up to three fellow victims of the Studium coming back for more.

Players take on the role of newcomers held at the mercy of the menacing forces that control the labyrinthian Studium. Having been brought together to form a house, they are tasked with delving into the darkest depths of the Studium to eradicate unspeakable horrors. While exploring the ruined halls of the Studium, players will be challenged by engaging story events. The choices that players make may have grave consequences for their characters, their companions, or even the whole of the Studium. Combat features a familiar dice rolling experience that uses each players individual equipment and skills to provide greater depth.

Studium | No Exit is intended to be played in multiple play-sessions, with player characters evolving over time. Shaping each character are milestones that provide vital enhancements to survivability. Milestones can reflect the accumulation of knowledge, the honing of battle crafts, the cohesion of working as a team, or even romantic bonds forged between two characters. Additionally, a unique “Reflection” system allows players to leverage the Studium’s abundant resources to forge formidable weapons, armor, and accessories.

Explore an ever-changing labyrinth of terror and battle unspeakable horrors. Unlock the secrets of the Studium, upgrade your gear, and join forces with fellow survivors if you hope to make it out alive... Studium | No Exit aims to be the horror-filled board game adventure that you won't soon forget.

—description from the publisher

Lebanon is a country with a rich history and a bright future, but with today's rampant corruption, we need a reset. A Game of Corruption takes you back in time to Lebanon’s golden years and gives you a chance to fix what went wrong and change the course of history.

Each player is randomly and secretly assigned a role, either Reformist or Corrupt Elite, and then works on campaigning in the different districts to amass the most seats in the parliament.  On your turn, you can either swap districts with other players, draw or replace a new secret objective, or spread influence by campaigning in swing districts or by battling against other players to take their districts. The game moves into the second phase of electing a president once 5 rounds are played or 6 objectives are completed. Would the corrupt succeed in sowing confusion and distrust and bluff their way to power? Or would the reformists be able to see through their game and vote them out?

The game of corruption is tricky and treacherous. Do you have what it takes to get to the presidential chair?

This card game simulates a 200 kilometer long cycling race. During each player's turn, they play a card from their hand. This could be an attack card, letting them escape from the peloton. Other cards cause accidents for your opponents, change the circumstances of the race, or advance the distance counter.

When the distance counter reaches predetermined values, there is an intermediate sprint by the leading cyclists. The winner of such a sprint wins a bonus card that can be played at the end of the race, to win the game.
At the end of the game, gain 1 victory point for each Military Victory token in your neighbors’ Cities

The march of technology across BattleTech’s eras is relentless—but some BattleMech designs never die. Word of the sheriff's demise has finally reached our neighboring towns, and their residents are lookin' to take advantage of our little slice of heaven. Foul Neighbors features a combination of Ruffians from Misty Bluff and Sprinkle Falls, alongside a couple new heroic townsfolk. You'll face off against hungry cannibals, dodgy salesfolk, diseased doctors, and plenty of other unsavory fellas this time around—each with their own unique method of making your lives miserable!

Foul Neighbors is a combination of the Ominous Skies and Eerie Neighbors expansion we had originally planned; by combining the two, we’re able to cut down on materials and reduce waste.

—description from the publisher

The Peddler has gotten herself into a bit of trouble. After a rough night of gambling with the locals, she bet it all and lost all her wares—but don't worry, the Peddler's always got a plan! In the Odd Jobs expansion, you’ll be taking on new roles and working some odd jobs to help the Peddler restock her shop. Scavenge the Hoselands’ nooks and crannies for anything that could be of use; you’ll need to get creative if you want to survive. With a new plethora of gear and roguelike elements, it's a new way to play Townsfolk Tussle!

boardgame: World of Warcraft Trading Card Game: Battle of the Aspects Raid Deck
description:
An asymmetrical two-sided card game where one player takes on the role of the raid bosses and their minions, and is opposed by one or more other players who take on the role of the raiding heroes.

The expansions contains the deck and special rule cards for the Raid player. The raiding heroes will need 60-card decks (not included) to defeat them.

This raid focuses on the final confrontation with Deathwing, the corrupted Aspect of Earth.

boardgame: World of Warcraft Trading Card Game: The Caverns of Time Raid Deck
description:
An asymmetrical two-sided card game where one player takes on the role of the raid bosses and their minions, and is opposed by one or more other players who take on the role of the raiding heroes.

The expansions contains the deck and special rule cards for the Raid player. The raiding heroes will need 60-card decks (not included) to defeat them.

This expansion will focus on several villains from across the history of Azeroth, such has Aeonus, Achimonde, Mal'Ganis, Mannoroth, and more.

Hulk was banished from Earth to the planet Sakaar, where he became a gladiator. Now he is stronger than ever before and blinded by rage over the death of his family. With the support of his Warbound, Hulk returns to Earth, bent on exacting revenge on those who wronged him, and anybody else who dare stand in his way! New York becomes a gladiatorial arena as the mightiest heroes must test themselves against World Breaker Hulk.

This expansion brings an epic war between the strongest characters in the world. Consumed by anger, Gladiator Hulk is ready to smash anyone who comes his way. When even the Olympian Hercules and Ares falter, Iron Man will don his massive Hulkbuster armor to try to stop Bruce Banner. The Sentry may be our last hope, as long as he can resist being consumed by The Void.

—description from the publisher

The Story - The Gypsy peoples originate from Sind region now in Pakistan.  When entering west Europe they initially had letters of protection from the King of Hungary.  This privileged situation did not last long as amazement at their way of life led to hostilities.  The Gypsy way of life still leads to hostilities from people of their host nations.  Europeans regard "private property" as sacrosanct, whereas gypsies do not have a word for possess which gives to two incompatible ways of life and a continual problem of gypsies being regarded as "thieves" from the European's view.

The Object - Earn as much gold as we can and bring it to the Big Gypsy Gathering.  We need to help finance the various Festivities of Weddings, Baptisms, Meetings, Feasts and Entertainment.  This must be done before border checkpoints are closed, preventing us from travelling and earning our living. We have only two rounds of events to accomplish our goal of raising money for the Big Gypsy Gathering.

The newest editions of the Novice Novice ("Nobi Nobi") TRPG series are Sword and Magic. The Magic set focuses on characters that fight with magic, such as Wizard, Priest, Hermit and Necromancer, but a with a few more unique additions like Clown and Demon King.

Novice Novice TRPG Magic may be played by itself, or integrated with Sword for a more exciting Sword and Sorcery themed Fantasy RPG. It's also possible to integrate with other games in the Nobi Nobi series.

Compared to the previous iteration of the game, Steampunk, there are no new mechanics, but the box has been made smaller for more ease of portability.

boardgame: Marvel United: Fantastic Four – The Coming of Galactus
description:
In his insatiable hunger to consume world after world, the cosmic behemoth known as Galactus has always relied on his herald to pave the way for his conquests. However, the Silver Surfer has betrayed him, forcing Galactus to look for new heralds. Searching the universe for suitable replacements, he conscripted four powerful beings, Air-Walker, Firelord, Nova, and Terrax. With their aid, can anyone stop the coming of Galactus?

The Coming of Galactus is a Kickstarter Exclusive expansion that brings a conflict at a truly epic scale to Marvel United. The Heroes must take on the insurmountable threat of Galactus himself, as well as the united might of his four Heralds.

This expansion presents players with a variety of challenges. Facing Galactus, with the four Heralds are his Threats, Heroes must complete a whole series of Missions under the destructive gaze of the Devourer of Worlds, in a desperate attempt to stop Galactus and save Earth. Heroes may also face the Heralds of Galactus directly, in an intense game mode where each of them brings their full might to the table. Finally, each of the Heralds can be faced on their own in a variety of encounters.

The Coming of Galactus Kickstarter Exclusive expansion comes with:

1 Galactus Game Piece
1 Air-Walker Game Piece
1 Firelord Game Piece
1 Terrax Game Piece
1 Nova (Frankie Raye) Game Piece
1 Galactus Villain Dashboard
24 Galactus Master Plan Cards
8 Galactus Threat Cards
8 Galactus Missions

12 Air-Walker Master Plan Cards
6 Air-Walker Threat Cards
1 Firelord Villain Dashboard
12 Firelord Master Plan Cards
6 Firelord Threat Cards
3 Firelord Missions
1 Terrax Villain Dashboard
12 Terrax Master Plan Cards
6 Terrax Threat Cards
1 Nova (Frankie Raye) Villain Dashboard
12 Nova (Frankie Raye) Master Plan Cards
12 Nova (Frankie Raye) Hero Cards
1 Heralds Villain Dashboard
12 Heralds Master Plan Cards
6 Heralds Threat Cards
8 Herald Tokens
6 Locations

boardgame: まじかる☆ベーカリー〜わたしが店長っ (Magical ☆ Bakery: I'm the Manager!)
description:
Corone, a Magical Girl who got tired of being overworked at a poorly managed "black" bakery, has declared independence and established her own store. Can she run things clean, or will she just end up producing another bakery filled with oven theft, embezzlement and underpaid workers?

This is the sequel to Magical ☆ Bakery, which is built on a completely new game system built around strategic store management, competing to grow your store via the use of various card effects.

A quick, family friendly dice game.
Players take turns adding dice from their personal dice pool to the snowball in the middle of the table, making it grow bigger. Roll all the dice in the snowball to see what happens. Sometimes you will get dice back, and sometimes you won’t. If you run out of dice, you are out of the game. Last player with dice wins!

Currently this is an inexpensive Print and Play game, containing only the instructions. Players are required to provide their own standard D6 dice.

-description from designer

Pas Assez, French for "Not Enough", is a tarot based climbing game with a rather unique twist: instead of playing elaborate melds of cards, players try to out-rank each other with increasingly high numeric values.

The game is a climb up to see who can take the most tricks and squeeze the most Major Arcana cards out of the opponents, and thus score the most points.

—description from the designer

Search History is the only party game that uses real searches in it’s hilarious and unique gameplay.

Read one of three prompts aloud and compete with friends and family to anonymously create the most believable search query.

Take turns guessing what the most commonly searched phrase is and score points if your answer is guessed by others.

Combine with the Family Edition for even more fun!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: まじかる☆ベーカリー ～叛逆の魔法少女～ (Magical ☆ Bakery: Magical Girl Rebellion)
description:
Korone's Magical Bakery, which earned sales and success by any means necessary, is now the number one bakery in town. But there's a problem: the part-timers working at the store don't think the manager is going to pay them properly, and they've challenged her to a magical duel, with their salaries on the line! But if the store doesn't stay open, no one gets paid at all. Can the workers prevail with victory uncertain, and their salaries at stake?

Magical Bakery - Magical Girl Rebellion is the sequel to Magical Bakery - I'm the Manager, and the third game in the Magical Bakery series, with its own unique gameplay.

One player is the manager, and the other 1-3 players play as the workers in a magical duel. The workers want to win their pay, but the manager's goal is not to pay them anything, and the manager has powerful magic conferring a significant advantage. Over the course of ten rounds, the workers and manager select magic cards, and then secretly pass them to the manager, who will then declare who won the round - but once per game, the manager can lie! If the workers catch them in a lie, they win the round, and if the workers win three rounds out of the ten, they win.

boardgame: まじかるキングダム ライスル帝国侵攻 (Magical Kingdom: Ricel Empire Invasion)
description:
First Expansion to Magical Kingdom. The Kingdom of Paneteria, the Country of Bread, has grown rapidly and is now seen as an existential threat by the Ricel Empire, the Country of Rice, and they have mounted an invasion.

This expansion adds new cards and powers to deepen the Kingdom Building strategy.

The game of strategy, sabotage, and espionage. In Black Tie you take on the role of director of intelligence for your own agency. You will have to manage and increase you assets, misguide and disrupt opponents, and most of all protect and collect secret data.

Black Tie is a twist on deck builders where players are competing to gain the most points. Players earn points by procuring cards, called assets, and by stealing data cards from other players.

On their turn players can play cards to perform a variety of actions including building security systems, activating agents, sabotaging other players, and stealing assets.

Black Tie takes hand management to a whole new level with mechanics such as card storage, partial hand retention, and the ability to increase hand size. Careful hand management is more important than ever thanks to Black Tie's unique card stacking mechanism where players may play multiple identical cards as one move in order to pull off more and more powerful actions.

The game ends immediately when all of the double agent cards have been purchased and played.

-description from designer

boardgame: まじかる☆キングダム - パネテリア王都争乱 (Magical Kingdom: Riot in the Royal Capital)
description:
Second expansion to Magical Kingdom. A magical girl studying a forbidden spellbook has her mind taken over by the evil Witch of Calamity, who incites a civil war that engulfs the capital of Panteria, the Country of Bread. The queen must call on the power of heaven to help bring the civil war to an end.

This expansion adds a new mechanic allowing buildings to be scrapped after they are built for bonuses, in addition to new cards.

Pineapple Royale is a card game in which players control different fruits in a battle to be the last one standing. Each fruit comes with its own unique stats and some have special abilities that can help them in battle. These may provide defenses, movement buffs, or ways to mess with your opponents. The fruits fight in an arena styled after a kitchen where there are objects such as countertops, kitchen sinks, and even an oven to move and position yourself around for battle. Some of these items take up territory, while others provide strategic benefits and threats to the players.

Players will need to collect kitchen utensil-themed cards to use against their opponents. You will start out with only a spork but can collect additional cards such as chef knives, graters, and chopsticks to increase your attack, or items such as fruit stickers to recover your health. Once a fruit runs out of their “servings” they are eliminated from the game.

The play area consists of a ring of randomly dealt cards that get shuffled around after each round. This creates constant variety as no two games or rounds will have the same layout to maneuver around. Movement is 2-dimensional around this circle, meaning your options can be limited depending on the randomly dealt battlefield.

During a game, you will find heavy amounts of social interactions as other players try to get you to gang up on other players, or keep the peace and draw a card instead of attacking them. All of the utensil cards (aside from the spork) can only be used once, creating a tricky balance between when to strike and when to wait for the chaos of the game to do the work for you. Once the first fruit is eliminated things will quickly spiral into chaos and bring about a quick conclusion to a game.

description from designer
publishers: Seat at the Table Games LLC

description:
Energy Management: Tactical is a paper-based simulation of tactical air-to-air combat using the innovative TRACE-R combat system.  The system uses energy-maneuverability theory to faithfully simulate close-range dog-fighting tactics and fighter performance with relatively high accuracy.

The system eliminates the limitations of hex and counter systems by replacing them with geometric construction methods using paper and common drafting tools to allow for quick, accurate, and fluid game-play.

Each game turn represents approximately 10 seconds of flight per aircraft.  During its turn, an aircraft may make any number of individual maneuvers (called “segments”).  Each segment is tracked on the aircraft’s flight log to calculate changes in the energy state of the aircraft.  The key to victory will be in managing an aircraft’s total energy while maneuvering into attack position.

—description from the designer

A game of cards and tokens where the objective is to embezzle your friends and proclaim yourself the winner by having the biggest loot. It has three game modes to play from 1 to 6 people.

In the first game mode, you can play alone or against another player, each player starts with 6 chips, in order to place bets, the first player to embezzle his opponent by taking all the chips wins the game.

The second mode can be played by 2 to 4 players, each one of them will take a card from the deck and depending on the action of each card you will be able to obtain chips, after four rounds the chips are counted and the player with the greatest amount wins the game.

The third mode can be played by 2 to 6 players, each player starts with 5 cards, they must exchange the cards as quickly as possible in order to build a deck that allows them to make the most points and be the first to reach 15 points. of victory.

-description from designer

La Quête du GRAaL is a push-your-luck cooperative game in which you take the role of Parcival, going on a journey to find 4 great relics.

On their turn, players flip cards one by one, trying to find certain symbols, which will allow them to push their journey further by adding cards to the common deck. They must be careful, though, not to flip 5 skull symbols or they will lose a life, thus adding another skull in the deck.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
A terrible sandstorm is raging in the desert. By dawn, when everything finally quiets down, the suricata family's burrow is covered in dust, their items scattered everywhere. Worst of all, Kiki, the youngest member of the family, is missing! The suricata family band together and do something they've never done before: they leave the burrow and its surroundings to travel the desert together to find Kiki. You control a member of the family, build your own deck and if you complete the adventure deck fast enough, you might just save Kiki.

The game's special feature is that you build not only your own deck, but also a shared deck. In your own turn, you first play some of your cards in order to:

     get a new card for yourself
     add a new card to the common deck
     perform a special action
     reveal the next adventure card
     "defeat" the current adventure card.


You can do any number of these as long as you have enough cards. At the end of your turn, you draw new cards.

This simple card game puts a twist on the familiar deck-building mechanic.

Can you hide from the eagles? Will you be able to defeat the rattlesnake? Or perhaps make new friends along the way? But most importantly, will you be able to save Kiki and bring the family back together? It depends on your decisions!

—description from the publisher

Choko is a traditional game from the Republic of the Gambia in West Africa. It’s a typical two-phase game: place pieces on the board and then move and capture. The unique aspect of Choko is a concept called “Drop Initiative.” This means that in the first phase of the game, a player may choose to switch to movement tactics until the other player chooses to switch back again to the placement phase. The drop initiative offers players a way to respond to their opponent’s initial placement and really change up the game before all the pegs have been placed. A very interesting variation on the usual jump-and-capture strategy game.

—description from the publisher

Four notorious prisoners may have escapes from London's Pentonville Prison. A selection of highly-skilled MI6 agents have been tasked with getting them back behind bars...

In Captivade, each player plays as both an escapee and an agent simultaneously. As an escapee, the objective is to avoid being caught for the duration of he game. As an agent, the objective is the re-capture the other escapees within the allotted number of weeks. You may work with your fellow agents to do this, but note that each agent believes that a different escapee was imprisoned unfairly and will wish to help them evade re-capture.

---

Played over 6-10 weeks (rounds), travel around the globe, help your allied escapee evade recapture by opponents' Agents, while also trying to track down opponents' Escapees.  You can work with other players to track down Escapees, but be careful as each Agent is secretly helping one Escapee to evade capture! Agents can only travel along sanctioned routes and must always show where they are.  Escapees travel hidden, along all routes, including flights if they can afford it, but might be seen moving and have to reveal their previous locations.

Capture all other players' Escapees before the end of the allotted number of weeks to win the game!

—description from the rulebook

description:
Those messy little monsters are at it again! They’ve snuck into your room and messed up all your things! Now you have to clean it all up before bedtime, but the monsters are still hiding in the mess and trying to keep your room dirty!

Can you outsmart them and get your room clean before the sun goes down?

In Messy Monsters!, players work together to clean up their messy room by matching two item tiles of the same color. Hidden within the pile of clothes, toys, and books, however, are monster tiles, which undo your hard work, and sun tiles, which signal that bedtime is getting closer. If you can match all the items before the sun sets, you win!

-description from designer

Any participant starts the game, then they take turns.
In his turn, the participant throws the dice two times consecutively. The first throw determines how long a block of cells it should place on the game plan. The second roll determines the number of such blocks that it should place.
The participant places blocks horizontally or vertically by crossing out the cells included in this block on the game plan. The thickness of the block is always one cell, the block can start from any cell, the blocks can touch the ends or sides, but cannot intersect or be placed on top of each other, the block cannot be curved, the block cannot go beyond the game plan.
The participant continues to roll the dice and place blocks as long as he can place blocks on the game plan. As soon as this opportunity disappears, he finishes the game and counts the number of penalty points, which is equal to the number of unclosed cells.
The winner is the participant who scored the least penalties.

—user summary

The participants roll the dice at the same time. Each adds up the dropped values. Further actions depend on the resulting amounts:

     If both participants received numbers in the range 4-10, then the participant who received the smaller number puts in the pot the number of chips equal to the difference between the numbers of participants.
     If one participant received the number 3 or 11, and the second – a number in the range 4-10, then the first participant takes 5 chips from the pot (or the whole pot, if there is a smaller number of chips in it)
     If one participant receives the number 2 or 12 (Strung combination), and the second participant receives any other number, then the first participant receives all the chips in the pot.
     In other cases, the dice are thrown. The participant who has collected all the chips wins.

Any participant starts the game, then throws are made in turn. In his turn, the participant throws both dice, sums up the values that have fallen out, after which he can put a chip of his color on any free field corresponding to a number that can be obtained by multiplying an integer by the amount received.
The winner is the participant who first laid out four chips in a row.
Variants

Any player starts the game, then dice throws are made in turn. On their first turn, the player throws all three dice, then using at least two mathematical operations, they combine the die results to get a number. When combining numbers, you can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. If the resulting number is present on the game board and is not marked, the player places one of their chips on the number, then passes the dice to the next player.

On their second and subsequent moves, the player uses any two mathematical operations — or one operation twice — to combine numbers, trying to get a number adjacent to the number previously marked by him. If successful, they place a chip on this number and score a point; if not, they place a chip without scoring.

If a player cannot combine numbers to create a number on the game board or the number is already marked, they receive a warning.

Based on old "Paper-and-pencil" game, Cochechs is a racing board game where nothing is left to chance. With no dice, player move their cars using vector moves.

The game includes 8 boards printed on both sides. Player will be able to build a different circuit for every race. There are a huge number of combinations available.

The game also includes cards that give to the pilots extra speed, extra break or they can disturb the rivals.

This sector may replace Survivor’s Colony in any scenario where that is present. Add 1 Bounty token to it during setup.

-No population. Do not use a Population die.
-Cannot be corrupted.
-When you successfully invade this sector, recall 1 Fleet Power from here. If this was your only Fleet Power present, you abandon the sector.
-If you control this sector, add +2 Science Production Level.
-Once per Focus, after you gain an Improved Technology, increase the Population of a pure sector or activate a Fleet Power.
-Voidborn Fleet limit is 0. When you abandon this sector, the Voidborn cannot take over and it becomes a sector without Fleet Power. Cannot be a target of a Skirmish.

The promo pack was available during Turczi Junior Promo Pack gamefound campaign.

Any participant starts the game, then throws are made in turn. On his first turn, the player throws all three dice and puts a chip on the field, which is the sum of the values of all three dice. On the second and subsequent moves, the participant performs any mathematical actions (addition, subtraction, multiplication and division) on the values of all three dice, resulting in a number that then closes on the game plan.
For each closed number, the player gets 1 point. The game goes eight rounds, the winner is the player with the highest number of points.

Set in Bahia, Brazil - circa 1808. Will you play it safe or partake heavily in political bribery to advance your position? In this map, participating in the auction is not a right, it's a costly privilege.

In 1807, Napoleon invaded the Iberian peninsula, where the local military was no match for the French army. On November 29th, 1807, Queen Mary the Mad, prince John VI, and the whole Portuguese crown fled to Brazil and continued their rule remotely. Their decision to move the Brazilian capital from Bahia to Rio de Janeiro created conflicting alliances among the families in Bahia. Should they be loyal to the crown and the new capital of Rio de Janeiro, or be allied to the former capital of Bahia when exporting their goods? In this time of political upheaval, corruption was rampant in the land of Bahia. To be successful, one must spend political influence wisely and cash in bribes at the opportune time...

Mint Italy is a mid-to-heavy economic game that involves area control, resource management, and market manipulation. Players compete to build settlements, trade goods, and complete contracts on a small island off of the coast of Italy.

Players must increase their wealth, upgrade their abilities, trade or produce goods, complete contracts, and compete for land in this engine-building strategy game.

Players take turns performing 1 of several player actions. Once all players have passed, the round is over and each player scores for an end-of-round scoring condition. After 4 rounds, the game is over. The player with the most victory points wins!

description:
Gambit: The Pyre Wars is a two player, semi-abstract game where each player uses their hand of cards to lead their units to victory through either gaining control of the Pyre, an ancient and ominous artifact, are by eliminating their opponent's leader.

Gambit: The Pyre Wars is played an a 7x7 grid of squares, which each player controlling an army consisting of five units -  one leader and four troops. Players take turns activating one of their units by playing of the order cards in their hand, allowing the chosen unit to move to another space or to attack an opposing unit in a particular space.

Players begin each round with a hand of five orders, and no new cards are drawn after playing one. The round ends when both players' hands are empty. At this time the positions of each players' units determine who controls the Pyre based on who has the most "presence" by having their units on various control points on the board. After then replenishing their hands and possibly replacing any lost troops, a new round begins. The game ends when a player has controlled the Pyre at the end of any three rounds or eliminates their opponent's leader.

Gambit: The Pyre Wars allows for dramatic plays thanks to a collection of unique units, each with its own special abilities and powerful Tactics cards that can alter the course of the game. Careful timing and positioning are required to take full advantage of these, however, and the player who manages their hand of cards and controls the battlefield the most effectively will be victorious!

Welcome to Esperaization! This is a co-operative language-creation game in which you create unknown languages ​​and develop civilizations. What kind of language and what kind of civilization it will be is up to you. To summarize gameplay:

1. The questioner explains the question in an unknown language, and the respondent guesses it.
2. Acquire new words as you solve more problems, and use them to create more futuristic and complex words.
3. What will be the end of your civilization?

description:
A strange light shines on civilization...

In the Esperaization: Entropy expansion, you meet even more new things — specifically, fifty theme cards — that will surely make your civilization stranger and more evolved. It will be something.

Relaxing during his annual vacation on Earth 1, Mr. Stickerbomb – the infamous mad scientist from Parallel Earth 85, has inadvertently opened a portal to the Multiburp (the craziest corner of the Multiverse), letting in the criminal mastermind Mr. Rabbit and his Odditorium. It’s your job to round them up and send them packing.

AIM OF THE GAME

Make chains of numbered cards to free as many Oddity cards from Mr. Stickerbomb’s Rabbit Holes before you run out of cards in your draw deck.

—description from the designer

The 5th century BC is the golden age of ancient naval warfare. It is the age of the Persian and Peloponnesian wars, of Salamis and the Arginusae, of Temistocles and Phormio, when triremes ruled the Mediterranean. Oi navarcoi! is a wargame ruleset to play fleet battles during that time.

The trireme was a long and thin unstable platform, with ram as the main armament. It was built for speed and for ramming, a man-propelled guided torpedo. Well trained crews could exploit the trireme's qualities with complex tactics such as the periplous or diekplous.

Oi Navarcoi aims to replicate this specific character of classical era naval warfare, which is different from the later Hellenistic and Roman periods – when ships got bigger and bigger and emphasis was definitely on boarding and missile fire (even with war engines!).

M'Guf-yn is back, awakened from his sleep, he is ready to take back what has been stolen from him: the entire world. After regaining his mighty scepter, he is determined to spread chaos, destruction and despair.

You are the only brave hero who can stop his diabolic plan to conquer the world. Are you ready to descend into the dungeon again? This time, other than his armies, you will need to defeat his terrible commanders, before reaching the final battle against the lord of the underworld himself.

Mari-Rama is a game intended to test an individuals ability to survive by finding and maintaining a foothold in the underground society of "Mari-Rama".

Before the game begins, each player receives $3000.00 in any denominations from the City Fund. Next dealers decide by mutual agreement who shall start the game and take turns in a clock-wise direction. The starting dealer rolls the dice, moves their colored pawn the appropriate spaces on the board and follows the instructions on the space on which they land.

Players land on either routes or "special spaces". If a dealer lands on a route space which is not yet invested in by another dealer, they may purchase it from the "city fund" at the investment price indicated on that space. If they do this they receive the route receipt proving their investment. When a dealer lands on a route that is already invested in by another dealer, they must pay the owner the "street price" indicated on the space.

If a dealer can acquire all routes of a particular type of marijuana, they may increase their investment in these routes by offering a greater quantity for sale.

The game ends when a dealer lands in jail for the fifth time or at the end of a predetermined time limit. The winner (of a time limit game) is the dealer with the largest holding of money plus assets.

Each player has 9 stacks of 4 cards. The goal of the game is to play every card in your 9 stacks. The play style uses some common techniques, such as matching the color or text of the previously played card, but has a number of interesting twists. A unique element of the game is that every number card played causes all players to switch to that numbered stack. You are constantly moving and reevaluating your strategy as the cards in your hand change. Also, some cards allow a player to swap a stack with another player, discard and entire stack at one time, choose an opponent's stack to look at, and cause an opponent to create a new stack.

Each player has an Immunity tile which can be played once per game to block a card played against them. There is an intriguing mix of strategy, random chance, and mischief that gets more fun the more times you play the game.

The game is designed for 2-4 players age 8+ and has been play tested with players as old as 80 years. The game consists of 200 game cards, 36 numbered tokens, 4 Immunity tiles and a direction of play disc. Play time tends to be 30-40 min. per game.

In the city of bees, where various species of insects live in harmony, strange characters interfere with the royal court. It is the representatives of various ancient cults who each try to invoke their demonic god. For this, they need to sacrifice souls and the more noble the sacrifice, the more valuable the soul. Which of these characters will be the first to gather the necessary number of souls to allow his god to reign chaos over the kingdom?

Vermines is a deck building game where players take turns clockwise. Their goal, to acquire cards of «Partisan» and «Object» in order to build a deck. To do so, they must use eloquence (game currency) that will be won thanks to the various cards acquired during the game. Each «Partisan» card has a soul value and can, after being played, be sacrificed to win souls (victory points). The first player who reaches 100 souls wins the game.

—description from the publisher

In Cat & Co., you're worried more about the company than the cat, trying to keep track of everyone you see at the animal party.

Each player starts with a set of twelve animal cards in hand. On a turn, a player shakes the ten animal dice in the box, lifts the lid for a brief time, then covers the dice once again. Starting with the dice shaker, players place one card from their hand into a row, trying to play cards to match which animals have shown up at the party. If you can't remember or don't think your cards match the dice, you can pass.

Once all players have passed, remove the lid once again and place dice one by one on the cards in order. If a player played a card that doesn't match a die, they receive a cat token worth -1 points. All cards that match the dice are removed from the game; all cards that collected cat tokens are returned to the hands of the appropriate players.

Keep playing rounds until someone has no cards left in hand or someone has collected at least three cat tokens. Each card left in hand is worth -1 point, and whoever has the best score wins.

Adventure through the sky and explore the magical world of Disney as you pilot a hot air balloon in Disney Around the World. Guide your balloon through six charming lands and collect stamps for your passport. Roll a die to see where you'll move. Sunshine is the best weather for flying...but watch out for the wind and clouds!

Be the first to collect four stamps from four different lands to win.

This is a cut-out-and-play game with a food/baking theme.

Players take turns to roll dice to build up ingredient tokens, then get a chance to make recipes from a random selection of cards.

First to complete each category of recipes wins!

This game is a variation of Dicing with Dinner.

Card game for the whole family; each player takes on the role of a powerful (but greedy) Leader in a Kingdom of Adventurers, who through cunning, courage and luck, sends his minions in search of fame and riches.

Suitable for all ages 6 and up, 2 to 6 players. A game can last from 20 to 30 minutes.

Game based on luck, but that requires a good level of tactics, skill and synergy to be mastered and dominate your minions!

The first player to get 10 Treasure Tokens wins the game!

There are two decks with two different Backs, called Adventure Deck and Adventurer Deck; divide them, shuffle the cards of each deck so that each game is different from the previous ones, keep them face down and place the two separate decks in the center of the table.
Each player draws 3 cards from the Adventurers deck. Also distribute 3 Supply Tokens(ST) to each player, represented by the Food image.

Procedure: The game is divided into rounds, at the beginning of each round the Adventure is determined by the top card of the corresponding deck. Don't reveal it yet, the only information you will have will be the Difficulty level, given by the number on the back of the card ranging from 1 to 3.
Warning, Lv 3 Adventures give more rewards but also hide traps!
After evaluating at the beginning of each turn whether to participate in that specific adventure, players can put one or more Adventurer Cards from their hand onto the field, initially face down.

boardgame: UNO Flip!: Marvel Super Heroes or Villains
description:
UNO Flip! card game adds an exciting twist to the classic UNO matching card action that everyone loves, and now the action includes heroes and villains from the Marvel universe! Play a Flip card and the deck FLIPS from the Heroes' side to the Villains' side which has penalties that are extra tough. A 'Draw 1' card on the Heroes' side becomes a 'Draw 5' card on the Villains' side! A Heroes' side 'Skip' card becomes a Villains' side 'Skip Everyone' card! Only another Flip card can change things back. And when you're down to one card, don't shout, 'assemble,' shout, 'UNO!'

UNO Flip! Marvel card game features a two-sided deck, Heroes and Villains, and the Villains' side has penalties that are especially tough!

UNO Flip! Marvel card game has special rules for both the Heroes' side and the Villains' side that really change the game!

description:
A trio of expansions by Neon Comet Games, first released at Tokyo Game Market 2023 and available for purchase on their website.

Honeybee Hive Mind is a map for 3-5 advanced players. Players represent bees working to pollinate their network of flower fields, but competition exists against ever-present rival bee populations, making deliveries painfully expensive. A map for advanced players. Players represent bees working to pollinate their network of flower fields, but competition exists against ever-present rival bee populations, making deliveries painfully expensive. Every turn gives players the opportunity to sting their opponents or fall mercilessly prey to other players’ schemes. Embrace the struggle, feel the pain!

Brooklyn is a map for 3-4 players. Fashion shows and set-collecting provide trendy players with special mid- and end-game bonuses. Fashion shows provide trendy players with special mid- and end-game bonuses. This map features two new special actions: "Runway Diva" and "Steal the Spotlight" - together these assist players with obtaining lucrative set-collection bonuses for big end-game payouts.

Escape Room is a co-opertunistic map for 2-4 players working collectively to solve four unique puzzles as they race against the clock. Special time-track discs are added to the board each round. There can only be one winner, but if the players fail to solve the puzzles before time runs up, everybody loses. In this co-opertunistic Age of Steam map, players work collectively to solve four unique puzzles as they race against the clock. Special time-track discs are added to the board each round, chipping away at the players' precious time. There can only be one winner, but if they fail to solve the puzzles before time runs up, everybody loses.

In Soulfall, the 6 powerful Lords brought the world back from the brink of destruction and are worshipped as gods.  In this combo building card game for 2 players, each player leads a fledgling tribe rebuilding after the near end.  Using 3 Action cards and multi-use Lord cards, players will call upon the Lords for help, expand their tribe, and search for shards of the old world.

Each Lord card can potentially be played 4 different ways: played for its ability, as a Devotion, as a Shard, or as a Nomad.

There are no points in Soulfall; the game ends and a player wins if they have a certain number and/or type of cards.

This is a card only, two player adaptation of the original 2015 Soulfall board game.  This adaptation, while it loses the board, as well as some of the original game's timing weirdness and confusing scoring, utilizes multi-use cards and the same action system to replicate the original in a quick playing, combo driven, card only game for two players.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
The safari explorers are getting ready to race through the park and see all the safari animals!  But they better be quick!  The sun is setting soon, and they won't be able to see any of the animals in the dark!  Help them race down the path and spot all the animals before the sun sets.

Players will play cards from their hand to move one of the explorers on the board by matching the icons on the card.  If they have a sun card, they'll have to play it instead, which will advance the sun token one space closer to sunset.  If the sun token makes it to the last spot before all of the explorers have reached the end of the park, then the game is over and the players lose.  If they can get all the explorers to the end of the path before that time then they win.

Have fun while learning new skills like cooperation, turn taking, strategic thinking and social skills!

In Picnic Twist, players try to organize their picnic meals and tablecloths in the best way possible, drafting domino-style cards, then placing them in their playing area to create the largest possible number of similar elements, both food and tablecloth patterns.

Picnic Twist includes several game modes and bonuses.

The players in the Mammothodus are put in a situation where they must work together as a team to survive and to build a portal to another world in order to avoid the humans that are coming to eliminate your clans. The gameplay is simple but challenging, as each player must strategize on how they can build the portal while defending themselves against the humans. However, the challenge is compounded by the fact that the players are limited in time and resources. Players have to work together to build the portal before the humans reach them and kill them all. The game also offers an expansion pack that provides more clans and resources to the players in order to increase the chances of survival and completing the mission. 
The game mechanics itself is simple yet challenging. Players take turns moving figures around a gameboard with the goal of placing their pieces in strategic positions to build a portal and win. Depending on the complexity of the game, it can take anywhere from 60 minutes to 2 hours to complete a full game.

boardgame: まじかる☆ベーカリー外伝 パンとごはんの魔女裁判（ポテトもあるよ）(Magical Bakery Gaiden: Bread and Rice Witch Trials (And Potatoes Too!))
description:
In this side story to the Magical Bakery and Magical Kingdom games, a small village on the border between the Kingdom Panteria, the Country of Bread, and the Ricel Empire, the Country of Rice, all the villagers divide themselves up into the Bread Faction and the Rice Faction.

But the village has been cursed by the Witch of Confusion, and now everyone has forgotten which faction they belong to. Uncertainty and hostility grow wild, and every attempt to vote on which faction is more popular fails due to rampant fraud.

Into all this confusion came a young girl, who asked the innocent question:

Which do you prefer? Bread? Rice? Or maybe you like Potatoes instead?

In this card game, each player has a faction which is hidden to them, but visible to other players. Play cards and use deduction to figure out which faction you currently belong to, out think your opponents who will try and change your faction, and make your faction the majority.

Change of Heart is a collection of trick-taking games from designer Chris Wray and artist Megan Russell.  Each game is a twist on the classic game Hearts. Each features an all-new mechanic.

Change of Heart: This is a new spin on the classic game of Hearts.
 In Hearts, the Queen of Spades was worth a whopping -13 points. Why put so much emphasis on one card? In Change of Hearts, all of the Queens are worth negative five points.

The idea was also to put a fresh twist on the Jack of Diamonds, which in some Hearts variants was worth +10 points. But again, why so much emphasis on one card? In this game, all of the Jacks are worth something, and worth even more the more you capture.

The high cards in the deck also have different backs, and players pick their hands using the limited amount of information that provides. The first pick goes to the player furthest behind, creating a sort of catch-up mechanic.

Look Ma, No Hands: A frequent knock on trick taking games is the luck factor. This game is, to the designer's knowledge, the first full-information, fully-determinative trick taking game designed. Many of the concepts that make this a no-luck game could work with other trick-taking games, but it felt most natural to marry them with the game of Hearts.

All cards begin on the table. Players play tricks, taking any card from the table, though they should generally follow if they can (there are rules if they don't). The winner of the trick gets positive points.  Everybody takes the card they personally played.

Thus, players in the first phase are building their hands with trick taking. Then they play a second hand, but it is the classic trick avoidance game of Hearts, with the negative points for Queens and Hearts.

In sum, there is full information, and the game very much feels like Hearts. But it has zero luck.

Go Shoot the Moon: The designer's favorite part of the game of Hearts was always trying to “shoot the moon.” In this game, the theme is to make the game of Hearts where one player attempts to shoot the moon each hand. There's a sort of auction at the start, where players can either take the moon shooters' hand (i.e. the hand with the highest cards; the suit back is different on the highest cards) or pass over it under the agreement that they will pass the moonshooter one of their highest cards.  There is a scoring mechanic for when the moon is not shot.

The Tin Man: The Tin Man has always wanted a heart, and with apologies to L. Frank Baum, the best way to get it is not from finding a wizard from Kansas, but rather by playing the game of Hearts.

This uses a sort of automated player called The Tin Man; he can win tricks. If the Tin Man captures the most Hearts, he will be happy, and the players will be rewarded (i.e. Hearts and Queens will score POSITIVE points). If not, he’ll be upset, and the players will suffer (i.e. Hearts and Queens will score NEGATIVE points).

Hearts is the classic “trick avoidance” game, where players usually avoid capturing tricks, since points are unwanted. But what if the game wasn’t necessarily a trick avoidance game, but could become a plain trick game (with positive points) if the players (with a little foresight from the track) caused a paradigm shift?

boardgame: Dat Hep Gestaan Op Feesboek: Internetgekkies Themaset
description:
Internetgekkies Themaset is an official expansion for de game Dat Hep Gestaan Op Feesboek. In this expensions you will find 8 new status cards and 24 new addition cards. You will also get the profile of mister Mandje, a unique action to get rid of your addition cards in an original way. MAND!

Every ecosystem is a complex combination of living organisms and the environment. Even a small change in any element affects the entire system, but balance is usually restored quickly. However, sometimes a random event leads to the butterfly effect – an unpredictable cascade of changes that can completely transform the whole biosphere.

Evolution: New World – Kickstarter Box includes The Butterfly Effect expansion and all additional exclusive and limited content from the crowdfunding campaign.

In the box, you’ll find:

     New Evolution and Area cards (including Exclusive Promo cards, alt-art cards and foil cards). Add more gameplay depth and create dynamic survival strategies for your animals.
     The Solo mode. In the Alien World scenario you will fight 1 on 1 against the merciless Dominator and insatiable Devastator for supremacy or create your own monsters to compete with.
     Additional tokens. With the expansion, up to 6 players can enjoy Evolution together.
     Butterfly Effect scenario. Start a chain reaction of random events that affect the game’s course and change the evolutionary process.

Lateral thinking game that invites us to create solutions to different problems using our ingenuity and imagination.

Joe has many problems, and we can help him solve them with different tools and our creativity.

Ayuda a Joe (Help Joe) is guaranteed fun for adults and children.

Each player in their turn chooses a situation to solve. They must use the most tools that have been given to them, in addition to all their imagination and creativity!

The other players will judge if the situation was correctly solved (using the right tools and no more tools than needed).

Game ends when there are no more situations to solve, no more tools available, or the ones available can't help to solve the situations.

Then, each solved situation is worth 2 points, and each used tool is worth 1 point. The player with most points win.

The duel is on to build the greatest castle! Each turn, players simultaneously roll their dice and select 1 or 2 for placement on each player's 6 tower castle card in their bid to claim the Crown but be careful in your use of the Curse dice. As towers rise, players may take on additional dice but they affect scoring differently and may allow your opponent to complete their build first for a bonus. We hope the simple rules and fast pace will bring your family joy upon the throne for many games to come!

—description from the designer

The Metal Totem Pawn can be used instead of the wooden Totem Connection Scoring Marker, or it can be used as a small expansion.

Set-up:
• Place the Metal Totem Pawn on top of the connection number “1”.

Gameplay:
• When a player places a Totem in a territory adjacent to the connection with the Metal Totem Pawn, the player is allowed to perform either of the two following actions, at the end of their turn:

A. Immediately score the connection with the Metal Totem Pawn for all involved players, even if the scoring doesn’t generate points to any player. Then, move to the next connection number in ascending order. If the Metal Totem Pawn is on the highest connection number and it needs to be moved, it has to be removed from the board.

B. Do nothing

"The Pantanal is the largest floodplain biome on the planet, located in three countries in South America: Paraguay, Bolivia and, for the most part, Brazil.

Every year, starting in November, rainfall largely increases and raises the level of the rivers, flooding the plains. In flooded areas, a large amount of fish is retained in lagoons and bays, attracting many birds in search of food. In May, the rains stop and the waters begin to slowly recede. This annual cycle of floods renews fauna and flora, maintaining the ecological balance of the biome.

In Pantanal, players add a Landscape Tile to the group of tiles in the center of the table, forming a landscape of fields, lakes and rivers throughout the game.

The tile placement is very important, as each player has a Character Card to score at the end of the game according to the distribution of that character in the tiles landscape. Each player keeps his identity a secret, being able, at any time during the game, to make guesses about other players' characters. Correct guesses score Victory Points at the end of the game.

When placing a tile, the player can also receive Guaviras tokens, which can be used to buy tiles or Biome Cards, with objectives that score extra points at the end of the game. The player with the most points is the winner.

-description from publisher

Things move fast as the high and low cards in play change quickly throughout the game. Manage your cards carefully so you have one that fits between the high and low cards on your turn. Wild and Reverse cards keep the game hopping. The first player to play all their cards wins!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Batman: Escape From Arkham Asylum – Suicide Squad: Assault on Arkham Asylum
description:
Join the Suicide Squad in this new game mode where you will have to accomplish a very special mission: to extract the Super-Villains. With this "Batman: Escape from Arkham Asylum" expasion you will be able to play the new Extraction game mode. Choose a Super-Villain and enter with the Suicide Squad into the Arkham facility to extract them as you face all the dangers hidden in the asylum.

Includes: 31 miniatures (7 super-villains, 24 adversaries), rulebook, 40+ cards. 5 game tiles, 1 extraction plan, 7 infamy trackers, 30+ tokens, 24 colored base rings

boardgame: Batman: Escape From Arkham Asylum – Riot in Blackgate
description:
Now we are the owners of Blackgate and no one, not even Nightwing, will be able to stop us.  There's a riot at Blackgate! The Super-Villains have managed to control the asylum.

Nightwing will enter Blackgate to stop the riot, but they won't make it easy for him. They are fully organized and armed. Black Mask knows Arkham Asylum very well, Deadshot is unstoppable, Lady Shiva is also in Blackgate after sneaking in to talk to Black Mask, Deathstroke is in Blackgate to finish a job, and Catwoman has sneaked into Blackgate to search for clues about her past. She won't let Nightwing get her for that reason. In addition, Ratcatcher will fill Blackgate with rats to give them a nice new home, adding an extra touch of danger to the place.

Use these characters to your advantage to stop Nightwing from restoring order to the prison!

They have obtained merchandise from the other side and have set up a small black market to purchase what they need, introducing a deck building mechanic to the game.

Includes: 37 miniatures (Nightwing, 6 super-villains, 3 followers, 27 adversaries), rulebook, 40+ cards. 25+ game tiles, 1 riot plan, 6 infamy trackers, 30+ tokens, 27 colored base rings

boardgame: Batman: Escape From Arkham Asylum – Ra's Al Ghul & the League of Assassins
description:
This expansion gives you 2 options - You can face Ra ’s Al Ghul as a boss or play him and his daughter Talia Al Ghul as Super-Villains to take advantage of his incredible abilities to escape from Arkham Asylum. You decide which one to choose!

This expansion gives a new challenge to the escape that will require all your skills to achieve your goal. With this expansion you can play with Ra's Al Ghul too, the Head of the Demon, the most infamous leader of any organization of the world, as a new Super-Villain, and also with its daughter, Talia Al Ghul, who will try to prove why she is a true and worthy heir for her father.

This expansion gives you 2 options - You can face Ra ’s Al Ghul as a boss or play him and his daughter Talia Al Ghul as Super-Villains to take advantage of his incredible abilities to escape from Arkham Asylum. You decide which one.

Includes: 20 miniatures (Ra's Al Ghul, Talia Al Ghul, 9 Scimitar Assassins, 9 Ranged Assassins), 1 Lazarus Pit Tilem 1 Escape Plan,, 20+ cards. 2 infamy trackers & tokens,

boardgame: Batman: Escape From Arkham Asylum – Quest at Arkham Asylum
description:
Batman enters Arkham Asylum to stop the Super-Villains. Their escape plan must be foiled to restore order to Arkham Asylum. For this reason, he will use his best detective skills in search of clues, but he will not be alone. Batman will receive the help of Alfred who will be represented by a miniature that will indicate where he has access to the information from the Batcave.

However, Batman is cunning, and will take all dangers into account while he obtains the necessary information to defeat the Super-Villains that will be waiting for him.

“Batman: Quest at Arkham Asylum” is for one player and will change the style of the gameplay. As with the core game, depending on which Super-Villain is the boss, the gameplay objectives and strategy will change.

Includes: 1 miniature (Alfred Pennyworth), 1 rulebook, 161+ cards, 1 Batman character card, 3 ability cards, 12 equipment cards, 7 boss cards, 25 adversary cards, 35+ event cards, 8 clue cards, 30+ villain plan card, 30+ boss combat cards, 10+ NPCs event cards, 1 Heroism tracker and tokens

boardgame: Batman: Escape From Arkham Asylum – Heirs of the Cowl
description:
With this expansion you can replace Batman as the stalking nightmare in the core box, as it gives you 6 exciting new characters.

You can also play these characters in the "Batman: Quest at Arkham Asylum" expansion. This means you have 6 additional heroes to play in solo mode, or you can increase the number of players for a fully cooperative experience.

Includes 6 miniatures (Red Robin, Robin (Damian Wayne), Batgirl, Huntress, Red Hood & Orphan), 80+ cards (6 character cards, 18 skill cards, 18 upgrade cards, 20+ equipment cards, 6 boss cards, 30 boss combat cards) and 6 heroism trackers
