boardgame: The New Game of Stock Exchange
description:
An auction game where each player selects one of five trades (fishmonger, farmer, poulterer, dealer in wild animals, and gardener). Each trade has five cards having values of 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10 counters. The object of the game is to try to collect all five cards of their chosen trade, which is considered completing one’s “Stock”.

All 25 trade cards (5x5) are shuffled with five dealt to each player. Each player also starts with counters totalling a value of 20. The first player selects a card from their hand to auction and places it face down on the table. The highest bidder wins the card and adds it to their hand, with the counters going to the seller. Should a player complete their “Stock”, it is announced (thus ending the game) and that player also receives a bonus of 10 counters.

The game also features 10 speculation cards - five of which earn additional counters (“pay”) and five deduct counters (“draft on bank”). These cards are placed face down and are usually drawn when a player is in financial distress. Players can also pawn any of their own trade cards for their face value in counters.

At the end of the game, any player who did not speculate during the game is required to draw one speculation card before determining final scores. The player with the highest score, calculated by adding the values of trade cards in hand plus the value of their counters, is the winner.

Dive into the world of delicious food, skilled cooks, and famous restaurants. With the game components in this box, you can play three distinctive games with your family and friends: 

     Manage the fast food market in Restaurante: Cook and Sell 
     Spend an easy, fast, and fun time in a pen and paper game Restaurante: Think and Write
     Show your dexterity skills in the game Restaurante: Food Fight


Brief description of Gameplay:

Food Fight


     Gets sets of three cards by cycling cards between players
     The first set will get you in possession of a precious burger
     The second set will grant you the right to shoot at another player
     Every set of cards is worth 1 point, and for every miss of yours or hit by another player you get 1 penalty point 


Cook & Sell


     Choose what food you want to sell. The offer from all players determines the initial price of the food. 
     Play various Action cards to change the state of the market.
     Fill your deck with new cards and upgrade your kitchen.
     Be the first to get a certain amount of money.


Think & Write


     Mark up to two food types to sell. Mark none or just one and save them for later sales.
     The offer from all players determines the initial price of the food.
     Circle Booster cells and change the price of products.
     Be the player with the most money after the 10th round. 


—description from the publisher

Welcome to the exciting ancient world of Constantine the Great!
During this game, you will use your tactical and strategic potential to fulfill the demands of the Roman Emperor Constantine.

Constantin Magnus is an:
 
     Action selection game in which every action affects all players and grants bonuses for a player who chooses the action
     Tile laying game because every chosen action gives, you and your opponents, tiles to be placed on your city maps 
     Resource management game where you get resources and with them complete different tasks, made by Emperor Constantine, that will grant you points


—description from the publisher

In the Royal Realms, you are hired by the king of England to participate in the royal auction to collect the best combos of various gems for his royal crown. This card-based auction-bluffing game is full of fun and tense moments with many unique twists and turns that hold the interest of all players until the last minute. YOU ARE NOT OUT OF THE GAME UNTIL THE LAST MOVE! LAST MINUTE! The player with the most points wins the game.

You all have the same limited number of money cards. The auction will last for 4 sessions and for 2 days. On your turn, to win a gem card you bid higher than the previous bid or pass. You have some information about upcoming gem cards for auction and for some gem cards, you bid blindly if you win those you keep them hidden from others which creates bluffing opportunities for you. By the end of session 2 and day 1, you will be calculating influence points the player with the least IP will lose the money from their hands accordingly.

You will carry over the remaining money cards in your hands for the next day. For day 2 you will have a bit more information about upcoming gem cards, by end of session 3 you simultaneously will be bidding for wild cards auction which you place face down on their right. [ hidden ], and by end of session 4 you once again simultaneously bid for wild cards which you place face down with your previous bid. Players will bank their remaining money cards which they will also place face down on their left.. [ hidden ]

The end of session 4 and the day 2 auction will end the game, all players will reveal their bid for the wild cards and will receive [ win ] the wild cards accordingly. Then all players will arrange their gem cards together with wild cards to create the best possible combos [ set of gems ] to win the most points. But the game is not over yet, finally, all players will reveal their banked money and the player with the most money banked will receive 20 points and with the least money will lose 20 points.

All rights reserved by HOBBY DOCTOR GAMES LTD, UK.
www.hobbydoctorgames.com

-description from publisher

Simple roll & move game from Poland about motorcycle speedway racing. 
While the game itself is very basic, it puts a lot of effort into realism, offering an option of chaining a number of games into individual or team championships, with scorepads for keeping track as in a real-life speedway league.

Kosovo and Metohija take you to the medieval world of endless fertile fields, next to which the most beautiful cities and monasteries rise.

Gameplay Overview:


     A mix of tile placement, resource collecting, and city building
     Every round one general event is activated that affects gameplay in various ways
     Build walls and recruit armies to save your precious lands from attacks
     Connect tiles and make yourself opportunities for crafting your own buildings which will bring new effects into the game, thus giving new ways for making points and making you closer to the victory  
     Includes solo variant


—description from the publisher

Welcome race fans and gaming enthusiasts to the annual derby boulevard soap box drag race event. The gathering entries in this year’s event are sixteen Peanut characters driving their own custom built soap box carts, and they are excited for the fast downhill racing action derby boulevard provides, although Joe Agate forgot to fill in some potholes. Your roll in all this excitement is to be the racing steward guiding them along this competition and making sure the whole gang follows the rules. Being the steward doesn’t mean you can’t root for your favorites, but just make sure they follow the briefing rules contained in this manual, let’s begin the racing fun on this beautiful day for the competition and crown a derby drag racing winner.

Each character has its own card to determine movement. The game uses a simple three die mechanic to the movement based off four variable skill movement characteristics: speed, soap box control, tire shake, and grooving. The result of the yellow D12 control die compared to the six sided red tire shake die will determine if the car drifts left or right along the track.

The game uses a sixteen single race elimination bracket, characters are randomly placed in the first round of the bracket and two drivers race against each other at a time, and the winner of each single race moves to the next round. Each race takes approximately five minutes to complete and there will be fifteen total races to complete the derby competition bracket and crown a winner. It's fast and exciting racing action for ages eight and above.

-description from designer

boardgame: Order & Opportunity: Making of the Post-Cold War World Order
description:
Order & Opportunity is a 2-4 player game (with a dedicated solitaire system) about the making of the post-Cold War world order covering the first decades of the 21st century. In the game, the United States, Russia, China, and the European Union project their economic, political, and military influence on the global scale. The powers seek to score victory points in various dimensions of game play such as:

-Players engage with a wide range of historical world events (e.g. the Libyan Civil War, Saddam Hussein, Ukraine, famines) seeking to resolve them or suffer from the destabilizing effects of continuing crises.

-In a semi-cooperative fashion, players champion their form of governance as members of either the Authoritarian or the Democractic blocs.

-There are also the slower moving continent-scale pivotal event processes - like the Color Revolutions in Europe or the Tiger Economies of Asia - that give scoring opportunities to players that are the best positioned to benefit from them.

-At the same time, players come under domestic pressures from terror threats to polarization in part as consequences of their own economic and political actions as global powers.

Order & Opportunity is more than just a power fantasy about global geopolitics. Grounded in topical global history of the 21st century, it puts players face to face with questions about Democracy and Authoritarianism, and about the nature of economic and other forms of power, in a game systems in which players themselves are the agents of these processes. Will you be the force for order, justice and the common good, or does economic opportunism and military adventurism win the day?

—description from the publisher

The Spectrum Board Game Kickstarter will include the Spectrum Cards & d12 dice expansions.  Backers of Spectrum will receive the 168 card Spectrum Cards Deck Expansion & the d12 dice expansion free with the Spectrum board game.
  
Spectrum the board game was debuted at Pax Unplugged in 2022 where it was named one of the Top 10 Indie Gems at Pax Unplugged.  The addition of the Kickstarter Exclusive free Spectrum Cards and d12 Dice expansions to give you over a dozen more Spectrum games to play!

Spectrum Game List

Spectrum Board Game

The strategic, abstract game of colors.
You decide what pawns to move, but the dice determine where.
You may only enter on the outer rim on one of the 6 primary colors.
To enter an intermediate color, a pawn must already be on the board.
Jump and capture opponents on the way to controlling a set amount of the 12 colors.
The game starts with 2 dice, and a 3rd and 4th die get added to the game as it advances.

Spectrum Cards Expansion

168 card deck based on the Spectrum Board Game. 
Featuring 12 colors and 12 unique wild cards it is bigger than 3 standard decks of cards!
The 12 suits enable twists on classic card games that give them a new, exciting, colorful life.
Combine the cards with the Spectrum Board Game, or use the Spectrum Cards for stand alone games.

Spectrum Board Game Variants with Cards

Spectrum Weights - Set asymmetric values for capturing each of the 12 colors.  Highest score wins.

Spectrum Missions- Adds a second, stealthy win condition.  Control 5 secret spaces on the board and win by stealth.

Spectrum Portals- Players own spaces on the board that allow them to activate transporters.

Spectrum Draw & Move - Replace the dice with the Spectrum Cards for a unique Draw & Move mechanic.

Spectrum Card Games

Spectrumo
Uno Spectrum Style with 12 unique wilds to spice it up.

Spectrump 
A trump game where trump is bad, and good.

Spectrum Solitaire
The ultimate solitaire challange,  12 tableau and foundation piles to unpuzzle and conquer.

Spectrum Cribbage
Bring back an old favorite with new numbers to aim for.

Spectrum 500
Rummy 500 with 12 suits.  What could be more fun?

Spectrum Casino
An obscure, classic card game brought back to life by the Spectrum Cards.

Spectrum Poker
Poker with 12 suits and new hands.   Play for jelly beans but please don't gamble.

Spectrum d12 Dice Expansion

Five d12 custom Spectrum dice with all 12 spectrum colors on the die.
All new rules and fun.  Use 3, 4, or 5 d12 Spectrum dice with all the colors on them!

Play Spectrum, and all the variants, using the d12 dice and the Spectrum Cards.
Use both the 5 d12 dice and the original Spectrum d6 dice for more unique games!

A New 9 Dice Spectrum Variant called Spectrum Escape!
Try to escape from the Spectrum board in this strategic & colorful king of the mountain game.

Roll & Color - A New Original Spectrum Roll & Write Game!
Try to score as high as you can, while making colorful dice sets, and series.

-description from designer

Discover Disney Pixar's Lightyear. Stranded on an uncharted planet, Buzz Lightyear and his crewmates will battle hostile plants, giant bugs and the ultimate threat, Zurg, to complete their mission and return home. This exciting skill and action game is inspired by the movie. Two teams of 1 to 4 players ages 8 years and up face off in a puck-flicking battle to launch Buzz's spaceship, the XL-15. The game board, fuel cells, pucks, and giant bugs all play a part, and the rocket really launches! Get ready for cosmic action as Team Star Command and Team Zurg collide head-to-head and puck-to-puck in a race to collect fuel cells. For each fuel cell collected, players can take one shot to launch the XL-15. If the special Hyperfuel Puck hits the launch pad on target, the starship will blast off. Score the most launches to win!

-description from publisher's website

Discover Disney and Pixar's Lightyear. Stranded on an uncharted planet, Buzz Lightyear and his crewmates will battle hostile plants, giant bugs and the ultimate threat, Zurg, to complete their mission and return home. In the exciting Seeker Squad board game, all players work as a TEAM in a race to find the Hyperspeed Crystals before Zurg strikes! Players cooperate to explore the board, leaving no stone unturned as they flip over pairs of tiles. If they match, take the tiles! If they don't, Zurg moves closer and his robots send the whole world spinning! Escape before Zurg reaches the end of the Danger Tracker and ATTACKS! The first side of the board provides a simple way to play that's perfect for Junior Patrols, and the second side delivers a more challenging experience for advanced Space Rangers.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Garden of the Queen of Hearts
description:
The Queen of Hearts told Trump soldiers to decorate the garden! Which trump card soldier will be able to decorate the garden most beautifully!

Players turn over 3 playing cards, select 2 cards, and complete them in the prettiest way in ascending and descending order on a 25 square grid!

[Scoring]
1 point for each ♤ in the largest group of horizontal lines (rows) of ♤, +5 points if all horizontal lines (rows) are filled with only ♤

1 point for each ♧ in the largest group of vertical lines (columns) of ♧, +5 points if all vertical lines (columns) are filled with only ♧

2 points for each ♡ in the largest group of ♡ squares (1, 4, 9, etc.)

2 points for each ◇ in the largest group of diagonals of ◇

-description from designer

ダンダンダン  dan … dan … dan  The hilarious shoot-‘em-up card game for 2 to 4 players aged 14 and up.

Say good-bye to your old, boring identity and find yourself as member of the Tekiya! They form Japan‘s more or less honourable group of food stall operators and hucksters. Once they‘ve paved the way for the notorious Yakuza, but no need to retire: the Tekiya are still around and active today. And, of course, they are all one „big family“. Except when talking money. Then they suddenly all turn into greedy bastards. And now this: Ichien san of the kakigori stall claims to make the best shaved ice, while all the other guys merely hawk inferior crap. Man, that means trouble! Anyone who publicly puts himself above the others is breaking law and order. No Tekiya will ignore that. But should you make a snitch out of yourself at the Oyabun and in the worst case lose your finger, Yubitsume style? No way! Breaking taboos is something you only do among yourselves – in an ominous corner of the park. And then, then, then it‘s dan ... dan ... dan! Which in our simple translation means „Bang, bang, bang!“. Here’s the good thing: Whoever survives the shootout of the gun toting brawlers will have a few more food stalls in the near future.

IN PLAIN LANGUAGE: WHAT IS IT ALL ABOUT?
Quickly and in turn, you turn over cards from your own stack and place them face up in the centre of the table. There, a full arsenal of handguns accumulates. Every time a loaded gun is pointed at two of you with its grip and barrel, a shootout takes place. If you are shot – or make a mistake beforehand – you are punished and must take all the cards from the centre of the table. You grease the police with the top card, the rest go face down under your stack. Then you continue the game by turning over a new card. And on it goes...

The first player who has no cards left, wins the game.

In addition, various abstruse special cards fuel future game rounds and keep the game challenging.
What more could you wish for?

Originally available as a promo at SPIEL '13 in Essen.

Field "Nebel" ("Fog")
No combat takes place here.
If ships of different players meets on this field, there will be no combat. They all remain on the field.
In addition the fog field cannot be taken by any player. No stone is placed to mark the field.

In addition, it includes 2 copies each of the new Close Action version 3.0 chart sheets--namely the light blue gunnery sheet, the dark blue gunnery sheet, the yellow critical hits tables, and the red movement sheet. Promo card X44 given with issue 2/23 of Spielbox Magazine

This is a green card costing 31M€ giving 2 titanium production and 3 titanium resources. It is worth 2VP at the end of the game.
artists: United States National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

description:
Have you ever wanted to fall in love at a very legitimate and not-at-all-sketchy art university? In the year 19XX, Johnathan Dromathan invented Art. It went well. He then used the funds to open up the first art academy, which you - the player - are attending...just to ignore your education in the pursuit of "love."

GOAL

Romance the group's love interest of choice by gaining the most affection via end of game cards, maximizing stats, and successfully beating the romantic interest's current affection threshold.

GAMPEPLAY

At the beginning of the game, 2-4 players each agree upon a game mode and love interest, placing their pawns in the center of the map on the Quad space. Players then take turns rolling the movement dice and moving that many spaces landing on one of several different type of spaces, skipping over spaces that have players already occupying them.

After the 5th turn of the 5th round, play ends. The player with the most affection is the winner of the game and the love interest's heart!

-description from publisher

A light strategy card game based on kendo, the traditional Japanese martial art, descended from the ancient samurai warriors.

An entertaining card game with real-time elements, Kendo playing cards are designed for 2 players. It mixes bluffing and deduction with strategic thinking to create a funny and exciting gaming experience.

The game simulates a modern kendo competition between two fighters whose goal is to defeat their opponent using familiar elements of kendo. Players score points by performing striking combinations against their opponents and can keep or earn their focus points for executing these combinations at the optimal moment of attack and defense. Each player has a pre-set number of playing cards and each draw will allow him to change their technical „inventory” through optimal hand management.

Every decision must be carefully analyzed because one little mistake can cost you the battle. The game uses a small amount of kendo-specific terms in order to be easily understandable and enjoyable even for those, who do not practice the art. During the game, you can get a little insight into the wonderful world of kendo, the Japanese Way of the Sword, which is worth giving a try in a real dojo, aka training hall as well.

-description from designer

The game is started by any participant, later they take turns.
In his turn, the participant consistently throws the bone twice. The first throw determines how large a cell block it should fit in the game plan. The second throw determines the number of blocks that must be placed on it.
The participant places the blocks horizontally or vertically, crossing out the cells entering the given block in the game plan. The thickness of the block is always one cell, the block can start from any cell, the blocks can touch the ends or sides, but cannot cross or place on top of each other, the block cannot be curved, the block cannot go beyond the game plan.
The participant continues to throw the dice and place the blocks as long as he can place the blocks in the game plan. When this opportunity disappears from him, he finishes the game and counts the number of penalty points equal to the number of unclosed cells.
The winner is the participant who scored the least number of penalty points.

Red Coats, Grey Jackets is an introductory level wargame on the battle of Chippawa. It is part of the Battles for Canada Series of games that portray famous battles fought for control of Canada during the War of 1812.

•    8 pages of rules.

Each hex is approximately 50 yards across, a regular or militia infantry unit is 250-350 men, First Nation and Dragoon units are 80 to 100 men, and artillery units represent 2 or 3 guns. A turn represents one hour of time.

boardgame: Tic Tac K.O.: Cute vs Evil – Boom Promo Card
description:

A solitaire version of the beloved trick-taking game. In this solitaire version of the classic game of Hearts, you will play against the game (“bot”), with the same basic objectives: win tricks, but avoid those with hearts or the queen of spades in them, which carry negative points.

description:
Convention Promo card for 2023

Card Text

Once per turn on your turn you may DISCARD a Magic card, then DRAW 3 cards.

Test your culinary knowledge by guessing the right ingredient in a funny spicy card game

Relaxing game for all ages

Fast, Funny and even a little strategic

Colorful, whimsical and you can bring it everywhere

Separate the contingency deck from the main deck, consisting of recipes and ingredients, which will be called the "pantry".
Shuffle the pantry deck and deal 4 cards face down to each player
You choose who starts, no matter how.
The starting player places a card face down from their hand in the middle of the table and loudly specifies which recipe it is.
The other players take turns, following the established order, play one of their cards from their hand, placing it face up in the middle of the table. Players must field an ingredient card that they believe is contained in the recipe in play.
Those who do not have the right ingredient or do not want to risk, can decide to fold, not playing any cards.
When each player has made their choice, the one who started turns their card face up, revealing it. Then he lists aloud the ingredients present in that specific recipe and at that point he takes all the cards in play, placing them next to him, in a dedicated pile.
The player (s) who have the wrong ingredient, playing a card that is not present in the specific recipe, draw 1 card from the pantry deck and 1 from the unexpected deck.
Finally, always following the order of the turns, the next player places his recipe card in the center of the table, and so on.

The objective of the game is to run out of cards in hand and when only one is missing, shout "Spiked", under penalty of having to draw 2 cards from the dispensation deck.

The player whose turn it is to put his recipe card into play can decide to bluff, especially if he doesn't have one. The other players can accuse him of bluffing but before verifying it everyone must have completed their turn. Whoever has been caught bluffing will have to draw 2 cards from the dispensation deck and one from the unexpected deck; Who has guessed the bluff can discard a card from his hand; finally, in the event of a mistake on the part of the accused, he will be the one to draw a card from the dispensation deck.

The Recipe cards have 3 precise and fundamental classifications: Origin, Flavor and Type of Cooking. Pay attention to these categories whenever you encounter Unexpected cards that can facilitate or compromise your final victory.

     Provenance means the origin of the dish and you just need to know the state of origin, represented on the card by a flag.
     The Flavors are divided into Salty - Spicy - Sweet - Sour and Vegetarian
     Finally, the types of Preparation or Kitchen are divided into Pot - Pan - Oven or Fridge

A game of positioning, strategy, and memory, played with a standard 52-card deck of playing cards.

Draw 10 cards and arrange them into a 3x3 grid. The remaining card starts your discard pile, used for scoring at the game's end. Each turn, flip a card and use its number power or its face ability. Capture your opponent's cards into your discard pile until the draw pile is depleted, and then compare discard piles. The one with more cards, wins.

Dicemon is an exciting board game for 2 to 4 players that combines monster catching and combat. Inspired by the popular monster catching video game, this game implements its mechanics in an accessible format without the need to purchase additional cards. The goal is to capture monsters and participate in trials and events during the adventure phase to earn victory points and be the first trainer to reach 20 points. Improve your decks and evolve your dicemons to increase your chances of victory in combat and become the best trainer.

—description from the designer

A Xor0 deck consists of 63 cards. Each card has a unique combination of six dots. Each dot is identified by both a color and a number. A player wins by quickly finding "Xor0" groups of cards. Techniques for finding Xor0 groups include visual pattern matching, dot counting, binary numbers math, or any combination thereof.

A Xor0 group is a group of cards where each dot color appears an even number of times. The name of the game derives from the fact that after summing up the numbers on each card and applying the mathematical XOR operation to them has the result zero.

The game is played in rounds. A round starts by taking seven cards from a shuffled deck and placing them face up on the table. There is always at least one Xor0 group present in the seven cards. The player who first spots a Xor0 group announces it by saying "Xor0!" and pointing out the group. All player collaboratively validate that the group is indeed a Xor0 group. If confirmed, the player who found the group takes the cards in the group. Otherwise, the player must pause until another player finds a valid group. After a player found and took the cards, the seven cards on the table are replenished from the deck and all players look for the next Xor0 group. The round ends as soon as the last card is drawn from the deck. The player who collected the most cards during a round wins. Ties are broken by number of dots collected.

The difficulty of the game can be adjusted by playing with a partial deck that omits one (or two or three) of the six dot colors.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Conquest: Two Player Starter Set – Nords Vs City States
description:
Conquest The Last argument of the Kings 2.0 (TLAOK2.0) is a tabletop gaming world produced by Para Bellum Wargames. Our flagship, “Conquest: The Last Argument of Kings” is a Mass Battle game and under its umbrella you will find our entire range of miniatures. Its sister system, “Conquest: First Blood 2”, is a skirmish game, played with the same minis and offering fast-paced combats. Both systems come with easy to learn rulesets, which also afford veteran players multiple levels of strategy to master. With both rulebooks available online for free and a “Living World” platform which allows the community to influence the lore, the world of Conquest invites you to an immersive gaming experience!

This Starter Box brings 2 armies

The Nordsmen (huge vikings with gigants) 
vs
City States

also with a new set of rules of game.

Who doesn’t like pizza? Get ready to experience an extremely fun family time with this easy to learn yet very challenging “ TOP UP YOUR PIZZA” game. But don’t be carried away by the cuteness, this game will have you on your toes throughout while improving your strategic,problem solving and observation skills.

All you have to do is top your pizza slices with matching toppings and score maximum points !

boardgame: Casting Shadows: Ravaging Draco Promo Card
description:
2023 Convention Promo Card

Card Text
Ravaging Draco
Companion
Each time you Cast an Attack Spell, deal 1 additional DMG for each Resource card in your Spell Book.

In this Game you go head to head with your friends for 3 Rounds, each of which ends with a Winner.
Win 2 Rounds to be the Champion, or face the other Winners in a final Tie Breaker!

To win a Round you must gain possession of The Pug, while getting rid of all other cards in your hand. There is only 1 Pug in play at a time, so pay attention to who has it, and most importantly bluff:
you shouldn’t let anyone know when you have The Pug!

Donkeys cannot be discarded in any way, so you cannot win if you have them in your hand.
You must find a way to give them to your opponents as soon as you can!

All other cards in your hand are Monkeys. They are of 4 colors: Blue, Green, Orange and White.
You must discard 2 Monkeys every Turn, and perform the Action corresponding to the colors of the pair. There are 6 Actions in total: use them wisely to get The Pug, give Donkeys away, or create confusion!

In addition, every Round is overseen by 3 Mythical Creatures and their super powers. Any player in trouble can summon a Mythical Creature to make an extra play, even outside their Turn.
With their extraordinary abilities, Mythical Creatures will flip even the most incredible Rounds!

—description from the designer

Serbs Stand Alone is a strategic level board game with two-player and solo options.

The playable sides are the two resistance movements, overwhelmingly (Chetniks) or mainly (Partisans, certainly until the middle of the war) manned by Serbs.

This is a game about the guerrilla, civil and ethnic war in the Kingdom of Yugoslavia (formerly Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes) that lasted from 1941 to 1945.

The game starts in Fall of 1941. In March of 1941, the Serbs, old French and British allies from WWI, rebelled against Yugoslavia joining the Tripartite Pact. Reacting to this, the Axis conquered and dismembered the Kingdom of Yugoslavia in April.

The Axis is not a playable side in the game however. The main fight is about who will be controlling the country after the war, and specifically for Mihailovich, limiting Serb casualties.

boardgame: A Steadfast and Violent Affair: The Battle of Lundy's Lane, July 25, 1814
description:
A Steadfast and Violent Affair: The Battle of Lundy's Lane is an introductory level wargame. It is part of the Battles for Canada Series of games that portray famous battles fought for control of that country during the War of 1812.

Each hex is approximately 50 yards across, a regular or militia infantry unit is 250-350 men, First Nation and Dragoon units are 80 to 100 men, and artillery units represent 2 or 3 guns. A turn represents one hour of time.

Players use a standard deck of playing cards (a custom series card set is available from the publisher) to activate their units. A six-sided die is used to resolve combats (assault and fire) as well as other game events.

—description from the designer

The player to collect and save the most fish wins Fishbowl Fiasco!

Objective:

Collect six(6) of your opponent's monkeys to win the game.  Get six water counters on an opponent's monkey to soak it.  Soaked monkeys are collected as trophies.  The first player to collect six trophies wins!

Start:

Each player sets aside one #1 monkey card from their deck.  Each player shuffles and offers their opponent a cut of their deck.  Each player puts the set aside #1 monkey card on the center square in the first row on their side of the board with one water counter on it.  Each player rolls the D6 die to see who goes first.  Highest roll wins. If a rematch the loser goes first.

Objective: Collect six(6) of your opponent's tanks to win the game. Get six damage counters on an opponent's tank to destroy it. Destroyed tanks are collected as trophies. The first player to collect six trophies wins! Contents: 1 game board 1 blue tank deck (18 cards) 1 red tank deck (18 cards) 1 D6 die and 50 damage counters Start: Each player sets aside one #1 tank card from their deck. Each player shuffles and offers their opponent a cut of their deck. Each player puts the set aside #1 tank card on the center square in the first row on their side of the board with one damage counter on it. Each player rolls the D6 die to see who goes first. Highest roll wins. If a rematch the loser goes first.

—description from the designer

700 years ago, a worldwide geological cataclysm changed the face of our planet and humanity.  Countries joined to survive and maintain what remained of society.  These two factions have discovered that the continent of Antarctica had shifted north and the icecap has completely melted.  A new war is beginning over control over Antarctica's resources.

RedTide is a real-time strategy (RTS) game that brings the experience of RTS video-games, i.e. Command and Conquer, to the tabletop. 
Players will build their base, train their army, collect ore, and be the first to complete their mission objectives to win.

Electronic fun in a flash for the whole family.  Electronic computer. Sound. Light. Instant cumulative scoring. Individual game selector. Designed for play on table or wall. Ready, Aim, Play.

Pinball: 6 chances to hit all 8 color panels and light all the lights for a top score. Bonus points if you hit more than one panel at a time.

All or Nothing: Half the lights are on and half are off. Use strategy and you can turn them all on or off and win.

Memory: The panels are given a different point value for each game. Aim for the top points without hitting the zero panel that will wipe out your score.

Moving Targets: Round and round the lights go and you've got 40 seconds to score by hitting the panel that is lit while the lights keep going.

Time Trials: Speed and accuracy count as 8 panels light at random 2 to 6 second sequences. The shorter time the panel is lit the higher the score for a hit.

Dinosaur Grześ loses ground under his feet. Whoever causes the fall loses! Carefully knock out the stones and try not to be the person who will cause this unfortunate event and save him from trouble.

How to play?

Spin the arrow and use the hammer to knock out the stone (or two or three) it points out. The player who causes the dinosaur to completely lose its balance loses… and must clean up. But he can also demand a rematch!

The game develops motor skills and logical thinking in the youngest players.

—description from the publisher

Dinosaur Game reimplements Penguin Trap, which has the same cubes (now called stones) and hammers, but different animal and different spinner. Apart from obvious thematic difference, there is a difference in mechanics, because in Dinosaur Game the spinner allows to knock 1, 2 or 3 stones, which makes the game much faster and more dynamic.

—user summary

Players complete taco recipes by rolling the dice and matching the dice to the items on their cards. A turn consists of a single player rolling and rerolling the dice, with all players utilizing the dice results. Once all dice have been rolled and rerolled, all players use the ingredients shown on the dice by marking off the corresponding ingredients on their Taco Cards with the dry erase marker. Once a card is completed, players draw a new card from the top of the deck or from 3 face-up options. Some cards have bonus ingredients that count for bonus points at the end of the game. Cards vary in difficulty with the most difficult cards being worth the most points. After a predetermined set of rounds the game ends and players add up the points on the cards they have completed. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Ranch Hand, the game that puts the ranch in your hand (figuratively).

You have decided to try your hand at ranching. You have found some land and a few animals to get started. Can you turn your little start-up into a bona fide ranch?

The game consists of 2 phases, the Auction Phase and the Disaster Phase.

Your goal is to meet the objective on your objective card by collecting the indicated animals on your ranch. To win you must establish 3 Ranches this way. To meet your objective, all cards must be the same breed of the animal indicated. You may change which breeds you are collecting as the game progresses. Your ranch also has a max capacity that cannot be exceeded.

During each auction round players have a chance to take 2 cards from an open draft style auction to add to their ranch. They may not exceed their ranch capacity and must discard any cards that take them over the limit.

The end of the auction triggers the disaster phase. The active player turns over the top card from the Disasters deck reads the card out loud. Every player follows the directions on the Disaster card, not just the active player. Players have the option of avoiding disasters with a dice roll indicated on some cards. Certain cards also allow players to avoid disasters. You must survive the disaster round to consider you objective completed. Once an objective is completed players draw a new objective and start over carrying over only a few cards from the previous objective. The first player to complete 3 objectives wins.

—description from the publisher

You are attempting to be the first player to get 50 points and 5 different terrain tokens (1 of each type) by camping in the national parks (and one National Wildlife Preserve). You get points based on the park you camp in and the camping gear you use.

Players collect camping gear and equipment cards and place them face-up in their campsite. Once they have certain camping equipment they can play park cards to camp in those parks with that equipment. Park cards have multiple camping options. Points are based on the camping equipment used and the park it is used in. Before a camper can complete their trip they must draw a trip card which tells them how their trip went and if they have any bonuses or penalties.

Players may also play action cards that allow them bonuses or penalize other players.

Park cards also have a terrain icon on them. Players must camp in at least one park of each terrain as well as earn 50 points (points are kept track of on a game board with player tokens) to win. The first person to complete both objectives wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: You're An Idiot...and your friends are, too
description:
No one is safe from judgment (including you) in this fast paced, easy-to-learn card game, which features you and your friends! How to play: Appoint one player as the judge. The judge spins the spinner, which then points to an Idiot at the table. The judge plays a card about said Idiot, and the rest of the players play a card from their hand to complete the sentence. The judge reads the card combinations aloud with the Idiot's name and chooses the best one to win the point. But before any cards are played, the Idiot spinner can give the judge the power to mix up everyone's cards. It's time for game night for Idiots everywhere!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Phyrexia: All Will Be One
description:
This expansion for Magic: The Gathering takes place on New Phyrexia, as the armies of the five praetors, led by Elesh Norn and her Machine Orthodoxy, prepare to launch a full-scale invasion of the rest of the multiverse using a corrupted seedling of Kaldheim's World Tree.

Phyrexia: All Will Be One features the return of the Proliferate mechanic (which first appeared in Scars of Mirrodin(, and the Compleated mechanic (which first appeared in Kamigawa: Neon Dynasty), as well as three new mechanics: Toxic, Corrupted, and For Mirrodin!

The set is sold in draft boosters, set boosters, collector boosters, Jumpstart boosters, and bundles (including a limited-edition Compleat Bundle with unique "oil slick" foil cards). Two preconstructed commander decks were also released alongside the main set: "Corrupting Influence," a white, black, and green deck built around corrupting opponents with poison counters; and "Rebellion Rising," a red and white deck built around attacking with token creatures.

boardgame: Bear Hunt 捕熊陷阱 [Bǔ xióng xiànjǐng]
description:
Postcard wargame of the battle of Hunhung Ni (Sinhung-Ni / Xinxing Li) in late November 1950 during the Korean War. Two players represent the Chinese 'volunteers' and the US forces respectively.
A standard hex-and-counter wargame (move-fight-recover for each side in turn) lasting six 1-day turns and containing only 21 counters. Map scale is 1km per hex. 
In an unusual mechanic, the Chinese use a d10 in combat, and the US a d6, but both seek to roll less that their own attack strength minus the enemy's defence strength.
The Chinese player must reduce US morale to 0 in order to win immediately, otherwise they win if Chinese units occupy the town of Hunhung Ni, while the US wins if it exists a given number of strength points off the map.

description: A deck of poker cards inspired by the Italian comic book Dylan Dog. It includes a manual containing 25 solitaire card games to be played with the deck, and named after iconic elements from the comic.

Battles of Zieleńce and Dubienka, fought in 1792 are the famous battles of the Polish history. Both took place during the war of Polish consitution against Russia.

In the battle of Zieleńce (18.6.1792) newly formed Polsh army led by general Józef Zajączek, succesfully stood against stronger and much more experienced Russian army. Russians suffered twice as much losses as Poles and had to retreat.

In the battle of Dubienka (18.7.1792) Polish troops led by general Thadeus Kościuszku fought an all day battle against Russians in the fortified positions. Despite the fact that Poles eventually left their positions, Russians suffered huge losses (about 4000 dead and wounded), while Polish army suffered smaller losses (about 900) and was still able to fight.

Game depicts both battles, along with hypothetical variants, showing "what if" situations.

Scenario list:
Zieleńce (historical scenario)
Zieleńce 2 (hypothetical scenario)
Dubienka (historical scenario)

description:
The players take on the Empire to try to free their crew members to get the 'last job' of the Rebel Alliance done.

Game contains five 'individual' scenarios which consist of multiple cards (over 280 cards in total). In each scenario you have to solve a series of riddles to succeed finally.

Star Wars is another installment in the Panini 'Esacape Game' series.

boardgame: Путь к успеху (Your way to success)
description:
The strategy of the game is based on the fact that success in life can be achieved through education and effective work.  Players perform the same actions as in real life: go to school and work, earn money, buy food, acquire personal property, pay utility bills.  Players all the time have to plan their income and expenses, as well as their actions during the working day, week, month.  The game helps to acquire financial decision-making skills and choose your development strategy on the path to success.

A player on his turn can visit from one of the three city buildings on the game board. Moving between buildings is possible both on foot and by taxi.

There are several types of buildings: buildings in which the player works; buildings in which the player spends earned money (shops); buildings in which the player receives improvements in personal skills (educational institutions).

An example of a building where the player earns money is a factory. Arriving at the factory, the player rolls a die and, depending on the result of the roll, the player receives a certain amount of money earned.

Gradually, the player buys more items for himself in the house. As soon as any of the players has 10 items at home, 5000 units of currency after paying off all loans and utility bills, then that player wins.

This game is a great conversation topic generator and a way to get to know your friends better.
Everything is very simple - decide which questions you will play with: with ordinary or piquant. Take a card and read it. Everyone who did what's described there, should drink.
The game ends when all cards have been played. You will learn a lot about your friends and you can hear unusual stories.

boardgame: Можно ваш телефончик? (Can I get your phone?)
description:
Get ready your phones
Now you will send all kinds of stuff to your friends, subscribers and random people from your phone book. It will be awkward, but funny.

Each turn, you roll a die and move your pawn as many spaces as you get. Then you take on the task. Perform it - leave the chip where it is. Merge - move it back.
On tasks, you will call or text to your contacts, sometimes post something on social networks. The tasks are shameful and provocative, the game will not be gentle with you and will set the heat. The one who reaches the finish line first wins. And will not burn with shame.

Shot it's a drinking game with tasks. All tasks are about holidays, the sea and the beach, so you can feel yourself on vacation even in January in your apartment.

On your turn, you either complete a task or drink. For tasks you get bonuses, but you decide whether to spend them or not. Sometimes special animals shows from the deck and make you move.

The game will end when the “Time to go home” card appears from the deck. The winner is the one who has accumulated the most completed tasks.

The Shot #unfiltered is a drinking game. You either complete the task or you drink. Tasks are about intimacy (up to kisses), about frank conversations and actions. And also about social networks - subscribers will be surprised. With each task and with each sip, the game is more fun and frank.

The game ends when the "Drinked" card shows from the deck. The winner is the one who completes the most tasks and still stands on his feet.

First module in Dungeon Blitz, a series of fast-playing solo adventure games.

Vardak Castle leers over the village of Greyhollow like a hungry vulture. For decades the villagers have shuttered their windows after dark and lived in fear of the evil that watches over them. You have journeyed along the Vova River to the haunted village, prepared to put a stop to whatever unnatural creature stirs in the cursed halls of the BLOOD CASTLE!

In each adventure you create a character and undertake an exciting and dangerous dungeon crawl with little more than your trusty weapon and wits. All you need is some paper, a pencil and three six-sided dice. The dungeon is randomly generated and you will map it as you play. As you discover new locations you might be attacked by monsters, set off a trap or find wondrous treasure. Eventually, you will find the terrible monster at the heart of the dungeon and either defeat it or die trying!

Dungeon Blitz is a series of fast-playing solo adventure games.

The Temple of Kur-Tar stood atop a hill in the lonely moors for a thousand years, until it was destroyed during a great battle. Now the land is haunted, the dead rise from their graves and demons crawl from their fiery pits. Someone must venture into the ruins and put an end to the evil that lurks there. Are you clever and lucky enough to survive the CATACOMB OF CHAOS?

In each adventure you create a character and undertake an exciting and dangerous dungeon crawl with little more than your trusty weapon and wits. All you need is some paper, a pencil and three six-sided dice. The dungeon is randomly generated and you will map it as you play. As you discover new locations you might be attacked by monsters, set off a trap or find wondrous treasure. Eventually, you will find the terrible monster at the heart of the dungeon and either defeat it or die trying!

boardgame: Шакал Архипелаг: Остров Весёлого Роджера (Jackal Archipelago: Jolly Roger Island)
description:
This is a new island for Jackal: Archipelago. An adventure awaits you on Jolly Roger Island. A rare elephant turtle lives here, and strange enchanted steering wheels are scattered around the island.

Mix this set with the ones you already have to build even bigger islands.

—description from the publisher

Wishland: Dreams from America is the first expansion pack for Wishland. It brings a new type of card that combines the effects of rides, actors, restaurants and hotels from the base game.

It will include:
- Components for a fifth player
- A new card type
- New achievement tiles
- Updates to the base game
- New entrepreneurs

-description from designer

Not a game for shy people!

The game's goal is to combine Question and Answers cards in the most nonsense way possible.
The game duration can be designed individually, but the best is to play until you get thrown out of the pub.

The moderator reads the drawn card aloud.
Then each player chooses one or more cards amongst his deck to fill the empty spaces in the Sentence card, to create the most exilarating result.
The moderator then closes his eyes, the payers place their cards on the table facedown, in reading order (the number of cards must be equal to the number of empty spaces on the Sentence card).

At this point, each player draw a number of cards until they have 8 again.
The Moderator role goes to the next clockwise player and a new turn begins!

Once every player has places his cards, the Moderator reads all the resulting sentences and chooses the one he thinks is mor fun.
The owner of the winning cards receives the Sentence card played as prize, and will keep it on the table in front of himself, face up.

In the box you will find the game rules, 100 green Sentence Cards and 300 white tremendous white Answer Cards.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un gioco da tavolo per persone senza vergogna!

Lo scopo del gioco è combinare nel modo più assurdo possibile carte Frase con carte Risposta.
La durata del gioco può essere progettata individualmente, ma è meglio giocare fino a quando non si viene gettati fuori dal bar.

Il moderatore del gioco legge la carta pescata ad alta voce.
A questo punto ogni altro giocatore sceglie la o le carte in suo possesso che, sostituite agli spazi vuoti nella carta Frase, hanno il risultato più esilarante.
Il moderatore chiude quindi gli occhi e i giocatori posizionano le carte al centro del tavolo, coperte, mantenendo l'ordine di lettura.
Il numero di carte selezionate deve corrispondere al numero di spazi vuoti sulla carta Frase in gioco.

In seguito, tutti i giocatori pescano il numero di carte Risposta necessario per tornare ad averne 8. Il ruolo di moderatore passa al giocatore successivo in senso orario e un nuovo, irriverente turno avrà inizio!

Una volta che ogni giocatore ha posizionato le proprie carte, il moderatore legge tutte le combinazioni ottenute e sceglie quella che più lo ha divertito.
Il proprietario delle carte vincenti riceve quindi come trofeo la carta Frase giocata che terrà davanti a sé, scoperta.

All'interno troverete le Regole del Gioco, 100 carte Frase verdi e 300 perfide carte Risposta bianche.

—description from the publisher

Participants roll the dice at the same time. Each adds up the deleted values. Further action depends on the resulting amount:
If both participants receive numbers in the range of 4-10, then the participant who receives the smaller number will put the number of chips equal to the difference between the number of participants into the pot.
If one participant receives the number 3 or 11, and the second-a number in the range of 4-10, then the first participant takes out 5 chips from the pot (or the entire pot, if there are a smaller number of chips in it)
If one participant receives the number 2 or 12 (string combination), and the second participant receives any other number, then the first participant receives all the chips in the pot.
•In other cases, roll the dice. The participant who has collected all the chips wins.

boardgame: Architect and the Multiverse of Greatness
description:
Embark on a journey through multiple dimensions, where you take on the role of an architect tasked with designing and constructing the greatest structures across the multiverse.

Architect and the multiverse of greatness is a party game for 3-99 players, each player is an architect who travels through the universe, and finds the perfect one to build their masterpiece.

In each turn gamers have to open several multiverse cards and architecture, and each player has to decide what place and what building is the best combo. But be careful, you must choose the unique one, so you have to guess what other players are thinking, the player who chooses the universe that the least player goes to gets the point.

Combo Cards present specific match-ups of aliens to have in a game, listed in order of player count.

The 8-player Combo Cards were promos developed for Escape Velocity, a science-fiction themed convention.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Nabis, le dernier Spartiate 195 av. J.-C.
description:
VaeVictis #167: Nabis, le dernier Spartiate 195 av. I.-C.

Nabis, le dernier Spartiate is the second wargame of the "Au Fil de l'Épée" Antiquity series, after "Alexandre contre la Perse".

In 195 BC, the king of Sparta Nabis sees his diplomatic maneuvers collapse. The powerful Roman legions of the proconsul Titus Quinctius Flamininus invade Laconia, accompanied by troops of the Achaean League. Refugee in Sparta with his motley army, Nabis fights a last combat to try to save his city. The game proposes to replay this battle which begins with a confrontation on the open ground and extends into street fighting, where all tactical stratagems, including the arson of the city, are good for repelling the Romans.

Can you save Sparta's independence or will you plant the Eagle of Rome in the heart of the city of Lycurgus?

One turn: 30 min.
1 SP = 200 men
Map: one hex. = 200 m
Complexity: 6/10
Solo play: 7/10
Duration of a game: about 2 hours

description:
Crazy Canteen is a roll & write game that you can print at home. In this game, players take the role of the manager of a canteen inside a company building, where employees go to have lunch.

What makes different this company canteen to the other ones is that employees are not allowed to go together to have lunch or to keep the same fix place or table every day. They are allocated individually by the canteen manager (you), who must decide where (in which seat and table of the dining room)sits an employee that comes to have lunch and with whom the table is shared.

Manager receive "satisfaction points" when sit properly the employees according to their preferences (those who like to share a table with others and the ones who prefer not to do it)and some actions they want to perform when they're having lunch. Be careful in your decisions and don’t sit at the same table employees that don’t want to have lunch together!

The game can be played in single player mode, but also with other players in a competitive game mode. Approximate playing time is 30 minutes.

boardgame: Chi è di scena!: Il gioco all'improvviso
description: All ready: lights, curtain and... the game begins! With Chi è di scena! you will immerse yourself in the unpredictable world of improvisation! Two teams of actors will compete in beats, facing improbable situations and taking on the role of the most diverse characters in order to win and get to the end of the show to take the thunderous applause of the public! Get out of the box, the only limit is your imagination!

OVERVIEWContinuum - a continuous, coherent whole characterized as sequence or collection of elements slightly different from each other that merge and pass one into the other...

Continuum is a 2 player card game in which players compete with each other in a dynamic duel, trying to outplay the opponent. By collecting, playing and manipulating cards accordingly, players put them together as profitable sets and valuable sequences as well as use them to thwart the opponent's plans. All that for gaining the highest score at the end and winning the game. There are several ways of gaining points and performing actions, so each player may find his own method for playing. To get more points, players have to push their luck hard to take certain actions in their favor or against the opponent. But they have to be careful - these actions may quickly turn against them because of immediate opponent's return actions during the same turn!

The gameplay combines core mechanics such as Push your luck, Set/Sequence Collection, Hand management and Gaining score. Taking into account the quickly passing time, players are forced to constantly balance their tactics as well as adapt their luck based decisions.

Besides the default multiplayer gameplay, Continuum also contains a solitaire variant which introduces a demanding Automa opponent to compete with. This mode imitates the standard multiplayer game. It includes all features and competitiveness of a 2 player game but without the need of a human opponent. The Automa cards determine the difficulty and aggressiveness of the Automa opponent. Player may tune the Automa profile to his liking during game setup.

Continuum offers an endless replayability. This, combined with a very quick setup and pleasantly short gameplay time, encourages players to play several games in a row. With handy components, the game fits perfectly for lunch breaks, trips and any occasions when there's only a short time available. Additionally, the digital version of the game will do great in remote, online play.


				
				
					Check the forum thread for game presentation and all official resources/files/links
[OFFICIAL] - Game presentation + PnP files + Links + WIP Thread + Digital versions

In this print and play game, you are Agent Pink of the International Intelligence Initiative. You are parachuted to a corporate-owned archipelago where a secret society of technoanarchists called Omniscient are assembling the last hardware modules of a powerful new A.I. called The Eye. If they succeed, machines will rule the world. You are Humanity's last hope!

Cards show icon sequences that can trigger game actions. Complete the sequences by matching dice to card icons. Get the four precious hardware modules and get out alive to win.

—description from the designer

Timescape, a new collection of escape games, to take you on a supernatural experience outside of space and time... and transport you here to 1888, one night, in the heart of London. Students of quantum physics professor Heinrich Zeist, you have been transported through space and time following a failed teleportation experiment, and, as if that wasn't enough, you learn that you must repair the disasters that your jumps in time cause, in order to be able to return.

Here you are in 1888, in the heart of London, the full moon has just risen, in front of you a half-wolf, half-man creature is staring at you, you hear the voice of the professor: "Run away, but you will have to undo the curse of the werewolf to be able to return! "Investigate, find ways to fight, don't delay! In the box, you'll find 60 cards to develop the scenario and its surprises, with puzzles to solve collaboratively.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Timescape, une nouvelle collection d'escape game, pour vous faire vivre une expérience surnaturelle hors de l'espace et du temps... et vous transporter ici en 1888, une nuit, au coeur de Londres. Elèves du professeur de physique quantique Heinrich Zeist, vous avez été transportés dans l'espace et le temps à la suite d'une expérience de téléportation ratée, et, comme si cela ne suffisait pas, vous apprenez, effet papillon oblige, que vous devez réparer les catastrophes que vos sauts dans le temps entraînent, pour pouvoir revenir.

Vous voilà en 1888, au coeur de Londres, la pleine lune vient à peine de se lever, face à vous une créature mi-loup, mi-homme darde son regard, vous entendez la voix du professeur : "Fuyez, mais vous devrez défaire la malédiction du loup-garou pour pouvoir revenir ! " Enquêtez, trouvez les moyens de lutter, ne tardez pas ! Dans la boîte, vous trouverez 60 cartes pour développer le scénario et ses suprises, avec des énigmes à résoudre de manière collaborative.

—description from the publisher

“East Rune” is a competitive card game played with a card set that reinterprets the rune cards used for divination so that they can be used in the game.

Let's compete for chair card by playing cards with matching runes.

When the game is over, you will score points for your chair cards collected and lose points for cards remaining in your hand.

The person with the highest score is the winner.

Villain is an immersive group/party game that gives each player roles and tasks to perform while trying to out the player who has the role of the “villain.”

The best part is that you can play it for 5 minutes or 5 hours and it will be just as fun! Ages 8+ recommended, but younger have fun too, and can be teamed up with older players.

In this fast and exciting party game, you will pretend that your house or other building you are in is a town. Each player will have a Character card that will tell you Who you are. You also get a Job card to Do. There are many Job cards in these decks, so you can continue having fun playing over and over!

However, if you are a citizen doing your job, you will want to watch your back because someone is a VILLAIN. The Villain is KNOCKING OUT (tapping), as many people as they can. If the Villain avoids getting caught and Knocks Out key citizens they win! But, if the Villain is caught and accused or enough tasks are completed CITIZENS win!

—description from the publisher

“Hear my words heroes, for the HeroQuest Frozen Horror Expansion pack is not an adventure to be trifled with! Many a valiant hero has not returned from its icy monster-strewn dungeons. Loretome has revealed a dangerous journey one hero may undertake to find a lost artifact of great power. Are you up to the task? The quest is calling!

HeroQuest Online Quest 2 – Into the Northlands is a solo quest for any hero. It is designed to prepare them for the powerful threats found in the HeroQuest Frozen Horror Expansion pack. For Zargon players, this quest gives some suggestions and tools to help scale the level of difficulty desired for their game. It also answers some commonly asked questions and provides errata to some game mechanics that felt more unfair than challenging.

This quest includes a set of playtest rules for bringing an animal companion on your solo adventures. A popular archetype in sword and sorcery tales is the friend of beasts. Great cats, horses, hawks, and all manner of small clever critters have a history of thwarting the machinations of foul sorcerers. We wanted to take this opportunity to playtest some new companion rules and tell a story of how your hero first encountered one of these faithful animals on their journey to the frozen north.

The Avalon Hill team uses an alternative to the roll to move mechanic in our playtesting. We included it in this quest so you could try it out! It keeps the drama of rolling double 1s (tripping on a loose dungeon cobblestone) in combat but keeps exploring brisk.

Cast the far voice spell and let us know what you think!”

-description from the publisher's website

The traditional sake businesses in Japan are facing new challenges and are in need of your strategic management skills. As a Kumamoto, the owner of a sake brewery, your job is to allocate your workers effectively to sell sake based on market demands that change every year. Become the best brewery by earning as many victory points as possible.

Kurasaku is a competitive board game where each player runs a sake brewery. With limited resources such as funds and personnel, players will need to secure their ingredients, invest in equipment, brew their sake, and sell it to run their business.

While monitoring other players and market demands, you might try mass production at low prices, or you can shoot for a come-from-behind victory by brewing and selling a finely aged and more expensive product. The game boasts the players can learn about brewing and the business while they play.

description:
FloriFauna is a card game with three game modes to enjoy with the family, while we get to know a total of 12 species of flora and 12 species of fauna in Argentine Patagonia.

It is an ideal gift since its rules are very simple, and invites to generate new game modes in case of being used with small children or older adults.

In game 1 (FloriFauna) we must quickly play our cards among the four piles on the table. Whoever manages to discard all his deck and cards in hand, will have won. Also, if someone finds a pair of Flora or Fauna among the stacks, he must mention them, causing his opponent to move that pair to the bottom of his deck.

In game 2 (Revelations) each player also has a personal deck, but no hand is used. Each player, in a clockwise direction, will reveal cards to the table. If any player at any time finds a pair of Flora or Fauna, he can cover them, and thus take them to his area on the table as a trophy. Whoever puts together the most pairs until the cards in each deck run out will have won. If any player incorrectly covers, he will not be able to cover until he passes his turn again.

Finally in game 3 (The Only One), we must go removing cards from the hands of others, to form pairs in our hand and thus discard them. Everyone will try to be without a hand, in order to get rid of it, and only one will be left with a card that does not have a partner.

"Simply S: Scribble is a dynamic game for 2 teams in which you come up with as many words as possible that match a random category and that all start with the letter S. Which team will think of the most unique words and win? Find out!


Po prostu P Pojedynek to dynamiczna gra dla dwóch drużyn, w której szukacie jak najwięcej słów pasujących do wylosowanej kategorii i jednocześnie zaczynających się na literę P. Która z drużyn wyjdzie z pojedynku zwycięsko i wskaże najwięcej unikatowych haseł? Sprawdźcie to!"

description: Players match pets with owners to adopt them. Each owner receives 2 pets, and has a preference of what pet they want, and each pet has a preference for their companion adopted by the same owner. Each owner has a base score and players earn bonuses or penalties based on pet and owner preferences matching. The specialist of the adoption agency with the highest happiness score wins the game.

In Grid Poker, players draft cards from a series of 3x3 grids to build 3 poker hands.

The first player chooses a row or column and takes those three cards. Then, the second player takes a remaining row or column, which may be only two cards. Discard the 3 or 4 remaining cards, or, with three players, give them all to the third player.

Lay out a new grid and rotate which player picks first. Draft 6 grids in total (all 54 cards; including Jokers, which are wild, but can only be used in straights and flushes).

After drafting, each player builds 3 poker hands of 5 cards each.

Scoring
Reveal and sort each hand based on its poker ranking. Compare each player's 1st, 2nd, and 3rd hand, with the best hand in each category scoring a point.

—description from the designer

A one map game.
88 0.75” counter game of the Monmouth Invasion in 1685 England.
Monmouth needs to either take Bristol or breakout towards London.
Simplest of CRTs, no supply, no stacking.
Small game with a lot of choices.
Recruitment, Variable Government Reinforcements will keep both sides guessing.

—description from the designer

description:
Lost Adventures is an adventure archaeology movie game that captures the cinematic sweep of this genre of film.  The game is played over two phases:

In the Map Phase, you will travel the world to acquire information about the lost temple, and select character cards to help prepare for its perils.  On each of your five turns, you perform four steps:
1. Travel to a different city
2. Face an encounter
3. View temple secrets cards
4. Add a character card to your tableau

In the Temple Phase, you will jockey for position against your opponents as you face the temple's perils.  On each, you write down a bid. You will move as many spaces on the temple track as the number of players with a lower bid.  Then, reveal the temple peril: cards in your tableau matching the peril offset your bid, the remainder must be paid by taking on hubris.

At game's end, you must try to purge your hubris in the final hubris challenge; failure means your face has melted off!  The player who is furthest along the temple track, with face intact, is the winner!

-description from designer
The Dragons (METD) (1996): The first expansion set for METW. As the name implies, deals mostly with dragons, their treasure and the northern regions.

Players race to make four-of-a-kind sets by swiftly swapping cards over and over in search of matches! Move fast and watch the cards because they can switch at any time! Find a set and ring that bell—ding!—and you’re one step closer to making all of your matches to win! It’s easy-to-learn, fast-paced excitement for the whole family!

Simple rules, funny situations resulting from imitating animal voices and the possibility of playing together with adults are the advantages of this game. You will need: great reflexes, perceptiveness and a good... voice. A very fun educational game for kids and adults of all ages. The person with the most worm tokens wins.

—description from the publisher

Players filp over face down animal tiles. When a thrid animal of a kind is flipped the player who imitate the voice of that animal first receives a worm token. There is an exception for one animal - the rooster. Just one rooster is enough to trigger the players imitating this animal's voice and that gives the first player two worm tokens award. Anytime a player makes a mistake they loses one token. When all animal tiles are filpped, the game ends and the player with most worm tokens wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Life Skills: Uczę się rozwiązywać problemy
description:
The World Health Organization has identified 10 life skills that are needed to grow into a better and happier person, who is able to lead a dignified life. And it is these skills that are developed in the games in this series, which are also great fun for the child. These are soft skills that are essential in everyday life. They allow children to get to know themselves, their emotions better, make contacts with other people easier or cope with stress.

This game teaches children to solve problems when they find themselves in a difficult situation. Children, using elements in the game, have to talk about a given situation and try to find a way out of it together with their parent.

—description from publisher

Every player receives at the start of the game 5 problem tiles. The goal is to solve those problems. Each turn a player spins a spinner which has 4 outcomes:

     Solve a problem: To solve a problem a player has to choose one of the solution tokens that is a dedicated solution for one of their problems - it is checked by flipping the token and check if the number on the other side match the number on the problem tile. If correct, the player flips their problem to the solved side. If not, nothing happens, end of player's turn.
     Solve a big problem: If a player has a medal, nothing happens, end of turn. If not, an opponent draws a big problem card and reads to the active player. There are always two questions with answers beneath them. If the active player replies to both questions correctly, they receive a medal.
     Change a problem: Player changes an unsolved problem tile for another one from the pool.
     Lose a solved problem: Player loses on of their solved problem, if any.


The first player to solve all 5 problems and get a medal wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Election: A Game of Secret States
description:
2 to 4 players vie to become President of the United States by earning the most electoral votes in the election. Players will choose a party at the start of the game: Defense, Economy, Government, or Society. Each party has unique special abilities. As a candidate, you will strive to earn states, especially high-value ones, and keep those states safely in your possession.

Players take turns drawing Campaign Cards to earn resources: either Cash or Support. At the same time, they will build piles of four Campaign Cards to earn State Cards. Also in the draw pile are Event Cards and Staff Boost Cards. Event Cards will have either positive or negative effects. Staff Boost cards provide a powerful bonus that lasts until removed by an opponent.

As players earn State Cards they will have to decide which ones to reveal by “securing” and which ones to keep secret. Players will form their strategy based on their state values, party abilities, and Staff Boost cards in play. They can spend resources to secure states, steal states from other players, and remove Staff Boost Cards. Some strategies might include, but are not limited to: bluffing, hoarding states, going after opponents, or timely use of party abilities.

Any player who has secured 10 states has the power to end the game, but must consider secured and secret (unsecured) states in their calculations. Once the election is held, all electoral votes (secured and unsecured) are tallied. Highest total wins.

—description from designer

Rough Draft is a screenplay drafting game where you generate your own screenplay, one scene at a time!

Players create a 3-act masterpiece across 6 rounds, with every round consisting of a Drafting and Voting Phase.

At the start of the game each player is given an Inspiration card they score from, and try to keep their inspiration hidden from the other players throughout the game.

Then all players are assigned a Role card that gives them unique benefits or perks.

Players draw 5 Scene cards in the Drafting Phase and create a unique part of the screenplay. Players try to include their scoring Inspirations in the scenes, but try to not give their hand away!

In the Voting Phase players use tucked cards as voting power and choose the screenplay that ends in the final Rough Draft.

The player with the highest number of scoring genre cards in the final screenplay wins! The winner names the masterpiece and everyone helps reenact their new favorite script.

Tell a one-of-a-kind story together and then pitch it to Hollywood!

-description from designer

Indio is a Euro-style board game for 2-4 players age 14 and up. It takes 60-90 minutes to play, during which you'll be using workers to collect resources, modify the production of the land, and build structures. Your goal is to have the most influence (victory points) and be crowned the ruler of Indio!

The game is divided into 7 rounds (called years) where each year includes five phases: Quests, Production, Crafting, Harvest, and Actions. Quests give you goals to accomplish in order to earn influence. Production adds resources to the game board. Crafting allows you to add resources and workers to your buildings. Harvest is a time to feed your workers and prepare them for the year. Finally, the Action phase is the core of the game where you'll use your workers to move, collect resources, improve the land, build structures, and even attack other workers.

-description from designer

boardgame: Pick The Pug: Eternals Expansion Pack
