One of you stinks and you must find out who it is. Everybody plays with five cards, and you draw cards after each turn. Plus cards increase the stink level and minus cards decrease the stink level. Special cards enable more advanced gameplay strategies and include Multiplier cards, Zero cards, and Any Number -5 to +5 cards. Your goal is to stay in the safe zone – 30 or less – and force other players to push the stink level above 30 into the danger zone. The game gets more and more intense as the stink level approaches the danger zone. Use challenge cards to immediately skip turn to your opponents and force them to take actions which greatly increase their chances of triggering the danger zone. The Stinkbomb card moves the stink level instantly to 30 and the challenged player must play with five new cards from the draw pile while trying to stay in the safe zone. The Stinkbug card forces players to play a random card from their hand and hope to land in the safe zone. This gets more and more challenging as the game progresses. The Stinkbomb Shield and Stinkbug Repellent cards push the challenge back to the player who started the challenge which leads to unexpected twists and turns with unpredictable outcomes.
If your card pushes the stink level into the danger zone you must flip over one of the five stink pile cards which are placed face-down in a fan-like pattern under the draw pile to see if you are the Stinker, or prove everyone wrong:

     If you get the You Stink card, bummer, you are the Stinker and you lost the game!
     If you get the Not Me card, great, you’re saved! Quickly drop your cards and yell “Not Me!!!”. Congrats, you won the game! All other players must hurry to quickly drop their cards as well. The last player to drop their cards is the Stinker!
     If you get the Stinkbug or Stinkbomb card, you can still win! Play them against any player to start the danger zone battle. The player’s goal in the danger zone battle is to bring the stink level back into the safe zone – 30 or less – or challenge another player!


You also win if you collect all the plus or minus cards, all cards of the same kind, or plus and minus cards that all add up to zero. Just drop your cards at any time and yell “Not Me!!! The last player to drop their cards is the Stinker!

In the fast card game Mango Cabana, players compete to schedule the best acts at their night clubs. Score points for placing the act on the best nights. Specialize in comedy, drama, or magic acts. Host divas but watch out as having too many might cost you points!

Players draft cards in order to place the best acts in their clubs. Select cards with a minimum of rules and assess the game state.  Schedule 6 performers each. Whoever has the best combination of talent scheduled while avoiding penalties wins.

To be the coolest nightclub in town, you have to get the hottest acts. Welcome to Mango Cabana- The Fruity Nightclub Game!

—description from the publisher

This solitaire game is a fan-fiction re-imagining of the story that unfolds in the comic Tales of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, volume 1, #2, “Nobody’s Fool”, published by Mirage Studios in July 1987.

It consists of a 54-card deck, and it is free to print and play.

Take on the roles of the four ninja turtles and their unexpected ally, “Nobody”.  Break up an unlawful weapons sale, leave evidence of the exchange for the police, and vanish into the night!

The game lasts 4 rounds.  In each round, you draft 13 cards from the deck into your hand.  Then, you play these cards from your hand to the tableau and/or use the actions on them in any order that you choose.  The order of card play and the timing of their use is the heart of the puzzle.

Each round has specific objectives that must be met, or you lose.  By the time the last plot card is played, your ninjas and their vigilante friend will have had a busy night.

—description from the designer
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
This is a print and play sports replay game using real-life player statistics to play a full T20 Cricket game in 30-45 minutes.

Scores are generated using three decks of cards based on the batting team's selection of how quickly they aim to score (6, 8 or 10 runs per over). Each card gives the result of the next four overs, allocating runs to each batsman, any extras scored, and whether there are any wicket chances. If there are, the skill ratings of the batsman and bowler are compared and a D6 dice roll will determine whether the batsman is out or not. Once five cards have been played, you'll have completed 20 overs and the bowling team then takes over for its innings.

boardgame: Dreaded Flags: Naval Conflict in the Age of Piracy 1568 - 1720
description:
In Dreaded Flags, players refight historical naval conflicts between small fleets in the Age of Piracy. Players take command of one or more ships in engagements between privateers, pirates and/or pirate hunters.

This book contains everything you need to play the game: the rules, 8 scenarios, maps, charts, ship’s log pages and all necessary counters. You only need 5 6-sided dice, optionally 2 extra colored 6-sided dice, and a pen per player at home! Available worldwide on Amazon: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0C6VYSPTX

The book comes with 8 scenarios. Depending on the scenario, the game is played solitaire ( 2 dedicated scenarios and most others are very suitable for 2-handed play) or with 2 or 3 players. Most scenarios can be played in one sitting, with playtime ranging from 30 minutes up to about 2 hours. To help learning the game, scenarios have a complexity rating, so when learning the game simply start with the easier ones.

Dreaded Flags balances historical accuracy with straightforward and intuitive game play: The various types of fire used in the game reflect the risky and perilous nature of the combat. Other era-specific factors are also taken into account: you will discover first-hand the effect of a good captain and a motivated crew.

If you like to play with miniatures (or don't like to cut the maps out of the book), all maps and extra components are also available as free Print and Play PDF's here on Boardgamegeek.

These battles are included:
The Trader (A learning Scenario) - 1716
The Ambush: John Hawkins vs. the City of San Juan de Ulúa - 1568
The Sacking: Henry Morgan vs the city of Maracaibo - 1669 (Solitaire)
The Heist: Henry Every vs. the Murghal Fleet - 1695
The Salvaging: Charles Vane and Henry Jennings vs. The Spanish Salvage Fleet - 1715
The Hunt: Frances Hume vs. John Martel - 1717
The Reckoning: Blackbeard vs. Stede Bonnet - 1718
The Menace: Black Bart vs. the people of Barbados - 1720 (Solitaire)

Now, hoist the dreaded flag and ready the guns!

—description from the publisher

An updated edition of the narrative Sci fi skirmish hit, containing all the rules needed to create your own warband of unique charactrers and play out fierce and lethal battles on the tabletop. Also included are complete vehicle creation rules, Solo and co-op play, campaign play and progression and a whole host of extra optional rules desined to make planet 28 into the ultimate toolbox of tabletop violence.

From power armourerd warriors wielding hightech plasma rifles, to blood stained barbarians fighting tooth and nail, to armoured behemoths decked out with city crushing cannons, Planet 28 allows you to create profiles for any combination of miniatures.

—description from the publisher

Stage Zero is more than a board game. Each element shares the story of Starship as players race to not only win the game but also solve side-quests and learn some aerospace engineering along the way. The game objective is to attain 42 Progress Points before the Boss returns by building the launch tower (Mechazilla), installing Raptor Engines on your ship & booster, all while surviving events occurring around the Launch Site, named Starbase.

Players are dealt a hand of 2 Tower Cards, and on their turn choose 2 of 4 available actions. 1) They can try to build Mechazilla, Starship, or its booster by trying to match one of their cards to another players. 2) Activate an Event Card and play out its effect. 3) Deliver engines for installation on the rocket. 4) Attempt to launch the rocket in its current status.

Turns continue clockwise, with the group gaining more Progress Points from how successful launches are. The game ends when the Boss token returns to Starbase after 42 turns. If enough points have been scored, everyone wins!

-description from designer

boardgame: Timeline: Il Était Une Fois L'Homme
description:
Timeline is a card game where each card depicts a historical event, invention or discovery on both sides, with the year in which that event occurred, invention or discovery was made on only one side. Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is removed from play and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all his cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

In this version cards represent all the tools and discoveries of the humankind, from the first stone tools to the rovers on Mars.

description:
Create spontaneous and fun stories by quickly connecting different elements. You will not have to pause until you reach the time set in the countdown.
3 different ways to play: Thinko Team (Cooperative game), Thinko Story (Creation of stories with a sequence of images), Thinko Race (Compete to be the winner by reaching the finish line first).
Have you counted sheep to sleep? THINKO, a very creative sheep is the one who creates dreams, mixing very strange things, animals and characters from LAND OF DREAMS, a place where anything can happen. From a fire-breathing apple that flies in the air, a fish with woman's legs, to a flying octopus, THINKO will create the funniest stories.
Have you thought what would happen if you never dreamed again? LAND OF DREAMS is in danger, ENZO together with other villains have come not only to create nightmares but also to steal all dreams, but THINKO together with the 5 GUARDIANS will prevent it, help them by creating with all your imagination and fantasy the most originals.

At the end of the rainbow there is a pot with gold coins, you have to try to reach it before the leprechaun does.

This game allows children ages 3 and up to have fun since they don't need to read or add to play. They can participate from 2 to 6 players.

The journey is made by the characters on the beautiful rainbow according to the colored clovers they obtain. But beware! the goblin can also advance and try to get there first.

Al Final del Arcoíris can be reached in two ways: in cooperative mode, collaborating among all the participants to reach the goal, or it can also be reached in competitive mode, trying to reach the goal before the leprechaun and before everything else. the rest of the participants.

boardgame: The Season: Love & Drama in the Regency Era
description:
Your skirts have been lowered, your hair pulled up, and London is buzzing with activity as more and more families move back to town. Invitations for tea parties, suppers, picnics, and all sorts of social affairs appear almost daily, and all conversations with your dearest friends now include speculation as to which gentleman will be present at this ball or that supper.

As a blooming debutante in the London season, your primary goal is to have the highest reputation points by enhancing your accomplishments, improving your reputation among the social elite, and, most importantly, encouraging proposals and getting married.

You may enhance your accomplishments directly through actions or with choices you make during special events. At the end of the game, each accomplishment point you have translates to one reputation point. Your reputation is improved by socializing and wearing the right outfits at various balls. Debutantes who wear matching dresses and accessories, especially those that match the balls’ themes, receive higher reputation points. You may encourage a suitor to propose by raising a suitor’s affection level to 10 and ensuring you are eligible for that suitor’s dowry and accomplishment requirements.

The lady with the highest amount of reputation points at the end of the game will be the winner of this exciting London season!

-description from publisher

Mikes Skirmish Game is a table top skirmish and RPG game. It comes with 3 ways to play right off the bat! Skirmish battles using our character cards or using your own created with the rules. A narrative or more linear campaign for the Campaign you are playing. And finally a Open World Campaign where party building, Base Building, and Character growth are paramount to the game.
This is a miniature agnostic system so all you really need to play is our Rules. 1D20, some index cards, and miniatures!

description:
("Hücrebaz" is defined as equivalent of "Cell Master")

How do mobile phones work? How do they communicate among themselves?How is communication provided by the switchboards?

Not through satellite communications as many people imagine. Satellites are only engaged in intercontinental and similar situations, but under normal conditions, communications are being established on the earth by means of radio and transmission antennas at the tower or building-like heights that are located on base stations in close proximity to each other.

The stations that are planned to be established on certain areas on the map provide service in those areas so that the subscribers in the area can communicate with mobile phones.

The stations required for communication with mobile phones are not able to provide service very far since the outpu powers are not very high and a new station must be installed after a certain distance.

Our game starts at this point... As your mobile phoine infrastructure service provider, your  aim and your job; to decide which base station should be installed in which area, to rent the area, to set up the required base station and to cover all the cells where  the subscribers is located on the map...

In this direction your goal to win the game is not to cover the areas more than your competitors, but the total number of subscribers you have covered have to be higher. As you upgrade your established towers, your coverage will expand to the extent.

In places where the towes are to be installed, it is necessary to obtain the right of installation by renting. Every cell that is rented or covered is painted in the player's own color and yet controlled by the player. Sometimes luck turns; extra prizes are won, but sometimes they do not get lucky; with public reactions, natural disasters or theft, the player may lose control of his place or resurces.

Rent Sites
Install Towers
Cover Cells

description: StoryCards: Die drei ??? Kids – Football-Stars is a storytelling game of sorts in which a player or team is confronted by a situation that presents them with two options for a journey. The more cards they lay down, the more they score. If a card is no longer available, however, then the game ends and the detective trio has failed.

Drivers! Start your engines! Get ready and…be dastardly!

Dastardly Drivers is a simple, fast-paced racing game where players compete against each other to cross the finish line first! But, to do that, they’ll need to employ some strategy, some guile, and a bit of luck to avoid any obstacles on the track and finish ahead of the other racers.

Each player secretly chooses their actions for the round and each action is revealed in player turn-order (which changes based on the player position on the track). Players can also receive items that will aid them along the track or help them hinder their opponents.

The game supports up to 6 players and features a modular track creation system so no two races need to be the same! Want to do 1 lap of a long 20-tile track? Perfect! Want to do 3 laps of a short 8-tile track? Sounds great! Dastardly Drivers provides the components you need to make the track to your desired specifications!

-description from publisher

Cyferki ("Digits") is a didactic puzzle for preschoolers, which familiarizes children with digits and numbers in a friendly way. Thanks to this educational game, children learn to count, train memory, stimulate imagination and expand their abilities.

When the stack is depleted the player that gathered most tiles wins.

description:
ANTARA is a solo tower defense game based on dice placement.

THEMATIC
Play as a group of heroes that must protect their city against the Darklord and his army.

GOALS
First, you must find and destroy 3 relic cards hidden in the threat deck. The relic cards give the Darklord immortality. After destroying the 3 relics, you can now damage the Darklord.

The game is won if you defeat the Darklord. You lose it if your city's defense level drops to 0.

GAMEPLAY QUICK OVERVIEW
Throughout the game, you will enter a succession of rounds until you reach the win or loss condition. A turn consists of 4 phases :

Phase I – "Preparation phase"
Launch all the action dice available.

Phase II – "Actions phase"
During this phase you may take the following actions anytime you want and in any order :
Play a hero - Place an action dice on the hero's card then resolve one of the 2 actions on the card.

●    Manipulate dice value - Spend mana points to reroll a dice or change his value by -1/+1.
●    Resolve a plot card -  Roll dice in the required combination.
●    Destroy a relic card - Resolve the specific action mentioned on the relic card.
●    Gain additional dice - Spend mana points to add a bonus dice to your dice pool. (3 max)
●    Increase defense level - Spend mana points to increase your defense level by 1.

Phase III – "Enemies phase"
In this phase, the enemy plays. You will need to resolve the different steps in the following order:

1. Reveal a new threat card and place it on the battlefield
2. Launch the threat dice. The result of the threat dice will determine the enemies who will be activated during this turn.
3. All activated enemies should be played from left to right and from bottom to top.
4. Reveal a scheme card and resolve it.

Phase IV – "Clean up phase"
During this turn, decrease the defense level by one for every hero that is K.O. (a hero becomes K.O. if his lifepoint falls to 0 during this turn).

The dice activated on K.O.'s hero should be placed in the Sanatorium and the others should be placed in the Barracks.

The dice in the Sanataorium can’t be played until you play a specific action.
The dice in the Barracks can be played for the next round.
Restore all the K.O.'s hero lifepoint.
Start a new a turn.

In this Monopoly Junior board game kids can imagine exploring the Mushroom Kingdom with Super Mario artwork, themes, and gameplay. Players move around the gameboard as Mario, Peach, Yoshi, or Luigi buying up properties and collecting coins. The player with the most coins wins. Players roll the power-up die to find out if they can press the Coin Sound Unit featuring the iconic coin sound from Super Mario, and collect coins. Board spaces feature scenes and themes Super Mario fans will recognize including Yoshi's Hill, Cooligan Fields, and Rock-Candy Mines. Monopoly Junior is a good introduction to the Monopoly game. It's designed to be fast, fun, and exciting for younger players.
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
The game is a trick-taking card game.
 Goal of the game: In Döttrar & Söner ("Daughters and Sons") every player plays as one of Gustav Vasa’s children: Erik, Johan, Katarina, Cecilia, Magnus, or Anna. In the game you have markers representing influence and are called influence markers. You must win as many influence markers as possible to convince the riksdag of estates that you should be Sweden’s next regent.

Who wins? The first person to get ten influence markers wins the game and is crowned regent.

Subliminal is a creative communication and association party game about reading minds, forming connections, and uncovering hidden messages.

Each round, five colored tiles representing different concepts are revealed. The active player secretly chooses one of them and creates a clue using randomly drawn letters and numbers. The challenge is to guide the other players toward the intended tile — without naming it directly.

The other players interpret the clue and secretly guess which tile the player had in mind. If at least one player guesses correctly, the tile is won and added to a growing puzzle in the center of the table.

As the game progresses, this shared puzzle gradually reveals the colors and symbols of the players’ hidden Archetypes.

But the puzzle holds an even deeper secret.

In the final phase of the game, the collected tiles form the basis of a hidden Subliminal Message. One player secretly constructs the message while the others attempt to decipher it word by word.

If the players uncover the message, the solver wins the game.
If nobody manages to reveal it, the creator of the message claims victory.

Blending creative association, interpretation, and deduction, Subliminal challenges players not simply to guess the answer — but to guess how others think.

Every round produces surprising connections, unexpected interpretations, and memorable moments around the table.

Updated description provided by the designer/publisher'

You will take on someone's figure, facial expressions, gesticulation and voice in turbulent life situations.

Some of the characters will be easier to relate to, some more difficult, but exploring another way of being is always an exciting adventure that will motivate you to discover hidden qualities within that you may not have pondered before, and it will make you laugh long after the game is over.

There are 54 characters and hundreds of funny situations in which they will get to express themselves.

But, there is the tricky part - they will have to hide a special word in their scripts and guess other players hidden words if they want to win.

In the first phase of the game, when players are guessing each others characters, they score points when someone guesses who they were and when they guess others characters. In the second phase, when secret word is involved, everybody tries to find "hard" word for others and to hide word given from others in self constructed one sentence script.
Each time players guess the secret word they advance on the scoring board and each time nobody guesses secret word, the author advances even more. First player to reach 24 points on the scoring board - wins.

-description from designer

Astonish everyone (including yourself) with your illustrating skills. In this creative game participants draw 6 cards from the deck. Cards contain shapes of head, eyes, nose, mouth, ears and hair. Each participant draws unique face using acquired cards. The result is number of different, funny faces and expressions.

Game consists of 4 decks of card. Each deck aims at different drawing: face, robot, dog or fish. Other side of cards hold parts of large puzzles.

There is non competitive mode and competitive mode of the game.
In non competitive mode player ore players randomly takes six cards numbered from one to six from one of decks and draws new characters by following suggestions on cards. In more advanced version, players mix all the decks and take randomly 12 cards and try to implement as much elements as they can in new drawings.

In competitive version, players play simple game of matching numbers by order. The first who puts all cards in order first ends the game. Players count who acquired the most sets of cards from 1 to 6. The players with the most "Face by Case" sets is the winner.

The game develops drawing skills, releases tension in children who are intimidated to draw, creates supportive environment for drawing and talking about emotions.

-description from designer

Maze building cardgame included with limited edition copies of Labyrinth of Galleria: The Moon Society. Players delve into the titular labyrinth to hunt down magical, cursed curios by building out the labyrinth and win by accrueing the most points.

Discover this new educational board game for 1-4 players to understand how vaccines are developed to fight diseases.

​You are the Leader of a Biotech developing the next generation of vaccines to protect populations against fast-spreading outbreaks. Discover potent antigenic formulations, launch your clinical trials, manage your production capacity and define appropriate vaccine prices to successfully eradicate life-threatening diseases with best-in-class vaccines.

About the Game

In a world threatened by persistent diseases and fast-spreading outbreaks, you are leading a biotech company developing the next generation of vaccines.

​Your goal is to protect the most at-risk populations against infectious diseases. You are challenged to develop effective vaccines by discovering the best antigenic formulations, to launch your clinical trials, to manage your vaccine production capacity and to define an appropriate pricing strategy. Pay attention to the prices you establish since the public opinion may influence your sales.

Always remember to invest in new technologies & expertise, and wisely manage your Research & Development budget in order to develop a successful vaccine portfolio to eradicate all the diseases and save lives!

Never the same game twice!
Erasable "Disease" & "Vaccine" cards for an ever-changing fight against infectious diseases

Game Principle
Create your vaccine formulas
Acquire Technologies & Expertise to accelerate your Research
Validate your vaccines with Toxicology & Clinical studies
Manage your vaccine production capacity
Launch your vaccines to fight diseases

The Game Modes

     Competitive
     Quick competitive
     Cooperative
     Solo

Vindication is a highly strategic, fantasy-based tabletop journey for 2-5 players. Play time is 15-30 minutes per player.

Thrown overboard for a life of wretchedness, you wash ashore a hostile island ruins — completely alone with nothing except the breath in your lungs and an undaunted spirit.

Through your advanced resource management, area control tactics, and free-form action selection, you’ll add companions to your party, acquire bizarre relics, attain potent character traits, and defeat a host of unusual monsters in the ultimate goal of mastering heroic attributes — and regaining honor.

You may perform 3-4 actions on each turn in the order you feel is most advantageous that turn: activate a companion, travel to a new location, and interact with a map tile. Many actions require the the use of your influence to gain attributes in a one-of-a-kind heroic attribute alchemy system, which is leveraged to gain the game's most powerful rewards. For example, you can meditate at a spire to gain inspiration. You can train at a fort to gain strength. But then you can combine your inspiration and strength to gain the courage (inspired strength) which allows you to perform a bounty hunt.

There are distinctive end-game triggers that can be affected through game play, unique card abilities that can be merged in unusual ways for potent combinations, and fresh tile placement each game for high replay-ability.

The Archive of the Ancients edition of Vindication includes the following previously existing content:

description: Doubles the trail or allows additional players.

In Bark Magic, 2-4 players compete to unearth mystic gems and be crowned Ultimutt Sorcerer. Players can only reach this goal by outwitting their opponents, winning heated battles, and gaining the strength needed to dig deep into the ancient ruins.

Players take on the role of anthropomorphic wizard dogs, playing skill cards and participating in events triggered by event cards.

Von Manstein’s Last Stand: Russia 1943-44, What If? (VMLS) is an low-complexity, two-player war game simulating the events that could have unfolded during the year following a major switch in east front strategy by Hitler made just prior to the launch of the Kursk offensive. That is, he cancels that ill-fated attack and instead transfers approximately two armies from the east to the west, there to use them against the Anglo-Allies, while leaving von Manstein in charge in Russia. Von Manstein rationalizes the situation by pulling back to the narrowest front possible, the Riga-Odessa line, and there prepares to make a final stand against the Red Army.

A complete game generally takes three to four hours to complete, and it is suitable for solitaire play.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A board-game about dig to find your family memories and be able to discover the family tree.

Each player must be able to complete the combinations of objects of the various ancestors found in the ground to reconstruct the family tree, within the time set by a 20-minute background music.

The game takes place in turns clockwise starting from the first player, everyone will show a certain number of land cards based on the tool unlocked up to that moment (using the coins) and will look for the correct combinations corresponding to the ancestors, so that they can keep  the terrain cards of the combination and being able to flip the corresponding ancestor card, placing it in the correct position with respect to your objective card.

The game ends when the game time runs out or when all ancestors are completed. Each player gets a final score based on how many ancestors they have revealed, where they were placed, and how many items they have found.

Developed during the Global Game Jam 2023 in Cagliari (Italy) by Stefano Fadda, Franco Sardo, Leonardo Tomasi, illustrations by Laura Sotgiu, Giulia Scalas, music by Giuliano Cau.

—description from the designer

Think you've got what it takes to rule the roost? Tactically outrank your opponents by using your cards to work your way up the mafia tree.

As you progress from poker chips and getaway cars, to underbosses and The Don, don't forget; as high as you make it up the Mafia tree, The Don's Momma always has the final say!

Get rid of your cards first to be named The Don, and avoid being labelled the traitor.

—description from the publisher

The Senior Architects Expansion takes the gameplay of the Reviving Kathmandu base game to the next level. Take on special abilities through your character cards (architects), collect more points for public objectives, and get ready to complete advanced missions.

The expansion includes:

     6 character cards (architects)
     10 public objective cards
     10 advanced mission cards


—description from the designer

boardgame: Goblin Vaults: Chaos & Order Promo Card
description:
The Chaos & Order Basic Goal Card was given to the purchasers of the first edition via the Thunderworks online store.

Chaos: Score each chamber and each tier with cards of different values.

Order: Score each tier with 4 cards whose values form a sequence in ascending or descending order.

The march of technology across BattleTech’s eras is relentless—but some BattleMech designs never die. Each installment of this PDF-only series not only includes a brand new BattleMech or OmniMech, but also details Classic ’Mech designs from both the Inner Sphere and the Clans, now fully rebuilt with Dark Age technology (3085 and beyond).

Volume 32 Includes Rules and Record Sheets for:

You thought after your last mission that you had finally successfully driven the cosmic hegemony out of the universe. Wrong! The three alien races known as The Red Hegemony are back and have already infiltrated several Intergalactic Alliance planets — but that's not enough for them! They want domination, no matter what the cost...

As a secret agent of the Intergalactic Council, it's your job to prevent this from happening. The fact that this sounds like a hopeless task doesn't stop you from grabbing one of the new ships and assembling a strong crew to embark on a journey to the farthest reaches of the universe and crush the Red Hegemony once and for all!

In Pulp Invasion X3, you have new spaceships and a new bridge crew to help you eliminate the threat once and for all. Use your skills in combat, diplomacy, and super science to track down their world-shattering super weapons before it's too late!
Not to be open until your party has unlocked and solved Envelope X.

Who will improve the CO2 balance and plant the most trees? Add to this the conversion of fossil power plants into renewable energy sources and the common goal of climate neutrality comes ever closer!

In the family game Das klimaneutrale Talent (DKT), the topics of environment, climate and sustainability take center stage, but without a raised forefinger, but with a lot of fun and a willingness to take risks, as well as a portion of luck. Based on the classic game Das kaufmännische Talent, which has been known for generations, especially in Austria, free areas may be leased here and then planted with trees (later with forests), which positively influences the climate marker. Environmentally conscious people use the train in the game and everyone tries to do their part in shutting down fossil fuel power plants, as this commitment is rewarded at the end of the game.

Depending on your willingness to take risks and your financial strength, you can also make lively investments in "The Climate-Neutral Talent", sometimes facing insolvency or even bankruptcy. Weather extremes, the CO2 tax and penalties for climate sinners provide additional excitement. However, one thing is always clear: the climate can only be saved by working together, which is why it's win or lose. Whoever has planted the most trees and forests is crowned climate hero.

Game target
The goal is to work together to achieve climate lity. To achieve this, you must either convert three fossil fuel power plants into renewable energy sources or reduce CO² emissions to a minimum.
In addition, the person who has planted the most trees will be crowned climate hero.

In a special way, The Climate Neutral Talent combines cheerful fun with informative facts about sustainable development and goals for climate neutrality according to the UN "Agenda 2030". More is vividly explained in the accompanying booklet.

Of course, Piatnik has not only placed value on sustainability editorially, but also in the game production. Das klimaneutrale Talent is produced entirely with materials from the EU, and the (pre-)materials are FSC-certified. Administration and production at Piatnik in Austria use 100% green electricity. However, this does not only apply to this game, but for years already to the majority of Piatnik products.

All components used in the game were produced from biodegradable materials!

—description from the publisher (translated)

The quiz for smart kids on the topic of the environment and sustainability.

What helps the environment? What is the "ozone hole"? And who is Greta Thunberg? Together you try to answer questions about the environment and climate and move your game piece forward. If someone doesn't know the answer, the team can help. Can you make it to the finish line?

Also includes rules for the game for 1 player: in and different difficulty levels!

—description from the publisher (translated)

What can you do for the climate? How do you protect the environment?

In this game you will try to slow down climate change. Work together to stop the approaching problems and keep global warming as low as possible. Because even if it takes the cohesion of everyone to improve the Earth: You can take action with small steps and be part of the solution.

With 92 quiz questions that teach children about sustainability.

The children's and family game "My green footprint" is suitable for 1-4 players:inside from 8 years and lasts about 30 minutes.

boardgame: Home Run: The Baseball Dice Game
description:
Recreate all the action of the ballpark with this tabletop baseball game that will have you getting base hits and scoring runs just by rolling the dice! With each combination of rolled dice representing a play, grab your teammates and see whose team can rack up the most runs and which team will strike out. It's just the thing to add a fun new twist to America's favorite pastime!

—description from the back of the box

The game board is a stylized baseball diamond, with a dry-erase scoreboard at centerfield, twelve metal pegs in two different colors, with holes for the pegs at each base location on the diamond. The pegs fit into two "dugouts" of six holes each on opposite sides of the board.

After determining team names and which team is home and visitor, the players write the team names on the scoreboard with the provided marker. The visitor player places one of their pegs at home plate as the batter, and rolls the two dice once. The result is compared to the chart on the playing board, and the runner is either out, or scores a single, double, triple, or home run. If the runner is out, that peg is placed on the lowest numbered open "OUTS" hole on the scoreboard. If the runner advances, the peg is placed in the hole at the proper base location. If a run is scored, the number on the scoreboard is adjusted with the marker and that peg is returned to the dugout.

Play proceeds until the visitor player has three outs, then the home player repeats the process for the bottom half of the inning. The two players may play a full nine innings, or some agreed-upon shorter game. The game can be played by two players or teams.

—user summary

boardgame: Lumen: A Game of Photographic Terminology
description:
Lumen is a game of photographic terminology and image analysis that challenges players to visually interpret concepts and critique images.

Designed as a fun and engaging educational experience, Lumen introduces players to terminology useful in photographic critique and discussion, while also encouraging critical thinking, debate, and analysis.

Lumen is a judging game that consists of four parts: reading a terminology card, submitting photo cards, judging, and scoring. Each round a judge reads a terminology card and players submit photo cards in response. The judge selects the photo card they feel best interprets the term and explain why they made their choice, then players engage in discussion. The first player to score five points by winning five rounds wins the game.

-description from designer

Easy to learn, simple to play, yet always a challenge. Complete your puzzle and find your mastercard  to win...but move the wrong way and you could be jiggered.

In Jiggered, players traverse a board with their markers collecting numbered personal puzzle pieces in order, then finally their mastercard. Dice is used for movement, but rolling a six means the board will be shifted from below, leaving unpredictable holes that players' markers can fall through and be lost. Each player has 5 markers, but if all are lost, they are out of the game. Solitaire variants exist, and the game also comes with poker chips to add a betting element to play.

—description from the back of the box

Deck 'Em: War in Europe 1756-1815 is a war game for 2-6 players with influences from Empires in Arms, Axis & Allies, and Conquest of the Empire II (among others). It utilizes an action point system used by the major powers while supported by card play based on the minor countries.

This is not your typical back-and-forth, territory-swapping, dice-rolling slaughterfest war game! Yes, there are dice, but the unique combat system does a better job of simulating the multiple battles and skirmishes that were a big part of campaign warfare during this time period. Players will make tough decisions on where to maximize their strength while working together with their allies.

I've created 5 campaign scenarios representing different years, each with unique and historically accurate alliances, starting territories and forces, and major power strengths. The game also gives you the freedom to create your own historical campaigns, or simply play a cut-throat, free-for-all style.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Ka’a is an abstract strategy game for two players that evokes the ancient Mayan ball game of Mesoamerica.

Play
Each player controls a team of three pawns (Jaguars or Eagles) on a 4-by-4 hex grid.

On your turn, you can perform two actions in any combination: hit or move. Or, you may promote one of your pieces by flipping it to the promoted side, giving up your actions for that turn.

Scoring
Players take turns until a player scores a point by hitting the ball into their opponent’s end zone. Promoted pieces can score points by hitting the ball into the stone hoops at the side of the board.

Actions
You can move your pawn one space in any direction.

You can hit the ball in any direction the number of spaces equal to adjacent friendly pawns: one, two, or three spaces. If the ball is not next to any of your pawns you cannot hit it.

Winning
The first player to score two points is the winner.

Ko Rule
A player must change the board state in some way on their turn. At the end of a player's turn, they cannot return the board to the previous scenario, undoing their opponent's turn.

A rules PDF is coming soon. For now, check out the explanation video.

-description from designer

A fast and fun WW2 fighter plane combat game.  Simple rules that fit on a single page.  All you need is an ordinary deck of playing cards which are used to determine possible manouvres, a few model planes (flat counters included if you don't have those yet), some friends that don't mind being shot down and a ruthless, killer instinct!

Optional rules allow for altitude advantage

description:
A game that recreates the first period of the roman epoch.

The players can control the political and military life of the newborn city, struggling to achieve supremacy over the other players and to survive to the multiple threats posed by hostiles neighbors, like Samnites, Etruscans and so on.

The game comes with separate rules for solitaire and multiplayer play.

During a turn of the solo play the player maneuvers the armies of Rome and may fight battles, lay sieges and intimidate and/or blackmail AI opponents. (No bot provided).

In the multiplayer game, the player that controls the King of Rome can choose the same actions of the solo game. At the same time, the players that control the other patrician families in Rome struggle to avoid an early victory of the King maneuvering the enemy peoples, the Senate and the Pontifex maximus to contrast the King's plans.

The game ends at the 24th turn, but sudden victories (and defeats) are possible, if certain conditions happens. IP points are the dial that indicates the victory of a player. They are accumulated (and lost) turn by turn in many ways.

-description from developer

boardgame: Shogun no Katana: Wandering Characters Expansion Set
description:
Wandering Characters
This set contains 7 Wandering Character miniatures each one with its own rules (and sometimes, tiles and tokens).  4 of them are randomly placed on the main gameboard at
the beginning of the game and can be activated by the players to get special bonuses (different per each Character).

When you decide to activate a Character, you must first of all move it to another empty space on the
board .
Choose carefully where to place the Character, because
you might help the next player going after you, since
they can use the Wandering Character just like you did!

Content
7 Miniatures
7 Character tiles
12 Master forger cards
15 Artist tiles
Plastic tray that fits with the base game

boardgame: Shogun no Katana: Wandering Character Unsuiso
description:
The Character
The Unsuisō (Wandering Monk) is one of the Characters that can be placed on the board at the beginning of the game.

He comes with a set of 4 Bowl tiles. Give one of them to each player. When you encounter him, you can decide to offer him 1 Coin by placing it on the Bowl tile in your game area. 
At the end of each round, score as many Points as the number of Coins in your Bowl.

Content
1 Miniature
1 Character tile
4 Bowl tiles

boardgame: Shogun no Katana: Wandering Character Geisha
description:
The Character
The Geisha is one of the Characters that can be placed on the board at the beginning of the game. She comes with a set of 15 Geisha tokens (3 per type of 5 types).

Each token represents one of the arts she has mastered. Randomly place 3 of them on her tile and leave the others in a pile nearby. When you encounter her, take one of the 3 tokens from her tile, then replace it with a random one from the pile. 
At the end of the game, score the points shown on her tile for each set of different tokens you have collected (based on the number of tokens in each set).

Content
1 Miniature
1 Character tile
15 Geisha tokens

A version of the popular Apples to Apples game with pictures only. Great for young kids and families.

The game play is the same as the original game where the judge reveals a green card and players each choose the most fitting red card from their hand to play that best matches the green card. The only difference is that you are trying to match pictures instead of words. The judge chooses the player whose card best fits the description given by the green card, and they get a point.

Contents: 168 cards quick play rules.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
You've discovered heretics' terrible plot to envelop the world in darkness. To protect your enjoyable everyday life, you'll have to band together with your fellow students to stop the heretics' assault and interrupt their dark ritual.

The players cannot fight the heretics directly. You must sniff out their target location and strengthen its defenses, laying traps to neutralize them. At times, you may receive assistance from your schoolteachers or from nature.

"Brigare" is a cooperative game where players do their best to improvise a solution within a limited period of time. Can your plans achieve victory over an overwhelming opponent?

A promo card distributed at Origins and Gen Con in 2018.

Empath: Shuffle with the Investigator cards, discard 1 and then shuffle in the Cultist cards.

"If a Cultist is Discovered, you become a Cultist."

boardgame: ワケアリガールコレクション (Wakeari Girls Collection)
description: You have died and been reincarnated into a video game called Love Period—only, Love Period happens to be notorious for the slogan "get too close to the heroine and you will certainly meet your death". In this bluffing card game, your aim is to direct the attention of the Love Period characters away from yourself and onto other players. Lay any number of cards from your hand facedown in front of another player and name a character. The other player either accepts your statement or calls your bluff. If they were wrong, attention is directed towards them, so they place the cards in front of them. If they were correct, attention is drawn back to you instead, and you take the cards. The other player may also choose to pass on the cards to a third player instead, but they must first turn one of them faceup, keeping it beside them; if it matches any cards they've previously flipped from passing on prior turns, they must place all those cards in front of them. Gain too many cards of any one character and you die, losing the game while everyone else wins. However, if all players happen to survive by the time the deck runs out, the player who gained the most cards wins.

boardgame: Victory of the RAF: The biggest match of modern times
description:
Vintage strategy board game about air battle with chess mechanic, square to square movement. Different airplanes (heavy bombers, diving bombers, fighters - each side owns 15 airplanes) and anti aircraft guns (each side owns 5 such a guns) have different actions.
Player, who first capture opponents town, wins the game.

Published in Brussel, Belgium.

—user summary
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description:
NAVKAKRI is the Modern avatar of Nine Men’s Morris Board game! The nine men’s Morris is one of the most played strategy board games dating back centuries. The earliest traces can be found in the Roman empire and possibly Ancient Egypt. This game is known by many names, such as Mill or Windmill. FROLIX has designed NAVKAKRI board game which improves skills in strategy and planning. NAVKAKRI follows all rules of Nine men's Morris Game which is a strategy board game for two players. The board consists of a grid with twenty-four intersections or points. Each player has nine pieces, or 'men'. Players try to form 'mills'—three of their own pieces lined horizontally or vertically—allowing a player to remove an opponent's piece from the game. The grid is equipped with series of smart sensors which makes it interactive and joyful.

—description from the publisher

This is a promotional game for "Lintsi", ie. Linnanmäki, the main amusement park of Helsinki since 1950. In this vaguely Monopoly-like roll-and-move game, the players first need to divide the various rides and attractions between themselves, using their starting cash in a small sub-game. The main game includes touring Linnanmäki for a pre-agreed number of rounds for each player (a minimum of five is recommended), collecting cash when passing Go, taking event cards as instructed, and visiting the various attractions and paying for a ticket to their owners - or possibly even buying the attraction from the bank if the previous owner already went bankrupt and is out of the game, and the attraction has thus become available in the open market again. The player with the most wealth in all collected assets - cash and attractions - at the end of the game wins.

Based on the Linnanmäki attractions included, the game is probably from 1975 at the latest.

A group of us treasure hunters landed on this island in search of a golden city that was lost long ago.
Legend has it that the island's civilization was destroyed by the wrath of an ancient god. Immediate-ty upon arrival, we were attacked by an unknown monster. Its tentacle-covered form was shrouded in fog and could not be fully identified. We sent a distress call, threw everything away, and ran for our lives. On the way, we encountered every danger imaginable, including the tentacled monster, zombies, pirates, etc., and we helped each other...though sometimes abandoning some to sacrifice...but we managed to get through it.

But just when we thought we had it all figured out, we were cornered on a suspension bridge over a great valley! There are zombies in front of the bridge, the tentacled monster behind us, and pirates in the river at the bottom of the valley, all trying to kill us. There is nothing left to do. This is an extraordinary island, which is infested with all kinds of evil things! But it's too late for regrets... We must somehow survive on this crazy suspension bridge until the rescue helicopter sees the distress call and comes for help!

Whine with your friends about the worst date you’ve ever been on, a time when no one believed you, or about the worst thing to forget to pack on a vacation. Whine Night is all about spilling the tea and laughing with your friends!

Up to 8 players take turns sharing stories to answer relatable and annoying prompts, then vote on who had the best story. At the end of the night, whoever won the most rounds wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crimson Skies: The Broadway Bombers Squadron Pack
description:
A set of four aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules.  The pack includes two E-1C Avenger miniatures (aircraft #1 and #2) and two F6II Brigand miniatures (aircraft #3 and #4).

All four miniatures are painted in a style common to the squadron with different details on each plane.

boardgame: Crimson Skies: The Fortune Hunters Squadron Pack

A set of four aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules.  The pack includes two P12-J MK III Devestator miniatures (aircraft #11 and #12) and two FB14 Vampire miniatures (aircraft #13 and #14).

All four miniatures are painted in a style common to the squadron with different details on each type of plane.

boardgame: Crimson Skies: The Hollywood Knights Squadron Pack
