A set of four aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules.  The pack includes two Bloodhawk miniatures (aircraft #5 and #6) and two Firebrand miniatures (aircraft #7 and #8).
All four miniatures are painted in a style common to the squadron with different details on each type of plane.

boardgame: Crimson Skies: The Red Skull Legion Squadron Pack
description:
A set of four aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules.  The pack includes two Peacemaker 370 miniatures (aircraft #17 and #18) and two Firebrand miniatures (aircraft #19 and #20).
All four miniatures are painted in a style common to the squadron with different details on each type of plane.

In this set collection game you are an art dealer trying to collect more and more art. As you draw more cards you will want to collect more of the same suit to have the highest total amongst your opponents. When you feel you have the highest total, you indicate that you are ready for everyone else to show their hand. The person with the lowest total will need to pay up from their stash of cash. But watch out! You may need to dip into your art collection to stay in the game. Collecting art buffers you from having to pay from your cash. It's brutal! But hey, it's art!

boardgame: Ridley's Games Room: Sing It Back
description: Step into the limelight with Sing It Back, the game that entertains regardless of whether you're out-of-tune or operatic. Simply sing or say the first line of the song, and see if your team can sing or say the next line back. It's time to warm up those vocal chords! (Earplugs not included)

Will you win the Beast Wars?

Play as a heroic Maximal or a villainous Predacon in an epic battle to secure vast quantities of Energon on a primitive planet!

Players move around the board trying to take over locations around the Ancient planet using Energon, then installing Statis pods to charge more rent.

Wield the power of the Golden Disk and obtain precious Stasis Pods! In this Monopoly board game for Transformers fans, claim the Golden Disk for gameplay perks but watch out … another player can steal it at any moment

14 playable tokens! Switch between tokens throughout the game to play in robot mode or alt mode! The player with the most Energon wins the game!

Includes gameboard, 14 character tokens, 7 character cards, 6 reference cards, 1 Golden Disk, 1 Golden Disk card, 20 Title Deed cards, 16 Maximal cards, 16 Predacon cards, 16 Stasis Pods, 1 die, 100 plastic Energon pieces (55 Energon Crystals, 45 Energon Cubes) and game guide

America's New Block Game, Quadro!

This set of 20 blocks comes with four different games to play as well as three puzzles. Nineteen blocks use the numbers 0 to 6, as in a double-6 domino set, plus one "Q-Quadro", which acts as a joker or wild.

Quadro is in the vein of dominoes, where players are trying to place blocks that have ends which add to a multiple of 5.

Pung requires the two players to form two 2x2 squares, where all the inner numbers match domino-style.

Put and Take has the players attempting to fill their hand with a single suit of blocks. As in dominoes, a suit is a set of blocks that all have the same number on them.

Match is a simple game for children, where the players score points for matching numbers on the blocks. Each player gets 4 turns, then the totals are scored.

The fate of the empire hangs in the balance. You are the final champion. Grab your sword and prepare for battle. It has come to this. Centuries of war have brought death and destruction upon the people of the empire. Warriors from the every clan have ravaged the once beautiful landscape, in an effort to bring their masters closer to complete domination. It is only now that all sides have reached a stalemate. The only remaining solution is a duel of champions. With their glowing swords of power, the fabled blade masters have been summoned to one final contest to determine not only the fate of your clan,but the future of the empire of swords.

Set up the game by shuffling the 18 cards and dealing 7 to each player. Gameplay consists of each player playing a card from their hand face down, then simultaneously revealing. Thrust beats slash, slash beats chop, chop beats thrust. If your card beats the opponents card, you land a HIT. Parry blocks any of those. A successful hit after a Parry lands two HITs. There is also an unlockable strike card that cannot be stopped.

The game ends when either or both players run out of cards or someone receives 5 hits. The one to run out of cards or the one to have received 5 hits loses. If both players run out of cards and neither player has received 5 hits, the player that landed the most hits wins the game.

Malekith, the thirteenth son of a thirteenth son, is the ruler of the Dark Elves of Svartalfheim and a bitter enemy of Thor, who uses magic and deception to bring his enemies down and wage war across all the Realms. He transforms mortals into his unwitting agents as a master manipulator with a silver tongue and the capacity to assume the form of another.

He is the Master of the Wild Hunt with the means to summon an army of voracious hounds to pursue anything he wishes.

Contents:
1 Miniature
  ‣ Malekith
1 Round Scenic Base (65mm)
1 Character Stat Card
2 Team Tactic Cards
7 Tokens

The superpowered card game!

KAPOW! is a fast, thrilling card game in which you have to pit the might of your superhero squad against the world, in your quest to become the very best in the universe. The heroes in your team might not exactly be A-listers – but what they lack in talent and useful, appealing superpowers they more than make up for with enthusiasm. And weaponry.

Lead an unlikely cast of characters as you smash through your opponents’ defenses, knock out their headquarters and barricade yourself against counter-attacks. But be wary of that grotesque gang of supervillains lurking in the shadows – they play by their own rules and want nothing more than to put a dent in your plans.

Now, more than ever, we’re all holding out for a hero – or even better, a team of them. And while KAPOW! might not be a game about the heroes we need, it’s definitely one about the heroes we deserve.

—description from the designer

The game where you find dates for your mates!

How well do you know your friends? What are their hot buttons? Their red flags? Do they prefer soul-searchers to stock market surfers, and what toxic traits will they overlook in someone who can make their inhibitions disappear quicker than you can type “u up?”

Grab a handful of cards and wish the perfect partners into existence. In this hilarious game for 2 to 20 players you’re guaranteed all of the laughs and at least some of the exhilaration of dating – without any of the downsides.

—description from the designer

Karzar is a card game. In it, each player starts his game by secretly choosing a front, Iran or Turan. With access to hero cards and support cards, players simultaneously send their heroes to the battlefield during two rounds and then help cards. There is only one rule in the battlefield, the winner is whoever is stronger at the end.

Story
"Finally, when the great battle has arrived, the Iranian and Turan armies have set up their tents and raised their shutters on the bank of the Jeyhoon river. There is an uproar. A cloud of dust covers the sun and the sound of sharpening swords reaches can be heard from all sides. All are eager to start a war. The agreement is that according to ancient customs, the warriors of both lands will fight face to face. But the strength of arms and the art of war are not their only helpers, and supernatural forces influence their efficiency from all corners, until the end of the battle is turned to one side every time, and now the history of this bloody war is in your hands..."

Calamandara is a competitive and strategic board game, playable by 2 to 4 people. Here the playeres, called Travelers, must compete against each other to reach the center point of the board. Each traveler starts the game at the beginning of their respective board and to move his pawn around the the grid board he needs to use Dogmatic Symbols, which are tokens that have specific attributes.

Each Dogmatic Symbol has a different function that will help travelers navigate the board. As an example, some symbols help the traveler move faster while others can disrupt their opponents' journey to the center of the board.

While the use of Dogmatic Symbols happens and the players' pawns advance across the board, the travelers also use the Memory Cards, which serve to intensify their moves or cause sudden changes in their opponents' strategy. Those special cards are limited and travelers need to choose the right time to use them wisely.

Each Traveler will perform 4 types of actions or phases on their turn:

1st Phase: The traveler will Acquire Dogmatic Symbols using Symbol Cards.

2nd Phase: The traveler will be able to Insert the Dogmatic Symbols on the board to start setting up his movement strategy.

3rd Phase: The traveler will be able to Move his Traveler Token (pawn) on the board, according to the positioning of the Dogmatic Symbols on his board.

4th Phase: The traveler will be able to Reserve, Discard and Acquire Memory Cards.

The Memory Cards can be used at any time in the game, providing a unique dynamism in all matches.

To achieve victory, it is necessary to create a good strategy and make the right decisions, thinking about the present and the future. The multiple possibilities of using Dogmatic Symbols and Memory Cards will make players explore reasoning and risk management in their decisions.

And the objective is simple: Whoever reaches the center of the board first wins!

—description from the publisher

Pointing fingers is usually considered rude, but this time it’s compulsory! One player reads a question aloud and then all count 1-2-3 together and point to the person they think it fits best. All players in majority group win a card and first to 5 cards wins the game. Flex your fingers and enjoy…

—description from the publisher

A Shakespearean card game for Rhymesters, Rulers and Star-Crossed Language Lovers.

Build the most valuable London theatre season by collecting characters from thirteen of Shakespeare’s best-loved plays. This illustrated card-based party game combines Shakespeare, drama, and money—it’s catnip for theatre buffs, language nerds, and book clubs. 
 Collect complete plays with all four of their leading characters. The wild cards can increase or decrease your fortune. If you fill in the missing word in the lines of dialog on the bottom of the cards at the end of the game, whether on your own sets of cards or others', you earn extra points for each correct quote. May the biggest Bard lover win!

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Emma Frost & Psylocke
description:
Two powerful psychic mutants await in Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Emma Frost & Psylocke.

First is Emma Frost, a powerful telepath with the ability to turn her body into organic diamond. Joining her is Psylocke, who can manifest the focused force of telepathic power as a "psychic blade". This pack adds these characters to their collections with three beautifully sculpted miniatures and three stat cards: one for Psylocke and one for both Emma Frost's normal and diamond form. Beyond their basic abilities, this pack invites players to unlock even more potential in these characters with six Team Tactics cards, including one that gives Emma Frost a leadership ability for the new Hellfire Club Affiliation!

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Wakanda Affiliation Pack
description:
Players can begin assembling their own team of Wakandans thanks to the Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Wakanda Affiliation Pack.

Bringing together Black Panther, Killmonger, Shuri, and Okoye from earlier releases in one convenient package, this pack gives players the miniatures that form the cornerstone of the Wakanda affiliation. Along with these characters, players will also find stat cards for all four characters as well as the Usurp the Throne, Wakanda Forever, Advanced R&D, and Vibranium Shielding Team Tactic cards, ensuring they have everything they need to lead Wakanda into the battles of Marvel: Crisis Protocol.

Con Jobs is a 55-card deck of convention events that can enhance any version of Lords of Vegas or any of its expansion boards. Depending on which kind of casino you focus on during your turn, you can host any event from a Route 66 Rally to a Petroleum Industry Summit — even the office favorite Clown College Reunion!

-description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Asajj Ventress Operative Expansion
description:
A sinister new character awaits in Star Wars: Legion – Asajj Ventress Operative Expansion.

Asajj Ventress is a talented warrior who wields two curved lightsabers in a fighting style known as Jar'Kai with deadly efficiency. This pack adds her to the ranks of the Separatist Alliance along with four new Command Cards that give her the opportunity to unleash her unique skills on the battlefield.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Lysene Sellswords
description:
A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Lysene Sellswords adds new characters to this game, with the Lysene Sellswords giving other factions access to the Pillage mechanism that was previously available only to Greyjoy players. When attacking, they get stronger based on the number of pillage tokens they obtained.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Brazen Beasts
description: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Brazen Beasts add new characters to play.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description: The object of BrainBox: Pictures is to study a card for ten seconds, then answer a question based on the roll of a die. If the question is answered correctly, the card is kept and the person with the most number of cards after 5 or 10 minutes (or the deck is completed) wins.

Madrid Connection is a fast network-/portfolio-building train game with the objective of having the most valuable stock portfolio at the end of the game. Players must start new railroads, they may expand existing railroads and they are able to trade stocks in the railroads.

There are 6 colors of wooden locomotives, each color representing a railroad. Players are dealt random hands of 5-10 (depending on number of players) locomotives, which are essentially a stock portfolio; the rest of each color are placed in a pool. On your turn, you can start a railroad or increase the value of a single railroad by placing 1-5 locomotives from the remaining pool of that company or you can trade one locomotive in your portfolio for one or two locomotives from the remaining pool in a railroad.

The game board is a map of Spain, Portugal, Gibraltar and Andorra, with cities worth anywhere from 1-4 points. Connecting to a city adds to a company's value. The game provides a bonus for crossing national borders. The game holds 3-6 players for about 30 minutes.

The game ends when 5 railroads are devoid of cubes or when a railroad has built into Lisboa. Players' scores are determined by the value of each railroad at the end of the game, multiplied by the number of stocks the player has for that railroad.

Madrid Connection uses the components found in Queen Games’ Paris Connection game. The Madrid Connection game comes with 2 11x17 maps of Spain, Portugal, Gibraltar & Andorra that require trimming and the rules necessary to play Madrid Connection. You must own Paris Connection to play this game. It is possible to play Madrid Connection using the original Winsome SNCF game, but it is not convenient.

Venture into 5-6 player action with even more Patrons, Events, and Quests! Players will uncover new mechanics to help shake up their tavern with the introduction of Bards, Sorcerers, Fighters and Treasures!

This expansion must be used with the standard, Tavern Edition of the game.

Press the magic button in Popstix, and up pops a colorful stick. Place the stick on the table so that the colored ends match other sticks already in place; if you complete a triangle, place your token in the middle. Sticks can also give you wilds, skips, and steals — and ideally you'll be able to place all of your tokens first to win.

It's a who's who of whodunits! To start Guess the Link!, deal out a grid featuring sixteen celebrities and other notable characters. One player secretly comes up with a common bond that links three of them. Everyone else must work together to ask questions, eliminate suspects, and figure out the covert connection before running out of clues!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crimson Skies: Ace Pack #1 – East Meets West
description:
A set of two aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules plus two pilot miniatures for the base game's personal combat rules.  The pack includes one Bloodhawk aircraft miniature (aircraft #9), one E-1C Avenger aircraft miniature (aircraft #10), pilot miniature Charlotte "Charlie" Steele (pilot #1), and pilot miniature Loyle "Show-stopper" Crawford (pilot #2).

Also includes a pack of Navigation Cards.

boardgame: Crimson Skies: Ace Pack #2 – Deadly Duo
description:
A set of two aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules plus two pilot miniatures for the base game's personal combat rules.  The pack includes one P21-J III Devastator aircraft miniature (aircraft #15), one FB-14 Vampire aircraft miniature (aircraft #16), pilot miniature Maria Sanchez (pilot #3), and pilot miniature Nathan Zachary (pilot #4).

Also includes a pack of Navigation Cards.

boardgame: Crimson Skies: Ace Pack #3 – Aces Wild
description:
A set of two aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules plus two pilot miniatures for the base game's personal combat rules.  The pack includes one S2B Kestral aircraft miniature (aircraft #21), one Peacemaker 370 aircraft miniature (aircraft #22), pilot miniature Jonathan "Ghengis" Khan (pilot #5), and pilot miniature Louis "Wild Card" Thibodeaux (pilot #6).

The two aircraft miniatures are painted in a style common to their squadrons.

Also includes a pack of Navigation Cards.

A Malaysian produced Crazy Eights or UNO-style card game which differs from UNO in a few ways.

First the cards come in 5 colours, rather than 4. Second, the wild cards give you the choice of skip or reverse, and there aren’t any draw more cards. Third, the cards each have ‘living skills to be successful’ written on them, which don’t impact gameplay but might inspire you to do better. Finally, increased difficulty levels let you only play mis-suited cards in a certain order, following the Kaizen business management approach.

The game was created by a local consultant who wanted to spread the word about this approach to work and life.

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: War Stories #02 – Colombelles Castle
description:
Castle Of Blood

Behind the metallurgical factories of Colombelles is the castle of the same name. The building, subjected to heavy bombardment, miraculously escaped destruction. Elements of the 16. Luftwaffen Feld Division and the 21. Panzer Division were entrenched inside and slowed down the advance of the 3rd Canadian Infantry Division. The Régiment de la Chaudière, supported by tanks of the 1st Hussars were sent to dislodge the Germans. Almost 80% of the city of Colombelles was destroyed. On November 11, 1948 it was awarded the Croix de Guerre with a silver star.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: War Stories #03 – Juno Beach
description:
The Americans went through hell at Bloody Omaha, but the Canadians also had their beach bathed in blood and fury. Shortly after the offensive began, the infantry was pinned down on the beach under enemy fire.

Heavy support was urgently needed, but the sappers could not open enough gaps in the submerged defenses to land the tanks. The Landing Craft Tanks braved the danger anyway and dropped the tanks on the beach, risking grounding or hitting a mine. The arrival of these tanks changed the course of the battle, and very quickly the situation became unblocked.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
3 to 8 players act in the role of eight characters that represent 8 groups of people in the whole of human history: King, Thief, Minister, Executioner, Sheriff, and Peasant.
Every player has 5 coins in the game, 3 in her hand and 2 in the treasury which is the area in the center of players. The game is to divide the treasury between the players and when the treasury is empty, the winner is the player who has more coins in her hands.

The starting player shuffles the cards and places all 8 cards from the back on the table. Depends on the number of players from 6 to eight cards in the game. First, take these four cards out: "King, Thief, Minister, and Peasant"

Every player can do 4 things on their turn:

     Rotate the cards: by saying "one/two to right" every card goes to the spot in its right. By saying "one/two to left" we do the same to the left. 
     Pay a coin to the treasure and see her card.
     Claim a character and act!
     Change the card with the card in the center (not for 8 players)

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Pulp Stories #01 – Pumpkin's Revenge
description:
Since the United States entered the war, eerie things have happened during Halloween. Several years in a row, out of nowhere, a haunted Stuart tank had appeared. In a blaze of fire and fury, flanked by pumpkin-headed soldiers, it had trundled straight into enemy lines and wreaked havoc on any terror-stricken soldiers it encountered.

This time, German High Command hopes Father Leonhard, military chaplain and exorcist, will be able to send these demons back to the circle of hell they should never have left. He must celebrate a short mass in the sanctified chapel of Saint Gothard, near Cologne.

A Halloween themed mini expansion module.  Needs the base game Heroes of Normandie to play.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Big Red One Edition – Errata
description:
With this Errata pack you will be able to update the content of all V2 products related to the Heroes of Normandie – Big Red One Edition Kickstarter.


     The update sheet clears up a few rule points.



     The punchboard corrects a few counters (7) and brings the American army an M16 MGMC, an M8 Scott, and a Heroic Pin up. The German army also gets a heroic Pin up with this corrective punchboard.



     The corrective sticker sheets will allow you to update your booklets (Core Box BRO rules booklet & scenario booklet + Battlepack#1 Bloody Omaha booklet + Solo mode rules & scenarios booklet). Indeed, with these stickers, there is no need to reprint the latest version of the rules at home. We avoid waste and improve our products so that you get the best gaming experience. Our game books have so many elements, rules, and other things to consider that even with two fantastic proofreading teams, one for each language (3×2 people), a few mistakes still slip through.


—description from the publisher
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Big Red One Edition – More Bacon #2
description:
Who wouldn’t want a little extra bacon again?

This pack contains a new Hero for the Commonwealth, Lieutenant Lindsay Baker affiliated with the 43rd Infantry Division (Worcestshire Regiment), as well as a Piat Customization so that the King’s soldiers don’t find themselves without an answer in front of a tank. Also included is a new Hero for the German Army, Luka Röhr, Feldwebel in the 12. SS Panzer Division. It comes with a Life Point Customization that will allow you to make your characters survive one or two more turns.

1 Commonwealth Intelligence card, which can be added to your deck before a game, if your opponent also adds the one linked to his faction, of course.

And finally, 1 re-closable cloth bag with the effigy of the British Cockade, which will allow you to keep your orders and dice preciously between two fights.

Stretch goal for Heroes of Normandie Battle for Caen.

Samachar is a light-strategy card drafting game that was launched at MeepleCon, India 2023.

You take on the role of a Newspaper Publishing company and play against your friends to be the best Newspaper Company in India.

You draft cards and choose to use the same cards as either News Articles or Employees.

All players play simultaneously. Each player has 2 cards in their hands at the start of the game. They collect two cards from the deck. From the 4 cards, they play 1 card and discard 1 card to a discard pile. They then pass the remaining two cards to the player to their left.

You can choose to publish various types of News Editions: Daily, Movie, Sports, Regional, World or Current.

The game ends when one player publishes 5 News Editions or when 3 News Editions deck run out. Be the first to publish the most number and types of News Editions and win.

Concordia: Roma/Sicilia is an expansion for Concordia that provides a double-sided game board for two different experiences:


     In Roma, you build the eternal city with famous places like the Colosseum, Forum Romanum, Circus Maximus, or Pantheon. Send your colonist ships abroad to gather extra benefits.
     On the island of Sicilia, coincidental eruptions of the volcano Etna may hit you. It's a new element of luck, but Sicilia can be played without that option as well.


—description from the designer

boardgame: The Magical Card Battle of Britain
description:
Deck of 36 cards (78 x 108mm) in a rigid box. Includes an 8-page instructions leaflet.

Fight your rivals with our motley crew of Britain’s most celebrated occultists, witches, scholars of magic and folk horror characters, and channel the power of ritual objects, visions and magical sites.

Crowley VS Murray.
Hutton VS Dee.
Frazerian Neighbours VS Black Magic Bikers.
Stonehenge VS the Wicker Man.

Every round is a clash between the titans of Britain’s history of magic, real or fictional. Who will win this magical card battle?

2 playing modes: 

     Card Battle (plays like a classic Top Trumps game)
     Magic Families (plays like Happy Families)


Conceptualised, developed and written by Maria J. Pérez Cuervo. Art direction and illustrations by Nathaniel Hébert.

Six mind-bending maps for Maglev Metro!

Each double-sided, recessed map has new technologies just waiting to be explored, including new and enhanced actions, stations, and passengers.

New rules and mechanics make playing each map a unique experience, while still feeling very much like Maglev Metro.

—description from the publisher

Say hello to Bōks, a magical device combining the portability of a bag, the rigidity of a cube, and the timeless beauty of a rectangle. It’s emptiness, redesigned.

With a 3,500-card capacity, Bōks fits your whole collection and more (measures 15.5" x 9.5" x 5.75").
Sleek vegan-leather exterior and luxurious microsuede lining.
Includes five one-of-a-kind procedurally-generated cards.
Secrets.

—description from the publisher

The dragons are arranged face down around a volcano. When it's my turn, I uncover a dragon one by one. I have to say beforehand whether this dragon is faster or slower than its predecessor. If I am wrong, my turn is over. If I am right, I can continue until I have three dragons of one color face up. I can take these, which is rewarded with points.

The game using Monopoly mechanics under different design and brand. Sell and buy property to create a monopoly and become the richest millionaire. Additional JackPot mechanics are added to spice it up.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: The Dice Game – Promos
description:
These 17 cards were included as "Milestones" for backers of the Kickstarter.

Corporations:
Lakefront Resorts
Manutech
Polyphemos
Terralabs
Vitor

Project Cards:
Adaptation Technology
Bio Materials
Capital
Cartel
Diversity
Homeworld HQ
Immigrant City
Physics Complex
Thawing

Bonus Cards:
Efficient
Hive City
Scientific Development
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Elegance, class, and a little bit of…What are you doing? Eleanor, I will say it again, second position then pirouette.

Tonight, all the finest dancers are attending the Halcyon Ball to showcase their exquisite skills. You and your rival are both seeking to become the Royal Court Choreographer…Wait, is she doing that move again? Dearest Eleanor, I beg of you. We are here to win over the royal court, not act a fool… Yes, living the fantasy of being the Court Choreographer rests on tonight and your dazzling dancers.

Players play cards from their hand to either add or subtract to their total dance score with the goal of reaching 21-26 points per row. Beware, some dance move will flip the flow!

Your dancing queens have donned their finest attire, prepared their finest dance moves, and are ready to have the time of their…Eleanor! This is our big moment. Prepare your dancers to promenade, twinkle, turn and curtsy to win accolades and your dream job!

—description from the publisher

One player, the robber baron, commands ten black knights, which start at the castle. The other players get a grocer's cart and for guards and start in the corner of their color. Their goal is to get their cart to the opposite corner of the game board, while the black knights try to kill the guards to capture the cart.

The players take turns to move. The robber baron moves last. The knights and guards may move one space in any direction or jump over other knights, guards or carts. The carts may move only on the white and brown spaces in all directions but do not jump. They have to keep contact to at least one guard. The dark green spaces may not be entered by any figure.

Enemy figures are eliminated by jumping over them. The black knights capture a cart if they isolate it from the guards or kill all guards.

The players get coins for saving or capturing a cart. The player with the most coins after an agreed on number of games is the winner.

—user summary

Dead End is a dark-humored, action-packed solo game of thematic, turn-based survival that plops players in the stinky boots of three-armed anti-hero, Bobby Butcher.

Players utilize Action Point, Hand Management, and Hero Defense mechanics as they fight off an onslaught of unique, unrelenting enemies trying to sacrifice them to the Nexus.

Dead End is the core game in A. R. Curry’s Deadverse, and each game in the Deadverse offers bonus content compatible with Dead End. Because of this, Dead End is an expansion-driven game that will continue to grow and evolve along side the Deadverse.

—description from the designer

On your mark...set...loooos! The race of the small rattlesnakes through the desert begins. It's meandering and rattling like crazy! But strange: on one side a snake rattles and on the other not. A card is awarded for each correct guess, increasing players' chances of ending up with the most winning rattlesnakes!

Six colorful snakes in the form of rattlesnake cans go onto the starting space at Skunk Skip. One of the two color dice thrown is chosen by the player and determines which rattlesnake gets to move forward. But before you touch the snake, you guess whether it is rattling or not - then it is shaken horizontally and everyone listens, spellbound! If the tip is correct, you receive a rattlesnake card as a reward. The player then turns the can over and moves it one space further. Any snake that used to rattle will not do so the next time - and vice versa. If you remember well when the fastest rattlesnake reaches the goal, you have the best chance of winning snakes among your cards!
With professional version.

It may sound morbid but you won't believe how some of these famous people in history died! Match the historical figure's life to their death in this darkly comic and factual card game. The player with the most matches at the end is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Embark on a heroic journey to find the adequate medicinal plant to heal your villager. Your ultime goal is to become the new Druide.

In Plantes Enchantées, you will learn how to relieve everyday diseases (colds, sore throats, insect bites...) using medicinal plants. You don't need any previous knowledge to win the game. It's a fun, strategic and educational game accessible from 7 years old.

In the Middle Age, the frontier village of Larock was renowned beyond its borders. Nestled in the heart of the valley and surrounded by mountains, it is the home of the Druidess Eureka. Many villagers rush here in the hope of healing thanks to the advice of the Druidess. Unfortunately, she can't help all the visitors, she is overwhelmed. Your role as an apprentice harvester is to help her relieve the villagers.

The game can be played collaboratively or competitively.  If you play competitively, the winner is the first one to have the right plant card for his villager and to have it validated by the Druidess. Once a player has won, you can stop here or play until each player has healed its villager. In collaboration, the game ends when each player has relieved his own villager. 
May the journey begin!

Hell on Halloween is a 1-vs-all retro horror themed game. One person acts as the killer and tries to murder all players before midnight, while all victims try their best to survive until midnight on Halloween night.

There are six rounds indicated by hours during the night eg. 6-7pm. Each round consists these phases: stalking, trick or treat, location, encounter, combat, regroup.

Stalking is when the killer plans the round, drawing location cards and selecting a hidden area they are stalking. They also lay traps around town.

Victims will go trick-or-treating around the board, rolling for candy to use in the location phase. The killer also rolls for a chance to place razor blades in candy to cause injuries, forcing a victim to visit the hospital.

During the location phase, the killer reveals the traps they have laid causing further damage. The killer shows victims where they have been prowling, causing some to hide. Victims consume the candy they collected to relocate to better areas where they can search for supplies later on.

The encounter phase is when the killer reveals their hidden stalked location card. This phase will play out differently depending on where victims are in relation to the killer, but various things happen such as sabotaging supplies, finding fuel and med kits, and searching locations for weapons and trait cards. Relocating to stay in groups can also occur.

The Combat Phase initiates any time a victim is at the same location as the killer. Weapon cards would have been collected by now at locations and during game set-up. Each weapon card has allocated strike cards red/green. Each player will draw strike cards equal to their weapon cards. All hits are added together and combat is resolved, taking items or inflicting damage.

Regroup is when the board is reset for the next hour of Halloween night and the only time victims can share their items collected during the previous round/hour.

If at least one victim reaches midnight alive, the team wins, and if the killer murders all victims, they will be victorious.

—description from the publisher

A GAME AND A TOY TOGETHER FOR RESTLESS AND CREATIVE CHILDREN

This is an original proposal, ideal for playing at home: play with your Amigurumi doll, bring it to life, make it a part of your adventures through activities and cards with proposals for movements and spaces.

Mi Amigurumi brings two ways to play inside the houses: MOVE and SEARCH.

The first proposal is a game of motor skills, attention and dexterity, where the participants have to use their bodies to move the doll to different parts of the house. Ideal for restless and creative children.

The second combines memory and attention, with an original "treasure hunt" style proposal, where players must look for their hidden friend. Here memory and attention play a fundamental role.

WHAT IS A FRIEND?

This set comes with a knitted doll. His kind are called AMIGURUMI, what a strange name! It sounds that weird because they come from Japan.

They have been made there for many years with a particular technique and some people believe that they are special because by giving them away with good wishes, they become a faithful companion of whoever receives them. Give away a game and a toy together.

MORE THAN A GAME

The AMIGURUMI game is a game that helps us contribute to the cognitive and emotional development of children in a fun way. It seeks to articulate the natural tendency to learn by playing with the possibility of enhancing their abilities.

In the instructions you will also find a small guide and advice for adults; to help (us) to get more involved in the fantasy world, which is created by playing.

A game of visual speed where we are going to get to know the Latin American Empoderadas and their traces of life. Whoever discovers the ones with the most matches first wins.


It is a game from 7 years old where you will get to know women and dissidents from Latin America who made and are making history.

Discover that personal power and the symbols that represent them to go together and united in the fight.

Empoderadas is a project that was born from the inspiration that the struggles of women and dissidents from all over Latin America awaken in us.

Argentina is a vast and diverse country. ¡Llegué Bien! is a game that invites you to go through it mixing traditional amusements: cards, tic-tac-toe, memory, puzzles, clothes and folkloric mischief. It is also a book of couplets written by Mariano Medina on Argentine roads and illustrated by Istvansch, whose pages are loose: they are the cards with which you advance during the game.

General objective: Entertain by enriching knowledge about Argentina, motivating reading and promoting interpersonal communication through play and expression.

HOW IS IT COMPOSED?
It is an easily transportable box (fits in one hand), which contains a sheet-board (map approved by the National Geographic Institute), an instruction sheet, 48 cards to play and 60 cards with proposals for activities and clothing based on the natural and cultural diversity of the country.
The instructions present a basic game rule and then propose instances of complexity and entertainment that include virtual interpersonal communication. Some proposals include oral expression displaying imagination based on real data.

In the game there can be both competitive and collaborative moments, depending freely on the composition of the group of players.

It is suggested for individuals 7 years of age and older, a minimum of 3 players and a maximum of 5.

WHO ARE YOUR RECIPIENTS?
Although it is intended for a family audience, it is also recommended within educational and recreational settings, due to its entertaining information and the skills it favors: Orality, reading, psychomotricity, image recognition, memory development, deductive reasoning, creativity, communication. interpersonal, associative intelligence.

Who wins the game
It is a tour of the country, the player who obtains the most kilometers in three hands or the first to complete a distance set by the group before starting wins. There are six ways to collect kilometers: Arrive safely, assist the traveler, Ru-ta-ti, Recount the trip, send couplets by cell phone, complete puzzles, propose rings. It is established beforehand which of these options is going to be played with.

Vomitland is a board game that has players pushing their luck (and stomachs) as they whirl through roller coaster maneuvers trying to accumulate emotions and experiences without throwing up!

Use every opportunity available at the amusement park to have the most extreme day of your life, and of course, make your friends squirt vomit out of their nostrils!

Vomitland is a push your luck game for 3 to 6 player. Player will choose which place of the theme part to go. When going into the roller coaster, the main attraction of the game, they will open maneuver cards that award emotion symbols used either to complete objectives or make the player vomit. Once all objectives seleceted at the start of the game are completed, the game ends and the player with more victory points is the winner.

Let's play with fire ? 
Guessing games, Agility games and Puzzles

One game is 1-4 players and expandable. Each game more is up to 4 players more.

The game contains multiple games in one. For example, you can win in the different games by guessing the correct number, being the fastest or throwing the best. But most games are guessing games.

Indio: Renaissance is an expansion to the Indio base game. It adds new land, resources, workers, and cards.

The core gameplay of Indio continues as before, but players now have access to wood (from new forest lands) which fuel invention. Innovation cards can be purchased to provide new player powers, resource bonuses, and additional victory points. Merchants roam the land and give gold resources in trade. House cards allow players to start with asymmetric abilities. Honor cards give bonuses to players who accomplish their goals.

—description from designer

They say faith works miracles, but that's not true. The gods work miracles, and faith strengthens their power. However, when the ancient peoples die out, and the gods are worshiped by a smaller and smaller group of worshipers, their power decreases. So they set off - one by one - in search of new acolytes, and their eyes turn to the shores of Lumeria...

Lumeria is a battle card game, and Greeks vs Romans is one of the core sets for the game. Players take on the role of gods known from various mythologies and lead their armies while competing with the enemy on the battlefield. The aim of the game is to score the appropriate number of points for controlling the targets marked on the board. During the game, players move cards representing specific units on a grid of fields, clashing with the enemy army in a dynamic skirmish. The faction decks available in the game represent various civilizations known from the rich mythologies of polytheistic cults. Players will have to manage their units by performing a variety of actions that allow them to maneuver around the battlefield and harass the enemy. The constant economy system not only ensures a balanced gameplay, but also allows you to fix some decisions, making the gameplay more engaging. A wide range of special abilities of units and powerful gods create an amazing combination, allowing you to make bold plays on the battlefield and win glory for your faction!

They say faith works miracles, but that's not true. The gods work miracles, and faith strengthens their power. However, when the ancient peoples die out, and the gods are worshiped by a smaller and smaller group of worshipers, their power decreases. So they set off - one by one - in search of new acolytes, and their eyes turn to the shores of Lumeria...

Lumeria is a battle card game, and Slavs vs Vikings is one of the core sets for the game.

Players take on the role of gods known from various mythologies and lead their armies while competing with the enemy on the battlefield. The aim of the game is to score the appropriate number of points for controlling the targets marked on the board. During the game, players move cards representing specific units on a grid of fields, clashing with the enemy army in a dynamic skirmish. The faction decks available in the game represent various civilizations known from the rich mythologies of polytheistic cults. Players will have to manage their units by performing a variety of actions that allow them to maneuver around the battlefield and harass the enemy. The constant economy system not only ensures a balanced gameplay, but also allows you to fix some decisions, making the gameplay more engaging. A wide range of special abilities of units and powerful gods create an amazing combination, allowing you to make bold plays on the battlefield and win glory for your faction!

A push your luck party game with the goal of being the first player to get rid of all your pennies. Played with a box with 6 penny-sized numbered slots, roll the dice to fill each slot with a penny. Pass to the next player before you roll a number that's already filled with a penny to avoid taking all the pennies in the slots.

Shoot People in Space is a set of rules for science fiction skirmish battles. It is aimed at being dramatic, exciting and occasionally over the top game of battle and space adventure. It is not meant to be a hardcore military game and the results are not meant to be particularly realistic.

-description from rulebook

Battles are fought in phases with alternating activations: In the action phase your figures take turns carrying out one action (moving, firing, doing something else) while in the firefight phase most figures can fire (potentially shooting a second time).

Combat uses a simple 2D6 table with several unexpected factors built in: Firers can fumble or inflict critical hits and the use of a separate Threat die can cause surprise reactions such as the targets diving for cover or firing back. Gun battles plays out quickly and chaotically.

Intended for head-to-head play with 10 scenario objectives to pick from, but offers tools for non player forces and a random unit generator. These are great for solo play or to have a third party force show up mid battle if you like.

With about 8-15 figures per side, SPiS occupies a middle ground between the man-to-man combat of Five Parsecs From Home and the larger battles of Renegade Scout / Clash on the Fringe by the same publisher.

Through their love of tabletop games and boba tea, the founders of Landes Toys and Games LLC bring you BOBA GOBA!

The Goal of the game is simple, earn as much points as you can by collecting Boba cards but be careful because behind every turn of a card may be a hazard.

Game play is as simple as it gets, all the cards are distributed amongst the players and placed face down. Players then take turns flipping over cards that will either earn them points, affect other players or set aside to help themselves.

boardgame: Unreliable Wizard and the Unstable Spirit Towers
description:
The adventure fantasy Unreliable Wizard is a story about becoming a poor magician who has a good chance of becoming a different magic, saving heroes and princesses, and defeating the demon king at the end of the world... 20 stories. A card game for one player that can be enjoyed in minutes.

In this expansion set, you can enjoy adventures different from the basic set with new elements such as new maps, equipment to strengthen yourself, and enemies that change every time.

Stack, steal, and secure your herds in this fast-paced and exciting game. Play individually, or in teams, and work to outwit your opponents on your way to achieving Herdvana and victory. A bit of strategy, a bit of luck, and a whole lot of fun. Great for big groups, or even one-on-one action, each game will be a unique experience!

On a players turn they can take 1 of 7 actions: Create a herd of two animals, add to a herd, draw from the discard to add to a herd or create a herd, place a farmer to protect a herd, play an animal to steal an opponent's herd, play an alien to abduct a farmer, or discard. The game is played until the deck runs out. Herds are scored based on the cards value on the animals, and players score a bonus 10 points if the get a Herdvana (all 8 of an animal type).

Shuffle the cards in with the rest of the Damask cards into the bag. These three cards play as regular Damask cards but with three exceptions.

1. The pattern does not count as one of the patterns for the bonus points from mounting all 4 patterns and is never considered fashionable.
2. The pattern only matches up with other Gamefound promo patterns.
3. Each card only needs 2 background colour cubes to complete instead of 3.

Bandai's new trading card game, in this game, players can use characters from famous Japanese manga or anime such as hunter X hunter, Jujutsukaisen,Code Gease and so on to build their own deck and fight with the opponent.
To play this game，players should use 50 cards include character card,field card and event card to build their own deck and fight with each other, the player who defeat all life cards of the opponent will win the game.

Invisible - Unsichtbar is a solitaire wargame that depicts the struggles of a U-Boot Kommandant and his crew during World War II.

The game takes place through a series of turns where you will be patrolling for merchant ships and destroying as many of them as possible.

SETUP

Choose between the two types of U-Boots available: Type VIIC or IXC. Pick the ID, U-boot nickname and Kommandant name.

Place the U-BOOT markers at the first black dot to the right of the Approach box and the other one on top of the U-BOOT Depth box. Place the Escort marker on top of the Escort box.

Now you are ready to start your patrol.

THE GAME

Step 1: The Approach: Roll 2d6 to find out the events for the day. Check under the Approach box and resolve event.

Step 2: If you roll a SIGHTING, roll 3d6 and total it. Place the 3 dice (they represent 3 ships of a Convoy you Sighted) at that range total. Example: The roll is an 8, place dice on the Medium Rage (7-12)

Step 3: Roll 1d6 for the Weather/Time of Day.

Now you may decide if you want to engage the Convoy. Night and bad weather can really hurt your chances of accomplishing your mission before the Convoy gets away or you get chased off by an Escort...choose your engagements wisely. You are never obligated to engage with a Convoy but don't forget...your main goal is to sink as many merchant ships as possible and make your way back home.

If you do engage with the Convoy, follow the Sequence of Play thereafter until the Convoy gets away or you decide to let them go yourself, or you get sunk by an Escort : )

A Successful patrol is achieved when you make your way back home with 30+ tons of sunken ships.

I hope this is helpful to get the game to the table faster and expedite the beginning of your career as a U-BOOT Kommandant.

One more thing...enjoy the game!

BattleReady Wargames signing off.

ps: please leave a review on the bgg website (link below) so the game can get ranked. ? Thank you

https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/381111/invisible-unsichtbar

Example of play #1

Let's say I have a Sighting. Weather is Day/Good and rolled an 8 for Convoy placement (medium range - bottom dot. ), U-Boot at the Approach. I decide to engage with them.

Round 1

1- I decide to stay surfaced and move up 2 black dots which places me at the top dot in the close range.

2- ASW phase - nothing happens.

3 - Convoy moves up to the top dot in the Medium range.

4 - I decide to use 4 torpedoes. I have a +1 range DRM (I'm at the close range and Convoy is at the Medium range - only 1 range away)

My to hit number now is 4 + 1 DRM for a total of 5 or above. I shoot all 4 for 5,4,2,6. 2 hits, not bad at all. Now I must roll to see if they actually explode on impact. 1s are duds and everything else is an explosion. I roll 2,2 - phew that was close.

Now I roll for tonnage on the bottom of the page. 8 tons total, pretty good score so far.

5 - Damage control - nothing happens. No injuries and no damage to the boat yet.

Round 2

- I stay surfaced and move up 2 dots to the top dot of the Medium range. During Day/good per rules I'm automatically found.

-ASW - Escort moves down the escort track now. He is coming for me.

- Convoy moves up to the Long range dot.

- Since I'm reloading all of my torpedoes this turn I can't fire them yet. I decide to use my deck gun against the lonely ship.

I roll 2d6 to a base hit of 4 + 1 DRM range. (U-boot at Medium range and Convoy at the Long range) for a total of 5 or above to hit. I roll 3 and 4. Not great.

- Damage control phase - nothing happens.

to be continued.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Jabber Jaw is a hilarious alliterative description game. It’s your job to describe or mime out phrases like “the judges juggled,” or “the duck is downstairs” and to get your team to guess as many as they can in 60 seconds. Careful! When describing, you’re not allowed to say the two words that start with the same letter.

GNAM is an educational game for children in primary and secondary school, with the goal to help them develop correct food habits. The game is based on a deck of food/water cards, each of them showing nutrients (carbohydrates, fats, proteins, liquid) and a symbol to specify when the food is usually consumed during the day.
Players draft 6 food cards, trying to collect 2 of them for each of the daily meals (breakfast-lunch-dinner).
A player who collects too few liquids cannot win; the other players check whether they have eaten too many (or too few) nutrients. The most balanced diet wins the game!

—description from the publisher

A new promo pack with eight new Korean Themed clue cards and eight new means cards.

Gat

Agrochemical
Puffer Fish

Homi

Tteok

Finding Fishbone is a solitaire print-and-play, roll-and-write.

It involves roaming, swashbuckling, and treasure-hunting—normal pirate stuff.

Each turn you roll two dice to explore a new hex in the belly of a mighty whale. Some hexes will advance the story, some will award you with pirate treasure, and others will be nasty ambushes! When attacked, you'll compare strenth with the enemy with a simple dice-based combat system. The better your gear, the better your odds to prevail!

Each Clue hex brings you one step closer to finding the captain. Find them all, and the old corsair may yet live! Perish in combat and the adventure is over!

This exercise in adventure and peril requires a sharp pencil, a sharp mind, and 2 six-sided lucky bones.

A clean bottle is optional but strongly recommended.

-description from designer

A 26" square neoprene game mat with stitched edges that comes with two white acrylic disks, a score counter and a bonus rule set that can accommodate 2, 3 or 4 players in 6 additional game modes.

CONTENTS:

     1 single-sided 26" square x 3mm neoprene game mat with stitched edges
     2 white acrylic disks
     1 English/French rule book
     1 bonus cardboard score counter

boardgame: Pathways: Four Extra Disk Set Bundle
description: Four additional game disk sets in unique colors (blue, green, purple & orange) that allow for 4 player variations of the Pathways base game AND Pathways: Playmat alternative game modes. Each set of 12 disks comes with a draw-string cotton bag.

A solo, "DIE" rolling game of mitigating consequences where you play as Bobby Butcher, a three-armed anti-hero trying to speed away in his groovy van as re-animated roadkill attempt to chase him down.

Road Kill(ed) is played on 2 poker-sized cards with 7 dice and 2 penny-sized coins. You roll 3 Action Dice, then decide IF you'll spend your Skill Points to mitigate the dice or not. Choose wisely, because the dice values you end the round with could have dire consequences... 
 You win by reaching the end of the Distance Track and lose if your Fuel reduces to 1, either Tire falls beyond the third notch, or if the enemy die reaches the end of the Distance Track before you.

Road Kill(ed) is part of A. R. Curry's DEADVERSE, which is a series of dark-humored standalone solo games that will, over time, tell an overarching story. Each game in the series will come with bonus content compatible with DEAD END, the core game in the series.

—description from the publisher

New challengers have arrived to squabble over the last star in the known universe!

INFINITY adds 10 new aliens to Last Light and adds components to bring the game up to eight players while keeping the same speed of gameplay!

Welcome to Palermo, the home of “Cosa Nostra.” Here human life can have little value, but this crime shocks everyone. Who murdered the great Don?

This story does not seem to add up, so employ your instincts to solve it. Study the clues and solve the puzzles to find out who the culprit is.

Challenge Level 3/5

Contents:
1 Crime Scene image
1 Story Booklet
Game Guide
64 Cards

A band of robbers has just emptied the vaults of a bank, and now they are attempting a truly audacious escape.

Use all your instincts and wits, close off the city and try to catch them before they disappear.

Challenge Level 4/5

Contents:
1 Story Booklet
1 Map
Game Guide
64 Cards

Can you recall that song line, that ad, that funny or famous quote? Reach into the Corners of your mind to complete the tantalising snippet you’ve been given. From movies to TV, adverts to comedy, music to famous figures from past and present, there are snippets for all tastes and interests!

While readers risk chips on your likelihood of winning, you can go bold and take a 'blind' guess for a double bonus, or take one of the three options available, only one of which is the real answer.

SNIPPETS is the perfect pick-up-and-play game that’s tip of the tongue fun.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Quest for El Dorado: 2023 Promo Pack
description:
This promo pack was part of the Spielbox issue #1 from 2023. It contains a collection of old promos - The Quest for El Dorado: Promo Pack – Letter of Recommendation in its entirety and 2 of the 3 modules from The Quest for El Dorado: Promo Pack – Telegram.

3x Botanist (previously part of the "Letter of Recommendation"-Promo Pack)
3x Elevation Map (previously part of the "Telegram"-Promo Pack)
3x Missionary (previously part of the "Telegram"-Promo Pack)
3x Trail Chart (previously part of the "Letter of Recommendation"-Promo Pack)
4x Letter of Recommendation (previously part of the "Letter of Recommendation"-Promo Pack)

The cards are language independent, rules are in 5 languages (German, English, Spanish, Italian and French).
family: Game: The Quest for El Dorado

description:
Game for young Children where vehicles are hidden behind houses, are assigned to different places and compared to each other. There are three recommended modes to play for children in different ages. 

     discover and describe
     assigning 
     hide and seek


Goal of the latter ones is to find all hidden vehicles behind the houses and assign them to the right location.

Dark Omen is a unique role playing board game. It brings together elements of your favourite games growing up, with game mechanics which the industry has never seen. Dark Omen immerses players in a tangible world with an enthralling story, epic characters, and stunning artwork.

We call it an Adventure/Roll Playing game, but it is much more. Players set forth in a lost Nordic time, and are faced with strategic decision making, resources management, epic battles, and puzzle solving.

It is a 2-4 player cooperative game, explored campaign style through a variety of quests.

What makes Dark Omen special?
Well, there are several mechanics, which are not found in any other game.

The Unique Obstacle System:
The Dark Omen board has several transparent, moveable layers underneath the main (top) board. These layers move independently. Each layers has markings on it which represent the obstacles, or maze-like walls, which dictate how players can move around the board. From the view of the top board, the Obstacles (or walls), move underneath. In essence, this means that the board state changes throughout the game, and no two play throughs are similar.

The Rune Throw system:
At intervals during the game, players reach into a bag of Runes (4 sided tokens), take 5 pieces (of 16 total), and drop them on the board. Since there are 64 total possible sides of Runes, no two Rune Throws are the same.
Players then select a card from the Rune Cards, which correspond to the Runes which they have thrown.
The Rune Cards are a powerful handicap system which effect players in significant ways. These effect movement, battle, resources management, and much more.
The Rune effects stay in play for several Omens, so players need to adjust their strategies accordingly. Sometimes this can be a real pain, sometimes it makes things much easier. 
This is a very dynamic system, and is individual for each Rune Throw, Omen, and play-through.

Action Points:
Players have 6 Action points each per turn, which they can use  to move on the board, for battle, and most other game activities. Players don't need to use all of their AP at once and can choose to alternate spending AP during the same turn.

Morale Points:
In addition, each player has a number of Morale Points, which are tracked on their character board. Morale Points are heavily integrated into battle, overcoming board obstacles, and solving puzzles.

Battle:
Battle in Dark Omen is done by dice rolling, and incorporating  weapon and armor buffs. Battle has two stages: 1) Attack Roll, where players roll their dice against the dice of the enemy. The outcome of the Attack Roll leads to the Damage Roll, where the winning player (or enemy) rolls for damage against the other. Ties normally lead to no Damage Roll, however many game elements lead to advantages for ties, and other battle situations.

Win and Lose conditions:
Dark Omen has 4 chapters, each with 5-6 Omens (Quests). The goal of the game is play through all of these adventures, without losing to the Darkness. Each failed or succeeded Omen affects the Darkness meter. If the players lose too many Omens, then the Darkness vanquished, and the game is lost. By completing Omens, players hold the Darkness at bay, and can even move the meter to the light.

-description from publisher

Travel between Almanac: The Dragon Road and Almanac: The Crystal Peaks and craft a trip that no one has ever taken. The Treacherous Trails pack is a unique deck of encounter cards that will bridge both books of Almanac: The Dragon Road, and The Crystal Peaks so you can play them together!

—description from the publisher

This was originally available through Kickstarter, but was later sold separately.  This expansion includes:

15 Treacherous Encounter cards
12 Contract cards
10 Encounter cards
4 Advanced Starting Caravan cards
1 Trail Guide card

boardgame: Senjutsu: Battle For Japan – Winds of Change
description:
The Winds Of Change Narrative Campaign is a follow-up to the Path Of The Ronin. Featuring equally beautiful art and an immersive story line, guide a young Samurai through the changing landscape of feudal Japan as the European powers begin to influence the destiny of the Japanese people.

The outcomes of your efforts will shape the narrative as it unfolds and change your Samurai's abilities as you play.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: The Battle For Japan (Stone Sword Games)

boardgame: Star Wars Miniatures: Ultimate Missions – Revenge of the Sith
description:
Fight climactic battles against clone troopers, battle droids, Jedi, or Sith. Whether you join the crumbling Republic, the desperate forces of the Separatists, or the bold mercenaries of the Fringe, the future of the galaxy rests in your hands.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Ares Expedition – Orbital Outpost MVM Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter. This promo is also part of the Terraforming Mars: Ares Expedition Promo Cards #2 set with a different artwork as card [P22].

1 card (63x88mm):

P18
Orbital Outpost MVM
Cost: 16 MC
Tags: Building (brown)
Active Phases: I-II
Effects: purple (ongoing) - when you play a card with 1 or fewer tags, you pay 3 MC less for it.

Ever wondered what you'd look like as a Disney villain, or as Olaf? Find out in Drawsome Disney, a hilarious drawing party game from Ridley's Games!

Roll the dice at the beginning of each normal round to determine who everyone draws from their card, like a Mash Up combination of two characters or the movie companion of any character on their card. In the bonus round, cards will have challenges like drawing someone at the table as a specific character. The person with the most points at the end of five rounds wins.

Blither is a simple capture and territory game for two players.

It's an abstract-themed strategy game played on a unique hexagonal grid where each space corresponds to one of the three piece types. Players try to capture groups of their opponent's pieces. The goal is to be the first to have captured at least one of each of the opponent's pieces in each of the three types.

An optional add-on.

4 new tiles for Centurion:

Alaric the Goth
Gaius Julius Caesar
Hannibal Barca
Scipio Africanus

These tiles replace existing the following tiles in Centurion: Scipio Africanus = Funditores; Gaius Julius Caesar = Tribunus; Hannibal Barca = Exploratores; Alaric the Goth = Velites.

There are two ways to incorporate these tiles into the rules (both players should agree which rule they'll use before the start of play).

1. Players randomly determine who will choose first. The first player selects one of the four tiles from this expansion pack. The second players selects the next tile, then the first player selects the next tile and finally the second player selects the last tile. Both players remove the tiles corresponding to the tiles they've chosen (as noted above), place the new tiles in their bags and the game proceeds as normal.

2. For a more challenging game, players lay the four tiles from this expansion pack on the side of the board, then start a standard game. Whenever a players pulls one of the four (base) tiles noted above, they can choose at that moment to replace the drawn tile with the appropriate tile form this expansion pack. If the player decides not to replace the tile, he cannot replace the tile at any future time. Yes, this means a single player could have all four tiles on their side.

WEAKNESS (NEW MOVEMENT ICON)
If an enemy tile enters the indicated square, Gaius Julius Caesar is immediately captured.

boardgame: 5 Surprise Mini Brands!: Supermarket Race Game
description:
Get ready to race around the supermarket! This game uses 2 surprise Mini Brands Collectables as movers around the game board. Be the first to find 3 Mini Brands matching card sets; it won't be that easy though, the game is constantly in shift!

—description from the back of the box

The folding gameboard has six spaces. Players take turns moving one space each turn, then performing the action listed on the space: 'Draw three cards'; 'Steal a random card from opponent's hand'; 'Draw one card then play one card'; etc. The cards are a mix of Brand cards and Action cards. Use the Action cards in combination with the space instructions to block your opponent and increase your hand of Brands, but be careful not to exceed the seven-card hand limit. The object is to be the first to collect three sets of three of the same Brand cards.

—user summary

Kaël is a game of speed and strategy. The main goal will be to get rid of as many cards as possible to get the lowest score in your hands. However, pay attention to the cards of other players when you pronounce Kaël. Certain combinations of cards can be equal to 0 even though a player has 4 cards (example: -1/1/0/0 = 0).

This game is based on a point system. The goal is to be the last player to reach a score higher than 100. To do this, you must obtain the lowest possible score at the end of each round, the score is calculated by adding up the value of the cards in your hands at the end of each round.

At the end of the different rounds, the respective scores of each player will be added up. The first player to exceed 100 points will be eliminated and the game will continue until there is only one player below the 100 point threshold.

Kaël est un jeu de rapidité et de stratégie. Le but principal sera de vous délester d’un maximum de cartes afin d’obtenir le plus petit score entre vos mains. Faites néanmoins attention aux cartes des autres joueurs lorsque vous prononcez Kaël. Certaines combinaisons de cartes peuvent être égales à 0 alors même qu’un joueur dispose de 4 cartes (exemple: -1/1/0/0 = 0).

Le jeu est basé sur un système de points. Le but est d’être le dernier joueur à atteindre  un score supérieur à 100. Pour ce faire, il conviendra d’obtenir le score le plus bas possible à chaque fin de manche, le score se calculant en additionnant la valeur des cartes entre vos mains, à chaque fin de manche.

A l’issue des différentes manches, les scores respectifs de chaque joueur vont se cumuler. Le premier joueur à dépasser 100 points sera éliminé et la partie se poursuivra jusqu’à ce qu’il n’y ait plus qu’un joueur en dessous du seuil des 100 points.

boardgame: ドミノプラントカンパニー (Domino Plant Company)
description: A domino action game where you earn points by defeating plants planted in the forest with a special hammer and delivering them! However, if you knock down too many dominoes, you'll be forced to retire!? Determine your limits and plant plants where other players can't touch!

boardgame: Vindication: Man Vs. Meeple 2022 Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

Pair of themed promos for Vindication based on the Man vs Meeple podcast.

One trait named Acumen that doubles the count towards mastery of cards with 2+ influence at end of game.

One treacherous companion named The Shroud that allows you to destroy curse or treachery cards by transferring one power from yourself to the other card's controller.

boardgame: Cloud Nine: A Game of Wonderful Things
description:
Cloud Nine: A Game of Wonderful Things celebrates the things in life that bring us joy, with illustrations by Lisa Congdon.

Do you love campfires or video games? Wandering around a new city or falling in love? How about the feeling of losing track of time, being out in nature, or listening to music that moves you? We all have special things that make us feel alive and connected to the world, and playing Cloud Nine was made to share them with each other.

In Cloud Nine, players choose from over 300 Cloud Cards to build their personalized collection of nine treasured things, with 50 Action Cards to mix up gameplay.
The Cloud Cards represent a wide assortment of things and experiences, big and small, that bring out a range of emotions from joy and happiness to wonder and amazement, as well as simple pleasures like favorite foods.
Each Cloud Card also includes questions that players can use as conversation prompts to learn more about why we love the things we do.

How to play: To start the game, draw nine random Cloud Cards from the deck and place them face up in a 3x3 grid, known as “the Sky.” The Sky is shared by all players, and must always be filled as cards are taken.
On each turn, players can choose from one of two options:
Take a Cloud Card from the Sky. If you see something you love in the Sky, take the Cloud Card and add it to your own collection. Replace the card you took with a new card from the deck.
Remove a row of Cloud Cards from the Sky. If you don’t love any of the Cloud Cards, you can discard an entire row in any direction, replacing it with three new cards from the deck to choose from. If you still don’t love any of the cards, your turn ends without adding a new card to your collection.
If any card you’ve drawn from the deck is an Action Card, you must use it immediately, following its instructions before ending your turn.
The first player to collect nine Cloud Cards is the winner! Now try spinning all nine cards into a story about how you’d love to spend one beautiful day packed full with things you love. (Bonus: Go out and do it!)

—description from the publisher

"A dice game shaped for tactics"

A follow-up to the 2016 game Kivi (Finnish for "stone"). It's Yahtzee again with a game board instead of score sheets, but this time we have 5 dice instead of 6, the dice have six different symbols in two different colours instead of pips, and the game board grid is 5x5 instead of 7x7.

—user summary

Kivi Shapes is a tactical game of dice where the players score points by building lines of consecutive stones. The dice decide where a stone can be placed: The shapes on the dice should match a combination of shapes on the game board. A minimum of three stones in a vertical, horizontal or diagonal row are required to get points. Should you stake on your own row or block another player's game? When the game board is filled with stones, the game ends and it is time to find out who has the most points.

Kivi Shapes extends the Kivi game series: Easier tally and bigger parts are better suited even for younger players. Award-winning Kivi quality!

Contents: Game board, 5 dice with symbols, 64 stones

—description from the back of the box

"Stay on board!"

A dexterity game where players take turns loading plastic "stuff" onto a swaying cargo ship. Each player has seven containers and two barrels, one normal and one "explosive/radioactive" (twice as heavy), and a spinner determines what needs to be loaded on each player's turn. Containers must be loaded by a loading fork and must always touch previously placed containers from the same player, while barrels can be more freely placed by hand to either balance the ship or to make it trickier for the next player to load their cargo. Matters are further complicated by a pesky pelican who thinks that "Le Barjo" is simply the best perching point in the whole harbour, and always insist on sitting at the highest point of the boat.

Tipping cargo overboard is bad as you get all of your own tipped cargo back, while any other players' pieces are out of the game, making it easier for them; the first one to get rid of all their cargo wins.

Similar to:


     Riff Raff

"The game of stacking chairs - Stack the chairs while PETE goes around"

Similar to the dexterity game Chairs, players again need to get rid of all of their small plastic chairs by stacking them on top of each other in turn... Only this time things are made doubly difficult by the fact that the chairs need to be stacked on Pick-Up Pete, a battery-operated pick-up truck that's slowly driving in circles on the table! While Pete offers many ways to stack and hang the chairs creatively, such as side mirrors, fenders etc. it is also quite tricky to load your chair on it as it trundles past, without tipping the whole stack of chairs already loaded.

First one to get rid of all their chairs wins.

Box has 2-4 players, but many marketing blurbs say 1-4, and this could be easily played as a solo challenge too.

You are a planet.  You became self-aware about twenty minutes ago.  Five minutes ago, you discovered that you have the power to manipulate the environments of your surface.  Three minutes ago, you managed to create life out of the very elements.  Needless to say, these developments shocked and impressed the sentient life forms that had been forming tribes all over you for hundreds of generations.

Then, one minute ago, you felt the distinct sensation that you’ve awakened before.  What were you doing?  It seemed very important at the time.

Two Thousand Years is a Make-As-You-Play campaign of solitaire card games.  Each game represents 100 years of warfare between your tribes.  Each tribe is represented by a card.  You make the cards yourself, and they are modified during and after every game with advancements, corruption, extra staying power, and many other characteristics.

At the end of your campaign, whether you win or lose, you will have generated a history of your peoples (of any type that you imagine) and important landmarks (also pretty much up to you).

It is a game of patience and foresight and planning.  The board will want to clear itself, and you need to stop it by using clever sequences and mystic powers!  Rash decisions may carry brutal, permanent consequences.  You might want to have a space in your home where you can leave a game undisturbed.

—description from the publisher
