description:
In a galaxy far, far away… the Millennium Falcon crew are traveling to Jabba the Hutt’s Palace on the planet Tatooine. Your mission is to save Han Solo from his carbon freeze block. To complete your mission, you will need to move all the cards from the Flight Path to the Landing Pads in ascending order!

In order to win the game and free Han Solo, you'll need to move all of the 52 cards onto the four landing pads in ascending order from King to Ace!

You can achieve this by moving around the cards in your "Flight Path" and placing them onto cards that are one number higher and of a different colour.

When you can no longer move a card from the seven stacks in front of you, you can utilise the draw pile or "Power Core" to assist you!

Once you've progressed from Padawan to Jedi Master, you can add in the "Carbon Freeze Card" for something a little more challenging!

Suitable for 1 player or team.

—description from the publisher

The goal of Blocus is for each player (or team) to delimite as large a territory as possible on the board. The boundaries of this territory are determined by the movement of each player's (or team's) pieces. In fact, each player, while placing each of their pieces on a square, must simultaneously place a counter of their color (white, black, or red) on that square. It is these markers that determine the outlines of the borders of the territory conquered by the player (or team). There is no specific rule for a starting lineup of figures. Each player places his figures where he wants on his first moves and immediately marks the square with a marker in his color.

The figures have different shapes and colors so that each player (or team) can identify their own. However, all have the same value and move in the same way: like the chess queen, they can move horizontally, vertically or diagonally, in any direction, one, two or more squares.

However, under no circumstances may a figure cross a square already covered by a counter, regardless of the color of that counter. Also, a figure cannot land on a square that is already covered by a counter. It also cannot go over a square occupied by another piece and of course it cannot stop there either. A figure that can't move anymore is eliminated from the game.

A game ends when all of a player's (or team's) pieces are eliminated. Whether there are two, three or four players, a game also ends when a total of five figures have been removed from the game. The final score is determined by counting the empty squares within the borders. There is one point for each empty square.

—user summary

Park Racer is a ramming & skidding strategy auto racing game for 1 to 6 players where speeding moonshine cars thunder around the idyllic forest parkland.

Using an unique car movement mechanic where for each matching icon dice the car is driven into a colour space on the track matching the chosen die's colour. Dice are retained by rolling varying amounts of black and white dice according to the car's current gear in the search for matching icons, all single dice are discarded. Carve out the best route to increase gears and gain distance against your rivals.

Includes Auto Cars to race against for both solo and multiplayer races.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Maximum Apocalypse: The Few and Cursed
description:
In a post-apocalyptic world where 90 percent of the planet's water just disappeared, humanity was forced to learn to survive at any cost. Seventy years have passed and the moral codes that maintain society are increasingly folklore - it is now every man for himself and the strongest wins.

Based on the indie comic, The Few and Cursed, this expansion features a brand new Curse Chaser class based on the heroine of the series, the Redhead. With 60 monster cards, a 31 card Curse Chaser deck, 5 promo cards, 8 map tiles, 3 standees, 6 curse tokens, the Few and Cursed expansion introduces you to a brand new apocalyptic world full of dangers. This expansion introduces the Cursed status effect which affects how the cards and map tiles interact with your character. In this alternate Old West, the meek find an edge or they perish. Will you perish?

—description from the publisher

"Time is a tricky thing. You always try and do your best, but there's this thing called the butterfly effect... Yadda Yadda Yadda, I'm stuck in 1998 working with you revolutionaries trying to fix the whole mess. Anyways, I'd appreciate some help once we get away from these soldiers trying to kill us." - The Traveler

The Time Traveler Expansion, designed by Todd  Walsh (Set a Watch) and inspired by his love of Sci-Fi, features a quirky survivor who is great at avoiding combat. With 12 monster cards, 8 time anomalies, 2 standees, 2 map tiles, and a 30 card Time Traveler deck, this expansion introduces you to tons of time hopping hijinks. Whenever the Traveler is played, you introduce their Time Pod, which you can use to teleport around the map. Of course when you rip through space time there are bound to be consequences. You will have to watch out for time anomalies as well...

Maximum Apocalypse: Wasted Wilds is required to play!

—description from the back of the box

An extra card to add to Impression.

The Essen promo card is visibly placed on the table at the start of the game with the I side up.

The players may use the card actions on their turn. When the order cards from age I run out on the table, the card is flipped to the II-IV side for the rest of the game.

Card actions:
The player taking a turn may choose to keep all tradesmen on the board in the same location, and instead of moving them, lie one of them down and perform the promo card action instead.

I side
The player taking a turn lays down one of their tradesmen and takes 2 different resources from the supply. This is their action for the turn, after this, the next player takes a turn. The other non-active players can now choose to pay 1 mark to the active player and take 2 identical resources from the supply. The non-active players may only do this once each, and they pay the mark from their own supply. If they don't have money, they cannot make use of this action.

II-IV side
The player taking a turn lays down one of their tradesmen and chooses one of the following 3 options:
• Perform a free production. They don’t spend capacity or resources for this. One of the order tiles moves one slot to the right, if it can move. If the order is now completed, the player receives the points as a reward, if it contained a signboard, the benefits may now be gained. If the player had the correct factories on the technology board, they may also gain their benefits.
• Make a free movement step on the map, or move to the front on the order track. If the map move is chosen, the move is free, and if he should arrive in a city with this step, one city marker is placed there, and may also gain the city disc, should it be available. No factory may be built and no trade action can be done. If the order track is chosen, and if another player is further ahead, this player can be overtaken, irrespective of what space they are on.
• Carry out a typesetting action. They may take a lead letter from next to their board and move it to their composing stick board. If the letters match, the bonus is gained as usual, the contract slot can be unlocked, if the formation is right.

The Six Sided Summoner is a game in which players take control of summoners who try to save the fairy world from the onslaught of mysterious shadows that appear in the land with the arrival of night and spread terror among the inhabitants of the land. To this end, players roam the world, actively expanding it with the ability to summon, meet NPCs, complete quests for them and defeat monsters.

== Game Loop ==
During your turn, you will perform one of four actions, leading to interactions with the game world. As your adventure unfolds, you will develop your character, allowing you to take on more complex missions, discover Tori gates, and engage in epic battles against the most fearsome demons of the Yumegai world. (Boss fights are the only moments when your character can be eliminated from the game!) To win, you’ll need to earn the most glory points, defeat the boss, or achieve the scenario’s objective.

== Randomness & Luck Mitigation ==

    Adventure Decks: You will draw two cards and choose one. The card backs reveal the type of adventure, helping you plan your strategy.
    Fight System Based on Random Input: Dice rolls determine outcomes, but you will decide how to use the results. Will you prioritize a high-initiative attack, dealing more damage, or evading? Additionally, items in your inventory will allow you to modify these outcomes further.


== Replayability ==
Beyond proven features like a modular map and extensive character-building options (over 20 types of equipment to customize your character), we’ve introduced a time limit that ensures you won’t fully maximize your character in one game, forcing you to make meaningful decisions.

== Player Interactions ==
Combat is not the only form of interaction between players. On the contrary, it offers the smallest benefits and, importantly, does not lead to player elimination. More intriguing ways to hinder your opponents include toll mechanics, stealing items, and applying negative effects like poisoning. In scenarios for 2–4 players, you will experience balanced gameplay with less map congestion. In scenarios for 5–6 players, prepare for intense competition over resources and heightened tension, all while maintaining dynamic gameplay. While the game may include some cooperative elements, if you’re a fan of fully cooperative games, this might not be the right choice for you.

== Scenarios ==
The game offers scenarios with unique victory conditions, many of which introduce new mechanics, providing a variety of experiences. The pinnacle of these is a single-player campaign.

== Game Weight ==
We’ve created a special tutorial that takes just 15 minutes to play, smoothly introducing you to the game’s mechanics. You will find the game easy to learn, even as a new player, while still offering deep strategic complexity. Its weight is comparable to other adventure games, falling within the 2.80–3.00 range (according to the BGG scale).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Cypher Files: An Escape Room… in a Book!
description: The Cypher Files is an utterly unique reading experience. In this brain-bending interactive game, you'll solve puzzles on every page, and obtain keys to move forward by submitting answers online. To solve each puzzle, you'll need to think outside the book. You are an agent of CY.P.H.E.R., the secret international agency working on 'unsolvable' code-based cases. Called upon to investigate cryptic clues discovered in the wake of a series of mysterious disappearances, the clock is ticking to crack the codes before it's too late. To escape this book, you must write, draw, search, fold and cut pages, explore virtual escape rooms, and think laterally to identify the perpetrator and solve the mystery. All you need to play is a pencil, a pair of scissors, an internet connection, and a curious mind.

A serious game for the In-Habit project. Player must match dogs with families able to satisfy their attentions of Needs, Space, Activity. Advantages for the families and the Public Services in the District where they live make things easier. The first player to find 6 families for 6 dogs wins.

Give 5 is a board quiz in which players will have to guess the most popular answers to various interesting and sometimes strange questions. Players place bets, play various bonus cards and receive coins for correct answers to a question. The game is easy to learn, extremely gambling and exciting, perfect for playing with children. It can be played by a large company, divided into teams, you can play alone or one-on-one according to special rules.

Steig ein! is a simple game of dice in which 2 to 4 players try to get their playing pieces from their home bases into buses.

The board depicts a circle of 48 playing fields encircling a wheel with 4 buses. The buses each offer 4 seats, a door field and a door step. In each corner of the board is a home base where the four pieces of each player start.

The players take their turn by rolling the dice and moving any one of their pieces either over the 48 playing fields or into a bus. On the surrounding playing fields, there is no limit to the number of pieces on a field. Inside the buses, only one piece per field is allowed. On the door field and the door step, players can be captured and returned to their base.

If a player rolls a 6, they may not move a piece but has to roll again. The wheel is then turned clockwise the respective number of fields of the second roll. If the roll is a 6 again the wheel is turned 6 fields and the dice is rolled again and the wheel turned again.

The first player to move all four pieces into a seat of any bus wins the game.

—user summary

A trivia game that tests your knowledge of Shakespeare. 400 cards contain quotes from his plays, with one word missing. For one point each, fill in the blank, name the play, and name the character that said it. First person to score 30 points wins.

Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

Add the Shifting Story card to the Wisdom card holder. Place the rest of the cards from this deck as a separate deck in the box - referred to as the Shifting Deck.

Your goal is to look for the displayed objects on the Old Dreams. If you see an objection on the board, you may read the associated card in the Shifting deck. If you find all the objects in the Dreamworld, you will be able to read the story's conclusion.

description:

Shuffle the card into the rest of the level 3 customer cards and proceed as usual.

Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter and later from Game Boy Geek Season 12 (2024).

You may include this card as a bonus ability.

During setup, place this card next to the University Action space. Whenever a player takes the University action, they take this card (from the University or another player) and places it in front of them.

During the Plant Breeding Phase, the player who currently has this card gains a single "3rd shift" pick.

After any 2nd shift picks have been made, they may take any 1 die like normal. Research shift picks will then begin in the normal player order.

Alternately, the two opponents place a marble on a space of a 7x7 grid. As soon as a player has at least four of his own marbles in a consecutive row, he captures one (or more, in the case of longer rows) of his opponent's marble(s), but must take back at least two of his own with which he formed the winning row, thus suffering a positional disadvantage. An oversized marble called Queen may either be used to complete a Real Queen or because it is the last one remaining. If the Marble's Queen is part of a winning position, it allows players to earn an additional marble. If the opponent completes a row of four (Real) with at least one marble in the adjacent to the Queen, he has won immediately. If you have no more marbles in your supply, you can move the marbles on game board anywhere you like, but the Marble's Queen is only allowed to move to an adjacent square.

The rules are the same as regular chess, except for these changes. Every piece has a nominal value attached to them. E.g. The Queen is worth six, a Pawn is worth one. Pieces move like in traditional chess. You can capture opposing pieces, which will then transform them according to their former value minus the value of the opposing piece, into the piece which value it now represents. This applys vice-versa. For example: Queen(6) captures Pawn(1) and will transform into a Rook(5), or Pawn(1) captures Rook(5) which transforms said Rook into a Knight(4). The pieces that go below value one will be put outside the board. You can also capture your own pieces, which adds their values together and promotes the capturing piece accordingly.

To make transformation handier, the status of the set pieces (whether it‘s a Knight or a Bishop) is displayed on each side of a die. The board is a seven by seven squared chess board. To account for the six sides of a die, a new piece has been added, which we of today call Geminus, which acts like a doubled Pawn and can capture one square diagonally in any direction and can move one or two squares forward or one back.

The goal is like in traditional chess, to check mate the King of the opposing side.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Twilight Dance: Bluff & Counter Bluff in Cold War Europe
description:
“TWILIGHT DANCE is a Cold War themed ‘worker placement’ chaining game, in which two adversaries take turns to place and move hidden identity playing pieces on a four by four grid featuring sixteen European capitals. The city of Cold War Berlin is represented by an additional special space in the centre of the board.”

What the two adversaries need to play:

     The Print & Play game board (available in the files section)
     16 wooden playing pieces (tokens, pawns or blocks etc) [8 each of two different colours]
     The 16 Print & Play images that need to be affixed to the underside of the playing pieces [4 Agents, 2 Moles and 2 Decoys each]


Objective of the game and how to play:
The winner of the game is the Spymaster with the most points at the end of the game.  The game ends after all 16 pieces (representing KGB and SIS Agents (or Operations if you prefer) have been played out – i.e., both players’ hands of playing pieces are empty; or, after the conclusion of an agreed number of turns (usually set at 12).

Pieces are played to the board, which is a 4-by-4 grid forming a schematic representation of key European Cold War capitals, with the city of Berlin represented by an additional ‘special’ space in the middle of the board.  The cities represented - in addition to Berlin - are Oslo, Copenhagen, Stockholm, Helsinki, London, Amsterdam, Warsaw, Moscow, Paris Bonn, Prague, Vienna, Lisbon, Madrid, Rome and Sofia.
Points are awarded at the end of a game for each chain of two or three adjacent cities the two players have Agents or Moles in.  Chains can be orthogonal or diagonal.  Chains of two earn 2 points and chains of three 3 points.  Berlin cannot be used to accumulate points but has a special role in the game in that it is the only location on the board in which ‘stacking’ is allowed.  At player discretion however ‘supremacy’ in Berlin may be used to adjudicate ties.

Players can do one of the following five things on their turn:
1 Place one of their pieces in any one of the sixteen cities, provided the space representing that city is empty.
2 Relocate one of their own pieces already on the board to any other city, provided the space representing the new location is empty.
3 Place a piece as 1 above and then “shunt” it and all other pieces one location in any single orthogonal plane. *
4 Place a piece in Berlin or “stack” a piece on an opponent’s piece already there. (Maximum stack: one piece each.) 
5 Use the topmost piece in Berlin, if it is theirs, to shunt a piece or pieces along any one of the four diagonal planes extending from the city. *

[*Note: If a piece is shunted off the board, it enters the other player’s hand, irrespective of allegiance.]

Scoring examples are illustrated in the rules located in the files section.

Notes on restrictions and other essential rules:
In the basic game, players cannot “relocate” their adversary’s pieces.  They can only be moved by ‘shunting’.

Players cannot forego their turn if they possess unplaced pieces.

‘Shunting’ is orthogonal from the 16 cities comprising the 4-by-4 board.  It is diagonal from Berlin.

A piece passed to a player as a result of shunting should not be examined by the shunting player.  The player receiving the piece will be able to look at its hidden side once it is in their hand.

Pawns and stacking
Players preferring to use pawns for playing pieces can simulate stacking by the way the pawns are positioned in the Berlin space on the board – a Top and Bottom within the space is clearly apparent.
     
Variant rules
A number of variant rules can be adopted at set-up by mutual agreement of the players.  These are included with the base game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Offers two games.  A three dimensional four-in-a-row game that you can play against a human opponent or against 3 levels of AI or Lights Out, a game played solely against one of 3 levels of AI in which your goal is to turn off all the lit up spaces.

The four-in-a-row game, known as Smart Four, is similar to other four-in-a-row games, with a couple of differences.  First, the four in a row can happen in any dimensions, so four blocks stacked atop of each other would be a win.  The other is that the electronic board will confirm when a winning move has been played by flashing when a victory has been achieved.  This can be played versus a human player or against 3 levels of AI.

In the other game, Lights Out, played solely against one of three levels of AI, is one in which the goal is to shut all the lit up spots on the board off.  Depending on the difficulty level, some number of spots will be lit up.  When a player presses a spot on the board, all orthogonally adjacent spots will switch from off to on or on to off depending on their original state.  A player wins when all lights are out.

During the game, the player builds a deck of cards that represents a scientific base operating in alien territory.

The game switches between Relief Steps, where you choose new cards to add into your deck, and Combat Steps, where alien waves of increasing difficulty are fought. Both enemy waves and cards eligible to be recruited are randomized (drawn from a previously shuffled deck).

During Combat Steps, your military cards can be played to destroy aliens or to repair your defenses. Then, surviving aliens damage your base back, deactivating your Shields and eventually lowering its Constitution rating.

Your scientific cards grant victory points and useful abilities, but eventually clog up your deck leaving your base vulnerable. Pursuing sinergetic card combinations and keeping the right balance between scientific and military assets is essential.

Aliens cannot be defeated. Game continues until your base is destroyed (by reaching 0 Constitution) and the game is lost, or the player decides to evacuate, cashing out the acquired victory points into a final score rating.

From the moment the evacuation is requested until it actually happens, two more waves of aliens must be fought, so pushing your luck too far might end in failure.

-description by the designer

Aliens have no technological nor numerical advantage. Humanity is unable to stop their slow advance due to the strange dimensional energy that surrounds them, against which our weapons and defenses are unprepared. This Anomaly is an unknown force, that only our dreams and subconscious may perceive.

We're vulnerable to its terror and delirium, but our scientists claim that this weakness comes with the potential for mastering it. Many survivors of the invasion have been elevated to a higher psionic state.

Now we're focused on Astalos, a terraformed quadrant under enemy control, still wrapped in traces of Anomaly. Our plan is to deploy there different operational bases so that our scientific personnel can carry out tests and measurements.

Our presence will attract hostile response. General, your mission is to defend one of those bases to the best of your ability, so you can then withdraw with the maximum amount of data and samples. You'll be deployed and evacuated by a Class IV Frigate, that will be kept hidden in supraorbital location and therefore will only be able to provide limited support.

You'll get the details during the transfer. Dismissed.

-from the rulebook

description:
Simultaneous. Competitive. Short, but rewarding sessions. Asymmetric, but not heavily. Interesting combos. These are the primary tenets of Six Sisters.

Each player will manipulate between 2 and 6 Spell cards during the game. The cards are loaded each turn by up to 3 rolled Ingredients. As such, the decisions are not overwhelming. Often, the obvious choice is a good one. However, under that simple loop is a more complex system of counters and combos.

Six Sisters was explicitly designed with new or casual players in mind while offering significant depth to more experienced players.

—description from the designer

Valour & Fortitude (V&F for short) is a set of rules that you can download from this website and then use to fight tabletop wargames using the Perry Miniatures range of figures. The rules are complemented by a set of army sheets, that contain the information needed to use one of the armies from the Perry Miniatures range in V&F, and scenarios that provide all the information needed to set up a battle.

The rules, army sheets and scenarios are completely free of charge, and are designed to fit onto just half a dozen sides of A4 paper. All you need to do is download them and print them out and you are good to go! In addition a full colour printed version of V&F is available with the October 2022 issue of Wargames Illustrated magazine, and the magazine itself includes further V&F coverage. You can find out more by visiting the Wargames Illustrated website.

Your business as a local dog walker is booming! In Dog Park: New Tricks there are more dogs to walk - including rescues! - more places in the park to explore, and you’ve even hired a specialist trainer to help you expand your enterprise.

With your trainer’s help, all the pups in your kennel have the potential to learn exciting skills, and even old dogs can learn new tricks! With locations opening up in the park too, there is much to explore…

Dog Park: New Tricks adds three major elements to the Dog Park board game: Multi-breed dogs, Trick training, and Super Locations. You are still trying to become the most accomplished walker by earning the most Reputation, but now you have more layers of strategy to explore and use to your advantage.

This expansion also adds a fifth player, which can be played with or without New Tricks.

description:
The skies over the Verrotwood have turned a sickly grey. The hopes of men have withered as fast as the crops. Desperate for salvation the people have begun to turn to the long forgotten and unspeakable things that rest in slumber somewhere deep in the forest.

Pagan cults clash in the name of their gods. They do awful things to gain their favor, in the hope that they can awaken their gods.

Verrotwood is a grimdark fantasy miniatures agnostic skirmish game. The game's core rules are designed to be simple to pick up and play. It uses a ten sided dice pool system that helps to speed up play by removing the need for math at the table.

A simple but versatile cult customization system allows you to pick from a large list of unique rituals to cast upon your enemies. As you play through the competitive campaigns, your cult will gain power and grow stronger with an easy to follow leveling system. In-between games your cult will explore all of the horrifying things that the Verrotwood have to offer in a random event system.

—description from the designer

In Heroes of the Pacific, you take command of the American or Japanese armed forces in a no-holds-barred skirmish. The chosen scenario determines the number of troops you have under your command, as well as your objectives. All you have to do is win!

Fight on a battlefield made up of Terrain Boards containing Terrain Elements that your soldiers must deal with. 
On the field, your forces are represented by Unit counters. Each counter has all the information you need to play the Unit: Combat Value, Defense Value, Movement Value, as well as the Special Abilities the Unit can use.

To help you build and organize your army, Heroes of the Pacific offers a system of Recruitment Tiles and Recruitment Options. These tiles allow you to better track your army and its capabilities.

Each Game Turn, you secretly use your Order Tokens to command some of your Units and determine the order in which they will act.

Each of these Units may perform a Movement Action – to occupy a strategic position or capture an Objective – or attack the enemy by performing a Firing Action. You and your opponent activate Units one after the other, alternating between them. Once all your Order Tokens are activated, the rest of your forces will have the opportunity to maneuver. 
Then you prepare for the next turn.

But Heroes of the Pacific is not just a tactical combat game. It is also a heroic action game. For you will command not only loyal soldiers,
but also brave characters, cunning leaders with talents of their own. 

The whims and eccentricities of the gods of war are represented by Action Cards, which can give you that little edge you're missing, or turn the tide of battle completely with a well-planned strategy.

In the end, the commander who best leads his troops in accomplishing his objectives wins. So read on, soldier, and prepare for the test of war.

—description from the publisher

Anne-Claire is a playable heroine that can be used in any Bullet title. Requires Bullet♥︎ or Bullet★ to play.

Be the last player remaining in this fast-paced fantasy card game that thrives on re-playability, creativity and expanding gameplay. Enjoy this "strategy meets chance" game with large and small groups of people. The main deck is inspired by standard playing cards allowing you to enjoy traditional card games in style!

How to play:

Depending on player count, a specific number of cards are dealt out from the main deck to each player. These cards remain face down and are not looked at. The player to the left of the dealer chooses an opponent to battle. They then choose a "spirit" among their force to flip over. This attacking player then chooses a spirit among their opponent's force to be flipped over. The player with the higher number wins. Both flipped spirits are discarded and
the winner of the battle replaces their spirit with another from the main deck. The loser does not replenish and now has 1 less spirit in their force. This continues until there is only one player left.

Additional play:

Gifts are items(Supplies)and additional spirits(Supports)that are gained in battle. They have varying abilities that can help change the outcome of the game.

A Versatile Card Game:
This is the main way to play, but it is not the only way. Be creative and start up your own version of Spirit Master. Change some rules or add new mechanics altogether. Inside are a few game variations to get players started. The game is also modular and can have additional decks added or removed to extend or change your game. Players could even bring their own full Spirit Master collection and battle it out in a massive clash of spirits. As mentioned before the main spirit deck is inspired by standard playing cards, so go ahead and play some spirit poker. Future expansions will be themed decks and will be interchangeable, adding even further versatility.

Check out https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/spirit-master

Inspirations:
The card game War and Magic the Gathering.

All card art was created By A.J.F.Nicholson in Microsoft Paint.
Stone-Age Font Created by Dave Bastian

—description from the designer

Your goal in Less Chess is to place the opponent's king into check in three different ways at the same time.

To set up, lay out the 3x6 game board, then place each player's king on the designated space on the game board. Shuffle each player's deck of cards, then choose a start player.

On a turn, reveal the top card of your deck, then either take the depicted piece from your reserve and place it orthogonally adjacent to one of your pieces currently in play or move a piece on the game board of the depicted type following the normal movement rules of chess. You can capture any opponent's piece other than the king. If you move a pawn to the opponent's back line (or place a pawn there), you promote it to a piece not currently in play.

If you ever have the opponent's king in check by three pieces at the same time, you win.

Necromensia, a once great world of wonder and fnatasy, has fallen into ruin. Evil Entities have set their sights on a new world: Yours! Gather unlikely allies and stand your ground against an onslaught of bloodthirsty minions and a ravenous evil!

Animus: Nemesis is a 1 to 4 player card game in which the players draft a team of 9 unique characters from among a spanning multiverse to battle the onslaught of evil Nemesis enemies and their Minions.

Play solo or cooperative with up to 4 players.  Requires Animus core box to play.

boardgame: Assassin's Creed: Brotherhood of Venice – Apocalypse
description:
Brotherhood of Venice Apocalypse will immerse Assassins in a new region: the jungles of the Khmer Empire in the 16th century, a new setting never explored before by the Assassin's Creed video game license.

This expansion will rely on environments, enemies, weapons and equipment created from scratch by Triton Noir and will feature new gameplay, such as an open world system between each of the Memories.

—description from the publisher

How drunk will your team get you?

The baseball drinking game designed to keep everyone's attention on the actual live baseball game while still handing out plenty of drinks.

Gather your friends around a live baseball game on TV or in person and bust out this game to deal out drinks with trivia, challenges, group votes, and even secret actions that only you know to watch out for during the live game.

Can you guess which Secret Plays your friends have?

—description from the publisher

KANPUR: El Desierto Florido ("KANPUR: The Flowering Desert") brings up to four players to the vast and wide Atacama Desert, to witness how many species of flowers, within the wonderful landscapes of the driest non-arctic desert on Earth, bloom before and when spring arrives.

Players represent the north winds; they take turns drawing seed tiles, revealing cards that determine where water and sunlight will fall, and then strategically placing and/or moving their tiles depending on the flowers' requirements to bloom. Every flower, even similar species, requires its own conditions to show its colors.

Blooming is the main way to score, as well as Trophy cards that work as boosts for players to gain even more VP. Players use their wooden flower markers to track their score, and once a player fulfills a Trophy mission, that player keeps the card and the VP involved!

Rounds in the game represent one year seasons, changing weather. In addition, the board has different heights in the middle, making gardening a bit harder than expected, but no worries: Nature is amazing, as this phenomenon is seen every year in the "Norte Grande de Chile" (Great North of Chile).

The game has 4 game modes: Free for All, 2 vs 2, Solo, and Cooperative with 3 difficulty levels: Easy, Standard and Hard. Cooperative and Solo modes are challenges to overcome with strategy and skill.

Play as you like and take a view on these beautiful flowers!

—description from the designer

OutlanDish is a party game where players cook up a storm and compete for culinary glory!

The Head Chef chooses a scenario like "Grandma's weird favorite" and a star ingredient like "Pizza". Everybody assembles a dish with their own ingredients that they think will tantalize the palate or tickle a funny bone! The Head Chef mixes up the dishes, reads them out loud, and then everyone simultaneously places their order on the menu of the dish they want to order.

It's a bite-sized competition that will make you laugh out loud, and possibly never think of Ice Cream the same way ever again.

—description from the designer

Dice the Boss utilizes custom dice to bring together a co-operative group of 2-5 adventurers to take on the roles of heros, gain skills, then throw dice at a series of bosses until the party wins or loses.  With exponential combinations of parties and a solid epic 2 player mode this game is highly replayable and utilizes the fun and family friendly dice, art, and themes that Arcanist's Armory has developed.

Every hero has health, speed, defense, and attack stats as well as a few skills that will grow and develop as you defeat the zany bosses such as Quasi-Moto the hunchback biker or the sinister Dead of Knight.  This game is meant to fill the gap between serious campaign crawlers and simple dice games so that everyone of any skill level or strategy apetite can come together to play and enjoy.

—description from the publisher

Theme
Key serene nature music as fuzzy eager Beaver prepares to build its Dam. Then...BOOM! SNIP! SLAP! BOOM! Your beaver worldview now destroyed, you cannot help but be driven to utter madness as you try to quickly build your beaver Dam first, while opponents destroy your loving construction of sticks to make their own. SLAP! your opponents and steal their sticks, or make them dive deep and miss their turn. SNIP! your opponent's attempts to BOOM! your Dam to smithereens, then give them a big ol’ slap! Suffer the same fate as the other eager beavers slap you back and snip your Boom sticks.  Boomy Beaver is simple game of wooden chicanery of extraordinary proportions.

The Goal
Build your Dam using Stick cards to be the first player to get a total of 7 or more Stick Points (3-4 players) or 10 or more Stick Points (2 players) to win or have the most points (the biggest Dam) at end.

Gameplay
On your turn, lay a card to do the following.
STICK Cards are laid to build your Dam.
BOOM! Cards destroy part of a Player's Dam to thwart their progress.
SNIP! Cards counter a BOOM!
SLAP! Cards counter a SNIP! or a BOOM!, take card from another player, or make a player miss a turn.

—description from the designer

The second 6-card expansion for Galdor's Grip.

Using the clever deck construction feature in Galdor's Grip, players can (randomly) mix and match any number of non-essential cards from the Core Set with cards from an expansion to create new card and deck and interactions! This expansion offers new scoring objectives and ways to manipulate the order of the deck.

Galdor's Grip is a solo fantasy card game played completely in your hands. As you move through the deck, you’ll need to carefully manipulate the order and orientation of the cards to gain more knowledge of your surroundings and avoid hazards until Galdor is in position to neutralize Feragot. The base game is needed to play this expansion.

Christmas is just a few hours away and Santa is late.
He decides to hold a competition. The elf that obtains more Christmas Points becomes employee of the month!

Elves: Employee of the Month is a roll and write, print and play, Christmas-themed game about helping Santa to build toys and to fill stockings. Each player rolls dice. Two dice will determine which room on a central board you may enter. Then four dice will determine what items and materials you may pick up there. Your materials: wood, glue and paint can then be combined with the proper dice roll to build toys and earn Christmas points.

—description from the designer

In this game, you will be able to try to blunt the Japanese thrust into China and SE Asia. The campaigns will cover the fighting in Burma (particularly Rangoon), China and Indochina. There will be new pilots and targets, as well as new events and campaigns. The core game is based on the whole of the AVG with each squadron's pilots being identified by the squadron emblem.

There are also new rules governing starting skill levels and abilities of some pilots created. There are also rules for replacing the pilots or rotating the squadron out based on casualties. The event cards are based on actual events that the Tigers experienced in their short sojourn in defending SE Asia.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Siege of Cranes is a turn-based game where players must decide between adding cards to their collection to gain points or discarding cards to perform various actions, most of which affect other players. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins, with large collections of a single type of animal gaining the most points.

Each type of card is based on an animal and its noun of assemblage (e.g. a skulk of foxes).

—description from the designer

boardgame: Stroganov: Tsar's Wish S10 Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.


     Requires 4 wolverine furs to complete (1 fur returned to the bag)
     Scores 1 VP
     Ongoing benefit: when drawing furs from the bag, instead of following the usual procedure (draw two furs randomly, select one, and return the other to the bag), you may now look at the furs in the bag and select your choice.

boardgame: Block and Key: Hindsight Adventurer Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

- Hindsight allows you, once per turn, you may claim 1 completed Key without having contributed a face to it.

Pizza Tosser

-When added to the arena, change up to 2 cubes in another color

boardgame: Pizza: Mangy Cat Customer Promo Card

Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

Mangy Cat
-2 fish and 1 mushroom
-9 points first serving
-6 points otherwise

boardgame: Chronicles of Crime: 2400 – Man vs. Meeple 2022 Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

Includes 4 new characters to use in Chronicles of Crime: 2400 - 46, 47, 48, 49.
family: Series: Chronicles of Crime – The Millennium

boardgame: Mosaic: Man vs. Meeple 2022 Promo Pack
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

5-card expansion promo pack containing 5 Technology Cards not in the base game:

Codex
Garrisons
Paper
Spice Trade
Trireme

All are marked as promo cards with an star in the top right corner.

boardgame: Power Rangers Deck-Building Game: Shattered Grid
description:
Lord Drakkon is on the loose and seeks to destroy the Power Rangers, no matter where they exist in the multiverse! Rangers from across the eras must team up to stop him.

Power Rangers Deck-Building Game: Shattered Grid explores the storyline of the same name involving alternate universes, strange visitors, and time travel. Now is your chance to use all of your Power Rangers sets and expansions in one all-inclusive battle for the ages!

This expansion requires the Power Rangers Deck-Building Game Core Set or Zeo – Stronger Than Before to play.

Features:

     Allows you to use all your Power Rangers Deck-Building Game cards in an epic crossover experience!
     Team up with Rangers from across the eras to defeat Lord Drakkon!
     Take advantage of a temporary Secondary Grid using your previous sets!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: G.I. JOE Deck-Building Game: Raise the Flagg Campaign Expansion
description:
All hands on deck and set sail aboard the U.S.S. Flagg! With Admiral Keel-Haul and Shipwreck at the helm, the G.I. Joe team is ready to take to the seas and keep the world safe. However, the forces of Cobra aren't going to let the Joes have a day in the sun.

The G.I. JOE Deck-Building Game: Raise the Flagg Campaign Expansion introduces a brand-new campaign mode. Over several episodes, the stakes will be raised as your decisions, successes, and failures will compound to create your own narrative for the G.I. Joe team. Keep track of your progress in the campaign log and carefully choose how to equip the U.S.S Flagg as you confront Cobra Commander and his lackeys!

Features:

     Features new campaign mode! Evolves based on your decisions and the outcomes of your missions!
     Giant constructible aircraft carrier that will acquire upgrades as the campaign progresses!
     Secret envelopes and surprises await you!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: The Hunters & The Hunted
description:
When the celebrations and dancing have ended for the night, the citizens of Rio de Janeiro wander home, but in the darkness and afterglow, danger lurks: Coteries of vampires seek their prey, while Hunter cells research and investigate the unknown.

Rivals is an expandable card game for 2-4 players based on the popular roleplaying game set in the World of Darkness. Each player controls a group of vampires or Hunters who seek to dominate their Rivals by attacking their foes, starting Conspiracies, advancing Agendas, and hunting mortals and monsters. Both Hunters and vampires can appear in the same game, so be ready for anything!
  
Vampire: The Masquerade – The Hunters & The Hunted is a new core set that contains everything you need to play right out of the box, including four pre-constructed player decks: 2 Hunter Cells (Faithful & Inquisitive) and 2 Vampire Clans (Ministry & Caitiff). The game includes a crypt pack with additional cards to customize your decks.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Midnight Storm
description:
The wicked ninja master Storm Shadow has arrived. Assisted by his lieutenants, Slice and Dice, as well as an army of vile Red Ninjas, Storm Shadow seeks to destroy G.I. Joe once and for all! Thankfully, the likes of Jinx and Mutt (and Junkyard!) are here to stop him!

The G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Midnight Storm allows you to play as new heroes (Jinx & Mutt/Junkyard) and introduces a new boss, Storm Shadow, as well as two new lieutenants, Slice and Dice, and twelve Red Ninja foot soldiers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Vanguard Strike
description:
Time to kick in the door with Lady Jaye, Flint, and Barbecue! These members of G.I. Joe are experts at jumping feet first into the fray and coming out on top. They'll need every ounce of skill in order to stop Firefly and Aleph from plunging the world into terror.

The G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Vanguard Strike expansion allows you to play as new heroes – Flint, Lady Jaye, and Barbecue — while also featuring three new vehicles, a new lieutenant – Aleph, leader of the Night Creepers — and a new nemesis: Firefly.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Origins: First Builders – District Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

10 new District cards, marked #P01-#P10, to be shuffled into the existing deck of District cards.

After adding these District cards to your copy of the game, this end-game trigger:


    There are 3 or fewer colors of Tower disks remaining in the stock.


... should be played as ...


    There are 3 or fewer colors of Tower disks remaining in the stock AND at least half of the District cards (rounded up) have no Gold above them.


You may adopt this change as a variant rule even if you do not own a copy of these District cards.

boardgame: Corrosion: Man vs. Meeple Engineer Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the Man vs. Meeple Season 4 Kickstarter.

Suit: Gray
Grade: 4
Action: Produce 1 small/medium gear, 1 chrome gear, and 1 special point

Dragon Times is an adventure, fantasy card game suitable for ages 6 and up. The game helps children practice and memorize multiplication facts in a fun and exciting way. It includes 42 dragon cards, 54 spell cards, a spell book, an instruction manual, and a spinner. Children learn about factors, multiples, and division while saving and taming Dragons and restoring peace in a magical land. Choose the correct spells to save the Dragons of Times and set them free with the magic spinner.

This is a standalone expansion for Takaro! that can be played with the original game or on it's own.

Each card features a number of symbols. Each of these symbols will have a direct Māori translation, which players will need to memorise and learn.

Players will flip over two cards competing to find the matching symbols; the first player to call out the correct translation wins the round.

Tākaro can be played by ages 4+ with each round lasting 10-15 minutes. It will rely on the recognition of 31 various symbols, and the correct pronunciation of their Māori names.

—description from the publisher

A wealthy woman has hired you to do the impossible… to find the fountain of youth! She has stumbled upon the journal of the famed conquistador Ponce De Leon and she claims that it holds the route to this treasure. A Spanish fort was constructed deep within the swamps of the Everglades and it is said that the stream trickles through the center of the structure. Unfortunately, getting to the fort will be quite difficult, as the swamp is filled with alligators and venomous snakes. If you do manage to make the discovery, however, a wonder like no other awaits you… One sip of this water can heal any injury and it can make the old young again. A treasure like this is worth killing over! Let’s just hope that it won’t come to that…

About Escape the Crate escape room boxes:
Each box has over a dozen unique puzzles which all fit the theme. Every item in the box has a purpose and will help you escape in time. Clue are hidden everywhere and in this game you need to think inside the box. Each box is a complete story.

These missions can be challenging and it is very possible you won't escape before the time limit. You can always reset the game using our instructions or you can continue playing past the time limit to see the mission to completion.
Every challenge you will face will have a set of hints, which you can choose to reveal when your team needs them. If you are completely stumped, even after the hints, the solution is also provided.

Note: You will need a fully charged computer or mobile device to play this escape room.

The Explorers of Terra Fauna is an upcoming deckbuilding, resource management, and engine building game that takes place on a distant sci-fi world far in the future, where various animal and insect creatures have evolved to inherit a world abandoned by the “Ancient Giants”.

Players curate decks around one or more of the main archetypes - Nature, Technology, Beetles and Lizogs. They will use their Action, Day, and Creature cards to explore and travel between the two main market zones - the Wild and Edge. The Wild is a safer place, with less risk and easier engine building opportunities, but fewer ways to generate or gain victory points. The Edge, on the other hand, is a vicious and dangerous place, but every card is powerful and offers either inherent victory points or ways to generate them.

As the game progresses, the players will find that they have ventured perhaps a little too far, and terrifying Superpredators will enter the Edge deck as future Edge cards enter the market row face-down. Stumbling across a Superpredator will cause every player in the Edge to be ambushed by an incredible, devastating attack. Defeating even a single Superpredator will grant a player great renown - and lots of victory points - so players in a strong position will want to seek them out!

In addition to a player's turn having card plays and cleanup, the game progresses over several 'days', three-round periods where players will build their board states and prepare to fight stronger opposition. When night falls, players will discard their Day cards and Creature cards and begin again. The game takes place during a four-day expedition into the unknown, and follows a set escalation along that time frame. At the end of the fourth day, the player with the most points wins!

—description from the designer

Extend your Dice Splice gameplay with these glistening pearls! This ‘ansom set comes with three pearl dice, a printed dice bag and an extended set o’ rules to bring even more peril to yer game.

description from the publisher

boardgame: Championship Drag Racing: Pro Stock Series
description:
Re-live the excitement of a Pro Stock Season.

Anything that can happen in a real life drag racing event; can and will happen with the same frequency in Championship Drag Racing. Drivers will red-light, have tremendous reaction times, blow up their engines, and even crash with the same frequency as the year represented. And as with any replay game that is worth its salt there are allowances made within the confines of realistic frequency for ANYTHING to truly happen.

In general, your results will be very close to the on-the-track action of the season represented. However, keep in mind, that this game simulates hundreds of variables that can occur in any race. As with any sport, sometimes pure luck can be the deciding factor. That’s no exception with Championship Drag Racing. On the whole, you’ll experience very realistic results. But, within the confines of a single qualifying run or elimination race, anything can truly happen!

Championship Drag Racing: Pro Stock Edition gives you every Car and Driver that made an elimination run in a season. You also get all 23 circuits rated by lane making the lane choice strategy just as important as real life. A full schedule and complete points standings are included.

Can you set a new Elapsed Time Record, or a new Top Speed Record? Order today and find out!

In Flower Market (フラワーマーケット), the player becomes a flower wholesaler, buys flowers from the market, and sells them to the flower shop manager. Make a lot of profit and increase the trust of the flower shop manager. The player with the most trust wins.

There are two ways to earn trust in this game. One is to sell flowers to many flower shops. By doing so, you can earn the trust of the shop owner.

The other is to get a lot of profit. You can earn the trust of the market by earning big profits.

A characteristic of this game is that the player does not have any money, and borrows money to purchase flowers. Grab more trust while thinking about advertising, managing inventory, and more.

プレイヤーは花の卸問屋になり、花を市場から買い入れて花屋の店長さんに売っていきます。
多くの利益をあげ、花屋の店長からの信頼を上げてください。もっとも多くの信頼を集めたプレイヤーの勝利です。

このゲームで信頼を得る方法は2つあります。
１つは多くの花屋さんに花を売ること。そうすることで店主からの信頼を得ることができます。
もう一つは多くの利益を得る事。大きな利益を得ることで、市場から信頼を得ることができます。

このゲームで特徴的なのは、プレイヤーは所持金を持っておらず、借金をして花を仕入れていくことです。宣伝や在庫の管理などを考えながら、より多くの信頼をつかみ取ってください。
publisher's summary
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

boardgame: Championship Drag Racing: Pro Stock Motorcycle
description:
Re-live the excitement of a Pro Stock Motorcycle Season.

Anything that can happen in a real life drag racing event; can and will happen with the same frequency in Championship Drag Racing. Drivers will red-light, have tremendous reaction times, blow up their engines, and even crash with the same frequency as the year represented. And as with any replay game that is worth its salt there are allowances made within the confines of realistic frequency for ANYTHING to truly happen.

In general, your results will be very close to the on-the-track action of the season represented. However, keep in mind, that this game simulates hundreds of variables that can occur in any race. As with any sport, sometimes pure luck can be the deciding factor. That’s no exception with Championship Drag Racing. On the whole, you’ll experience very realistic results. But, within the confines of a single qualifying run or elimination race, anything can truly happen!

Championship Drag Racing: Pro Stock Motorcycle Edition gives you every Bike and Rider that made an elimination run in a season. You also get all 17 circuits rated by lane making the lane choice strategy just as important as real life. A full schedule and complete points standings are included.

Can you set a new Elapsed Time Record, or a new Top Speed Record? Order today and find out!

Game intended to teach some aspects of musical composition.
The object is to collect cards together using four different actions.
Matching: Just matching cards in your hand to those on the table.
Pairing: Taking one card with another card in your hand that forms the interval of a Major or Minor third.
Chording: Taking one card with another card in your hand to build a major or Minor chord.
Sweep: Clearing out all the table cards.
First to 21 wins (which may take several games to do that according to the rules).

—user summary

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: Action Deck
description:
For the Gamer who wants all the advantages of a dedicated Card Deck, but wishes to use just one for all his TSATF battles.

An "Action Deck" is a set of Cards that serves the role previously played by the Standard Card Decks of 800FE and TSATF, determining the order of Movement and Fire, and for TSATF, casualties.

The 2.5" x 3" TSATF Action Decks cue movement and fire order, but also specify casualties, identifying which Leader, "Key," and Other Ranks have been hit. Wound severities ("Serious" and "Light") have been added, but are entirely optional.

Unless stated otherwise, most TSATF Action Decks consist of 63 Cards: 52 for Movement/Fire/Casualties with the full degree of detail required by the rules. There are also 10 Optional Cards, 8 of which provide specific Events equally affecting both sides, and 2 "Halt!" Cards which can end the Movement and/or Fire Phases early. For the more sadistic among you, there are 2 "blanks" for creating your own Events. Finally, there is a Card providing some instruction on use of the Deck.

Using contemporary full color illustrations and terminology specific to the foe facing the British/Imperialist players, each Action Deck not only speeds the steps of play, but heightens the "atmosphere" to make every exciting game even more so!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
Group game with secret roles.
A robbery has just taken place in the town's bank and the thief has managed to escape.
The Sheriff is going to interrogate all the witnesses present to try to draw his portrait.
But it is very likely that the bandit had accomplices, they will certainly lie and give false clues.
The Sheriff will have to rely on the help of the real witnesses to avoid mistakes and will have to be careful with the lies of the accomplices who will know how to defend themselves and accuse the others.

In Witness, you will be either the sheriff, a witness who only tells the truth, or an accomplice who will lie and accuse others.
Witness is a new type of secret role-playing game that will bring you a lot of fun with friends or family.

—description from the publisher

Elleven is a free dice game with upgrades, an eleven-of-a-kind, and no pencils!

THE FAMILIAR:

     Roll dice to create sets.
     You get 2 re-rolls of any number of dice.


THE NEW: 

     Use upgrades to modify rolled dice.
     Complete sets to score points and reclaim used upgrade dice.
     Shoot the moon and go for an eleven-of-a-kind to score big.
     No pencils required to play!


Requires 6 shared dice, plus 5 dice per player.

—description from the designer

A local mining clerk in the swedish town Falun, Gisle Jacobson, published the text, entitled 'Ett litet Tidhfördriff' (A small pastime) in 1613. The booklet describes a ritualized dice game used by the miners at Stora Kopparberg in order to help the miners to make decisions in connection with the organization of this hazardous work.

The reader shall throw three dice, and then look for the same score in the booklet. Three six-sided dice, numbered from one to six, give a total of 56 different combinations if you throw all three simultaneously. Consequently, the text itself consists of 56 rhymed stanzas, combined with schematic pictures of three dice that are linked to all possible combinations.  Every poem gives the miner different rules for living and moral advice as an answer to a wish made before he threw the dice.

Those who manage to score highest may be first in the pair line* and have an advantage over the others, who must wait further down the pair line. But the one coming last will be stuck there because of his low and unlucky throw. This may be due to a three, four and six on the dice. Those who come first are likely to get copper, and those who are last get just rocks.


    Pair line = The term was used to denote the order in which the mining teams took turns to break ore in the mine.


—user summary

A twist on Rummy.

The aim is to arrange your hand into four melds as follows:

     Three or four cards in suit and sequence (straight flush), Ace counting low only.
     Three or four cards in sequence but all of different suits (blank straight)
     Three or four cards of the same rank
     Three or four cards of different suits totalling 21, counting Ace 1 and face cards 10 each.


At each turn you play as follows:

     Take one card from the spread and add it to your hand
     Make one meld (not more), if you can and wish, by placing three or four cards face up before you
     Make one discard face up from your hand to the spread. Your discard must not be the card you just picked up.


Play continues until one player has laid out four melds. The other(s) may then each make one more draw and meld, if possible.

Scoring
For your initial four-card meld you score the face value of its highest card. For each of the three three-card melds you score the face value of its lowest card. Whoever was first to make their initial four-card meld adds a bonus of 10.

This wily band of cats is on an epic quest to face off against the dreaded Doggo of the dungeon! Add them to your next game of Munchkin!

Munchkin Kitten Adventurers includes five brand-new standard Munchkin cards as well as two brand-new Munchkin Dungeon cards.

Kitty-cat caricatures courtesy of Katie Cook!

—description from the publisher

Rocky ‘Roid is an asteroid-mining roll and write in which you individually try to find the best asteroids to mine and then race back to sell the ore you’ve mined in the shared market.

In the first half of the game players choose two of three shared dice to decide where to move and what asteroid they find at that location, optionally using additional fuel to modify the dice for themselves.

Once players have headed home they sell the minerals they have collected one at a time in the order they arrived home, with each sale reducing the price for all other players.

The game comes with multiple maps, each with slightly different rules and more maps will be added over time.

—description from the designer

EKAT is a French board game in self-publishing.

Totally made in France and ecologically responsible.

Dive into a world of strategy and intense battles with this battle royale card game on the island of Ekat where you will have to face the other players to end a conflict that has been going on for years.

Play your cards with cunning and tactics to overcome your opponents and win.

Two nations have been at war for years. Deaths are piling up on both sides... The leaders of the two countries decide to send their best champion for a duel to the death on the island of Ekat.

You play as one of these two champions.

Collect ammunition, weapons, and food to survive this fight and end this endless war.

—description from the designer (translated)

EKAT est un jeu de société français en autoédition.

Fabriqué totalement en France et éco responsable.

Plongez dans un monde de stratégie et de combats intenses avec ce jeu de cartes battle royale sur l'île d'Ekat où vous devrez affronter l'autre joueur pour mettre fin à un conflit qui dure depuis des années.

Jouez vos cartes avec astuce et faites preuve de tactique pour triompher de votre adversaire et remporter la victoire.

Deux nations se déchirent et sont en guerre depuis des années. Les morts s'accumulent de part et d'autre des deux camps... Les dirigeants des deux pays décident d'envoyer chacun leur meilleur champion pour un duel à mort sur l'île d'Ekat.

Vous incarnez un de ces deux champions.

Récoltez des munitions, des armes, des vivres pour survivre à ce combat et mettre fin à cette guerre interminable.

—description from the designer

OLLO is an abstract game that can be played by 2 to 4 players.

Played on a board with 12 marbles glass of each color, the goal is to align 4 marbles of its color.

The rules are very simple, the first player put a marble glass on a corner of the board, then the other players must put their marbles on the board by pushing one or multiple marbles from the outside of the board.

Second and last rule, no marbles can be ejected from the board.

—description from the designer

It’s the annual sand castle building competition again, and this time it's your turn to compete!

In Sand Castles you'll search the tidal pool for sets of decorations to build your castle walls. You can stop any time you like, but watch out — if you push your luck too far, you might drop what you've collected or get caught in a wave! Construct your 3D sand castle by slotting the wall tiles into a wooden base. Prettier walls are worth more points, and most points wins the game.

Wards of Stonefall is a competitive print & play roll-and-write campaign game for 1-6 players that offers a robust narrative and gameplay. All you need is a printer and some 6-sided dice! You can even use the digital print files to play on your smart device. This starts with Chapter 1 of 5 that are currently planned. Each chapter comes with 3 different game maps (1 for each act). Each act is an evolution of the previous game narrative with deeper rules and story points.

Players roll and draft dice to determine what kind of Guardians they can place to defend each of the 6 stones housed in protective sanctums. Remaining dice determine the type of creature (called Whims) that will be attacking. Players then roll another die to determine which sanctum each Whim will attack.

The goals of each act in Chapter 1 are for survival, to protect the stones from destruction. Future chapters may have different but similar style of gameplay following the narrative as the story delves deeper into the mystery behind the onslaught of Whims.

—description from the designer

From the mind of internet funny-guy Jerma985 comes the Grotto Beasts! Trading Card Game!

Players build a deck of cards they will use to battle against an opponent to become a champion. Cards come in four types: Beast, Wish, Grotto and Challenger. Beast cards use their power to attack an opponent's team, while the Grotto cards boost the power or abilities of Beasts. The Wish cards give players extra tricks up their sleeves to pull off special effects. Challengers are used for the special Jerma Rumble variant of the game, letting players team up with the characters of Jerma985's jermaverse.

As players summon cards from their hand, they add cards to their Summoning Pool while their opponent draws cards from their Summoning Pool. The more cards a player draws, the stronger the cards a player may summon.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Stones and Rivers is a thoughtful game of mindful placement. Each player has 14 pieces with two sides, a stone side for blocking and scoring and a river side for sweeping movement and scoring.

Will you flip a piece over and reveal a river for you or your opponent to use? What piece could you push? Where is the best place to place your stone piece? Balance your pieces and be mindful to be the first to place 5 stones in your opponent's score area!

—description from the designer

It is early in the 20th century. You are a member of mafia. There is a series of horse races coming up and Don has an errand for you. He will be glad if a certain horse ends the season at a certain rank and he will appreciate your help in the matter. Other players are Don's other fixers with similar, non-contradictory errands. You will play as a team trying to accomplish as many orders as possible, because if someone screws up, Don will not care who and will be disappointed in all you. Yet he does not allow you to tell your order to others because the old man is not a believer of sharing information.

Code of Silence is a euro style cooperative game with limited communication. It is played with 3-6 players and also includes a solo variant. Regardless of how many players there are around the table, there are always 6 horses and 6 secret orders in the game. In the beginning everyone knows one secret order but some orders may not be known by anyone.

On your turn you will choose a race card from your hand to play. Every card moves multiple horses and makes progress towards one useful action or another. You will need to make hard decisions where all your moves are ambiguous and what benefits a task often harms another task. The game also includes events that introduces further randomness, a betting system and secondary tasks that give some immediate benefits and victory points.

Code of Silence is a funny yet challenging game with medium complexity where strategy, communication, deduction, intuition play equal parts.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Robot Pollinators Promo Card
description:
Spring Season Promo 2023 of FryxGames, which you can buy on its own, or get for free if you order something at their webstore during Spring 2023.

Robot Pollinators is an automated card (green) with no tags
Requires 4% oxygen.

Increase your plant production 1 step. Gain 1 plant for every plant tag you have.

Promo card X45.
This expansion for Ragnarok Star contains the elements for adding a 5th player to the game.

TV and movie fans will love playing The Ultimate Entertainment Game. Suitable for three or more players, it's the laugh out loud TV and movie themed game which will test your drawing, miming, describing, trivia, and humming skills.

Take it in turns to draw, mime, describe, question, or hum the TV or movie title on your card. If someone guesses correctly within the one minute limit, the player that did the challenge gets to keep the card. The first player to collect five cards wins the game!

The Ultimate Entertainment Game is fun for all the family. Included in the box are 50 answer cards, a dice, a pencil, a pad of paper, a sand timer, and full instructions. Recommended for ages eight and over.

—description from the publisher

Twelve intrepid heroes fight to preserve the world of Xhandar. After a tremendous shock, the shards of knowledge have shattered and their pieces have scattered everywhere. To save Xhandar and preserve the knowledge of their clan, the heroes compete to collect the crystal pieces.
Heroes of Xhandar is a trick-taking game for 2-6 players. Use your hero's unique abilities to collect the number of crystals needed to win before your fellow players.
Duel your friends in different game modes, explore powerful synergies, and foil their plans before their eyes. One fierce battle should take about 30 - 45 min.

Enter a world of strategy and competition where every decision counts. With 76 cards and 12 unique hero cards, this game is a complex dance of wits and strategy. Each player draws a hero card with a unique victory condition that must be met to win the game. The game begins with players being dealt seven cards, and each player takes turns placing cards in the trick zone until no player has any cards left in their hand. Points are scored by winning tricks and placing cards with diamonds into the stash zone. The game is won by the player who meets their hero card's victory condition with the largest surplus. The game also features action cards that can change the course of the game in an instant. Will you be able to outmaneuver your opponents and emerge victorious? The only way to find out is to take the challenge.

Game modes:

     Heroes of Xhandar
     Draft
     2v2
     Duel


Playing Heroes of Xhandar in the different game modes adds even more variety and excitement.

—description from the designer

A party game inspired by bad dates!

A collection of 200 terrible things to say on a first date turned into a fun party game! Great for lively game nights, hen nights, pre-drinks, or if you just need something to spark conversation and debate.

Perfect for those, who do not like reading complicated rules, or plotting strategy.

Best played with groups of 3 to 8 people aged 17 and over. Flexible play time. Game box includes instructions for two simple game modes, and more game-play options on their website.

—description from the publisher

Gra Roku is a quick and very fun family game where you create your year consisting of up to 12 cards (you play cards representing the 4 seasons: SPRING, SUMMER, AUTUMN, and WINTER.). You try to have cards that give you the most points in your year.

However, you have to be careful because - as you know - nothing happens twice, so each card of the year must have a different symbol! For every card with a symbol you already have in your year, you'll get a negative point! The person who creates the year with the largest number of points wins.

—description from the publisher

Create your own deck with 8 creature or incantation cards and face off against other Lord-Sorcerers to destroy their Stronghold!

This 36-card standalone expansion from Clash of Decks Season 3 adds Counter-Order and Retreat special abilities.

Counter-Order: When the owner is played, move the leftmost creature or incantation card to the rightmost in its owners hand.

Retreat: During the Invocation phase, you can sacrifice the owner in order to gain half its Mana cost, rounded down.

It's night and you've got lost, but luckily you've seen an old mansion with a hotel sign on your way. Later, in your hotel room, you hear strange noises, a scratching and pawing, a tripping and breathing and then you realize you are not alone.
Suddenly you notice that something is crawling into your bed, from all sides and then they are there, the bed pugs. Where do they all come from?
They need a lot of space in your bed and like your body warmth. It doesn't look like they're leaving voluntarily.
You are looking for a solution to get rid of the small dogs. Maybe they would feel comfortable with another guest?

An entertaining game for 2 to 4 people. You have the task to throw as many of the "Bed Pugs" out of your bed as possible and place them in the beds of the other players. Not all dogs are so easy to convince to leave your bed. Action cards can help you with this. But the clock is ticking and of course you want to start the next day as well rested as possible. But the Pugs also have some tricks up their sleeves to spend as long as possible in your warm bed. The goal of the game is to spend as quiet a night as possible and to wake up rested. For every more or less quiet hour of sleep you get corresponding points. The different ways to get a peaceful sleep are manifold in the game and also depend on the different hotel rooms. Unfortunately, the reception of the hotel is not really reliable...

—description from the publisher

Create your own deck with 8 creature or incantation cards and face off against other Lord-Sorcerers to destroy their Stronghold!

This 36-card standalone expansion from Clash of Decks Season 3 adds Blackmail and Immolation special abilities.

Blackmail: As long as the owner is in game, its owner must play with 1 less card among the 4 leftmost cards in his hand (never less than 1).

Immolation: As long as the owner is in game, each time any player play a card on the same line, they suffer 1 Damage to their Stronghold.

Create your own deck with 8 creature or incantation cards and face off against other Lord-Sorcerers to destroy their Stronghold!

This 36-card standalone expansion from Clash of Decks Season 3 adds Reinforcement and Enchain special abilities.

Reinforcement: When the owner returns to its owners hand, summon the leftmost available creature with Mana cost 4 or less from and without the Reinforcement ability from your Hand.

Enchain: When the Owner ist placed, take the leftmost creature card or incanation from the enemys hand. Place it under the owner. When the owner is destroyed, return the face down card to its owners Hand.

Create your own deck with 8 creature or incantation cards and face off against other Lord-Sorcerers to destroy their Stronghold!

This 36-card standalone expansion from Clash of Decks Season 3 adds Cursed and Backlash special abilities.

Backlash: When the owner is played, deal as much damage to the opponent creature closest to the bridge in the same row as the number of cards on the left side of your Stronghold in your hand.

Cursed: When the owner should return to the rightmost position in its owner's Hand, it returns to the leftmost position instead.

Broblivion is a promo champion for backers, who support the "GOLDblivion"-Kickstarter and the "Clash of Decks Season 3"-Kickstarter.

The card needs a starter kit/ advanced deck to be playable.

Broblivion
Mana Cost: 5
Attack: 3, Defense: 2

The special abilities are:
Berserc
Rage
Splash

boardgame: Clash of Decks: Champion – Micka the Henchman
description:
Micha the Henchman is a promo champion for backers, who support the "Henchmania"-Kickstarter and the "Clash of Decks Season 3"-Kickstarter.

The card needs a starter kit/ advanced deck to be playable.

Micha the Henchman
Mana Cost: 4
Attack: 1, Defense: 4

The special abilities are:
Blackmail 
Defender
Reinforcement
Retreat
Vanish

In Burguerre each player must create the best burger and ruin those of others.

In his turn, a player can add food to anyone's burger! he can give himself a good steak, or put disgusting food on his enemies' burgers (rats, fingers, etc.) The aliments gives or substract point to the burger.

The intrigue of the game is the fact that the foods given cover the old, so you have to memorize what you all receive.

This game also have actions cards and counter cards to make the game more interesting, like swap two burgers !

At the end we count the number of points for each burger, the winner is the one with the most.

Every player starts with 4 face down cards. When the game starts each player look at one of his card.
On your turn, you start by picking a new card from the discard or draw pile. This card can be played then discarded or used to replace one card from your game.
A card is either a number from 0 to 15 or an action (or both).
The goal is to have less point in front of you than other players. When you think it's the case, say 'Valhalla', each player can then play a last time.
Win 3 times to win the game.

—user summary

Le Clash des Vikings !

Dans ce jeu de défausse mêlant mémoire et stratégie, chaque joueur devra se débarrasser de ses cartes pour atteindre le Valhalla. Mais attention à ne pas provoquer le Ragnarok !
Pour aider les joueurs, les chefs Vikings pourront solliciter l’aide des Figures Divines afin d’attaquer les autres clans.

Qui remportera le Bouclier d’Or et atteindra le Valhalla ?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Wyprawa do El Dorado: Mokradła i Smoki
description:
2 expansions in 1 box! Contains expansions Dangers & Muisca and Dragons, Treasures & Mysteries.

New challenges await you during your journey: swamps full of crocodiles, rushing rivers, a local tribe, dangerous dragons and areas covered with thick fog. You will also encounter mysterious temples and treasures that you can collect!
