Both extensions consist of many modules that you can freely combine with each other!

The Treasures module provides new treasure chest tokens that may be spent to move your explorer up to 2 spaces. However, if you collect 4 treasure chest tokens, you win the game!

The Dragons module contains 4 dragon pawns each in different colors corresponding to the cards' colors. Dragons block the route of the expedition and do not allow passage in their vicinity. However, once you play a card whose color matches the dragon, you may move the creature one space, potentially moving it out of your way and blocking your opponents!

The Mysteries module introduces new, smaller terrain tiles with dark mists. They are placed onto normal terrain tiles to add areas of mysterious fog. Once a player stops next to a mystery tile, it's flipped over and the layout underneath becomes visible.

But it is not everything. Various new dangers await you on the terrain tiles.

Dangerous places are places with a skull symbol. They are found on many new tiles. You may move through these spaces, but you may not end your turn on them.

Mountains pass tiles include a narrow pass surrounded by impassable mountain terrain, allowing you to create their own shortcuts or bottlenecks if you wish (they also can contain a skull symbol).

The tiles with Water Vortex feature arrows that push your explorer one space further if you complete a card’s movement on them.

Swamp tiles require you to play any card with a strength equal to the number of crocodile symbols on those tiles. So to enter a tile with one crocodile, you must play a single machete, paddle, or card with one coin. 
 Shrines change the rules of the game a bit. They are placed out along the route to El Dorado, and players must stop at each one and collect all four artifacts before you can enter the gate to El Dorado and win the game.  Rather than be a simple race to the finish line, this forces you to stop at four checkpoints along the way.

Finally, the Muisca tribe is a deck of 8 cards that is placed next to the market board. Two cards are drawn from the top of the pile and displayed next to the market, increasing the card purchase options from 6 to 8. When you draw one card into your hand, you can play it and discard it as usual, or set it aside (max. 2 Muisca cards) and keep it for next turn. Thanks to this, you can use the saved Muisca card exactly when you need it most.

It's high time to start a new adventure full of dangers. Who will reach the gates of El Dorado first?
family: Game: The Quest for El Dorado

description:
A trivia game about beer-related stuff for two or more players or teams.
Each question card has three questions from different categories. On your turn, another player will tell you the three categories. You choose one, answer the question, and if correct, you keep the card. At the end of the game (after 15 minutes), each card is a point. Most points win.

There are nine categories: Film & TV, Food & Drink, Politics & History, Geography, Music, Sports, Culture, Nature & Science, and Beer.

The game suggests that the loser buys a round - and that you drink responsibly.

The game has been made in a collaboration between the brewery Grimbergen and the game studio Bezzerwizzer.

You have delayed cleaning your room for a while... there is junk in all your drawers! Luckily with your eagle eyes you will spot whatever is needed in no time! Flip checklist cards to find out which objects you need to find to complete your task. Take turns with your friends peeking into the drawers of your choice, and call it out when you think you know where each item is. Win pin tokens when everything you need is found in the jumble!

This game utilizes essentially a roll and move mechanism, but some differently for four separate stages of the game. The first stage allows you to collect friend tokens which can be used later for aid.
The second stage is an area where you have to roll 4-6 on the dice or lose your turn.
The third stage has two paths, where the short path has a bigger risk of drawing a card that might send you to jail.
To escape the jail or to find the cheese in stage 4, there is a stone scissor paper mini-game you must win.
The Golden Cheese is hidden in a memory like mini-game, where your friend tokens allow you to look at more tiles.

—user summary

The author Camilla Brincks world of Musse and Helium is now a board game. Enter this fantastic world and help them in the battle against the mean pidgeon hunters. It takes guts, cunning and a little bit of luck with the dice to find the Golden Cheese. A lovely and exciting game that requires the kids attention and focus. Fun for all treasure seekers of all ages!

—description from the back of box (translation)

Carrion Eaters is a solo, dice-rolling game of calculated risks for ages 12+ that's played in 5-15 minutes on one poker-sized card using 7 six-sided dice.

You play as carrion eaters attempting to swoop down and pick off re-animated roadkill as they chase after Bobby Butcher. Be careful. If you stay too low to the ground, they may just get you instead!

—description from the designer

ALP is a game that is creepy enough to be seriously fun where you get to hunt ghosts with friends. And the ghosts get to hunt you back.

Tired of babyish ghost games for scaredy cats, but not about to bust out the ouija, because you know better? Ready for a real ghost themed board game to shiver your timbers while you battle your friends?  This one's real... Real fun.  Up to 6 players can play as skeptics OR believers. Compete to collect evidence cards and survive ghosts effects from each haunted location you dare to investigate. But beware, when you play with ghosts, they play back.  Will you be the Psychic, the TV host, The Camera Guy, The Agent, The Priest or the Skeptic? And will you beat the bumps in the night? Roll the dice, spin the ghost effects planchette. Be the first to prove or debunk, while sabotaging your meddling colleagues. Don't get labeled a quack and hold on to your evidence! The hunt is on.

—description the designer

UL'GorRoth's Revenge is a solo, dice-rolling game of mathematic thinking for ages 12+ that's played in 5-15 minutes on one poker-sized card.

UL'GorRoth has latched on to your vehicle. You'll need to roll three dice and add, subtract, divide and/or multiple any combination of the dice rolled to equal UL'GorRoth's current level. Succeed, and you'll loosen his grasp on you. Succeed enough, and you may escape with your life. However, failure, means you'll be devoured!

—description from the designer

boardgame: 7 Wonders: Societas Pictorium Guild Promo Card
description:
At the end of the game, gain 1 victory point for each different card color in your city.
First distributed at the Festival international des jeux (FIJ) 2023, in Cannes.

Dead End - Deadly Expansion adds more Enemies, more Bystanders, more Decoys, more Hazards, more Items, more Special Items, more Voices, more Actions, more... Well, pretty much more of EVERYTHING that was so awesome about Dead End. This Deadly Expansion even adds new stuff, like Helping Hand cards that allow players to do Epic Wedgies, Demonic Uppercuts, Purple Nurples, and even fear-inspiring Belly Button Pokes!

That's not all though. Now players will be rewarded or punished for their deeds with a Karma-based system. And if players find themselves overwhelmed and outmatched, there's still a chance, albeit small. The personification of Luck may be on their side!

—description from the designer

Kdovíkdo is a party game based on association, clues and speed.

Each round you draw one of the famous personalities and you try to give your opponents a hint using well-chosen clues.

Your goal is to guess and be guessed. But beware, you only have three chances to guess the best clues. Even you must have fast hands.

A battle to build the wildest, craziest story.

A storytelling card game of mashed up storylines where story cards are broken into three categories: Scenario, Character, Action.

Players hold a hand of cards and one player draws a top card from any of the three categories, ie "Character: A ballet dancer with chronic diarrhea", and the other players then build a story from that card with the cards they have in their hand.

The player who drew the top card, selects which story they liked the best, and rewards the winning player with that top card.

Players can either build a story individually or as a group, with the player who's won the most story battles after 10 rounds (received the most reward cards) winning the game!

It's fun, simple to play, and ridiculous stories each round.

Unicorns To The Moon is an action-packed tabletop game for 1-4 players that turns your dreams of being a hotshot venture capitalist (VC) into a reality! Your job as a VC? Invest in ‘Unicorns’ (companies worth a billion or more dollars), and send them to the moon.
In this game you’ll start by defining your strategy. Then, you’ll manage your fund by investing in companies and resources to help those companies become unicorns (worth over $1B). Finally, take your shot at sending unicorns to the moon (worth over $10B), to see who is the best VC! The player with the most money at the end of the game wins!

Path of Conquest is a two-player strategy game with elements of fantasy. 
Each player starts the game with 22 pieces that are placed on a board with 10x10 tiles. Then each player chooses 1 of the 4 different available so called Commanders and places it on their side of the board. Every Commander has unique special abilities that allows each player to exhibit a different play-style.
The game is simple, maneuver through the battlefield and capture the opposing Commander.

—description from the publisher

In Line Dice (ラインダイス), your goal is to reach your finish line first with your six dice.

Each player has their own movement track with six lanes for their six racing dice. The racing dice all start off the board on their 1 symbol, which is also wild. On your first turn or two, you'll roll two six-sided dice, choose a die, flip a racing die to the matching number, then place it in the "start" space at the beginning of the lane; you'll then repeat this process with the other die.

Once you have at least one die in a starting space, on a turn you roll the two six-sided dice, then use each die to either change the face of one of your racing dice or advance a racing die of the matching number. If you create a line of racing dice of the same number, say, two 4s that are adjacent to one another, then you can use a rolled 4 to move both of the racing dice four spaces at the same time. (A 1 counts as all numbers, so it automatically creates a line when adjacent to another racing die.)

If you land one of your dice on a change space in a lane, you can change an opponent's die to the value of your choice.

You must land a racing die in the goal space at the end of the lane by exact count. If you would have extra movement, then you move the racing die backwards in the lane until the full value of the die is used. However, if you land a racing die in the penultimate space in a lane, then when you roll that number again, you can push that racing die forward one space onto the goal.

Get all six of your racing dice onto the goals first, and you win. Alternatively, you can play multiple games, with each player scoring points for the face value of the racing dice in the goals and losing points for dice that didn't make it, with jokers now counting for -10 points.

The Paperboy miniature was available for free to early backers of Scarface 1920, or as an add-on for an addition fee if you weren't an early backer.

The figure replaces the time marker token, but it also comes with three exclusive News cards to add to the game.

     Gang Leader Killed
     Gang Boss gets Eleven Years
     World's Fair Opens

boardgame: 美術大戦〜モナリザVS牛乳を注ぐ女VSサトゥルヌス (Art War: Mona Lisa VS Milk Maid VS Saturnus)
description:
Art War ~ Mona Lisa VS Milkmaid VS Saturnus is a curating card battle where you fight magically remodeled masterpieces to compete for paintings and create the strongest museum.

Each player combines eight decks with the theme of eight different masterpieces, and uses the effects of the cards to attract visitors to the museum. The player who can attract the most people to the museum can acquire the museum, the paintings displayed in it, and add the paintings to their own gallery.

Ebb and Flow is a hilarious two-person strategy game that's perfect for family game nights, date nights, or anytime you want to have a good time! In this fast-paced game, you and your opponent race to reach each other's bases while outsmarting each other with a variety of silly and surprising moves. With easy-to-learn rules and quick gameplay, Ebb and Flow is the perfect game for kids and adults alike. But don't be fooled by its simplicity – Ebb and Flow is packed with strategy and surprises that will keep you coming back for more.

—description from the designer

Waffle Topple is a game suitable for 2-6 players aged 6 and up. Players roll a die and try to add a waffle or topping piece to a stack of waffles without toppling it over. Each piece added earns one point, and bonus points are awarded for having the most waffle pieces in your pile and having one of each topping type (excluding waffle). The game is designed for friends, families, and waffle and brunch lovers, and has an average gameplay time of 10 minutes. The game includes 12 waffle pieces, 24 topping pieces, a balancing fork, a die, and instructions. The game's packaging is eco-friendly.

—description from the publisher

Flora & Fauna is a single-player card game from Ridley's Games that takes place in the Amazon rainforest. The objective is to help the rainforest thrive by arranging plant and animal life into healthy ecosystems within the forest canopy. Players earn points by creating as many Canopy Structures as possible before running out of Species and Food cards. Canopy Structure cards act as blueprints for each ecosystem, showing the order in which Species cards must be collected, corresponding with the four rainforest layers. The game is calming and promotes mindfulness and nature connection, making it suitable for players aged 8 and up. Flora & Fauna includes 96 Species cards, 40 Food cards, 10 Canopy Structure guides, 1 plot marker, 1 reserve plot marker, 1 player piece, and instructions. The game is made using FSC materials and printed using soy inks.

—description from the publisher

Kick It can be played in two ways - in a league or knockout tournament style. Each match consists of six rounds of multiple Attack vs Defense moves from each player, and the player who scores the most goals when the Full Time whistle blows wins the game. In the event of a tie, a Penalty Shootout round will be played to determine the ultimate champion. The game includes 36 player cards, 8 time cards, 4 coach cards, 4 subs cards, 2 penalty round cards, 18 goal tokens, and instructions. Kick It is a perfect game for families, football/soccer fans, and non-sport fans alike and has an average gameplay of 20 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Taco Party is a dice-rolling, push-your-luck game by Ridley's Games for 2-6 players aged 8+. Players roll dice to collect food and drink cards by matching the correct ingredients to the dishes they want to make. The more correct ingredients they roll, the harder it will be for other players to challenge them. However, rolling too many wrong ingredients can cause players to lose their card. Each food card collected earns points, with bonus points for completing a whole meal. The winner is the player with the highest-scoring cards in their pile. Taco Party is suitable for friends, families, and anyone who loves tacos. It comes with 32 food cards, 12 drink cards, 8 ingredient dice, and instructions, and has an average gameplay time of 30 minutes. Printed using FSC paper and with soy inks.

—description from the publisher

FarWater is a marine board game set in the time of the great discoveries in which 2 to 5 players gather riches through trade and piracy, exploring and settling an archipelago of islands they created.

FarWater modular board creation system is designed to create unique basins for movement of ships, as well as islands of any size and shape. This is done by players that add more hexes to the board as the game progresses creating a unique archipelago with each game.

The game starts with each player taking control of a single ship on a single starting hex. They proceed to take turns until all of the hexes are added to the archipelago or one of the players gathers the necessary amount of gold to win.

Each turn consists of adding another hex to the board with possibility to settle a new land and then rolling a custom dice. The possible results of the roll are:

    Set Sail! Each of the players can move, trade or explore using controlled ships. They can also choose to expand their fleets.
    Jolly Roger. Player that rolled the dice can choose to rob ships of his competitors.
    Storm. A tropical disaster sends all ships not protected by ports to the bottom of the sea.


FarWater gameplay and the shape of the archipelago depends on the players imagination and their strategy.  Their collective choices will shape both the trade and travel during their games.

—description from the designer

boardgame: WW84: Wonder Woman – Jeu de Cartes
description: Memory game

Flipa is a fun word game for 1 to 12 people. Plan your way across the board to form the longest word and show off your vocabulary in your new favorite word making game.

Make Words, Flip Letters. Flipa!
This game of words will become the delight of wordsmiths in almost any part of the world. Follow the best path to form the longest words adapting yourself to the changing board. Give free rein to your vocabulary and Flipa with friends, family or at school with its many game modes for up to 12 people

How to Play Flipa?

     Shuffle and place the letters on a table forming a symmetrical structure.
     In turns, we will form words following a path of adjacent tiles. Each letter will give us a point.
     Turn over all the tiles used to form the word. The next person to play will have to form a word with the new board.
     Whoever manages to exceed 20 points will win the game.
     Flipa is also a party. Play in teams and compete in its fun word-making challenges.


—description from the publisher

Yin Yang is a majority control game for 2 people. A pocket abstract with a constant tugof-war for the control of the five elements and the balance of the Three Pures.

Release Your Yin and Control Your Yang to Reach the Equilibrium
An abstract strategy duel in which we will fight to unbalance the balance through the favor of Yin or Yang. Master the elements, anticipate your opponent's moves and expand your energy to its maximum potential. Find the perfect balance and pick your bets wisely to achieve victory in this battle like no other

How to Play Yin Yang?

     Choose yin or yang and place the playing area according to the initial placement rules.
     Each turn we will activate a tile under our control, choosing between its element or its pure (number) and trigger its effect.
     At the end of our turn we can take a tile under our control of an element we control(have the majority) and place it in our betting area. These tiles will give us a bonus score at the end of the game if the majority of its element is still under our control.
     We will play for 6 turns and we will check the final score based on the control of each element and adding the scores of our bets if they were successful.


—description from the publisher

9D6 Quest is a solo dice-management fantasy adventure game. To play you need the game book, 9 six-sided dice, and a pencil. Optional paper miniatures are available for print and play.

You play as a Knight, Bard, Rogue, Dragonslayer, Thief, Scout, Veteran, Witch-Hunter, Chaos Slayer, Cleric, Barbarian, Mage, or Ranger. You travel on one of six different hex maps trying to complete 9 different missions for treasure and glory. Completion of all 9 missions will complete the campaign and allow your hero to retire as a legend.

Each mission plays quickly (10-30 minutes each). You could easily finish a full campaign in one or two sessions.

—description from the publisher

Chapter Pack 1 features ten unique cards designed to augment decks and introduce players to the story of the mysterious disappearance of Natalie Neil.

Chapter Pack 1 Content:

10 deck-building cards, each with a unique puzzle.
10 digital card images that allow you to start solving card puzzles immediately upon purchase.

Each card in this chapter pack features a unique puzzle that will take players on a digital treasure hunt that moves the story forward and unlocks bonus cards for Chapter Pack 2. Additionally, each card is a powerful addition to your deck. Every player who purchases this chapter pack will receive the same ten cards – but these cards will never be printed again after the conclusion of Season 1.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Lunar Twilight Knight
description:
The Twilight Knight celebrates the first new moon of the year with a festive presentation. A painters scale model perfect for hobbyists and a gamers scale model for gamers looking for a fun survivor stand-in for their gaming sessions by new moon light. Includes a new hunt basic hunt event and the chance to gain a festive fish dish.

The Lunar Twilight Knight is packaged in an illustrated box that includes:
1 x Hard Plastic Painters Scale Lunar Twilight Knight
1 x Hard Plastic Gamers Scale Lunar Twilight
1 x Exquisite Aroma hunt event card
1 x Fish of Abundance gear card
1 x Large Matte art print
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 50mm round base and scenic insert
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round base and scenic insert

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Death Crown Inheritor Aya
description:
Toying with the foreboding crown, she casts her mind back to the events that led her to this moment. Her memory is just a pink haze bringing a vague smile to her face but never taking form.

The Death Crown Inheritor Aya is packaged in a box that includes:

1 x Hard plastic Death Crown Inheritor Aya miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
Large matte art print
2 x Heart Bow, Heartstop Arrows pattern gear cards
2 x Heart Bow, Heartstop Arrows patterns cards
1 x Eon Heart Legendary Phoenix AI card
1 x Undying Heart strange resources
1 x Install guide

In Poker for Couples two players (of any gender) attempt to score points both by creating the best poker hand and by winning tricks. For each point scored, they move their pawn one step on the game board track and collect different sexy reward cards on the way.

The game also comes in two different versions depending on if you want to play a quick and simple game, or something a bit more complex and strategic. Both versions are of course included in the same box, and regardless of which version you decide to play, it’ll probably be the most fun you’ll ever have with a deck of cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fast Action Racing: Open Wheel-Stock Car
description:
Fast Action Racing brings you all of the drama and excitement of motor racing to your tabletop. Do not let the name fool you. This is NOT a quick play game. However, it does play quickly. Our unique grid system allows you to play out an entire race in 20 minutes, once you have learned the rules.

Our grid system compresses each race into segments, making game play smooth, effortless and fast. Do not worry though, you will not miss any of the action. While you will not get all the action in minute detail, you will not miss out on lead changes, wrecks, great pit work, and mechanical problems that shape every race so uniquely. The game also lets you witness the challenges made to the race leader as well as the struggle of back markers to avoid being lapped or to rejoin the lead lap. All this action happens in a linear time span from green to checkered flag.

Fast Action Racing captures every important element of a race, produces very realistic results, and plays very quickly. And best of all, it feels like racing. No dice rolling exercises, no gimmicks, no math equations, or unrealistic tallying of numbers to determine a winner. Fast Action Racing is just that, racing, pure and simple.

The Base Game comes with everything needed to play. (Other than 4 six-sided dice for E-Book purchasers)

The Base Game also comes with the Short Track Legends Year Book, which is the perfect set to learn the game. It brings you 36 of the top short track drivers from all facets of the sport. From open wheel legends like AJ Foyt and Tony Stewart to Stock Car Legends like Ned Jarrett and Junior Johnson to Sprint Car Legends and Saturday Night drivers like Sammy Swindell, Steve Kinser and Frank Kimmel. The set also features individual track cards for 20 real-life short tracks.

—description from the publisher

Bring the whole family together for fun and adventure with our family-friendly Zoontalis Battle Royale. Perfect for game nights and family gatherings, Zoontalis provides endless entertainment for players of all ages.

Created by a father, son and daughter team Zoontalis Battle Royale usurps the evil ‘screen time’, replacing it with family time full of adventure, creativity and enjoyment.

Battle with gangs of animal warriors through a variety of Zoontalis scenarios that’ll provide family entertainment for game nights. Easy to follow rules ensure everyone can enjoy the fun whilst providing a highly engaging creative adventure.

Set in a land that pits different factions against each other, the first thing you’ll notice is the awesome figures. They are without a doubt some of the fiercest creatures you’ll have seen in the tabletop gaming world. They are amazing and have awesome powers that compliment each other and make them into a real team.

Based on animal warriors and magicians the quest can be set up easily and there’s multiple scenarios for different types of gameplay.

So much so that the rule book, which is downloadable, comes with a few setups for you to try, but we’re always hearing about how players adapt to what’s happening and we love that.

—description from the publisher

Each Doomlings: Overlush Mystery Expansion Box will come with 4 packs, each containing 15 cards. The exact cards in each pack are a mystery.

All Overlush cards are divided into a new 5-tier rarity system. The first 7 cards you’ll see when you open a pack will be from the Common tier. These are standard traits and ages to expand your Doomlings experience.

The next 5 are Unusual cards. The traits and ages in this tier feature more intricate effects for seasoned Doomlings players.

The 2 cards that precede the final card will be from 1 of 3 tiers: Scarce, the first tier with dominant traits; Endangered, featuring more unique and exciting cards; and Legendary, a tier reserved for the most elusive traits and ages.

The last card in every pack will be a guaranteed holofoil version of any 1 of the 170+ cards in the Overlush collection. It can be a trait or age from any of the aforementioned rarity tiers.

Doomlings: Overlush Mystery Expansion Boxes are designed to work just like any other expansion: simply open your packs, add the cards to your game, and you're ready to play!

—description from the publisher

A game with a fast paced mechanic based on puzzling in which players try to put in his/her turn as many pieces of his/her painting in an area delimited by a frame.

Each player has a different puzzle but one condition is always true: they both has the same cutting pattern. As there is not enough space for all the pieces of inside the frame, on his turn a player try to put as many pieces as he/she can in order to contribute the most to the whole puzzle, which will result a hybrid of both images.

The game ends when the frame is completed. The winner is the player who has put more pieces inside the frame.

Tails of the Sea is a strategy game including a hybrid of area control, pick up and deliver and light economic mechanics. Take the helm as Captain of a dog-pirate ship and command your pack of Muttley Crue to become victorious!

Be the first to build a wealth of bones, through exploration, trade or piracy! Play competitively or in co-op and solo games modes against the menacing Measley Moggy Fleet!

Build your pack of Muttley Crue Meeples at Paw't St. Bernard each time you visit, for the cost of a single bone. Each meeple adds a movement to your ship, or allows you to capture territory on islands (one of three victory conditions). However, each meeple takes up a cargo space, as do resources and other items like cannons.  Managing your cargo capacity and prioritizing which items you carry is crucial for your strategy to succeed. A ship expansion for more cargo capacity may be one option that you will want to consider...but that's gonna cost you! Variable turn order based on your position on the board will give you the opportunity to place yourself to go first or last in the round, depending upon your chosen strategy.  Multiple win conditions make it a dog eat dog competition.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Eclipse: Second Dawn for the Galaxy – Expanded Galaxy
description:
Eclipse: Second Dawn for the Galaxy – Expanded Galaxy includes extra components and rules needed for 7-9 player games. This includes extra Technology Tiles, Ship Part Tiles, Reputation Tiles and 2 Start Player Markers. This expansion also includes an Alliances variant, that can be used with 4+ players.

Please note that you'll also need extra species to add more players to the game. Both Seekers & Outcasts species expansions will allow 2 extra players per expansion to the game. The player count cannot be increased above 9 players.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Eclipse: Second Dawn for the Galaxy – Warped Universe
description:
Eclipse: Second Dawn for the Galaxy – Warped Universe is a Warp Sector variant that can be used to shrink down the playing area. This variant can be used either for more balanced games with 5 players, or for tighter play using 2 Warp Sectors for 2 or 4 players.

This pack contains enough tiles to make up one Warp Sector.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Familien Edition – Kellogs Kartensatz
description:
A German language promo expansion from Kellogs from 1998 for the Familien edition.

It consists of 54 cards (in yellow and blue). Each pack contained 6 cards and a collapsible cube in a bag. The cards are numbered with a K number in the bottom right corner.

Soccer fever has gripped the island of Catan. You and your fellow Catanians are swept up in supporting your hometown teams. Will they win the most matches and finish the season with the championship cup?

CATAN: Soccer Fever is a lighthearted and simple scenario for the CATAN base game that's perfect for soccer and football fans everywhere. Every time you build a settlement or city, it's match day on the island of Catan. Winning soccer matches provides you with benefits, and the team that leads the league standings gets extra victory points.

Holding a soccer match in CATAN: Soccer Fever is ridiculously fun yet simple! Assemble the packaging folio into a soccer pitch and flick your soccer ball at the goalie! If it lands color side up, it's a GOOOOAAAAL! Score the most goals to win the match.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Imperial Eagles: Air War in the Pacific 1941-43
description:
Imperial Eagles returns GMT’s Down in Flames World War II air combat card game system to the Pacific. Volume VI in the series is a stand-alone game in which you can recreate many of the air battles of the first two years of the war between the Japanese and the Western Allies. 65 different aircraft models (19 never before represented in DiF) are depicted with full-color cards, all rated according to the latest research.
 As in Wild Blue Yonder, each side uses its own functionally-identical Action card deck to complete Missions lasting multiple turns. Fighters are normally played as Elements of two aircraft. The Leader of each Element has its own hand of cards, played both offensively during your turn and defensively when attacked by an enemy. The second aircraft is a Wingman, supporting the Leader in its attacks and defending the Leader when the Element is engaged. Unlike Leaders, Wingmen draw and play a few cards only when needed for attacking or defending—you’re never sure how effective they will be.

The simplest games are Dogfights in which two to eight players each control one or two fighter Elements in fast-paced air-to-air action. Besides classic matchups such as F4F-4 Wildcats vs. A6M2 Zeroes or P-40E Warhawks vs. Ki-43-I Hayabusas, in Imperial Eagles you can explore the capabilities of lesser-known types including the Seversky P-35A, the Curtiss-Wright CW-21B, the Nakajima Ki-27, and the Mitsubishi F1M2. Mid-war aircraft such as the P-38F Lightning, the F4U-1 Corsair, the F6F-3 Hellcat, and the Ki-61-Ib Hien also return to the series.
 Campaigns offer more depth as you play three or more historically-based missions in which escorted bombers face interceptors and anti-aircraft defenses in trying to destroy enemy targets such as ground forces, airfields, and ships. Dive bombers such as the SBD-3 Dauntless (and its USAAF version the A-24 Banshee) and the D3A1 “Val,” medium bombers like the B-25C-5 Mitchell and the G4M1 “Betty,” and heavy bombers such as the B-17D Flying Fortress and the B-24D Liberator feature prominently in these missions. Even reconnaissance aircraft such as the F-5A Lightning, the PBY-5A Catalina, and the large but fragile H6K4 “Mavis” are given their due. Five previously-published land campaigns (including the small “Marshall Islands Raid” which serves as an abbreviated introduction to campaign mission mechanics) have all been updated to current standards as in WBY.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You caught our attention. You are special.

We’re looking for people who see the world in a slightly different way. People who notice things others don’t. And we think that might be you. We just need to, well, get to know you a little bit better. See where your strengths lie. You could be a great asset to us. And we could change your life.
The Department of Distractions, a clandestine organisation that claims its job is to plant stories in the world “to make life more interesting”, is on the search for new recruits… and they think YOU might be just what they need.
The Distraction Agents is a game, a challenge, a puzzle and a story in a box.
Receive your package through the post, your instructions via email and online films, and let the Agents guide you through the challenges. Tackle the tasks and reveal the code to unlock your prize!

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 90 wins the game.

—user summary

This is a rummy variation for 2 players.

The object of the game is to make four runs of three consecutive cards, one in each suit, with bonuses for matching sets made of the same numbers.

A player may take either the upturned card or the top card of the stock as in standard Rummy. If a player wishes to lay down a set, he must do so before discarding.

Aces are wild within their own suit - i.e. Ace of Spades can be any Spade, Ace of Hearts can be any Heart, etc.

Once one player has laid down all their cards, both players work out their scores as follows:

For each set laid down score 20 points.
For each matching set laid down score an additional 50 points - e.g. 3,4,5 of Hearts matches 3,4,5 of Spades.

The first player who lays down all their cards also scores points for all the cards left in their opponent’s hand. Both players score points for all sets laid down, regardless of who lays down first. In fact it is possible for the player going out first to score less than their opponent if the other player has matching sets.

First to 1000 points wins.

Food Truck Face Off, a tasty treat of a game that is fun to play with the whole family, and strategic enough to play with your gaming group. Race to build your business as one of four specialty food truck owners.

After a unique auction to determine the variable play order, you’ll have some tough choices to make. Do you have your employee race to the market to buy your ingredients, or do you grab the location with the most customers? Perhaps you’ll ask Granny for a new recipe to increase your variety of foods to sell to those customers.  Or maybe your time is best spent getting some free ingredients for that day. Is your strategy to steal a recipe from a rival food truck or to padlock your own recipe so that it can never be stolen?

Possibly you should upgrade your truck to be more profitable by planting a garden.  Or maybe you upgrade yourself by taking some cooking classes so that your food is tasty enough that you can charge more for it.  You'll want to do everything, but that's impossible, even if you hire that new employee to help out.  So which paths fit best with your overall strategy?  Do you have what it takes to maximize profits in this supply and demand economics game?

—description from the publisher

Promo card with stats adding the new player character Gizmo to the game.
Gizmo is the kitty of Magpie Games' director of community relations, Kate, and was the Gencon kitten for 2020!

Gizmo's advanced side has:

     SLING: Send one of your cards to another ritual circle
     SWAT: Discard two of your cards
     SWITCH: Swap the location of any two cards
     SWAG: Choose another kitty; they must either give you a card from their circle (and you place it anywhere in yours), or let you draw two cards from the deck

This Wizard Kittens Card Game bonus content includes:

1 Exclusive Grey Spell Token

7 Exclusive Character Cat Playmats, featuring: Cinder, Bandit, Orion, Whiskitos, Daisy, Marquise de Cat, and Ser Isaac McFluffykins

2 Exclusive New Rules Cards

4 Exclusive Monsters

2 Exclusive Curses

This pack is full of exclusive Kickstarter content, including new kitties chosen by backers and an exclusive Cinder character mat and additional spell token.

—description from the publisher

Blank ME is a monologue-reading party game with a simple rule set, fast rounds and big laughs.

Using a short prompt, three players contribute responses to a hidden monologue that is then read aloud or (ideally) performed with gusto by an unsuspecting fourth. Responses get their meaning from the monologue so things get hilarious and mature, making this game 17+.

There are two official ways to play included in the game box and on the website: the 'Blank Me' party mode where players take turns contributing hidden responses to the monologues that pass around the table, and the 'Blank Yourself' crafting mode where players keep a monologue in front of them and draft their 3 responses from the hands of response cards that are passed between players.

Blank Me was successfully crowdfunded through Kickstarter in 2023!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Over a number of rounds, players compete to score the most points by having their parasols as close to sea as possible and in clusters of adjacent parasols.

Players draft domino shape cards and place them on a communal play area so they can score more points and block other players.

A solo roguelite in a mint tin, with dice driven combat.

A hero must fight through a forest dominated by monsters, servants of a powerful demon, so they may fight and defeat the demon itself.

The forest comprises several levels of cards which are flipped to reveal monsters (or occasional pickup bonuses or traps) to fight. As the player progresses they can upgrade their hero's attributes with each victory, providing greater power or defense needed to tackle the stronger monsters deeper in the forest.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A low complex version of roll and write game "Crazy Canteen". 
Mechanics are quite similar, but level of decision is reduced.

Card game in which players link the cards in their hand to form an item, title, person, and so on.
Examples: A letter and a piece of chain = Chainmail
A Crossbow and an Apple = William Tell
A Ring, a light Bulb and a Bath = Ring of Bright Water
Having laid down a 'Link', the other players vote if they think the link is acceptable. Once accepted scores are awarded:
2 Card Link = 2 Points
3 Card Link = 10 Points
4 Card Link = 25 Points
5 Card Link = 50 Points
6 card Link = 100 Points
The player with the most points at the end of the game, wins.

WHO DOESN'T HAVE A DRAWER LIKE THIS IN THEIR HOUSE?
There are drawers to store cutlery.
There are drawers to store underwear.
And there are drawers to store all the rest….

Always, in each house, there is a space where they will keep the most crazy, disparate and random things.
True boxes of surprises and wonders. What was this or that for? We almost no longer recognize the things that are there, but they remain there as if they were telling a story.

THIS GAME IS THE ZEUS LEVEL MEMORY CHALLENGE!

The game consists of observing the drawer for a while and writing as many objects and the main colors as they can remember.

Do you think it's easy?

TO CHALLENGE SOLO OR IN GROUPS!

Ideal for a quick tabletop challenge.
But also to challenge yourself and exercise your memory.

The cards represent different ingredients from the famous Rougaille Dodo recipe (onions, garlic, etc.). A player places a card in front of him, face down, and announces an ingredient to the opponent of his choice. He will then have to decide to accept or refuse (Manze pa manze?) the card. If the player refuses (He must say: “pa Manze”), the person who presented the card takes it back and places it face up in front of him (in his collection); but if he accepts it (he must say “Manze”), he is the one who takes it into his collection! It is also possible to pass the dilemma to another player.

Trick-or-Treat across the neighborhood with dice and unique Hustle Cards to collect a massive candy stash and complete Challenges Cards. Keep creativity alive by equipping costume upgrades that make your costume even more awesome (a robot with a magical staff? Sign me up!). But beware, even veteran Trick-or-Treaters’ skills are tested since the competition will surely use devious tricks to secure their spot on top. Yet, there is no time to delay. The night is short, and only the Trick-or-Treater with the most points will stand on top as the king or queen of Halloween.

An Immersive murder mystery game solved in 10-15 hours.  In each case, you will work directly with local Detectives as they take you on a serial killer's journey with multiple crimes, deaths, and injuries. All seasons are between 1 to 5 episodes long. Each episode is packed with evidence, clues, puzzles, and more. Choose this experience if you are looking for a fully immersive murder mystery that can be played over time. It can be played in a group or alone.

A party game where the judge reveals a question card, and the other players submit answers from their own hand of answer cards. The judge awards the question card to his preferred answer. The judge rotates around the table. The game ends when a player collects three question cards, or after a predetermined number of rounds.

Players act as health inspectors or diner employees trying to be the first to find health code violations. This is accomplished through movement of the players (or the irate chef) and cardplay. Cards allow various actions, including peeking under tables and removing violations. The more of a type of card that is played, the stronger the action, but once a type of card is maxed out, ALL cards go back to the beginning.

The winner is the first to remove a set number of violations.

Players use a collection of unrelated items contributed by other players, and weave them into a story. Each story is scored by the other players, who contribute a voting card with a number of points and an "accolade" or mini-review. After a predetermined number of rounds, the storyteller with the most points wins.

Dinn Chapter Packs allow you to dive deeper into the mysteries of Dinn’s first season, as well as customize your decks with powerful new expansion cards.

Chapter Pack 2 continues the investigation into the mysterious disappearance of Natalie Neil with 10 new puzzle cards that will introduce players to new events, characters, and strange notifications in Rock Springs, Wyoming.

—description from the publisher

A children's counting and matching game. Draw a card from the middle and count the number of chick's, if it matches a number from your coop you can fill that spot. Be careful to avoid the broken eggs because they will cover one of you coop spaces, and then you will need to draw a hen card to lay a new egg and clear your space. The first person to fill their coop is the winner.

A simple matching game with a monkey theme. Draw from a pool of facedown cards and attempt to be the first person to get your monkey fully dressed. Avoid the banana peels though because they will take up room on your board and you will have to find a broom to clean them up before you can finish dressing your monkey.

It's Christmas Eve. You're way too excited to sleep. Creeping downstairs for a late-night snack you find a mysterious gingerbread house and just can't resist taking a huge bite. But before you know it you've shrunk to the size of an ant and are trapped inside! While the delicious sights and smells are enough to make your mouth water, you only have one hour to make your escape before you're trapped in there forever!

Solve each of the four puzzles to work out which one of the 12 different gingerbread men you need to eat to unshrink yourself. Only by solving the puzzles correctly will you be able to safely make your escape and be back in time for Christmas morning.

--description from publisher's website

Rocket Fire: A Space Exploration Board Game
A competitive family "turns base" dice and card game, that includes resource cards, fuel cards, power cards, and chips. Use resource strategy management to maximize dice roll chances to score points. Gain enough rocket fuel to be the first to launch 6 rockets into outer space to win the game. 
2 to 4 players.
In Rocket Fire, the goal is to become the first player with six or more rockets ready to launch into outer space.

To complete this, players must use resource cards, point cards, fuel cards, and chips to collect their materials. Power cards can be used to fight back, and cubes can be collected to exchange for coveted rocket ships. With a great plan and skillful resource management, each roll of the die can be used with maximum efficiency.

Fuel the Fire

Undermine other players and give yourself explosive advantages with Power Cards. After resolving dice rolls and with enough chips, players can take control of the game using as many power cards in their hands as they can afford. Plays include:

Booster: This allows you to change the roll number of a reserved card, helping you or harming another player.
Forced Trade in which players can swap any reserve card on the board with another reserve card, as long as it is either a point or resource card.
A Recall that causes all players to return chips of the color chosen by the player that plays a Chip Dump Power Card.
Power Shield Blocking a power card from another player on any turn.
Calling a # Freeze which allows players to prevent a roll from counting, as long as it lands on a number specified when the # Freeze Power Card is played.
Using a Reserve Dump to discard a point card of their choice from their reserve slots.
Allowing a ∞ Chip that allows that player to choose a chip color and take a chip from that color’s chip bank.
Using Stolen Power Power Cards to take any power card that has been discarded into that player’s own hand.
To plan their competitive strategies, players have one of three available actions per turn:

Reserve resource cards by keeping them on your own slots on the playboard.
Purchase a fuel card using the cost listed on the desired card.
Buy a power card using a chip of any color, then hold onto it until the perfect moment!
Still, everyone starts somewhere, and these actions can’t just be used right away. The first round differs as each players prepares their strategy and their hand with the resources available at the start of the game. However, for every round after the first, the gloves are off, and the scheming begins!

Eyes to the Skies

Though creating setbacks for other players can be fun, don’t lose sight of your ultimate goal: becoming the innovator that sends six or more rockets into the endless web of space and discovering all of its secrets.

-description from publisher

In 2439 the Terran Hegemony unleashed the Mackie — the first BattleMech — setting off an arms race across the Inner Sphere. The Proliferation Cycle anthology tells the story of how all six Houses employed force of arms, infiltrations and espionage to field cutting-edge new ’Mechs of their own. The volume also includes the story of the first Clan OmniMech.

This companion ForcePack includes all seven original ’Mechs, which can be fielded on any game table using the rules from Interstellar Operations: Alternate Eras. Includes seven MechWarrior pilot cards and seven Alpha Strike cards.

7 Mech  Miniatures
  ‣ Coyotl
  ‣ Firebee
  ‣ Icarus II
  ‣ Gladiator
  ‣ Battleaxe
  ‣ Ymir
  ‣ Mackie

-description from publisher's website

Loved and hated in equal measure, over the last four decades the UrbanMech has become the mascot of BattleTech. Recently, the UrbanMech has been turned into multiple plushies, appeared in a company box, and even a full-scale inflatable display!

This ForcePack brings a new pose of the original UM-R60 variant from the UrbanMech Salvage Box, along with three additional variants, including a brand-new ilClan Era design! Includes four MechWarrior pilot cards and four Alpha Strike cards.

—description from the publisher

With a reputation for unorthodox tactics and a fetish for collecting lost artifacts and treasures, you never know what you’re gonna get when Snord’s Irregulars hit the field. Yet they always seem to scrape out of the tightest spots.

Unleash the Snord’s Irregulars Assault Lance! Included is the new Spartan and unique-to-the-Irregulars FrankenMech, a jumping Guillotine, and new variant of the Highlander, along with four MechWarrior pilot cards and four Alpha Strike cards.

The march of technology across BattleTech’s eras is relentless—but some war machines never die. As the ilClan era dawns, the most recognizable BattleMechs stand ready to carry your banner into the future, bristling with cutting-edge weapons and armor.

Recognition Guide, vol. 1: Classics collects some of the best-known Inner Sphere, Clan and ComStar BattleMechs, rebuilt with the latest technology to keep them competitive on modern battlefields. This volume is the companion to Recognition Guide, vol. 2: ilClan, and contains material originally published in the PDF-only BattleTech Recognition Guide: ilClan series.

Each machine is fully detailed with an illustration, in-universe development notes, battle histories, and notable pilots for each unit.

boardgame: Hellboy: The Board Game – The Lobster
description:
This limited edition mini-expansion for Hellboy: The Board Game brings The Lobster to your adventures.

Contains:

1 x pre-assembled plastic The Lobster (Comic Book Version)

1 x Agent Board

2 x Starting Cards

1 x Deck of Doom Card

Map is 34″ x 22″, the perfect “two-map” size for most games. The maps are high-resolution with great color quality, making them some of the most beautiful and durable BattleTech maps ever produced. The neoprene is also thin for ease of storage, and double-sided for versatility.

-description from publisher's website

Map is 34″ x 22″, the perfect “two-map” size for most games. The maps are high-resolution with great color quality, making them some of the most beautiful and durable BattleTech maps ever produced. The neoprene is also thin for ease of storage, and double-sided for versatility.

—description from the publisher

A perfect companion to both the Alpha Strike box set and the Commander’s Edition rulebook:


     3 heavy-stock reference sheets of the most commonly used game tables
     6 punchboards, including additional movement markers and trees from the Alpha Strike box, as well as all-new terrain such as rivers and roads, large game templates, and more.
     A second complete set of the fifteen fold-up cardstock buildings from the Alpha Strike box

The Scenario Designers Guide is a 72-page manual provides everything needed to start designing your own ASL scenarios!  From conception to research to design to playtesting to publishing, it gives expert advice and guidance.

If you have never tried designing scenarios, the Scenario Designers Guide makes it easy.  It also includes valuable appendices such as Victory Condition and Scenario Special Rule compendiums.

The Scenario Designers Guide includes two FREE scenarios.  These challenging tournament-sized scenarios take place on the island of Java at the end of World War II.  One scenario features the last independent offensive by the Imperial Japanese Army!  The other features British and Japanese troops fighting together on the same side!

It continues the Desperation Morale tradition of offering tools and resources for the ASLer.


TABLE OF CONTENTS

Author’s Preface
Introduction. Three Thousand Keys to Success
Chapter One. Scenario Ideas: Theory and Practice
Chapter Two. Scenario Sources and Research
Chapter Three. Creating the Landscape
Chapter Four. Creating ASL Orders of Battle
Chapter Five. Miscellaneous Scenario Concerns
Chapter Six. Playtesting
Chapter Seven. Okay, So It’s Done, Now What
Appendix A. Victory Conditions
Appendix B. SSR Compendium
Appendix C. Scenario Design Example: The Java Scenarios
Scenario Java 1. The IJA’s Last Battle
Scenario Java 2. Unlikely Allies

—description from the publisher (adapted)

In this alternate history game, a nuclear event began to devastate the world and most of the world is now covered in the ocean.
To avoid extinction, the survivors have built massive floating cities that need to be constantly powered to keep the cities moving.
This requires expeditions to the few remaining land masses and even the deep ocean to find the necessary materials for the cities. But resources are scarce...

In Scarce players need to finish the construction of the nuclear plant that powers their floating city. Each player is in control of a dragonfly ship, a vehicle that can travel in the air, on the ocean surface, and in the deep ocean.
Players can upgrade and manage the crew and modules of their ships to maximize resource gathering. All this while avoiding a doomsday cult, hellbent on destroying the city. The cult has its city on the way and several scouting forces already in the vicinity of the player city. Players will need to move carefully to avoid enemies and be time-efficient to get their city moving before the enemy city arrives. The deep sea is not safe either as strange creatures lurk in the dark.

Scarce offers versatile gameplay, allowing you to engage either cooperatively, where collaboration is key to city repair, or competitively, where captains vie against each other to claim the title of the most successful.

- Explore resource tokens above and below the ocean surface as you move your craft on a multi-dimensional gameboard. This is done while avoiding dangers on both levels. Outmaneuver or defeat these threats as you see fit!

- Upgrade your craft by attaching modules, recruiting crew members, and acquiring ability cards to maximize the efficiency of your turn. You will also need to play your cards right! Use your deck of cards to maneuver the craft, assign crew members to new posts, and execute powerful abilities. Using the right card at the right time might be enough to avoid disaster.

- Manage your resources well! You will need to use them to build the nuclear plant to achieve victory but you will also need to use them to slow the progress of the enemy city that is constantly closing in on you. If the time is up and the enemy city reaches you, it is too late and all is lost.

This is a game with a lot of choices, possibilities, strategies, and changing conditions. If you´d like a  gateway into heavier games with several mechanics but in an easy-to-learn package,  this game is for you!

Core mechanics:  
Card management  
Tech Tree  
Resource management  
Worker placement
Exploration
Multi-dimensional gameboard

Facebook community group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/920067722311252

Tabletop simulator: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2979255652

—description from the designer

Every pig farmer has his/her own secrets of pig farming, while every pig trader has his/her own purchasing list. As an owner of a pig farm, your tasks are helping your piggies grow up healthy to breed more and completing complicating orders from the pig traders. Don’t forget your competitors also cast covetous eyes on those orders. The one who can use strategies to prosper his/her pig farm will be the best pig farmer of the year!

boardgame: Teotihuacan: City of Gods – Deluxe Master Set
description:
Travel back in time to the greatest city in Mesoamerica. Witness the glory and the twilight of the powerful pre-Columbian civilization. Strategize, accrue wealth, gain the favour of the gods, and become the builder of the magnificent Pyramid of the Sun. Now with all the expansions, promos and more, packed in one box and with upgraded deluxe components.

In Teotihuacan: City of Gods, each player commands a force of worker dice, which grow in strength with every move. On your turn, you move a worker around a modular board, always choosing one of two areas of the location tile you land on: one offering you an action (and a worker upgrade), the other providing you with a powerful bonus (but without an upgrade).

While managing their workforce and resources, players develop new technologies, climb the steps of the three great temples, build houses for the inhabitants of the city, and raise the legendary and breath-taking Pyramid of the Sun in the center of the city.

Each game is played in three eras. As the dawn of the Aztecs comes closer, player efforts (and their ability to feed their workforce) are evaluated a total of three times. The player with the most fame is the winner.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
"The invaders are atting the planet!
An evil army from outer space decided to destroy the Earth by attacking its great capital cities. Thankfully, the Earth has ace pilots who, thanks to their proton turrets, will try to put an end to the invasion... and possibly to destroy the enemy for good.
You are one of these pilots. Take place in your cockpit and prepare to become a legend!"

This game belongs to the Cosmo Duck Roll & Write Collection.

Players each play a spaceship trying to ward off aliens represented by five rows of little spaceships, each with a unique pair of dice faces above them. Each turn, the starting player will throw 4 dice and will pair them two-by-two as they like. For both pair of dice obtained, they can destroy the alien spaceship linked to that dice combination.

It a full row of ships has been destroyed, the player crosses it - it will gain them additional points at the end of the game.

Once a full colums has been destroyed, the player can take on the Mothership. This Mothership has 6 dices attributed to it, and the player must take 3 shots in total to destroy it (each shot crossing two of the dice next to it).

Once a player destroyed the Mothership, all player tally their scores. They add up the scores attributed to all the spaceship they destroyed (the ones with harder combination are worth more points, and they are color-coded) to the score of the rows they cleared. The player with the most points wins!

In MetalleFest players form their band by drafting musician cards, then draw and select song cards to compose a song. The groove of the song draws fan cards to your stage. The music skills used to play the song impress fans gathered at your stage. Impressed fans will buy your merch! The first band to sell all their merch wins.

—description form the publisher

A smart strategy game for the whole family. Easy to play, tricky to win.

In this game the participants compete in who can build the longest train. There are six different types of engines that can be coupled to cars of the same colour. Who can build the most long trains wins!

—description from the back of the box

Tåget ("The Train") is a single-deck card game about building trains - the longer the better! There are six different suits of trains, with 3 locomotives and 8 cars in each. Players must lay down two to five cards to a common display each turn, either lengthening an existing train or starting a new one, and collecting train(s) for points if they wish to, provided they have a locomotive with at least two cars attached. A player who meets or exceeds the length of the so far longest train gains the First Class Ticket which is worth points at the end, while the player who makes a train as short or shorter than the currently shortest one gets the Canceled Departure which conversely carries a penalty. Watch out for cards left in your hand at the end of the game, as they are worth minus points too!

A light, quick-to-play set collection game with oversize (long) cards and simple rules.

—user summary

An escape-room like game meant to be played by 1-6 cooperative players. A series of key and combination locked containers must be opened by studying clues and solving puzzles. The difficulty level is designed for teens and adults or ambitious youngsters with a little help. In "Spy", a mad scientist in Seattle is about to do something bad. Players must stop him before it is too late.

Pieces are creatures who love to do imitations. One by one, they all imitate something different. Not a single one has a fixed form. None of them have a fixed form.

Pieces (ピーシーズ) is a sandbox game containing 35 3D-printed pieces in a drawstring bag. Each piece is a different simple shape that the players can use to communicate with each other. The intention is that players will use the included rules or create their own games that use the Pieces. Instructions are included for sharing the games you create through social media.

The game includes rules for at least eight different games that use the Pieces, including a twist on Shiritori (a word chain game where each new word begins with the last letter of the word before), charades (using combinations of Pieces in a guessing game), group storytelling using drawn Pieces to suggest where the story goes, and Shape Poker (where you try to create poker-style groupings). The game includes a small three-by-three board for games like Triple Word Chain, where you try to get three words chained in a row.

Set on a floating aquatic city in the Mid-Pacific, Neon Reef focuses on the first Hydro-Habitat west of the USA. As the city grows out of its infancy, factions compete to shape the future of the Reef. Will Neon Reef become a scientific utopia nurtured by a peaceful group of Botanists, or a hub of offshore cyber-crime overseen by the Glitch Hunters?
       
Neon Reef is a game that breaks away from traditional genres by combining tile placement with draftable factions. Over the course of the game, up to 6 players choose a faction, place buildings, and build their economy to find a unique path to victory.

—description from the designer

On that day, the school was cut off from the outside world. The only way for the trapped students to escape is to challenge the school's underground labyrinth, which is infested with curse gods, and defeat the god of the underworld, Tsukiyomi, in the deepest part. However, only one person can enter the labyrinth per night. Of course, lives lost in the middle of the road will never return. 10 students trained for this day bet everything on only 10 chances.
