description:
You learned magic called Magical at the Academy of Magic.
Collect and combine magicka cards, which are pieces of magic,
Let's make it a magic formula and create a big magic!
Can you pick up discarded cards? Better if you wake up! ?
Read the flow, seize your destiny, and aim for the greatest wizard!

A building game that can be enjoyed by children and adults alike! Look carefully at the theme cards and pile up the cats so that they have the same shape! A first-come, first-served party game that is irresistible for cat lovers!

A certain European country where tensions continue.
Here, spies from all over the world are fighting day and night for confidential information.
As a spy dispatched to this country, you must also interfere with spies (other players) from other countries, outwit them, and obtain more confidential information than any other country.

Are you nostalgic for the days when romance meant making a mix-tape for someone? Feelings could be shown through only music and the decorations on a cassette tape.

Romance (ロマンス) is a reverse butting game. In most butting games, players try to avoid making choices that match those of other players, but in Romance, you try to match with exactly one other player. The game includes 56 cards with 8 different scenes or situations, and each card has a combination of scenes printed on both sides. The creates 28 possible combinations, and there are two of each combination in the deck. There are also cassette player cards where players will stack their scored cards. If you wish to play a 2-player game, the game includes rules for a dummy player.

Following the draw each turn, all players choose a card from their public hands and simultaneously play it. Any matching cards with the same situation showing are stacked together, and cards are drawn until there are as many situations on the table as there are players. Players then simultaneously select a situation. If exactly one player choose a situation, the card or cards go in their reserve. If three or more players have a matching choice, those cards remain in the play area. But if exactly two players choose a situation, they will score those cards. Once players have finished these steps, they may flip their entire public hand over so that the other side is now visible or return their hand to the deck and redraw.

Players place any scored situation cards on the cassette player cards that include both of their player colors. They score any cards from the stack in the center of the table, any visible matching cards from the players' hands, up to one card with a hidden matching side from each player, and any matching cards from their reserve. Each card will be worth one point. Once any cassette player has 10 cards stacked on it or the draw deck runs out of cards, players will add up their points from all cassette players that match their color. The player with the highest score wins.

A trivia game for multiple generations to play... at the same time!

Divide the teams and let the youths, parents, grandparents play against each other or mix the teams evenly. Which generation is the most knowledgeable?

Unique to the Generations Battle are the digital challenges. Listen to music and watch video clips and answer correctly directly on your phone, no need to download an app, it works directly in the web browser.

To win, the team must complete a full lap around the game board and answer questions from all four different generations. A game for all occasions and all ages!

Every Viking knows the gods' power is everywhere. In the rocks under their feet. In every drop in the dangerous sea. From the steel of their swords, to the grain they sow in the fields.  Their shaman consults the cryptic runes to decipher their god's divine will. These soothsayers channel their energy into mystic cairn stones, and in doing so, infuriate their terrifying deities.

Shamans is a mini-expansion first made available with the Deluxe Edition of Tiny Epic Vikings during its initial Kickstarter campaign.

The Dark Emperor threatens to conquer the world! The 5-Color Sentai (okay, there are actually 7 colors) must work together to stop him. Fight monsters, learn new super moves, and rescue your captured friends. If you can defeat the Dark Emperor, the day is saved!

5-Color Sentai (五色戦隊) is a semi-cooperative worker-placement game. Each player controls an identical team of 5 sentai superheroes represented by red, pink, yellow, green, and blue squares. Players also begin with black and white squares that represent unavailable captured teammates. Each turn, players place one of their sentai on a matching space on a card in the row of monster cards. Players compete to be the one to play the last required sentai, because defeating the monster grants points and an effect or the ongoing ability to perform a special move. Once all players have played all their possible sentai, players take back all their sentai and a new round begins.

All players begin with a selection of special moves that allow them to place more than one sentai at a time. By defeating monsters, a player may unlock additional special moves like Bingo Attack, Triple Fireball, and the Rainbow Ray. Victory may also trigger special situations, such as taking the start player card or rescuing captured sentai teammates. But be careful: some cards can make you lose other cards or even one of your own sentai.

The Dark Emperor is always the final card to appear. Once a player is capable of defeating the Dark Emperor, they must do so. At that point, the world is saved and everyone wins. But the player with the most accumulated victory points on their cards is the biggest winner.

At the stardust festival on the planet Ultia, sweet pieces of stardust called Copets rain down. If you collect or eat Copets according to your fortune, you are promised happiness in the coming year. Greedy princes want all the happiness for themselves! Of the three brothers, who will be the happiest prince･･･!?

COPET (コペット) is a set collection game for three players. The box acts as the randomizer, having small holes in the sides of the top and bottom that can align to allow players to shake out small plastic red, blue, yellow, and white pieces. The pieces resemble konpeitō, brightly colored Japanese sugar candy.

On a player's turn, they may choose to collect a Copet, consume a Copet by placing it on their Tummy Tracker, or draw an Astrology Card. After they've consumed certain amounts of Copets, they may also switch a Copet with another player or place one of their Copets on anoter player's Tummy Tracker. Collecting a white Copet allows a player to draw and reveal a special White Astrology Card.

When the box is emptied of Copets, players reveal their cards and score points according to the Copets and Astrology Cards they've collected. For instance a player might score 2 points if they have 2 red Copets on their Tummy Tracker, or they might score 5 points because the player to their right consumed 2 blue Copets.

SCP The Board game is a Asymmetrical role based game. Each player is given a random role. The first person to complete their roles objective wins.

You can play as up to 6 different SCP's, an NFT combatant, a Chaos Insurgency Combatant, A D-Class Personnel, or a Scientist.

Each role navigates a maze in an attempt to find other players or locations.

The map is segmented into 20 different rooms. Unless a player is in the room the room is hidden. The full map is never revealed to the players.

Welcome to the Launchpad Spacedock! As a skilled builder, help build and launch rockets capable of reaching beyond the speed of light. Compete against other players, earn the respect of robots, and become the best builder in the galaxy!

Robots & Rockets: Launchpad is a competitive rocket-building card game for 2 to 4 players. The goal is to build and launch rockets to collect the greatest number of rocket parts by the end of the game.

Build and launch rockets to collect the best combination of robots and rocket segments by the end of the game. Players take turns clockwise, starting with the first player. On their turn, they choose SWAP or PLAY cards from their hands.

At the end of a turn, if the deck is empty or all Robot tokens are claimed, all players get one more final turn each (including the player whose turn just ended).

This is a special edition made for the Launch Tabletop Launch Kit.

boardgame: Krimi total Fall 22: Die Rückkehr des Dr. Danger
description:
The secret criminal organization GIGA is in turmoil. Just before Dr. Danger could reveal his brilliant plan to his closest confidants, which would bring the syndicate unlimited power and wealth, a terrible catastrophe occurred. The island with the secret base was destroyed by a devastating volcanic eruption!

An emergency crisis meeting has been called to determine who is responsible for the death of Dr. Danger and the destruction of the headquarters. Because the eruption of the volcano at this critical moment cannot be a coincidence!

However, surprisingly, a person has appeared at this meeting who claims to be Dr. Danger. Has he miraculously survived the volcanic eruption?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Die geheime Verbrecherorganisation GIGA ist in Aufruhr. Kurz bevor Dr. Danger seinen engsten Vertrauten seinen genialen Plan enthüllen konnte, der dem Syndikat unbegrenzte Macht und Reichtum bescheren würde, kam es zu einer schrecklichen Katastrophe. Durch einen verheerenden Vulkanausbruch wurde die Insel mit der geheimen Basis vernichtet!

Ein sofort einberufenes Krisentreffen soll aufklären, wer für den Tod von Dr. Danger und die Zerstörung des Hauptquartiers verantwortlich ist. Denn dass der Vulkan in diesem kritischen Moment ausgebrochen ist, kann kein Zufall sein!

Allerdings ist überraschend eine Person bei diesem Treffen aufgetaucht, die behauptet, Dr. Danger zu sein. Hat er wie durch ein Wunder den Vulkanausbruch überlebt?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tenpenny Parks: Fast Pass Mini Expansion
description:
Improve your actions in Tenpenny Parks with the Fast Pass mini-expansion. The expansion includes rules and four fast pass tokens -- Arborist, Banker, Contractor and Realtor. Players gain a special bonus when placing their worker on a space featuring a fast pass token.

—description from the publisher

Get Ready... get steady... and try not to get wreckt! In Battle for the Hill, players collect points in a fast-paced futuristic race of sports stars with amazing abilities. Use action cards to slow down other players or join together and K.O. the many obstacles standing in your way. Stuck? Players can reprogram “cobot” referees, who are standing by on the sidelines waiting to help them out.

What’s a cobot?
A cobot, short for “collaborative robot”, is a robot that helps people and works alongside them.

The game includes four sports-themed card suits: Boxing, Football, Basketball, and Weightlifting. A fifth cobot-themed suit allows players to affect the different sections of The Hill, boosting their performance. On your turn, you can play one (or sometimes more!) cards to move your team up The Hill, slow down your opponents, OR change the cobot programming to give you a strategic advantage. So, bring your A game, and try not to get sacked!

An educational game to learn how to consume in a healthy and ethical way!

A fun and innovative educational learning game to :

Raise students' awareness of the impact of nutrition on their health and the environment.
    Raise their interest in the origin of the products on their plate.
    To exchange and debate about sustainable food, which seeks to respect and preserve the environment and animals without over-consuming.
    To make the pupils actors of their reflection, to help them to verbalize their thoughts and to argue their choices, while stimulating them by the game.

Each player in turn draws food cards and places them side by side on the table, face up.
table, face up. He can stop drawing at any time and keep the cards he has drawn.
cards. He puts them back into his hand or places them face down in front of him.
The player draws as many cards as he wishes, provided that he does not draw :

     a Food card from a family already drawn during this round;
     a Red Zone card.


In these two cases, the player's turn ends immediately.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un jeu pédagogique pour apprendre à consommer sainement et de manière éthique !

Un jeu éducatif d’apprentissage ludique et innovant pour :

Sensibiliser les élèves en douceur à l’ impact de la nutrition sur leur santé et l’environnement .
    Susciter leur intérêt pour l’ origine des produits qu’ils ont dans leur assiette.
    Échanger et débattre autour d’une alimentation durable , qui cherche à respecter et à préserver l’environnement et les animaux sans surconsommer.
    Rendre les élèves acteurs de leur réflexion , les aider à verbaliser leur pensée et à argumenter leurs choix, tout en les stimulant par le jeu.

Chaque joueur à son tour pioche des cartes Aliment et les pose côte à côte sur la
table, face visible. Il peut s’arrêter de piocher à tout moment et conserver les cartes
piochées. Il les range dans sa main ou les pose devant lui, face cachée.
Le joueur pioche donc autant de cartes qu’il souhaite, à condition de ne pas piocher :

     une carte Aliment d’une famille déjà piochée lors de ce tour ;
     une carte Zone rouge.


Dans ces deux cas, le tour du joueur s’arrête immédiatement.

—description from the publisher

An educational game to raise awareness of sustainable development!

A fun and innovative educational learning game to :

Raise students' awareness of the impact of their daily actions on the planet and enable them to acquire the basics of sustainable development .
    Exchange and debate about actions that protect the planet and environmental issues.
    To make the pupils actors of their reflection, to help them to verbalize their thoughts and to argue their choices, while stimulating them by the game.

Players take turns playing and, when it is their turn to play, they can perform the following actions in any order:

     draw 2 cards ;
     play up to 3 cards;
     discard cards from their hand.


—description from the publisher (translated)

Un jeu pédagogique pour sensibiliser au développement durable !

Un jeu éducatif d’apprentissage ludique et innovant pour :

Sensibiliser les élèves en douceur à l’impact de leurs gestes quotidiens sur la planète et leur permettre d’acquérir les bases du développement durable .
    Échanger et débattre autour des gestes qui protègent la planète et des enjeux environnementaux .
    Rendre les élèves acteurs de leur réflexion , les aider à verbaliser leur pensée et à argumenter leurs choix, tout en les stimulant par le jeu.

Les joueurs jouent chacun à leur tour et, lorsque c’est à eux de jouer, ils peuvent
réaliser les actions suivantes dans l’ordre qu’ils veulent :

     piocher 2 cartes ;
     jouer jusqu’à 3 cartes ;
     défausser des cartes de leur main.


—description from the publisher

Last Stand in Hokkaido 199x is an "alternate history" Operational level two-player board-wargame set in the early 1990s when the Soviet Union invaded Hokkaido. In reality, this did not happen, and at that time the Soviet Army did not have the ability to land multiple divisions and maintain logistics, but the Soviet leader, who judged that the United States could temporarily gain sea and air control by allocating resources to other regions (such as the Middle East, as in history), judged that the military, economic, and political gains were balanced. Suppose you have made the decision to gather landing boats and transport ships and land and invade Hokkaido.

One person is in charge of the Self-Defense Forces and the other is in charge of the Soviet Army.


Published with Banzai magazine 14.

description:
Split the Party is a dice-placement board game themed around fantasy roleplaying, where you are taking on the role of an adventuring party made up of classic themes such as Mage, Warrior, Rogue, etc. The hook for this game, however, is that each of these character archetypes are represented by a custom d10 die, with different skill faces on each one. You place these dice around the board in areas that represent places an adventurer might visit in a fantasy town and its surrounding areas, such as the Tavern, Shop, Wizard's Tower and Dungeons. Then after each player has placed all of their dice, players encounter cards at those locations, trying to succeed at checks to defeat or acquire those cards by rolling the dice workers themselves, earning victory points or gold to buy useful items, etc. The player with the highest total victory points at the end of five rounds is the winner!

—description from the designer

You reach the top of the summits, not the highest, but the most beautiful, for sure. So many tests to reach the ban.. You enter the temple courtyard, a monk logger puts away wood, he greets you and welcomes you, on your left, two residents in full meditation.

Yautman and El Choucas, these two legendary guides, congratulate you on having arrived so far...

...But rest, you will need it, they are giving you a new challenge!

The Ultimate Trek contains 3 Mountains: the bottom one with white squares, the middle one with green squares, and the top one with blue squares.
You can play Ultimate Trek in Expedition Mode (Multiplayer or Free Solo). Namely that the Shipping is done in one go and not in 3 steps. The end goal is to achieve the highest score (you don't earn reputation stars during the Trek ultimatum)

The normal rules of Trek 12 apply, plus the following constraints:

     It is mandatory to start from the bottom mountain, in one of the 4 lowest squares. Then, the classic rules (enter a value in an adjacent box) apply until the 3 mountains are completely filled.
     Each mountain has its own table for choosing the outcome of the dice. You must necessarily fill in the boxes of a mountain with the table specific to this mountain (it is for example forbidden to fill in a box of the white mountain using the table of the blue mountain). (to help you: pay attention to the arrows).
     The passage from one mountain to another is done only via Rope Paths (represented by dotted junctions). It is forbidden to move from one mountain to another with a Zone.
     BONUS: the first player to connect a lower square (therefore without a compass) with a square adjacent to a flag surrounds the latter. This will bring him a corresponding bonus at the end of the game. In the event of a tie, both players score the bonus. In Free Solo, there is no need to indicate anything since both players score the bonus anyway. Better to score it anyway in order to try to beat the absolute record of the ascent!
     The use of Assistance cards is possible (but not compulsory, we advise you to warm up with a simple game before!). Obtaining an Assistance Card is done in different ways:
         Setup: Shuffle all assist cards and make a face down draw pile.
         Each time an area of 0, 1 or 2 is made, draw 3 cards and keep one. Discard the other 2.
         When the stack is empty shuffle the discard pile and create a new stack.
        You can use as many Assistance Cards as you want during a game, but the limit of 3 Assistance Cards in hand is still valid. At the end of the game, the unused cards in hand always bring a bonus of 3 points!

Your treasure awaits, come seek, come find,
Follow the clues, don't get left behind!

One person prepares the trail of 10 clues. First clue is placed on the game board (e.g. BED). Next - in the location hinted by the clue (e.g. under the bed one puts TABLE card) etc. In the final location a treasure is placed. Once all clues are hidden, the game begins. Each time a player discovers a clue, they place it on the board for others to see and may get a jewel. The game ends when the treasure is found.

The game can be played in a competitive mode, in which the person with most jewels at the end wins, or in a coop mode, in which everyone wins once the treasure is found.

The Middle Ages. Despite the severe restrictions on movement during this period, there were still those who traded. The lords with their intricate fiefdoms and the rise of the City relies on these traders. You are a major distributor across Central Europe... But you don't yet have the trading posts to support your commerce. Scavenge for materials, fulfil contracts and venture out into the outside world. Lead the youth of the Family in building up a network of trading posts and trading houses from village to village and town to town.

Age of Arms is a middle-weight euro game that combines resource management, building, and board movement. The game has two pillars.

The first is to collect a tableau of cards to fulfil contracts. The cards you collect are also resources for fulfilling additional contracts. By accumulating cards of the same colour, special effects can be obtained and the game is accelerated. The completed contract cards also dictate which direction and how far you can move.

The second pillar is the 'Traders Journey'. By utilising the movement dictated by your contracts, the players trade caravan travels around the 12 cities of Central Europe. Along the way, you can set up trading posts, build trading houses and score points in the cities.

Which of the trading companies will befriend the most noblemen and become the champions of the age?
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
A new threat, the "Shell-Type Clad", attacks the last city of humanity, Encratia.
Witches of unknown affiliation appeared in response to Clad's invasion.
Those who desire the destruction of the world. resister. and a watcher.
As the battle rages on, destinies intertwine.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Becomes a mystery? Case solved? A detective and the true criminal face off over seven incidents.
The existence of the true culprit involved in all the incidents...
The showdown between the great detective and the true criminal begins over seven unsolved cases!
If the detective solves one case, new facts will be obtained.
The investigation is really full-blown. Which faction will be able to read the opponent and control the confrontation?
publishers: JUGAME STUDIO (ジュゲムスタジオ)

description:
A game of fragile alliances and utter tomfoolery. Vikings compete for loot as Ragnarök approaches. A card game for 3 to 6 players, featuring Nordic-themed puns that you may take quite the Viking too.
Play cards to gather loot and utilise the oxymoronic protection of the Doomsday deck.  The player with the highest value loot and gold in their stash at the end of the game wins.

An exciting psychological warfare game for two players! Predict the last remaining tile while outsmarting your opponent!
A battle-type psychological game in which 9 tiles arranged in 3x3 are alternately thrown away and the last tile is predicted. In a maximum of 7 matches, the first to win 3 or the one with the most wins wins.

Welcome to a world of floating castles, airships and dragons! Castles float by power of a mystic stone called Dragonite. Merchants and regents maneuver the skies in elegant airships. Dragon Lords dominate the clouds.

In MEGA Mini Dragons, players choose a dragon and interact with the merchants and regents who come to the valley to trade or beg protection. They come bearing dazzling gems, precious Dragonite or even a rare dragon egg - all being the preferred currency of the Dragon Lords. Gems are crafted into precious items like rings, necklaces and scepters. Eggs are hatched and the Dragonite hoarded. Dragon Riders accumulate these items to elevate their status in dragon society.

MEGA Mini Dragons is a 1-4 player, strategic board game of resource management with exclusive dragon meeples. Each turn, new merchant cards are dealt beneath the floating castle cards. Players spontaneously decide to which castle they send their Dragon Rider. If two or more players choose the same castle, the player with the higher status sends the contenders to a different castle. Castles and merchants offer different resources and crafting choices. Every turn, Dragon Council cards are dealt face down, which dictate item values at the end of the game. Players may choose to spend an action to sneak a peek at values, or even bribe a Council member - and switch a value card for one that pays proper respect to their pile of dragon loot!

Dragon Lords, take to the skies! The merchants come bearing gifts. Royalty from distant lands throw themselves at our mercy. The world is ripe. Can your Dragon Rider scale the peaks of dragon society in the clouds? Jump on your dragon in the next game of Mega Mini Dragons to find out!

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Floating islands in the sky

description:
Become a barista at a popular coffee shop and fulfill various orders.
The secret to its popularity lies in its perfect recipe.
I'm not allowed to arrange recipes just because I'm busy.
Whether or not you can serve the food with the correct recipe during business hours is a showcase of your skills as a barista.
Sometimes I cooperate with my friends, eat cookies and take a break.
A first-class barista can be said to be a first-class barista not only in terms of techniques and knowledge about coffee, but also in providing the best relaxing space for customers.
publishers: JUGAME STUDIO (ジュゲムスタジオ)

description:
The game contains:
1 wooden board
80 double-sided (red and black) numbered tiles from 1 to 8
Book with 100 puzzles
Single-player and multiplayer instructions

Basic information:
There are 8 lines, 6 circles, and 1 head.
The HEAD is contained of 8 main squares and extra 4 squares showing the directions as a reminder. All 8 main squares should contain the numbers from 1 to 8.
Direction of each line always starts from inside out. Each LINE has 8 squares in total (2 squares inside the head and 6 squares outside the head). Each line should contain the numbers from 1 to 8.
Each CIRCLE should contain the numbers from 1 to 8. All 6 circles are located outside of the head. Each circle is formed of 8 squares connected
with thin lines.

Rules:

     Each number from 1 to 8 can appear only once inside the head.
     Each number from 1 to 8 can appear only once in a single line.
     Each number from 1 to 8 can appear only once in a circle outside the head.
     HIGH TIDE, LOW TIDE, CARIBBEAN, AEGEAN, PACIFIC, ATLANTIC, INDIAN  and ARCTIC line can appear only once in a single game. 


MULTIPLAYER
Set up the board according to the puzzles from the book.
Set a stopwatch for each player and measure the time.
FOR EXAMPLE
If there are three players:
Each player takes a separate wooden board, and the fastest player wins the game.

SINGLE PLAYER
Set up the board according to the puzzles from the book.
Set a stopwatch and measure the time.
You become a pro once you solve the puzzles within the following minutes:
BEGINNER < 3
EASY < 4
MEDIUM < 6
HARD < 8
EXPERT < 10

The players are rolling dices and following a Dice Result Table to make their way up the mountain, gathering information about the condition of the slopes. The more information they have gathered, the faster they can move. When they've reached the top, they start making their way down along the Piste, unlocking a new Dice Table. The player who first has reached the Goal at the bottom right corner of the Ski Slope has won the game.

—description from the designer

Cooperate with each other to guess how the judge is feeling!

I felt... is a cooperative game in which you are divided into “the judge” and “the respondent”. The challenge is to guess the feelings of the judge within the set time limit by asking questions and discussing with your fellow respondents. The game ends when each player has played the role of the judge. The goal is to get all the questions right and become “umomoi”!

Even through the same experience, how one reacts varies from person to person. Sometimes understanding how others feel can be confusing. But understanding how others are feeling is marvelous and exciting. I felt... is a party game that will continue to stimulate conversation even after the game is over!

—description from the publisher

The cards show 1 or 2 of 6 possible colors. There are 3 spaces on the table for a card to be placed face up. The first card of the stack shows the actually blocked colors. On your turn, play a card without a blocked color in a vacant space or onto a face up card with matching at least one color. If all 3 cards show a common color, the table is cleared and the neighbors of the active player are punished with cards. 
The last card of your hand must be placed onto the blocking card matching at least one color. The first player who got rid of all his cards is the match winner. The last player will be punished with a personal blocking card for the next match. After 8 or 12 matches, the player who won the most matches wins the game.

The land of Wild is full of surprises–new shores to explore, new relics to unearth, and new wonders to discover. With the Wildscapes expansion, you will unlock a new card each time you create a protected valley. How far will your river flow?

This 6-card expansion adds new locations which can drastically reshape your landscape. These cards are not shuffled into the deck; instead, whenever a valley is created, one of the expansion cards is added to the selection row, acting as a normal card for the purposes of selection and placement.

—description from the publisher

Making history isn't always easy. Celebrate your triumphs with grand monuments while managing the misfortunes that plague your growing civilization.

This 6-card expansion randomly replaces some of the standard game’s Districts with special Districts featuring Monument and/or Misfortune blocks.

Monuments to your civilization can be constructed without any cost and score at the end of the game, or can be covered to gain a free Resource. Misfortunes are negative events that apply when they are in your Realm; you can cover them up to eliminate their effect or suffer through them to score points for your resilience at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

Doodle Mayhem is a fast-paced party game where everyone draws at the same time! The sooner you finish your doodle, the more points you can win. However, make sure that your doodle is clear, you'll win points if your team mates can guess your sketch - but you'll lose them if they can't!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ichabod Jones: Monster Hunter – The Card Game
description:
A psychopath escapes from a mental asylum and becomes a monster hunter during the apocalypse - with the unwelcome help of the voice in his head. A story about perception, turned into a game inspired by the prisoners dilemma. Fast-paced and easy to learn with multiple layers of strategic finesse.

In this exciting monster fighting game based on the hit comic book series "Ichabod Jones: Monster Hunter" by Russell Nohelty, you’re never sure who you’re really fighting against. Work together to build up epic attacks or take a risk and turn against your allies! Are you killing monsters - or is it all in your head? Do you hear that voice? Ichabod Jones: Monster Hunter Card Game might seem like a cooperative game, but it really isn’t.

Battle monsters cooperatively by playing the right cards. But in the end you're all by yourself, scoring points depending on your remaining hand cards!

Combine two copies of the game to play with up to 8 players.

—description from the designer

PanDogMonium is a frantically fast-paced card game full of pawesome pooches where the aim is to win as many cards as possible.

Collect cards by using fast reflexes to slap the pile whenever any of these sequences appear:
A consecutive pair of dogs,
A pair of dogs with a different dog in-between them,
3 consecutive dog toys,
3 different dog toys played consecutively,
When a bone card is played.

Whoever has the most cards at the end of the game is the top dog!

Welcome to the dark ages of the Middle Ages: sex, torture and magic in the second chapter of Squillo TIME TRAVELS. In the days of the Inquisition and the Black Death, Hags and Harlots solace the barbaric fiefdom.
Grant the favors of your Harlots to the Emperor and send them to relieve to the Crusaders. Ask your Harlots to invoke the Evil One and cast Bills against other Pimps. In collaboration with *Feudalism and Liberty* who helped best set the cleverly constructed game between *Holy Inquisition* and *Crusades*. Introduces the *Bill* cards.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Benvenuti nell’epoca oscura del Medioevo: sesso, torture e magia nel secondo capitolo di Squillo TIME TRAVELS. Ai tempi dell’Inquisizione e della Peste Nera, Megere e Meretrici sollazzano il feudo in preda alle barbarie.
Concedi i favori delle tue Meretrici allo Imperatore e mandale a dare sollievo
ai Crociati. Chiedi alle tue Megere di invocare il Maligno e lancia Fatture contro gli altri Papponi. In collaborazione con *Feudalesimo e Libertà* che ha contribuito ad ambientare al meglio il gioco costruito con astuzia tra *Santa Inquisizione* e *Crociate*. Introduce le carte *Fattura*.

—description from the publisher
