boardgame: Fugitive (Second Edition): Flash of Insight Promo Card
description:
This is a promo card for Fugitive 2nd edition. It is a additional SHIFT card to add to the game.

Text says:
Discard this to peek at the top 2 cards in any deck. Then choose 1 to keep and put the other card back on top of its deck.

Kakurenino is a three-dimensional deduction game. Each player has a set of wooden blocks made up of wooden squares; the pieces are different colors and both players have the same set of pieces.  In addition to the basic cubes, there are "decoy" cubes.

Simultaneously, each player builds a 2 or 3 dimensional structure using their pieces.  Optionally, they can include some decoy blocks.

When both players are ready, they stare at their opponent's structure trying to deduce which pieces their opponent did not use. If you have correctly guessed within the time allowed, you win!

Kairos is a domino shedding game wherein the first player determines what combination can be played but the second player sets the direction of play (ascending or descending). The goal is to be the first player to shed all their hand of dominos.

In Goat Restaurant, the players are chefs at a restaurant visited by goats, trying to make quirky paper meals which will satisfy the goat customers.

The game includes several categories of cards representing the goats' preferences which guide how the players will arrange the game's unique paper cards.  The shapes have slits, holes, are are non-traditional in shape.

Each player is given the same set of cards and one card from each category is revealed.  The first player to create a structure which satisfies the criteria is awarded 2 points and other players who can complete the task within the time limit are given 1 point.  The player with the most points after three rounds wins.

SHIJU TORITE is a perfect information trick-taking for for 3 to 4 players. The game uses a single deck of cards which consists of both the traditional playing cards (featuring a suit and rank) and goal cards (such as win a trick with a red card.)

This deck is shuffled and a face up market is created. Players take turns drafting cards from the market into a face up hand in front of them.  Once the drafting is complete, the players play a standard "must follow" trick-taking game, marking their goal cards as complete whenever they have been achieved.

At the end of the hand, the player with the highest sum of points from their goal cards wins.

Barentor consists of 12 cards.  Each round, a subset of these cards are given to one player who selects some to keep, and passes others to the next player.  Typically, a card is removed randomly before the next player looks at them; the next player also chooses two cards and passes them on.  A variation of this happens for each player (and player count.)

Once everyone has two cards, their effects are resolved in order, beginning with the 1 and ending with the 12.  Then, each player sums the cards in front of them, taking into account any effects. Each player compares their sum with the sum of the first player only. For each pair making the comparison, the person with the higher sum earns 1 point.

The game lasts a number of rounds and the person with the most points wins.

In Moments, players will work together. Some players will be giving clues to describe their cards which will then be mixed with random cards from the deck. The other players will be working together to identify the chosen cards. If the players are able to identify all the cards correctly, the group will score a point for the round. Can your group get a perfect score? Moments features 120 beautiful cards which are compatible with Keepers (sold separately).

Moments features a selection of breathtaking photography from the natural world with scenic views, animals, insects, and abstract photography. All artwork in Moments has been hand selected from Byron Jorjorian’s portfolio of over 600,000 images of the natural world.

Defend the Orion System from the Fission Parasite in this small expansion for Astro Knights.  Also includes new Knights and market cards.

Contents:
1 new Boss, Fission Parasite
2 new Knights, Alexios and Deleth
1 new Homeworld, Orion III
8 new Market Cards

Buried Beneath: A Shallow Grave is an expansion for Buried Beneath.

The gameplay is the same as the base game but the expansion adds tons of new content and also brings new modes and features to make play more difficult, varied and give you more ways to mount your defense.

New Content:
7 Heroes,
8 Monsters,
4 Bosses,
40 Items,
28 Locations.

New Actions/Stats:
You’ll find new symbols strewn across the village. The Looking Glass          and Hammer    offer new actions while the Gold Coins   
offer both a new action and act as a new stat.

New Mechanics:
The shady Shop Keeper may offer helpful weapons and accessories that boost stats and give you access to one time bonuses or abilities. Make sure you buy what you want though as it might get more expensive or go out of stock!

New Party Mode: (Adds Cooperative support)
“As chance would have it, an old party member of yours was visiting the day of the attack and the two of you have taken up arms once again, just like the good old days...”
Manage the party solo or invite a friend to help you save the village!

"Rumors tell of a Travelin’ Merchant more keen on linin’ ‘is pockets wit’ gold than worry about the danger of stayin’ here.

Oy, it’s said he’s already setup in the village, and that ‘is wears is worth a lookin’… What say ye, shall we pay ‘im a visit and see for ourselves?"

-description from designer

Story
In the land of ancient Greece, the noblest of all men have set their sights on a journey to the top of Mount Olympus, where the Gods themselves reside. Enter the world of Favor of the Gods. Set in ancient Greece, you’ll take on the role of a nobleman seeking to prove your worth to the Gods of Mount Olympus. You must fight, pray, and offer the skulls of your enemies to gain the admiration of the Gods and earn your place amongst them. As you journey through the land of Greece, you’ll encounter rivals, temples, wild boar and much more. You will use your private deck of cards to unleash powerful abilities, gain the Favor of the Gods, and rest to regain your strength. You must choose your actions wisely as the Gods’ Favor is a limited resource, and you’ll need to be cunning to outwit your opponents. Only the first player to prove their worth through strength, merit, and devotion will be welcomed to Mount Olympus where eternal paradise awaits. So, gather your hoplites and pray that the Gods show you their Favor as your try to earn your place amongst them, in Favor of the Gods.

Gameplay Overview
This game puts you into the role of a Greek noble trying to do anything in his power to please the Gods, whether that be through strength, merit, or devotion.  You with a small private deck of cards. On your turn, you can use a cards special ability which is limited in scope but powerful when used correctly, discard a card to gain Favor of a God, make an offering of your enemies’ skulls to the Gods, or rest to regain your cards. The game will end when one player proves their worth among the Gods by completing at least 1 of each category of victory conditions.

—description from the designer

The deckbuilding battle for all creation!

You and your opponents start with identical decks of low-level angels.  Each turn, you will have the opportunity to purchase cards to add to your deck.  Angels…will form your “host” army.  Demons…will oppose the actions of your opponent’s host.  Relics can be attached to angels, making them more powerful.  Humans…can also be added to your host and your horde, granting group bonuses.  And miracles, bring incredible powers into play each time they are drawn.

Build your host…and when you have enough points, challenge one of the three locations in play.  If your host is strong enough and can survive a horde challenge from your opponent, the location will be yours.  The harder the location…the greater the rewards.

Once three locations have been won by either player, it is time for the final battle.  You’ll examine the rewards granted by conquered locations:  They will allow you to add a certain number of angels and demons to your final army.  You’ll search your deck, pull your rewards, form your army…and compare against your opponent.

Greatest power among final armies wins all!
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Captain Marvel's Own Game, SHAZAM! (1946)

Here's a good, clean, wholesome game for the whole family. Wonderful for entertaining your guests and friends. It's the progressive building type of game, featuring CAPTAIN MARVEL and BILLY BATSON, in which each player turns the spinner to determine the extent of his progress. First player to complete the building subject repeats the magic word "SHAZAM" to declare himself the winner. Exciting! Entertaining!

-

Captain Marvel memorabilia is extremely rare and highly sought by golden age comic fans, but other than collectors, people don't know this game exits.

There have been questions pertaining to the publication date. Out of curiosity, we paged through all the Captain Marvel Adventures comic books from 1944 to 1946. We spotted the first ad for this game in issue 56. March 15, 1946.

boardgame: World War II: The Struggle for Europe and Asia
description:
Designed to be a perfect mix of the aesthetically appealing but short strategy war-games with the more realistic, longer, and difficult conflict simulations. World War II: The Struggle for Europe and Asia contains a truly unique and challenging game system. Play tested for more than 10 years, the game is a labor of love for its designers and artists that has a good balance between playability and historical accuracy.

The core mechanic is an alternating movement system based on leadership initiatives. Players are always involved managing such actions as mechanized breakthrough's, unit operational ranges, reinforcements going into battles, and naval fleet pursuits on a die-12 combination with CRT's. Game features setup for 1939 and 1941 starting dates, historical build limits, and optional political cards for players to fight on a beautiful global map. Players may discover that many of the decisions made then may be the right ones for them now or explore other options as they present themselves.

Turnip28 is a tabletop miniatures wargame for two or more players set in a Napoleonics-inspired post-apocalypse. Players command regiments of a couple of dozen models with rusted bayonets and an obsession with root vegetables.

The rules are light and highly lucky, but with strategy coming from having your incompetent units support each other, and manoeuvring your opponent into dangerous terrain.

Although there are some official models available, converting historical models from other companies is encouraged. The rules are available online for free.

description:
In the vast territories of Argatoria every day, wild tribes of Barbarians, Reptilians and other races fight, led to battle by great generals and mighty champions. Overwhelmed by the vision of the power or the whispers of the gods, they unleash great wars, which are harnessed by huge beasts, machines and magic. The voice of the drums mixes with cries of warriors, big cities and strongholds are burning, and great armies are coming from each side. It's the time of blood, time of war, time of Argatoria!

In Argatoria Wargame you command one of four armies - Arox, Sorgax, Vaendral, or Dirandis. Your army may include from a few to over a dozen units, as well as champions, great beasts, prayers and unusual spells that will allow you to fight even more exciting fights.

Each turn you can move, charge, melee, shoot etc with your army units. Too win you have to destroy your opponent army och do the mission in the scenario your plaing.

description: An exciting quartet jump-off game for children 4 years and older. Choose from foxes, rabbits, bumblebees, trees and flowers and grab your opponent's cards. Gradually, varied extensions and special rules can be added to the game. This is not a normal quartet game, but due to its different special rules it is an own exciting game.
The goal of the game is to get rid of your cards by playing them in common decks. First on to do so wins the game.

The Har Kuron Hurricane has descended on the Gnarlwood, and Gryselle the Slaughterer leads her band of gladiators on a mission of sanctified murder to hone their skills. The Arenai are masterful warriors, each a veteran of countless mortal duels – but they are also peerless performers, their acrobatic leaps and dazzling flourishes making every act of carnage a prayer to their bloody god. Gryselle’s coterie of killers each has her own speciality – Retaria vaults across the battlefield while Kalexis launches lightning-fast attacks with her bladed armour. Thrialla uses a barbed whip to toy with her victims, as Traxya punishes each misstep made by her opponent with her blade and buckler.

Gryselle's Arenai are an aggressive warband seeking to drown the Gnarlwood in blood. Thanks to their persistent Flourish ploys and deadly Combo attacks, these fast-moving aelves can put out a huge amount of damage while nimbly dancing away from any reprisals. This set includes a full Rivals deck to use straight out of the box, and the models are all push-fit – so you can get playing nice and quick.

This set includes:

     5x push-fit Gryselle's Arenai miniatures – no glue required to assemble, cast in bloody red plastic to stand out even when unpainted



     5x double-sided fighter cards, one for each warrior in the warband – Gryselle the Slaughterer, Retaria the Entangler, Kalexis the Silvered Blur, Thrialla the Lash, and Traxya the Aegis



     1x Gryselle's Arenai Warband Background / Rivals Deck Card


A 32-card Rivals deck consisting of:

     12x Gryselle's Arenai objective cards
     10x Gryselle's Arenai gambit cards
     10x Gryselle's Arenai upgrade cards


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Gnarlwood – Beastbound Assault Rivals Deck
description:
The root-halls under the Gnarlwood are uncharted territory, where lurking horrors prey on the unwary. Yet even these savage denizens can be bound – whether through magical means or impressive feats of animal handling – and trained to fight alongside warriors with the willpower to command them. Such a warband can charge into battle alongside a terrifying menagerie of beasts which tear into their enemies with vicious stingers, gnashing teeth, and rending claws in a true Beastbound Assault.

Get stuck into Warhammer Underworlds or mix up your game with the complete Beastbound Assault Rivals deck, usable by any warband – including those from past seasons. These cards can be used as a standalone deck in Rivals games, mixed with a warband's unique cards for Nemesis games, or used to build a deck from the ground-up in Relic games. This deck doesn’t include any spells, so warbands from any season can put it to good use.

Each card is brand new to the game, focused on making aggressive plays that can flex into a control style thanks to unique Denizen upgrades. Warbands containing a number of Minions and Beasts, such as Hexbanes's Hunters, the Gnarlspirit Pack, the Exiled Dead, and Blackpowder's Buccaneers will do especially well with this deck.

This deck contains a 32-card universal Rivals deck, consisting of:

     12x objective cards
     10x gambit cards
     10x upgrade cards


1x background card containing a deck list of the Beastbound Assault cards

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Gnarlwood – Rivals of Nethermaze
description:
Skittershank’s Clawpack and the Shadeborn take their shadowy battles out of the Nethermaze and into Ghur, the Realm of Beasts. Originally available in the Warhammer Underworlds: Nethermaze Core Set, this kit contains the miniatures and fighter cards for both warbands, as well as a complete Rivals deck of 32 cards for each. It’s a fantastic way to expand your collection with two amazing warbands – and you can even add the miniatures to your Warhammer Age of Sigmar armies.

This set contains:


     The complete Shadeborn warband – Murderous aelven Shadowstalkers that utilise the umbral arts, melting into the shadows and then reappearing to strike down their foes from behind. Quickly assemble your warband with four push-fit models, cast in a deep mauve to stand out even when unpainted, plus fighter cards and a full Rivals deck



     The complete Skittershank’s Clawpack warband – Skaven assassins that exhibit all the cunning and cruelty of the Clans Eshin. With five push-fit models in skulking brown plastic, plus fighter cards and a pre-made Rivals deck


—description from the publisher

Humans are such weird little creatures. It must be fun to be one. Why not dress up in your finest human costume and find out what they're really like by joining their party? Just be on your best human behavior or they'll figure out you're not supposed to be there!

In this game you play as non-humans attending a costume party hosted by a human. It is your job to blend in without your non human traits ruining the party. Since all guests are in costume, it shouldn't be too hard to blend in! All you have to do is act human, which we all know how to do right?

—description from the publisher


You are not a human, but you like human things like small talk and parties. You and some of your other non-human friends decide to dress up as humans and attend a human party. However, your non-human quirks show up at the most inconvenient times. Try to make your way through the party without it getting too out of hand.

Players gamify small talk in this larp, improvisation, awkward game of trying to not show your true self. Create basics of a non-human, flip over a card to see what the small talk conversation topic is, and then talk away!

—description from the designer

うんこふんだ！ (Unko Funda!) is a bluffing game from Japan. The game title translates literally as 'to step in poo!'. Players are walking around town trying to avoid stepping on dog poo on the pavement.

The game has 41 cards. Some are poo cards, some are score cards and some score cards have special actions. The deck is shuffled and the cards are all laid out face-down. They can be set out how you like, in a grid, in a loose mess, anyway you like as long as they are laid out separately.

On a player's turn, they take a card and look at it secretly. Then they choose to put it back face-down, or to keep it. If they keep it, and it is a poo card, it goes face-down in front of them and has no benefit. If it is a score card, it goes face-up and if it is a special action, that takes effect.

If the player puts the card back, it goes back face-down. The other players now have a chance to challenge the turn player, calling out Unko Funda! if they do. The card is turned up. If the card is poo, the turn player is eliminated from the game and scores zero at the end. The player who called out Unko Funda! keeps the poo card face up in front of themself, and it scores 2 points at the end.

But if the card is not a poo card, the turn player collects it face up, and the challenger is eliminated. So as players draw and play cards face down, they trying to bluff each other whether or not the card was poo.

Players use a mix of bluff, deduction and knowing what cards they have seen to work out if the card played is poo or not.

At the end of the game, the players still in the game calculate their scores, and the most points wins.
user summary

boardgame: ブラックジャンケン！ (Black Janken!)
description:
ブラックジャンケン！ (Black Janken!) is a shedding card game from Japan. The game uses a sock-Scissors-paper mechanism (called Janken in Japan), but the winner is the first person to clear their hand.

The game has 9 yellow and 9 white cards, and 3 black cards, showing rock/scissors/paper evenly. However, the white cards show the RSP on both sides, giving information to the opponents. And if a player wins a round with a black card, that card is picked up by the loser.

The game is for 3 to 7 players, and card distribution varies according to the number of players. But players end up with a random selection of cards in hand. Cards are held so that opponents can see clearly who has what colour cards in hand.

On their turn, a player nominates another to battle. The players call out Rock Scissors Paper PON! as a 1-2-3-Go! call, and play their card immediately on the final shout.

If the battle is won, the winner sheds their winning card, unless it was a black card in which case the loser collects it to their hand. The loser collects their losing card back into their hand, and the turn moves to the next player after the turn player.

When one player has cleared their hand, they win.
user summary

A card game for the whole crew. Whether it's games night at yours, or maybe just that deck of cards you take with you on holiday, this will be the game to play and your soon to be favourite.

You start off with a Castle Keep and a hero to defend the Keep.  You have a hand of five cards.  On your turn you either play up to 2 cards or discard up to 5 cards and redraw.  When you play a card, you can either attack your opponent by playing attack cards to reduce the strength of the hero and/or keep, or you play a card with a special ability.  When you are being attacked you can either play a shield to defend the attack or play a counter attack card to inflict damage on the attacker.  At the end of your turn redraw up to 5 cards.  When a player has lost their Keep, they are out of the game.  The last player standing wins.

boardgame: ウィッチズウィスク (Witches Whisk)
description:
ウィッチズウィスク (Witches Whisk) is a game from Japan, with apprentice witches at a magic school flying on broomsticks over the sports field. The game can be played as a competitive game for a single winner by scoring victory points, or as a co-operative game trying to stop the escaping magical creatures. The main rules apply for both games and the co-op game can be played solo.

The game has a subtitle ～ジェレミア魔法学校の訓練法～(~Jeremiah Magic School Training Method~).

Players use dice to place against three actions: Activating Magic, Movement, and Student Skills.

By placing dice against the six different magic circuit cards, a player can gain magic spells from them. Magic is used when attacking other players, or the creatures. Dice used on the magic broom of a student allows the player to move that witch across the playing field. Each student has special skills which are used once per round with the dice.

The game ends after six rounds. Players need to balance the development of their students, combining attack and defence, and movement and growth of skills. The more a student develops, the more they can achieve by the final round.

In a 2 player game, each player controls two witches. In a co-operative game, players can pass on good dice rolls. In the competitive game, getting to be start player gives you advantages.

The second edition released in 2022 had improved components and box art, but the gameplay was the same.
user summary

boardgame: Quests Over Coffee: Expansion #04 – Magic or Magic
description:
Is it magic or is it magic? Our world is filled with what we know to be magic, which is often confused with parlor tricks disguised as magic. Who can tell the difference? Is it illusion? Is it true magic? Magic. Magic, magic, magic-magic! Magic.

—description from the publisher

Expansion 4 - Magic or Magic expands Quests over Coffee, offering new cards:


     5 Quest Cards
     3 Item Cards
     1 Environment Card

Quests Over Coffee: Dice Game is a dice rolling game where you are trying to get the highest score against your opponent. It is a childhood game that has been experienced by many of the different patrons in the Quests Over Coffee world, but each with their different spin on scoring and winning the game.

—description from publisher

Supremacy Expandable Card Game is a game based in science fiction universe where battle is fought by alien races for control of the galaxy. Player's mission is to defend their own base (HQ) while destroying the opponent's base, as is known from RTS computer games.

Each player has two decks, the main deck contains 40 cards, the support deck contains a maximum of 40 cards, and the HQ card. Players construct decks having infantry, drones, mechs, spaceships, battle stations, and creatures from outer space regions.

At the beginning, each player takes 7 cards from the top of the main deck, and then they are placed face down near HQ card represent life of that HQ. Next, takes 4 cards into player's hand. Each turn, players take total of 2 cards into their hand to choose from two decks (except for the first turn).

—description from the designer

An unofficial promotional Role card “Teacher”, released as part of the "Pandemic: Teaching STEM Through Public Health" campaign, in collaboration with the Global Citizenship Experience Lab School. This role and its special abilities were envisioned to represent the way teachers work with students in order to further the reach of information. By passing on knowledge, we are able to continue to “stand on the shoulders of giants.”

In addition to the role card, you will need one pawn to represent the teacher and two pawns to represent students.

Rules:

The Teacher has a unique Role effect: You may use an action to ‘teach’ and create a new pawn in the city you are in. This new pawn is a student pawn. It can be used to do all basic actions in the game but does not have the special ability to create a student pawn.

The teacher and student(s) will share access to the same information and will both use your hand of cards. However, you are still subject to hand limits.

At the start of your turn, choose either the Teacher pawn or one of your student pawns. That pawn will complete the four actions for the turn.

Clarifications: 
- Only the teacher pawn can create student pawns. If you are using multiples of the same color pawn, keep track of which is the teacher pawn with a mark of some sort.
- Only two student pawns can be created during each game.
- Once you start using a pawn, you must finish the turn using just that pawn. You may choose a different pawn on your next turn.

Try and recognize natural phenomena, world traditions, places and animals from amazing photographs on cards. One player selects one of the open cards and everyone writes what they see and where in the world they think it might occur. Both the right place and phenomenon score you points, and the most points wins.
family: Series: Of the World (Tactic small square box)

description:
A game for 2-4 treasure hunting little monkeys. Sneak into the Pirate cove and snag gold coins to your own base. Beware of the pirate!

Roll the die, and move your monkey pawn according to the color shown on the die face. Once you get to a ladder space, you can enter the treasure chamber and put one of the coins on your monkey. Return to home base with the coin and you score a point. If you roll the pirate, you can move it and try to scare the other monkeys.

First one to get to 3 gold coins wins.

Who will take over the Mexican’s business to grow the business?

Play as one of the eight characters from "Les Tontons Flingueurs" - "Crooks in Clover" universe: Fernand, Raoul, Me Folace... and turn around the places where the Mexican managed his small business to harvest the "grisbi" (Loot).

At each turn of the game, you will have to secretly choose the place of destination of your character with regard to the expected gain in it and provide it with a Temperament card to settle its accounts with the competition, show trickery or even increase the collection that you will not fail to make once you have arrived at your destination.

When at the end of the seven rounds everyone will have explained themselves and will have collected the money, it will be time to count their loot.
The richest player wins and Patricia marries the enraged Treble Clef!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Qui prendra la succession des affaires du Mexicain pour faire fructifier le business ?

Incarnez l'un des huit personnages de l'univers des Tontons : Fernand, Raoul, Me Folace… et effectuez la tournée des lieux dans lesquels le Mexicain gérait ses petites affaires pour récolter le grisbi.

Chaque joueur incarne l'un des personnages fameux de l'univers des Tontons. Il se déplacera secrètement vers différents lieux afin d'y récupérer le maximum de pognons tout en se débarrassant de la concurrence à l'aide de cartes tempérament qui lui sont associées ou en augmentant ses gains. L'interaction entre joueurs est permanente. Jeu rapide de bluff et de coups fourrés.

Lorsqu'à l'issue des sept tours chacun se sera expliqué et aura engrangé le pognon, il sera temps de compter son butin. Le joueur le plus riche l'emporte et Patricia se marie avec l'enragé de clef de sol !

—description from the publisher

boardgame: サキュバスフィーバー (Succubus Fever)
description:
The succubus boom is unprecedented in the human world. The Succubus Breeding Center in the demon world is busy trying to ride this wave.
This is a card game in which you aim to minimize the loss of points as much as possible by placing cards on the common field to satisfy the color and number restrictions and by using up all the cards in your hand.
